title: harmonik run --max-concurrent 2 hangs after run_started — no claude spawn, no further events
description: Repro 2026-05-20 02:29 UTC. Command: harmonik run --beads hk-ynn8u,hk-uhvjo --max-concurrent 2. Daemon emitted run_started events for both beads (run_ids 019e43db-ecde-... and 019e43db-ee8b-...) at 22:29:08. NOTHING followed for 7 hours — no handler_capabilities, no session_log_location, no skills_provisioned, no launch_initiated, no agent_ready, no agent_heartbeat. No claude processes ever appeared in ps. No tmux windows ever appeared for either run_id. Compare prior healthy runs (20:54, 21:13): handler_capabilities arrives within ~90ms of run_started, agent_ready within ~1s. Affected version: harmonik built from main at a61823d (post hk-wx8z8 parallel-pane-allocator fix + post hk-cj0gm runWait reconciliation). Hypothesis: parallel SpawnWindow path has a runtime deadlock not caught by unit tests (composition-root-wiring miss per L-049). Files to look at: internal/daemon/tmuxsubstrate.go SpawnWindow + per-session paneID code, internal/daemon/handler*.go launch path, internal/daemon/workloop.go dispatch loop. Recovery: SIGKILL only — see sibling bead for SIGINT/SIGTERM ignored. Evidence: .harmonik/events/events.jsonl tail at 22:29:08 + 7-hour gap. Queue renamed to queue.json.silent-after-runstarted-20260520.
close_reason: Subsumed: landed as a92090b

title: MVH-P4: reset script for exploratory wave
description:
EXPLORATORY_TESTING_PLAN.md §4 P4. A shell script that, given a project directory, resets it to a clean exploratory-test starting state.

Location: NEW `test/exploratory/reset.sh`.

Behavior:
1. Accepts `--project <DIR>` (required) and `--fixture-corpus <DIR>` (required; path to a pre-seeded `.beads/` snapshot — see P1 bead once it lands).
2. Removes `<project>/.harmonik/` recursively if present.
3. Force-removes any git worktree under `<project>/.claude/worktrees/agent-*` that exists.
4. Removes `<project>/.beads/` if present.
5. Copies the fixture-corpus snapshot into `<project>/.beads/`.
6. No network access required.

Acceptance:
- Bash-shellcheck clean.
- Idempotent: running twice in a row succeeds and produces the same end state.
- Errors clearly if `--project` or `--fixture-corpus` is missing.

Out of scope: the fixture corpus itself (separate bead P1 — file after this one if not already filed).

title: EV-001 violation: busimpl.Emit writes payload-only to JSONL, omitting all envelope fields (event_id, schema_version, type, timestamp_wall, source_subsystem)
description: Exploratory tester T5 probe (TestT5_EnvelopeFieldsInJSONL) confirmed: busimpl.Emit writes only redactedBytes (the payload map) to the JSONL file via JSONLWriter.Append. The full core.Event envelope is constructed at line 201-204 of busimpl.go for in-memory consumer dispatch but is NEVER serialized to disk. Result: every JSONL line is missing event_id, schema_version, type, timestamp_wall, source_subsystem, and the outer payload wrapper. Spec EV-001 (event-model.md §4.1) requires all six fields in every JSONL record. Fix: busimpl.Emit must serialize the complete core.Event struct (including all envelope fields) to JSONL, not just the payload bytes. Location: internal/eventbus/busimpl.go line 188-190. Reproduced: yes (probe test FAIL output). Severity: HIGH — JSONL is not spec-conformant; any consumer or reconciliation tool reading the log will fail to find required fields.
close_reason: fix: Emit now marshals complete core.Event envelope to JSONL; TestT5_EnvelopeFieldsInJSONL (S2) green

title: daemon/reviewer: review-target.md never written before reviewer pane launch — 'structural invariant violated: task file absent'
description:
On hk-xegej run (run_id 019e4e06-66f1-7c8e-8990-2939e9906833), implementer phase succeeded (commit fc51bea landed in worktree), reviewer_launched fired at 21:56:13, agent_ready fired at 21:56:14, but reviewer pane received no brief because .harmonik/review-target.md was never created.

Console error:
  daemon: pasteinject: reviewer: tmux: structural invariant violated: task file absent: /Users/gb/github/harmonik/.harmonik/worktrees/019e4e06-66f1-7c8e-8990-2939e9906833/.harmonik/review-target.md (skipping inject)

Worktree inspection showed only agent-task.md present in .harmonik/; review-target.md missing entirely.

Symptom: reviewer claude sat idle for 45+ minutes with no work, daemon stuck waiting on the dead reviewer, blocking the rest of a 6-bead batch (hk-ndysh,hk-wkzlc,hk-8uy6m,hk-59lg8,hk-jon6r all stranded pending).

Recovery (manual): kill harmonik, ff-merge worktree branch to main, close bead, file this bead, re-dispatch remaining beads with --no-review-loop as workaround.

Likely fix: internal/daemon/reviewloop.go must write review-target.md (containing the review brief: bead, commit SHA range, implementer's exit summary, the agent-reviewer prompt) before signaling reviewer_launched. Composes with HARD-RULE 'EVERY BEAD GETS A REVIEW PHASE' — without this, --review-loop is silently broken.

Repro: any review-loop dispatch where implementer commits successfully → reviewer launched → review-target.md missing → 'skipping inject' error.

Refs: hk-xegej (fc51bea — successful implementer), hk-930o3 (similar brief-delivery bug class but on implementer path).
close_reason: Fixed via 61f084a — sub-agent added WriteReviewTarget call before reviewer claudeRunCtx construction.

title: HITL decision-surfacing: aggregate decisions-needed-by-a-human across agents/threads so they do not get buried (kerf + harmonik)
description: PROBLEM: Agents work through issues then periodically hit a DECISION POINT needing a human (a product call, an authorization, a pick among options). Today that decision-context gets BURIED in a long agent thread/session: the human sees it only if actively reading that thread, the agent stalls or guesses, and the decision can get lost. Nothing aggregates open decisions-awaiting-a-human across agents/works. EVIDENCE this session: B11 (parked awaiting go), the 4 agent-comms problem-space positions (awaiting confirm), the reset call, the doc runtime-only call all sat buried in-thread, surfaced only because the user was actively reading. IDEA: a first-class decision-needed surface. KERF angle (probable home): a blocked-on-human / decision-needed state on a bead or work; a kerf decisions view aggregates all open human-decisions across works into ONE what-needs-me queue. Each decision = question + concrete options + context-link (bead/work/thread) + who-is-blocked + a resolution slot. HARMONIK angle: since harmonik manages the running agents, an agent hitting a HITL point emits a structured decision_needed EVENT on the event bus; harmonik aggregates + a harmonik decisions command / operator surface lists open ones; the human answer flows back to unblock the agent. This is the agent-to-human DUAL of the agent-to-agent comms work (hk-uxm0j): same bus + typed message, routed to/from the human and tracked as a decision-with-resolution. RELATED: hk-uxm0j (shared bus/delivery), hk-ekap1 (an agent blocked-on-decision is a supervisor-known state). WHY: unblocks agents faster, gives the human ONE cross-agent what-needs-me queue, removes the read-every-thread burden. STATUS: idea write-up (user 2026-06-01, can come back to it). Likely a kerf work later. contrib-open: any agent may add angles.

title: imrest-followup: audit-log actor=project_hash provenance for orphan-sweep bead-reset
description:
Follow-up to hk-iuaed.4 (PL-006 sixth-bullet sweep). At MVH, lifecycle.SweepStaleInProgressBeads uses local claim-intent presence as the sole provenance signal — which conflates with exclusion (a) (live claim intent re-driven by BI-031), making the reset path effectively unreachable in production: a bead with claim intent is excluded by (a); a bead without claim intent fails provenance.

The lifecycle code now exposes a ProvenanceChecker seam (OrphanSweepConfig.BeadProvenance) that, when wired, establishes ownership independently. Unit + daemon-level integration tests exercise the reset-firing path via this seam.

To make the path reachable in production, either:
1. Beads v0.x changes the audit-log actor field to carry project_hash on harmonik-issued status_changed events (the spec PL-006a primary signal), and harmonik wires a ProvenanceChecker that consults br audit-log; OR
2. Harmonik writes a per-project ownership marker on claim (e.g., a separate .harmonik/beads-owned/<bead-id> sentinel file that outlives intent-log cleanup).

Wire whichever path is chosen via daemon.OrphanSweepConfig.BeadProvenance in internal/daemon/daemon.go.

Spec ref: process-lifecycle.md §4.5 PL-006 sixth bullet (provenance OR clause); §4.4 PL-006a (project_hash discipline).
Discovered while implementing hk-iuaed.4.

title: queue-submit: HandlerAdapter discards mutated *Queue and events; QueueStore never updated
description: The composition root wires queue.NewHandlerAdapter (rpc.go:452) with only ledger + projectDir. HandlerAdapter.HandleQueueSubmit (rpc.go:458-474) calls HandleQueueSubmit which validates and persists queue.json on disk, then discards the returned *Queue and []LedgerDepPair. The daemon-singleton QueueStore (daemon.go:466, threaded into workloop at daemon.go:558) is never populated by submit; only LoadQueueAtStartup populates it. Consequence: after 'hk queue submit <file>', the workloop's deps.queueStore.Queue() returns nil (workloop.go:457-458) and the queue-pull path is skipped until the daemon restarts. Same gap for queue-append (rpc.go:484): mutated queue + events discarded, never written back to QueueStore and never persisted. Fix: HandlerAdapter needs a *daemon.QueueStore reference so it can call SetQueue (submit) / LockForMutation+SetQueue (append) after Persist. Also: queue_submitted / queue_group_started / queue_appended / queue_item_deferred_for_ledger_dep events returned by handlers are never emitted (no bus injection in HandlerAdapter).
close_reason: Fixed in 40eeb9b: HandlerAdapter now threads QueueStore+bus; calls qs.SetQueue after Persist on submit. New tests in internal/scenario/queue_setqueue_wiring_test.go.

title: hk dogfood smoke test — verify the loop runs against a real Claude subprocess end-to-end
description:
## Why this is top priority

Workflow-modes corpus (epic hk-7om2q) and post-MVH parallelism (epic hk-e61c3) both landed. The loop is wired end-to-end in tests (twin handler + fixture stubs). But **no one has run hk against a REAL Claude Code subprocess on a real bead end-to-end yet**. Before any further investment (DOT workflow format, multi-bead dogfooding, etc.), we need a smoke run that catches the gaps fixture tests cannot.

## Likely failure surfaces (from loop investigation report)

1. **Prompt/instruction injection** — what hk passes to the `claude` subprocess. Does it inject the bead body? Role/system prompts? The verdict-format expectation (subprocess must write `review.iter-N.json` that `workspace.ReadReviewVerdict` parses)?
2. **Authentication / session resumption** — `ClaudeCodeAdapter.RotateAccount` was a stub. `ParseClaudeSessionID` exists (T-WM-018) but real-world session reuse untested.
3. **Worktree CWD / env** — does the Claude subprocess inherit the right working directory?
4. **Output parsing** — adapter's `DetectReady`/`DetectRateLimit`/`CleanExitSequence` rely on text patterns; the real Claude CLI may not emit exactly what they expect.

## Children

- `<smoke-1>` — TRACE (read-only research): document what hk feeds to the Claude subprocess and what it expects back. Output is a short markdown doc.
- `<smoke-2>` — RUN: create disposable bead in an isolated `.beads/` directory, run hk against it, capture observations.

Follow-up beads will be filed by the smoke-2 implementer as gaps surface — they should NOT try to fix gaps in line; the smoke is observational.

## Out of scope
- DOT workflow format (deferred until smoke is green).
- Multi-bead dogfooding (also deferred).
- Fixing whatever the smoke surfaces — that's separate, child-of-this-epic beads to be filed by the agent.

## Acceptance
- Smoke-1 doc landed.
- Smoke-2 produces a report with: command invoked, JSONL trail, verdict file contents (or absence), bead close status, worktree state.
- All gaps surfaced are captured as follow-up beads with this epic as parent.
close_reason: Dogfood smoke loop verified end-to-end against real claude subprocess. GREEN run 2026-05-15 (hk-kqdpf.5): marker.txt mutated, commit landed, outcome_emitted recorded, bead closed only on real work. See docs/dogfood-smoke-run-2026-05-15-bridge-substrate.md.

title: Smoke-1: trace what hk feeds to the Claude subprocess and what it expects back
description:
## Goal
READ-ONLY investigation. No code changes. Produce a short markdown doc that the smoke-2 implementer can read before running hk live.

## What the doc must answer

1. **Subprocess invocation.** How does hk invoke the Claude CLI? Cite `internal/handler/handler.go` Launch + `internal/handler/adapter_claudecode.go`. What is the exact argv? Environment variables passed/inherited? Working directory?
2. **Prompt injection.** What text does hk feed to Claude as the task description? Trace from `LaunchSpec` construction in `internal/daemon/reviewloop.go` (implementer path) through to the subprocess stdin / argv. Does it inject the bead body? The role? Any system prompt? Citing files + lines.
3. **Verdict expectation.** What file is hk waiting for? `workspace.ReadReviewVerdict()` — what path, what JSON schema? Cite `internal/workspace/`. What does an APPROVE verdict look like on disk? A REQUEST_CHANGES? A BLOCK?
4. **Single-mode vs review-loop-mode.** If the smoke bead has no `workflow:<mode>` label, what mode does hk default to (per moderesolve.go)? What's the implementer expected to do in single mode vs review-loop mode?
5. **Auth & session.** Does hk pass any auth-related state to the subprocess, or does it rely on the user's local Claude CLI auth? Cite `ClaudeCodeAdapter`.
6. **Worktree.** What does the Claude subprocess see when it starts? CWD is the worktree path (`workspace.CreateWorktree` output)? What's already in that worktree — a fresh checkout of main + the bead worktree branch?

## Output
A new file `docs/dogfood-smoke-trace.md` with the above structured as section headers. Cite file:line throughout. Don't write more than 300 lines — this is a navigation aid, not a re-implementation.

## Acceptance
- Doc exists at `docs/dogfood-smoke-trace.md`.
- Every question above is answered with at least one file:line citation.
- The next bead's implementer (smoke-2) can read this doc and know how to invoke hk + what to look for in the run output.

## Process note
The orchestrator should run this as ONE sub-agent with the Explore subagent type — it's pure read research, no writes other than the output doc.
close_reason: TRACE delivered as docs/dogfood-smoke-trace.md; 8 audit goals covered, 3 fragile predictions for smoke-2 implementer.

title: Smoke-2: run hk against a disposable bead in an isolated test environment, capture observations
description:
## Prerequisites
- Smoke-1 doc exists at `docs/dogfood-smoke-trace.md`. Read it first.
- The `claude` CLI is installed and authenticated on the host (this is the user's local install).

## Setup
Create an ISOLATED test environment. Do NOT run hk against the real `/Users/gb/github/harmonik/.beads/` — that has 130+ open beads and hk would claim a real one. Instead:

1. `mktemp -d` a scratch project directory (e.g., `/tmp/hk-smoke-XXXX`).
2. Initialize a fresh git repo there with one commit on main.
3. Run `br init` (or copy the minimal `.beads/` skeleton) inside that scratch dir to create a fresh beads DB.
4. Create ONE disposable bead in that scratch DB. Bead acceptance: "Add a single line `// hk-smoke-test marker <iso-date>` to a file `marker.txt` in the worktree." Trivial, observable, idempotent. Label it `workflow:single` so it skips the review-loop and goes through the simplest mode (or `workflow:review-loop` if you want to also exercise the review path — pick one and document).
5. Build the hk binary: `go build -o /tmp/hk ./cmd/harmonik`.

## The run
Invoke: `/tmp/hk --project /tmp/hk-smoke-XXXX --max-concurrent 1` (or whatever flags the binary actually exposes; check `cmd/harmonik/main.go`).

Watch:
- The terminal output (stderr/stdout from hk + the Claude subprocess).
- The events JSONL inside the scratch dir's `.harmonik/events.jsonl` (or wherever it lands).
- The worktree dir under `.claude/worktrees/agent-<run_id>/` — what's in it before, during, after?
- The bead status in the scratch beads DB — does it transition to `in_progress` then `closed`?
- The verdict file: does `review.iter-1.json` (or equivalent) appear? What's in it?

Let the run go to completion (or hang for a configurable timeout — kill it with SIGINT and capture the partial state if it doesn't finish in 5 minutes).

## Report
Capture observations in `docs/dogfood-smoke-run-<date>.md`:

- Exact command invoked + binary version (git SHA of HEAD at build time).
- Whether the Claude subprocess started at all (process started? signal sent?).
- The hk events JSONL trail, copied verbatim.
- The verdict file contents, if any.
- The final bead state.
- The final worktree state (cleaned up? still present?).
- ANY surprises, errors, hangs, or panics — verbatim.

## Follow-ups
For EACH gap surfaced (auth failure, missing prompt injection, malformed verdict, hang, etc.), file a NEW bead with parent `hk-1n0cw`. Each follow-up bead names ONE concrete fix. Do NOT try to fix anything inline. The smoke is observational.

## Acceptance
- Run report exists at `docs/dogfood-smoke-run-<date>.md`.
- Every observation in the "Report" section above is recorded.
- Follow-up beads (≥0) are filed under `hk-1n0cw` for whatever needs fixing. If zero — hk worked end-to-end on first try — note that explicitly in the report (and we celebrate).

## Process note
The orchestrator should dispatch this as ONE implementer sub-agent (worktree isolation, model=sonnet, effort=high). The agent has license to file as many follow-up beads as needed.
close_reason: RED: daemon mechanics work; claude protocol surface entirely missing — 5 follow-up beads filed (hk-keb6o P0, hk-w5vra P0, hk-do7te P1, hk-nvrvp P1, hk-mz0x4 P2)

title: session-keeper Phase-2 cycle core: handoff(nonce)->confirm->clear->resume with fail-safe abort + events (fakes-only tests)
description:
Phase-2 CYCLE CORE (codename:session-keeper, parent hk-ekap1) — the intent-preserving reset. Builds on the foundation (internal/keeper/gates.go: CrispIdle, HoldingDispatch) + warn-mode watcher.go.

SAFETY (MANDATORY — read first): this performs a DESTRUCTIVE /clear on a managed session. (a) Act ONLY on an agent with a .harmonik/keeper/<agent>.managed marker (existing opt-in guard) — never otherwise. (b) ALL tests MUST use FAKES (spy injector, injectable clock + session-id source, fake handoff-file path) — NEVER spawn a real claude, NEVER run a real /clear, NEVER target a real agent name (flywheel/named-queues/controlpoints) or a convention-resolved real pane. (c) Fail-safe: NEVER inject /clear unless the handoff is positively confirmed (step 3).

CYCLE (fires only when gauge pct >= act_pct [default 90] AND CrispIdle(agent) AND NOT HoldingDispatch(agent) AND not suppressed):
1. Open cycle journal .harmonik/keeper/<agent>.cycle {cycle_id (monotonic), phase, opened_at} BEFORE any injection.
2. Inject '/session-handoff HANDOFF-<agent>.md' with a directive to embed '<!-- KEEPER:<cycle_id> -->' as a line in the handoff (keeper-supplied nonce, unique per cycle).
3. CONFIRM: poll HANDOFF-<agent>.md until it contains '<!-- KEEPER:<cycle_id> -->' (timeout handoff_timeout, default 180s). On timeout: ABORT — emit session_keeper_cycle_aborted{reason}, close journal, leave session UNTOUCHED (never /clear an unconfirmed handoff). This nonce replaces the non-unique date+branch stamp.
4. Inject '/clear'; journal phase=cleared.
5. Clear-readiness (best-effort, NOT a hard gate — spike proved resume survives /clear FIFO): wait up to clear_settle (default 3s) for the gauge to emit a NEW session_id, then proceed; if absent, proceed anyway and log session_keeper_clear_unconfirmed.
6. Inject '/session-resume HANDOFF-<agent>.md'; re-bind keeper per-session state to the new session_id.
7. Close journal; emit session_keeper_cycle_complete.
EVENTS (add constants to internal/core/eventtype.go; emit via the keeper FileEmitter): session_keeper_handoff_started, session_keeper_cycle_complete, session_keeper_cycle_aborted, session_keeper_clear_unconfirmed.
(Anti-loop suppression + crash recovery are the NEXT bead; for now just record the last cycle_id and do NOT re-fire within the same gauge session_id.)
TESTS (FAKES only): happy-path ordering (journal->handoff->nonce-confirm->/clear->resume->close); ABORT path (nonce never appears => timeout => NO /clear, session untouched, aborted event); gating (no fire unless pct>=act_pct AND CrispIdle AND not HoldingDispatch); no re-fire within the same session_id.
DONE: build+lint+gofmt green; tests pass with FAKES only; no real session ever touched. Refs: hk-22i70.
notes: Independent adversarial review (flywheel, 2026-06-08) of commit 67a74def: VERDICT APPROVE_WITH_NOTES. All 4 safety properties PASS — /clear is provably unreachable without a positively-confirmed current-cycle nonce; abort-path test locks it in; tests are FAKES-only; build/vet/gofmt/test all green. 5 defects found, NONE defeating the core fail-safe (and the Cycler is not yet wired live, DEFECT-5, so nothing destructive can fire today). DEFECT-1 (HIGH, empty session_id -> unbounded destructive re-fire) + DEFECT-3 (.managed guard only in caller) are HARD PREREQUISITES before live-wiring. All 5 defects consolidated into follow-up hk-kct9t (now P1). Full review captured there.
close_reason: done

title: harmonik queue submit while daemon idle doesn't advance pending → active — no wake-up signal
description: When the daemon is running idle (no active runs) and a fresh queue is submitted via 'harmonik queue submit', the group stays in status=pending forever. AdvanceGroup is only invoked from advanceQueueOnTerminal (workloop.go:1730) which fires on bead-terminal-transition. There is no path that wakes the workloop on RPC queue-submit. Workaround: kill daemon → restart (PL-005 step 8a loads queue.json + advances). Fix needed: queue-submit RPC must signal the workloop to re-evaluate group statuses (call AdvanceGroup on all groups, or trigger an immediate poll). Source: dogfood session 2026-05-21 testing append; queue 019e4b7b sat pending despite being kind=stream/status=active.
close_reason: done

title: Re-run spec-drift audit with full per-spec new-req enumeration (close the sampled-not-proven false-negative gap from hk-12ke1)
BI-003 br-serve sensor false-positives on .harmonik/worktrees/ during daemon activity
description: TestBRServeBI003_ForbiddenInvocation (internal/core/brserve_bi003_test.go) walks the repo tree for the literal 'br serve' and excludes its own source + docs/, but does NOT exclude .harmonik/worktrees/. When the daemon has ACTIVE bead worktrees, each contains a copy of brserve_bi003_test.go (which legitimately contains 'br serve' in comments/patterns), so the sensor finds those copies and FAILS with a false positive. REPRO: run 'go test ./internal/core/' on the main repo while the daemon has >=1 active worktree under .harmonik/worktrees/ (in-worktree runs pass since that .harmonik/worktrees/ is empty, which is why daemon-dispatched runs go green but a main-repo run fails). FIX: exclude .harmonik/ (or at least .harmonik/worktrees/) from the sensor's filesystem walk. Anchor: internal/core/brserve_bi003_test.go scan skip-list. Surfaced 2026-06-02 during hk-j3hrn coverage work; core suite was otherwise GREEN at 95.8%.
close_reason: done

title: DOGFOOD-BLOCKER: tmux pane race — 'no window spawned yet' on harmonik run
description: First dogfood of harmonik run --beads <X,Y> --max-concurrent 2 --review-loop revealed a tmux pane race. Daemon emits: 'pasteinject: implementer-initial SendEnterToLastPane: tmuxSubstrate.SendEnterToLastPane: no window spawned yet: tmux: structural invariant violated'. Subsequent 'WriteLastPane' fails for the same reason. Then 'reviewer: task file absent ... (skipping inject)'. Then 'reviewloop: verdict absent at iteration 1' → queue paused-by-failure. SECONDARY BUG: bead got marked CLOSED via br close from inside the worktree even though no implementation landed. The closes leaked back to main's .beads/issues.jsonl. Two beads (hk-x0y2k, hk-75rij) were reopened by orchestrator. P0 BLOCKER: dogfooding cannot proceed until the pane-race is fixed AND bead-close-without-commit-protection is added (or close is gated on a commit being present).
close_reason: Fixed at HEAD via stash recovery after merge-dance loss. reviewloop substrate wiring + agent-task bead-lifecycle guard.

title: kerf init: prints two overlapping but inconsistent AGENT SETUP INSTRUCTIONS blocks
description:
A single 'kerf init' invocation prints two distinct AGENT SETUP INSTRUCTIONS blocks back-to-back with different jig lists, commands, and headings (apparently one hard-coded by init and one from 'kerf setup', but unlabeled). An agent reading the output linearly cannot tell which instruction-set wins. Also neither block mentions any of the new commands (next, triage, pin, map, areas, work edit), so agents adopting them verbatim never learn the modern surface.
Discovered: docs/kerf-beta-feedback.md — '## kerf init' section, items 5 and 6.
Severity: MAJOR.

title: Orphan sweep on daemon startup doesn't reset beads stuck in_progress after SIGKILL recovery
description: Repro 2026-05-20: after SIGKILLing harmonik (hk-qd3f4 wedge), hk-ynn8u and hk-uhvjo remained in beads.jsonl status=in_progress. Next harmonik run startup fired daemon_orphan_sweep_completed with bead_in_progress_reset=0 — meaning the sweep did NOT detect them as orphans. Subsequent 'harmonik run --beads hk-ynn8u,hk-uhvjo' immediately exited with 'bead is not in a claimable state (status=in_progress; want open)'. Required: orphan-sweep must walk beads where status=in_progress AND no corresponding live run is observable in queue.json/runRegistry, and reset them to open. The 'no live run' detection is the missing piece. Manual workaround: 'br update <id> --status=open' before re-dispatch. Filed by user-driven re-probe of hk-012af fix.

description: FULL: Make standard-bead.dot the daemon dispatch default: embed it as the workflow.dot fallback so every bead with no explicit ref runs implement->gate->review->merge; keep review-loop as the floor fallback | SCOPE: cmd/harmonik/main.go + workloop.go default-workflow resolution + daemon.go | Source: harmonik-productization-design workflow 2026-06-02. codename:productization; integration-branch P0s gate the work-project deploy; the 3 beads editing mergeRunBranchToMain (target-branch threading, start_from retarget, post-merge build gate) MUST be dispatched SERIALLY.
design:
DESIGN: make standard-bead.dot the daemon dispatch DEFAULT. DEPENDS ON hk-p0kum (T1, the graph). This is a daemon dispatch-PATH change.

HOOKS (file:line on the design-time tree — re-confirm against current main):
1. cmd/harmonik/main.go:498 — flip the --workflow-mode default review-loop -> dot.
2. internal/daemon/moderesolve.go:99 — Tier-4 hard fallback WorkflowModeReviewLoop -> WorkflowModeDot.
3. internal/daemon/workloop.go:1869 — DOT-path resolver: when there is no itemWorkflowRef AND no <projectDir>/workflow.dot, load the EMBEDDED graph via a new //go:embed in internal/daemon/standardgraph.go + a new workflow.LoadDotWorkflowFromBytes (trivial: dot.Parse already takes a string).

REVIEW FLOOR (two layers, MUST preserve — never silently dispatch unreviewed):
(a) the embedded graph CONTAINS a reviewer node, so the DOT default is reviewed by construction;
(b) fail-safe: if the embed fails to load, fall through to the hardcoded review-loop, NEVER to single.
single must stay reachable ONLY via an explicit workflow:single label / --workflow-mode single (which already emits review_bypassed at moderesolve.go:68-73). VERIFY neither Tier-3 nor Tier-4 can resolve to single.

DEPLOY DISCIPLINE (same class as hk-9vp51 — this changes the dispatch default for ALL beads):
after merge + go install + daemon restart, MANDATORY live-smoke under the supervisor BEFORE declaring done: dispatch a bead with NO workflow ref and confirm it runs the DOT graph (implement -> commit_gate -> review -> close), NOT single/review-loop. Does NOT touch mergeRunBranchToMain.

Note: should also cover QM-002a Beads cross-check behavior on restart (dispatched items reverted to pending if Beads shows open). Deps: T31 (hk-fwpc0), T80 (hk-8vokz). Part of epic hk-lj0pb.

title: T6 finding: daemon work loop never reads bead body content
description: The daemon runWorkLoop dispatches a handler for each ready bead but never reads the bead body from Beads-SQLite. The ClaimBead call takes only the BeadID; the body stays in the DB. The handler subprocess (e.g., claude) would need to call br show itself to obtain the work spec. This is architecturally intentional (the bead body is the agent's brief, not the daemon's), but it means the daemon cannot validate that the body is valid/non-empty before claiming, and the handler must know to call br show <beadID> as its first action. Verified: a bead with 100KB body and a bead with empty body both dispatch and close identically from the daemon's perspective.
close_reason: Finding documented in beadLedger godoc (workloop.go); architecturally intentional, no code change required

title: Author AGENT_OPERATING_MANUAL.md distilling AGENTS.md + orchestrator-rules + 5 private mem
description: FULL: Author AGENT_OPERATING_MANUAL.md distilling AGENTS.md + orchestrator-rules + 5 private memory-note gotchas (env-strip billing, wide-waves, epic-dep, $TMUX, stale-binary) into one ordered operating doc; link DOWN into AGENTS.md, don't duplicate | SCOPE: /Users/gb/github/harmonik/AGENT_OPERATING_MANUAL.md (new) | Source: harmonik-productization-design workflow 2026-06-02. codename:productization; integration-branch P0s gate the work-project deploy; the 3 beads editing mergeRunBranchToMain (target-branch threading, start_from retarget, post-merge build gate) MUST be dispatched SERIALLY.
design:
IMPLEMENTER CONTEXT (orchestrator-injected 2026-06-03):

Create /Users/gb/github/harmonik/AGENT_OPERATING_MANUAL.md — one ordered operating doc for an agent running harmonik. DISTILL from AGENTS.md + docs/orchestrator-rules.md and LINK DOWN into them (do not duplicate large blocks). The unique value of this doc = the 5 hard-won operational gotchas below, which are NOT currently written anywhere in the repo. Fold each in as a numbered "Gotcha / Pitfall" giving the symptom + the fix:

1. ENV-STRIP / BILLING: Never put ANTHROPIC_API_KEY in an auto-sourced repo .env that a bare `harmonik --project` daemon can inherit. Daemon-spawned claude sessions inherit the parent env; an inherited API key makes claude bill pay-per-token API instead of the Max subscription -> fast credit burn (2026-05-30 incident: all credit consumed in ~2h). The credential deny-list scrubs ANTHROPIC_API_KEY / ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN / CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH* from every daemon-spawned claude; only `harmonik supervise start` reads .env and injects the key into Pi (the flywheel cognition process). Always start the daemon in --no-auto-pull (quiet) mode.

2. WIDE-WAVES (disk + CPU): --max-concurrent >= 6 exhausts disk (each isolated worktree ~= 26 MB; dozens add up) AND oversubscribes CPU (~10-core box; each implementer runs multi-core go build/test, so 8-10 wide makes every bead crawl and trips the ~10-min run_stale false alarm). Throughput knee is 4-5 wide. Biggest safe disk reclaim is `go clean -cache`. ALWAYS `git fetch && git reset --hard origin/main` before `go install` (the daemon pushes per-bead merges but local main lags; rebuilding from stale main silently ships a daemon WITHOUT the just-landed fix).

3. EPIC-DEP BLOCKS DISPATCH: `br dep add <task> <epic>` makes the task blocked-by the OPEN epic -> the daemon silently insta-fails dispatch (queue goes paused-by-failure with failed>0 but ZERO run_started; no implementer ever launches). Attach a bead to its kerf work via the `codename:<name>` LABEL, never an epic dependency.

4. $TMUX REQUIRED: `harmonik run` and `harmonik status` hard-fail ("$TMUX is not set", exit 1, no daemon spawns) when invoked from a shell not inside tmux. Wrap launches in a detached tmux session (`tmux new-session -d -s ...`). The persistent daemon is launched the same way.

5. STALE BINARY: After changing harmonik, ALWAYS `go install ./cmd/harmonik` AND restart the daemon (kill its tmux session / pkill, let the supervisor revive, or relaunch) before relying on the fix. A stale binary is the #1 cause of "but I already fixed that." Pairs with gotcha #2's reset-before-install.

STRUCTURE: ordered sections (orientation -> start the daemon -> the daily loop -> monitoring/subscribe -> failure triage -> the 5 gotchas -> comms bus). Keep it skimmable; prefer links over re-statement. SCOPE: only the new AGENT_OPERATING_MANUAL.md.
close_reason: done

title: DOT live run hangs: agent prompt pasted but never submitted (missing Enter) → run_stale
description:
P0 — blocks ALL DOT-mode live execution with real agents. First live 'harmonik run --workflow-mode dot --workflow-ref specs/examples/review-loop.dot --beads hk-3yz2d' hung at the implementer node.

ROOT CAUSE (high confidence): the DOT-mode agentic-node dispatch pastes the launch prompt into the claude tmux pane but NEVER SUBMITS it (no Enter/carriage-return). The agent sits with the prompt typed-but-unsent and idles until the stale-watcher fires.

EVIDENCE:
- Timeline (events.jsonl, run_id 019e6d27-3d84-7870-8fa0-d0db6c93a99b): 22:55:49 run_started → node_dispatch_requested(start) → node_dispatch_decided → node_dispatch_requested(implementer) → agent_ready; then NOTHING until 23:06:18 run_stale (10m29s). No node_completed, no commit.
- tmux pane %339 current screen showed the claude input box containing: '❯ Please read .harmonik/agent-task.md and begin.' — typed but NOT submitted (no response rendered above it; pane otherwise blank).
- The prompt string 'Please read .harmonik/agent-task.md and begin.' = 46 bytes, matching the daemon_pane_write log 'purpose=task payload_bytes=47'.
- Task delivery itself WORKS: .harmonik/agent-task.md (2478 bytes, well-formed hk-3yz2d task) was correctly written into the worktree. ONLY the submit is missing.
- Run worktree at HEAD=main (23e6d99), no implementer commit; docs/qa-scratch/ never created.

WHY TESTS MISSED IT: the daemon DOT e2e (internal/daemon/workloop_dot_mode_e2e_hklphyf_test.go) uses a stub /bin/sh handler that bypasses the tmux paste→submit path entirely. The submit step is only exercised by the real claude tmux dispatch, which no automated test covers.

FIX: in the DOT cascade agentic-node dispatch (internal/daemon/dot_cascade.go driveDotWorkflow + the agent-launch it calls), submit the pasted prompt the same way the builtin review-loop path does (internal/daemon/workloop.go / reviewloop.go + internal/daemon/pasteinject.go). Likely the DOT path loads+pastes the buffer but omits the send-Enter that the builtin path performs.

ACCEPTANCE: a live 'harmonik run --workflow-mode dot --workflow-ref specs/examples/review-loop.dot --beads <throwaway>' drives the implementer agent to actually start, commit, hand off to reviewer, reach the 'close' terminal, and close the bead. Source: QA session 2026-05-27, first live DOT run.
close_reason: Submit-hang FIXED + proven end-to-end (commit 704ddc0). Live DOT re-run of hk-3yz2d walked start→implementer→reviewer→close: implementer agent started, created the marker file, committed (aff08aa), reviewer returned APPROVE, cascade reached the 'close' terminal. The agent_ready gate works. (Run did not close the bead due to a SEPARATE merge bug — dirty .beads/issues.jsonl breaks the rebase merge — tracked in a new bead.)

title: queue submit/dry-run CLI may not route the queue name (file "name" field + --queue flag) — new-name submit rejected queue_already_active while only 'main' was active, despite per-name guard (NQ-A1) landed
description:
OBSERVED 2026-06-02 (named-queues session, durable anchors):

REPRO: with only the 'main' queue active (8 others paused-by-failure), both
  (a) harmonik queue dry-run /tmp/batch.json   (file had "name":"extqueue")
  (b) harmonik queue dry-run --queue extqueue --beads hk-...
returned queue_already_active (-32010). After 'harmonik queue cancel main', the SAME submit passed the single-active guard (then failed later on bead status, not the guard). => the guard fired on 'main' being active even though the target name was 'extqueue'.

ROOT-CAUSE HYPOTHESIS: per-name single-active guard itself is correct + landed (NQ-A1 hk-tigaf.2 = 1588d773; internal/queue/validation.go:236-318 'this per-name slot, not a global singleton'). So the likely gap is the CLI submit/dry-run path NOT forwarding the queue name to the daemon (file 'name' field per QueueSubmitRequest.Name json:"name", AND/OR the --queue flag), so the request defaults to QueueNameMain. Note: a separate named queue 'extq' DOES exist (created in a prior session), so name-routing is not uniformly broken — needs an investigator to find which submit path drops the name.

ANCHORS: internal/queue/types.go QueueSubmitRequest.Name (json:"name"); internal/queue/cli/submit.go (raw-JSON forward path, ~:54-60 per hk-xyycc note); cmd/harmonik submit/dry-run flag parsing (--queue); validation.go:291-318 (per-name guard + name-validation order). Investigate: does the daemon RECEIVE name=extqueue, or does the CLI send name=main/empty?

IMPACT: blocks the multi-queue daily-loop the named-queues epic just shipped — agents 'route work to your OWN --queue' can't if the CLI collapses every submit to main. Workaround in use: append to the already-named live queue ('extq').
close_reason: done

title: queue dry-run: human-readable default output; --json opt-in
description:
Depends on: hk-5553i

## Current behavior

harmonik queue dry-run writes raw JSON response to stdout unconditionally.
Code path: internal/queue/cli/dryrun.go RunQueueDryRun() → client.go handleResponse() (line 136).
handleResponse() at client.go:143 calls fmt.Fprintf(out, "%s\n", data) with the daemon's raw JSON.

## Desired behavior

Default: human-readable validation summary. Example output (valid):

  dry-run: OK
  items: 2 (hk-abc123, hk-def456)
  validation: passed — queue would be accepted

Example output (invalid):

  dry-run: FAILED
  error: validation error (code -32012)
  reason: bead hk-abc123 is not in a claimable state (status=closed)

With --json / --format json: existing raw JSON behavior, unchanged.

## Implementation sketch

1. Add --json / --format flags to RunQueueDryRun argument parsing (dryrun.go).
2. Add a renderQueueDryRunResponse function. On ok=true: print item list + passed message. On ok=false: print error code + reason as plain English.
3. --json path unchanged.

Note: dry-run validation errors are especially important to be readable — they surface bead-state mismatches that the operator needs to act on. Human-readable output here directly reduces turnaround time when fixing a broken queue submission.
close_reason: queue dry-run: human-readable default implemented; --json opt-in works

title: Daemon merge-to-main leaves working tree out of sync with HEAD
description:
After hk-ftyvo's mergeRunBranchToMain helper advances main's ref via git update-ref + push, the main repo's working tree is NOT refreshed to match HEAD. Running git status shows README.md modified (HEAD has new content, working tree has old). Reproduced 2026-05-15 after Phase 2 first-demo run (commit d50393b): git status showed staged 'modified: README.md' and an unstaged 'deleted' counterpart. Workaround: orchestrator ran 'git reset --hard HEAD' to resync. 

Suggested fix: in internal/daemon/workloop.go mergeRunBranchToMain, after successful update-ref + push, also run 'git reset --hard HEAD' OR 'git checkout-index -f -a' in the project root to refresh the working tree. Open question: what if the operator has uncommitted local changes? Spec the merge step's working-tree-refresh policy in execution-model.md (likely a new EM-054 alongside EM-052/053).
close_reason: Subsumed: landed as ref'd commit

title: Daemon FALSE-CLOSES a no-commit run as success: pasteinject quit-on-commit total-timeout path emits bead_closed+auto-close exit=0 when HEAD never advanced (hk-tigaf.4 closed with NQ-B1 code absent from main)
notes:
INVESTIGATION (do-not-change-status): root cause + blast radius + fix.

== ROOT CAUSE (file:line) ==
internal/daemon/workloop.go:1959-1976 — the SINGLE-MODE no-commit guard (hk-mmh8f) has a buggy `mainAdvanced` escape hatch (hk-cwxow). Guard logic:
  if worktree HEAD == headSHA (no commit by THIS bead's agent):
     mainAdvanced = (refs/heads/main != headSHA)
     if !mainAdvanced -> ReopenBead(no_commit)  [correct path]
     // else FALL THROUGH to auto-close
The escape hatch checks whether refs/heads/main moved past the run's PARENT sha — NOT whether THIS bead's work landed. With concurrent dispatch, sibling beads (hk-tigaf.8 @19:32, .10 @19:42, .7 @19:43) merged to main while hk-tigaf.4 sat in its 30-min commitPollTimeout. By the time hk-tigaf.4's guard ran at 19:47, main had advanced 4065d37d -> 5a7d5adb for UNRELATED reasons, so mainAdvanced=true, the guard was bypassed, and execution fell through to workloop.go:2003 (socketOutcome==nil && exit==clean). There mergeRunBranchToMain returns {noChange:true} (runTip==headSHA, workloop.go:2804 hk-cwxow guard) -> success else-branch -> emitOutcomeEmitted(approved) + CloseBead + emitDone(true,"auto-close: exit=0"). NOTE: this did NOT go through pasteinject's noChange-subsumed check (beadAlreadySubsumedInMain) — that path emits "noChange-subsumed: bead found in main", a different summary. pasteinject fired /quit correctly; the false-close is purely the workloop guard bypass.

The "noChange-subsumed check false-positives" hypothesis in the bead is NOT the cause here. beadAlreadySubsumedInMain was never reached for this run.

The reviewloop path (reviewloop.go:567-576, hk-9c1v4) is CORRECT: it compares headSHA==parentSHA with NO mainAdvanced escape, so it would have failed this run properly. The bug is single-mode-only; these final beads ran single-mode (summary "auto-close: exit=0", not "APPROVE at iteration N").

== BLAST RADIUS (full events.jsonl history) ==
40 auto-close:exit=0 runs ever. EXACTLY ONE matches the bug signature (auto-close success + implementer_phase_complete commit_landed=false):
  run 019e80f8-4e05-79a4-96f6-d0f9615a2cf1  bead hk-tigaf.4  — origin/main Refs hits=0, QueueName/per-queue-gate ABSENT from origin/main, bead currently OPEN (was falsely closed then reopened).
All other auto-close runs (incl. sibling hk-tigaf.1/2/3/5/6/7/8/9/10) had commit_landed=true and ARE on origin/main. No earlier silent drops. Only victim: hk-tigaf.4.

== PROPOSED FIX ==
workloop.go:1959-1976 — the guard's "main advanced" condition is necessary-but-not-sufficient. main advancing does NOT prove THIS bead's work landed. Replace the mainAdvanced escape with a positive subsumption check, OR drop the escape entirely and gate it on this bead's work being on main:
  - Best: when worktree HEAD == headSHA, fail as no_commit UNLESS beadAlreadySubsumedInMain(ctx, deps.projectDir, beadID) (same grep used at workloop.go:2104). i.e. replace `if !mainAdvanced {Reopen}` with `if !beadAlreadySubsumedInMain(...) {ReopenBead(no_commit); return}` and only fall through (to close-subsumed) when the bead's OWN Refs trailer is on main.
  - The hk-cwxow concern (avoid false non_ff_merge) is independently handled in mergeRunBranchToMain:2804 (runTip==headSHA -> noChange), so the workloop guard does NOT need the mainAdvanced escape at all; gating on beadAlreadySubsumedInMain both prevents the false non_ff AND closes this false-success hole.
Add a regression test: HEAD==headSHA, main advanced (sibling merge), bead NOT in main -> expect ReopenBead + run_failed(no_commit), NOT auto-close success.

== CONFIDENCE: HIGH ==
Causal chain fully corroborated by events.jsonl timestamps, git log merge times, the runTip==headSHA noChange path, and the single matching bug-signature run. Fix location is precise (workloop.go:1959-1976).
close_reason: Fixed: no-commit guard now checks per-bead landing via beadAlreadySubsumedInMain instead of 'did main move' (66bd1042); reviewer APPROVE; regression test fails-on-baseline/passes-on-fix. Residual unanchored-grep follow-up filed.

title: Spawn-semaphore exhaustion: launch_initiated emitted before SpawnWindow blocks on full slot pool -> launches wedge silently, no_commit fail at 30min
description: ROOT CAUSE (investigated 2026-06-03; SUPERSEDES the earlier WRONG tmux-orphan claim -- see comment history). launch_initiated is emitted BEFORE Launch/SpawnWindow (reviewloop.go:330-332; single-mode workloop.go:2033-2034). SpawnWindow then BLOCKS on the spawn semaphore (internal/daemon/tmuxsubstrate.go:216-224) when all slots are taken -> the run sits at launch_initiated with NO tmux window and never reaches implementer_phase_complete. Slot pool cap = maxConcurrent*2 (cmd/harmonik/main.go:668-679) = 12 at -c6. A slot releases ONLY on session.Kill (tmuxsubstrate.go:683-685). Each bead consumes an implementer slot, releases it (reviewloop.go:533), then acquires a reviewer slot; review-loop iterations accumulate forceTeardownSession defers (reviewloop.go:367,857) that release only at runReviewLoop RETURN. Under 6-wide concurrency this saturates the 12-slot pool -> further SpawnWindow calls block silently. INTERMITTENCY: below saturation launches succeed (13:09-13:21 batch ran fine); once enough impl+reviewer slots are concurrently held, new launches wedge -- state-dependent on live slot occupancy, NOT the binary. The fabled SELF-RECOVERY ~30min = commitPollTimeout (pasteinject.go:290): a sibling times out/completes, its Kill frees a slot, one blocked SpawnWindow proceeds; otherwise the wedged run FAILS no_commit at the timeout (observed: hk-rqx5o). run_stale at 10min (stalewatch.go:46) is mere detection. CONFIRMED NOT session-keeper (internal/keeper unreferenced in internal/daemon). MITIGATION (validated this session): restart resets the semaphore to 12 free; run reduced concurrency (set-concurrency 4) for headroom until fixed. FIX: (a) emit launch_initiated AFTER SpawnWindow returns so the event reflects a real spawn; (b) add a timeout + diagnostic to the semaphore acquire (tmuxsubstrate.go:216) emitting a spawn_cap_blocked event instead of blocking silently; (c) audit reviewer-phase slot release so a bead never holds 2+ slots across review-loop iterations -- release-before-reacquire OR raise cap to maxConcurrent*2 + headroom. P1: takes down dispatch under load for ALL agents.
close_reason: Landed 9b2848e3 (bounded spawn-cap acquire + launch_initiated reorder), deployed on pid 56791, reviewed APPROVE. Verified via strings <bin>|grep spawn_cap_blocked.

comments.author: gb
comments.text: CORRECTION (named-queues, 2026-06-03): my earlier description edit claiming the 128 tmux-orphan pile-up is THIS bug's root cause is DISPROVEN. After reaping all 122 orphans, a freshly-queued bead (hk-mgoo7) STILL wedged at launch_initiated with only 5 tmux sessions present. Evidence (events.jsonl): stuck runs emit run_started/handler_capabilities/session_log_location/skills_provisioned/launch_initiated then NOTHING -- the implementer is never spawned into a tmux session (0 created) -- vs a healthy run that reaches implementer_phase_complete ~63s after launch_initiated. And it does NOT self-recover: hk-rqx5o FAILED no_commit at the ~30min timeout. This looks like a SPAWN-PATH HANG that started for runs launched after ~13:17 today; prime suspect is session-keeper Phase-1 (injector, deployed 12:23, commit 35d8750c) -- possibly a DISTINCT regression from this historical self-recovering launch-stall. Investigator running; will split into a separate regression bead if confirmed. The tmux-orphan LEAK is still real and worth a boot-reap (folded to hk-nlhys) but is NOT this stall's cause. Priority left at P1 pending verdict.

comments.author: gb
comments.text: ADDITIONAL EVIDENCE (named-queues, 2026-06-03, post-restart): after a full daemon restart (fresh in-memory semaphore, set-concurrency 4), a SINGLE new bead (hk-mgoo7) still wedged at launch_initiated for 6min+ while active_runs was only 2 (hk-mgoo7 + a peer hk-g4ei7 which DID spawn+run fine). A single bead cannot block a 12-slot pool unless slots are held OUTSIDE the active-run set. Two non-exclusive mechanisms: (1) the forceTeardownSession defer slot-leak (slots from completed/reaped runs not released); (2) the boot RECONCILER re-adopting previously-wedged in-flight runs and acquiring/holding their semaphore slots without ever spawning (so a 'fresh' daemon starts with a drained pool). The fix MUST (per investigator a/b/c): emit launch_initiated AFTER SpawnWindow; add a timeout+spawn_cap_blocked diagnostic to the semaphore acquire (tmuxsubstrate.go:216) so this is never silent again; AND ensure slots are released-before-reacquire and that reconciler re-adoption does NOT hold a slot for a non-spawned run. Authoring via worktree+manual-merge (daemon too flaky to dispatch its own fix).

title: T6 finding: br enforces 100KB body validation ceiling; 1MB body test blocked
description: Attempted to seed a 1MB bead body via br create --body. Two independent limits block it: (1) macOS ARG_MAX = 1048576 bytes — the --body argument alone hits the limit; OS returns E2BIG (fork/exec: argument list too long). (2) br validates description size and rejects anything > 100KB with 'Validation failed: description: exceeds 100KB'. The 100KB ceiling is a br-CLI constraint, not a Beads-SQLite constraint. The ceiling means the max test-injectable body via CLI is 102400 bytes (100KB exactly). Tested and confirmed: 100KB body round-trips correctly via br show (as 'description' field in JSON output). Recommendation: add --body-file option to br create to allow larger bodies without hitting ARG_MAX.
close_reason: Finding documented in test/exploratory/findings-T6.md F-T6-002 and t6_scale_shape_test.go T6-2; no code change needed

title: CI cadence: run scenario suite on PRs (smoke tier) vs nightly (full)
description:
Per specs/scenario-harness.md §4.9 cadence tags (smoke|regression|nightly): wire the scenario suite into CI with a two-tier cadence:

- smoke tier (per-PR): the cheapest 1-2 scenarios per umbrella fix, budgeted to keep total PR runtime under ~60s. Each scenario boots the daemon + twin as real subprocesses; estimate 2-5s per scenario, so ~10-20 scenarios fit.
- regression+nightly tier: full suite including jitter sweeps, crash-recovery scenarios (HC-024), silent-hang scenarios (HC-026), and any real-claude conformance scenarios that exist by then.

Recommendation (subject to revision when the harness lands): scenario suite on every CI build at the smoke tier. Rationale: the 11 umbrella fixes took 13 smoke runs to land; a regression in any of them costs more than 60s of CI time to discover via real dogfood. Nightly takes the long tail.

Depends-on hk-i0tw.* harness binary + hk-wuu5h umbrella's first scenarios actually existing.

title: cli-ux: human-readable output by default; --json opt-in
description:
## Policy

All harmonik subcommands MUST default to human-readable output. Machine-readable JSON MUST require an explicit opt-in flag.

**Chosen flag:** `--json` (shorthand) and `--format json` (long form). Rationale: `--json` matches the shorthand already established by `harmonik handler status` (see handler.go:579), keeps typing minimal for the common machine-consumer case, and the `--format json|text` long form is consistent with the handler precedent. Using both forms together — one for discoverability, one for quick use — is already the in-codebase convention.

## Current subcommand format inventory

| Subcommand | Default output today | Status |
|---|---|---|
| harmonik queue status | JSON (raw daemon response via handleResponse) | NEEDS FIX |
| harmonik queue submit | JSON (raw daemon response via handleResponse) | NEEDS FIX |
| harmonik queue append | JSON (raw daemon response via handleResponse) | NEEDS FIX |
| harmonik queue dry-run | JSON (raw daemon response via handleResponse) | NEEDS FIX |
| harmonik handler status | human-readable text (--format json opt-in exists) | OK |
| harmonik handler resume | human-readable | OK |
| harmonik reconcile | human-readable (stderr) | OK |
| harmonik run | human-readable progress (stderr) | OK |
| harmonik tmux-start / hook-relay / daemon | no structured output | OK |

All four queue subcommands share a single JSON-output path: internal/queue/cli/client.go handleResponse(), which unconditionally writes JSON to stdout. The fix surface is therefore small — primarily handleResponse() plus flag-parsing in each of the four queue command files.

## Motivating friction

harmonik queue status returns a raw JSON blob to stdout:
  {"queue":{"schema_version":1,"queue_id":"...","status":"active","groups":[...]}}

A human reading it must mentally parse nested JSON to answer "is my queue running?". A one-liner like:
  queue: active | 2 items pending (hk-abc123, hk-def456)
answers the same question in 0.5 seconds.

## Agent note

Agents reading CLI output as captured text benefit from terse human-readable output too. Fields-as-prose ("queue: active") are faster to extract from unstructured text than navigating JSON paths. Default-human-readable is not a tradeoff against agent consumers.

## Child beads

One bead per affected subcommand will be filed as children of this bead.
close_reason: Policy implemented: all 4 queue subcommands converted to human-readable default with --json opt-in

title: attractor-parity: graph goal + --param template substitution (WG-044/045/046)
description:
T5 — Graph-level goal + template-param substitution (component E). Two coupled mechanisms, one component, can land together.

GOAL (WG-044): add 'goal' to reservedGraphAttrs (parser.go:509) + the graph-attr switch (parser.go:534-562) → typed dot.Graph.Goal + core.Workflow.Goal. A 'goal' on a node/edge → reserved-out-of-position strict error. Thread the (substituted) Graph.Goal into every agentic node's brief via the run-level ExtraContext channel (rc.extraContext / AgentTaskPayload.ExtraContext → '## Extra Context' section) — composes with bead body and per-node prompt (T2); does NOT double-inject (distinct channel, confirmed in internal/workspace/agenttask_chb028.go: Body precedes ExtraContext).

TEMPLATE PARAMS (WG-045/046): accept repeated --param KEY=VALUE on 'harmonik run' → map[string]string, sealed into Run-record template_params at claim time. Apply ONE substitution pass over the RAW .dot source string (grammar __[A-Z][A-Z0-9_]*__) BEFORE parse — i.e. before dot.Parse inside / ahead of LoadDotWorkflow (loader.go:56; daemon call site workloop.go:1277). After the pass, any residual __TOKEN__ is a LAUNCH-TIME ERROR (run refuses to start, names the offending token). Non-recursive; param values are trusted/un-sanitized. Token-free source → byte-identical no-op. Ordering invariant (WG-046): read → substitute → parse → validate → dispatch.

DELIVERABLES: dot.Graph.Goal/core.Workflow.Goal; parser graph-attr change; --param CLI flag on 'harmonik run' (+ queue-item launch context); the pre-parse substitution + residual-token check (a new function ahead of LoadDotWorkflow, or a LoadDotWorkflowWithParams variant); Run-record template_params seal; goal→ExtraContext threading in the dot driver; unit tests for substitution/residual/no-op/ordering.

ACCEPTANCE (matches scenario bead hk-4bn9o + exploratory bead hk-9ohjf): a .dot with goal="Fix #__ISSUE_NUMBER__" + --param ISSUE_NUMBER=172 substitutes before parse, threads the substituted goal into the brief; a missing param → launch-time residual-token error; token-free .dot → byte-identical no-op; replay re-substitutes identically.

SPEC SECTIONS: WG-044/045/046 (§I.7-I.9), Workflow-RECORD goal (§II.4), Run-RECORD template_params (§II.5), §7.5 launch surface (§II.9), B↔E composition (§II.10).

DEPENDS ON: T0 (spec landed). Independent of T1-T4 except the B↔E composition read-only agreement with T2 (ExtraContext-vs-Body channel split must agree; no line conflict). T5 can run fully concurrent with T1 and the agentic cluster.

- `go build ./...` and `go test ./...` green.

title: Scenario test: second-daemon pidfile exit-5 + stale-pidfile recovery (PL-001/002)
description: GAP: no scenario test asserts PL-001 (a second daemon started against the same project exits code 5, pidfile locked) or PL-002 (a STALE pidfile left by a crashed daemon is removed and startup continues). Both CRITICAL: two daemons racing the queue file corrupt shared state. Currently only conceptually covered. title: Fix hk-5dewt root cause: raise UnavailableRetryCap from 2s to 15s (or pass --lock-timeout to br)
description: Root cause confirmed in docs/kerf-feedback/2026-05-19.md §hk-5dewt: harmonik's CloseBead runs up to 11 br attempts with UnavailableRetryCap=2s; each attempt holds .write.lock for up to 15s (10s timeout + 5s SIGTERM grace), so retries pile up and queue manual closes behind them. With no contention br close is 0.35s. Fix: raise UnavailableRetryCap in internal/brcli/dblockretry.go:53 from 2*time.Second to at least 15*time.Second so retry attempts don't overlap a previous attempt's SIGTERM-grace window. Update internal/brcli/unavailableretrywidened_hkekz5v_test.go expected value too. Validate by running 'harmonik run --beads <id>' and confirming the bead auto-closes within seconds. Once green, also archive .beads/.br_history.226mb-archived/ directory mentioned in HANDOFF.md.

title: T3 finding: PL-006 orphan sweep does not remove worktrees without lease-locks; they need reconciliation routing
description: workspace.SweepStaleLeaseLocks (PL-006 step b) only removes the lease-lock file when the recorded PID is dead. It does NOT remove worktree directories. Additionally, the MVH work loop never calls workspace.WriteLeaseLockAtomic, so worktrees created by the work loop have no lease-lock. On next startup, DiscoverWorktrees finds these worktrees with dw.LeaseLock == nil; SweepStaleLeaseLocks skips them (no lease to sweep). They become WM-003a bare-worktree-no-lease orphans. This is the correct MVH behavior per spec: the orphan sweep handles the lease-lock file; reconciliation routes the unlocked worktree. The lease-lock write path (workspace.WriteLeaseLockAtomic) is a post-MVH gap that, when closed, will allow the sweep to handle signal-killed worktrees directly. No action needed for MVH beyond acknowledgment.
close_reason: SUBSUMED: orphansweep.go:69-72 already skips dw.LeaseLock==nil worktrees with correct WM-003a comment; no-lease-lock-skipped test in orphansweep_wm033_test.go already covers this case; behavior is correct per spec, no MVH code change needed

title: FIX: br exec wall-clock timeout 10s too tight; manual br close takes 19s on 32MB DB
description:
2026-05-19 dogfood diagnostic from hk-u9kn5 (commit f518d8b) revealed:

After 4 dogfood runs, every CloseBead failure pattern is:
  '11/11 BrUnavailable, 0/11 BrDbLocked, last attempt: brErr= exit=0 stderr=""'

Manual `time br close hk-vqoh2 ...` clocks **19.4 seconds**.

Reads are fast: `br list --status=open --format json` = 0.18s, `br show <id>` = 0.16s.
Only WRITES are slow — and 19s exceeds the 10s wall-clock timeout in `internal/brcli/timeout.go:96-143`.

DB state:
- .beads/beads.db: 32MB
- .beads/beads.db-wal: 12MB (NOT checkpointed!)
- .beads/issues.jsonl: 2.3MB, 1408 lines
- .beads/.br_history/: 202 snapshot entries

FIX (pick one or both):
1. **Bump timeout** from 10s to 60s (or make configurable via Config.BrExecTimeout). The 10s default predates the corpus being this size.
2. **Add WAL-checkpoint advisory** to harmonik run startup: log a warning if beads.db-wal > 5MB and suggest `sqlite3 .beads/beads.db "PRAGMA wal_checkpoint(TRUNCATE);"`. Or have br itself checkpoint on its own writes.
3. **Investigate .br_history bloat** (202 entries) — does br auto-rotate, or does it grow unbounded?

The root cause is NOT concurrent locking (the 3-retry → 10-retry widen was on the wrong axis). It IS the 10s timeout being too tight for the current DB write performance.

This is consistent with all 4 dogfood-2/3/4/3 runs failing AT CLOSE TIME after work succeeded:
- hk-75rij → 02e31e1 + manual close
- hk-pxrv6 → 54fa28a + manual close
- hk-vqoh2 → 2748c51 + manual close (this run, 19s)

P0 — every dogfood requires manual close. Once timeout is bumped, dogfood loop is unattended.
notes: POST-ROTATION VALIDATION: rotation pre-flight at f87704f works (200→20 in 26ms). But br close STILL takes 19.25s with 22 entries. The 0.15s test (4 entries) was likely a fluke. Reopen: real root cause is NOT WAL bloat AND NOT .br_history bloat. Rotation+WAL are kept as defense-in-depth. Need deeper investigation: profile br exec; check if it's git-fetch, network calls, or sqlite VACUUM-needed.
close_reason: e960b6d raised UnavailableRetryCap 2s→15s — root cause was retry-loop self-contention on .write.lock

comments.author: gb
comments.text: ROOT CAUSE CONFIRMED (sub-agent investigation 2026-05-19): .write.lock fcntl contention. br acquires fcntl LOCK_EX on .beads/.write.lock for ALL writes with NO timeout. Reproduced: python3 holding .write.lock for 19s → br close takes 18.97s. WAL size (tested up to 41MB), .br_history entry count, and DB corruption are NOT the cause. The 19.25s 'isolated' test was NOT isolated — the harmonik daemon's 11 retry attempts (UnavailableRetryMax=10) were still spawning br processes. Fix: raise UnavailableRetryCap from 2s to ≥15s or pass --lock-timeout to br. Details: docs/kerf-feedback/2026-05-19.md §hk-5dewt

title: DOT cascade missing no_progress detector (EM-015e not enforced)
description:
The review-loop builtin path has a diff-hash no-progress detector (reviewloop.go:557-579) that early-exits a REQUEST_CHANGES loop when the implementer makes zero new changes across iterations and emits no_progress_detected. The DOT cascade (dot_cascade.go) has no equivalent — it relies solely on traversal_cap + dotMaxNodeVisits. Effect: a no-progress loop burns the full cap instead of early-exiting, and emits no no_progress_detected event. EM-015e is thus not enforced in DOT mode.

RESOLUTION (orchestrator decision A, 2026-05-29): the prior attempt's code was correct but the reviewer flagged SPEC_DRIFT because event-model.md §8.1a restricts no_progress_detected to workflow_mode=review-loop. We are making DOT no-progress detection NORMATIVE. As part of this bead you MUST ALSO amend specs/event-model.md §8.1a: (a) update the §8.1a preamble + the no_progress_detected payload so the event is permitted from DOT mode (not review-loop-only); (b) exempt DOT mode from the review_loop_cycle_complete pairing/ordering rule (DOT does not emit review_loop_cycle_complete) OR document DOT's no-progress termination. Land code + spec together so the reviewer APPROVEs.
notes: no-progress detected at iteration 2: diff hash unchanged
close_reason: done

title: Reconciler observes reconciliation_mismatch_observed for runs orphaned by queue-cancel+restart but neither re-adopts NOR reaps them — orphaned in limbo, can never merge
description: Repro: cancel a queue that has in-flight runs (hk-4kuvj makes this easy since cancel hits the active queue), then restart the daemon. The in-flight runs lose their owning queue (archived); on restart the reconciler emits reconciliation_mismatch_observed but takes NO action — does not re-adopt them into a queue, does not reap (worktree-remove + free the bead). Result: worktrees intact, beads stuck in_progress, no queue tracks them, so they can never merge — orphaned in limbo. title: Test gap: concurrent work-loop stub does not enforce ClaimBead exclusion — production SQLite atomics unverified by test
description: TestT4_ConcurrentLoops probes two ExportedRunWorkLoop goroutines sharing the same stub ledger. The stub dequeues one bead per Ready call, so dispatch is naturally serialized. However the stub does not enforce the claim exclusion that production brcli.Adapter provides via 'br update --claim' (atomic in SQLite WAL). If both goroutines raced on Ready and both received the same bead ID before the queue drained, both would proceed to ClaimBead and beyond — a double-dispatch the stub would not catch. A real-DB concurrent integration test is needed to prove production safety. Repro: TestT4_ConcurrentLoops in internal/daemon/t4_exploratory_test.go (T4 exploratory wave).
close_reason: Real-DB concurrent ClaimBead test implemented in internal/daemon/t5_realdb_concurrent_test.go; key finding: br update --claim (v0.1.45) does not reject duplicate concurrent claims — double-dispatch possible; test documents this as FINDING_DOUBLE_DISPATCH and will detect if br behaviour changes

title: Parallelism prep: JSONLWriter fsync latency concentration
description:
Roadmap row's blocker C from POST_MVH_PARALLELISM_ROADMAP.md §1. Fix-before threshold: N>5 concurrent runs.

Location: internal/eventbus/jsonlwriter.go:29

Problem: JSONLWriter holds a sync.Mutex; one writer for the whole daemon. Each fsync() from one run's F-class event (e.g. run_started) stalls all other Emit callers on the mutex. Latency concentrator on the critical path.

Note: Not a correctness blocker — O_APPEND guarantees no interleave within PIPE_BUF. Pure throughput hazard at N>5.

Options (decide in design): (a) per-run-id queue + single drainer goroutine, (b) batched fsync with bounded latency, (c) accept the cost at N≤5 and defer.

Acceptance: At N=10 concurrent runs each emitting F-class events at 10/sec, P99 Emit latency stays under 50ms. Benchmark in internal/eventbus/.

Size: M. Lower priority than blocker A/B/D — only bites at N>5.

Anti-pattern (§6): Do not shard JSONL file per run. EV-020 mandates single-file-per-project. Keep by run_id in envelope (row #1 already landed).
close_reason: batching drainer goroutine coalesces concurrent F-class fsyncs; P99<50ms at N=10 confirmed; commit 44c6644

title: MVH-R4: wire RunOrphanSweep into daemon.Start before socket bind
description:
MVH_ROADMAP row #4. Per PL-005 step 3 (specs/process-lifecycle.md), the daemon must sweep orphan child processes from a prior crashed run before binding new sockets/listeners.

Edits to `internal/daemon/daemon.go`'s `Start`:
1. After pidfile acquire (from MVH-R3) and BEFORE any socket/listener bind, call `lifecycle.RunOrphanSweep(ctx, cfg.ProjectDir)` (or whatever the actual entrypoint is — read `internal/lifecycle/` for the exact symbol; the orphan sweep code "exists, never called from Start" per MVH_ROADMAP).
2. Surface sweep errors as warnings via the bus (emit an `orphan_sweep_completed` event with count + any errors), but do NOT abort Start on sweep error — orphan presence is recoverable.

Read `internal/lifecycle/` orphan-sweep source first to pin the function signature and what state it expects on disk (`.harmonik/run/`, probably).

Acceptance:
- Sweep runs once on every Start before downstream wiring.
- Test: seed a fake stale child-state file under `.harmonik/run/`, call Start, assert the file is gone and the event was emitted.
- `go build ./...` + `go test ./...` green.
close_reason: RunOrphanSweep wired after daemon_started, before socket bind; daemon_orphan_sweep_completed emitted O-class; sweep non-fatal; sensors green.
