An orphaned task branch from a prior run whose bead has since been re-claimed MUST classify as Cat 5 for the old run.
close_reason: done

title: Ordering uses git DAG parentage and UUID v7, not wall clock
description: Per RC-011: detectors MUST order checkpoints within a run's task branch by **git DAG parentage** (parent-pointer chain) and events by **UUID v7 `event_id`** per [event-model.md §4.1]. Wall-clock timestamps (`timestamp_wall`, git commit `author_date`/`committer_date`) MUST NOT drive classification. Most-recent checkpoint identified as the tip of the run's task branch (commit with no child in run's branch-subgraph), NOT the commit with the latest wall-clock timestamp.
close_reason: done

title: Cat 0 pre-check runs before any other detector
description: Per RC-012: before any §8 category detector executes, daemon MUST verify infrastructure prerequisites per §8.1: `br --version` returns within timeout T (default 5s) AND reports a version compatible with the pin per [beads-integration.md §4.8]; a trial `br list --limit 1` or equivalent returns without error; `git rev-parse HEAD` succeeds; `.harmonik/` is writable. Any prerequisite failure MUST halt classification, emit `infrastructure_unavailable` per [event-model.md §8] naming the specific prerequisite, transition to `degraded` status per [process-lifecycle.md §4.2], and retry at configurable cadence. No in-flight run is classified until Cat 0 clears.

title: Post-`ready` Cat 0 does not transition daemon state
description: Per RC-012a: a Cat 0 prerequisite failure observed AFTER daemon has reached `ready` (per [process-lifecycle.md §4.3 PL-009]) MUST emit `daemon_degraded{reason=infrastructure_unavailable}` per [event-model.md §8.7.5] and surface the failing prerequisite via [operator-nfr.md §4.9 ON-037] health probe, but MUST NOT transition the §6.1 daemon-status enum from `ready` to `degraded`. Daemon-level `degraded` enum entry is reserved for pre-`ready` Cat 0 failures per PL-010. Reentrant `degraded` would tangle operator-control with reconciliation per the PL-010 NOTE.

title: Detector emits `reconciliation_category_assigned`
description: Per RC-013: after classifying an in-flight run into a §8 category, detector MUST emit `reconciliation_category_assigned` per [event-model.md §8] carrying run_id, assigned category, detection-rule name. Emission MUST precede dispatch of any reconciliation workflow or auto-resolver. Consumers MUST tolerate duplicate emissions (e.g., from crash-restart re-dispatch); dedup key is `(target_run_id, category, snapshot_token.git_head_hash)`.
close_reason: done

title: JSONL divergence-evidence scope is bounded
description: Per RC-014: detectors and investigators MAY read JSONL only for: detecting checkpoint commit referenced in `checkpoint_written` event missing from git (Cat 6b); detecting JSONL corrupt past byte offset (Cat 6b); detecting `transition_event` exists but transition-record sibling file absent (Cat 3 per [execution-model.md §4.4 EM-018]); Cat 2 liveness probe via controlled indexed query (NOT live-follow tail); supplying observational context to investigator. FORBIDDEN: using JSONL as source of `run_id`/`state_id`/`transition_id` for in-flight runs (those derive from git per [execution-model.md §4.7]); using JSONL to decide which bead is in-flight (Beads via `br` is authoritative per [beads-integration.md §4.7]); reconstructing `state` or `transition` from JSONL payloads. On detected divergence, detector emits `store_divergence_detected` with `corroboration ∈ {git-corroborated, beads-corroborated}` per EV-023a; un-corroborable observations route to `divergence_inconclusive` per [event-model.md §8.6.10] (Cat 6a/6b per RC-003a).
close_reason: done

title: Reconciliation workflows emit exactly one checkpoint commit
description: Per RC-002: a reconciliation workflow MUST emit exactly one checkpoint commit — the **verdict commit** — on the investigator-run's task branch. The verdict commit MUST carry the `reconciliation_verdict_emitted` event's payload (per [schemas.md §6.1]) plus any evidence the investigator produced under `.harmonik/reconciliation/<investigator_run_id>/`. Intermediate state transitions within the reconciliation workflow MUST NOT be checkpointed. Explicit exception to [execution-model.md §4.5 EM-023]; keyed on workflow-library metadata tag `workflow_class = reconciliation` (declared in [schemas.md §6.5] as RC's extension to the Workflow record owned by [execution-model.md §4.1]; OQ-RC-002 tracks EM acceptance).
close_reason: SUBSUMED: RC-002 checkpoint contract covered by hk-63oh.73 (reconciliationworkflow_rc001_test.go:151 TestRC002), hk-63oh.76 (verdictexecutionharness_rc020_test.go:618 RC-025a 7-step), and durability.go:22-34 EM-026 exception docstring

title: Evidence-corroboration contract for store-divergence events
description: Per RC-019a: every `store_divergence_detected` emission MUST adhere to EV-023a per [event-model.md §4.5]: detector MUST classify the candidate divergence into the `corroboration` enum value (`git-corroborated | beads-corroborated`) per [event-model.md §8.6.8] payload. Single-source observations whose corroboration cannot be established MUST emit `divergence_inconclusive` per [event-model.md §8.6.10] instead, NOT `store_divergence_detected`. Cat 6b (post-emission corroboration impossible) is reachable when neither git nor Beads is readable; Cat 6b emissions are exempt from corroboration via the dedicated escalation path of §8.11.
close_reason: spec-corpus sensor rc019a_evidence_corroboration_test.go — 9 checks all PASS

title: Detector cadence (startup / on-demand / hourly scheduled)
description: Per RC-020a: detectors MUST run at three dispatch points: (a) **Daemon startup** — full scan of in-flight runs after orphan sweep ([process-lifecycle.md §4.2 PL-005]) and before transitioning to `ready`; (b) **On-demand operator command** — `harmonik reconcile [--run <run_id>]` triggers scoped detector run (grammar OQ-RC-005); (c) **Scheduled cadence** — background scan at configurable interval; MVH default **hourly**, configurable via operator YAML per [operator-nfr.md §4.3] (post-MVH tuning OQ-RC-004). Detectors MUST be idempotent across dispatch points: re-running on same `(target_run_id, snapshot)` MUST produce same category assignment. Concurrent detector runs for same `target_run_id` serialized by RC-002a registry lock.
close_reason: done

title: Detector panic recovery (per-detector recover() barrier)
description: Per RC-020b: a detector that panics during evaluation per RC-003a's first-match priority order MUST be caught by a per-detector `recover()` barrier (per [process-lifecycle.md §4.6 PL-018a]'s per-goroutine recover obligation extended to detector functions). On panic, detector is suspended for the daemon's lifetime and priority-order evaluation falls through to the next detector. A diagnostic event `reconciliation_detector_panic{detector_class, error_class}` (cross-spec coordination request to EV) MUST emit before fall-through.
close_reason: done

title: Investigator inputs are bound by snapshot token (logical view)
description: Per RC-015: investigator subprocess receives a standard LaunchSpec per [handler-contract.md §6.1] (`agent_type=claude-code, role=investigator`). `LaunchSpec.snapshot_token` field (declared `String|None` in HC-006) MUST carry the JSON-serialized SnapshotToken record per [schemas.md §6.1] (`{git_head_hash, beads_audit_entry_id, captured_at_timestamp}`). Investigator does NOT receive a pre-assembled `InvestigatorInput` payload; the InvestigatorInput shape per [schemas.md §6.1] is a documented LOGICAL VIEW the investigator constructs at runtime by querying its Beads-CLI skill (BI-027/BI-028 for BeadRecord), git-inspection skill (per RC-016 playbook for checkpoint commit + transition-record sibling), workspace-inspection skill, bounded JSONL divergence-evidence reader (RC-014). The snapshot token IS the bounding discipline. Reads beyond `git_head_hash` or `beads_audit_entry_id` MUST be classified out-of-scope per RC-014 forbidden-uses.
close_reason: done

title: Investigator is an HC handler (canonical agent_type/role pair)
description: Per RC-015a: investigator subprocess is a handler-contract handler per [handler-contract.md §4.1]. Launch follows standard handler-launch sequence — dispatcher constructs LaunchSpec per HC-006, calls launch primitive, watches via HC-011 for outcome emission. `agent_type=claude-code` and `role=investigator` are canonical pair (see also CP-039). Reconciliation-specific behavior beyond standard launch is captured in investigator playbook (RC-016).
close_reason: done

title: Investigator playbook per category (S01 YAML obligation)
description: Per RC-016: for each category with an investigator (§8.3 Cat 2, §8.4 Cat 3 generic, §8.11 Cat 6a), the S01-shipped YAML policy MUST define a **playbook** — a specific ordered sequence of checks producing evidence for the verdict. Playbooks MUST name the investigator's expected evidence outputs (captured in the reconciliation commit) and the verdict-selection rubric.
close_reason: done

title: Every reconciliation workflow declares a wall-clock budget
description: Per RC-017: every reconciliation workflow per RC-001 MUST declare a wall-clock budget — hard ceiling, measured from `run_started` to terminal event, beyond which daemon forcibly terminates. Declared as YAML policy field `wall_clock_seconds` (positive integer, required) attached to reconciliation workflow's DOT via `budget_ref` per [control-points.md §4.5]. In absence of explicit budget, S01 packaging (RC-004) MUST supply per-category default: Cat 2 default 600s; Cat 3 generic default 300s; Cat 6a default 900s.
close_reason: done

title: Budget exhaustion terminates with fallback verdict (5-step sequence kept as one bead per F8b)
description: Per RC-018: on wall-clock budget exhaustion, daemon MUST: emit `reconciliation_budget_exhausted` (payload `BudgetExhaustedPayload` per [schemas.md §6.1]; WHEN/WHY here, WHAT in [event-model.md §8]); issue **default verdict of `escalate-to-human`** on outer (target) run, indistinguishable from investigator-emitted `escalate-to-human` in operator-facing surface; kill investigator subprocess per [handler-contract.md §4.6] cleanup rules; NOT write a reconciliation commit. Step ordering: (1) terminate investigator subprocess (SIGTERM, then SIGKILL after [handler-contract.md §4.3 HC-018] interval); (2) wait for watcher-observation per HC-011; (3) emit `budget_exhausted` (class F per [event-model.md §8.4.3]); (4) emit fallback `escalate-to-human` verdict (class F per RC-021); (5) verdict-executor (RC-025a) consumes fallback as if investigator-emitted. Steps (3) and (4) are NOT atomic but each is fsync-boundary; on crash between them, next daemon startup detects budget-exhausted event with no subsequent verdict commit and routes through Cat 3b retry cap (RC-026a). Per F8b cohesive-function-body tiebreaker (F-pilot-RC-5), keep as ONE bead — all 5 steps share goroutine state in the budget-exhaustion handler; do NOT mint step beads. Because reconciliation workflows are not checkpointed mid-investigation (RC-002), budget exhaustion MUST NOT leave an in-flight reconciliation state for re-classification.
close_reason: done

title: Investigator captures WIP before emitting `reopen-bead`
description: Per RC-019: before emitting a `reopen-bead` verdict, investigator MUST capture any recoverable WIP from outer run's worktree into the reconciliation commit. Concretely: (a) `git status --porcelain` and enumerate untracked files; (b) capture diff plus file listing; (c) include the capture in reconciliation commit's body and/or as annotated files under `.harmonik/reconciliation/<investigator_run_id>/wip-capture/`. Mandatory for `reopen-bead` verdicts; OPTIONAL for other verdicts (which keep the worktree and retain WIP by default).
close_reason: done

title: Verdict vocabulary is the seven-value enum
description: Per RC-020: a verdict event's `verdict` field MUST be exactly one of the seven enum values listed in [schemas.md §6.1 Verdict]: `resume-here`, `resume-with-context`, `reset-to-checkpoint`, `reopen-bead`, `accept-close-with-note`, `no-op-accept`, `escalate-to-human`. Every verdict MUST have defined mechanical semantics (see [schemas.md §6.2] verdict-execution table; [spec.md §8.12] is authoritative on prose semantics; [schemas.md §6.3] is authoritative on mechanical dispatch). Any other value is a malformed verdict and MUST be handled per RC-023. `no-op-accept` is the verdict for "investigator confirms current state is legitimate; no action required" — differs from `accept-close-with-note` in that the bead is NOT closed.
close_reason: done

title: Bounded recursion follows from the verdict-only commit rule
description: Per RC-003: a daemon crash during reconciliation MUST leave no mid-investigation durable state. On restart, the outer run whose reconciliation was interrupted MUST be re-classified from its original category against unchanged git + Beads state, and a fresh reconciliation workflow MUST be dispatched. Reconciliation-workflow nodes MUST NOT be classified under §8 Cat 1 or Cat 2; the recursion-bounding rule of RC-002 ensures no in-flight investigator state exists for a detector to classify.
close_reason: SUBSUMED: RC-003 bounded recursion covered by hk-63oh.78 — crashrecovery_rc031_test.go:48,76,194,511 explicitly cite RC-003 no-mid-investigation-durable-state guarantee

title: Exactly one verdict event per reconciliation workflow
description: Per RC-021: a reconciliation workflow MUST emit exactly one `reconciliation_verdict_emitted` event over its lifetime. Emission of the first verdict event MUST mark the workflow terminal; any subsequent verdict event from the same workflow is a structural violation and MUST be handled per RC-023 with malformation reason `multiple-verdicts`.
close_reason: done

title: Verdict commit lands on investigator's task branch
description: Per RC-022: verdict event's emission MUST be atomic with the investigator's verdict commit — a single `git commit` on the investigator's task branch carrying the verdict event's payload (per [schemas.md §6.1]) and any evidence files under `.harmonik/reconciliation/<investigator_run_id>/`. No intermediate checkpoints are written per RC-002. Resolution of `resume-here` / `resume-with-context` dispatch targets (which node to re-spawn on the outer run) MUST cite the orchestrator's current-node resolution contract in [execution-model.md §7.1] and, for worktree interactions on `reset-to-checkpoint`, [workspace-model.md §4.4] (worktree reset) and [workspace-model.md §4.6] (merge).
close_reason: done

title: Verdict emission via outcome envelope (HC-008 outcome_emitted)
description: Per RC-022a: investigator subprocess emits its verdict by writing a structured outcome via standard handler-contract outcome path (HC-008 `outcome_emitted`). Outcome's `outcome_kind` MUST be `reconciliation_verdict`; outcome payload MUST be the `VerdictEvent` record per [schemas.md §6.1]. Handler-contract watcher (HC-011) consumes the outcome event and surfaces VerdictEvent to daemon's reconciliation-verdict-executor (RC-025). Investigator subprocess does NOT directly write the verdict commit; daemon's verdict-executor MUST construct and commit the verdict-and-verdict-executed commit pair on investigator's task branch. Cross-spec coordination request to EM (OQ-RC-010): extend `Outcome` record in [execution-model.md §6.1] (or add new variant) to carry the verdict envelope.
close_reason: done

title: Malformed-verdict handling produces a fallback escalation
description: Per RC-023: on any malformation condition (`unknown-verdict-value`, `missing-required-field`, `extra-fields`, `wrong-type`, `multiple-verdicts`, `verdict-after-terminal` per MalformationReason enum [schemas.md §6.1]), daemon MUST: emit `reconciliation_verdict_malformed` (payload `MalformedVerdictPayload` per [schemas.md §6.1]); issue **fallback verdict of `escalate-to-human`** on the target (outer) run; terminate the reconciliation workflow (kill investigator subprocess); NOT attempt to interpret the malformed payload. Trusting LLM-generated text to conform to a schema is a ZFC violation per [architecture.md §4.2]; this path converts cognitive failure into deterministic escalation.
close_reason: done

title: Verdict staleness check precedes execution
description: Per RC-024: before executing a verdict per RC-025, daemon MUST re-capture current `(git_head_hash', beads_audit_entry_id')` and compare against snapshot token captured at investigator-dispatch time (RC-015). Verdict is **stale** if either: target run's checkpoint trail has gained a new commit since the snapshot, OR target run's bead has changed status in the Beads audit log since the snapshot. On staleness: emit `reconciliation_verdict_stale` (WHEN/WHY here; payload schema `StaleVerdictPayload` in [schemas.md §6.1]; WHAT contract owned by [event-model.md §8]); NOT execute the verdict; re-dispatch a fresh reconciliation workflow against the new state. Changes to sibling beads or daemon's JSONL event log MUST NOT trigger staleness.
close_reason: done

title: Verdict execution is durable and idempotent
description: Per RC-025: when reconciliation workflow emits a verdict event (per RC-020) and staleness check (RC-024) passes, daemon MUST: perform the verdict's mechanical action per the verdict-execution table ([schemas.md §6.2]); emit `reconciliation_verdict_executed` (payload `VerdictExecutedPayload` per [schemas.md §6.1]); append a second commit to investigator's task branch carrying the trailer `Harmonik-Verdict-Executed: true` (payload-free presence-only marker; trailer shape declared in [schemas.md §6.4] as RC-owned since the EM v0.2.0 trailer rollback). Each verdict's mechanical action MUST be idempotent: `reopen-bead` invokes BI adapter's reopen path per [beads-integration.md §4.4 BI-010] (BI-010a status-mapping table for verdict→op binding) with adapter's BI-031 status-check-before-reissue handling idempotency; `resume-here`/`resume-with-context`/`reset-to-checkpoint` idempotent at dispatch layer; `accept-close-with-note` idempotent on re-run; `no-op-accept` no mechanical action; `escalate-to-human` deduplicated by `target_run_id`.
close_reason: done

title: Daemon-side verdict-executor commits the verdict pair (7-step sequence kept as one bead per F8b)
description: Per RC-025a: the daemon-side verdict-executor (a deterministic Go subroutine, NOT a workflow node) consumes the VerdictEvent from RC-022a's outcome envelope and: (1) validates the verdict per RC-020/RC-023; on validation failure, routes through fallback per RC-023; (2) re-captures snapshot per RC-024 staleness check; on stale, routes through Cat 3b re-execution per §8.5; (3) constructs and commits `reconciliation_verdict_emitted` commit (verdict body + Harmonik-Verdict-ID trailer) on investigator's task branch; (4) mechanically applies the verdict's action per the table in [schemas.md §6.2]; (5) constructs and commits `reconciliation_verdict_executed` commit with `Harmonik-Verdict-Executed: true` trailer per [schemas.md §6.4] as a descendant of the verdict-emitted commit; (6) emits `reconciliation_verdict_emitted` and `reconciliation_verdict_executed` events; (7) releases the RC-002a lock per RC-002b. Verdict-executor MUST be panic-safe (per [process-lifecycle.md §4.6 PL-018a]); on panic mid-step, next daemon startup re-classifies via Cat 3b (verdict-emitted-but-unexecuted) per §8.5 and verdict-executor re-runs idempotently. Per F8b cohesive-function-body tiebreaker (F-pilot-RC-5), keep as ONE bead — all 7 steps live in the verdict-executor's single function body sharing goroutine state (snapshot, verdict envelope, target-run-id) accumulated through prior steps. Do NOT mint step beads — contrast ON-027's 8 drain steps that delegate to 8 distinct subsystems.
close_reason: done

title: Verdict-execution discovery on restart (Cat 3b classification)
description: Per RC-026: startup detector of [process-lifecycle.md §4.2 PL-005] MUST treat a reconciliation workflow as resolved ONLY if both the verdict commit AND the verdict-executed commit (per RC-025) are present on the investigator's branch. A reconciliation workflow with a verdict commit but no verdict-executed commit MUST be classified as §8.5 Cat 3b with the dedicated auto-resolver re-attempting the verdict's mechanical action under a fresh staleness check (RC-024). Daemon startup sequence MUST dispatch reconciliation-workflow classification pass BEFORE any ordinary workflow dispatches — reconciliation detectors run before daemon transitions to `ready`; ordinary dispatch is gated behind detection completion. Fail-open reconciliation escalation tracked OQ-RC-003.
close_reason: done

title: Cat 3b re-execution retry cap (default N=5; cap → Cat 6b)
description: Per RC-026a: a reconciliation Cat 3b verdict-execution that fails on a fresh staleness check (RC-024) MUST be retried with a durable attempt counter, recorded in `.harmonik/reconciliation-attempts/<target_run_id>.json` (atomic temp+rename+fsync per [workspace-model.md §4.7 WM-026]). Retry cap defaults to N=5; on cap exceeded, the run escalates to Cat 6b (operator escalation) per §8.11. Each retry MUST emit `reconciliation_verdict_execution_retry{target_run_id, attempt}` (cross-spec coordination request to EV).
close_reason: done

title: Operator verdict-override surface (confirm-verdict / veto-verdict)
description: Per RC-027: a per-reconciliation-workflow policy option MUST allow operators to pause the daemon's verdict-execution step until operator confirmation or veto via `harmonik confirm-verdict <run_id>` / `harmonik veto-verdict <run_id> [--promote-to escalate-to-human]`. Default: execution proceeds without operator confirmation. Operators opt in by policy. Applies to all investigator-dispatched categories (Cat 2, Cat 3 generic, Cat 6a per §8.12). Final grammar / authorization model owned by operator-CLI spec per [operator-nfr.md §4.10] (OQ-RC-005).
close_reason: done

title: At most one reconciliation workflow per target run (flock)
description: Per RC-002a: for any given `target_run_id`, at most ONE reconciliation workflow MUST be in-flight at a time. Daemon MUST hold a per-run reconciliation lock at `.harmonik/reconciliation-locks/<target_run_id>.lock`, acquired via `flock(LOCK_EX|LOCK_NB)` per the fd-lifetime advisory-lock primitive of [process-lifecycle.md §4.1 PL-002a]. Lock acquired before the reconciliation workflow's first emission (RC-013 category-assigned); released ONLY after one of: verdict-executed commit lands (RC-025); budget exhaustion (RC-018); malformed verdict (RC-023); investigator-process crash (HC-011 watcher); operator pause (ON-027 entry). On daemon startup, orphan-sweep ([process-lifecycle.md §4.2 PL-006]) enumerates locks and removes stale (PID gone). Two concurrent investigators producing verdicts is structural violation routed through RC-023 with reason `multiple-verdicts`. Second dispatch on `EWOULDBLOCK` emits `reconciliation_dispatch_deduplicated` (cross-spec coordination request to EV §8.6) and skips without re-classification.
close_reason: done

title: `reopen-bead` verdict triggers a new run on subsequent claim
description: Per RC-028: a `reopen-bead` verdict MUST clear the in-flight tracking for the target bead. A subsequent claim of the bead MUST produce a new run with a fresh worktree and a fresh branch per [workspace-model.md §4.9 WM-034]. The new run MUST receive a fresh `run_id`; continuation of the prior `run_id` after a `reopen-bead` verdict is forbidden.
close_reason: spec-corpus sensor rc028_reopen_bead_new_run_test.go — 9 checks all PASS

title: Intra-run rollbacks keep the worktree and run_id
description: Per RC-029: a `reset-to-checkpoint` verdict is an intra-run rollback. The worktree and `run_id` MUST be preserved; the run reverts to the named checkpoint and re-runs from there per [execution-model.md §4.10 EM-044] (transition_kind + rollback_to_state_id representation).
close_reason: done

title: Reconciliation does NOT drive intra-run loops
description: Per RC-030: intra-run loops (workflow edges routing back to an earlier node) are NOT produced by reconciliation. Those loops are ordinary workflow-graph traversal handled by edge conditions and Guard/Gate control-points per [control-points.md §4.2, §4.4]. Reconciliation handles only restart and store-divergence cases.
close_reason: done

title: Crash-recovery-tested (every detector + verdict-execution path)
description: Per RC-031: every detector under §4.3 / §8, every verdict-execution path under §4.5, and every staleness-check path under RC-024 MUST be exercised by at least one crash-recovery scenario test before landing. Crash-recovery test layer described in `docs/methodology/TESTING.md`; this obligation migrates to a `[testing.md §<layer>]` cross-reference within one revision cycle after `testing.md` is finalized (OQ-RC-006).
close_reason: SUBSUMED: hk-63oh.78 (closed) — crashrecovery_rc031_test.go covers all 6 crash injection points (a-f) per RC-031: every detector (§4.3/§8) + verdict-execution path (§4.5) + staleness-check (RC-024)

title: Sensor: reconciliation-as-workflow uniqueness across daemon lifecycle
description:
Per RC-INV-001: across every daemon lifetime, every reconciliation dispatch — including budget-exhausted fallbacks, malformed-verdict fallbacks, Cat 3b re-executions, Cat 3c auto-verdicts — MUST run as a DOT-tagged workflow with `workflow_class = reconciliation` AND MUST emit at most one `reconciliation_verdict_emitted` event per dispatch (RC-021). At no point during daemon's lifetime may reconciliation "ride on top of" ordinary workflow state (i.e., a non-reconciliation workflow node MUST NOT invoke verdict-execution mechanics of RC-025). Cuts across RC-001, RC-002, RC-002a, RC-021, RC-025; closes the "could an agent in a normal workflow issue a verdict" ambiguity.
Sensor: (a) Daemon MUST tag the Workflow record in its registry with `workflow_class`; startup audit log samples emitted workflows and asserts every `reconciliation_verdict_*` event traces back to a Workflow whose `workflow_class = reconciliation`. (b) JSONL query at audit time filters `reconciliation_verdict_emitted` events and joins against Workflow registry; any event whose source workflow's class is NOT `reconciliation` fails the audit. (c) Per-daemon-lifecycle audit log sample: one tag check per N verdicts (default N=10) at runtime, full scan at shutdown. Body explicit ID cite: `[execution-model.md §5 EM-INV-005]` ("git wins on completion") fires sensor→sensor edge `rc-inv-001 → em-inv-005` per F-refs-EV-6 v0.8 sensor→sensor explicit-ID-cite extension.
close_reason: done

title: Sensor: evidence-corroboration guarantee across detector runs
description:
Per RC-INV-004: every `store_divergence_detected` emission traced through any detector dispatch (startup, on-demand, scheduled per RC-020a) MUST carry a non-`inconclusive` `corroboration` value per EV-023a. Cuts across detector dispatch points and the upstream EV contract: a single-source observation whose corroboration cannot be established MUST emit `divergence_inconclusive` instead. Bounds false-positive investigator dispatches; reconciliation-side projection of [event-model.md §8.6.8] EV-023a.
Sensor: detector emission layer MUST validate `corroboration ∈ {git-corroborated, beads-corroborated}` before allowing the event to be written to JSONL; an `inconclusive` corroboration MUST route to `divergence_inconclusive` instead. Audit-log sample at daemon startup checks every `store_divergence_detected` in the last N events carries a non-`inconclusive` corroboration.
close_reason: done

title: Define `SnapshotToken` record (schemas.md §6.1)
description: Per [schemas.md §6.1 SnapshotToken]: 3-field record bounding the investigator's view of state — `git_head_hash` (SHA of project HEAD or reference investigator reads from), `beads_audit_entry_id` (ID of most recent Beads audit-log entry at capture time), `captured_at_timestamp` (RFC 3339 wall-clock; advisory display only per [event-model.md §4.3]). Captured at investigator-dispatch time per RC-015; consumed by RC-024 staleness check (re-capture and compare); JSON-serialized form carried by `LaunchSpec.snapshot_token` per RC-015.
close_reason: SUBSUMED: SnapshotToken record was already implemented at internal/core/snapshottoken.go (commit history pre-dates this resume), as a prerequisite of hk-b3f.106 VerdictEvent. Full struct + Valid() + tests + downstream consumers (verdictevent.go) all in place. Confirmed by 2026-05-08 pre-dispatch grep per directives v14.

title: Define `InvestigatorInput` record (schemas.md §6.1) — LOGICAL VIEW
description: Per [schemas.md §6.1 InvestigatorInput]: documented LOGICAL VIEW the investigator self-assembles at runtime by querying its skills bounded by `snapshot_token` per RC-015. Fields: snapshot_token, target_run_id, target_workflow_id, target_workflow_version, target_bead_id, bead_record (BeadRecord as-of beads_audit_entry_id), last_checkpoint, last_transition (per [execution-model.md §6.1]), jsonl_tail (events since last checkpoint; observational only per RC-014), workspace_observation (WorkspaceObservation per §6.1; renamed from workspace_state 2026-05-09 hk-63oh.80), session_log_ref (CASS handle per [workspace-model.md §4.7]), category, playbook_ref (RC-016), budget_wall_clock_seconds (RC-017). NOT a daemon-assembled record — investigator queries Beads-CLI, git-inspection, workspace-inspection, bounded JSONL reader; the daemon does NOT pre-assemble an InvestigatorInput.json file.
close_reason: Merged 2026-05-09 as 9081615. 14 fields per schemas.md §6.1 (bead body said 13 — stale transcription drift; spec wins per directives). Typed wrappers for run_id/workflow_id; *string nullability for target_bead_id and session_log_ref; reuses existing BeadRecord, Checkpoint, Transition, SnapshotToken, WorkspaceObservation, ReconciliationCategory; JSONLTail deferred via hk-63oh.82 (EventEnvelope) per typed-alias-deferral. APPROVE clean from reviewer; 2 cosmetic MINORs (godoc prose 'zero UUID' vs 'zero RunID/WorkflowID', bead-body field count drift); neither flagged as defect.

title: Define `WorkspaceObservation` record (schemas.md §6.1)
description: Per [schemas.md §6.1 WorkspaceObservation]: 5-field record describing investigator-observable workspace state — `path` (absolute path to run's worktree), `path_exists` (filesystem probe result), `branch_tip_hash` (current task-branch tip; null if worktree missing), `wip_present` (true if `git status --porcelain` is non-empty or untracked files exist), `git_in_progress_op` (none|rebase|merge|cherry-pick|bisect; Cat 6a trigger when not none). Renamed from WorkspaceState 2026-05-09 (hk-63oh.80 spec-fix) to avoid cross-spec collision with workspace-model.md §6.1 ENUM (already implemented at internal/core/workspacestate.go).
notes: Unblocked 2026-05-09 by hk-63oh.80 (spec-fix renamed RECORD to WorkspaceObservation).
close_reason: Merged 2026-05-09 as a462595 (84482b1 RECORD + a462595 §8.11 citation chore). 5 fields per schemas.md §6.1 with nullable *string for branch_tip_hash, Valid() invariants, full test coverage; gofmt-clean. APPROVE from reviewer with 1 MINOR (§8.11 citation typo) inline-amended on worktree before merge per v16 reviewer-MINOR pattern.

title: Define `GitInProgressOp` ENUM (schemas.md §6.1)
description: Per [schemas.md §6.1 GitInProgressOp]: 5-value enum — `none`, `rebase`, `merge`, `cherry-pick`, `bisect`. Used in WorkspaceState; non-`none` values are Cat 6a triggers per §8.11 detector rule.
close_reason: Merged at HEAD. GitInProgressOp 5-value enum (specs/reconciliation/schemas.md §6.1) — none, rebase, merge, cherry-pick, bisect. kind.go-precedent rhythm. APPROVE.

title: Lock acquisition and verdict-executed-commit are NOT atomic
description: Per RC-002b: lock acquisition (RC-002a) and verdict-executed-commit emission (RC-025 + [schemas.md §6.4]) are two physically distinct write operations and CANNOT be made atomic. On daemon startup, a stale lock file at `.harmonik/reconciliation-locks/<target_run_id>.lock` whose target run's investigator task branch carries a `Harmonik-Verdict-Executed: true` commit MUST be deleted (lock outlived its useful purpose). Stale lock without verdict-executed commit MUST route the target run through Cat 3b per §8.5. Per §2.2 F8c constraint-adjacent pattern, this is a constraint requirement adjacent to RC-002a + RC-025.
close_reason: done

title: Define `VerdictEvent` record (schemas.md §6.1)
description: Per [schemas.md §6.1 VerdictEvent]: payload of `reconciliation_verdict_emitted` event. 8 fields — `verdict` (one of seven Verdict enum values), `investigator_run_id` (run_id of reconciliation workflow), `target_run_id` (run_id of outer run being reconciled), `evidence_ref` (git commit hash of reconciliation commit carrying evidence), `context` (required iff verdict=resume-with-context; MUST be empty otherwise), `checkpoint_ref` (transition_id; required iff verdict=reset-to-checkpoint; null otherwise), `snapshot_token` (bound investigator view; consumed by staleness check RC-024), `schema_version` (N-1 readable per [spec.md §6.4]).
close_reason: SUBSUMED: VerdictEvent record was already implemented at internal/core/verdictevent.go (commit history pre-dates this resume), as a prerequisite of hk-b3f.106. Confirmed by 2026-05-08 pre-dispatch grep per directives v14.

title: Define `Verdict` ENUM (schemas.md §6.1) — 7-value vocabulary
description: Per [schemas.md §6.1 Verdict]: 7-value enum — `resume-here` (re-dispatch current node, no context change), `resume-with-context` (re-dispatch with investigator-supplied context injected), `reset-to-checkpoint` (intra-run rollback to named checkpoint; keeps worktree + run_id), `reopen-bead` (mark bead `open`; subsequent claim produces new run with fresh worktree), `accept-close-with-note` (close legitimate; annotate audit gap), `no-op-accept` (current state legitimate; no mechanical action; run continues), `escalate-to-human` (investigator cannot resolve; pause affected run, emit operator-observable event). Consumed by RC-020 (vocabulary lock), RC-025 (mechanical execution table per [schemas.md §6.2]).
close_reason: SUBSUMED. Verdict enum was added in commit 2e44ba6 (feat(core): implement VerdictEvent 8-field RECORD (hk-b3f.93)) as a prerequisite for VerdictEvent. All 7 spec values match specs/reconciliation/schemas.md §6.1; VerdictEscalateToHuman is independent of GateActionEscalateToHuman. No new commit needed.

title: Define `ReconciliationCategory` ENUM (schemas.md §6.1) — 11-value taxonomy
description: Per [schemas.md §6.1 ReconciliationCategory]: 11-value enum — `cat-0` (infrastructure unavailable), `cat-1` (idempotent rerun), `cat-2` (non-idempotent in-flight), `cat-3` (store disagreement generic), `cat-3a` (torn Beads write), `cat-3b` (verdict-unexecuted), `cat-3c` (inverse premature-close), `cat-4` (recoverable known state), `cat-5` (clean restart), `cat-6a` (integrity violation, LLM-triageable), `cat-6b` (integrity violation, mechanically unrecoverable). The vocabulary backing the 11 §8 taxonomy beads (rc-error.cat-N); consumed by every detector and dispatch contract.
close_reason: Merged at d065bd9 — internal/core/{reconciliationcategory,reconciliationcategory_test}.go. 11-value ENUM per reconciliation/schemas.md §6.1 (cat-0, cat-1, cat-2, cat-3, cat-3a, cat-3b, cat-3c, cat-4, cat-5, cat-6a, cat-6b). Reviewer APPROVE, table-driven coverage including error-message-content assertion.

title: Define `MalformationReason` ENUM (schemas.md §6.1)
description: Per [schemas.md §6.1 MalformationReason]: 6-value enum classifying RC-023 malformed-verdict triggers — `unknown-verdict-value`, `missing-required-field` (e.g., context when verdict=resume-with-context), `extra-fields`, `wrong-type`, `multiple-verdicts` (workflow emitted >1 verdict event), `verdict-after-terminal` (verdict event emitted after workflow's terminal event). Consumed by RC-023 fallback path; carried in MalformedVerdictPayload.

title: Define `Harmonik-Verdict-Executed` git trailer (schemas.md §6.4) — RC-owned
description: Per [schemas.md §6.4]: RC owns the shape of the `Harmonik-Verdict-Executed` commit trailer as of spec v0.3.0 (reclaimed from execution-model.md after EM v0.2.0 removed the trailer from its §6 trailer registry). Trailer shape — key `Harmonik-Verdict-Executed` (exact key string, case-sensitive per git trailer grammar); value `true` (fixed literal; any other value is malformed per RC-023); presence_semantics `marker-only` (presence marks execution; no payload). Placement: trailer MUST appear on a commit that is descendant of the verdict commit on the same investigator task branch; MUST NOT appear on the verdict commit itself. Emission contract: emitted exactly once per reconciliation workflow whose verdict is executed; if verdict re-executed under Cat 3b auto-resolution (RC-026), a new verdict-executed commit is appended (prior NOT rewritten). Cross-spec coordination: EM §6 trailer registry MUST note via revision history that this trailer is owned here; any EM trailer-lint tool MUST treat it as RC-owned extension.
close_reason: merged: 2238318

title: Category orthogonality via priority-ordered first-match
description: Per RC-003a: detection categories are NOT mutually exclusive on evidence; a single in-flight run may satisfy multiple §8 categories. Detectors MUST apply priority order and emit the FIRST category whose rule fires: `Cat 0 → Cat 6b → Cat 6a → Cat 5 → Cat 3c → Cat 3b → Cat 3a → Cat 3 → Cat 2 → Cat 4 → Cat 1`. Rationale: infrastructure/integrity evidence (Cat 0/6b/6a) dominates; clean-restart (Cat 5) dominates orphans; specific Cat 3 sub-cases dominate generic; Cat 2 dominates Cat 4 (investigator subsumes auto-resume for non-idempotent); Cat 1 is terminal (cheapest). Resolves Cat 3 vs Cat 6a workspace-missing tension, Cat 3c vs Cat 3b verdict-unexecuted-on-merged-run tension.
close_reason: SUBSUMED: RC-003a priority-ordered first-match covered by hk-63oh.73 — reconciliationpriority_rc003a_test.go:5,51 (TestRC003a_PriorityOrderIsComplete) + crashrecovery_rc031_test.go:578,610,717,799

title: Define `WorkflowClass` Workflow-record extension (schemas.md §6.5) — RC-owned
description: Per [schemas.md §6.5]: RC extends the Workflow record owned by [execution-model.md §4.1] with a subsystem-local tag — `workflow_class : WorkflowClass` (optional tag, absent means "ordinary"). WorkflowClass enum: `reconciliation` (the workflow implements an RC-category investigator or auto-resolver dispatch; scoped to S01-shipped DOT per RC-004). Semantics: a Workflow whose `workflow_class = reconciliation` is subject to RC-002 (exactly-one-checkpoint commit per workflow), RC-002a (at most one per target_run_id), RC-INV-001 (uniqueness audit sensor). Workflows without `workflow_class` set are ordinary; reconciliation requirements do not apply. Ownership: extension is RC-owned; acceptance into EM's canonical Workflow record tracked in OQ-RC-002. If EM accepts, RC-002 and RC-INV-001 will re-point at [execution-model.md §4.1]; until then, schemas.md §6.5 is the canonical declaration. Future workflow classes (improvement-loop, operator-cli-handler) reserved for future use.
close_reason: merged: ff9113a (impl 4e2f63b + cleanup ff9113a)

title: Reconciliation-category taxonomy umbrella (§8.1–§8.11a + §8.12 dispatch)
description: Implement the §8 reconciliation-category taxonomy umbrella + §8.12 action-mapping dispatch table. The taxonomy contains 11 detection categories (Cat 0/1/2/3/3a/3b/3c/4/5/6a/6b — see per-category beads `rc-error.cat-N`) with priority-ordered first-match resolution per RC-003a (Cat 0 → 6b → 6a → 5 → 3c → 3b → 3a → 3 → 2 → 4 → 1). Action-mapping dispatch: §8.12 prose authoritative on semantics; [schemas.md §6.3] tabular schema authoritative on mechanical dispatch; divergence is a lint failure. The umbrella owns the cross-category invariants (priority order, dual-table sync, amendment-protocol gate per RC-009). Per discipline v0.9 §2.11(c.2), this bead is a vocabulary OWNER for the ReconciliationCategory enum; consumer §4 reqs that cite a category by ID block-on this umbrella OR the specific per-category bead. The taxonomy bead's own predecessors are limited to schema-shaped beads it consumes (rc-schema.reconciliation-category enum bead) — taxonomy does NOT block-on §4 reqs. 11 per-category beads parented per §2.10.
close_reason: SUBSUMED: hk-63oh.79 (closed) — taxonomyconformance_rc007009_test.go covers RC-007..009 + §8.12 dispatch; reconciliationcategory.go:1 defines 11-category enum; hk-63oh.52 (closed) defines ReconciliationCategory ENUM

title: Cat 0 — Infrastructure unavailable (§8.1)
description: Per §8.1: detection rule — `br --version` fails within T=5s; or Beads SQLite locked by non-harmonik; or git index locked; or `.harmonik/` unwritable; or filesystem full. Pre-check is mandatory per RC-012. Default response: halt classification at detector layer; daemon transitions to `degraded` status (pre-`ready` state) and waits for infrastructure resolution. Escalation: operator clears prerequisite (restart `br`, unlock git, free disk, fix permissions); daemon retries at configurable cadence. Emitted event: `infrastructure_unavailable` naming the specific prerequisite. Investigator: No. Auto-resolver: Yes (wait-and-retry). Co-owned with [process-lifecycle.md §4.2 PL-010] (degraded-state transition).

title: Cat 1 — Idempotent rerun (§8.2)
description: Per §8.2: detection rule — last checkpoint's node has `idempotency_class = idempotent` per [execution-model.md §4.2 EM-009]. Default response: auto-resume by re-spawning interrupted node. Escalation: none at this layer; subsequent failure classified under [execution-model.md §8] failure taxonomy. Emitted event: `reconciliation_category_assigned` (category=1); dispatch produces `node_dispatch_requested` per handler contract. Investigator: No. Auto-resolver: Yes (re-spawn). Examples: reviewer agent mid-run, researcher agent, mechanism-tagged non-agentic nodes (lint, test, typecheck). Reconciliation-workflow nodes never classified here per RC-002 (not checkpointed mid-investigation).
close_reason: Spec-corpus sensor for §8.2 Cat 1; TestRCSection8CategoriesSpec in internal/specaudit/rc_section8_categories_test.go; commit 9742bf9

title: Cat 2 — Non-idempotent in-flight (§8.3)
description: Per §8.3: detection rule — DOT node with `idempotency_class ∈ {non-idempotent, recoverable-non-idempotent}` per [execution-model.md §4.2 EM-009]; AND bead `in_progress`; AND no `run_completed` or `run_failed` event since the checkpoint (per [event-model.md §8] per-event subsections). Cat 2 detection MUST use a controlled, indexed query against the bounded JSONL divergence-evidence reader (RC-014), NOT a live-follow tail. Default response: dispatch a Cat 2 investigator workflow with the per-category playbook (RC-016). Escalation: investigator verdict — typically `resume-with-context`/`reset-to-checkpoint`/`reopen-bead` (RC-020); budget-exhausted dispatches default-escalate (RC-018). Emitted event: `reconciliation_category_assigned` (category=2); investigator emits `reconciliation_verdict_emitted`. Investigator: Yes. Auto-resolver: No.
close_reason: Spec-corpus sensor for §8.3 Cat 2; TestRCSection8CategoriesSpec in internal/specaudit/rc_section8_categories_test.go; commit 9742bf9

title: Cat 3 — Store disagreement, generic (§8.4)
description: Per §8.4: detection rule — git/Beads/JSONL tell inconsistent stories about the same run, and no Cat 3 sub-category (3a/3b/3c) matches. Examples: duplicate `transition_id` across commits in same run; bead `in_progress` but worktree missing without run-terminal marker. Default response: dispatch Cat 3 generic investigator workflow with git-wins orientation per RC-INV-001. Escalation: investigator verdict — typically `accept-close-with-note`/`reopen-bead`/`no-op-accept`. Emitted event: `store_divergence_detected` + `reconciliation_category_assigned` (category=3). Investigator: Yes. Auto-resolver: No (escalates through investigator).
close_reason: Spec-corpus sensor for §8.4 Cat 3; TestRCSection8CategoriesSpec in internal/specaudit/rc_section8_categories_test.go; commit 9742bf9

title: Cat 3a — Torn Beads write (§8.4a)
description: Per §8.4a: detection rule — adapter intent file present at `.harmonik/beads-intents/<idempotency_key>.json` per [beads-integration.md §4.10 BI-029] AND corresponding bead's current Beads coarse-status (read via `br show` per BI-031) is neither pre-state nor post-state of intended transition. Adapter's status-check-before-reissue (BI-031b) classifies as divergence; Cat 3a auto-resolver routes through same adapter. Default response: auto-resolve via adapter's BI-031 protocol — status-check classifies (status_match → no-op, pre-state → reissue, schema_mismatch → BrSchemaMismatch); status neither pre nor post → route to investigator dispatch. Escalation: if audit log ambiguous or adapter raises BrSchemaMismatch, escalate to Cat 3 generic. Emitted event: `store_divergence_detected{class=torn-beads-write}` + `reconciliation_category_assigned` (category=3a). Investigator: No (common case). Auto-resolver: Yes.
close_reason: done

title: Cat 3b — Verdict-unexecuted (§8.5)
description: Per §8.5: detection rule — investigator-run's task branch contains a `reconciliation_verdict_emitted` commit AND no subsequent `Harmonik-Verdict-Executed: true` commit on the same branch (per RC-025, [schemas.md §6.4]). Default response: auto-resolve via RC-026 re-execution — daemon performs staleness check (RC-024); if stale, re-dispatches fresh reconciliation; if not stale, executes verdict per RC-025 and writes verdict-executed commit. No new investigator spawned. Escalation: if re-execution itself fails (e.g., `br` errors), route to Cat 3 generic. Retry cap N=5 per RC-026a; cap exceeded → Cat 6b. Emitted event: `reconciliation_category_assigned` (category=3b). Investigator: No. Auto-resolver: Yes.
close_reason: Spec-corpus sensor for §8.5 Cat 3b; TestRCSection8CategoriesSpec in internal/specaudit/rc_section8_categories_test.go; commit 9742bf9

title: Cat 3c — Inverse premature-close (§8.6)
description: Per §8.6: detection rule — merge commit on target branch (main or integration per [workspace-model.md §4.7]) tagged with `Harmonik-Run-ID R` for run R whose workflow reached success terminal state; bead for R still `in_progress` in Beads; no subsequent in-flight checkpoints for R. Default response: auto-verdict `accept-close-with-note` with mechanical close-write (routed through idempotency-keyed adapter per [beads-integration.md §4.10]). No investigator spawned. Escalation: if mechanical close-write fails persistently, escalate to Cat 3 generic. Emitted event: `reconciliation_category_assigned` (category=3c); downstream `reconciliation_verdict_executed`. Investigator: No. Auto-resolver: Yes. Rationale: git is authoritative for completion (RC-INV-001); divergence is deterministically resolvable in Beads's direction without LLM reasoning.
close_reason: Spec-corpus sensor for §8.6 Cat 3c; TestRCSection8CategoriesSpec in internal/specaudit/rc_section8_categories_test.go; commit 9742bf9

title: Cat 4 — Recoverable known state (§8.7)
description: Per §8.7: detection rule — agent was in well-defined retry/backoff state at crash time (e.g., rate-limited pending retry timer, waiting for human gate). Default response: auto-resume with pending action — re-spawn with prior retry/backoff timer, or re-present gate to relevant actor. Escalation: if retry attempt cap reached, reclassify per [execution-model.md §8] failure taxonomy. Emitted event: `reconciliation_category_assigned` (category=4). Investigator: No. Auto-resolver: Yes.
close_reason: Spec-corpus sensor for §8.7 Cat 4; TestRCSection8CategoriesSpec in internal/specaudit/rc_section8_categories_test.go; commit 9742bf9

title: Crashed reconciliation-workflow branch classification (Cat 5)
description: Per RC-003b: a reconciliation workflow whose task branch exists but whose investigator subprocess died mid-run (no verdict commit yet emitted) MUST be classified as Cat 5 (clean re-dispatch) on subsequent reconciliation cadence ticks, NOT Cat 6a (multiple task branches). Discriminator is the `Harmonik-Workflow-Class: reconciliation` trailer per RC-002 (cross-spec coordination request to EM trailer registry; tracked OQ-RC-002). RC-003a priority order applies; this rule scopes the Cat 5 vs Cat 6a tiebreak for the reconciliation-workflow case.
close_reason: done

title: Cat 5 — Clean restart (§8.8)
description: Per §8.8: detection rule — nothing in-flight for this run. Includes orphaned branches from prior runs of beads that have since been re-claimed (per RC-010), AND crashed reconciliation-workflow branches (no verdict commit yet emitted) discriminated by `Harmonik-Workflow-Class: reconciliation` trailer per RC-003b. Default response: normal startup; proceed to `ready` per [process-lifecycle.md §4.2]. No-op. Escalation: none. Emitted event: `reconciliation_category_assigned` (category=5). Investigator: No. Auto-resolver: Yes (no-op).
close_reason: Spec-corpus sensor for §8.8 Cat 5; TestRCSection8CategoriesSpec in internal/specaudit/rc_section8_categories_test.go; commit 9742bf9

title: Cat 6a — Integrity violation, LLM-triageable (§8.11)
description: Per §8.11: detection rule — structurally wrong data whose cause an investigator can reason about and whose resolution might be operator-actionable. Detectors: workspace path referenced by in-flight bead does not exist on disk AND sibling's transition-record file is absent (RC-003a priority places this here for workspace-missing + sibling-absent); trailer-vs-sibling-file mismatch on checkpoint commit (e.g., `Harmonik-Transition-ID` trailer present but sibling file missing per [execution-model.md §4.4 EM-018]); worktree has in-progress git operation (rebase, merge, cherry-pick, bisect) that is not the work product being tracked (`.git/rebase-merge`, `.git/rebase-apply`, `.git/MERGE_HEAD`, `.git/CHERRY_PICK_HEAD`, `.git/BISECT_LOG` checks); bead `in_progress` with two+ task branches each advertising `Harmonik-Run-ID` without `Harmonik-Verdict-Executed` (reopen-bead landed, Beads audit entry missing). Default response: dispatch Cat 6a investigator workflow. Default verdict: `escalate-to-human` (RC-020); investigator MAY downgrade to repair path. Escalation: operator intervention via escalation event. Emitted event: `store_divergence_detected` + `reconciliation_category_assigned` (category=6a); downstream `reconciliation_verdict_emitted` (typically escalate-to-human). Investigator: Yes. Auto-resolver: No.
close_reason: Spec-corpus sensor for §8.11 Cat 6a; TestRCSection8CategoriesSpec in internal/specaudit/rc_section8_categories_test.go; commit 9742bf9

title: Cat 6b — Integrity violation, mechanically unrecoverable (§8.11a)
description: Per §8.11a: detection rule — structurally wrong data with no recovery path an LLM could meaningfully affect. Detectors: JSONL is corrupt/unparseable past byte offset (divergence-evidence reader RC-014 cannot make determination); checkpoint commit hash referenced in JSONL missing from git's object database; git object database itself corrupted (`git fsck` fails). Default response: auto-escalate to operator without investigator spawn. Emit `operator_escalation_required` with Cat 6b reason and target-run context. No reconciliation workflow dispatched. Escalation: operator intervention — restore from backup, rebuild worktree, repair git object database. Emitted event: `reconciliation_category_assigned` (category=6b) + `operator_escalation_required`. Investigator: No (spawning to "explain why git is corrupt" burns tokens on operator-only problem). Auto-resolver: N/A (operator intervention). Cat 6b emissions are exempt from corroboration via dedicated escalation path per RC-019a.
close_reason: done

title: Reconciliation-as-workflow + uniqueness fixture (RC-001..RC-006, RC-002a/002b/003a/003b, RC-INV-001)
description: Per §10.2 (RC-001..RC-006): structural tests verifying reconciliation workflows carry the `workflow_class = reconciliation` tag; integration tests verifying S01 packaging ships required DOT + YAML per RC-004. RC-002a concurrent-reconciliation: dispatch two reconciliation workflows against same `target_run_id`; verify `flock(LOCK_EX|LOCK_NB)` on `.harmonik/reconciliation-locks/<target_run_id>.lock` causes second dispatch to emit `reconciliation_dispatch_deduplicated` and skip. Stale-lock recovery test: kill daemon mid-investigation, restart, verify orphan-sweep cleans lock via PL-006 and next reconciliation cadence proceeds. title: Cross-spec name collision: WorkspaceState (RECORD vs ENUM)
description:
Two specs declare a type named WorkspaceState with different shapes:

1. specs/workspace-model.md §6.1: ENUM (7 values — created/ready/leased/merge-pending/conflict-resolving/merged/discarded). Already implemented at internal/core/workspacestate.go.

2. specs/reconciliation/schemas.md §6.1: RECORD (5 fields — path, path_exists, branch_tip_hash, wip_present, git_in_progress_op). Bead hk-63oh.48 is the implementation tracker.

Same name, different specs, different shapes — cannot co-exist in internal/core/.

Resolution candidates:
A. Rename reconciliation's RECORD to e.g. WorkspaceObservation, InvestigatorWorkspaceSnapshot, or ReconciliationWorkspaceView.
B. Rename workspace-model's ENUM to WorkspaceLifecycleState.
C. Put one in a sub-package (internal/reconciliation/).

Per CLAUDE.md (specs are normative) and HANDOFF.md directives v14 (cross-spec ENUM inconsistency = spec-fix not implementation-choice), this is the spec-fix flow. Surfaced 2026-05-08 during pre-dispatch grep for hk-63oh.48.

Blocks hk-63oh.48 (cannot implement without name resolution).
close_reason: Resolved 2026-05-09 by spec-fix commit fed2377: reconciliation RECORD renamed to WorkspaceObservation (workspace-model.md ENUM preserved as-is per existing impl at internal/core/workspacestate.go). Unblocked hk-63oh.48 + downstream hk-63oh.47. Bead bodies for .48/.47 updated.

Currently no such helper exists; the registry encodes the shape but `hk-63oh.59` test `TestVerdictExecuted_NonTrueValueFailsEnum` (internal/core/trailers_test.go) flagged the gap explicitly. This helper unblocks trailer-lint implementation and lets the registry's EnumValues=["true"] declaration on Harmonik-Verdict-Executed actually enforce the RC-023 malformed-value rule.

title: DOT: non-committing agentic node mode (SUCCESS without HEAD advance)
description:
T3 — Non-committing agentic node mode (SUCCESS without HEAD advance). Cite WG-041 (§I.4), EM-015d carve-out (§II.2), EM-058 non-committing sub-note (§II.8), HC §4.2a SUCCESS-without-commit note (§III.5).

WHAT: Gate the HEAD-advance failure check at dot_cascade.go:585-591 on node.NonCommitting, DOT MODE ONLY.
- Add non_committing (boolean, default false) to dot.Node + core.Node and the parser reserved node-attr set (agentic-only; on reviewer/non-agentic/gate → v1 WARNING, retained, ignored).
- In the implementer-class post-launch branch: if node.NonCommitting is true, a clean exit yields Outcome{SUCCESS} WITHOUT requiring postHeadSHA != preHeadSHA. If false (default), today's behavior (no-advance → node failure). BOTH modes: an unresolvable HEAD (resolveWorktreeHEAD error at :585-587) remains a daemon-side error (broken worktree is a real failure).
- dot-mode-only: the review-loop implementer-MUST-commit path (EM-015d) is UNTOUCHED — v69 must not regress. The relaxation is gated on the per-node attr and never reaches the review-loop path.
- REJECT auto_status as a node attribute (NOT in the reserved set; but add an explicit ingest error/warning so an author porting auto_status=true gets a clear 'use non_committing' message — pairs with T7 hk-9t892).
- A non_committing node produces no committed work product; authoring rule (WG-041, non-enforced): pair every non_committing node with a downstream validating tool node.

DELIVERABLES: dot.Node/core.Node non_committing field; parser changes; the gated check in dispatchDotAgenticNode; unit test: non_committing implementer clean-exit-no-commit → SUCCESS; default implementer no-commit → failure; review-loop path unchanged; broken-HEAD → error in both modes.

ACCEPTANCE: a dot agentic node non_committing="true" that exits clean without a commit returns SUCCESS and the cascade continues; the same node without the attr fails; a review-loop run still requires the implementer commit (regression guard).

DEPENDS ON: T0 (hk-jyqxe). CO-EDITS dispatchDotAgenticNode with T2 (hk-sdnzj) + T4 (hk-q8nqr) — C touches the post-launch HEAD region; B/D touch pre-launch (non-overlapping lines, same function) — SERIALIZE the cluster to avoid conflicts. Source: kilroy gap analysis 2026-05-28.
notes: implementer agent_ready_timeout at iteration 2

title: comms presence unreliable: 'harmonik comms who' reports a live, active agent as offline/stale (presence beacon not refreshed during work)
description:
SYMPTOM: 'harmonik comms who' reports a live, actively-working agent as offline/stale (operator-reported 2026-06-02; corroborated — who said 'no agents online' while the daemon was dispatching peer beads).

ROOT CAUSE (CONFIRMED, independently re-verified by a 2nd reviewer): presence is a hand-kept 120s-TTL heartbeat that NOTHING refreshes during work. EmitAgentPresence (internal/daemon/commspresencehandler_7t27s.go:96) has ONE product caller, reachable only via the comms-presence socket op (socket.go:482) from runCommsPresenceSubcommand (comms.go:648), verb->reason hard-coded to join/leave only (comms.go:694-699). comms-send/comms-recv/subscribe never touch presence. AgentPresenceReasonRefresh='refresh' (internal/core/agentcommspayloads_djqc9.go:43) is defined+validated but NEVER assigned (dead path); doc comment :132-134 promising auto-refresh during long recv/subscribe is unimplemented. who: ComputePresenceRegistry (comms.go:607-638) keeps latest agent_presence per agent; IsOnline (642-644)=Status==online && time.Since(last_seen)<120s (line 588); prints 'no agents currently online' on empty (863-867).

FIX (#1+#2, refined by independent review):
(1) Activity-derived liveness + honest output. effective_last_seen = max(latest agent_presence beat, latest agent_message send/recv for that agent). Fold BOTH into the ONE existing events.jsonl scan (who already full-scans via ScanAfter comms.go:611 — do not add a 2nd pass).
(2) MANDATORY / load-bearing (NOT optional): emit a reason:'refresh' agent_presence beat when the daemon handles send/recv/subscribe for an agent (wire the dead AgentPresenceReasonRefresh path). WHY: #1 alone does NOT cover a receive-only agent (subscribes, never sends => no agent_message); #2's refresh-on-recv is what keeps it online.
STATE MACHINE for who (pin this; do not invent): online = effective_last_seen<120s; stale = 120s..10m, printed 'stale (last seen Xm ago)'; offline = >10m. A clean 'leave' beat (status=offline) MUST short-circuit to offline immediately — NEVER 'stale' — else a cleanly-departed agent inverts the signal. A missing beacon must never read as a confident offline while activity is recent.
SKEW NOTE: last_seen stamped daemon-side, compared against the reading process's now() (cross-process wall-clock). #1 does NOT fix this; acceptable single-host — do not claim resolved.
CAVEAT: 'active daemon run' liveness needs submitting-agent attribution the queue lacks (keys on run_id) — same gap as session-keeper dispatch-gating; lean on agent_message as primary, run-attribution is a follow-up, do NOT block on it.
TESTS: (a) sent a message 30s ago => online despite no beat in 200s; (b) receive-only agent that received within 120s => online (via refresh beat); (c) last seen 5m => 'stale (5m)' not absent; (d) after 'leave' => offline immediately, not stale; (e) refresh beat emitted on send AND recv. Refs: hk-6vwi3.
close_reason: done

title: Add 'subscribe' socket op + 'harmonik subscribe' CLI; replaces JSONL-tail pattern
description: Long-running socket op streams NDJSON events on the connection until close. Request shape: {op:'subscribe', types:[...], since_event_id:'', heartbeat_seconds:60}. CLI wrapper: 'harmonik subscribe --types ... --heartbeat 60s --json' composes with Claude Code Monitor tool. Replaces the brittle 'agents must know to tail .harmonik/events/events.jsonl' pattern. Internally: thin wrapper registering eventbus.Subscribe observer with buffered channel; back-pressure = drop-oldest with sequence-gap event. ~180 LOC subscribe.go + 30 LOC socket.go dispatch + allowlist update in oninv006_no_control_surface_bypass_test.go with operator-nfr.md citation + 70 LOC cmd/harmonik/subscribe.go. Heartbeat (default 60s, max 600s, min 10s): when subscription idle, emit {type:'heartbeat', active_runs:[{run_id,bead_id,age_seconds}], last_event_id}. Heartbeats are subscription-only — NOT bus-published — don't pollute events.jsonl.
close_reason: Landed as d73bdbc; reviewers all reported (2 APPROVE + 1 REQUEST_CHANGES with follow-ups filed as hk-mkroe/hk-a5sil/hk-bra0j/hk-6232r; cleanup already landed in 4ea1deb)

title: daemon: implementer claude escapes worktree and writes files into main's working tree (cross-contamination)
description:
Root cause confirmed via RCA sub-agent (2026-05-22). NOT a daemon launch bug — daemon correctly launches the implementer with cwd=<worktree>. The escape mechanism is path-anchoring in the implementer prompt:

1. Implementer starts in correct worktree (verified: session-log line 2 'cwd=/Users/gb/github/harmonik/.harmonik/worktrees/<run_id>' in all 4 cases)
2. Implementer runs discovery: 'find /Users/gb/github/harmonik/internal/core -type f -name "*.go"' (absolute MAIN path as argument)
3. find returns main-repo absolute paths
4. Implementer's subsequent Write/Edit calls use those exact paths
5. Files land in main; worktree branch tip = parent SHA

Smoking gun (run 019e4e62-c670 / hk-wkzlc run-2, session log /Users/gb/.claude/projects/-Users-gb-github-harmonik--harmonik-worktrees-019e4e62-c670-7ae1-b649-b0d2d057dccf/019e4e62-ca82-735f-a559-f4caa0ee74c3.jsonl):
- L16 Bash: find /Users/gb/github/harmonik/internal/core ...
- L141 Edit: /Users/gb/github/harmonik/internal/core/eventtype.go (MAIN, NOT worktree)
- L145,161 Writes also to main paths

New-file vs modify-only correlation: confirmed. New-file work triggers wide find/ls discovery up front (anchors to main); modify-only work usually Reads then Edits a specific known path (less discovery, less escape).

FIX (2-layer):
1. PRIMARY: inject preExecMsg telling implementer 'your working directory is <workspacePath>; all file edits MUST be paths inside it. Never edit files under /Users/gb/github/harmonik that are not inside your worktree.' One-place edit in internal/daemon/claudelaunchspec.go (or its prompt builder) where workspacePath is already in scope.
2. DEFENSE IN DEPTH: daemon-side post-implementer assertion. After implementer exit, git -C <main_repo> status --porcelain — if dirty (new files/modifications in main), restore main + fail the run with new implementer_escaped_worktree event.
3. DIAGNOSTIC (optional): emit no_commit_progress warning at 5min so orchestrator can surface stuck runs before the 10min kill.

Daemon paths:
- launch cwd: internal/daemon/claudelaunchspec.go:397 (rc.workspacePath)
- tmux -c: internal/lifecycle/tmux/osadapter.go:476-488
- 10min wall kill: internal/daemon/pasteinject.go:104 (hk-7srrd tracks replacement)

Status: ready for implementer dispatch with full RCA + fix design. Recovery patches saved at /tmp/escape-recovery.patch + /tmp/escape-recovery-untracked.tgz.
close_reason: Fixed via eb43a6b

title: Docs: add 'work project deployment' section documenting --target-branch/--protect-branch,
description: FULL: Docs: add 'work project deployment' section documenting --target-branch/--protect-branch, the branching.yaml work-project template, migration (branch integration off main, set protect_branches, restart daemon), integration->main as a human PR step; update supervisor launch line | SCOPE: AGENTS.md / docs/orchestration-protocol-v2.md / supervisor script | Source: harmonik-productization-design workflow 2026-06-02. codename:productization; integration-branch P0s gate the work-project deploy; the 3 beads editing mergeRunBranchToMain (target-branch threading, start_from retarget, post-merge build gate) MUST be dispatched SERIALLY.
design:
IMPLEMENTER CONTEXT (orchestrator-injected 2026-06-03):

Add a "Work-project deployment" section documenting how to point harmonik at a repo that must NOT auto-push main.

COORDINATION (important): the bead dispatched immediately before you (hk-y5ke5) flips docs/orchestration-protocol-v2.md header to `Status: SUPERSEDED` (its normative content now lives in AGENTS.md). THEREFORE: add your new "Work-project deployment" section to **AGENTS.md** (the live normative doc), NOT to the now-superseded orchestration-protocol-v2.md. Touch orchestration-protocol-v2.md only if you must, and never re-add normative content there. Your file scope is effectively: AGENTS.md + scripts/hk-keeper.sh (supervisor launch line).

CONTENT to document (facts, landed under codename:productization):
- The integration-branch flags on the daemon: `--target-branch <branch>` (daemon merges+pushes there instead of main), `--protect-branch <branch>` (deny-list; daemon fail-closes and refuses to push it), `--forbid-default-main` (refuse to start if target resolves to main). Enforced fail-closed at boot, dispatch, and in-merge.
- The `config/branching.yaml` work-project template: `protect_branches: [main]` + a `lands_on`/target branch. Show a minimal example.
- Migration steps: (1) create an `integration` branch off main, (2) set `config/branching.yaml` protect_branches:[main] + target=integration (or pass the flags), (3) restart the daemon so it picks up the new config/binary.
- integration -> main is a HUMAN PR step (the daemon never auto-merges integration into main). Note the future `harmonik promote` helper (hk-gax8v) will open that PR.
- Update the supervisor launch line in scripts/hk-keeper.sh to show how to pass the target/protect flags (or reference branching.yaml) for a work-repo deploy.

SCOPE: AGENTS.md + scripts/hk-keeper.sh. Keep it concise and example-driven.
close_reason: done

title: Daemon escape-detector poisons ALL dispatch on net-new untracked repo-root files (AGENT_COMMS.md/HANDOFF-*.md) — only pre-start files baselined
description: Escape-detector (implementer_escaped_worktree) fails the bead being finalized whenever the MAIN working tree is transiently dirty from ANY source, mis-attributing the dirt to that bead. Two confirmed sources under concurrency: (1) the daemon's OWN one-at-a-time merge of a SIBLING bead to main (flywheel repro: hk-vhped false-failed blaming internal/daemon/sessioncontext_chb023.go, which was actually landed by hk-gtgwn's merge d2100524 during hk-vhped's run); (2) net-new untracked repo-root scratch files an agent drops mid-run (AGENT_COMMS.md; cost hk-c6grw 3 spurious fails). hk-ooexj only baselined PRE-START untracked files, so neither case is covered. Systemic: any bead running while another merges/dirties main gets false-failed. FIX DIRECTION: diff against the run's OWN worktree (not main's global git status), or exclude main-worktree deltas caused by daemon merges + a repo-root scratch allowlist. Blocks reliable >1-wide concurrent dispatch; blocks hk-c6grw.

title: Parallelism prep: in-flight run registry on Daemon struct
description:
Roadmap row #4 / part of blocker E from POST_MVH_PARALLELISM_ROADMAP.md §1.

Location: internal/daemon/runregistry.go (NEW)

Problem: No in-flight registry exists. Concurrent task runs need a shared map run_id → RunHandle so the work loop can track live runs, enforce MaxConcurrent, and route operator signals.

Fix: Create RunRegistry as a field on Daemon struct (NOT package-level — that breaks concurrent tests per §6 anti-pattern). sync.Map or mutex-guarded map keyed by run_id → *RunHandle. RunHandle holds: bead ID, worktree path, watcher, started_at, cancel func.

API: Register(runID, *RunHandle), Unregister(runID), Get(runID), Len() int, Snapshot() []*RunHandle.

Acceptance: Unit test with N=100 concurrent Register/Unregister calls under go test -race passes. Snapshot returns stable results during concurrent mutation.

Size: S
close_reason: RunRegistry implemented with mutex-guarded map, full API (Register/Unregister/Get/Len/Snapshot), N=100 race test green

title: Review-loop iter-2 feedback never injected: reviewer-feedback.iter-N.md absent -> implementer resumes blind -> no-progress fail (breaks ALL REQUEST_CHANGES iteration)
description: With hk-lckbv (buffer-name) fixed, the iter-2 implementer-resume no longer wedges but now fails differently: the reviewer feedback file (.harmonik/reviewer-feedback.iter-1.md in the run worktree) is ABSENT when the daemon injects it -> log 'implementer-resume feedback iter 1: tmux: structural invariant violated: task file absent: .../reviewer-feedback.iter-1.md (skipping feedback inject)' -> iter-2 implementer resumes with NO reviewer feedback -> reproduces same diff -> 'no-progress detected at iteration 2: diff hash unchanged' -> run_failed. EFFECT: ANY bead drawing REQUEST_CHANGES cannot be iterated; review-loop iteration is broken end-to-end -- only clean iter-1 APPROVE beads succeed. REPRO: wave-4 hk-a8bg.17 (run 019e728a-7a23, Guard reorders edges); same end-state as wave-1 hk-5e9yj. ROOT: mismatch between where the reviewer writes feedback (verdict -> review.json) and where resume reads it (reviewer-feedback.iter-1.md, which is never written). FIX: (a) reviewer writes its REQUEST_CHANGES notes to reviewer-feedback.iter-N.md, OR (b) resume derives feedback from review.json (verdict+notes already there). Add E2E test: a REQUEST_CHANGES verdict must inject feedback to iter-2 AND the diff must change. Companion to hk-lckbv (layer 1 = buffer-name; this = layer 2).
close_reason: done

title: Spec amendment: model-selection resolution chain + ModelPreference descriptor
description:
## Scope

Single bead covering the normative spec amendment for the model-selection epic (hk-cfhj2). Mirrors the spec-first pattern used by the branching epic's hk-ordd3.

## Changes

### specs/execution-model.md §4.3 (around EM-012a — workflow-mode precedence)

Add a new normative section adjacent to EM-012a that defines a **4-tier resolution chain for model/effort selection**, mirroring EM-012a's structure but for a different decision domain:

- **Tier 1 (per-bead):** labels `model:<alias>` and `effort:<level>` on the bead. Multi-label conflict → emit `bead_label_conflict` event and treat tier as absent (same shape as EM-012a tier-1 conflict).
- **Tier 2 (per-project):** `.harmonik/config.yaml` `agents.<agent_type>.model` and `agents.<agent_type>.effort`. Absent file = absent tier. Parse error = daemon refuses to start.
- **Tier 3 (per-agent-type compiled default):** static map keyed by `core.AgentType`. Lives next to the adapter wiring.
- **Tier 4 (built-in fallback):** empty — the underlying tool applies its own default.

Walk tiers in order, first non-empty wins. Resolution runs ONCE at claim time. The resolved `(model, effort)` pair is sealed into the Run record and surfaced on `run_started`.

### specs/handler-contract.md (new section — ModelPreference descriptor invariant)

Add a normative section defining **ModelPreference** as the structured descriptor harmonik passes to the handler:

```
ModelPreference {
  model:  string  // opaque to harmonik below the descriptor layer
  effort: string  // one of low|medium|high|xhigh|max OR empty
}
```

Invariants:
- Harmonik validates the **shape** of `model`: non-empty (when populated), no shell-metacharacters per a documented regex (e.g. `^[A-Za-z0-9._:-]+$`), bounded length.
- Harmonik does **NOT** validate the **value** of `model`. Future handlers (codex, pi) can accept arbitrary model strings; a closed enum at the harmonik layer would prevent forward-compat.
- Handler-side rejection (launch failure with stderr-captured error) is the authoritative compatibility check.
- `effort` is closed-enum at the harmonik layer (5 values + empty) because the flag shape is shared across all current claude-family handlers.

### specs/handler-contract.md §4.2 HC-055 (flag allow-list)

Extend the allow-list to admit `--model <value>` and `--effort <value>` as optional trailing flags. Document that both flags are optional and omitted when the resolution chain produces empty.

### specs/handler-contract.md §4.x (new — ModelPreference resolution callsite)

Specify the handler's contract: receive a `ModelPreference` from the LaunchSpec; translate it to handler-tool-specific argv (for claude: `--model <m> --effort <e>`); fail the launch if the tool rejects.

### specs/claude-hook-bridge.md CHB-008 (if it enumerates argv)

Note the new optional flags in the argv enumeration.

### Out of scope

- Per-phase / per-node selection (Phase 3 DOT).
- Implementation in claudelaunchspec.go (separate bead hk-xo03m).
- Per-project config loader implementation (separate bead hk-bfvk7).
- Label parser on claim path (folded into hk-bfvk7 or a small follow-up).
- `model:` alias-vs-full-name decision (defer to impl bead — spec just says "string").

## Exit criteria

- New §s landed in execution-model.md, handler-contract.md.
- HC-055 allow-list amended.
- ModelPreference invariant explicitly states "opaque-to-harmonik value, validated by handler".
- One commit on the worktree branch. Subject: `spec(model-selection): 4-tier resolution chain + ModelPreference descriptor`.

## Notes for implementer

- Spec is normative — match the prose register of surrounding sections (EM-012a for tier-chain style, HC-001/HC-055 for handler-side prose).
- Do NOT touch code under internal/ — this bead is spec-only.
- Do NOT close the parent epic hk-cfhj2.
close_reason: Spec amended: EM-012b (4-tier resolution chain), HC-055a (ModelPreference invariants), HC-055 allow-list extended with --model/--effort; commit b2d3489

title: Extend core.EdgeKind to tolerate Beads's broader dep-type surface (related, etc.)
description:
Surfaced from hk-872.15 ShowBead implementation. Beads CLI 'br dep add --type' accepts 'blocks, parent-child, related, etc.' but specs/beads-integration.md §6.1 declares EdgeKind as exactly {parent-child, blocks, conditional-blocks, waits-for}. The orchestrator's own closed-bead-blocks-conversion protocol uses '--type related', so any bead with converted edges (including hk-872.15 itself) cannot currently be parsed by ShowBead — UnmarshalText rejects 'related' as unknown.

Resolution options: (a) add EdgeKindRelated as a 5th constant + spec amendment to §6.1 ENUM EdgeKind. Per the BI-007 read-surface tolerance principle (CoarseStatus precedent at v0.4.1), the read surface should tolerate any value Beads exposes; harmonik's WRITE-edge surface remains the spec subset. (b) Make EdgeKind extensible like CoarseStatus (Valid() returns false for unknown but UnmarshalText accepts and pass-through). Decision belongs in a spec-amendment kerf or BI follow-up; this bead tracks it.

Concrete known Beads values today: blocks, parent-child, related. The 'etc.' in br dep add help suggests more exist — implementer should enumerate via Beads CLI source or by trial.

Implementation MUST NOT widen the WRITE surface (harmonik MUST NOT introduce edges of new kinds via 'br dep add' until the spec amendment lands).
close_reason: Implemented in 3caf32d04 (rebased): core.EdgeKind read-surface tolerance per BI-007 precedent (UnmarshalText accepts unknown verbatim, Valid()=false; MarshalText still rejects). ShowBead now parses 'related' edges. Reviewer APPROVE.

title: HP-035 spec/code drift: IsPaused() lock discipline (RWMutex or relax spec)
description:

The code comment at line 140 explicitly explains the choice.

Pick one:
(a) Upgrade HandlerPauseController to sync.RWMutex; use RLock in IsPaused() / PausedEpochFor(). Preferred for hot-path dispatch loop.
(b) Amend HP-035: "MUST be safe for concurrent readers (via RWMutex read-lock or atomic.Value)" — removing "lock-free" word.

Also: specs/handler-pause.md frontmatter says status: draft; should be status: reviewed to reflect normative elevation.

The ctx.Done() branch at line 458 trusts this and may report exitCode=0 for a PID now owned by another user. Fix: errors.Is(err, syscall.ESRCH) explicitly; treat EPERM as alive. (2) The three smoke tests in stophook_smoke_hkcj0gm_test.go exercise socket plumbing (waitWithSocketGrace / hookSessionStore), NOT the runWait ctx-cancel→processDead→exitCode=0 path. Add a unit test that drives tmuxSubstrateSession.runWait through ctx.Done() with a dead pid and asserts exitCode=0; another with alive pid asserts exitCode=-1.

title: MVH-R6: Session wrapping exec.Cmd + WaitOwner
description:
MVH_ROADMAP row #6. Concrete `Session` type that wraps a running subprocess (Claude Code or twin) so the daemon can drive it.

Location: NEW file `internal/handler/session.go` (create the package). Tests in `internal/handler/session_test.go`.

Contract:
type Session interface {
    SendInput(ctx context.Context, line string) error  // writes to child stdin (line + '\n')
    Kill(ctx context.Context) error                    // SIGTERM the pgid; ctx deadline → SIGKILL
    Wait(ctx context.Context) error                    // blocks until process exits (uses internal/lifecycle.WaitOwner)
    Outcome() Outcome                                  // exit code, signal, duration, captured stderr tail
}

Implementation:
- Construct via `NewSession(ctx, cmd *exec.Cmd) (Session, error)`. Caller already has the command configured (env, args, dir, SysProcAttr from `internal/lifecycle.SpawnChildSysProcAttr`). Session takes ownership.
- Open stdout, stderr, stdin pipes via `StdoutPipe`/`StderrPipe`/`StdinPipe` BEFORE `cmd.Start`.
- Expose `Stdout() io.Reader` (and `Stderr() io.Reader`) so the caller (Handler.Launch in row #7) can attach a `handlercontract.Watcher` via `SpawnWatcher`.
- `Wait` delegates to `internal/lifecycle.WaitOwner` to get the pgid-aware reap.
- `Kill` sends SIGTERM via `syscall.Kill(-pgid, SIGTERM)`; if ctx fires before Wait returns, escalate to SIGKILL.
- `Outcome` is populated once `Wait` returns; capturing the last ~4KB of stderr is sufficient (ring buffer).

Tests: drive a tiny `sh -c` child (e.g. `sh -c "echo hello; sleep 5"`); assert SendInput delivers, Kill terminates within deadline, Wait returns, Outcome reflects signal/exit.

Out of scope: Adapter logic (row #8), Handler.Launch wrapper (row #7), Claude-Code-specific NDJSON parsing.
close_reason: Session interface + concrete impl in internal/handler/session.go; 7 tests green; composes with WaitOwner per PL-014
