title: Design: .beads/issues.jsonl worktree-merge story (child-bead-spawn safety, integration-branch coordination)
description: Current state: daemon resolves .beads/issues.jsonl conflicts with `git checkout --theirs` (main wins). Mostly safe because daemon drives canonical transitions and agents are advised not to br-close from worktrees. BREAKS when harmonik drives multi-bead workflows where one bead spawns child beads (research → 5 task beads) — child-bead creates are silently dropped on the --theirs resolution. Also breaks across integration-branch coordination: main-branch harmonik instance can't see beads created on an integration-branch worktree's JSONL. Two parallel research sub-agents dispatched 2026-05-21 to investigate (a) gitignore-JSONL + daemon-reconciliation, (b) br server/daemon mode, (c) custom git merge-driver (union-by-ID), (d) tag-and-extract worktree writes. Reports pending. NEXT STEP: spawn kerf work `bead-ledger-worktree-merge` once research lands; this bead is the umbrella tracking that work. Reference: session 2026-05-21.
close_reason: Design complete (kerf work bead-ledger-worktree-merge reached 'ready' 2026-05-30) AND implementation landed: custom union merge-driver hk-jon6r CLOSED, cmd/harmonik/beadsmerge.go present, wired via .gitattributes (.beads/issues.jsonl merge=beads-merge) + git config merge.beads-merge.driver. The git-checkout--theirs approach is replaced. No orphaned impl. Reassessment 2026-06-02.

title: Daemon owns exactly one watcher goroutine per active session
description: Per HC-011: daemon (S01) MUST spawn exactly ONE watcher goroutine per active handler session. N sessions = N watchers; watchers MUST NOT share state.

title: Watcher liveness + panic recovery
description: Per HC-011a: watcher body MUST install recover() barrier (panic → agent_failed{ErrStructural, watcher_panic}); subscriber-panic isolated per-subscriber + dead-lettered (HC-027). Per-watcher last_read_event_at updated on every successful socket read. Daemon supervisor checks at <=T/4 cadence that every active watcher advanced last_read_event_at within T/2; non-advance → agent_failed{ErrStructural, watcher_wedged} daemon-defect classification. Watcher-to-bus publish channel MUST have small bounded buffer (default 8); buffer-full → dead-letter route per HC-027 (NOT block).
close_reason: done

title: S04 owns the per-agent-type adapter
description: Per HC-012: Agent Runner (S04) MUST expose one Adapter per registered agent_type and MUST NOT hold per-session state or spawn per-session goroutines. Per-session state MUST live in watcher's stack/closure. Adapter methods are synchronous callbacks invoked by watcher on lifecycle events.
close_reason: AdapterRegistry in adapterregistry_hc012.go; commit ef05d78

title: Adapter surface is fixed
description: Per HC-013: every Adapter MUST provide DetectReady(event), DetectRateLimit(event), CleanExitSequence(ctx, session), RotateAccount(ctx) (returning ErrDeterministic if not supported). Adding callback requires foundation amendment per AR-020.
notes: Adapter interface already defined with exactly 4 methods per HC-013 in internal/handlercontract/adapter.go (commit dff0400): DetectReady, DetectRateLimit, CleanExitSequence, RotateAccount. Amendment requirement enforced by Go interface immutability at compile time. Subsumed by hk-8i31.73 implementation.

title: RotateAccount fires only at clean turn boundaries
description: Per HC-013a: Adapter.RotateAccount(ctx) MUST NOT be invoked while subprocess has in-flight LLM turn. Watcher MUST schedule rotation at next clean boundary (after agent_completed, before next dispatch). Mid-turn rotation forbidden — would interrupt provider HTTPS, leave worktree mid-transaction, and per HC-028 cannot mutate spawn-time HARMONIK_SECRET_* env. If no quiescent boundary within configurable window (default LaunchSpec.timeout), RotateAccount returns ErrTransient. Provider-secret rotation out of scope; account rotation in scope.
close_reason: done

title: Channel closure rule
description: Per HC-014: emitter of cross-subsystem channel MUST close on end-of-stream; consumers MUST treat closed channel as end-of-stream, NOT as error. Applies to watcher-to-bus channel + any future cross-subsystem channel.
close_reason: APPROVED. HC-014 channel-closure rule binding test landed at internal/specaudit/hc014_channel_closure_test.go. 5 sub-tests verify spec contains: (b) emitter MUST close, (c) consumer MUST treat closed as EOS, (d) NOT as error clause, (e) concrete instance 'watcher-to-event-bus', (f) Tags: mechanism. Reviewer nit (failures[] slice + parallel sub-tests = effectively dead summary log) is cosmetic — actual PASS/FAIL signaling via t.Error is correct. Forward-looking contract; no Go channel implementation yet.

title: Mutex discipline (per-run write lock; no mutex held across S04 adapter call)
description: Per HC-015: state transitions MUST acquire per-run write lock; event publication MUST NOT block state lock; consumers read from per-subscriber channel; no mutex MAY be held across call into S04 adapter.
close_reason: specaudit sensor TestHC015MutexDiscipline in internal/specaudit/hc015_mutex_discipline_test.go; commit 074ab1e

title: Work queue per agent role
description: Per HC-016: orchestrator MUST maintain one work queue per agent role per [architecture.md §4.8]. Workers drain own queue; cross-queue handoff MUST go through explicit transitions per [execution-model.md §4.6], never shared memory.
close_reason: HC-016 work queue per agent role: WorkQueueSet[T] + 7 sensors (298b8f9)

title: Session is the Go interface defined in §6.1
description: Per HC-002: every `Handler.Launch` success MUST return a `Session` satisfying the §6.1 interface; lifetime begins with `Launch` return, ends when `Wait` returns; methods MUST be safe to call from any goroutine.
close_reason: SUBSUMED by hk-8i31.72 Session interface implementation: internal/handlercontract/session.go already declares 'type Session interface' per §6.1 with all 6 methods. No additional code needed.

title: Orphan-reconnect window retry against daemon_not_ready
description: Per HC-016a: handler subprocess receiving typed daemon_not_ready{reason=unknown_run_id} from daemon (between socket bind and in-memory model build per [process-lifecycle.md §4.2 PL-003b]) MUST retry per PL-009b backoff schedule (initial 100ms, doubling, max 2s/attempt, capped at T_ready_wait=60s wall-clock). Watcher MUST NOT classify as session-failure during retry window. After cap exhaustion: ErrTransient + agent_failed{daemon_startup_window_exceeded}.
close_reason: done

title: Every public method takes ctx as first parameter
description: Per HC-017: every public method on Handler and Session MUST take ctx context.Context as first parameter. Implementations MUST propagate ctx to subprocess via wire protocol (timeout/deadline in LaunchSpec; cancellation via explicit Kill).

title: Cancellation terminates in-flight ops within bounded intervals
description: Per HC-018: ctx cancellation MUST cause in-flight Go-side ops to return context.Canceled (wrapped as ErrCanceled per §4.5) within 500ms; subprocess cleanup within 5s. Exceeding subprocess cleanup bound triggers escalation to hard termination per §4.6.
close_reason: Implemented in 958d8f0 (cherry-pick): HC-018 CancelGoSideBound=500ms + CancelSubprocessBound=5s + 5 sensor tests including ErrCanceled wrap and CleanExitSequence bound.

title: Context values restricted to observability metadata
description: Per HC-019: ctx values MUST NOT carry business data (run fields, outcomes, bead IDs); MAY carry only observability metadata (trace IDs, correlation IDs, operator-identity tokens). Business data flows via explicit parameters / LaunchSpec / event payloads. Restriction applies to every cross-subsystem call.
close_reason: Implemented in c4b63fd (cherry-pick): HC-019 static-grep sensor walking internal/+cmd/ for context.WithValue paired with business-data key patterns (16 patterns).

title: Five primary sentinel classes plus two structural sub-sentinels
description: Per HC-020: HC defines 5 primary sentinel error classes (ErrTransient, ErrStructural, ErrDeterministic, ErrCanceled, ErrBudget) + 2 structural sub-sentinels (ErrProtocolMismatch, ErrSkillProvisioningFailed — both wrap ErrStructural). Every error returned across subsystem boundary MUST wrap exactly one primary class via fmt.Errorf '...: %w'. Consumers use errors.Is / errors.As (NOT string matching); dispatch narrowest-first.
close_reason: HC-020 sentinels landed at internal/handler/errors.go (5 primary classes ErrTransient/Structural/Deterministic/Canceled/Budget + 2 ErrStructural-wrapped sub-sentinels ErrProtocolMismatch/SkillProvisioningFailed). New package internal/handler created. Sub-sentinels constructed via fmt.Errorf %w at declaration so wrap chain is structurally true. Reviewer APPROVE-CLEAN; all checks green.

title: ErrProtocolMismatch is a structural sub-sentinel
description: Per HC-021: ErrProtocolMismatch (version-negotiation failure / handler_capabilities absence within 5s / NDJSON line-cap exceeded) MUST wrap ErrStructural. Re-plan space: swap handler_ref, change pinned commit, upgrade daemon. Orchestrator retry policy per [execution-model.md §8.2] MUST distinguish protocol-mismatch re-plans from transient re-plans to avoid retry-spin.
close_reason: SUBSUMED: internal/handlercontract/sentinels.go:97 — ErrProtocolMismatch already declared wrapping ErrStructural; sentinels_test.go:53 already sensors errors.Is(ErrProtocolMismatch, ErrStructural)

title: ErrSkillProvisioningFailed is a structural sub-sentinel
description: Per HC-022: ErrSkillProvisioningFailed (skill-injection structural failure per §4.11.HC-048) MUST wrap ErrStructural. Outer sub-sentinel for skill-specific failure reporting; ErrStructural is routing fallback. Transient provisioning failures (HC-048a) wrap ErrTransient directly, NOT this sub-sentinel.
close_reason: Subsumed: ErrSkillProvisioningFailed sub-sentinel already shipped under closed sibling hk-8i31.24 ('Five primary sentinel classes plus two structural sub-sentinels'). Verified at internal/handler/errors.go:96 — sentinel wraps ErrStructural per HC-022; ErrTransient (line 21) is separate per HC-048a. Tests at internal/handler/errors_test.go:97 (TestSubSentinelErrSkillProvisioningFailed) + lines 175-179 (narrowest-first match) cover the acceptance criteria. No new code needed.

title: Error classification is mechanism-tagged
description: Per HC-023: mapping subprocess exit state / adapter-detected condition to sentinel class MUST be deterministic from structured fields (exit code, payload flags, typed adapter return). No cognition in classification; semantic interpretation belongs in reconciliation-investigator nodes.
close_reason: v41_impl_04aeb01

title: Subprocess crash emits agent_failed with typed class
description: Per HC-024: subprocess crash (exits non-zero without prior outcome_emitted) → watcher MUST emit agent_failed whose payload includes mapped sentinel class per §4.5. Routing of agent_failed to retry/re-plan/terminal owned by [execution-model.md §8].
close_reason: ClassifyCrash + CrashWithoutOutcomeSubReason + CrashAgentFailedPayload added to internal/handlercontract/outcomedelivery.go; 4 TestCrashClassFixture_* tests in outcomedelivery_test.go; commit a222aa6 on worktree-agent-hc-3

title: Socket-level I/O error distinct from subprocess crash
description: Per HC-024a: socket I/O errors (EPIPE, ECONNRESET, socket unlinked, decoder error per HC-007b) distinguished from subprocess termination. First occurrence without prior agent_completed/agent_failed: watcher emits agent_failed{ErrTransient, socket_io_error} + ONE reconnect attempt within 500ms. Reconnect succeeds + subprocess alive → resume; else reclassify to ErrStructural{progress_stream_broken} + SIGKILL + session terminated. Sessions are single-socket-lifetime at MVH. Distinguishes socket-broken-subprocess-alive from silent-hang (HC-026).
close_reason: done

title: Handler selection is config-level
description: Per HC-003: handler-binary choice MUST be derived from DOT node attributes (`handler_ref`, `agent_type`) and YAML policy resolved at workflow-load time per [execution-model.md §4.9]. Daemon MUST NOT carry test-mode branches between real/twin handlers at runtime.
close_reason: Implemented in 7636277: HC-003 static sensor (handlerselect_hc003_test.go) walking internal/ and cmd/ for forbidden runtime real/twin branching patterns.

title: Rate-limit events distinct from failure events
description: Per HC-025: when adapter's DetectRateLimit returns limited=true, watcher MUST emit agent_rate_limited (NOT agent_failed) carrying retry_after. On clearance: agent_rate_limit_cleared. Rate-limited sessions are NOT failures. Daemon's rate-limit policy is exponential backoff within wall-clock budget.
close_reason: done

title: Silent-hang detection state machine
description: Per HC-026: watcher MUST detect silent-hang (subprocess alive — socket connected, Wait not returned — but no progress-stream message INCLUDING heartbeats per HC-026a — for threshold T). Detection + escalation per §7.1 state machine (active → warning → soft-terminating → hard-terminating). Terminating error class MUST be ErrStructural.
close_reason: done

title: Handler heartbeat obligation (<=T/2 cadence)
description: Per HC-026a: every handler MUST emit agent_heartbeat at <=T/2 cadence while subprocess alive and no outcome_emitted. Payload: {session_id, phase} where phase in {starting, reasoning, tool_call, waiting_input, rotating, shutting_down} (extensible enum, additive-only). LLM wrappers without output channel during reasoning MUST synthesize on internal timer. Heartbeat resets silent-hang timer per §7.1; rate-limit windows continue heartbeats (waiting_input); shutdown window suppresses heartbeat (silent-hang suspended). Scenario-mode (twin scripted) carve-out: heartbeat_mode: scripted declared on script for byte-reproducible tests.
close_reason: done

title: Drain-forced silent-hang synthesis is ON-classified
description: Per HC-026b: when operator-initiated drain SIGKILLs still-running agent (per [operator-nfr.md §4.7 ON-029]), the resulting agent_warning_silent_hang{reason=drain_forced} synthesis is governed by [operator-nfr.md §4.9 ON-040], NOT HC's silent-hang taxonomy. Watcher MUST cooperate by NOT also emitting an HC-classified silent-hang for the same run/node. Single-emitter (ON-side) preserves HC-INV-006. Acceptance clause; enforcement in operator-nfr.
close_reason: done

title: Dead-letter behavior for undeliverable events
description: Per HC-027: events that cannot be delivered by watcher to in-process bus (bus full, subscriber panic) MUST be routed to dead-letter destination declared by [event-model.md §4.3]. Watcher MUST NOT drop events silently.
close_reason: done

title: Secrets injected via environment variable (HARMONIK_SECRET_* prefix)
description: Per HC-028: secrets MUST be delivered as env vars with stable prefix HARMONIK_SECRET_*. LaunchSpec MUST NOT carry secret values; the requirement-of-secret MAY be encoded indirectly (via freedom_profile_ref resolution) but value travels only via process environment.
close_reason: done

title: agent_started event MUST NOT include environment variables
description: Per HC-029: agent_started event payload MUST NOT include handler subprocess's environment. Launch provenance fields (binary path, commit hash) permitted; env-var maps not.
close_reason: APPROVED. HC-029 agent_started no-env-vars binding test landed at internal/specaudit/hc029_agent_started_no_env_test.go. 7 sub-tests verify (a) heading present, (b) MUST NOT include prohibition, (c) environment named, (d) permitted clause (provenance fields binary path / commit hash allowed; prevents over-broad misread), (e) environment variable maps named precisely, (f) Tags: mechanism, (g) §6.4 agent_started bullet cross-references HC-029. Forward-looking sensor — no agent_started emitter exists yet. i3136 helper prefix throughout.

title: Redaction registry middleware
description: Per HC-030: daemon MUST install redaction-middleware function in event-bus producer path applying HC-031 + HC-032 rules to every event payload + every structured log line before emission. Mechanism-tagged; no cognition.
close_reason: runtime sensor added: internal/eventbus/busimpl_test.go TestBusImplEmit_RegistryValuePatternRedactedBeforeDispatch (HC-032 via NewBusImplWithRegistry) + TestBusImplEmit_NilRegistryFallsBackToHC031Only; middleware wiring in busimpl.go:86, daemon.go:51 already complete

title: Common prefix redaction rule
description: Per HC-031: any field whose NAME matches case-insensitive regex (secret|token|password|api[_-]?key|auth) MUST be replaced with literal '<redacted>' before emission. Match is on field name, not value; intentional serialization of matching field is producer bug, not redaction bypass.
close_reason: HC-031 common prefix redaction: RedactByFieldName + 8 sensors (8c48a04)

title: Per-handler redaction patterns
description: Per HC-032: each handler spec MAY contribute additional value-shaped regex patterns for handler-specific secret formats (e.g., Anthropic API keys matching sk-ant-*). Patterns MUST be declared in handler's subsystem envelope as redaction entries + registered at daemon init. Values matching ANY registered pattern MUST be '<redacted>'. Review MUST reject handler spec that ships secret provider without declared pattern when shape-detectable.
close_reason: SUBSUMED: internal/handlercontract/redaction_hc028_test.go — fixture + HC-032 sensors already in place

title: Launch is idempotent on (run_id, node_id)
description: Per HC-004: `Handler.Launch` MUST be idempotent on `(spec.run_id, spec.node_id)` within one daemon generation — second `Launch` returns existing `Session` (or `ErrTransient` if prior session is terminating), MUST NOT spawn duplicate. Concurrent-launch second call MUST block on first's handshake outcome. Reconciliation-driven re-launches after daemon restart are new daemon generation = new launch.
close_reason: done

title: Compile-time event-schema payload check
description: Per HC-033: event-schema registry MUST verify at startup that no registered event type's payload schema declares a field whose name matches HC-031 regex. Any such field MUST be startup-time error, NOT runtime warning. Prevents schema drift that would silently ship unredacted secrets.
close_reason: specaudit sensor TestHC033EventSchemaPayloadCheck in internal/specaudit/hc033_event_schema_payload_check_test.go; commit 19b1b15

title: Secrets never appear in event log or unredacted session log
description: Per HC-034: no secret value MAY appear in any persisted event record, audit record, or session log as stored to disk. Operator debugging uses redacted forms; full-secret access requires filesystem-level privileges outside harmonik's control surface.
close_reason: v41_impl_0ddc097

title: Twins implement the same Handler interface
description: Per HC-035: twin handler (canonical real-handler substitute for scenario-test harness + CI) MUST implement Handler interface of §6.1 with SAME method signatures + SAME error-class discipline + MUST be launched as separate subprocess (per HC-045). Selection real-vs-twin is config-level per HC-003. Carve-out: hand-written in-process fakes used in Go unit tests for per-adapter / per-watcher / per-error-classifier targeted testing are NOT twins — not subprocesses, not subject to wire-protocol parity.
close_reason: done

title: Twin subprocesses honor the same wire protocol
description: Per HC-036: twin handler subprocess (per HC-035) MUST emit same progress-stream message types (HC-007/007a) + same outcome-delivery rule (HC-008) as real handler. Permitted differences: (a) script drives output instead of LLM, (b) no model-budget charging, (c) binary name <real>-twin or declared alias. Applies to subprocess; in-process fakes per HC-035 carve-out unaffected.

title: Twins carry identical boundary-classification tags
description: Per HC-037: twin handler's mechanism/cognition tagging MUST match real counterpart. Twin doesn't make decisions its real counterpart would delegate; it scripts them. Tagging deviation is a twin defect.

title: Twin conformance drift detection scoped to S07
description: Per HC-038: ongoing obligation to keep twins honest against real-agent drift is scoped to scenario-harness (S07) spec, post-MVH. This spec establishes parity contract; S07 owns drift-detection workflow.

title: Ready-state signaled by agent_ready event
description: Per HC-039: every handler subprocess MUST emit single agent_ready event on startup to signal it can accept work. Payload MUST include session_id + capabilities[] at minimum; full payload schema in [event-model.md §6.3]. Daemon MUST NOT dispatch work to session before observing agent_ready for that session.
close_reason: AgentReadyMsg with session_id + capabilities[] required fields — commit 4bf2dfb

title: Twins MUST emit agent_ready identically
description: Per HC-040: twin handlers MUST emit agent_ready with same shape and timing as real counterparts. Skipping in twins is twin-parity violation per §4.8.

title: Adapter-level ready detection
description: Per HC-041: Adapter.DetectReady callback (per HC-013) MUST return true exactly when adapter has observed agent_ready event for session in question. Adapters MUST NOT synthesize ready-state from other signals (e.g., first output chunk).

title: Handler subprocess launched from repo-relative path
description: Per HC-042: every handler subprocess MUST be launched via absolute path resolved from repo-relative prefix configured at daemon startup. PATH lookup forbidden for in-repo binaries. Handler with system_handler=true (e.g., Claude Code CLI installed by operator) MAY resolve via PATH; all others MUST NOT use PATH and MUST fail launch if configured absolute path is absent.
close_reason: HC-042 launch-path resolver landed via 6-chain pipelined merge at 4a316c6. internal/handler/launchpath.go: ResolveLaunchPath(repoRoot, binaryRef, systemHandler) + ErrLaunchPathMissing sentinel. Six branches per HC-042 (system+bare→PATH; system+rel-with-sep→join; system+abs→as-is; non-system+abs→error; non-system+rel→join; empty→error). No filesystem stat. internal/handler/launchpath_test.go: 7 tests, all named *HC042*, 100% coverage on internal/handler. Reviewer APPROVE-CLEAN. make check green.

title: LaunchSpec delivery is JSON-on-stdin or file argument
description: Per HC-005: daemon MUST deliver LaunchSpec via JSON-on-stdin (default, payloads <=1 MiB) or `--launch-spec <path>` file argument (>1 MiB). Handler MUST accept both; selection is payload-size-driven, NOT handler-type-driven.
close_reason: done

title: Commit-hash check for in-repo binaries
description: Per HC-043: before launch, orchestrator MUST verify in-repo handler binary's embedded commit hash matches expected hash configured for agent type. Mismatch MUST fail launch with ErrStructural + emit agent_failed. System handlers MAY log --version at startup in lieu of hash check; no signature verification at MVH.
close_reason: Landed (2-commit, fix-iteration): handler/commithash.go VerifyCommitHash(binaryPath, expected) — bytes.Contains scan of ldflags-stamped commit-hash; mismatch wraps ErrStructural directly per HC-043. Initial review (REQUEST_CHANGES, MAJOR) flagged that the implementer added a third structural sub-sentinel ErrCommitHashMismatch which would expand HC-020's enumerated 'five primary + two sub-sentinels' set without a foundation amendment; fix-agent removed the sentinel and wrapped ErrStructural directly (commit a68e545; pure-deletion, re-review skipped). Follow-up bead hk-uwie filed for the integration test against a real ldflags-stamped twin binary (deferred until hk-ahvq.48.4 lands the producer side).

title: Subprocess is a child of the daemon
description: Per HC-044: daemon MUST spawn every handler subprocess as direct child process (per [process-lifecycle.md §4.5]). Subprocess MUST communicate back on Unix domain socket at .harmonik/daemon.sock (per [process-lifecycle.md §4.1]); same socket as progress stream per HC-007/007a. One bidirectional socket-backed channel per session; no separate control channel at MVH. Socket authenticity: filesystem-permission-based at MVH (0600 owned by daemon user); per-connection challenges deferred post-MVH. Linux subprocesses SHOULD install PR_SET_PDEATHSIG(SIGTERM); macOS no equivalent (subprocess-survival-across-daemon-death addressed by HC-044a).
close_reason: APPROVED. HC-044 subprocess-as-child landed via SpawnChildSysProcAttr cross-platform helper in internal/lifecycle/. Linux variant sets Setpgid+Pgid+Pdeathsig=SIGTERM; darwin omits Pdeathsig (field absent on darwin's SysProcAttr). Distinct from PL-006a's SpawnSysProcAttr (provenance vs HC-044 daemon-child concerns). Reviewer nits cosmetic.

title: Launch MUST fail-fast on orphan-held workspace
description: Per HC-044a: before Launch returns Session, daemon MUST verify target workspace_path not held by prior-generation handler. Detection: pidfile at .harmonik/worktrees/<run_id>/.lock written atomically at spawn, removed on clean termination. On Launch: pidfile exists + recorded PID live + live process NOT owned by current daemon generation → ErrStructural{workspace_held_by_orphan} + agent_failed carrying offending PID. Daemon MUST NOT silently reclaim — two concurrent subprocesses writing same worktree silently corrupts artifacts; fail-fast mandatory. Stale pidfiles MAY be reclaimed. Reconciliation startup sweep (per [reconciliation/spec.md §4]) subsumes post-MVH. Socket file from prior generation MUST be unlinked before bind per [process-lifecycle.md §4.1].
close_reason: HC-044a: WorkerPidfilePath/WriteWorkerPidfileAtomic/RemoveWorkerPidfile/CheckOrphanHeldWorkspace in orphancheck_hc044a.go; 21 tests green

title: Twin binaries obey same launch rules
description: Per HC-045: twin binaries MUST be launched from known repo-relative path with expected-commit-hash check. Twin's expected commit hash MUST be pinned at workflow/policy configuration time.
close_reason: Implemented in c8b50a78 (rebased): TwinLaunchConfig + VerifyTwinLaunch composing HC-042 path rule and HC-043 hash gate, 11 HC045 tests pass. Reviewer APPROVE.

title: Skill provisioning + resolution surface (coalesce)
description: Per HC-046 + HC-047 (coalesced per discipline §2.3): handler MUST ensure agent subprocess has every skill named in LaunchSpec.required_skills[] available in agent-type-specific shape (file drops / CLI binaries on PATH / MCP-server registrations / reference-doc bundles; future shapes per per-handler specs) BEFORE subprocess emits agent_ready (per HC-049). Resolution MUST be deterministic: resolve name against LaunchSpec.skill_search_paths[] in order, take first match. No cognition participates. Until agent-configuration spec lands, skill packages resolve against [docs/foundation/components.md §10] directory layout (bootstrap; migrates when finalized).
delete_reason: orphan duplicate of hc-046 from prior killed mid-load 2026-04-28; canonical bead is hk-8i31.55 with full edge linkage

title: Skill provisioning + resolution surface (coalesce)
description: Per HC-046 + HC-047 (coalesced per discipline §2.3): handler MUST ensure agent subprocess has every skill named in LaunchSpec.required_skills[] available in agent-type-specific shape (file drops / CLI binaries on PATH / MCP-server registrations / reference-doc bundles; future shapes per per-handler specs) BEFORE subprocess emits agent_ready (per HC-049). Resolution MUST be deterministic: resolve name against LaunchSpec.skill_search_paths[] in order, take first match. No cognition participates. Until agent-configuration spec lands, skill packages resolve against [docs/foundation/components.md §10] directory layout (bootstrap; migrates when finalized).
close_reason: done

title: Fail-launch on unresolvable required skill (structural)
description: Per HC-048: if any name in required_skills[] cannot be resolved against skill_search_paths[] at launch time, handler MUST fail-launch with ErrSkillProvisioningFailed (wrapping ErrStructural per HC-022). Handler MUST emit (via progress stream) agent_failed carrying unresolved skill name; watcher publishes corresponding bus event. Run MUST NOT proceed.
close_reason: done

title: Retry with backoff on transient provisioning failure
description: Per HC-048a: name resolves but provisioning of resolved package into agent-process shape fails at runtime (network timeout, registry 5xx, disk-full, MCP handshake network error) → handler MUST classify per per-agent-type adapter heuristic: transient (network errors, 5xx, timeout) wrap ErrTransient; structural (package-integrity check failure, unsupported manifest, permission denied) wrap ErrSkillProvisioningFailed. Transient retries in-handler with exponential backoff (base 1s, cap 16s, max 4 attempts) bounded by LaunchSpec.provisioning_timeout (default 60s). On timeout/cap exhaustion: reclassify ErrStructural per §8.2 + fail-launch. provisioning_timeout distinct from LaunchSpec.timeout.
close_reason: done

title: Emit skills_provisioned before agent_ready
description: Per HC-049: after successful provisioning and before agent_ready, handler MUST emit skills_provisioned event (payload schema in [event-model.md §6.3]) carrying installed skills, source path resolved per skill, package version where available.
notes: SkillsProvisionedMsg wire struct + SkillProvisionedEntry in internal/handlercontract/skillsprovisioned_hc049.go. 7 tests cover constant value, JSON field names, omitempty version, and spec-corpus sensor verifying skills_provisioned precedes agent_ready per HC-049/HC-INV-004. Commit 7d50bbf.

title: Twin-parity for skill provisioning is wire-only
description: Per HC-049a: twin-parity invariant (§4.8) applies to wire signal of skill provisioning (the skills_provisioned event), NOT to filesystem side effects. Twin MAY skip filesystem + process-state side effects of real provisioning BUT MUST emit skills_provisioned with same claimed skill set as if provisioning had occurred. Downstream nodes inspecting worktree artifacts to verify skill installation are NOT part of handler-contract parity surface; SHOULD use event payload as authoritative record. Twin failing to emit violates HC-INV-002 even if filesystem untouched.

title: LaunchSpec is the record defined in §6.1
description: Per HC-006: delivered LaunchSpec MUST conform to §6.1 record shape. Required fields enumerated; optional `bead_id` (per [execution-model.md §4.3]), optional `snapshot_token` (per [reconciliation/spec.md §4.4] — bootstrap form).

title: Skill declarations are read from LaunchSpec
description: Per HC-050: handler MUST consult LaunchSpec.required_skills[] and LaunchSpec.skill_search_paths[] only; MUST NOT read DOT node attributes or YAML policy documents directly. Workflow-load-time resolution per [execution-model.md §4.9] and [control-points.md §4.11] populates LaunchSpec; handler consumes resolved set.
close_reason: done

title: Handler contract is the deterministic-daemon / execution-shape seam
description: Per HC-051: handler contract (this spec, in entirety) is architectural seam between deterministic daemon (workflow state, routing, dispatch per [process-lifecycle.md §4.6]) and execution shape (ntm + tmux + subprocess today). Proposal that would couple daemon to specific execution shape (e.g., importing ntm-specific types into daemon's routing) MUST fail this boundary check.
close_reason: Landed but stuck in_progress: HC-051 seam check shipped (internal/handlercontract/seamhc051_test.go + .golangci.yml depguard); blocker closed. Reassessment 2026-06-02.

title: Execution-shape evolution re-implements the adapter, not the watcher
description: Per HC-052: future replacement of execution shape (custom tmux + agent-profile library, cloud-execution shape, remote-container shape) MUST re-implement per-agent-type adapter of HC-012 without altering watcher of HC-011. Concurrency pin is load-bearing: new shapes provide new adapters; they do NOT move concurrency boundary.
close_reason: specaudit sensor TestHC052ExecutionShapeEvolution in internal/specaudit/hc052_execution_shape_evolution_test.go; commit 074ab1e

title: Cross-subsystem surface MUST remain stable across shape evolution
description: Per HC-053: cross-subsystem surface defined by this spec — Handler interface (§6.1), LaunchSpec record (§6.1), error taxonomy (§8), emitted event set (§4.2.HC-007) — MUST remain stable across execution-shape evolution. Execution-shape change altering any of these surfaces is breaking change requiring foundation amendment per AR-020.
close_reason: Landed as a693d09

title: Sensor: exactly one watcher goroutine per active session
description: Per HC-INV-001: for every active handler session tracked by daemon, exactly one live watcher goroutine owned by S01 MUST exist. Zero watchers = daemon defect; >1 watcher per session = daemon defect. Observable via daemon health-check surface per [operator-nfr.md §4.9]. Sensor (invariant test): §10.2 HC-011..HC-016 group — invariant test asserts exactly-one-watcher-per-session via daemon introspection.
close_reason: done

title: Sensor: twin handlers indistinguishable from real handlers
description: Per HC-INV-002: from daemon's perspective (interface, wire protocol, event schema, error-class discipline, tagging), twin handler MUST be indistinguishable from real counterpart. Daemon MUST carry zero conditional logic varying on handler-is-twin. Verification: reviewing daemon codebase yields zero `if isTwin` / `if agent_type == "*-twin"` branches (corpus-wide static-analysis lint).

title: Sensor: exactly one terminal event per session

Double terminal emission = daemon defect. budget_exhausted is pre-launch denial, NOT terminal event for this invariant. Dirty exit without prior terminal MUST emit agent_failed. title: Wire protocol: progress-stream messages over Unix domain socket (coalesce)
description: Per HC-007 + HC-007a + HC-007b (coalesced per discipline §2.3): handler subprocess connects back on `.harmonik/daemon.sock` (per [process-lifecycle.md §4.1]), emits typed NDJSON-framed progress-stream messages (1 JSON object + newline; max-line-cap 1 MiB enforced; embedded newlines forbidden). Watcher consumes messages, publishes corresponding bus events using EV envelope per [event-model.md §4.1]. Partial-message at EOF MUST be discarded; malformed JSON closes session with ErrStructural partial-message / malformed_progress_message sub-reason. Best-effort stream — durable record is the session-log file. The 12 message types: handler_capabilities, agent_ready, agent_started, agent_output_chunk, agent_completed, agent_failed, agent_rate_limited, agent_rate_limit_cleared, agent_heartbeat, session_log_location, skills_provisioned, outcome_emitted.

title: HC error sentinel set + 5-class detection-and-emission table
description: Implement the 5-primary + 2-sub-sentinel error-class taxonomy. Sentinels (Go var): ErrTransient, ErrStructural, ErrDeterministic, ErrCanceled, ErrBudget (primary); ErrSkillProvisioningFailed, ErrProtocolMismatch (sub-sentinels — each wraps ErrStructural). Detection-and-emission rules (§8.1-§8.7): per-class detection rule + emission rule. 8.1 Transient: network-transient, rate-limit-transient, sub-silent-hang timeout, first-occurrence socket I/O. 8.2 Structural: wrong plan, silent-hang, post-outcome shutdown timeout, partial/malformed message, watcher wedge/panic, sustained socket break, workspace held by orphan. 8.3 Deterministic: confirmed-bug from structured fields. 8.4 Canceled: operator/policy cancel on ctx. 8.5 Budget: pre-launch budget-exhausted (emits budget_exhausted, NOT agent_failed). 8.6 SkillProvisioningFailed: unresolvable per HC-048 OR resolved-but-structural-cause-failure per HC-048a. 8.7 ProtocolMismatch: no mutual version, handler_capabilities absent within 5s, NDJSON line cap exceeded. Routing of these classes to retry/re-plan/terminal owned by [execution-model.md §8].

title: harmonik run: hang on bead failure + always exits 0 + silent queue overwrite
description:
Reviewer BLOCK on commit 938459e (harmonik run <id>). Three issues:

1. **Hangs on bead failure.** daemon.Start blocks at <-loopDone forever because cancelOnQueueDrain is withheld on failure but nothing else cancels the context. Unusable in scripted/CI contexts.

2. **Exit-code always 0.** daemon.Start (internal/daemon/daemon.go:593-596) discards the workloop error and always returns nil. runBeadSubcommand therefore returns 0 regardless of bead outcome.

3. **Silent queue.json overwrite.** cmd/harmonik/run.go:164-170 calls queue.Persist with no prior queue.Load check. An existing active or paused-by-failure queue is silently overwritten, violating QM-027.

## Acceptance

- harmonik run <id> against a bead that the daemon FAILs returns non-zero exit AND does not hang.
- harmonik run <id> against a successful bead returns 0.
- harmonik run <id> when .harmonik/queue.json already has a non-completed queue refuses with a clear error and returns non-zero.
- All three scenarios have integration tests.

## Remediation sketch (per reviewer)

- daemon.go: expose workloop outcome via returned error or Config-provided channel.
- run.go: wire outcome signal; map paused-by-failure → exit 1; add cancelOnFailure hook to unblock the loop on failure.
- run.go:164: add queue.Load / refuse-if-active guard before Persist.
close_reason: Subsumed: landed as 1efde07

title: Per-bead branching config: base_branch + target_branch carrier and lifecycle
description:
## Problem

Today the daemon's worktree factory (internal/daemon/workloop.go productionWorktreeFactory → resolveHEAD) cuts the task branch from `git rev-parse HEAD` of the project repo. WM-005 spec text says "the branch is created off the current integration branch" but the wired path is HEAD-only and has no notion of either a *base* (origination) branch or a *target* (landing) branch per-bead.

In a team project this means agent commits effectively land relative to whatever main happens to be at daemon-start, and the operator has no per-bead lever to say "this work should start from integration/feature-x and land back on integration/feature-x." The user explicitly called this out as the blocker that drove them away from a prior orchestration tool: agents committing to main when the work belonged on an integration branch is unacceptable for team workflows.

## Two fields needed per bead

- `base_branch` — what to cut the worktree from. Default `main`.
- `target_branch` — where the squash-merge / cherry-pick lands. Default `main` (or `harmonik/integration` per current WM-008 default).

The DOT-graph contract is preserved: each node still commits to the task branch (`run/<run_id>`). Only the *base* of that task branch and the *destination* of its squash-merge change.

## Carrier options (decide as part of this epic)

(a) **Bead-body fields.** A structured `## Branching` section in the bead body (or the `agent-task.md` analog) parsed by the daemon at claim time. Pros: lives with the bead, survives JSONL roundtrip, reviewable in `br show`. Cons: parser surface; spec must pin the format.

(b) **Bead labels.** `base:main`, `target:integration/feature-x`. Pros: trivially queryable via `br list --label=...`, no new parser. Cons: labels can't carry refs with `/` cleanly without escape; label namespace pollution.

(c) **Per-project config file + per-bead override.** A `.harmonik/branching.yaml` (or equivalent) with project default base/target; bead overrides via (a) or (b). Pros: most projects want one answer for all beads; cuts boilerplate. Cons: two sources of truth to reconcile.

**Recommendation (provisional, to be confirmed by the epic):** (c) for project default + (a) for per-bead override. (b) is rejected because integration branch names commonly contain `/`.

## Scope of this epic (child beads)

1. Worktree-cut amendment: daemon honours `base_branch` from the bead (separate bead — impl).
2. Landing-strategy selector: merge vs cherry-pick, plus where it lands (separate bead — spec + impl).
3. Optional config-file scaffold for project defaults (separate bead — design).

## Key design tensions to resolve in the epic

- **Granularity of `target_branch`:** per-bead (most flexible, most config), per-DOT-graph (one target for one workflow shape), per-umbrella (one target for a feature's whole bead set — most aligned with "integration branch per feature"). Trade-off: per-umbrella is the cleanest mental model for team review, but harmonik doesn't currently have an umbrella primitive — features live in beads via dependency edges only. Surfacing an umbrella concept here may be premature; per-bead with a default inherited from the parent bead's `target_branch` is probably the right interim.

- **Mid-run rebase of base_branch:** when a sibling lands work on `base_branch` while a run is in flight, does harmonik fetch + rebase the task branch? Open: surface as a follow-up bead. Default for MVH-of-this-epic: no automatic rebase; conflict surfaces at merge-back per existing WM-024 conflict-resolution path.

- **Cherry-pick vs squash-merge at landing:** WM-019 currently mandates squash-merge. Cherry-pick is required when target_branch is shared by multiple sibling runs whose commits need to interleave. Selector probably belongs in the same per-bead carrier (`landing_strategy: squash|cherry-pick`).

- **Reviewer agent operates from implementer's worktree branch.** Today's reviewloop attaches to the same workspace per WM-010 lease-by-run rule. Should already be satisfied — confirm in the impl bead.

## Spec amendments anticipated

- workspace-model.md WM-005: task branch base is `base_branch` (from bead) not "the current integration branch" implicit.
- workspace-model.md WM-006/WM-008: target branch becomes per-bead `target_branch` with documented default.
- workspace-model.md WM-019: landing strategy becomes selectable (squash | cherry-pick).
- beads-integration.md: declare the `base_branch` / `target_branch` / `landing_strategy` field carrier and parse contract.

## Exit criteria

- Carrier decided and documented (config-file + bead-body override, per provisional rec).
- Daemon cuts worktree from `base_branch` (not raw HEAD).
- Landing-strategy selector wired or explicitly deferred with a follow-up bead.
- Spec amendments landed in workspace-model.md and beads-integration.md.
close_reason: All four scoped children landed: hk-ordd3 (spec WM-005b/019b/BI-009b), hk-zy9s3 (YAML loader), hk-oe6zt (factory honours start_from), hk-icgp1 (landing_strategy selector), hk-umxx4 (resolution-chain unification).
