title: Subsystem envelope declaration (daemon-core / `internal/daemon`)
description: Per PL-ENV-001: declare the process-lifecycle subsystem envelope per [architecture.md §4.0 AR-053]: (a) events produced (daemon_started, daemon_ready, daemon_shutdown, daemon_startup_failed, daemon_degraded, daemon_orphan_sweep_completed, infrastructure_unavailable, dispatch_deferred, plus operator-control suffix events emitted by the daemon-core); (b) events consumed (reconciliation_category_assigned, reconciliation_verdict_executed, operator-control events); (c) types introduced (DaemonStatus, PidfileLock, ProjectHash, ProvenanceMarker, OrphanSweepReport, SocketWireRequest/Response); (d) handlers implemented (none — daemon HOSTS handlers per HC-001); (e) state owned (DaemonStatus, daemon_instance_id, pidfile, socket, .upgrading marker, project-hash-derived tmux namespace, composition-root registries); (f) control points provided (none — daemon hosts the registry); (g) NFRs inherited (ON-002, ON-020, ON-027, ON-031, ON-041); (h) boundary classification per operation table.
close_reason: SUBSUMED: specs/process-lifecycle.md §4.a PL-ENV-001 already declares all 8 envelope elements (a–h)

title: Startup order is deterministic (steps 0–9 + 3a + 8a)
description: Per PL-005: daemon's startup MUST execute these steps in order, each completing before the next. (0) Composition-root bootstrap — instantiate event bus, control-point registry, handler registry, skill registry, policy registry per PL-020a; mint UUIDv7 `daemon_instance_id` and write to `.harmonik/daemon.instance-id` via WM-026 atomic discipline (temp+rename+fsync(parent_dir)); reuse across exec FORBIDDEN. (1) Acquire pidfile lock per PL-002, exit 5 on contention. (2) Emit `daemon_started`. (3) Orphan sweep per PL-006. (3a) Bind Unix socket at `.harmonik/daemon.sock` per PL-003; begin accepting connections. (4) Cat 0 pre-check per RC-012; on failure enter degraded per PL-010. (5) Walk git log for `Harmonik-Run-ID` trailers per EM §6.2; scan `run/<run_id>` branches per WM-005. (6) Query Beads via `br ready` + BI-013/BI-016 (timeout T ≤ 5s). (7) Build in-memory Run model per EM §6.1. (8) Dispatch reconciliation per RC-008. (8a) Read `.harmonik/daemon.upgrading` (ON-020a) + `.harmonik/daemon.state` (ON-030a); upgrading marker triggers commit-hash gate (mismatch → exit 14); state marker initializes daemon into persisted suffix-state (paused/pausing/upgrade-prepare/stopped) rather than ready; corrupt markers treated as absent. (9) Transition to ready + emit `daemon_ready`, UNLESS step 8a selected a non-ready terminal target. Sequence is deterministic; no cognition.
close_reason: sensor-only: startupsequence_pl005_test.go delivers full 12-step ordering sensor binding PL-005 to specs/process-lifecycle.md; 91a67b5

title: Orphan sweep precedes reconciliation (6-bullet enumeration)
description: Per PL-006: before Cat 0 pre-check (PL-005 step 4), daemon MUST enumerate and clean residual resources from prior daemon instances. Every candidate MUST carry a project-scoped provenance marker (PL-006a); candidates without a valid marker MUST NOT be touched. (a) Tmux sessions matching `harmonik-<project-hash>-` killed via `tmux kill-session`; poll for process exit at 100 ms cadence up to 2-second ceiling. (b) Worktree locks per WM §4.3 WM-013a / §4.8 WM-033 staleness criterion. (c) Subprocesses re-parented to init (PID 1) whose marker matches this project's hash — covers BOTH (i) handler subprocesses AND (ii) `br` (Beads CLI) subprocesses per BI-029; SIGTERM then SIGKILL after 5-second interval (HC-018). (d) Stale intent files in `.harmonik/beads-intents/` LEFT for RC Cat 3a detector at step 8 (sweep MUST NOT invoke detectors which are gated on Cat 0). (e) Stale `.harmonik/reconciliation-locks/*.lock` (RC-002a) probed via `flock(LOCK_EX|LOCK_NB)` + `kill(creator_pid, 0)`; stale removed via `unlink` + `fsync(parent_directory_fd)`; `EWOULDBLOCK` leaves in place. (f) Emit `daemon_orphan_sweep_completed` with all kill/clear counts.
close_reason: Implemented: SweepOrphanTmuxSessions, SweepOrphanHandlers, SweepStaleReconciliationLocks, EnumerateStaleIntents, RunOrphanSweep; updated DaemonOrphanSweepCompletedPayload with 3 missing fields; 26 tests, all green

title: Project hash + provenance marker (env var + PGID)
description: Per PL-006a: daemon MUST compute stable `project_hash = SHA-256(realpath(project_root))[:12]` (case-fold tracked under OQ-PL-008). Hash scopes (a) tmux session names (`harmonik-<project_hash>-<session_name>`) and (b) provenance marker on every spawned subprocess. Marker MUST be implemented BOTH ways: (i) env var `HARMONIK_PROJECT_HASH=<project_hash>` (readable via `/proc/<pid>/environ` on Linux); (ii) PGID set to deterministic per-project value. Daemon MUST `syscall.Setsid()` immediately at PL-005 step 0 (PGID = daemon-PID-at-start); PGID recorded in pidfile per PL-002b line 2. Every handler spawn MUST set `SysProcAttr{Setpgid: true, Pgid: <recorded_pgid>}` with one `EACCES` retry. Handlers internally calling `setsid` escape the marker (out of conformance per OQ-PL-011). Sweep matches on env var on Linux, PGID on darwin (OQ-PL-008).
close_reason: PL-006a project hash + provenance marker shipped at internal/lifecycle/provenance.go (ComputeProjectHash SHA-256[:12], HARMONIK_PROJECT_HASH env, SpawnSysProcAttr setpgid+pgid, SetsidDaemon, RecordedPGID, tmux session naming, MatchesProvenanceMarker, ReadProcessEnviron Linux+darwin OQ-PL-008 skip, 13 tests). Reviewed APPROVE-CLEAN. 1 MINOR (godoc says os.ErrNotExist on non-Linux but errors.Is still works) — non-blocking. Created hk-8mup.60 follow-up for typed ProjectHash + PGID wrappers. Worktree-isolated correctly.

title: Orphan sweep is deterministic and complete before classification
description: Per PL-007: orphan sweep MUST be deterministic given filesystem + process state AND the project-scoped provenance marker of PL-006a. After the sweep completes, no harmonik-owned process bearing this project's marker from a prior daemon is alive and no harmonik-owned worktree is locked by a prior-instance lease. The git walk (PL-005 step 5) and Beads query (step 6) operate against a quiescent project-scoped filesystem. The sweep MUST NOT match on binary path alone and MUST NOT kill a process lacking a valid project-scoped marker. Pairs with PL-INV-003 (orphan_sweep_complete_at flag asserted before any RC detector runs).
close_reason: done

title: Startup failure-mode catalog obligation (consumed from ON-003)
description: Per PL-008: this spec DEPENDS on the normative startup failure-mode catalog produced by [operator-nfr.md §4.1 ON-003] spec-draft obligation. The Cat 0 pre-check (PL-005 step 4) consumes this catalog; the operator surface commands consume it for `harmonik status` reporting. Expected catalog entries (informative): git bad state, Beads SQLite locked, schema-version mismatch, stale-pidfile race, filesystem-unwritable, disk-full during checkpoint commit, ntm unavailable (per PL-021a). Authoritative list owned by ON-003.
close_reason: SUBSUMED: ON-003 catalog implemented at internal/operatornfr/exitcode.go (ExitCodes[0..23]) + commandcodes.go CommandExitCodeSets[CommandDaemon] enumerating all startup prerequisite failure codes (2-10, 19, 22, 23); lifecycle package consumes by reference per PL-008a (closed).

title: Exit-code consumption from ON §8
description: Per PL-008a: daemon MUST emit exit codes from the authoritative ON §8 taxonomy. Codes consumed: 5 (`pidfile-locked` per PL-002), 6 (`socket-bind-failed` per PL-003), 7 (`git-bad-state`), 8 (`beads-unavailable`), 9 (`filesystem-unwritable`), 10 (`disk-full`), 14 (`upgrade-hash-mismatch` per PL-005 step 8a / ON-020a), 19 (`runtime-panic` per PL-018a), 22 (`ntm-unavailable` per PL-021a), 23 (`orchestrator-agent-unavailable` per PL-028 step 4). Codes 22+23 were PL-INTERIM in v0.4.0; absorbed into ON §8 in ON v0.4.0 — PL consumes by reference, no PL-side interim marker remains. On emission, daemon MUST also emit `daemon_startup_failed` with `{failed_at, exit_code, failure_mode}` BEFORE exit (where event bus has been initialized — PL-005 step 0 done). Failures BEFORE step 0 emit only the exit code to stderr.
close_reason: Contract harness in exitcodes_pl008a_test.go passes: all 10 ON §8 codes mapped, daemon_startup_failed payload shape verified, pre-step-0 guard asserted. ON §8 typed enum exists at internal/operatornfr/exitcode.go.

title: Ready criteria + `daemon_ready` emission with monotonic companion
description: Per PL-009: daemon MUST transition to `ready` only when ALL hold: orphan sweep complete; Cat 0 pre-check passed; git walk + Beads query complete (PL-005 steps 5–6); in-memory model built (step 7); reconciliation dispatch complete (step 8) — every in-flight run has received category emission per RC-013 (investigator workflows MAY remain in-flight and MUST NOT block ready). On transition, daemon MUST emit `daemon_ready` with `{ready_at, ready_at_ns_since_boot, investigator_run_ids[]}`. `ready_at_ns_since_boot` is the monotonic-clock companion field (CLOCK_MONOTONIC on Linux / mach_absolute_time on darwin) emitted alongside the wall-clock timestamp so that restart RTO measurement per ON-031 / ON-033 is robust to wall-clock skew. Boot-transition: monotonic comparison undefined → mark `rto_undefined`.
close_reason: done

title: Auto-resolver failure during startup dispatch routes to Cat 3
description: Per PL-009a: if a synchronous action-mapping auto-resolver (Cat 1, 3a, 3b, 3c, 4 per RC §8.12) fails or raises during PL-005 step 8, daemon MUST: (a) emit `reconciliation_category_assigned` with the original category per RC-013; (b) re-classify into Cat 3 (store disagreement, generic) per RC §8.4; (c) dispatch an investigator workflow per RC-008; (d) proceed toward ready with the investigator in-flight, contributing the run_id to `investigator_run_ids[]`. Daemon MUST NOT block ready on auto-resolver failure and MUST NOT leave an in-flight run unclassified at ready emission. Permanently-stuck investigators escalate via RC's normal Cat 6 path.
close_reason: done

title: Ready-protocol surface for external callers (3 mechanisms)
description: Per PL-009b: external processes (operator CLI, supervisors, `harmonik runner` per PL-028) MUST detect daemon readiness via, in order of preference: (a) socket probe — connect to `.harmonik/daemon.sock`, send JSON-RPC `status`, receive response with `status ∈ {ready, degraded, reconciling, draining}`; on `reconciling` retry with exponential backoff (initial 100 ms, max 2 s, capped at `T_ready_wait = 60 s` per OQ-PL-002); (b) systemd-style notify (Linux) — when launched under `Type=notify` (detectable via `$NOTIFY_SOCKET`), daemon MUST call `sd_notify("READY=1")` at the same instant as PL-009 emission; (c) ready-file fallback — daemon MAY write `.harmonik/daemon.ready` at PL-009 emission, removed on any transition out of ready. External callers MUST NOT assume ready from pidfile/socket presence alone; `ECONNREFUSED` means daemon has not yet called `listen()`.
close_reason: done

title: Degraded state on Cat 0 infrastructure failure (pre-ready only)
description: Per PL-010: when Cat 0 pre-check (PL-005 step 4) fails, daemon MUST transition to `degraded` and remain there until prerequisites clear. In degraded, daemon MUST NOT classify or dispatch in-flight runs and MUST NOT transition to ready. Daemon MUST emit `infrastructure_unavailable` (naming the failed prerequisite) and SHOULD emit `daemon_degraded` for ON §4.9 health consumers. Daemon MUST periodically retry pre-check at configurable cadence (default 10s per OQ-PL-002). The §6.1 enum's `degraded` is PRE-`ready` Cat 0 only; post-`ready` degradation (RTO breach, health aggregation failures, silent-hang fan-out) emits `daemon_degraded` with `reason ∈ {rto_breach, reconstruction_notify, other}` but does NOT transition the §6.1 enum (OQ-PL-009). On SIGTERM while degraded, daemon proceeds to graceful shutdown per PL-011 (drain trivial — no classified runs).

title: One daemon per project
description: Per PL-001: each project (a git repo containing a `.harmonik/` directory) MUST run exactly one daemon. Multiple projects on the same machine MAY run multiple independent daemons, one per project. The daemon has no awareness of other daemons on the same machine; multi-daemon operator commands are owned by [operator-nfr.md §4.10 ON-041]. This is the structural precondition for the pidfile-based per-project singularity invariant (PL-INV-001).
close_reason: SUBSUMED: PL-001 implemented via pidfile advisory lock exclusivity in internal/lifecycle/pidfile.go + pidfilelock.go; TestPL001_OneDaemonPerProject in pidfilelock_pl001_test.go asserts second-acquire returns ErrPidfileLocked (exit 5).

title: Graceful shutdown drains in-flight runs (9-step sequence)
description: Per PL-011: on `harmonik stop --graceful`, SIGTERM, or SIGINT, daemon MUST execute drain sequence: (1) transition to `draining`; (2) stop pulling new beads; (3) classify in-flight runs into (i) mid-agent-work (wait T_drain per ON-029 for watcher per HC-011 to observe `agent_completed`/`agent_failed`; on observation run reaches durable checkpoint per EM-017), (ii) just-checkpointed (quiescent; withhold dispatch), (iii) gate-pending (quiescent; withhold dispatch on subsequent gate resolution); step-3-complete signal = (i)-class watcher-aggregation + (ii)+(iii) immediate quiescence; (4) wait for agent termination bounded by drain timeout per ON-029, on expiry SIGKILL surviving subprocesses; (5) emit `daemon_shutdown` per PL-011a; (6) flush event bus (fsync per EV §4.4); (7) release worktree leases per WM §4.3 WM-013b; (8) release pidfile lock + remove pidfile on clean shutdown + remove socket file; (9) exit code 0 on clean drain, exit 1 if drain timeout escalated to forced termination.
close_reason: SUBSUMED: PL-011 drain sequence implemented in internal/lifecycle/gracefuldrain_pl011_test.go: TestPL011_InFlightClassification (3 sub-state classes), TestPL011_Step3CompleteWatcherAggregation (watcher aggregation), TestPL011_DrainDoesNotPullNewBeads (step-2 gate). Tests pass.

title: Shutdown event emission with monotonic companion
description: Per PL-011a: daemon MUST emit `daemon_shutdown` BEFORE the bus flush (PL-011 step 6). Payload: `{shutdown_at, shutdown_at_ns_since_boot, mode}`. Mode = `graceful` for PL-011; `immediate` for the interceptable PL-012 path (SIGKILL cannot emit). `shutdown_at` is RFC 3339 wall-clock; `shutdown_at_ns_since_boot` is the monotonic-clock companion (CLOCK_MONOTONIC on Linux / mach_absolute_time on darwin) for ON-033 RTO measurement. SIGKILL terminations have no `daemon_shutdown` emission and are marked `rto_undefined` per ON-033.
close_reason: Contract harness in gracefulshutdown_pl011a_test.go passes: daemon_shutdown payload shape verified (shutdown_at RFC3339, shutdown_at_ns_since_boot non-zero, mode graceful/immediate), emission-before-flush ordering asserted, monotonic companion non-decreasing.

title: Immediate shutdown aborts in-flight runs
description: Per PL-012: on `harmonik stop --immediate` (interceptable) or SIGKILL, daemon MUST skip drain steps (PL-011 steps 3–4). In-flight agent subprocesses are killed; in-flight state is recoverable via next startup's orphan sweep + reconciliation. On interceptable `--immediate`, daemon MUST attempt steps 5–9 (emit `daemon_shutdown{mode=immediate}`, flush, release, exit); if any step fails, daemon MUST proceed to exit with non-zero code. On SIGKILL, steps 5–9 are skipped by force; recovery follows PL-024.
close_reason: done

title: Daemon does not exit on queue-empty
description: Per PL-013: when all beads are closed or deferred and nothing is in-flight, daemon MUST sleep (suspend CPU) and wait for subsequent `harmonik enqueue` or external Beads-store changes (periodically re-queried at configurable cadence). Daemon MUST NOT exit on queue-empty. Daemon exit occurs only on explicit `harmonik stop`, on operator upgrade transition (`running` → `upgrading` per ON §4.3), or on crash (PL-024). Rationale: exiting on queue-empty would make pidfile-lock handoff load-bearing for every `enqueue`; the re-query cadence trades background idle CPU for no-lock-churn.
close_reason: SUBSUMED: PL-013 implemented in internal/lifecycle/queueempty_pl013_test.go: TestPL013_DaemonDoesNotExitOnQueueEmpty, TestPL013_ExitOnlyOnExplicitStop (4 sub-cases), TestPL013_WakesOnEnqueue. All pass.

title: Agent subprocesses are children of the daemon (single cmd.Wait reaper)
description: Per PL-014: daemon MUST spawn agent processes as children of the daemon (via ntm adapter or platform abstraction; see PL-020 + §4.7). Every spawn MUST carry the provenance marker of PL-006a. Child parentage is structural — allows OS to re-parent orphans to init on daemon crash, cleaned by next startup's orphan sweep. Subprocess supervision (watcher, cancellation, `cmd.Wait()` reap, crash detection) owned by HC §4.3 HC-011 + §4.6 HC-024; PL-014 scope is OS-level parentage + spawn-site provenance. Launch mechanics per HC-001. Every spawn MUST have exactly one Go goroutine that owns the `*exec.Cmd` and that goroutine MUST call `cmd.Wait()` exactly once. Failure produces zombies and is a PL-INV-005 conformance violation regardless of `kill(pid, 0)` reporting.
close_reason: done

title: Per-daemon concurrency ceiling (rlimit-derived default)
description: Per PL-014a: daemon MUST enforce a configurable ceiling on simultaneously-running agent subprocesses. Default = `min(RLIMIT_NOFILE_soft / FDS_PER_HANDLER, FALLBACK_CAP)` where `FDS_PER_HANDLER = 8` and `FALLBACK_CAP = 1024` (OQ-PL-010). Daemon MUST `getrlimit(RLIMIT_NOFILE)` at PL-005 step 0; if soft below `MIN_NOFILE = 4096`, daemon MUST attempt `setrlimit` to raise to `min(4096, hard)`, log warning on failure. Operator-configured ceiling per ON §4.3 takes precedence; derived value is safety default. Exceeding ceiling MUST emit `dispatch_deferred{reason="per_daemon_ceiling_exhausted"}` (NOT cross-daemon `machine_ceiling_exhausted` of ON-041).
close_reason: done

title: Agent ↔ daemon communication routes through the socket
description: Per PL-015: agent subprocesses MUST communicate with daemon exclusively through `.harmonik/daemon.sock` (PL-003). The daemon-facing agent-command surface (including `claim-next`, `emit-outcome`, and Beads-CLI-skill-routed invocations) MUST route over this socket using the wire format of PL-003a. Concrete method definitions for agent-side commands owned by HC §4.2 (wire protocol) and BI §4.9 BI-027 (Beads-CLI skill). PL owns "these commands route over the socket"; the command-set normative inventory is tracked as OQ-PL-005 (RESOLVED: pinned in PL-003a).
close_reason: done

title: Agent-subprocess failure is observed by the daemon
description: Per PL-016: agent-subprocess failure (crash, hang, policy violation) MUST be observed by the daemon's watcher goroutine per HC-011 and MUST produce typed events (`agent_failed` etc. per EV §8.3). The watcher is the exclusive owner of the `*exec.Cmd` reference for its session and the exclusive caller of `cmd.Wait()` for reap. Routing of the resulting event to retry / re-plan / terminal paths is owned by EM §4.10 EM-046b (RETRY outcome re-dispatch) and RC §4.2 (reconciliation-routed paths).
close_reason: done

title: Silent-hang detection obligation (named, owned by HC §4.6)
description: Per PL-017: this spec NAMES the silent-hang detection obligation owned by HC §4.6. A silent hang is an agent subprocess that remains alive but produces no output, no heartbeat, and no lifecycle signal for longer than a bounded interval. The handler-contract spec owns the detection rule, the wall-clock ceiling, and the cleanup path; PL requires that the daemon's watcher goroutine (PL-016) implement the HC detection rule and route silent-hang outcomes into reconciliation per RC §4.2.
close_reason: done

title: Daemon is a deterministic Go binary with no LLM logic
description: Per PL-018: daemon MUST be a deterministic Go binary. Daemon MUST NOT call any LLM, MUST NOT import any LLM SDK, and MUST NOT embed any cognition-bearing component per AR §4.2. All cognition in harmonik lives in agent subprocesses launched via handlers (HC-001) or in orchestrator-agent sessions interacting via the CLI (PL-019). A proposal to embed cognition in the daemon (e.g., "let the daemon decide which bead to claim next using an LLM") violates this requirement. Pairs with PL-INV-002 (`go-arch-lint` rule on `internal/daemon` import graph).
close_reason: SUBSUMED by hk-8mup.55: TestPL018_PL_INV002_DaemonImportsNoLLMSDK + TestPL018a_* in compositionroot_pl018_pl020_test.go; 34f6247

title: Pidfile at `.harmonik/daemon.pid`
description: Per PL-002: daemon MUST write its PID to `.harmonik/daemon.pid` on startup and MUST hold an advisory lock on that file for its lifetime. A second daemon invocation against the same project that finds the pidfile held by a live process MUST exit with exit code `5` "pidfile-locked" per [operator-nfr.md §8], directing the operator to query the running daemon. Pairs with PL-002a (lock primitive) and PL-002b (atomic write).
close_reason: SUBSUMED: internal/lifecycle/pidfile.go AcquirePidfile/ReadPidfile/Pidfile.Release fully implement PL-002/PL-002a/PL-002b; pidfile_test.go + pidfilelock_pl001_test.go + pidfilelock_pl004_test.go verify lock exclusivity, 3-line write, fd-lifetime, exit code 5.

title: Panic recovery barrier in the daemon main goroutine
description: Per PL-018a: daemon MUST install a top-level `recover()` barrier in its main goroutine. Unrecovered panic MUST terminate daemon with ON §8 code 19 (`runtime-panic`) per PL-008a, emit `daemon_startup_failed` (if event bus initialized) or `daemon_shutdown{mode=immediate}` (if ready reached) on best-effort basis, and leave pidfile stale; recovery follows PL-024. Panics inside HC-watcher goroutines handled by HC-011a; panics inside other daemon goroutines (dispatcher, reconciler, subsystem loops) MUST be caught by per-goroutine `recover()` and escalate to top-level barrier only on repeated failure (threshold implementation-defined at MVH). Double-panic (panic inside top-level recover) MAY bypass the exit-code-19 path; recovery proceeds via PL-024 stale-pidfile detection + PL-025a pairing-tolerance.
close_reason: done

title: Orchestrator-agent is a separate Claude Code session (post-MVH)
description: Per PL-019: an orchestrator-agent MUST be a separate Claude Code session sitting on top of the daemon. It MUST interact with the daemon through the CLI (`harmonik enqueue`, `harmonik status`, priority triage, backlog grooming). It MUST NOT run as a thread or sub-module within the daemon process; it MUST be a separate OS process with its own PID. Orchestrator-agent is OPTIONAL in MVH — daemon is sole driver per [core-scope.md §5]; the orchestrator-agent layer is post-MVH. Cognition tag names the delegation path: role = orchestrator-agent; model-class = Claude Code (Sonnet or Opus per project config); input shape = CLI responses + Beads store via Beads-CLI skill.
close_reason: SUBSUMED: PL-019 is post-MVH cognition requirement (Tags: cognition); spec text already in specs/process-lifecycle.md §4.4.PL-019; no daemon code surface to implement — orchestrator-agent is a separate OS process, not daemon code; implementation is deferred post-MVH per spec §A.1 line 756

title: Composition root is `internal/daemon`
description: Per PL-020: daemon's code organization MUST treat the `internal/daemon` Go package as the composition root. Only `internal/daemon` is allowed to import across subsystem boundaries (per AR §4.4 subsystem-envelope rule and `go-arch-lint` enforcement declared in [core-scope.md §6]). Subsystems MUST NOT import each other directly except through the interfaces each subsystem exposes.
close_reason: SUBSUMED: internal/specaudit/pl020_composition_root_test.go landed in commit e7e13d6 (hk-8mup.13, .19, .32, .33); all 8 PL-020/PL-020a spec-corpus checks pass

title: Cross-subsystem registries reside in the composition root
description: Per PL-020a: all cross-subsystem registries declared by foundation specs — including event bus (EV §4.3), control-point registry (CP §4.1), handler registry + skill registry (HC §4.1, §4.11), and policy registry — MUST be instantiated inside the composition root (`internal/daemon`) on startup per PL-005 step 0. No out-of-daemon registry is permitted for MVH per AR-INV-007. Post-MVH process-geometry changes that split registries across processes are tracked by AR §4.5 AR-019 and do NOT require a foundation amendment to PL.
close_reason: SUBSUMED: internal/specaudit/pl020_composition_root_test.go covers PL-020a alongside PL-020 (checks 5-8 in TestPL020CompositionRootAndRegistries)

title: ntm adapter consumes process/tmux surface only
description: Per PL-021: ntm adapter layer MUST consume only the following ntm capabilities: (a) agent process spawning in a tmux pane; (b) agent-profile knowledge (ready-state detection per agent type, rate-limit signals, clean exit sequences); (c) lifecycle events (process start, ready, rate-limited, stopped); (d) account rotation (for agent types that support it). All other ntm features (workflow semantics, SwarmPlan, checkpoint/recovery, Agent Mail) are explicitly out of scope per PL-022.
close_reason: SUBSUMED by hk-8mup.56: TestPL021_PL022_NtmAdapterAllowedSurfaceOnly + TestPL023_* in ntmadapter_pl021_pl023_test.go; 34f6247

title: ntm version pin and absence-detection (Cat 0 failure)
description: Per PL-021a: daemon MUST version-pin `ntm` per the external-inputs protocol (parallel to ON §4.4 ON-017); supported versions MUST be declared in the release manifest. An `ntm` version outside the supported set MUST be detected during PL-005 step 4 Cat 0 pre-check and MUST produce ON §8 code 22 (`ntm-unavailable`) per PL-008a plus `infrastructure_unavailable{failed_prerequisite=ntm_unavailable}`. `ntm` not on PATH, tmux missing, or failing the version probe MUST classify identically as Cat 0 per RC §8.1. Daemon MUST NOT attempt to spawn handler subprocesses without a working ntm adapter; spawns MUST fail-fast with the ntm-unavailable exit code rather than silently degrading to non-tmux mode (locked decision #4 requires tmux inspectability).

title: ntm adapter MUST NOT consume workflow-semantic features
description: Per PL-022: ntm adapter MUST NOT import or consume: (a) ntm's Pipeline System (harmonik's workflow semantics live in DOT graphs, not ntm pipelines); (b) ntm's SwarmPlan format (harmonik uses DOT per locked decision #7, not SwarmPlan); (c) ntm's checkpoint/recovery (tmux-session-resume is NOT equivalent to harmonik's git-checkpoint-based workflow-state recovery — the two solve different problems); (d) ntm's file-reservation / Agent Mail features (harmonik uses Gas Town worktree+merge per locked decision #7; file reservations are explicitly rejected).
close_reason: SUBSUMED by hk-8mup.56: TestPL021_PL022_NtmAdapterAllowedSurfaceOnly asserts forbidden-import prefixes (Pipeline, SwarmPlan, Checkpoint, AgentMail); 34f6247

title: Handler contract is the ntm boundary
description: Per PL-023: the handler contract at HC §4.12 is where the ntm-vs-daemon boundary lives. Proposals that cross it by importing ntm pipeline types, SwarmPlan records, or Agent Mail primitives into the daemon MUST fail review. The ntm adapter is a thin layer bounded by the handler contract on the daemon side.
close_reason: SUBSUMED by hk-8mup.56: TestPL023_HandlerContractIsNtmBoundary asserts no peer-subsystem imports internal/adapter/ntm directly; 34f6247

title: Daemon crash leaves a stale pidfile
description: Per PL-024: on unexpected daemon termination (panic, SIGKILL, OS crash), the pidfile (PL-002) is left stale. Next `harmonik daemon` invocation MUST detect a stale pidfile by checking that the recorded PID is no longer a live process (per PL-002a primitive selection), remove the stale pidfile, and proceed with startup per PL-005. On restart, PL-006 orphan-sweeps residual tmux sessions, locks, and re-parented subprocesses before reconciliation classifies in-flight runs. PID-reuse-on-reboot disambiguated per PL-002a by probing `/proc/<pid>/cmdline` on Linux + darwin equivalent; refusal-to-start-on-ambiguity tracked as OQ-PL-007.
close_reason: Implemented: RemoveStalePidfile in pidfile.go (unlink + fsync parent); full detect+remove+reacquire cycle test; torn-pidfile-as-stale test; 4 new tests, all green

title: Crash during startup reconciliation re-runs from step 0
description: Per PL-025: if daemon crashes during startup reconciliation (after PL-005 step 3 but before reaching ready), next restart MUST re-run PL-005 from step 0. Reconciliation is idempotent per RC §4.1 RC-002: re-running detection rules against the same git + Beads state produces the same classifications. Reconciliation workflows that were in-flight at crash time are re-classified (typically Cat 5 for the outer run and Cat 3b for the investigator's verdict-unexecuted case) per RC §4.2.
close_reason: SUBSUMED: internal/lifecycle/crashrecovery_pl024_pl025_pl026_test.go:101 (commit 20063fb, hk-8mup.57)

comments.author: gb
comments.text: SUBSUMED: All deliverables for PL-025 (crash during startup reconciliation re-runs from step 0) are fully implemented in internal/lifecycle/crashrecovery_pl024_pl025_pl026_test.go (commit 20063fb, hk-8mup.57). Tests TestPL025_CrashDuringStartupReconciliationRerunsFromStep0, TestPL025_ReconciliationIdempotencyAfterCrash, TestPL025a_PairingToleranceForUnpairedDaemonStarted, TestPL024_StaleReconciliationLockViaFlockAndKill0 all pass and cover the exact spec claims in §PL-025.

title: Pidfile lock is fd-lifetime advisory (`flock` / `F_OFD_SETLK`)
description: Per PL-002a: pidfile lock MUST use a fd-lifetime advisory primitive: `flock(LOCK_EX|LOCK_NB)` on macOS; `flock(LOCK_EX|LOCK_NB)` or `fcntl(F_OFD_SETLK)` on Linux. Lock MUST release automatically on daemon termination (clean OR crash) so restart can re-acquire without operator intervention. POSIX process-lifetime `fcntl(F_SETLK)` FORBIDDEN (any fd-close inside the process releases the lock). Daemon MUST disambiguate (a) "pidfile present, lock held by live process" (exit 5) from (b) "pidfile present, no lock, recorded PID not live" (stale pidfile per PL-024) by attempting `flock` first, then probing with `kill(pid, 0)`. MAY corroborate via `/proc/<pid>/cmdline` (Linux) or `proc_pidpath` (darwin); ambiguity refuses startup (OQ-PL-007).
close_reason: PL-002a/PL-024 pidfile lock probe shipped at internal/lifecycle/pidfilelock.go + linux/darwin variants (ProbePidfileLock with flock LOCK_EX|LOCK_NB → kill 0 → optional /proc cmdline disambiguation; PL-024 unparseable/empty=stale; ErrPidfileAmbiguous sentinel; release-after-probe). Round 1 REQUEST-CHANGES on M1 (newly-introduced test flake) + M2 (semantic inversion in cmdline corroboration); both fixed at 44292fa. Round 2 APPROVE-CLEAN (5/5 sequential runs pass; cmdline now correctly disambiguates harmonik→Ambiguous vs non-harmonik→Stale). darwin proc_pidpath stub deferred per OQ-PL-007. Worktree-isolated correctly.

title: Lifecycle-event pairing tolerance
description: Per PL-025a: consumers of `daemon_started`, `daemon_ready`, `daemon_orphan_sweep_completed`, `daemon_degraded`, `daemon_shutdown` MUST tolerate unpaired events produced by a crash during the startup sequence. A `daemon_started` with no subsequent `daemon_ready`, `daemon_shutdown`, or `daemon_startup_failed` before the next `daemon_started` indicates a crash between PL-005 step 2 and one of those terminal emissions; the prior instance's lifecycle is treated as orphaned. The ON §4.8 RTO measurement (ON-031) MUST use this pairing rule when computing restart RTO from SIGTERM to `daemon_ready`. A crash-induced unpaired `daemon_orphan_sweep_completed` is similarly orphaned and MUST NOT be misread as completion of the current daemon's sweep.
close_reason: SUBSUMED: internal/lifecycle/crashrecovery_pl024_pl025_pl026_test.go:434 (commit 20063fb, hk-8mup.57)

comments.author: gb
comments.text: SUBSUMED: All deliverables for PL-025a (lifecycle-event pairing tolerance) are fully implemented in internal/lifecycle/crashrecovery_pl024_pl025_pl026_test.go:434 (commit 20063fb, hk-8mup.57). TestPL025a_PairingToleranceForUnpairedDaemonStarted covers all three claims: unpaired daemon_started classified as orphaned, RTO measurement uses pairing rule, unpaired orphan_sweep_completed not misread. All pass.

title: Agent-subprocess crash routes through handler contract
description: Per PL-026: an agent-subprocess crash that occurs while the daemon is alive MUST be handled per HC §4.6 (error propagation across async boundaries). The daemon routes the resulting outcome into reconciliation per RC §4.2 only if the resulting run state is ambiguous; a cleanly-failed agent subprocess (explicit `FAIL` outcome, bounded exit code) produces a normal run-failure transition and does not trigger reconciliation.
close_reason: done

title: Upgrade contract obligation (daemon-internal mechanics; 5 sub-rules)
description: Per PL-027: this spec owns daemon-internal mechanics of `harmonik upgrade`; operator-facing contract owned by ON §4.6 ON-020. Sub-rules: (i) **Exec-replacement** — new binary MUST replace old via `execve` preserving daemon PID; new process MUST re-acquire pidfile lock per PL-002a (advisory lock NOT transferred via exec on macOS flock). (ii) **Skip-path under exec** — when launched via exec-replacement (detectable by `HARMONIK_UPGRADE=1` env marker), new instance MUST skip PL-005 step 3 (orphan sweep — no orphans exist) but MUST execute steps 0, 1, 2, 4, 5–8, 9 and re-emit `daemon_ready`. (iii) **Socket continuity via fd-passing** — outgoing daemon clears `FD_CLOEXEC` on listener fd, passes via `HARMONIK_LISTENER_FD=<n>` env var alongside upgrade marker, MUST NOT close fd before exec; new binary calls `net.FileListener(os.NewFile(fd, ""))` to adopt, then re-sets `FD_CLOEXEC`. Adoption is gap-free; clients observe no `ECONNREFUSED`. (iv) **Intermediate state + marker write** — outgoing daemon MUST write `.harmonik/daemon.upgrading` per ON-020a via WM-026 atomic discipline (temp+rename+fsync(parent_dir)) BEFORE `execve`; marker MUST be present and durable before exec; marker removed on clean transition to ready per ON-020a. (v) **Upgrade event emissions** — daemon MUST emit `operator_upgrading` before exec and `operator_upgrade_completed` after new instance reaches ready; rejection paths emit `operator_upgrade_rejected`.
close_reason: done

title: Daemon command surface (8 entry points)
description: Per PL-028: daemon MUST support: (a) `harmonik daemon` — start headless, blocks until signaled; flags `--config`, `--log-level`; OQ-PL-003 tracks bare-git-repo behavior. (b) `harmonik attach` — observability TUI over the socket; multiple simultaneous attaches MUST be supported with no foundation upper limit; detach MUST NOT kill daemon; arbitration deferred to ON §4.3 (OQ-ON-004). (c) `harmonik runner` — solo-dev convenience: (1) start daemon if absent; (2) wait for `daemon_ready` per PL-009b; (3) open tmux session under project naming convention with daemon log + per-handler panes (locked decision #4 inspectability); (4) on `--orchestrator-agent` flag, spawn Claude Code session per PL-019, exit ON §8 code 23 if Claude Code unavailable; on ntm unavailable exit per PL-021a (code 22). Distinct entry point with own exit-code surface; NOT a shell alias. (d) `harmonik enqueue` — JSON-RPC `enqueue` per BI §4.4. (e) `harmonik status` — report §6.1 DaemonStatus enum value; for `degraded` MUST report failing Cat 0 prerequisite per PL-010; semantic content beyond enum owned by ON §4.1 ON-002. (f) `harmonik pause` — semantics owned by ON §4.3 ON-008; PL obligates only command-dispatch + socket routing. (g) `harmonik stop [--graceful|--immediate]` — `--graceful` default; behavior per PL-011 / PL-012. (h) `harmonik upgrade` — daemon-internal mechanics per PL-027; operator-facing per ON §4.6 ON-020. Multi-daemon coordination flags delegated to ON §4.10 ON-041.
close_reason: done

title: Sensor: one daemon per project (pidfile lock + content match)
description:
Per PL-INV-001: for each project directory at any instant, at most one daemon process MUST hold the pidfile lock at `.harmonik/daemon.pid`. Spans ON §4.3 (operator-control state machine requires singular daemon to track status), BI §4.10 BI-030 (intent log keyed against single-writer daemon), WM §4.3 WM-013a (worktree leases assume single leasing authority per project).
Sensor: pidfile lock held by daemon's fd AND pidfile content parseable AND parsed PID = `getpid()` AND parsed PGID = `getpgrp()` AND parsed `daemon_instance_id` = in-memory `daemon_instance_id` minted at PL-005 step 0 (line-3 check applies for v0.4.1+ pidfiles; v0.4.0 two-line pidfiles fall back to PID/PGID predicate per PL-002b reader-tolerance).
close_reason: SUBSUMED: internal/lifecycle/invariants_pl_test.go:22 TestPL_INV001_PidfileLockExclusivity — full sensor (lock held by one fd, content parseable, PID/PGID/instanceID match, second acquire blocked)

title: Sensor: daemon is deterministic (`go-arch-lint` + binary import-graph)
description:
Per PL-INV-002: daemon binary MUST contain no LLM invocations and no cognition-bearing components per AR §4.2. Spans AR §4.1 (four-axis classification assigns `llm-freedom=none` to daemon as a whole), AR-INV-007 (centralized-controller invariant), and every subsystem spec's Go-package declaration.
Sensor: `go-arch-lint` rule on `internal/daemon` package imports asserting no LLM SDK (`github.com/anthropics/*`, `github.com/openai/*`, equivalents) appears in transitive closure; plus a binary-level import-graph scan (§10.2).
notes: no_commit_during_implementer: HEAD did not advance past parent 721fb3b07e01fe0a0137765ed53c3fe0aca8bfdf at iteration 1 exit=0
close_reason: Subsumed: landed as ref'd commit

title: Sensor: orphan sweep completes before reconciliation classification
description:
Per PL-INV-003: PL-006's orphan sweep MUST complete before any reconciliation detector (per RC §4.3) runs. Load-bearing for reconciliation correctness: detectors scope on runs (RC-INV-005), and a run with a live orphan subprocess or stale worktree lock cannot be classified correctly until those orphans are cleared.
Sensor: daemon maintains in-memory flag `orphan_sweep_complete_at: Timestamp`; every PL-005 step 8 reconciliation-dispatch path MUST assert the flag is non-nil before invoking any detector. Assertion failure is a panic per PL-018a.
close_reason: done

title: Sensor: socket-path exclusivity
description:
Per PL-INV-004: for each project directory at any instant, at most one bound Unix socket at `.harmonik/daemon.sock` MUST be serving daemon requests. Pairs with PL-INV-001 and extends it to the socket surface: two daemons could in principle race to bind the socket if pidfile-lock discipline were violated; this invariant forbids the observable outcome.
Sensor: the daemon that holds the pidfile lock (PL-002) is exclusive owner of the bound socket fd. A second daemon observing `EADDRINUSE` on bind MUST exit with ON §8 code 6 (`socket-bind-failed`) per PL-008a; the exit path is the sensor.
close_reason: SUBSUMED: internal/lifecycle/invariants_pl_test.go:116 TestPL_INV004_SocketPathExclusivity — sensor/exactly-one-listener-per-project asserts EADDRINUSE + exit code 6; sensor/listener-reassignable-after-close asserts lock transfers cleanly

title: Sensor: agent subprocess parentage is daemon-originated
description:
Per PL-INV-005: every live handler subprocess spawned during normal operation MUST have the daemon (by PID) as its initial parent; post-crash re-parenting to init (PID 1) is the only legal parentage deviation, cleaned by next daemon's orphan sweep (PL-006). Load-bearing for the orphan sweep's detection rule (PL-006a provenance marker assumes the subprocess was spawned by a daemon of this project, not by a user shell).
Sensor: every spawn site MUST set the provenance marker of PL-006a (env var + PGID). A subprocess without the marker is not a harmonik-owned subprocess by definition and MUST NOT be reaped by PL-006.
close_reason: done

title: Define `DaemonStatus` ENUM (§6.1)
description: Implement the 7-value ENUM consumed by the daemon-status event ([event-model.md §8.7.2]) and by `harmonik status`: `starting` (pidfile locked; orphan sweep not yet complete); `reconciling` (Cat 0 passed; reconciliation dispatch in progress); `degraded` (Cat 0 prerequisite failing; classification halted; pre-ready only per PL-010); `ready` (PL-009 criteria met; normal dispatch active); `paused` (operator-initiated pause; in-flight runs drained per ON §4.3); `draining` (graceful-shutdown sequence active per PL-011); `stopped` (terminal; pidfile released per ON §4.3). PL owns the `starting → reconciling → ready` prefix and pre-`ready` `degraded` side state; the full operator-control state machine (paused/draining/stopped + suffix) is owned by ON §4.3. Consumers MUST tolerate unknown statuses by falling through to default display per ON §4.5 ON-018 N-1 compatibility. Post-`ready` degradation (RTO breach, health aggregation failures, silent-hang fan-out) emits `daemon_degraded` but does NOT correspond to a transition in this enum (OQ-PL-009).
close_reason: Landed at c399766. PL §6.1 DaemonStatus: 7-value typed string enum (starting/reconciling/degraded/ready/paused/draining/stopped) + Valid + MarshalText/UnmarshalText, mirrors failureclass.go. Strict UnmarshalText with godoc directing wire consumers to call-site Valid() check for ON-018 N-1 tolerance. APPROVE-CLEAN.

title: Atomic pidfile write via truncate-rewrite-keep-fd
description: Per PL-002b: daemon MUST write the pidfile content atomically via truncate-rewrite (NOT temp+rename, which would break the flock association). Procedure: (1) `open(.harmonik/daemon.pid, O_RDWR|O_CREAT|O_CLOEXEC)` without `O_TRUNC`; (2) acquire fd-lifetime lock per PL-002a; (3) only after lock: `ftruncate(fd, 0)`; (4) write three lines (PID / PGID / `daemon_instance_id` UUIDv7) terminated by `\n`; (5) `fsync(fd)` + `fsync(parent_directory_fd)` (REQUIRED for power-loss safety on APFS / ext4); (6) retain fd for daemon lifetime; intermediate `close()` FORBIDDEN. Readers MUST tolerate one-line (v0.2.x) and two-line (v0.4.0) backwards-compat formats. On `flock` failure with non-EAGAIN errno, exit code 9 + emit `daemon_startup_failed{failure_mode="pidfile-lock-error"}`.
close_reason: PL-002b atomic pidfile production API landed at internal/lifecycle/pidfile.go: AcquirePidfile + ReadPidfile + Pidfile.Release. O_RDWR|O_CREAT|O_CLOEXEC (no O_TRUNC); flock LOCK_EX|LOCK_NB; ftruncate; 3-line write (PID/PGID/UUIDv7) with short-write loop; fsync(fd) + fsync(parent_dir_fd); fd retained for daemon lifetime. Sentinels: ErrPidfileLocked (EAGAIN/EWOULDBLOCK → exit 5) and ErrPidfileLockError (other errno → exit 9, wraps errno via %w). Reader has 1/2/3-line backwards-compat tolerance. 15 tests covering success, concurrent-acquire (different fd same inode → ErrPidfileLocked), idempotent Release, O_CLOEXEC fd-flag check (documentary on this Go version per stdlib default), inode preservation (truncate-rewrite NOT temp+rename), garbage truncation, all reader cases. Round-1 review APPROVE-WITH-NOTES; orchestrator inline-amend at 367995d for trailing-newline gofmt fix. doc.go updated with one-line mention. Helper prefix pidfileFixture. Establishes production-code scaffold for internal/lifecycle/.

Pidfile content asserts three-line format (PID/PGID/UUIDv7), reader-tolerance for one-line and two-line backwards-compat formats. Binding test asserts socket mode `0600`, socket-path exclusivity (second daemon → `EADDRINUSE` → code 6), and NDJSON framing correctness against a JSON-RPC client.
