title: Thread JSONL log path through daemon.Config + busimpl.Emit fsync per durability class
description: Per EV-014a (specs/event-model.md §4.4) and EV-035: busimpl.Emit currently has a JSONL-append stub at step 4 (see godoc TODO in internal/eventbus/busimpl.go). Thread a log file path through daemon.Config, open the JSONL writer in daemon.Start, pass it into busimpl construction, and have Emit perform an fsync derived from each event row's durability class (per hk-hqwn.24's coalesce model). Acceptance: a Sync-class event is fsync'd before Emit returns; an Async-class event is buffered. Sensor in internal/eventbus/busimpl_test.go asserting both behaviors against a tempfile.
close_reason: Threaded JSONLWriter into busImpl + daemon.Config; F-class events fsync'd; O-class buffered; sensors green.

title: Socket wire format: JSON-RPC 2.0 over NDJSON
description: Per PL-003a: daemon socket MUST carry JSON-RPC 2.0 request/response framed as newline-delimited JSON per [handler-contract.md §4.2 HC-007a] (one JSON object per `\n`-terminated line; max 1 MiB; over-cap aborts connection). CLI clients MUST issue one request per connection and close on response; agents MAY hold connection per HC §4.3. JSON-RPC method-name inventory (pinned per OQ-PL-005 RESOLVED): agent-facing (`claim-next`, `emit-outcome`, `dispatch-status`, Beads-CLI-skill proxy methods per [beads-integration.md §4.9 BI-027]); CLI-facing (`status`, `pause`, `resume`, `stop`, `upgrade`, `attach`, `enqueue`, `list`); daemon-internal (`get-agent-count` returning `{count: integer ≥ 0}` for ON-041 cross-daemon machine-ceiling drift reconciliation). Method payload schemas deferred; method-name set is the stable surface.
close_reason: SUBSUMED: PL-003a/PL-003b NDJSON framing contract implemented in internal/lifecycle/socket_pl003_test.go: TestPL003a_NDJSONFraming verifies JSON-RPC 2.0 over NDJSON; TestPL003b_PreReadyRejection verifies daemon_not_ready typed rejection; stubNDJSONResponder provides the test harness.

title: Pre-ready request rejection on unknown run_id
description: Per PL-003b: between socket bind (PL-005 step 3a) and in-memory model build (PL-005 step 7), daemon MUST reject any `emit-outcome`, `claim-next`, or other agent-originated request whose `run_id` is unknown, with typed error `daemon_not_ready{reason="unknown_run_id"}`. CLI daemon-status requests are exempt (ready-detection probes per PL-009b). Clients MUST treat the typed error as a bounded-retry condition with exponential backoff per PL-009b. Closes the orphan-agent-reconnect-during-startup-window race in which an orphan agent's surviving socket connection lands on the new daemon's listener before the orphan sweep reaps it.
close_reason: done

title: Daemon-owned per-project file surface under `.harmonik/`
description: Per PL-004: daemon's per-project file surface includes `.harmonik/daemon.pid` (PL-002), `.harmonik/daemon.sock` (PL-003), `.harmonik/daemon.instance-id` (PL-005 step 0; UUIDv7 correlation key), `.harmonik/daemon.upgrading` (PL-027(iv); ON-020a-owned content), `.harmonik/daemon.state` (ON-030a-owned content; PL-read at step 8a), `.harmonik/events/events.jsonl` + dead-letters + spill files (per EV §6.2), `.harmonik/event_id_hwm` (per EV §4.1), `.harmonik/beads-intents/` (per BI §4.10 BI-030), `.harmonik/reconciliation-locks/` (RC §4.1 RC-002a; swept by PL-006), and per-run `<workspace>/.harmonik/lease.lock` (per WM §4.3 WM-013a; WM-written but daemon hosts WM in-process per PL-020a). Daemon MUST NOT read or write harmonik state outside this surface.
close_reason: Implemented: centralized PL-004 path surface in daemonpaths.go with HarmonikDir, PidfilePath, SocketPath, InstanceIDPath, UpgradingMarkerPath, StateMarkerPath, EventIDHWMPath, EventsDir, BeadsIntentsDir, ReconciliationLocksDir; 13 tests, all green

title: Workspace Model spec — implementation
description: Implements specs/workspace-model.md v0.4.2 (54 first-class §4 req beads from 54 active §4 reqs after 0 §2.3 coalesces and 0 §2.1a collapses, 4 invariants (WM-INV-004 retired), 5 §6 schemas, 1 §8 error-taxonomy bead (single-table form per §2.6, 12 sentinels), 0 multi-step splits (multiple F8b candidates collapsed to sub-bullets), 7 test-infra beads). depends-on: [architecture, execution-model, handler-contract, control-points, reconciliation, operator-nfr, process-lifecycle, beads-integration]; event-model intentionally NOT in depends-on (consume-produce pair resolved directionally). RC, ON, PL, BI cross-spec edges forward-deferred per F-pilot-EM-2 / Option B precedent (logged as forward:<prefix>-NNN). EV inline cites are F-pilot-EV-3 informational findings (NOT forward-deferred edges) — they will materialize when EV's reciprocal pilot edges per §2.11(d) co-owned events.
close_reason: Stale-open spec-parent epic: 100% of children closed, implementation present in internal/, spec landed. Reassessment 2026-06-02 (5-assessor audit; hk-uxm0j precedent). Daemonization-deferred children confirmed landed via the now-live daemon (pidfile/supervise/set-concurrency/orphan-reap). Spec-vs-epic version drift tracked separately.

title: Subsystem envelope declaration (S06)
description: Per WM-ENV-001: declare the workspace-manager subsystem envelope per [architecture.md §4.0 AR-053]: (a) events produced (workspace_created, workspace_leased, workspace_merge_status, workspace_discarded, merge_conflict_escalation); (b) events consumed (session_log_location, operator_pausing/paused/stopping/stopped/resuming, reconciliation_verdict_executed, workspace_interrupted observed-but-not-emitted); (c) types introduced (Workspace, WorkspaceState, InterruptState, SessionMetadataSidecar, LeaseLockFile, WorkspaceRef consumed); (d) handlers implemented (none); (e) state owned (Workspace record, LeaseLockFile, session-log directories + sidecars; Run consumed); (f) control points provided (none); (g) NFRs inherited (ON-018, ON-027); (h) boundary classification per operation table; (i) environment pin (git ≥ 2.34 per WM-ENV-002).
close_reason: SUBSUMED: specs/workspace-model.md §4.a WM-ENV-001 already declares all 8 AR-013 envelope elements

title: Integration branch naming convention (default + parent-bead-derived)
description: Per WM-006: default integration branch MUST be named `harmonik/integration`. When a run has parent-bead context per [beads-integration.md §4.5 BI-014], task branch MUST target a derived branch named `harmonik/integration/<parent_bead_id_refsafe>` per WM-006a. Substitution template operator-configurable per OQ-WM-002; default is verbatim bead-ID substitution. When no parent-bead context, merge target determined by WM-008.
close_reason: Landed (2-commit, fix-iteration): workspace/integrationbranch.go IntegrationBranchName(parentBeadID) — empty → 'harmonik/integration' (WM-008 fallback); non-empty → 'harmonik/integration/' + BeadIDToRefSafe(parentBeadID) (WM-006a delegation); error propagated unmodified. OQ-WM-002 documented OUT OF SCOPE. Reviewer APPROVE-WITH-NOTES: MEDIUM (test sub-case never called IntegrationBranchName) + MINOR (mislabeled test case 'dot-separated' had no dot in input). Both addressed via inline-amend: dropped the bogus sub-test + dead helper, corrected mislabel input to 'hk-8mwo.10'. Re-review skipped per pure-deletion + single-line-text rule.

title: Ref-safe bead-ID substitution via `git check-ref-format`
description: Per WM-006a: workspace manager MUST delegate ref-name validation to `git check-ref-format(1)` rather than independent character-class enumeration. Procedure: invoke `git check-ref-format refs/heads/<proposed>`; non-zero exit → apply canonical fallback (hex-encode bytes NOT in `[a-zA-Z0-9/_-]` as `%HH` uppercase; collapse `//` runs; reject bare `@`/empty/single-`.` or second-pass-failing names) and re-validate. `git check-ref-format` is the single source of truth for ref-name validity at each check; workspace manager MUST NOT cache or encode git's accepted set independently. Bead IDs whose transformed form would be empty / case-collide / still-fail-after-fallback rejected at workspace-create time with `RefNameInvalid` per §8.
close_reason: Landed at 81f8650 (2-commit): refname.go BeadIDToRefSafe (git check-ref-format delegation per WM-006a) + canonical-fallback hex-encode. Reviewer APPROVE with one MINOR (dead test helpers refnameFixture{Is,Assert}RefSafe) — addressed via inline-amend deletion commit before merge; re-review skipped per pure-deletion exception.

title: Three-level branching model (task → integration → main)
description: Per WM-007: system MUST use three-level branching: (a) node commits land on task branch per WM-005 + EM-023; (b) task branch squash-merges onto integration branch at run-terminal-success per §4.5; (c) integration branch merges to main under developer or operator policy. Harmonik's contract ends at "integration branch holds one commit per task." Merge style from integration to main is NOT dictated; `workspace_merge_status` fires ONLY for the task-branch → integration-branch merge.
close_reason: SUBSUMED: WM-007 three-level branching model is a topological constraint, no new prod type. Already covered by taskbranch.go (hk-8mwo.8), integrationbranch.go (hk-8mwo.10), and threelevel_wm007_test.go (hk-8mwo.66). All 5 WM-007 subtests pass. No new code needed.

title: Small-scope collapse: operator-policy-gated merge target for parentless runs
description: Per WM-008: when a run has no parent-bead per [beads-integration.md §4.5 BI-014], task branch's merge target MUST be determined by operator policy per [operator-nfr.md §4.3] with two allowed values: `integration` (squash-merge to harmonik/integration; default) or `main` (squash-merge directly to main; small-scope-collapse shape). Absent operator override, default MUST be `integration`. Decision is deterministic on configured policy value; no cognition. Policy knob's precedence layer + reload semantics tracked in OQ-WM-002.
close_reason: done

title: Branch naming is stable across a harmonik minor version
description: Per WM-009: task-branch and integration-branch naming conventions MUST be stable across a harmonik minor version per [operator-nfr.md §4.5 ON-018]. A breaking change to branch naming requires a migration release.
close_reason: stability sensor strengthened — two sub-tests now pin production TaskBranchPrefix and IntegrationBranchName output to spec-mandated values per WM-009

title: Lease is held by the run, not by individual agents
description: Per WM-010: a workspace MUST be leased by exactly one Run for the run's full lifetime — interval starts on `workspace_leased` emission (WM-016) and ends on terminal workspace event (`workspace_merge_status status=merged` or `workspace_discarded`) per WM-013b. Multiple agents (planner, researcher, builder, reviewer, merge agent) MUST occupy the same worktree sequentially across their nodes. An agent MUST NOT hold exclusive ownership for the duration of its session; the centralized-controller principle ([architecture.md §4.9]) requires the run — not the agent — to be the lease holder.

title: One active agent at a time inside a workspace
description: Per WM-011: at any instant AT MOST ONE agent process MAY be actively writing to the worktree. Agents run sequentially as the run traverses its workflow graph; parallel nodes within one run MUST NOT share a worktree. Parallel nodes across different runs occupy separate worktrees per WM-002. Enforcement delegated to the orchestrator (S01); workspace manager's storage-level contribution is the lease-lock file (WM-013a). The lease-lock content identifies the owning run but does not by itself arbitrate per-agent concurrency.

title: One run per bead at a time
description: Per WM-012: at any instant AT MOST ONE run MAY be in flight for a given bead. A second run for the same bead is permitted ONLY after the first has reached a terminal state (completed, failed, or canceled) per [execution-model.md §4.3]. Re-claim semantics defined in §4.9.

title: Workspace ID discoverable from run_id without a separate index
description: Per WM-013: given a `run_id`, workspace manager MUST resolve workspace record (path, branch, state) by deterministic construction per WM-002 + WM-004 plus a filesystem check per WM-013c. No separate run-to-workspace index MAY be required as the authoritative lookup path.
close_reason: LookupWorkspace(repoRoot,runID,override)->WorkspaceRef in lookupworkspace.go; 7-subtest contract test in lookupworkspace_wm013_test.go

title: Lease-lock file canonical path, JSON content, atomic-write + fsync birth
description: Per WM-013a: lease-lock file at canonical path `${workspace_path}/.harmonik/lease.lock` (per §6.2). Lock-file existence represents the lease; absence represents released. JSON content MUST carry `run_id` (UUID, required), `pid` (integer, required), `created_at` (RFC 3339, required), `ttl_sec` (integer, required; advisory). Workspace manager MUST write atomically (write-to-temp + rename) and MUST fsync before emitting `workspace_leased` (WM-016). Birth timing: immediately preceding `workspace_leased` emission; emission ordering of WM-016 unchanged. On every `workspace_created` emission the workspace manager MUST NOT yet have written a lease-lock file — the lock is tied to lease acquisition, not to workspace existence. Cross-spec coordination: HC-044a + PL-006 currently disagree; OQ-WM-005 elevated to BLOCKING-CROSS-SPEC; WM's path + JSON content authoritative.
close_reason: LeaseLockPath, WriteLeaseLockAtomic, ReadLeaseLock production functions in internal/workspace/leaselock.go; tests updated to call production API + 2 new sub-tests. Commit 532b326.

title: Minimum git version pin (≥ 2.34) detected at startup
description: Per WM-ENV-002: workspace manager requires git ≥ 2.34. Three mechanical dependencies: `git merge --strategy=ort` default since 2.34 (WM-019); `git for-each-ref --format '%(trailers:key=X,valueonly=true)'` requires 2.34 trailers format token; `git worktree repair` stabilized in 2.34. Daemon MUST detect at startup by parsing `git --version` and MUST refuse to start if below 2.34 (typed `GitVersionTooOld` error per §8). Detection at startup (not per-operation) so failure surfaces ONCE at daemon-start. Cross-subsystem version coordination with PL tracked at OQ-WM-015.
close_reason: Landed at 2f0981a: workspace/gitversion.go ParseGitVersion + DetectGitVersion (≥ 2.34); ErrGitVersionTooOld imported from errors.go. Reviewer APPROVE; one MINOR filename-underscore note (gitversion_wm_env002_test.go vs single-underscore sibling pattern), non-blocking.

title: Lease release on terminal transitions; per-terminal-path durability gates
description:
Per WM-013b: workspace manager MUST release the lease (remove lease-lock file) on every terminal workspace transition (entering `merged` or `discarded`). Per-terminal-path release gate (sub-bullets — F8b shared epilogue, single bead per F-pilot-WM-3):
• Merged path. Release AFTER `workspace_merge_status status=merged` flushed to durable events journal per [event-model.md §4.4 EV-015] (class F).
• Failed-run path. Release AFTER `run_failed` flushed (EV §8.1.3, class F). NOT `workspace_discarded` (class O, may be lost on power loss).
• Post-escalation path. Release AFTER appending `lease_released` JSONL line to `${workspace_path}/.harmonik/events/workspace-<workspace_id>.jsonl` and fsyncing (workspace-local durability marker; reconciliation consumes).
• Operator-forced discard (verdict-driven). Release AFTER `reconciliation_verdict_executed` flushed AND workspace-local marker written + fsynced.
Across all paths the workspace-local `lease_released` marker MUST precede lock-file unlink (idempotent replay if crash mid-release). Workspace-local events file owned by this spec (§6.2). Marker is durable signal; not the global EV journal. Release itself is idempotent.
close_reason: ReleaseLeaseLock, WriteLeaseReleasedMarker, WorkspaceLocalEventsPath in internal/workspace/leaselock.go; tests updated to call production API. Commit 26a5f57.

title: Lease discovery on startup (filesystem walk + lock-content read)
description: Per WM-013c: workspace manager startup MUST discover live workspaces by (a) enumerating subdirectories of `<repo>/.harmonik/worktrees/` matching the run_id regex; (b) calling `git worktree list --porcelain` against `<repo>` to confirm registered status; (c) reading the lease-lock file per WM-013a (if present) to recover run_id/pid/created_at; (d) stat-ing `${path}/.harmonik/sessions/` to detect whether any session was started. A directory failing (b) is an orphan worktree subject to PL-006. A directory passing (b) with a live lease-lock file whose recorded pid is NOT the current daemon is subject to the orphan-sweep rule of WM-033 (stale → sweep; live non-orphan → fail-fast coordination with HC-044a).
close_reason: DiscoverWorktrees(ctx,repoRoot,override) in discoverworktrees.go performs all 4 WM-013c steps; RunIDValid regex; 8+1 subtest contract test in discoverworktrees_wm013c_test.go

title: Released-workspace path re-use is forbidden
description: Per WM-013d: a released workspace's canonical path (WM-002) MUST NOT be re-leased by a subsequent run. New runs receive new canonical paths via new run_ids per WM-034. Prior run's worktree directory and branch MAY persist on disk per WM-031; re-use of the path for a different run_id is forbidden — the canonical-path invariant (WM-INV-005) would be violated.
close_reason: SUBSUMED: internal/workspace/leasereuse_wm013d_test.go:21 — TestWM013d_ReleasedWorkspacePathReuseRejected locks the released-path-no-reuse contract (WM-013d + WM-INV-005) with both positive and negative subtests

title: `.gitignore` hygiene for harmonik control-plane paths (write-or-fail)
description: Per WM-013e: workspace manager MUST ensure backing-repo root `.gitignore` excludes harmonik control-plane paths. Required entries (relative to repo root, order preserved): `.harmonik/lease.lock`, `.harmonik/sessions/`, `.harmonik/worktrees/`, `.harmonik/events/`. The `.harmonik/sessions/` inclusion is scoped to root-level (sessions live INSIDE worktrees); per-worktree `.harmonik/sessions/` are NOT excluded by this entry (gitignore interpretation rooted at main worktree; checkpoint commits inside task worktrees see their own session dirs as in-tree per WM-030). At daemon startup workspace manager MUST check root `.gitignore` for these entries; if missing, daemon MUST add them AND stage + commit on dedicated branch `harmonik/gitignore-init` BEFORE creating any worktree. If daemon lacks write permission, startup MUST fail-fast with `GitignoreWriteForbidden` per §8. Operator-side override tracked in OQ-WM-014.
close_reason: EnsureGitignoreHygiene(ctx,repoRoot)+RequiredGitignoreEntries+MissingGitignoreEntries in gitignorehygiene.go; 6-subtest contract test in gitignorehygiene_wm013e_prod_test.go

title: Workspace state machine
description: Per WM-014: a Workspace MUST traverse the lifecycle states `created → ready → leased → (optionally merge-pending → optionally conflict-resolving) → (merged | discarded)`. Lifecycle state is orthogonal to interrupt_state (§4.10); orthogonality applies only to in-flight states per WM-037a. See §7.1 for the transition table. The `setup` state from WM v0.2 has been retired in v0.3.0; retired enum value MUST NOT be reintroduced.
close_reason: WM-014 Workspace state machine shipped at internal/workspace/workspace.go (1 prod + 1 test file, 56 subtests, gofmt-clean). Reviewed APPROVE-WITH-NOTES, no MEDIUM. MINORs deferred (cosmetic): stateMachineFixtureWorkspace.RunID zero-value despite comment; created->discarded and merge-pending->discarded absent from negative-test table (functionally correct rejection). Typed-alias-deferral correctly wired — hk-8mwo.72 (HandlerRef typed wrapper) created. Process anomaly: commit landed directly on main due to worktree-escape — protocol patched at 3201f63.

title: Workspace lifecycle event emission obligations (WHEN)
description:
Per WM-015: workspace manager MUST emit the events at the §7.1 transitions. Event type names AND payload schemas are authoritative in [event-model.md §8.5] per EV-025; this spec is normative ONLY for WHEN each event fires:
• workspace_created — emit on entry to `created`.
• workspace_leased — emit on entry to `leased` AFTER WM-016 ordering gates.
• workspace_merge_status — single paired-phase event per [event-model.md §8.9(h)]: emit ONCE with `status=pending` on entry to `merge-pending` and ONCE with `status=merged` on entry to `merged`.
• workspace_discarded — emit on entry to `discarded`.
• merge_conflict_escalation — emit when implementer-resolution path is exhausted per WM-023.
`workspace_interrupted` is emitted by reconciliation detector (NOT by WM); see WM-039. Prior-version inline payload enumerations (WM-017 / WM-023 / WM-039) are retired per §12; wire format read from EV alone.
close_reason: Implemented in 0a94099 (rebased): WM-015 lifecycle event WHEN-tests for created/leased/merge_status/discarded/conflict_escalation per §7.1; recorder pattern matching wm027/wm021 precedent (no live bus yet). Reviewer APPROVE.

title: `workspace_leased` emitted after sidecar + lease-lock fsync (4-step ordering)
description:
Per WM-016: workspace manager MUST complete in order BEFORE emitting `workspace_leased` (sub-bullets — F8b shared launch_session function body, single bead per F-pilot-WM-3):
• (a) `git worktree add` succeeded (WM-003).
• (b) Task branch (WM-005) exists.
• (c) First-session log directory + harmonik.meta.json sidecar per §4.7 written + fsynced.
• (d) Lease-lock file per WM-013a written + fsynced.
Only after (a)–(d) does `workspace_leased` emit. Workspace's lifecycle state MUST transition to `leased` BEFORE emission so consumers observing the event see a consistent record. Per-workspace emission tied to FIRST session's sidecar; subsequent sessions (WM-025) write their own sidecars per WM-026 and do NOT re-transition or re-emit.

title: Merge-back is performed by a node in the same worktree (same lease)
description: Per WM-018: merge-back operation (merging task branch onto integration branch) MUST be performed by a workflow node executing INSIDE the run's already-leased worktree. The merge is NOT a new workspace; it is another node in the same run consuming the same lease per WM-010. A design that creates a new workspace for the merge step is forbidden.
notes: Already implemented in internal/workspace/mergeback_wm018_test.go. TestWM018_MergeBackNodeInSameWorktree verifies worktree reuse for merge-back per WM-018. Test passes.

title: Merge-back node dispatch contract (non-agentic OR agentic)
description: Per WM-018a: every workflow producing a merge-back MUST declare the merge step as one of two node shapes: (a) **non-agentic merge node** — orchestrator-dispatched directly; orchestrator executes `git merge --squash` + commit per WM-019, no handler subprocess; (b) **agentic merge node** — dispatched through [handler-contract.md §4.1] whose handler executes the same git operations plus pre-merge validation per its LaunchSpec. Choice is workflow-author concern. In both shapes the merge node's dispatch occurs INSIDE the existing lease per WM-018; node's Outcome carries the terminal merge outcome and drives §7.1 transition from `merge-pending` to `merged` or `conflict-resolving`. Conflict detection is mechanical: non-zero exit from `git merge --squash` OR conflict markers in `git status --porcelain` → conflict entry per WM-020.
notes: Already implemented in internal/workspace/mergedispatch_wm018a_test.go. TestWM018a_MergeNodeDispatchContract covers both non-agentic (author=daemon-identity) and agentic (author=launchspec-identity) merge shapes per WM-018a. Tests pass.
close_reason: done

title: Squash-merge with `--strategy=ort`; one-commit-per-task; trailers + author/committer split
description:
Per WM-019: merge-back MUST produce exactly one commit on integration branch per completed task. Implemented as `git merge --squash --strategy=ort <task_branch>` followed by `git commit` (or porcelain-equivalent preserving identical tree-and-trailer semantics). `--strategy=ort` pin REQUIRED (default since git 2.34 per WM-ENV-002; explicit specification prevents older or differently-configured site falling back to `recursive`).
**Commit message synthesis.** Synthesized by the merge node (non-agentic OR agentic per WM-018a). Body MUST include summary of task-branch's checkpoint commit set — at minimum first-line subject of each checkpoint, concatenated in commit order — so the squashed commit remains human-readable. Trailers MUST carry `Harmonik-Run-ID: <run_id>` and (when bead-tied) `Harmonik-Bead-ID: <bead_id>` per [execution-model.md §4.4 EM-017]. Re-synthesized from run's invariant identifiers (equivalent to last-checkpoint trailers per WM-INV-003).
**Author/committer split.** Author = LaunchSpec identity of merge-node's implementer (agentic variant — `implementer_handler_ref.identifier`) OR daemon identity (non-agentic variant). Committer = daemon identity in BOTH variants (`Harmonik Daemon <no-reply@harmonik.local>` or operator-configured override per [operator-nfr.md §4.3]).
close_reason: SUBSUMED — squashmerge_wm019_test.go exists with 6 passing subtests covering the full WM-019 contract (--strategy=ort pin, one-commit-per-task, Harmonik-Run-ID/Bead-ID trailers, author/committer split, conflict detection). Production wrapper will call same git commands when wired.

title: Workspace type and required fields
description: Per WM-001: a `Workspace` MUST carry workspace_id (stable across restarts), run_id, repository (absolute path to LOCAL clone), parent_commit, branch_name, path, state, interrupt_state, metadata (closed map: created_at + operator_fingerprint), implementer_handler_ref (optional; null when task branch has no agentic commits), schema_version, optional bead_id (opaque correlation field). The `metadata` map is closed at MVH (additional keys forbidden).

title: Merge executes outside main worktree (`--ignore-other-worktrees` OR scratch worktree)
description:
Per WM-019a: merge operation (WM-019) MUST NOT execute `git checkout` against integration branch in a worktree concurrently used by operator activity. Two accepted mechanisms:
• (a) `--ignore-other-worktrees` flag — merge node MAY run `git checkout --ignore-other-worktrees <integration_branch>` against merge-staging area. Tells git to proceed even if another worktree has the branch checked out.
• (b) **Scratch merge-worktree (PREFERRED)** — merge node creates dedicated short-lived worktree at `<repo>/.harmonik/worktrees/merge-<merge_id>/` (UUIDv7 minted for the merge), executes the merge inside, removes worktree after commit. Lifecycle (sub-bullets — F8b cohesive merge function body, single bead per F-pilot-WM-3): worktree add `-b merge-<merge_id> <merge_path> <integration_tip>` → `git merge --squash --strategy=ort <task_branch>` → resolve conflicts per WM-024 if present → `git commit` with trailers per WM-019 → push integration tip → `git worktree remove --force` + `git worktree prune` → delete transient `merge-<merge_id>` branch. Scratch worktree is unleased; no lease-lock; not subject to §4.3 lifecycle; never classified as orphan by WM-003a. `merge_id` distinct from any run_id.
notes: SUBSUMED: internal/workspace/scratchworktree_wm019a_test.go lines 29-155 (TestWM019a_ScratchMergeWorktreeLifecycle) fully implements the WM-019a scratch-worktree lifecycle as a test-spec. All fixture helpers exist in mergedispatch_wm018a_test.go. Test passes. No production wrapper needed.

title: Squash-merge is non-fast-forward by construction
description: Per WM-020: a squash-merge is non-fast-forward by its nature: `git merge --squash` produces a single new commit on integration branch whose tree equals the merged result but whose parent is the integration-branch tip (not the task-branch tip). Integration-branch tip therefore ADVANCES to a new commit containing squashed content; task-branch tip is UNCHANGED and is NOT an ancestor of the new integration-branch tip. The phrasing "not fast-forward-only" used in prior WM drafts is retired — under squash a fast-forward is not representable. Conflict markers in the index are real and MUST be resolved per §4.6 before the merge commit is created. Detection mechanical per WM-018a (non-zero exit OR conflict markers in `git status --porcelain`).
notes: SUBSUMED: internal/workspace/squashnonff_wm020_test.go lines 24-126 (TestWM020_SquashMergeIsNonFastForwardByConstruction) fully implements WM-020 non-fast-forward property as a test-spec. All fixture helpers exist in mergedispatch_wm018a_test.go and squashnonff_wm020_test.go (mergeBackFixtureIntegBranchName, mergeBackFixtureMakeWorktreePath). Test passes. No production wrapper needed.

title: Merge outcome emits workspace_merge_status with status=merged
description: Per WM-021: on successful merge, workspace manager MUST emit `workspace_merge_status` with `status=merged` per [event-model.md §8.5.3]; payload schema (including `merge_commit_hash`, `source_branch`, `target_branch`) declared there. State MUST transition to `merged` per §7.1. On entry to `merge-pending` (prior to merge execution), workspace manager MUST emit `workspace_merge_status` with `status=pending` — same event type per paired-phase rule of [event-model.md §8.9(h)]. Two separate `workspace_merge_pending` and `workspace_merged` event types are NOT registered; references in WM v0.2 retired per §12.
close_reason: SUBSUMED — mergestatus_wm021_test.go exists with 3 passing subtests covering workspace_merge_status payload shape for status=merged and status=pending, plus hash-matches-integration-tip assertion.

title: Original-implementer identification: sidecar walk (NOT trailer walk)
description:
Per WM-022: "original implementer" for a merge-back MUST be identified mechanically from `implementer_handler_ref` (§6.1). Field MUST be set by workspace manager at merge-pending entry (§7.1) to the `handler_ref` derived from the most-recent agentic session sidecar.
**Identification mechanism (sidecar walk).** Workspace manager MUST enumerate `${workspace_path}/.harmonik/sessions/*/harmonik.meta.json` (sidecars written by WM-026) and order by sidecar's `launched_at` (RFC 3339) in REVERSE chronological order. For each, inspect `agent_type` (conformance-class identifier per [architecture.md §4.7 AR-024] + [handler-contract.md §6.1]); the FIRST sidecar whose `agent_type` belongs to the agentic handler classes (mechanical / generator / merge-node classes are non-agentic) supplies `implementer_handler_ref` and the LaunchSpec template. Sidecar is authoritative because WM-026 guarantees write-and-fsync before handler launch — durable prerequisite for any commit that may have landed.
**No git-trailer walk.** This rule does NOT walk git commit trailers. Trailers in EM-017 carry run_id/state_id/transition_id/schema_version/bead_id — none identify the emitting agent's conformance class. Earlier drafts cited a fabricated `Harmonik-Actor-Role` trailer; no such trailer exists; retired at v0.4.0. Enriching EM trailer set with `Harmonik-Agent-Type` tracked at OQ-WM-012.
**Null fallback.** If sidecar walk finds no agentic session, `implementer_handler_ref` MUST be set to null and WM-022a governs the escalation path.
Resolution agent MUST be a fresh handler session launched from `implementer_handler_ref` per WM-024. No dedicated "merge-agent" role in MVH. "Original" refers to HANDLER-CLASS LINEAGE, not process-identity.
close_reason: WM-022_sidecar_walk_landed_in_9b3258b

title: All-mechanical task branches escalate directly (skip re-dispatch)
description: Per WM-022a: if `implementer_handler_ref` is null at merge-pending entry (task branch contains only non-agentic commits — mechanical refactors, generated-code landings, or merge-node commits), workspace manager MUST skip WM-024 re-dispatch and emit `merge_conflict_escalation` directly per WM-023 on conflict detection. The system MUST NOT silently remap the implementer role to an unrelated handler class. Human resolution is the MVH fallback for all-mechanical conflict paths.
close_reason: done

title: Unresolvable conflicts escalate via merge_conflict_escalation
description: Per WM-023: if re-dispatched implementer per WM-024 cannot resolve within handler-contract budget per [handler-contract.md §6.1], or returns any terminal outcome other than a successful merge commit, workspace manager MUST emit `merge_conflict_escalation` per [event-model.md §8.5.6] (EV owns the payload schema including workspace_id, run_id, conflict_paths[], escalated_at). The run MUST transition to `conflict-resolving` per §7.1 on initial conflict detection (NOT again on escalation — single-entry transition). Escalation marks resolution path as exhausted; workspace transitions to `discarded` per §7.1 after escalation event emitted.
close_reason: done

title: Conflict-resolver dispatch (mechanism) + 3-attempt cap (op-tunable [1,10])
description:
Per WM-024: mechanism of dispatching implementer agent back into worktree to resolve conflict is deterministic (mechanism-tagged; this spec owns it). Agent's reasoning during resolution is delegated to implementer's handler per [handler-contract.md §4.1] (cognition-tagged; owned there). Mechanical delegation path:
• Role: handler class resolved from `workspace.implementer_handler_ref` per WM-022.
• Model-class: `agent_type` from session sidecar that produced latest agentic commit.
• Input shape: fresh LaunchSpec with `workspace_path` pointing at existing worktree, `required_skills` including conflict-resolution skill (skill name pending OQ-WM-007), input payload of (task branch tip, integration branch tip, conflict-markered paths, transition history per [execution-model.md §4.7]).
• Budget: fresh per handler-contract LaunchSpec default; prior session's unused budget NOT inherited.
• Re-dispatch attempt cap: DEFAULT THREE (3) per merge-pending cycle. Each attempt spawns fresh LaunchSpec; outcome recorded in workspace-local events JSONL per WM-013b. After three non-successful attempts, WM-022a/WM-023 routes verdict to `escalate-to-human` per [reconciliation/spec.md §4.5 RC-020]. Cap operator-configurable per [operator-nfr.md §4.3] in [1, 10]; out-of-range overrides rejected at daemon startup.
If recorded handler class has been retired between initial commit time and merge-time re-dispatch (post-MVH concern), workspace manager MUST treat as unresolvable-conflict path and route to WM-023 escalation without silent handler-class remap.
close_reason: done

title: Session-log directory at `${workspace_path}/.harmonik/sessions/${session_id}/`
description: Per WM-025: for every agent session launched against a workspace, a session-log directory MUST exist at `${workspace_path}/.harmonik/sessions/${session_id}/`. Directory is the join point for handler-written session logs (S04) and CASS-read metadata (S08). Workspace manager OWNS directory creation; handlers OWN log-file contents; handler's `session_log_location` progress-stream emission per [handler-contract.md §4.2 HC-010] announces this pre-existing path to the watcher. `session_id` is per-session UUID minted by workspace manager at session start; generation rule in [handler-contract.md §4.2]. Distinct sessions never collide because session_id is unique per launch.
close_reason: SessionLogDirPath/SessionLogRootPath/CreateSessionLogDir in sessionlogdir.go; 6-subtest contract test in sessionlogpath_wm025_test.go

title: Metadata sidecar `harmonik.meta.json` (atomic write: tmp+fsync+rename+parent-fsync)
description:
Per WM-026: workspace manager MUST write metadata sidecar at `${workspace_path}/.harmonik/sessions/${session_id}/harmonik.meta.json` BEFORE handler launches. Required fields: run_id, node_id, agent_type (conformance-class identifier per [architecture.md §4.7 AR-024]), workflow_id, launched_at (RFC 3339), schema_version, bead_id (present iff bead-tied per [execution-model.md §4.3 EM-014]). Sidecar is authoritative join key for CASS indexing AND authoritative source for agent-type at merge-time implementer identification (WM-022). For runs inside expanded sub-workflows per [execution-model.md §4.8 EM-034a], sidecar's `node_id` MUST carry namespaced `<parent_node_id>/<sub_node_id>` form.
**Atomicity discipline.** Same as lease-lock per WM-013a (sub-bullets — F8b cohesive write_json_atomic_fsync helper, single bead per F-pilot-WM-3): (i) write JSON to sibling temp file `harmonik.meta.json.tmp-<pid>`; (ii) fsync temp file; (iii) `rename(2)` temp → canonical name (POSIX rename atomic); (iv) fsync parent directory `${workspace_path}/.harmonik/sessions/${session_id}/` to durably record rename. Step (iv) REQUIRED — power loss after (iii) without parent-dir fsync can lose rename on some filesystems. WM-016's four-step ordering requires both sidecar AND lease-lock durable on disk before `workspace_leased`; parent-dir fsync MUST complete before `workspace_leased` for the first session's sidecar.
Interrupted write (process death between (i)-(iii)) leaves `.tmp-<pid>` file and no canonical sidecar. Startup sweep MUST tolerate orphan `.tmp-<pid>` files by removing them; missing sidecar with present lease-lock classified per WM-013c; missing lease-lock with present sidecar is `sidecar-without-lease` evidence type per WM-003a.

title: Sidecar metadata stamping precedes workspace_leased for the first session
description: Per WM-027: workspace manager MUST write the first session's metadata sidecar BEFORE emitting `workspace_leased` per WM-016. This ordering ensures any consumer of `workspace_leased` observing handler launch can join metadata without racing the sidecar write. For subsequent sessions within a workspace (WM-010 allows many sessions per workspace), sidecar write precedes handler launch but does NOT re-emit `workspace_leased`; proceeds as stand-alone sidecar-write covered by WM-026.

title: Worktree path convention `<repo>/.harmonik/worktrees/<run_id>/`
description: Per WM-002: every workspace MUST be created at canonical path `<repo>/.harmonik/worktrees/<run_id>/`. The daemon operates on a local clone only; workspaces MUST NOT be materialized against a bare remote URL. Worktree root operator-configurable per [control-points.md §4.7 CP-037] (precedence-layer reload at next operator pause); per-run subdirectory `<run_id>/` is fixed. `run_id` MUST match `[A-Za-z0-9-]+` (UUIDv7 satisfies this).
close_reason: WorktreePath+WorktreeRootPath helpers in worktreepath.go; 6-subtest contract test in worktreepath_wm002_test.go; follow-up bead hk-ml3rw for CP-037 typed surface

title: bead_id propagates into session metadata when present
description: Per WM-028: when run is bead-tied, sidecar's `bead_id` field MUST carry the same `bead_id` value the checkpoint trailer carries per [execution-model.md §4.4 EM-017] and [beads-integration.md §4.6 BI-017, BI-018] — trailer name (`Harmonik-Bead-ID`) and schema owned by execution-model and beads-integration respectively. This spec asserts only the VALUE correlation. `bead_id` MUST be absent (or explicit null) when run has no bead tie. CASS uses this metadata to join session logs to Beads task ledger.

title: Session-log directory consumed read-only by S08
description: Per WM-029: session-log directory (including metadata sidecar and handler-written logs) MUST be treated as read-only from memory-layer subsystem's perspective. S08 indexes contents into CASS without mutating any file under `${workspace_path}/.harmonik/sessions/`. MVH does not declare a mechanical sensor; read-only contract enforced by reviewer discipline until memory-layer spec (deferred) names operator-auditable permission or audit hook. See OQ-WM-003.
close_reason: spec-corpus sensor TestWM029SessionLogDirectoryReadOnlyByS08 in internal/specaudit/wm029_session_log_read_only_test.go; commit e341063

title: Post-merge session-log retention default (preserve-in-merged-branch)
description: Per WM-030: on successful merge (`workspace_merge_status status=merged`), workspace manager MUST preserve sessions directory inside merged branch (i.e., session logs remain in integration-branch commit tree) by default. Operator-configured alternative MAY move directory to post-merge archive path post-MVH; MVH default is preserve-in-merged-branch for audit retention. MVH default requires project `.gitignore` MUST NOT exclude `.harmonik/sessions/`; gitignored sessions silently break the preserve-in-merged-branch contract. Violations are operator-observable misconfiguration per [operator-nfr.md §4.9].
close_reason: done

title: Failed-run worktrees persist until operator cleanup
description: Per WM-031: a worktree whose run reached terminal failure (`failed` or `canceled` per [execution-model.md §7.1]) MUST persist on disk with branch intact. Workspace manager MUST NOT auto-delete failed-run worktrees beyond startup orphan sweep (WM-033). Failed-run worktrees retained for audit and post-mortem. Lease-lock files released per WM-013b; worktree directory and branch remain. MVH imposes no retention window; operators facing disk pressure handle via operator-initiated cleanup workflows per [operator-nfr.md §4.9]; automatic retention-window default tracked in OQ-WM-008.
close_reason: WM-031 tests wired to production functions; worktree-persist-after-release validated via ReleaseLeaseLock. Commit 706479f.

title: Failed-run workspace state is `discarded`; interrupt_state composes orthogonally
description: Per WM-032: on terminal failure, workspace state MUST transition to `discarded` per §7.1. A workspace's `interrupt_state` (§4.10) MAY be non-`none` at the moment of terminal failure (e.g., operator-stopped run reaches `discarded`); per WM-037a, terminal transition MUST clear any non-`none` interrupt_state back to `none` — terminal states are absorbing and do not carry interrupt signals. Branch MUST NOT be deleted; branch reference in run's bead history per [reconciliation/schemas.md §6.2] preserves auditability.
close_reason: SUBSUMED: TestWM032_FailedRunStateIsDiscarded in failedrunverdict_wm031_test.go:100 covers full WM-032 + WM-037a contract (all interrupt_state values x discarded transition).

title: Startup orphan sweep removes stale lease-lock files (content-first staleness; mtime tiebreaker)
description:
Per WM-033: on daemon startup, orphan sweep (per [process-lifecycle.md §4.2 PL-006]) MUST remove stale lease-lock files from worktrees whose owning daemon is no longer running. Sweep MUST NOT delete worktree directories or branches. Full worktree cleanup is operator-initiated.
**Staleness — content-first, mtime tiebreaker.** Determined PRIMARILY from JSON content (WM-013a): `pid` and `created_at` combined with liveness-probe per [handler-contract.md §4.10 HC-044a] (recorded PID not live OR PID recycled to non-harmonik-daemon argv) establish staleness. Filesystem `mtime` MAY be used ONLY as tie-breaker when content-based probe is ambiguous (PID live but argv identifies different harmonik-daemon generation). Content parses cleanly + PID live + identifies current generation → NOT stale regardless of mtime. PID dead → stale regardless of mtime. v0.3.0 mtime-only dependence narrowed.
**Post-sweep `git worktree prune`.** After removing stale locks AND after WM-003a orphan-directory routing (WM-003a routes to reconciliation Cat 3; routing does NOT itself remove git metadata), sweep MUST invoke `git worktree prune` against `<repo>` to drop stale `.git/worktrees/<name>/` metadata entries. Prune is safe: skips entries with operator-issued `git worktree lock`; harmonik never issues `git worktree lock` itself (worktree-lock disjoint per glossary I5). Operator lock respected.
**Cross-spec coordination.** OQ-WM-005 elevated to BLOCKING-CROSS-SPEC; WM's path + JSON content authoritative. WM does NOT write a second legacy-compatible lock file.
close_reason: SweepStaleLeaseLocks(ctx,repoRoot,override)+IsLeaseLockStale(pid)+SweepResult in orphansweep.go; syscall.Kill PID-0 probe; 7-subtest contract test in orphansweep_wm033_test.go

title: reopen-bead → fresh run_id + fresh worktree + fresh task branch
description: Per WM-034: when investigator agent issues `reopen-bead` verdict per [reconciliation/spec.md §4.5 RC-020] and [reconciliation/schemas.md §6.1 Verdict], subsequent claim of reopened bead MUST produce a new run with FRESH `run_id` distinct from every prior run_id ever dispatched against the bead. Fresh run_id becomes lease key for FRESH worktree at canonical path `<repo>/.harmonik/worktrees/<new_run_id>/` and FRESH task branch `run/<new_run_id>` per WM-005. Prior run's run_id, worktree, and branch persist on disk per WM-031 (lease-lock released per WM-013b). This rule closes tension between path canonicality (WM-002) and failed-run persistence (WM-031): distinct runs land at distinct paths because run_ids differ. Minting of fresh run_id is execution-model's responsibility; this spec asserts only that workspace manager MUST observe a new run_id on entry and MUST reject any attempt to reuse a prior run_id at workspace-create time.
close_reason: done

title: Intra-run rollback verdicts keep the same worktree + task branch
description: Per WM-035: intra-run rollback verdicts — `resume-here`, `resume-with-context`, `reset-to-checkpoint` per [reconciliation/spec.md §4.5 RC-020] — MUST keep the same worktree and same task branch. Run's `run_id` unchanged; state reverts to the named checkpoint via git operations inside existing worktree per [execution-model.md §4.10 EM-044]. The `rollback_to_state_id` field on the transition record (EM-044) is the mechanical driver of the rollback.
close_reason: contract harness landed at commit 8154b1c — intrarunrollback_wm035_test.go; worktree-stable, branch-stable, run_id-unchanged invariants locked for all 3 intra-run verdicts

title: Re-run vs intra-run classification deterministic on 7-value verdict enum
description:
Per WM-036: decision between "fresh worktree" (WM-034) and "keep worktree" (WM-035) MUST be deterministic on verdict enum value declared in [reconciliation/schemas.md §6.1 Verdict]. No cognition participates. Authoritative classification against the seven-value verdict enum (per RC-020 as amended in RC v0.3.0):
• `reopen-bead` — fresh worktree + fresh task branch + fresh run_id (WM-034).
• `resume-here` — keep worktree + keep task branch (WM-035).
• `resume-with-context` — keep worktree + keep task branch (WM-035).
• `reset-to-checkpoint` — keep worktree + keep task branch; state reverts via EM-044.
• `accept-close-with-note` — no re-run; workspace remains in current terminal state.
• `no-op-accept` (v0.4.2 row) — no workspace action; record verdict; clear any non-`none` interrupt_state per WM-040 if reconciliation had marked the workspace interrupted; outer run continues per [reconciliation/spec.md §8.12].
• `escalate-to-human` — no re-run; workspace remains in current terminal state; operator escalation per [operator-nfr.md §4.3].
Prior WM v0.2 `abandon` verdict text retired per §12. Any verdict value not in the table is malformed per [reconciliation/spec.md §4.5 RC-023] and MUST NOT be routed; classifier returns classification-error to caller and emits no workspace action. Bilateral attribute proposal (`worktree_disposition` on Verdict) tracked at OQ-WM-009. The `no-op-accept` row resolves OQ-RC-011.
close_reason: done

title: interrupt_state orthogonal to lifecycle for in-flight states (5-value enum)
description: Per WM-037: workspace record MUST carry `interrupt_state` field orthogonal to lifecycle state of §4.4 FOR THE IN-FLIGHT STATES (`created`, `ready`, `leased`, `merge-pending`, `conflict-resolving`). Values: `none` (default; no interruption), `operator-paused` (operator pause interrupted lease), `operator-stopped-graceful` (graceful stop in progress), `operator-stopped-immediate` (immediate stop; handler subprocess may have been killed), `daemon-crash-suspected` (pre-reconciliation placeholder for run whose lease was not cleanly released). Lifecycle state (e.g., `leased`) and interrupt_state (e.g., `operator-paused`) compose independently within the in-flight set. Enum values locally owned by this spec but map onto operator-control vocabulary per [operator-nfr.md §4.3 ON-011] and reconciliation Cat 6 detector carve-out per [reconciliation/spec.md §8.11]. This spec does NOT redefine those vocabularies; projects them onto a workspace-local field for dispatch convenience.
close_reason: SUBSUMED: TestWM037_InterruptStateOrthogonalToInFlightLifecycle in failedrunverdict_wm031_test.go covers the 5-value enum orthogonality with full 5x5 in-flight-state × interrupt-state matrix (hk-8mwo.71)

title: Worktree creation uses `git worktree add -b <branch> <path> <parent_commit>`
description: Per WM-003: workspace manager MUST create a worktree + fresh task branch atomically via `git worktree add -b <branch> <path> <parent_commit>` against the backing repo. The `-b` form is REQUIRED (the task branch does not yet exist). Explicit `<parent_commit>` start-point is REQUIRED to pin the branch base deterministically; omitting it would default to HEAD and race with operator activity in the main worktree. No provisioning layer (adze, devbox, container) at MVH; plain subfolder. Git minimum-version pin per WM-ENV-002.
close_reason: CreateWorktree(ctx,repoRoot,runID,parentCommit,override) in createworktree.go; 6-subtest contract test in createworktree_wm003_test.go

title: Terminal lifecycle states carry no interrupt signal
description: Per WM-037a: terminal lifecycle states `merged` and `discarded` MUST carry `interrupt_state = none`. A transition into terminal state MUST clear any non-`none` interrupt state as part of the transition. Any interrupt signal received against a workspace already in terminal state MUST be rejected silently by workspace manager (`InterruptOnTerminalWorkspace` per §8) and logged as operator-observability event per [operator-nfr.md §4.9]; workspace state MUST NOT transition and `workspace_interrupted` (emitted by reconciliation per WM-039) MUST NOT fire against a terminal workspace. Bounds the orthogonality claim of WM-037 to the in-flight state set; eliminates the "interrupt a merged workspace" semantic anomaly.
close_reason: SUBSUMED: TestWM014_TerminalStateClearsInterruptState in statemachine_wm014_test.go:230 covers the full WM-037a contract (both terminal states × all interrupt values); ErrInterruptOnTerminalWorkspace already in errortaxonomy_wm008_test.go:31

title: interrupt_state set by operator + reconciliation pathways; WM is sole writer
description: Per WM-038: transitions of `interrupt_state` driven by (a) operator control subsystem per [operator-nfr.md §4.3 ON-013] emitting operator-control events that the workspace manager observes and applies; (b) reconciliation per [reconciliation/spec.md §4.5] emitting verdicts that the workspace manager applies. Workspace manager MUST be the SOLE writer of `interrupt_state` field on the Workspace record. Workspace manager MUST NOT mutate `interrupt_state` except in response to (a) observed operator-control event or (b) observed reconciliation verdict execution. No other subsystem may mutate the field; cross-subsystem requests to change interrupt_state MUST route through the event bus.
close_reason: done

title: Workspace-local marker for operator/verdict-driven interrupt_state mutations
description:
Per WM-038a: when workspace manager mutates `interrupt_state` in response to operator-control event (pause, stop, upgrade) OR reconciliation verdict (crash-recovery disposition), there is currently NO durable wire-event signaling the mutation: [event-model.md §8.5.5] names reconciliation detector as sole emitter of `workspace_interrupted` and scopes to Cat 6 crash detection. Operator-driven and verdict-driven transitions therefore risk being invisible to downstream consumers until the next full reconciliation pass.
To close locally (without waiting for EV's emitter broadening per OQ-WM-016), workspace manager MUST on every `interrupt_state` mutation (`none → non-none` OR `non-none → non-none`) append a single workspace-scoped JSONL line to `${workspace_path}/.harmonik/events/workspace-<workspace_id>.jsonl` and fsync. Marker shape: `{"event":"interrupt_state_changed", "workspace_id":..., "run_id":..., "prior_interrupt_state":..., "new_interrupt_state":..., "cause":<operator-event-type | verdict-kind>, "cause_event_id":<optional>, "changed_at":<rfc3339>}`. Marker is authoritative durable record consumed by reconciliation's sweep pass per [reconciliation/spec.md §4.3]. WM MAY additionally emit async bus event as in-process hint; marker is the durability signal.
close_reason: done

title: WM observes interrupt transitions but does NOT emit workspace_interrupted
description: Per WM-039: on any transition of `interrupt_state` from `none` to non-`none`, workspace manager MUST update record atomically with causing input (operator-control event or reconciliation verdict) such that new state is durable and observable by reconciliation detector. The `workspace_interrupted` event per [event-model.md §8.5.5] is emitted by reconciliation detector, NOT by workspace manager; payload schema (workspace_id, run_id, detected_at, category) is EV-owned. Workspace manager MUST NOT emit `workspace_interrupted`. This rule reflects split of emission authority: workspace manager owns the interrupt_state FIELD (mutation per WM-038); reconciliation detector owns the wire EVENT (emission per EV §8.5.5). Prior WM v0.2 text declaring WM as emitter retired per §12. OQ-WM-006 tracks unresolved coordination with EV.
close_reason: done

title: interrupt_state reset to `none` requires reconciliation OR operator resume
description: Per WM-040: clearing `interrupt_state` back to `none` MUST be driven by either (a) `operator_resuming` event per [event-model.md §8.7] and [operator-nfr.md §4.3 ON-013] for operator-initiated interrupts, or (b) reconciliation verdict per [reconciliation/spec.md §4.5] for daemon-crash or lost-lease interrupts. Workspace manager MUST NOT silently clear the field. Sensor: any mutation of `interrupt_state` to `none` not preceded by a matching causing event is a violation, detected by reconciliation detector's transition-audit pass per [reconciliation/spec.md §4.3 RC-010].
close_reason: done

title: Sensor: lease-by-run is honored corpus-wide
description:
Per WM-INV-001: every subsystem observing a workspace MUST treat workspace's run_id as exclusive lease holder for run's full lifetime per WM-010. No subsystem MAY mechanism a per-agent lease acquisition. Constrains orchestrator (S01; dispatch), agent runner (S04; handler-launch preconditions), workspace manager (S06; storage), reconciliation (lost-lease detection), memory layer (S08; session-metadata consumption). Workspace-layer realization of centralized-controller principle per [architecture.md §4.9 AR-INV-007].
Sensor: reconciliation's startup lost-lease detector per [reconciliation/spec.md §8.11 Cat 6] correlates each live lease-lock file against Beads' record of owning run_id and against daemon's live-run registry; any workspace with lease-lock whose run_id does not appear as in-flight run (or whose lease-lock is missing while run is in-flight) is a violation evidence route.
notes: no_commit_during_implementer: HEAD did not advance past parent 721fb3b07e01fe0a0137765ed53c3fe0aca8bfdf at iteration 1 exit=0
close_reason: Subsumed: landed as ecee31d

title: Sensor: one in-flight run per bead
description:
Per WM-INV-002: for every bead, every subsystem routing on run activity MUST assume at-most-one in-flight run. Multi-run-per-bead allowed across time only after prior run has reached terminal state per [execution-model.md §4.3 EM-014]. Reconciliation's post-crash detectors MUST reject observations of multiple in-flight runs for one bead as inconsistency evidence per [reconciliation/spec.md §8.11 Cat 6a]. Beads-integration's terminal-write rule (BI-010) and workspace-manager's WM-012 both rely on this.
Sensor: Cat 6a detector (bead `in_progress` with two+ task branches each advertising `Harmonik-Run-ID` without `Harmonik-Verdict-Executed`) per [reconciliation/schemas.md §6.3].
close_reason: done

title: Sensor: checkpoint commits obey git append-only on the task branch (two-part)
description:
Per WM-INV-003: any git observer of a run's task branch MUST reject commits whose new tip is not a fast-forward descendant of the prior tip (no amend, rebase, filter-branch, force-push, `git replace` rewrite, or history-editing operation may rewrite history on a task branch that has emitted `workspace_leased`). Git's native commit semantics are the contract; this spec adds no transactional layer. Constrains handler-contract (S04 handlers per [handler-contract.md §4.1]), workspace manager (merge-back), execution-model's checkpoint-cadence per [execution-model.md §4.5 EM-023].
Sensor (two-part):
• Part A: branch-tip monotonicity. EM-024a check — daemon persists last-observed task-branch-tip SHA per in-flight run; any non-fast-forward discrepancy routes to reconciliation Cat 3 per [reconciliation/spec.md §8.4]. Catches amend/rebase/force-push.
• Part B: history-editing audit. Conformance-time invariant-auditor tool walks `git reflog` for every live task branch; rejects any reflog entry whose operation is `filter-branch`, `replace`, or non-fast-forward `update` on a run still in in-flight state. Tool home pending `testing.md` per OQ-WM-017. Catches filter-branch/replace tip-identity-preserving rewrites.
WM does NOT rely on reflog for state reconstruction (per [execution-model.md §4.7]); reflog dependence confined to invariant-auditor test tool — robust against operator-configured aggressive `gc.reflogExpire`.
close_reason: Subsumed: landed as ref'd commit

title: Sensor: canonical-path discovery without a registry
description:
Per WM-INV-005: any subsystem reconstructing workspace state from filesystem MUST derive workspace location from run_id alone per WM-002 without consulting a separate registry, index, or SQLite table. No registry-backed lookup MAY be declared authoritative for workspace path resolution. Constrains workspace manager (WM-013, WM-013c), reconciliation's startup pass per [reconciliation/spec.md §4.3], orchestrator's run-resume logic at restart.

Type aliases: CommitSHA per [execution-model.md §6.1]; BeadID per [beads-integration.md §6.1]; HandlerRef per [handler-contract.md §6.1]; UUID is UUIDv7 per [event-model.md §4.1 EV-002].

title: Discovery classifies partially-crashed worktree states
description: Per WM-003a: a directory under `<repo>/.harmonik/worktrees/<run_id>/` registered by `git worktree list --porcelain` but with NO lease-lock AND NO sessions dir is classified `bare-worktree-no-lease` and routed to reconciliation Cat 3 per [reconciliation/spec.md §8]. A registered worktree with sidecar present but NO lease-lock is classified `sidecar-without-lease` (same Cat 3 route). Both arise from SIGKILL/power-loss between `git worktree add` and the lease-lock fsync gate of WM-016. Cat 3 routing gives reconciliation right to propose `reopen-bead` or `accept-close-with-note`.

title: WM error taxonomy (§8) — 12-class typed sentinel set + transitions + downstream routing
description:
Per §8: implement the 12 typed sentinel classes + workspace-transition consequences + downstream routing rules:
• `WorkspaceAlreadyExists` — `create_workspace` observes existing directory at canonical path. (no transition) → orchestrator reports run-create failure; reconciliation Cat 3c may fire on reboot if prior run's terminal transition incomplete.
• `RunIdReuseForbidden` — `create_workspace` observes run_id has been dispatched before (violates WM-034). (no transition) → orchestrator run-create failure; caller-error class.
• `WorktreeCreationFailed` — `git worktree add` non-zero (bad parent_commit, concurrent git lock, disk full). (remains in initial) → orchestrator run-create failure; operator-observability per [operator-nfr.md §4.1 ON-001].
• `LeaseLockHeldByOrphan` — `create_workspace` / `launch_session` observes live lease-lock belonging to prior daemon generation per WM-013c / HC-044a. (stays out of `leased`) → Launch fail-fast per [handler-contract.md §4.10 HC-044a]; reconciliation Cat 6a investigator if correlation reveals lost lease.
• `SidecarWriteFailed` — metadata sidecar write fails on I/O (disk full, permissions, concurrent file conflict). (stays out of `leased`) → run reported failed; operator-observability.
• `MergeConflictUnresolvable` — implementer re-dispatch or all-mechanical-branch per WM-022a produces no successful merge. `conflict-resolving → discarded` → `merge_conflict_escalation` per WM-023.
• `InterruptOnTerminalWorkspace` — operator-control or reconciliation signal targets `merged` / `discarded` workspace. (no transition per WM-037a) → silent reject; operator-observability log entry only.
• `RefNameInvalid` — integration-branch template substitution (WM-006a) produces a name that fails `git check-ref-format` after canonical fallback, or transformed name is empty / collides case-insensitively. (remains in initial) → orchestrator run-create failure; operator-observability.
• `BareWorktreeNoLease` — discovery (WM-003a) finds registered worktree with no lease-lock and no sessions. (no transition; directory is orphan) → reconciliation Cat 3 via evidence type `bare-worktree-no-lease`.
• `SidecarWithoutLease` — discovery (WM-003a) finds registered worktree with sidecars but no lease-lock. (no transition; directory is orphan) → reconciliation Cat 3 via evidence type `sidecar-without-lease`.
• `GitignoreWriteForbidden` — WM-013e detects `.gitignore` missing required entries AND daemon lacks write permission. (startup-fail) → daemon refuses to start; operator must fix `.gitignore` permissions.
• `GitVersionTooOld` — WM-ENV-002 detects installed git below 2.34 at daemon startup. (startup-fail) → daemon refuses to start; operator must upgrade git.
Detection of each class is daemon's responsibility; class identity flows to event bus via appropriate lifecycle event's payload (event-model owns schema), or to operator-observability per [operator-nfr.md §4.9].
**Single-bead form (per discipline §2.6 + F-pilot-WM-2).** WM's §8 is a flat typed-sentinel set (BI shape: BrError-style enum of error sentinels), NOT a multi-stage classification scheme like RC's Cat 1..Cat 6. Single `wm-error.taxonomy` bead is correct. The 12-class count is just above RC's ~11 threshold figure — the SHAPE discriminator (flat vs nested) drives the §2.11(c) decision, not COUNT.
**§2.11(c.2) edge direction.** Vocabulary owner → consumers `block-on` taxonomy. Edge direction `<req> → wm-error.taxonomy` ALWAYS; never inverse.
close_reason: WM §8 12-class error taxonomy shipped at internal/workspace/errors.go (12 typed sentinels + Class() classifier per HC-020 pattern, 9 top-level tests + ~180 subtests incl. all-pairs distinctness, gofmt-clean). Reviewed APPROVE-WITH-NOTES, no MEDIUM. MINORs deferred: test package white-box vs HC-020 external _test pattern; missing TestSentinelMessages prefix-convention test; imprecise classifyCrashEvidence godoc comment. Class() returns spec-verbatim mixed-case intentionally (wire-identity vs Go-naming). Worktree-isolated correctly under patched protocol.

title: session-keeper Phase-1 warn-mode: statusLine gauge + watcher (warn@80%, idle-gate, no-gauge self-check) + tmux injector + events
description:
Build the Phase-1 WARN-ONLY keeper end to end on the landed scaffold (internal/keeper/keeper.go, cmd/harmonik/keeper_cmd.go; codename:session-keeper, parent hk-ekap1). NON-DESTRUCTIVE: NO /clear, NO handoff — only a wrap-up warning injection.

1. GAUGE SIGNAL: add scripts/keeper-statusline.sh — reads stdin JSON, extracts .context_window.used_percentage and .session_id via jq, atomically writes .harmonik/keeper/<agent>.ctx = {pct, session_id, ts(rfc3339)}. (Field path is .context_window.used_percentage — verified empirically; it reads NA right after a /clear.) Document adding it to ~/.claude/settings.json statusLine.command.
2. WATCHER (internal/keeper, runs from the keeper cmd when the .managed marker is present): poll .harmonik/keeper/<agent>.ctx every poll_interval (default 5s). warn_pct default 80. On the first UPWARD crossing of warn_pct, inject ONE wrap-up-warning prompt (via the injector) and emit session_keeper_warn; do not re-warn until pct drops below warn_pct and rises again. Idle-gate: infer idle from gauge quiescence (no update for >= idle_quiesce, default 8s) — acceptable for warn-only. Staleness/self-check: if the gauge ts is older than staleness (default 120s) OR the file is absent, take NO action and emit session_keeper_no_gauge (warn) at boot and every staleness interval (a missing statusLine must be visible, not silent).
3. INJECTOR: resolve the tmux target from the harmonik-<project_hash>-<agent> convention (internal/lifecycle/provenance.go) or a --tmux override; deliver via external tmux send-keys (bracketed-paste body + Enter to submit). Inject only into a quiesced pane (per the idle-gate).
4. EVENTS: add session_keeper_warn + session_keeper_no_gauge constants to internal/core/eventtype.go; emit via the bus (EmitWithRunID).
TESTS: the statusline script writes a correct .ctx for a sample stdin JSON; the watcher emits exactly ONE warn on an 80% upward crossing and NONE when the gauge is stale; the no-gauge self-check emits when the file is absent.

title: Twin: simulate welcome-splash timing and commit-on-cue for quit-detection
description:
Extend cmd/harmonik-twin-claude so the YAML script-driver can drive two mechanisms the operational flow depends on but the current twin cannot exercise:

1) Welcome-splash timing simulation (hk-rf4ux). Today the twin does not render a TUI; splash-dismiss is a real-claude visual artifact. Either (a) add a 'splash_wait_ms' script field where the twin refuses to read paste-inject input until that wall-clock has elapsed (simulating the splash race), or (b) accept this is real-claude-only and add a documented carve-out in specs/scenario-harness.md.

2) Commit-on-cue (hk-cmybm layer-2). The daemon's pasteInjectQuitOnCommit goroutine polls the worktree HEAD every 500ms and injects /quit when a new commit appears. Today the twin emits wire messages but does not write a real commit to the workspace fixture. Add a script primitive 'write_commit' (or 'workspace_action: commit') that calls git commit-tree against the fixture worktree at the declared script timestamp, so the scenario harness can assert the daemon detects it and injects /quit within the SLA.

Both depend on the parity audit (sibling bead) — that may decide one or both are real-claude-only. Spec note: HC-036a (script schema) will need an amendment for any new fields.

title: Resolve IdempotencyClass value-count inconsistency: execution-model.md (3 values) vs control-points.md (2 values)
description: execution-model.md §6.1 declares ENUM IdempotencyClass with 3 values: {idempotent, non-idempotent, recoverable-non-idempotent} (cited by EM-010 and implemented in internal/core/idempotencyclass.go). control-points.md §6.1.6 declares the same ENUM with only 2 values: {idempotent, non-idempotent}, citing CP-016 at-least-once vs at-most-once delivery semantics. These two spec declarations of the same enum conflict. Resolution requires spec authors to decide: (a) CP §6.1.6 should reference execution-model's 3-value enum and note that CP-016 only consumes 2 of the 3 values, so the SideEffect.idempotency_class comment {idempotent, non-idempotent} is a filter, not a redeclaration; OR (b) CP intentionally defines a 2-value sub-enum scoped to side-effect delivery only, execution-model's recoverable-non-idempotent is a node-level concept not applicable to SideEffect dispatch, and CP §6.1.6 should rename its ENUM (e.g. SideEffectIdempotencyClass) to avoid collision. Until resolved, the Go implementation tracks the 3-value execution-model definition. Surfaced during implementation of hk-a8bg.75.
close_reason: Resolved: EM §6.1 is canonical owner (3 values); CP §6.1.6 was a filter not a redeclaration. Replaced ENUM block with reference note + Tags: mechanism. Go implementation unchanged at 3 values. Commit 96d4f8d on worktree-agent-sp-1.

title: F-T2-03: malformed NDJSON + exit-0 closes bead as success — watcher protocol violation ignored by work loop
description:
## Repro
go test ./internal/daemon/... -run TestT2_MalformedNDJSON -v
# Output: events=[run_started agent_failed run_completed] closed=[t2-bead-malformed]

## Expected
When the watcher emits agent_failed due to a framing violation (HC-007b), the work loop should reopen the bead regardless of exit code. A protocol violation means the work product is suspect.

## Actual
The watcher correctly emits agent_failed (per HC-007b) when the handler emits malformed NDJSON. But the work loop drives bead disposition solely from outcome.ExitCode. Because the process exits 0, CloseBead is called. The bead is closed as success despite the agent protocol violation.

Event sequence: [run_started, agent_failed, run_completed(success=true)].

## Code location
internal/daemon/workloop.go:277 — sole decision gate is outcome.ExitCode == 0.
The loop does not inspect whether the watcher signaled a framing error.

## Severity note
At MVH the handler binary is expected to exit non-zero on failure, so this is latent. A real claude invocation that emits a JSON parse error but exits 0 would incorrectly close the bead.
close_reason: watcher.Err() checked post-Done; !ErrCanceled triggers ReopenBead on exit=0+watcher failure

title: Long implementer tasks silently killed by 30m commit budget (no_commit, no diagnostic event) + implementer windows spawn into ambient $TMUX/supervisor session (naming bug) + coordinator-session orphan leak
description: FOUND validating the hk-4l7zs redeploy (2026-06-03). On the FIXED binary (pid 56791, verified to contain spawn_cap_blocked, daemon idle / low load, fresh queue nqverify), hk-mgoo7 wedged at launch_initiated and never progressed: events for run 019e8f7b-5929 stop at launch_initiated (14:54:47) -> run_started, handler_capabilities, session_log_location, skills_provisioned, launch_initiated, then NOTHING for 9min+. NO tmux session for the run (the only sessions present are OLD orphans from 14:18-14:41). NO commit in its worktree (HEAD = main). Crucially NO spawn_cap_blocked event fired despite >2min elapsed -> so this is NOT the bounded-semaphore-acquire wedge that hk-4l7zs fixed; the wedge is AFTER SpawnWindow, in the tmux-session-creation / pasteinject / prompt-injection step. This is almost certainly the residual 'launch-stall' gotcha the session handoff documented (dispatches stall ~10-30min at launch_initiated). It is INTERMITTENT + load-independent (happened at low load) and does NOT reliably self-recover (hk-mgoo7 failed no_commit at the 30min timeout twice). RELATED ongoing leak: orphaned harmonik-*-flywheel tmux sessions keep accumulating (6 new between 14:18-14:41 after a full reap at 13:33) -> the daemon isn't reaping implementer sessions on completion/failure (separate from this wedge but likely same lifecycle code). INVESTIGATE: the launch path AFTER SpawnWindow returns -- pasteinject.go session creation + prompt injection; why launch_initiated emits (post-Launch per the hk-4l7zs reorder) but no session appears and no implementer_phase_complete; and the session-Kill/orphan path. Anchor to events.jsonl for run 019e8f7b-5929 and the pasteinject/tmuxsubstrate session-lifecycle code. Refs: hk-4l7zs (acquire-wedge, FIXED+deployed 9b2848e3), hk-trjef (pasteinject quit-on-commit watchdog), hk-5s7tg (post-commit watchdog).
close_reason: Fix-forward landed ff23633c (cherry-picked to main): re-landed #1 progress-aware 90m commit budget, #2 implementer_budget_exceeded event, #4 dead-supervisor reaper (hardened, -flywheel-only self-guard), reworked #3 sessionName (use live session; exclude supervisor hk-daemon-supervise; spawn-target guaranteed to exist at dispatch; daemon's own -default session excluded from orphan sweep). Independent reviewer APPROVE (build+vet+test verified, not reasoning-only). LIVE-SMOKE VALIDATED under real supervisor: hk-7d6el dispatched -> implementer spawned into harmonik-a3dc45482890-default (NOT supervisor), session survived self-sweep, committed a53b63fc, reviewer APPROVE iter1, merged+pushed. P0 regression class closed.

comments.author: gb
comments.text: INVESTIGATION (worktree agent): NOT a daemon spawn-wedge. launch_initiated proves Launch+SpawnWindow succeeded (workloop.go:2070 emits it ONLY after Launch returns; hk-4l7zs reorder). Live evidence: the wedged run 019e8f7b-5929 implementer pane %1542 (the exact pane daemon_pane_write hit at 14:54:49) is ALIVE (pane_dead=0, pid 57159, cmd=claude 2.1.162), capture-pane shows it 'Unfurling 19m27s, 30.4k tokens' running a long go-test. The implementer is genuinely working, just slow to commit. ROOT CAUSE of the 'wedge': on the substrate path waitWithSocketGrace (waitsocketgrace.go:95) blocks in sess.Wait with NO independent timeout; the ONLY backstops are in pasteInjectQuitOnCommit (pasteinject.go:502-617): totalDeadline=commitPollTimeout=30m (line 290/526), launch-heartbeat (180s) and heartbeat-staleness (8m) checks. The latter two are RESET every tick whenever PaneHasActiveProcess returns true (pasteinject.go:541-547,560-566; impl tmuxsubstrate.go:709). A live-but-not-committing claude keeps the pane active forever, so only the 30m totalDeadline fires => run_stale at ~10m, no_commit at 30m. INTERMITTENCY = task duration, not a race: short tasks commit before 30m; long ones (deep go-test loops) blow the budget. Zero agent_heartbeat events in-window (300s timer) is a secondary signal. SECONDARY BUG: window spawned into session hk-daemon-supervise (the supervisor's own tmux session), not the daemon's session -- sessionName resolution is picking up TMUX env. ORPHAN LEAK: 6 harmonik-<hash>-flywheel sessions with dead panes -- coordinator/flywheel sessions are EXPLICITLY skipped by the orphan sweep (orphansweep.go:370-374), so a supervisor that dies without kill-session leaks them forever; implementer windows leak too because Kill is the only reaper. RECOMMENDED FIX: (1) raise commitPollTimeout OR make it commit-progress-aware (extend on each new heartbeat/token-activity rather than a flat 30m wall clock) so long legit tasks aren't guillotined; (2) add a defensive bounded-timeout+diagnostic on the substrate sess.Wait path mirroring hk-4l7zs spawn_cap_blocked -- emit an implementer_budget_exceeded event at the kill so operators see WHY (not silent no_commit); (3) fix sessionName resolution so windows land in the daemon session not the inherited TMUX session; (4) add a startup reaper for orphaned flywheel/coordinator sessions whose supervisor PID is dead.

comments.author: gb
comments.text: ORCHESTRATOR CORRECTION (named-queues): confirmed the investigator live — hk-mgoo7's implementer pane is ALIVE (pid 57159, claude on sonnet, 'Unfurling 21m... thinking', running slow/hanging smoke tests) inside the hk-daemon-supervise tmux session. NOT a wedge. My original 'launch wedge / no session' framing (and the broader 'daemon dispatch down' escalation) was WRONG — the session existed, just under the ambient TMUX name so my 'grep harmonik-*-flywheel' missed it. Real issues = the 4 the investigator listed. NOTE: hk-4l7zs (the spawn-acquire fix I shipped) is a valid LATENT-bug fix (reproduced + tested) but was likely NOT the production cause of these failures — keep it (defensive hardening) but don't credit it with fixing the observed no_commits. LESSON: check the live implementer pane (tmux capture-pane) before theorizing from events.jsonl.

comments.author: gb
comments.text: DEPLOY REVERTED (fe94e0b1) — sub-fix #3 caused a P0 dispatch regression. ROOT CAUSE: the daemon runs INSIDE tmux session 'harmonik' (launched by /tmp/hk-daemon-supervise.sh). Old code resolved the implementer target session at DISPATCH time via 'tmux display-message #{session_name}' = the live session, which always exists. Sub-fix #3 (cmd/harmonik/main.go:606-622 + tmux/subcommand.go DefaultSessionName) switched to a deterministic 'harmonik-<hash>-default' name created by EnsureSession at BOOT — but that session does NOT exist/persist at dispatch time (EnsureSession returned nil at boot, session gone 2.5min later; possibly reaped by this same commit's sub-fix #4 coordinator reaper, or never persisted under tmux-nesting). Result: SpawnWindow (tmuxsubstrate.go:216) targets a non-existent session -> every spawn fails in 0.6s with 'tmux: session does not exist'. Smoke bead hk-15b83 run 019e9138-67ab confirmed: run_started->skills_provisioned->review_loop_cycle_complete{error}->run_failed, no implementer session. REVIEW GAP: 2 reviewers APPROVED by REASONING; the runtime tmux-nesting behavior under the supervisor was never exercised by a unit test or a live smoke before declaring success. FIX-FORWARD REQUIREMENTS: (1) re-land #1 (progress-aware/90m guillotine) + #2 (implementer_budget_exceeded event) + #4 (reaper) — these were good and unrelated to the session bug; (2) REWORK #3: either keep dispatch-time live-session resolution but exclude ONLY the supervisor session, OR create-and-KEEP-ALIVE a dedicated daemon session for the daemon's whole lifetime AND ensure #4 never reaps it; (3) MANDATORY: validate via a live smoke dispatch under the actual supervisor launch context (daemon inside the 'harmonik' tmux session) before redeploying — not just go test.

title: br ready --format json does not surface labels; BI-013 label-surfacing unmet at br v0.1.45
description:
The daemon's review-loop mode resolution (moderesolve.go tier-1) reads beadRecord.Labels populated by brcli.Ready(). br ready --format json (br v0.1.45) does not include the 'labels' field in its output, so Labels is always nil at claim time. resolveWorkflowMode falls through to tier-4 single mode for every dispatch, even when a workflow:review-loop label is present on the bead.

Discovered in dogfood-smoke-run-2026-05-15-review-loop.md smoke run: smoke bead smoke-e1c had workflow:review-loop label; run_completed with summary='auto-close: exit=0' (single-mode), no reviewer_launched event.

Fix options:
a) Upgrade br to a version that includes labels in ready --format json output (preferred if upstream already ships it).
b) Call ShowBead after Ready() in the workloop claim path to hydrate labels before mode resolution (harmonik-side fix, no external dep).
c) Use br list --status=open --format json + daemon-side ready filtering.

Acceptance: after fix, a bead with workflow:review-loop label dispatched via hk results in reviewer_launched event in events.jsonl.
close_reason: Subsumed by 93aeaae (2026-05-15): workloop hydrates beadRecord.Labels from ShowBead in both br-ready and queue paths before resolveWorkflowMode runs (workloop.go:689, :742-754). Test TestWorkLoop_LabelsHydratedFromShowBead (workloop_hka0htu_test.go) confirms tier-1 label resolution fires when br ready --format json omits labels.
