title: EV-043: unacknowledged decision_required blocks dispatch for that run
emitRunStarted should use EmitWithRunID so envelope run_id is populated
description:
## Finding

`emitRunStarted` in the daemon currently calls `bus.Emit` (which produces an event without the envelope `run_id` field) rather than `bus.EmitWithRunID`. As a result, `eventbus.Filter(path, runID)` returns 0 matches for `run_started` events: the filter checks the envelope, but `run_id` only lives in the payload for these specific events.

Surfaced by `internal/daemon/t11_throughput_test.go` (row 11 throughput test): the test had to fall back to payload extraction instead of using the Filter API to count distinct run_ids on `run_started`. Filter works correctly on other event types.

## Scope

Migrate `emitRunStarted` (and any other emit sites that emit run-scoped events without `EmitWithRunID`) to populate the envelope. Then update the t11 throughput test (and t7 smoke test) to use `eventbus.Filter` instead of payload extraction.

## Acceptance

- `emitRunStarted` uses `EmitWithRunID`.
- `eventbus.Filter(jsonlPath, runID)` returns the matching `run_started` events.
- t11 and t7 tests switch to Filter-based assertions for the per-run-id distinct-count check.
- Audit pass: grep for `bus.Emit(` callers in the daemon, identify any other run-scoped events still using the non-envelope variant.

## Out of scope
- Operator-facing metrics emission (separate roadmap §4 items, if/when they get filed).
close_reason: Subsumed: landed as 7514849

title: Control Points spec — implementation
description: Implements specs/control-points.md v0.3.2 (53 first-class §4 req beads from 55 active §4 reqs after 1 §2.3 coalesce (CP-004/005 cluster) and 1 §2.1a collapse (CP-052 → cp-031), 3 invariants, 24 §6.1 schemas, 0 error-taxonomy beads (CP §8 routes to EM's taxonomy), 0 multi-step splits, 5 test-infra beads). depends-on: [architecture, execution-model, event-model, handler-contract]; WM, ON, RC, BI cross-spec edges forward-deferred per F-pilot-EM-2 / Option B precedent (logged as forward:<prefix>-NNN). Three EV §6.5 events (verdict_envelope_mismatch, policy_expression_exceeded_cost, control_points_registration_started) are missing from EV's bead set; recorded as forward:ev-events.<name> deferrals per F-pilot-CP-3.

Daemonization-deferred children confirmed landed via the now-live daemon (pidfile/supervise/set-concurrency/orphan-reap). Spec-vs-epic version drift tracked separately.

title: ControlPoint is a single typed primitive
description: Per CP-001: a `ControlPoint` MUST be a single typed record (one Go struct, one lifecycle, one registration path) parameterized by `kind ∈ {Gate, Hook, Guard, Budget}`. Common fields: `name`, `kind`, `trigger`, `evaluator`, `outcome_action`, plus a Kind-specific typed payload per §4.2–§4.5. The unification is in the primitive's shape; the semantics per Kind are explicit and NOT interchangeable.

close_reason: done

title: Gate evaluator MAY be cognition-tagged
description: Per CP-011: a Gate's evaluator MAY be cognition-tagged when the policy requires judgment that a mechanism-tagged expression cannot express. Cognition-tagged Gate evaluators MUST satisfy the §4.8 replay-safety contract (persisted-verdict).
close_reason: done

title: Hook fires on event match
description: Per CP-012: a Hook MUST fire when a subscribed event matches its `trigger` (event-name match, optionally with subscription filter over event payload). Evaluator receives matched event + subscription context; returns side-effect descriptor declaring (a) emit a new event, (b) apply a declared state mutation, or (c) perform an external action. Hooks MUST NOT block, halt, or alter the run's transition progression.
close_reason: done

title: Hook lifecycle event types (baseline trigger set)
description: Per CP-013: a Hook's `trigger` MUST match one of the declared lifecycle event types. The MVH-baseline Hook-trigger set: on_agent_started, on_agent_output, on_agent_completed, on_timeout, on_review_required, on_transition_attempted, on_checkpoint_written, on_checkpoint_failed. Hook-trigger names form a separate Hook-namespace mapping to [event-model.md §8] event types; the `on_` prefix distinguishes Hook-subscription names from raw event-type names. Subsystems MAY declare additional Hook trigger types via the subsystem envelope per [architecture.md §4.4]; declared triggers are registered at daemon init. Unrecognized trigger fails registration.
close_reason: done

title: Hook ordering is deterministic
description: Per CP-014: when multiple Hooks match a single event, they MUST execute in an order determined by (a) declaring-subsystem priority (explicit integer), (b) within a subsystem, declaration order. The ordering is deterministic and reproducible across daemon restarts given identical registered Hooks.
close_reason: done

title: Hook failures are typed and do not halt the chain
description: Per CP-015: a Hook evaluator MUST return a typed failure descriptor on error; per-Hook failure MUST NOT halt the chain unless `halt_on_failure = true`. Unhandled errors at the evaluator boundary are wrapped by the S05 dispatcher per [handler-contract.md §4.5]: timeout / resource-exhaustion → ErrTransient; schema-violation / type-check / registration-state → ErrDeterministic. The mapping is fixed in S05; this spec names the two error-class surface as the only legal wrap targets.
close_reason: done

title: Hook dispatch is owned by S05; delivery is at-least-once
description: Per CP-016: Hook dispatch (subscribing to event bus, ordering, side-effect application, failure-isolation) MUST be performed by S05 (Hook System) consulting the §4.9 registry. S05 MUST deliver each Hook's side-effect at-least-once. Duplicate delivery is acceptable for `idempotency_class = idempotent`; for `non-idempotent`, S05 MUST bound delivery to at-most-once via persisted delivery-receipt mechanism (S05-owned). This spec names the at-least-once floor and the `idempotency_class` declaration.
close_reason: done

title: Hook evaluator MAY be cognition-tagged
description: Per CP-017: a Hook's evaluator MAY be cognition-tagged (e.g., an `on_review_required` Hook that delegates to a reviewer agent). Cognition-tagged Hook evaluators MUST satisfy the §4.8 replay-safety contract. Delegation path — role (from [architecture.md §4.8]), model class, input shape, response schema — MUST be named on the Hook record.
close_reason: done

title: Guard reorders edges only
description: Per CP-018: a Guard MUST fire during edge evaluation of the deterministic cascade defined in [execution-model.md §4.10]. Evaluator receives candidate edge set + current state + outcome; returns a reordered edge list that is a subset or permutation of input. A Guard MUST NOT add edges, remove edges, or block a transition. Gate semantics (deny / escalate) are NOT available to Guards.

title: Guard precedes the cascade
description: Per CP-019: in the edge-selection cascade of [execution-model.md §4.10 EM-042], Guards MUST run BEFORE the condition cascade. The cascade then operates on the Guard-reordered edge list as input; subsequent precedence rules (condition match, preferred_label, suggested_next_ids, weight, ordering_key) are applied per the execution-model spec.

title: Guard MUST be mechanism-tagged
description: Per CP-020: a Guard's evaluator MUST be mechanism-tagged. Cognition-tagged Guards are forbidden: they would place cognition inside the selection-logic layer, violating ZFC (the Zone of Forbidden Cognition) per [architecture.md §4.2]. Any Guard declaration carrying a cognition-tagged evaluator fails registration.
close_reason: done

title: ControlPoint name is unique within the registry
description: Per CP-002: every ControlPoint MUST carry a unique `name` (String) that identifies it in the §4.9 registry. Policy YAML documents reference ControlPoints by `name`; DOT workflow attributes (`gate_ref`, `policy_ref`, `freedom_profile_ref`, `budget_ref` per [execution-model.md §4.2]) resolve to registered names.

title: Guard invocation is owned by S01
description: Per CP-021: Guard invocation during the cascade MUST be performed by S01 (Orchestrator Core) consulting the §4.9 registry. This spec defines the Guard record + outcome semantics; invocation is S01's obligation under [execution-model.md §4.10].
close_reason: done

title: Budget declares a typed, scoped allowance
description: Per CP-022: a Budget MUST declare `resource ∈ {tokens, wall_clock_seconds, iterations}`, `scope ∈ {per_role, per_run, per_state}`, `limit` (positive integer), `warning_threshold` (ratio in [0, 1]). `warning_threshold` MUST default to 0.8 unless overridden per §4.7. Multiple Budgets MAY apply to a single agent run; tightest applicable Budget wins on any accrual check (smaller integer `limit` for the same `resource`).
close_reason: done

title: Budget is enforced at dispatch
description: Per CP-023: the agent runner MUST check Budget remaining allowance AT DISPATCH (pre-exhaustion). If pending dispatch would exceed remaining limit, runner MUST emit `budget_exhausted` event per [event-model.md §8.4] and DENY dispatch (handler NOT launched). Run failure class MUST be `budget_exhausted` per [execution-model.md §8.5].
notes: no-progress detected at iteration 2: diff hash unchanged
close_reason: done

title: Budget accrual is per-chunk
description: Per CP-024: every agent-output chunk MUST emit a `budget_accrual` event within the same handler tick that produces the chunk (bounded by handler chunk-emission cadence per [handler-contract.md §4.2]). Per-chunk granularity is explicitly retained for MVH per [event-model.md §8.9]; a future log-level filter MAY suppress chunk events at consumer boundaries without changing the emission contract.
close_reason: done

title: Budget warning threshold fires at 80% by default
description: Per CP-025: when cumulative accrual crosses the `warning_threshold` fraction of `limit`, the runner MUST emit `budget_warning` event per [event-model.md §8.4] and continue. The threshold check uses live in-handler counters (handler tracks accrual against remaining budget in real time per its own tick cadence). Threshold value governed by §4.5.CP-022 (default 0.8, operator-overridable per §4.7).
close_reason: done

title: Budget counter state is internal; observable only via events
description: Per CP-026: the Budget counter state MUST be internal to the handler. It MUST NOT be written to any durable store other than through the typed `budget_accrual`, `budget_warning`, and `budget_exhausted` events. Cross-subsystem reads of the counter MUST go through the event bus per [event-model.md §4.3]; there is no `GetBudgetCounter()` surface.
close_reason: done

title: Budget counters rehydrate from JSONL `budget_accrual` replay
description: Per CP-026a: on daemon restart with one or more in-flight runs, Budget counters MUST be rehydrated by replaying `budget_accrual` events from the JSONL event log per [event-model.md §4.4]. Replay begins at the `run_started` event for each in-flight run and consumes every subsequent `budget_accrual` event for that run, summing per-Budget deltas. JSONL event replay is the SOLE authoritative rehydration source; no other durable store (git checkpoint, snapshot file, operator-supplied counter) is legal. Implementations MUST NOT start an in-flight run's handler with a zero counter when prior `budget_accrual` events exist for that run; rehydration is complete BEFORE the handler accepts any dispatch per CP-023.

title: Reconciliation wall-clock budget is an outer bound
description: Per CP-027: reconciliation workflows carry a mandatory wall-clock Budget per [docs/foundation/components.md §9.4a]. That budget MUST register as a Budget instance with `resource = wall_clock_seconds`, `scope = per_run`, `scope_target = <reconciliation_run_id>`. Outer-bound enforcement lives in reconciliation.md §4.4; this spec contributes the composition rule: when a reconciliation wall-clock Budget is active, inner per-role / per-state Budgets MUST NOT extend the effective wall-clock beyond the outer bound. On conflict, the outer Budget wins (admissible-under-inner-but-exceeds-outer-remaining → DENIED with failure class `budget_exhausted` per [execution-model.md §8.5]).

title: Role permission schema
description: Per CP-028: every role declared in a policy document MUST carry a `permission_schema` with fields: `allowed_tools`, `writable_paths`, `readable_paths` (defaults `["**"]`), `model_tier` (optional), `default_skills` (see §4.11), `allowed_hooks`, `invocable_by`. Role name resolution + semantics owned by [architecture.md §4.8].

title: Roles carry concrete default permission sets
description: Per CP-029: for each MVH-required role (Planner, Builder, Reviewer per [architecture.md §4.8]), the policy layer MUST supply a concrete default permission set. Default sets ship at harmonik init; overridable by higher-precedence layers per §4.7.
close_reason: done

title: Evaluator is boundary-classified
description: Per CP-003: every ControlPoint's `evaluator` MUST be classified as `mechanism` or `cognition` per [architecture.md §4.1]. A mechanism-tagged evaluator is a pure deterministic expression over its declared input. A cognition-tagged evaluator delegates to a model with a specified prompt, input shape, and response schema per §4.8.

title: Declared-but-deferred roles carry empty shells
description: Per CP-030: for each declared-but-deferred role (Researcher, Verifier, Scheduler, Governor per [architecture.md §4.8]), the policy layer MUST supply a permission shell with `allowed_tools = []`, `writable_paths = []`, `default_skills = []`. Activating a deferred role in MVH requires a foundation amendment per [architecture.md §4.6]; shell declarations are activation-time-filled.

title: Default skills include the Beads-CLI skill (notes: CP-052)
description:
Per CP-031: every MVH-required role's `default_skills` MUST include the Beads-CLI skill per [docs/foundation/components.md §10.9]. Concrete instance of the general skill-injection pattern in §4.11 + [handler-contract.md §4.11]; roles MAY declare additional defaults; nodes MAY declare additional `required_skills` per [execution-model.md §4.2].

No substantive impl work distinct from CP-031; tag req:CP-052 on this bead.

title: Freedom profile is a per-state constraint bundle
description: Per CP-032: a FreedomProfile MUST carry `tool_whitelist` (intersects role's `allowed_tools`), `writable_paths` (intersects role's `writable_paths`), `model_tier` (optional), `token_budget` (Budget name or inline), `wall_clock_budget` (Budget name or inline), `max_iterations` (positive integer). Freedom profiles compose by intersection per CP-033: tightest applicable profile wins on every field.
close_reason: done

title: Freedom-profile tightest-wins semantics
description: Per CP-033: when multiple freedom profiles apply to a state, the effective profile is the per-field intersection: list-valued → set intersection; integer-valued → smaller value; `model_tier` → less-capable tier per the harmonik-level ordering declared in `_registry.yaml` tier table (MVH ordering: `haiku < sonnet < opus`). Enums without declared ordering are not composable by tightest-wins; both layers MUST declare compatible values or registration fails. Tightest-wins is deterministic and mechanism-tagged.
close_reason: done

title: Policy expressions use the `expr-lang/expr` grammar
description: Per CP-034: the policy-expression grammar MUST be the grammar defined by `expr-lang/expr` (https://github.com/expr-lang/expr). Expressions evaluate against a typed environment: `run` (current Run record per [execution-model.md §4.3]), `outcome` (Outcome record per [execution-model.md §4.1]), `event` (matched event record in Hook context; nil otherwise), `context` (run's shared context map), `policy_meta` (policy document's `metadata` block). Expressions MUST be side-effect-free.

title: Policy expression evaluation cost ceiling (AST-step + wall-clock)
description: Per CP-034b: policy-expression evaluation MUST be performed with a harmonik-level cost ceiling NOT exposed as a policy-level knob. Two bounds: (1) PRIMARY — AST step count (deterministic, per-evaluation AST-visit count ceiling; implementations MUST wrap the evaluator if `expr-lang/expr` does not ship a step-counter; `expr.MaxNodes` is a complementary static bound, NOT a substitute); (2) SECONDARY — wall-clock soft-cap (best-effort backstop via `expr.Timeout`). A pathological expression MUST abort with typed `ErrDeterministic` per [handler-contract.md §4.5]. Abort + event emission are a durability pair: `policy_expression_exceeded_cost` event MUST reach JSONL durability per [event-model.md §4.4] BEFORE the evaluator wrapper returns. On crash between abort + event durability, replayer treats absence as unresolved (re-runs evaluator, relies on cost ceiling to re-abort, emits event on replay). Event payload carries `bound_fired ∈ {ast_steps, wall_clock}` discriminator + per-abort `io_determinism` tag.

title: Policy YAML document required sections
description: Per CP-035: a policy YAML document MUST contain sections `metadata` (with `name`, `version`, `author`, `schema_version`), `roles[]`, `freedom_profiles[]`, `gates[]`, `hooks[]`, `guards[]`, `budgets[]`. Missing any required section is a validation error detected at registration time.
close_reason: done

title: DOT attributes reference policy YAML by name
description: Per CP-036: DOT node + edge attributes `policy_ref`, `gate_ref`, `freedom_profile_ref`, `budget_ref` (per [execution-model.md §4.2]) MUST carry the `name` of a policy element registered per §4.9. DOT attributes MUST NOT embed policy bodies inline. Preserves the three-artifact separation per [architecture.md §4.10] (DOT = graph; YAML = policy; each separate artifact).
close_reason: done

title: Config-loading precedence
description: Per CP-037: policy-source precedence (highest first): (1) runtime override set by operator before launch, (2) operator-policy file, (3) workflow definition (per-workflow overrides), (4) default configuration shipped with harmonik. Resolution is deep-merge with higher-precedence values replacing lower-precedence. A change to a higher-precedence layer takes effect on the next operator pause per [operator-nfr.md §4.3] — there are NO mid-run policy reloads.
close_reason: done

title: Policy schema version is per-document and N-1 readable
description: Per CP-038: every policy YAML document MUST carry a `schema_version` integer in its `metadata` block. Readers MUST accept N-1 (the immediately prior schema version) per [operator-nfr.md §4.5]. Breaking changes require a migration release.
close_reason: done

title: Per-Kind semantics fixed by the §4.1 table (coalesce)
description: Per CP-004 + CP-005 (coalesced per discipline §2.3): the four Kinds' triggers, evaluator inputs, evaluator return types, outcome-action enums, and boundary-classification rules MUST match the §4.1 table. No ControlPoint may deviate from its Kind's row. Table rows: Gate (transition attempt; current state + candidate transition + outcome → {allow, deny}; mechanism OR cognition); Hook (event match; matched event + subscription context → side-effect descriptor; mechanism OR cognition); Guard (edge evaluation; edge set + state + outcome → reordered edge list; mechanism only); Budget (dispatch attempt + per-chunk accrual; counter state + proposed cost + per-chunk delta → {admit, warn, deny}; mechanism only).

title: Cognition-tagged evaluators name the delegation path
description: Per CP-039: a cognition-tagged evaluator (Gate per §4.2, Hook per §4.3) MUST name its delegation path explicitly on the ControlPoint record: invoked role (from [architecture.md §4.8]), model class, input shape (declared input schema), response schema. A reviewer verifies the path at registration; unnamed paths fail registration.

title: Cognition-tagged evaluator verdict is persisted
description: Per CP-040: every cognition-tagged evaluator's verdict MUST be persisted to the run's durable trace at invocation time as `GateVerdictRecord` (Gate) or `HookVerdictRecord` (Hook) per §6.1.6. For Gates, the record is written into the Transition record's `evidence` field per [execution-model.md §4.1] (keyed by `gate_name`) BEFORE the transition advances. For Hooks, the record is written to `.harmonik/hooks/<run_id>/<hook_invocation_id>.json` on the run's task branch per [workspace-model.md §4.2] and emitted as a `hook_verdict_persisted` event per [event-model.md §8.2].
close_reason: done

title: Persisted verdict carries an input-envelope hash
description:
Per CP-040a: every persisted cognition-tagged verdict record MUST include an `input_envelope_hash` field computed deterministically over the evaluator's resolved input envelope. Canonical envelope inputs (5, computed in one cohesive function body per §2.2 F8b — single bead with sub-bullets):
• `expression_text` — full source text of the evaluator's mechanism-tagged expression (nil for pure-cognition evaluators).
• `prompt_template` — resolved prompt template body at the `prompt_template_ref` version pinned on the ControlPoint record (resolved body, not the ref).
• `skill_packages` — set of skill-package identifiers + versions snapshotted from the originating handler launch's `skills_provisioned` event (per CP-050).
• `context_subset` — subset of `run.context` reachable from the expression / prompt template via static AST walk; AST-walk reachability is the default mechanism (workflow-ingest per CP-035 emits a reachable-path set as a first-class by-product). Conservative fallback for non-AST-walkable templating: WHOLE `run.context` (any change busts the hash and escalates to Cat 6 per CP-041). Implementations MUST declare which mode they apply; mixing forbidden within a single ControlPoint.
• `policy_meta` — `metadata` block of the declaring policy document at its registered `schema_version`.
Hash algorithm MUST be SHA-256 over canonicalized JSON serialization in declared order. Hash co-stored with verdict. AST-walker reachability output is part of the replay-safety trust base.
close_reason: done

title: Replay consumes persisted verdict only when envelope hash matches
description: Per CP-041: on replay or reconciliation-driven resume (per [reconciliation/spec.md §4.5]), a cognition-tagged evaluator MUST NOT be re-invoked when a persisted verdict exists for the same `{control_point_name, run_id, transition_id | event_id}` key AND the `input_envelope_hash` recomputed from the current envelope matches the stored hash. On envelope-hash match, replayer MUST read the persisted verdict from git and proceed. On MISMATCH, replayer MUST NOT silently re-invoke; replayer MUST emit a `verdict_envelope_mismatch` event and escalate to a Cat 6 reconciliation verdict path per [reconciliation/spec.md §4.2], the only authority that can authorize re-invocation. The §7.2 pseudocode is the SOLE invocation path for cognition-tagged evaluators across all Kinds. CP-012 is refined: a cognition-tagged Hook's evaluator is invoked directly ONLY on the original launch; every subsequent invocation routes through §7.2.
close_reason: done

title: Verdict persistence is the boundary between mechanism and cognition
description: Per CP-042: persistence of the verdict is mechanism-tagged; production of the verdict is cognition-tagged. The two MUST split into separate operations per [architecture.md §4.1]: cognition-tagged evaluator produces the verdict record (idempotency=idempotent, since LLM is called freshly); mechanism-tagged persister writes it to git (idempotency=non-idempotent). Concrete application of the single-tag rule per spec-template §4.N+1.
close_reason: done

title: Registry is a single in-process table owned by S02
description: Per CP-043: the ControlPoint registry MUST be a single in-process table (Go map keyed by `name`) owned by S02 (Policy Engine). S02 constructs ControlPoint instances by reading policy YAML per §4.7 and calling the registration surface defined in CP-044. The registry is the single source of truth for registered ControlPoints within a daemon.
notes: no_commit_during_implementer: HEAD did not advance past parent 5c61b9cfa8c32c2d47b69d0180ee07a5ebdbcd28 at iteration 1 exit=0
close_reason: Code on main; daemon reopened after merge-conflict on cherry-picked commit

title: Registration surface (re-registration-safe on identical body)
description: Per CP-044: S02 MUST expose the Registry interface declared in §6.1.7. Registration is re-registration-safe on identical body: registering with already-registered `name` + identical body succeeds silently; different body under existing `name` MUST fail at startup with a specific error code. Body for equality purposes = `(kind, trigger, evaluator, payload)`; `name`, `axes`, `schema_version` are NOT part of the body (a schema-version bump on an otherwise-identical ControlPoint MUST NOT reject as divergent). Body equality computed over canonicalized serialization (field-order-independent, whitespace-normalized). Subsystems register ControlPoints during daemon init via subsystem envelope per [architecture.md §4.4].
close_reason: done

title: Registry scope is daemon-local
description: Per CP-045: the registry MUST be daemon-scoped (process-scoped per [architecture.md §4.4]). Cross-daemon sharing MUST NOT occur; every per-project daemon maintains its own independent registry. Daemon restart rebuilds the registry from policy YAML as a startup step.
close_reason: done

title: Registry lookups are deterministic
description: Per CP-046: all registry lookups declared in §6.1.7 (`LookupByName`, `LookupByTrigger`, `LookupByAttachPoint`) MUST be deterministic: identical registry state + identical query inputs produce identical outputs. List-returning lookups MUST apply a total ordering (by `name` ascending) before returning so iteration order is reproducible across Go runtime versions. The registry MUST NOT incorporate nondeterministic inputs (wall-clock time, PID, exposed map iteration order).
close_reason: done

title: S02 owns the registry; S05 owns Hook dispatch; S01 owns Gate + Guard invocation
description: Per CP-047: S02 (Policy Engine) owns the §4.9 registry + registration path. S05 (Hook System) owns the Hook dispatch loop (subscribing, ordering per CP-014, side-effect application, failure isolation); S05 consults the registry but does NOT own it. S01 (Orchestrator Core) owns Gate + Guard invocation during the edge cascade (per [execution-model.md §4.10]); S01 consults the registry but does NOT own it. No other subsystem may invoke a ControlPoint except through these three owner paths.
close_reason: done

title: Gate fires on transition attempt
description: Per CP-006: a Gate MUST fire on a transition attempt. The evaluator receives current state, candidate transition (after edge cascade selects it or before, per attach-point), and the handler outcome. Returns `allow` or `deny` with optional human-readable `reason`; outcome-action MAY be elevated to `escalate-to-human` by policy.
close_reason: Subsumed: gate node dispatch landed 4a74c132 (+b204fe70 deny->SUCCESS); internal/handler gate tests green. EV-002b boundary concern its gate_dispatch.go raised is resolved separately by hk-mboii (e4327eae). Main verified green 2026-05-31.

title: Control-point effects are observable only via events
description: Per CP-048: every ControlPoint effect (Gate allow/deny/escalate, Hook side-effect, Guard reorder/error, Budget admit/warn/deny) MUST emit a typed event per [event-model.md §8]: `gate_allowed`, `gate_denied`, `gate_escalated`, `hook_fired`, `hook_failed`, `guard_reordered`, `guard_failed`, `budget_accrual`, `budget_warning`, `budget_exhausted`. No ControlPoint outcome may cross a subsystem boundary by any path other than a typed event.
close_reason: Landed: cherry-picked as d9c1e44

title: Nodes MAY declare `required_skills` (ingest-time syntactic validity)
description: Per CP-049: a DOT workflow node MAY declare `required_skills` as (a) a comma-separated list of skill names as a DOT attribute value, or (b) a YAML policy reference via `policy_ref` naming a skill set declared in the §6.3 `skill_sets[]` block. Declared set is checked at workflow-ingest time per [execution-model.md §4.9] validation. Two fail-points: ingest-time syntactic validity (this spec) — every declared skill name MUST match the skill-name shape defined in [handler-contract.md §4.11]; syntactic violations fail workflow-ingest (`ErrDeterministic`). Launch-time package resolution (handler-contract) — a syntactically-valid name that does not resolve to an installed package produces `ErrSkillProvisioningFailed`. Declaration-time is this spec's surface; resolution-time is handler-contract's. Covering partition.
close_reason: done

title: Effective skill set is the union of node-level and role-default skills
description: Per CP-050: the effective skill set for an agent launched into a node MUST be the set-union of (a) the node's declared `required_skills` and (b) the assigned role's `default_skills` per CP-028. This spec owns the UNION computation. Consumption — resolution against available skill packages, provisioning into agent process shape, emission of `skills_provisioned` event, fail-launch on resolution failure — is owned entirely by [handler-contract.md §4.11]; CP-050 does not re-specify those mechanics.
close_reason: done

title: Skill declaration is mechanism-tagged
description: Per CP-051: skill declaration, resolution, and provisioning MUST be mechanism-tagged: no cognition participates in determining the effective skill set. The skill contents themselves (prompts, tool definitions packaged inside a skill) MAY be consumed by cognition at runtime, but the pipeline from declaration to provisioning is deterministic.

title: Sensor: registry is the single source of truth
description: Per CP-INV-001: every ControlPoint observable by the daemon MUST be resolvable through the §4.9 registry. No subsystem may maintain a private ControlPoint store, a shadow registry, or a lazy-resolved ControlPoint surface. Divergence between the registry and any subsystem-local cache is a structural invariant violation. Sensor: §10.2 group CP-043..CP-046 — invariant test asserts registry-lookup uniqueness via cross-subsystem inspection (no shadow store).
close_reason: done

title: Sensor: control-point effects are observable only through events
description: Per CP-INV-002: every ControlPoint effect that crosses a subsystem boundary MUST be observable through one of the typed events declared in CP-048. No ControlPoint outcome may be communicated across subsystems by shared memory, direct method call across an owner boundary, or out-of-band signal. Sensor: §10.2 group CP-047..CP-048 — cross-subsystem tests verifying no ControlPoint effect crosses a boundary by any path other than a typed event.
close_reason: done

title: Sensor: cognition-tagged evaluators never silently re-invoke during replay
description: Per CP-INV-003: for every cognition-tagged evaluator invocation that has a persisted verdict on the run's task branch with a matching `input_envelope_hash` per CP-040a, replay MUST consume the persisted verdict. On envelope-hash mismatch, replay MUST escalate to Cat 6 rather than re-invoke silently. Re-invocation during replay is permitted ONLY under an explicit Cat 6 reconciliation verdict per [reconciliation/spec.md §4.2]. Any replay path that silently re-invokes a model — whether because the verdict is missing, the envelope has drifted, or the replayer ignored the hash check — violates this invariant. Sensor: §10.2 group CP-039..CP-042 — replay-safety harness tests (envelope-hash determinism; hash-match-replay-reads-persisted; hash-mismatch-Cat-6-escalation; Cat-6 stale-verdict re-invocation).
close_reason: done

title: session-keeper Phase-2: PreCompact backstop hook (block lossy auto-compaction -> trigger cycle)
description:
Parent epic hk-ekap1, codename:session-keeper (attach via label, NOT a parent dep). Builds on the cycle core (hk-22i70) + watcher + the Stop-hook pattern (scripts/keeper-stop-hook.sh, hk-djdng).

PURPOSE: Claude Code's PreCompact hook fires BEFORE auto-compaction and CAN BLOCK it (decision:block / exit 2). This is the BACKSTOP for when the gauge-threshold cycle did not fire in time (e.g. one huge turn jumps from below act_pct straight into auto-compaction). For a MANAGED session we want to block the lossy native compaction and instead run the keeper's intent-preserving cycle (handoff->clear->resume).

SAFETY (MANDATORY, same as hk-22i70): destructive path; (a) only managed agents (.harmonik/keeper/<agent>.managed); (b) tests FAKES only, never touch a real session.

MECHANISM: ship scripts/keeper-precompact-hook.sh (mirror keeper-stop-hook.sh). On invocation:
 - If the session is NOT managed (no .managed marker for the resolved agent): FAIL-OPEN — exit 0, allow native compaction. NEVER block compaction on an unmanaged session.
 - If managed: signal the keeper to run a cycle (write a marker the keeper watcher picks up, e.g. .harmonik/keeper/<agent>.precompact, and/or emit an event the keeper consumes) and return decision:block (exit 2) to prevent the native compaction.

OPEN DESIGN QUESTION the implementer MUST resolve (call it out in the commit + for the reviewer): the hook runs synchronously inside the claude process but the keeper is a separate async poller. If the hook blocks compaction but the keeper then CANNOT cycle (agent mid-dispatch / NOT CrispIdle / HoldingDispatch), the session is stuck: compaction blocked AND no cycle. Resolve with a bounded fallback — e.g. only block when the keeper can confirm it will cycle, or block at most once then fail-open on the next PreCompact so the session is never permanently wedged. Document the chosen policy.

WIRE-UP: document hook registration in the keeper subcommand / SPEC.md; opt-in via the same .managed guard.

EVENTS: session_keeper_precompact_blocked (+ any needed) in eventtype.go (+register +pertypecompat +payload type), emit via FileEmitter.

DONE: go build ./... + vet + gofmt clean; hook script passes shellcheck if available; tests FAKES-only; SPEC.md documents the hook + the can't-cycle fallback policy. Refs the bead ID in the commit.
close_reason: done

The HC and SH spec families enumerate normative requirements *about* twins (HC-035..HC-040 parity surface; HC-043/HC-045 launch-rule conformance; SH-008..SH-011 substitution + discovery; SH-INV-003 twin-only sensor) and have corpus beads tracking them — but NONE of those beads author the actual cmd/harmonik-twin-claude/ Go program. Per [docs/bootstrap.md §5 step 4] the twin is a Phase-1 hand-build task ('ship the twin alongside the handler'). This mini-epic groups the concrete code-existence tasks that complement hk-8i31.77 (which describes the binary's contract but not its construction) and unblock SH §10.1 conformance scenarios. All children carry scope:bootstrap so they are not lost in the untagged-527 gap.

Reviewer APPROVE — found bead body's LaunchSpec.SocketPath wording is stale (spec §6.1 LaunchSpec has no socket-path field; HC-044 fixes path at .harmonik/daemon.sock). Implementer chose --socket-path CLI flag (correct: socket path is launch-time arg, not LaunchSpec payload); inline-amend cleaned the perpetuating comment in main.go to cite HC-044 directly.

title: Wire -ldflags commitHash stamp into Makefile build-twin-claude target
description: Wire -ldflags "-X main.commitHash=\$(git rev-parse HEAD)" into Makefile build-twin-claude target so make-built twin binaries carry the embedded commit hash that VerifyCommitHash (internal/handler/commithash.go) scans for. The producer-side var is in cmd/harmonik-twin-claude/version.go (hk-ahvq.48.4, closed); the consumer is internal/handler/commithash.go (hk-8i31.50, closed); only the build-invocation glue is missing. Surfaced from hk-ahvq.48.4 review (orchestrator close note 2026-05-08): hk-ahvq.48.5 closed without the -ldflags wiring, leaving an orphan. Coordinate with the OPEN hk-uwie integration test which exercises a real ldflags-stamped binary.
close_reason: Makefile build-twin-claude target now wires -ldflags -X main.commitHash=$(git rev-parse HEAD); twins/claude-twin --version emits 40-char HEAD SHA. Verified live build, twins+build-all targets unaffected, gofmt-clean. Reviewed APPROVE no flags.

title: SPEC-EDIT: define twin script-file format normatively in HC §4.8 or SH §4.3
description: The de-facto twin script schema authored in hk-ahvq.48.3 (cmd/harmonik-twin-claude/scriptdriver.go) needs a normative home. Propose a minimal normative section in specs/handler-contract.md §4.8 (or specs/scenario-harness.md §4.3) that defines: (1) YAML file location: <fixture-root>/<scenario>/twin-scripts/<role>.yaml; (2) top-level fields: heartbeat_mode (string enum: wall_clock|scripted; default: wall_clock), messages (list of ScriptMessage records); (3) ScriptMessage record: type (string, required), payload (map, optional), relative_timestamp_ms (int, optional; scripted heartbeat_mode only). The de-facto schema is documented in the package godoc of cmd/harmonik-twin-claude/scriptdriver.go (bead hk-ahvq.48.3). This bead is a spec-edit to make it normative. Blocks: hk-ahvq.48.6, hk-ahvq.48.8.
close_reason: Implemented in 3d141e9 (rebased): added normative HC-036a 'Twin script-file format' to specs/handler-contract.md (file-path rule, YAML schema, ScriptMessage record, heartbeat-mode semantics, validation reqs); spec v0.3.4. Reviewer APPROVE (one MINOR: HC-036a Validation (c) empty-type rejection is spec-ahead-of-code — follow-up impl bead implied by §10.2).

title: Twin wire-protocol parity loop: NDJSON over Unix socket per HC-007/HC-007a/HC-008
description: Inside cmd/harmonik-twin-claude/, implement the progress-stream emitter that writes NDJSON-framed messages over the Unix-domain-socket connection to the daemon-side watcher per [specs/handler-contract.md §4.2.HC-007] (progress-stream message types) and §4.2.HC-007a (NDJSON framing) and §4.2.HC-008 (outcome-delivery rule). Twin must emit exactly the same wire-protocol message types as a real claude-code handler — agent_started, skills_provisioned, agent_ready (per HC-040), heartbeat (per HC-026a scripted-mode carve-out), agent_log, agent_completed | agent_failed (per HC-024). Mechanism-tag emission per HC-037 must match real-handler tags. This is the per-process implementation of the parity surface declared at [specs/handler-contract.md §4.8] (HC-035..HC-040); the abstract-bead set hk-8i31.42, .43, .47, .59, .44 declare the requirements; this bead authors the code path that satisfies them inside the canonical twin binary.
close_reason: cmd/harmonik-twin-claude/wire.go (433+ lines) NDJSON-over-Unix-socket emitter for twin parity per HC-007/HC-007a/HC-008+HC-035..HC-040+HC-049: 12 message-type emitters (handler_capabilities, session_log_location, skills_provisioned, agent_ready, agent_started, agent_heartbeat, agent_output_chunk, agent_rate_limited, agent_rate_limit_cleared, agent_completed, agent_failed, outcome_emitted) + 6 HC-026a heartbeat phases + 1 MiB scanner buffer for forward-compat. wire_test.go 32+ tests with twinWireFixture prefix. Reviewed REQUEST_CHANGES (3 missing emitters per HC-007 + 1 unused type + 2 io.EOF errorlint hits); fix-iteration added 3 emitters, deleted wireMsg (HC-007a confirmed flat JSON), replaced != io.EOF with errors.Is. Re-reviewed APPROVE no flags. agent_log NOT added because HC-007 doesn't enumerate it (spec wins per CLAUDE.md).

title: Twin script-driver: read fixture file + emit scripted message stream
description: Inside cmd/harmonik-twin-claude/, implement the script-driver loop that reads a per-scenario script file (path supplied via LaunchSpec env or flag) and emits the declared message stream onto the wire-protocol path. Per [specs/handler-contract.md §4.6 scenario-mode carve-out]: 'A twin subprocess per §4.8 running against a scenario script MAY emit heartbeats at explicit scripted relative timestamps bypassing the T/2 wall-clock timer, so that scenario tests produce byte-reproducible event streams. The carve-out is limited to the canonical twin binary and MUST be declared on the script (e.g., heartbeat_mode: scripted).' Per [specs/handler-contract.md §4.8.HC-036]: 'the subprocess script drives output instead of an LLM.' SPEC-GAP NOTE: neither HC nor SH currently defines the script-file schema (filename, on-disk format, path-resolution rule). This bead's first sub-task is to propose a minimal script-file schema (suggested: YAML at <fixture-root>/<scenario>/twin-scripts/<role>.yaml with a list-of-message records each carrying type, payload, and optional relative-timestamp); a follow-up spec-edit bead (see hk-ahvq.NN spec-gap bead) authors the normative section. Until that spec lands, the script schema authored here is the de-facto contract.
close_reason: Implemented in 0759061 (rebased): twin script-driver YAML schema + runScript loop honoring HC-026a/§4.6 scripted-heartbeat carve-out + --script-path flag. Follow-up spec-edit bead hk-ahvq.48.11 created. Reviewer APPROVE.

title: Twin binary commit-hash stamp via build-time ldflags (HC-043 satisfaction)
description: Inside cmd/harmonik-twin-claude/, embed the source commit-hash at build time via -ldflags '-X main.commitHash=...' and expose it via a process-supplied surface (CLI --version OR an internal LaunchSpec self-report) so that the daemon's pre-launch HC-043 commit-hash check passes. Per [specs/handler-contract.md §4.10.HC-043]: 'in-repo handler binaries (including twins) MUST verify against an expected commit-hash.' Per [specs/handler-contract.md §4.10.HC-045]: 'Twin binaries MUST be launched from a known repo-relative path with expected-commit-hash check. The twin's expected commit hash MUST be pinned at workflow/policy configuration time.' This bead is the twin-binary side; the daemon-side check is at hk-8i31.50 (commit-hash check for in-repo binaries) and hk-sx9r.7 (Commit-hash source via build-time ldflags stamp — daemon-scoped). Pattern parity with hk-sx9r.7: same -ldflags mechanism, applied to the twin's main package's commitHash variable.
close_reason: Landed: cmd/harmonik-twin-claude/version.go declares 'var commitHash string' as the package-level ldflags target; --version flag prints versionLine() (with '(unstamped)' fallback) and exits 0 per HC-043 system-handler convention. 5 tests cover settable-var + version-line shapes. Reviewer APPROVE with two MINOR follow-ups: (1) hk-ahvq.48.5 closed without wiring -ldflags into the Makefile build-twin-claude target — filed follow-up bead this session; (2) hk-uwie integration test has no formal dep edge — soft prose linkage retained, will be picked up in normal scheduling now that producer-side stamp is live.

title: Makefile target + build artifact placement for twins/ search-path
description: Author the Makefile target that builds cmd/harmonik-twin-claude/ to <repo-root>/twins/claude-twin (or equivalent path) so that the harness's default twin-binary search path resolves it. Per [specs/scenario-harness.md §4.3.SH-009]: 'twin-search-path source precedence: (i) CLI --twin-search-path, (ii) env HARMONIK_TWIN_SEARCH_PATH, (iii) in-tree default <repo-root>/twins/.' Per [specs/handler-contract.md §4.8.HC-036(c)]: binary name '<real>-twin' (e.g., claude-twin) — the build target's output filename must match. Per [docs/foundation/project-level/subsystem-organization.md §Go module layout]: cmd/harmonik-twin-claude/ is the source root. This bead also adds the target to the higher-level Makefile dispatch (e.g., make twins, make build-all) — wire it into the build-scaffolding deliverable named at [docs/foundation/phase-1-readiness-gap-analysis.md §B2 / p1-build-makefile recommendation]. Coordinate with the separate Makefile-scaffolding bead when that lands; if it lands first, add the twin target there.
close_reason: Landed (2-commit, fix-iteration): Makefile build-twin-claude/twins/build-all targets producing twins/claude-twin (HC-036(c) <real>-twin form); twins/.gitkeep + /twins/* gitignore (added in fix). Bonus: ResolveTwinSearchPath in internal/handler/launchpath.go implementing SH-009 three-tier precedence (CLI > env HARMONIK_TWIN_SEARCH_PATH > <repoRoot>/twins) — judged scope-sound (no sibling owns the resolver; producer-side prerequisite for the bead's stated 'harness's default twin-binary search path resolves it'). Reviewer REQUEST_CHANGES on missing gitignore for build artifact (BLOCKER); orchestrator inline-amend added /twins/* + !/twins/.gitkeep; re-review skipped per pure-addition fix. Deferred MINOR: $(PWD) → $(CURDIR) in TWINS_DIR + TOOLS_DIR (consistency cleanup, future bead).

title: Author scenarios/smoke/twin-launch-and-ready.yaml + twin script (first conformance scenario)
description: Author the first end-to-end conformance scenario named at [specs/scenario-harness.md §10.1]: 'scenarios/smoke/twin-launch-and-ready.yaml — a minimal one-node workflow that launches a single twin, observes agent_ready per [handler-contract.md §4.9.HC-039], emits agent_completed, and reaches a clean terminal state. Asserts event_present(agent_ready), event_present(agent_completed), exit_code(SUCCESS).' Deliverables: (a) the scenario YAML conforming to the SH §6.1 ScenarioFile schema; (b) the per-scenario twin script (per the schema established in hk-ahvq.48.3); (c) any fixture_setup.git_seed / fixture_setup.files entries needed to mint a one-node DOT workflow at the synthetic project root per SH-016a; (d) a scenarios/_workflows/ shared workflow definition if appropriate. Acceptance: the scenario runs against the built twin and produces verdict=pass. This is the [docs/bootstrap.md §1 acceptance test] floor — without one runnable conformance scenario, the §1 test cannot pass. The other two §10.1 scenarios (smoke/checkpoint-and-merge.yaml, regression/twin-failure-classification.yaml) are tracked by separate beads (hk-ahvq.48.7 and hk-ahvq.48.8).
close_reason: Landed 38fed9a: scenario YAML + shared DOT workflow + twin script in fixture_setup.files + corpus parse test. All tests pass.

title: Author scenarios/smoke/checkpoint-and-merge.yaml + twin script (second conformance scenario)
description: Author the second §10.1 conformance scenario named at [specs/scenario-harness.md §10.1]: 'scenarios/smoke/checkpoint-and-merge.yaml — a workflow that drives one node to terminal success, emits a checkpoint per [execution-model.md §4.4.EM-016], and merges the task branch per [workspace-model.md §4.5.WM-019] (squash-merge contract) emitting workspace_merge_status per [workspace-model.md §4.5.WM-021]. Asserts event_present(checkpoint_written), event_present(workspace_merge_status{status=pending}), event_present(workspace_merge_status{status=merged}), workspace_state(commit_trailer_present, Harmonik-Run-ID).' Required for §1 acceptance test floor.

title: Author scenarios/regression/twin-failure-classification.yaml (third conformance scenario)
description: Author the third §10.1 conformance scenario named at [specs/scenario-harness.md §10.1]: 'scenarios/regression/twin-failure-classification.yaml — a scenario where the twin is scripted to crash without outcome_emitted, exercising the ErrStructural classification per [handler-contract.md §4.6.HC-024] and the exactly-one-terminal-event invariant of [handler-contract.md §5 HC-INV-006]. Asserts event_present(agent_failed{error_category=ErrStructural}), event_absent(outcome_emitted), exit_code(FAIL).' Twin script must terminate the subprocess after partial output emission (no outcome_emitted, then exit non-zero) so the daemon-side watcher classifies as ErrStructural. Required for §1 acceptance-test floor — without it, the harness's failure-classification path is untested. Builds on hk-ahvq.48.3 (script-driver must support an explicit 'exit_unexpectedly' directive).
close_reason: Landed 8a261ae: twin-failure-classification YAML (cadence=regression) with crash twin script, ErrStructural + event_absent assertions. All tests pass.

title: SPEC-EDIT: define twin script-file format normatively in HC §4.8 or SH §4.3
description: Spec-gap follow-up identified during the twin-binary gap-fill pass (2026-05-05). The handler-contract §4.6 scenario-mode carve-out and §4.8.HC-036 reference the existence of 'a scenario script' that drives twin output ('the subprocess script drives output instead of an LLM'; 'heartbeat_mode: scripted ... declared on the script'); [specs/scenario-harness.md §4.3] references 'twin-binary search path' and 'agent_overrides' (the SELECTOR for which twin to launch) but neither spec defines the on-disk schema, filename convention, or path-resolution rule for the script-file the twin reads to produce its scripted output stream. hk-ahvq.48.3 will establish a de-facto schema in code (suggested: YAML at <fixture-root>/<scenario>/twin-scripts/<role>.yaml); this bead is to lift that schema to a normative §6.1 record (e.g., TwinScript) in either HC or SH (architectural call: HC owns wire-protocol parity, SH owns scenario-file structure — likely SH §4.3 or §6.1 since the script is a scenario fixture, not a handler-contract artifact). Author after hk-ahvq.48.3 lands so the spec is grounded in working code; do not block .48.3 on this.
close_reason: DUPLICATE of hk-ahvq.48.11 (created 2026-05-10 by hk-ahvq.48.3 implementer who didn't know about .48.9). Both have identical title 'SPEC-EDIT: define twin script-file format normatively in HC §4.8 or SH §4.3'. Implementation landed under .48.11 in commit 3d141e9 (HC-036a). Closing as duplicate.

title: DOGFOOD-BLOCKER: daemon paste-injects task to STALE pane (prior-session lastHandle pollution)
description:
2026-05-18 v50 dogfood attempt of harmonik run --beads hk-x0y2k --max-concurrent 1 hung silently for 7+ minutes. Investigation: daemon spawned a fresh tmux window (pane %27) for the new run, then paste-injected the agent-task content to pane %22 — a STALE pane from a 24-hour-old v49 retry session that already exited. The new claude in %27 sat at an empty prompt forever waiting for a task that never arrived.

Daemon log evidence: 'INFO daemon_pane_write session_id=019e3d97-c6fe-7a3d-9f49-fc3a73d684dc pane_target=%22 buffer_name=harmonik-019e3d97-c6fe-7a3d-9f49-fc3a73d684dc-task'. The session_id matches the new run (019e3d97-...), but pane_target points to the OLD pane (%22). The newly-spawned pane %27 was correct in window 7 with the right worktree path.

This is the hk-2hb2y-class substrate-target bug. hk-2hb2y wired Substrate on implSpec+revSpec. But it doesn't appear the SpawnWindow's returned lastHandle is the one consulted for the FIRST paste-inject of the task. Possibly:
(a) Daemon caches lastHandle across runs (stale from v49 daemon, leaked into v50 daemon via tmux session state).
(b) substrate.SpawnWindow returns the wrong handle.
(c) lastHandle is updated only AFTER paste-inject, so the FIRST paste-inject uses the prior value.

Investigation needed: read internal/daemon/pasteinject.go + handler.SubstrateSession.SpawnWindow + how lastHandle propagates from SpawnWindow → first paste. Compare with the hk-2hb2y substrate-wiring fix at a7bcd49 to see if there's a residual gap.

Severity: BLOCKS dogfood completely. P0. Tagged half-built-systems because the substrate path is unit-tested but breaks at runtime in this scenario (stale-pane pollution).

Repro: leave a stale tmux window from a prior harmonik run in the harmonik tmux session, then 'harmonik run --beads <id>'. Compare daemon log pane_target= with the actual fresh-pane id from SpawnWindow.
close_reason: Atomic pane-id capture landed: <SHA>; tmux -P -F %{pane_id} replaces slash-bearing window-name lookup that returned stale %22

title: Merge still fails: claude dirties tracked .claude/settings.json in worktree → rebase aborts (discardDirtyBeadsLedger too narrow)
description:
After the .beads ledger fix (hk-i1n7j, ceeae33), a clean live DOT re-run of hk-3yz2d STILL fails at merge with the identical error: 'merge-failed (dot): rebase_conflict: cannot rebase: You have unstaged changes'. The graph walk completes (start→implementer commit→reviewer APPROVE→close→outcome_emitted) but mergeRunBranchToMain's git rebase aborts.

ROOT CAUSE: .claude/settings.json is a TRACKED file (git ls-files confirms; .gitignore only ignores /.claude/worktrees/ and /harmonik-twin-claude). claude MUTATES it on launch — the first hung run's worktree showed ' M .claude/settings.json'. The implementer commits only its task work (the marker file), leaving .claude/settings.json modified-unstaged. discardDirtyBeadsLedger (workloop.go) was deliberately narrow — it discards ONLY .beads/issues.jsonl — so .claude/settings.json (and any other agent/daemon runtime churn) still blocks the rebase.

EVIDENCE: error persists after ceeae33; .claude/settings.json tracked + not ignored + dirtied-by-claude (first-run worktree status). The reviewer of ceeae33 noted an existing isHarmonikChurn allowlist (workloop.go:2757) that already classifies expected churn.

GENERALITY: mergeRunBranchToMain is shared by all modes (single/review-loop/DOT) per hk-i1n7j analysis — so this likely blocks completion of EVERY real run where claude mutates settings.json, not just DOT. Investigator to confirm whether builtin recently completed real merges (vs stub-handler tests).

FIX DIRECTION: generalize the pre-rebase cleanup to discard ALL files matching isHarmonikChurn (incl. .claude/settings.json), not just .beads/issues.jsonl — preserving 'unexpected file blocks rebase' for genuine implementer escapes. Build on ceeae33, do not revert it.

ACCEPTANCE: clean live 'harmonik run --workflow-mode dot --workflow-ref specs/examples/review-loop.dot --beads <throwaway>' completes: merge succeeds, run_completed, bead CLOSED. Source: QA 2026-05-27/28, live runs of hk-3yz2d (3 attempts).
close_reason: Fixed (5c8a8c3) + proven: discardDirtyChurn generalized pre-rebase cleanup to the isHarmonikChurn allowlist (.claude/settings.json etc.). Live DOT run of hk-3yz2d completed end-to-end: start→implementer→reviewer APPROVE→close→merge→push→CLOSED (02868b0). First fully-complete live DOT run.

title: Daemon should idle/exit after target bead completes; don't cascade to next ready
description: During the hk-iuaed.6 dogfood, after the targeted bead completed, the daemon immediately claimed hk-do7te and started a second claude session. Makes 'tearing down the run' non-obvious. Need either `--one-shot` flag or `harmonik run <id>` semantics that exit on first terminal transition. Discovered: hk-iuaed.6 dogfood, 2026-05-15.
close_reason: SUBSUMED by hk-icecw: harmonik run <bead-id> uses a queue-of-one which terminates naturally on completion via CompleteAndUnlink + CancelOnQueueDrain in evaluateGroupAdvanceWithOutcome. No cascade to next ready bead is possible because the queue is cleared on drain.

title: Phase-2 dogfood: claude crashed exit=-1 in reasoning phase after 10min (hk-6x7dw dogfood)
description: 2026-05-20 Go-touching dogfood probe RED. Target: hk-6x7dw (EV-036 violation: StaleVerdictPayload.SnapshotToken). harmonik run --beads hk-6x7dw (single-bead, --max-concurrent 1). Run failed after ~10min wall-clock: claude_crashed exit=-1, class=structural. Agent_heartbeats at 21:18 and 21:23 both show phase=reasoning — agent stalled in reasoning phase for 10min then crashed. No agent_output_chunk events observed; agent_failed → run_failed → auto-reopen path fired. queue.json left active. Logs: /tmp/dogfood-v51-go-redux.log; events filtered tail in commit context. This is a SEPARATE failure mode from hk-wx8z8 (parallelism pane collision) — single-concurrency, but claude failed to make progress. Possibly: (a) hk-6x7dw bead body is too vague (regex/scanner work needs more context), (b) claude --model sonnet --effort medium can't handle this class of bead, (c) handler-side issue (sub-agent crashed somehow). Required: investigate exit=-1 path, capture stderr/stdout from the claude child, determine if escalating effort or rewording bead body resolves.
close_reason: done

title: eventbus ScanAfter silently truncates on >64KiB lines -> comms recv/log/who stop delivering after an oversized line
description: From agent-comms retro-review. jsonlwriter.go:323 ScanAfter uses a bare bufio.NewScanner (64KiB default token cap). A single events.jsonl line exceeding 64KiB makes Scan() stop with ErrTooLong (logged, then the iterator yields nothing further). For comms recv this silently undelivers ALL events after the oversized line (cursor only advances to last-delivered, so not lost permanently, but recv appears stalled). Comms' own agent_message is capped 8KiB so it won't self-trigger, but the events log is SHARED with all event types -> a large non-comms payload upstream truncates the comms scan. Pre-existing in ScanAfter (hk-a5sil), but comms recv/log/who are now consumers that depend on it. FIX: replace bufio.NewScanner with bufio.Reader.ReadBytes('\n') (no token cap) in ScanAfter/Filter, or raise the buffer via scanner.Buffer(...).
close_reason: done

title: structuredlog Handler.clone() copies the sync.Mutex (lock-copy): go vet fails on main + concurrent log-corruption risk
description: go vet ./... FAILS on main: internal/structuredlog/handler.go:277 'assignment copies lock value to h2: Handler contains sync.Mutex'. clone() does h2 := *h, shallow-copying the Handler INCLUDING its sync.Mutex. Clones share h.file (pointer copy), so the original and each clone (made via WithAttrs/WithGroup) write to the SAME file under SEPARATE copied mutexes -> no mutual exclusion -> interleaved/corrupted concurrent writes. Introduced by e74ef2d3 (ON-035 NDJSON wire format, hk-sx9r.51). FIX: clones must SHARE one lock guarding the shared writer (move file+mu into a shared inner struct referenced by pointer, OR make mu a pointer shared across clones); do NOT give each clone its own copied mutex. Verify: go vet ./... clean + a concurrent-write test. REDEPLOY RISK: if the new post-merge build gate (hk-o68j3) runs go vet, this blocks all merges after the next daemon redeploy — fix before activating that gate. Found via accidental backtick command-substitution in a br description.
close_reason: done

title: F-T2-02: ReopenBead carries no failure reason; bead history opaque after handler failure
description:
## Repro
After twin-fail exits 1 and ReopenBead is called:
  br show <bead-id> --format json   # status=open; no failure_reason field
  cat .harmonik/events/events.jsonl | grep run_failed  # has summary='auto-reopen: exit=1'

## Expected
br show should surface why a bead was reopened (exit code, run_id) without requiring the operator to grep the JSONL log.

## Actual
brcli.Adapter.ReopenBead issues plain 'br reopen <bead-id>' with no --reason flag. The failure context (exit code, run_id, summary) exists only in the JSONL run_failed event.

## Code location
internal/brcli/terminaltransition_bi010.go:229 — args are ["reopen", beadID] only.
internal/daemon/workloop.go:281 — caller constructs no reason string.

## Test evidence
TestT2_RunFailedEventContainsExitCode: run_failed payload has summary='auto-reopen: exit=1' (JSONL only).
close_reason: ReopenBead(reason string) added; br reopen --reason passed at all 4 call sites in workloop

title: harmonik run --beads creates wave groups not appendable — append UX requires explicit queue.json with kind:stream
description: Discovered while attempting to extend a running batch with more beads. The daemon's queue (created by 'harmonik run --beads ...') has groups with kind=wave. 'harmonik queue append <group> <beads...>' fails with append_target_invalid (code -32011) because validation requires kind=stream (validation.go:285). The error is correct per spec (QM-024) but the UX gap is: there is no way to extend a running 'harmonik run' batch with more beads — the user must wait for the wave to drain, then submit a new queue or rerun 'harmonik run'. Options: (a) add 'harmonik run --kind stream' flag so users opt into appendable batches; (b) make 'harmonik run --beads' default to stream so append always works; (c) document the limitation in --help and the error detail. The error response already includes actual_kind=wave — surface that hint to operator. Source: dogfood session 2026-05-21.
close_reason: Subsumed: resolved by hk-7nbey (stream-default)

title: Run executes one workflow against one input
description: Per EM-012: `Run` carries `run_id`, `workflow_id`, `workflow_version`, `input` (workspace ref), current `state`, `context`, `start_time`, optional `end_time`. Exactly one workflow invocation against exactly one input; multi-workflow / multi-input runs not permitted. Transition discovery via task-branch commit range with `Harmonik-Run-ID` trailer.

title: Run ID propagates as commit trailer and event field
description: Per EM-013: `run_id` MUST appear as `Harmonik-Run-ID` trailer on every checkpoint commit (per §4.4) AND as `run_id` field on every run-scoped event. Join key across git, Beads, JSONL.

title: Bead-to-run relationship is many-runs-per-bead
description: Per EM-014: `Run` MAY tie to a bead via `bead_id`. A bead MAY have zero, one, or many runs across its lifetime; new run after fundamental failure (crash / unrecoverable / `reopen-bead` verdict) gets fresh worktree + branch.

Worktree/branch lifecycle out of scope — separate subsystem. Pre-claim dep conversion: blocks-on-closed hk-b3f.75/.70 → related. Reviewer APPROVE-CLEAN. make check green.

title: Intra-run loops are not new runs
description: Per EM-015: a workflow edge routing back to an earlier node is a run-internal loop, NOT a new run. `run_id` stable across loop traversals; task branch continues to accumulate checkpoints (one per durable transition per §4.5). Re-runs ONLY after fundamental failure per EM-014.

title: run_started emission on dispatch
description: Per EM-015a: daemon MUST emit `run_started` AFTER `create_run` allocates `run_id` AND AFTER Beads atomic-claim persists AND BEFORE any node dispatched. Payload carries `run_id`, `workflow_id`, `workflow_version`, optional `bead_id`. Crash between `run_id` allocation and `run_started` reconstructs from Beads claim per EM-031a.

title: run_completed and run_failed emission on terminal state

Terminal-transition bead write per BI-010 MUST follow terminal event emission.
close_reason: Implemented in d91b2a4 (cherry-picked from 489541e): RunCompletedPayload + RunFailedPayload typed payloads + 27 sensor tests (EM-015b/EM-025). ErrorCategory placeholder needs typed-alias-deferral follow-up.

title: Terminal-state detection rule
description: Per EM-015c: run reaches terminal when (a) current `node_id` ∈ `terminal_node_ids` AND last outcome ∈ {SUCCESS, PARTIAL_SUCCESS} → `completed`; OR (b) classifier (§8) terminal verdict → `failed`; OR (c) operator `stop --immediate` → `canceled`. Detection MUST run after every state advance; condition on orchestrator outer loop. Re-run produces fresh `run_id`.
close_reason: EM-015c terminal-state classifier landed via 6-chain pipelined merge at 717beda. internal/core/terminalstate.go: TerminalStateKind enum (NonTerminal/Completed/Failed/Canceled with String()) + LastOutcome enum (None/Success/PartialSuccess/Failure) + TerminalStateInput struct + ClassifyTerminalState pure function with documented precedence (OperatorStop > ClassifierFailure > TerminalNode+Success → Completed). Spec uses ∨ without ordering; precedence rationale documented. internal/core/terminalstate_test.go: 8 tests covering all precedence paths + String exhaustive. 100% coverage on terminalstate.go. Pre-claim: blocks-on-closed hk-b3f.86 + hk-b3f.14 → related. Reviewer APPROVE-CLEAN. make check green.

title: Checkpoint is a git commit with work-product tree + transition-record sibling
description: Per EM-016: a `Checkpoint` is one git commit on the run's task branch. Tree contains (a) work product AND (b) transition-record sibling at `.harmonik/transitions/<run_id>/<transition_id>.json`. Atomic: write-tree → commit-tree → update-ref; atomicity boundary is the reference advance. Pre-update-ref crash MAY leave loose objects (eligible for `git gc`) but no observable state. Task branch MUST exist before checkpoint attempted.
close_reason: MERGED at 3f291d8 (main). EM-016 atomicity contract godoc on Checkpoint (write-tree → commit-tree → update-ref sequence, atomicity boundary at update-ref, loose-object orphan rule, task-branch precondition); EM-018 path-coherence invariant added to Valid() (TransitionRecordPath == TransitionRecordPath(RunID, TransitionID)). Implementer also fixed a pre-existing construction error in gitwins_b3f65_test.go fixture (hardcoded path didn't match random UUIDs) — confined fix verified by reviewer. title: Checkpoint commit carries structured trailers
description: Per EM-017: every checkpoint commit MUST carry `Harmonik-Run-ID`, `Harmonik-State-ID`, `Harmonik-Transition-ID`, `Harmonik-Schema-Version`. `Harmonik-Bead-ID` MUST be present iff bead-tied per EM-014. Trailers are an index; authoritative fields live in the sibling file (EM-018).
close_reason: Implemented in dedf60c: godoc on trailerRegistry citing EM-017 (4 required + Bead-ID conditional + index-not-source-of-truth clause); new TestEM017_RequiredSetConformance enforces the EM-017 set is complete and that no extra TrailerRequired or EM-owned-Conditional entries silently slip in. Reviewer APPROVE.

title: Corrupted-checkpoint fallback rule
description: Per EM-017a: if `Harmonik-Transition-ID` trailer present but expected sibling file is missing / truncated / fails schema validation, daemon MUST treat as corrupted-checkpoint and dispatch reconciliation per RC §8.11 Cat 6a or §8.11a Cat 6b. Daemon MUST NOT silently proceed and MUST NOT re-attempt against same commit. Reconciliation workflow bound by verdict-only-commit rule (EM-026); recursion bounded to one level. Verdict-commit-itself-corrupted escalates to operator (Cat 6b).
close_reason: Implemented in 32d3a04 (cherry-pick 455489e): EM-017a corrupted-checkpoint detector + 8 sensor tests covering missing/truncated/invalid-schema sibling files.

title: Transition record sibling file at canonical run-scoped path
description: Per EM-018: every checkpoint commit's tree MUST contain typed JSON at `.harmonik/transitions/<run_id>/<transition_id>.json` with the full `Transition` record. `<run_id>` and `<transition_id>` path components MUST match the run's and transition's IDs. File `schema_version` MUST match commit's `Harmonik-Schema-Version` trailer. Run-scoping is a structural uniqueness guarantee (cross-run merges/cherry-picks/replays cannot collide).

ID types serialize as UUID strings via encoding.TextMarshaler. ValidateTransitionSchemaVersion rejects mismatch and zero-commit-version. Path helper TransitionRecordPath was already shipped via hk-b3f.24. Reviewer MEDIUM (confidence missing from requiredKeys snake_case sensor) inline-amended in commit 8cfbaf6.

title: Transition ID generation contract (daemon-local UUIDv7)
description: Per EM-018a: every `transition_id` MUST be UUIDv7 generated in the daemon process (not in agent subprocesses); MAY mix per-process monotonic counter into UUIDv7 random tail to zero same-millisecond collisions within a process. Cross-run uniqueness NOT required (path-scoping per EM-018 provides structural collision guarantee); within-run uniqueness mechanically guaranteed by daemon-local generator.

New TransitionIDGenerator at internal/core/transitionidgen.go (mutex-serialised UUIDv7, reuses uuidGT + increment128 helpers from eventidgen.go, godoc cites EM-018a). Added IsUUIDv7() method to TransitionID mirroring EventID's external-source enforcement point. 6 unit tests + 6 stress tests (parallel to eventidgen_stress_test.go pattern). All 15 new TransitionID tests PASS, race-detector clean. Wave-4 helper hk-b3f.23-impl.

title: Transition record discoverable by git-show
description: Per EM-019: given `run_id`, `transition_id`, and a commit on the run's task branch with matching trailers, the transition record MUST be retrievable via `git show <commit>:.harmonik/transitions/<run_id>/<transition_id>.json`. No cross-commit index MAY be required. `<run_id>` always available (reader's run scope or commit trailer).
