Reviewer APPROVE-CLEAN. make check green.

title: Transition records are immutable
description: Per EM-020: once committed, transition-record file MUST NEVER be rewritten. New transition adds new file under new `transition_id`; MUST NOT modify prior file. History-rewriting (amend, rebase, filter-branch) against committed records is policy violation detected by validator + audit tooling per EM-020a.
close_reason: MERGED at 44d01b1 (main). EM-020 write-once immutability contract encoded in Transition godoc (cites EM-020/EM-020a/EM-018a + audit-tool dep hk-b3f.26). New TestTransitionRecord_ImmutabilityDistinctPaths structural sensor (distinct UUIDv7 → distinct sibling path). Includes additive correction of stale EM-036 → EM-028 citation in pre-existing godoc (EM-036 does not exist in spec; EM-028 is the correct anchor; reviewer verified). Reviewer: APPROVE. Wave-4 helper hk-b3f.25-impl.

title: Audit tool detection rule for transition-record integrity
description: Per EM-020a: post-hoc audit tool MUST, for every commit reachable from every active or archived task branch, parse `Harmonik-Run-ID` and `Harmonik-Transition-ID` trailers and verify exactly one matching sibling file exists. Five flag conditions: (a) trailer pair with no sibling, (b) sibling under `.harmonik/transitions/` not matching any trailer pair, (c) `transition_id` appearing on >1 commit within a run's sub-directory, (d) `schema_version` mismatch, (e) path `<run_id>` component disagreeing with trailer. Use `git interpret-trailers --parse` (trailer-block-only). Flagged commits MUST route to RC §4.3 RC-010.
close_reason: Implemented in ae7c9d6 (cherry-pick): EM-020a AuditViolationKind 5-value enum + AuditViolation record with Valid(); 17 sensor tests.

title: Large evidence payloads externalize under transition directory
description: Per EM-021: large `evidence` / `verifier_metrics` payloads MUST externalize to sibling files at `.harmonik/transitions/<run_id>/<transition_id>/evidence/*` referenced by relative path. Externalized files inherit EM-016 atomicity boundary (NOT written outside the tree). Primary `<transition_id>.json` SHOULD remain single-digit KB.
close_reason: Implemented in 3b08b86: EvidenceExternalDir path-builder in internal/core/transitionpath.go returns canonical .harmonik/transitions/<run_id>/<transition_id>/evidence per EM-021. evidence.go + verifiermetrics.go godoc encode MUST externalize, EM-016 atomicity inheritance, single-digit-KB SHOULD. Reviewer APPROVE.

title: Checkpoint schema is N-1 readable
description: Per EM-022: transition-record sibling file + commit trailers MUST carry `schema_version` integer that increments on normative change. Readers MUST accept N-1. Breaking changes require migration release.
close_reason: EM-022 N-1-readable schema invariant cited at 3781ac8 across transitionWire, MarshalTransitionRecord, ValidateTransitionSchemaVersion, Harmonik-Schema-Version trailer entry, and SchemaVersion field. Distinct from EM-018 (file-path) which was already cited. Approved.

title: Durable transition: one commit per durable transition + decision procedure (coalesce)
description: Per EM-023 + EM-023a (coalesced per §2.3): exactly one checkpoint commit per durable transition. Transition is durable iff `transition_kind ∈ {forward, local-patchback, architectural-rollback, policy-rollback, context-restore}` AND `outcome.status ∈ {SUCCESS, PARTIAL_SUCCESS}`. RETRY/FAIL/gate-deny/validator-rejection NOT durable. PARTIAL_SUCCESS Transition record carries `partial_success=true` evidence flag. Transition record carries `outcome_status` field as decision-procedure input (no implicit reconstruction). `context-restore` and reconciliation transitions are daemon-produced; daemon synthesizes `Outcome{status=SUCCESS, actor_role ∈ {daemon, reconciliation}}` recorded in `evidence.synthesized_outcome=true`.
close_reason: Implemented in d4ba2f7: new internal/core/durability.go with IsDurable(TransitionKind, OutcomeStatus) bool encoding EM-023a — durable iff kind ∈ {forward, local-patchback, architectural-rollback, policy-rollback, context-restore} AND status ∈ {SUCCESS, PARTIAL_SUCCESS}. New EvidenceKeyPartialSuccess constant. Full 5×4 truth-table test + 4 sensors. Reviewer APPROVE.

title: State is a position in a run's progression
description: Per EM-003: `State` carries `state_id`, `run_id`, `node_id`, `entered_at`, `transition_history` (commit range filtered by run trailer). New states MUST NOT be observable until checkpoint commit lands.
close_reason: Implemented in a1df8b2: godoc on State type encoding EM-003 observability invariant (new states not observable until checkpoint commit lands; cross-reference to Checkpoint EM-016 atomicity boundary). Reviewer APPROVE.

title: Git always knows the last durable state of every in-flight run
description: Per EM-024: at any time, for every in-flight run, the tip of the run's task branch MUST be the run's last-durable-state checkpoint commit. Precondition for state-reconstruction (§4.7) and reconciliation detectors.
close_reason: Implemented in e7857c8: EM-024 branch-tip-equals-checkpoint sensor in internal/lifecycle/branchtip_em024_test.go; 4 tests covering durable advance, non-durable static, multi-run isolation, ancestor monotonicity.

title: Branch-tip monotonicity check (vs external force-push / reset)
description: Per EM-024a: daemon MUST persist per-run last-observed task-branch-tip SHA in run metadata (e.g., `.harmonik/run-tips/<run_id>`). On any subsequent read (startup per EM-031a, dispatch per §7.4, checkpoint advance per §7.2), daemon MUST verify new tip is fast-forward descendant of persisted prior. Non-fast-forward → route to RC §8.4 Cat 3; MUST NOT advance run against new tip. Missing prior-tip file on first observation initializes; not a violation. Defends EM-024 against external force-push / `git reset --hard` / CI auto-rebase.
close_reason: Implemented in ed60c44 (cherry-pick): EM-024a branch-tip monotonicity detector + 12 sensor tests; ErrBranchTipRewound + ReadPersistedTip/WritePersistedTip/IsFastForwardDescendant/CheckBranchTipMonotonicity in internal/lifecycle/branchtip_em024a.go.

title: Failed transitions MUST NOT create checkpoint commits at MVH
description: Per EM-025: failed transition (FAIL or classifier verdict) MUST emit failure event per EV §8 with `last_checkpoint` SHA correlation field but MUST NOT create checkpoint commit at MVH. Post-MVH failure-commit introduction (for `git bisect` over failures) is additive.
close_reason: Implemented in 5806555: godoc on Checkpoint type encoding EM-025 — Checkpoint only for successful durable transitions per §4.5.EM-023a; failed transitions emit failure event with last_checkpoint correlation but produce NO checkpoint commit at MVH; post-MVH additive note included. Reviewer APPROVE.

title: Emission ordering: transition_event MUST NOT precede update-ref
description: Per EM-025a: pre-commit `transition_event` emission MUST NOT precede `git update-ref`. Order: update-ref returns success → transition event → checkpoint-written event → state-entered event per §7.2. Premature emission would leave observers with transition reference whose commit is non-durable on update-ref failure (e.g., ENOSPC), producing reconciliation-detector false positives. ENOSPC/EIO during checkpoint sequence classified `transient` per §8.1 with bounded retry; cap exhaustion → `structural` per §8.2. On retry, NEW `transition_id` generated per EM-018a; failed-attempt evidence files removed before retry (or reclaimed by periodic sweeper of unreferenced `<transition_id>` sub-directories).
close_reason: Implemented in 12b751d: godoc on Checkpoint type encoding EM-025a emission ordering — update-ref → transition_event → checkpoint_written → state-entered per §7.2; ENOSPC/EIO transient/structural classification; new transition_id on retry per EM-018a; orphan-evidence cleanup discipline. Reviewer APPROVE.

title: Reconciliation workflows are an explicit exception to EM-023
description: Per EM-026: reconciliation workflows MUST emit exactly one checkpoint commit per reconciliation-run - the verdict commit - and MUST NOT emit intermediate checkpoints. Exception keyed on `workflow_class = reconciliation` metadata; absence means ordinary workflow (obeys EM-023 unchanged).
close_reason: EM-026 reconciliation-workflow EM-023 exception cited at a1e7a15 across IsDurable godoc and Workflow.WorkflowClass field comment. TODO(hk-63oh.60) marker for typed alias. Approved.

title: Outcome spine: handler outcome threads through hook, gate, transition, event
description: Per EM-027: handler outcome → hook dispatch → gate evaluation → transition selection → transition event are one integrated flow. Each segment MUST consume prior segment's typed output and produce next segment's typed input; no segment may bypass another.
close_reason: Implemented in 67e8e02 (rebased): HookVerdictRecord per control-points.md §6.1.6 fills the missing segment-2 of the outcome spine; Valid() enforces Failed/Reason cross-field; CognitionMeta deferred to hk-a8bg.73; 15 tests pass. Reviewer APPROVE.

title: Transition record is canonical durable form; transition_event is its projection
description: Per EM-028: `Transition` record at `.harmonik/transitions/<transition_id>.json` (path-scoped per EM-018) is the canonical authoritative form. Transition event MUST be a projection citing `transition_id`, `run_id`, checkpoint commit hash; full record recoverable via `git show` per EM-019.
close_reason: MERGED at 8dfcc3c (main). New TransitionEventPayload type at internal/core/transitioneventpayload.go encodes EM-028 projection contract: exactly 3 required fields (TransitionID, RunID, CheckpointCommitHash); EM-029 no-duplication observed structurally (candidate_actions/evidence/verifier_metrics absent); Valid() enforces all three non-zero; godoc encodes canonical-vs-projection authority + git-show recovery (EM-019). Reviewer: APPROVE; flagged a follow-up MINOR (could add reflect-based field-count assertion for future EM-029 regression detection — not load-bearing). Wave-4 helper hk-b3f.25-impl (bundled commit).

title: Transition event MUST NOT duplicate full trace payload
description: Per EM-029: transition event payload MUST NOT carry full AlphaGo trace (candidate_actions, evidence, verifier_metrics). Those live only in sibling file. Prevents storage duplication and schema drift.
close_reason: SUBSUMED: internal/core/transitioneventpayload.go:10-12 + lines 34-48 already enforce EM-029 structurally and in godoc; transitioneventpayload_test.go:74-97 (TestTransitionEventPayload_ProjectionFields) guards against future violations. Verified vs specs/execution-model.md §4.6.EM-029. No code delta needed.

title: Consumers requiring full audit fidelity MUST read transition record from git
description: Per EM-030: consumer needing complete trace fidelity (post-hoc audit, improvement-loop, scenario-harness) MUST read sibling file via §4.4.EM-019. Streaming consumers needing only event-level metadata MAY read transition event from bus.
close_reason: SUBSUMED: internal/core/transitioneventpayload.go:22-25 already encodes both EM-030 clauses verbatim in godoc; CheckpointCommitHash field + Valid() enforce the recovery path. Verified vs specs/execution-model.md §4.6.EM-030. No code delta needed.

title: State reconstruction uses git plus Beads only
description: Per EM-031: on restart, daemon MUST reconstruct in-flight run durable state by walking git checkpoint trail (Harmonik-Run-ID trailers) + querying Beads via `br`. JSONL MUST NOT be walked for state reconstruction. JSONL divergence-evidence reads permitted but MUST NOT be relied on to reconstitute state beyond what git+Beads establish. JSONL torn-tail tolerance: unparseable final line not terminated by newline → discard, treat remainder valid (NOT a Cat 6b signal). Unparseable line elsewhere or terminated tail IS a Cat 6b signal. JSONL-only divergence MUST NOT trigger Cat 3 without git-side corroboration.
close_reason: Implemented in c6ed807: jsonldivergence_em031.go with torn-tail tolerance + reconstruction_em031_test.go with 9 sensors covering JSONL-not-walked-for-state behavioral assertions and torn-tail/mid-file-corruption Cat-6b classification.

title: Transition is the full AlphaGo trace record
description: Per EM-004: `Transition` carries `transition_id`, `run_id`, `from_state`, `to_state`, `actor_role`, `candidate_actions`, `chosen_action`, `policy_version`, `evidence`, `verifier_metrics`, `confidence`, `transition_kind`, optional `rollback_to_state_id`, `schema_version`. Durable per §4.4; reachable via the sibling-file contract.
close_reason: SUBSUMED: internal/core/transition.go declares all 15 EM-004 §6.1 fields with full godoc citation (line 6); Valid() enforces §4.1.EM-004 + §4.10.EM-044 + §4.10.EM-046 invariants; transitionrecord.go provides §4.4.EM-018 durability wire contract; transition_test.go + transitionrecord_test.go cover. Verified vs specs/execution-model.md §4.1.EM-004 + §6.1. No code delta needed.

title: Active-run discovery at startup
description: Per EM-031a: at startup, before state reconstruction begins, daemon MUST determine in-flight runs via Beads non-terminal query AND task-branch-naming-convention scan. Active-run set is the union of (Beads-linked runs) ∪ (branches whose tip carries Harmonik-Run-ID matching no terminal-state bead). Beads-unreachable defers classification to RC Cat 0 pre-check + enters `degraded` status (NOT silent git-only fallback). Pre-dispatch: daemon MUST NOT modify worktree (no `git clean`, `git checkout`, branch switch, file deletion); WIP evidence is investigator input per RC §4.4.
close_reason: Implemented in 3eab9583 (rebased): EM-031a active-run discovery via union of (Beads non-terminal query) ∪ (task-branch tip scan); ErrBeadsUnavailable halt for Cat 0 deferral; no worktree mutation. internal/lifecycle/activerun_em031a.go + internal/brcli/listbystatus_em031a.go (ListBeadsByStatus method on *Adapter). 17+9 tests pass. Reviewer APPROVE.

title: Deterministic replay contract
description: Per EM-032: given run's git checkpoint trail and Beads record, run state MUST be reconstructable to any point for debugging, audit, scenario-harness, restart reconciliation. "Transition history" refers to git checkpoint trail, NOT JSONL event tail.
close_reason: Implemented in 44786d1 (cherry-pick): EM-032 deterministic-replay sensor (5 tests) — sibling-via-git-show, multi-point trail, JSONL-not-needed, cross-run isolation, trail-walk reconstruction.

title: No workflow-level transactionality
description: Per EM-033: a run that commits N nodes and fails on node N+1 MUST leave all N prior checkpoints durable. NO rollback of prior checkpoints on later failure. State-at-failure preserved in git per EM-024; recovery handled by RC §8 categories.
close_reason: Implemented in 20ea059 (rebased to f88c252): EM-033 no-transactionality sensor in notx_em033_test.go; 4 tests covering N=0, N=3, repeated failures, N=10 checkpoint preservation.

title: Sub-workflow node expands in place within parent run
description: Per EM-034: `sub-workflow` node MUST expand in place; sub-workflow's nodes/edges become part of parent run's execution graph. Sub-workflow MUST NOT spawn child run; parent `run_id` is sole identifier for nested execution. Expansion keyed on sub-workflow's version resolved at workflow-load time; load-time pin survives until run terminal state (durably backed per EM-034c).
close_reason: Implemented in 4f9632d (cherry-pick): EM-034 SubWorkflowExpansion record + Valid() + 12 sensor tests including 10 invalid-field cases.

title: Sub-workflow node-ID namespacing
description: Per EM-034a: on expansion, every sub-workflow node's `node_id` MUST be rewritten to `<parent_node_id>/<sub_node_id>`. Parent's `node_id` unchanged. Nested expansions compose left-to-right (`A/B/C`). Source-level collisions are authoring errors detected by validator (EM-038). State and transition records carry namespaced `node_id`. Validator verifies resolvability + acyclicity but does NOT statically inline.
close_reason: Implemented in c599bf5 (cherry-pick): EM-034a NamespaceNodeID concatenator + 5 sensor tests including 3-level composition.

title: Sub-workflow reference graph MUST be acyclic
description: Per EM-034b: directed graph of (workflow, sub-workflow-reference) MUST be acyclic. Self-reference and mutual reference are authoring errors detected by pre-run validator per EM-038. Detection MUST occur during transitive resolution; cycle MUST fail validation before any node executes.
close_reason: Implemented in 7796da8 (cherry-pick): EM-034b SubWorkflowRefGraph with DFS 3-color cycle detection + 9 sensor tests.

title: Sub-workflow expansion pin durable on entry checkpoint
description: Per EM-034c: when parent enters sub-workflow node, entry checkpoint Transition record MUST carry `evidence.sub_workflow_pin` = `{sub_workflow_ref: String, sub_workflow_version: String, resolved_workflow_id: UUID}`. On restart, daemon MUST reconstruct pinned expansion by reading this evidence key from most recent `sub_workflow_entered` transition record on task branch (NOT by re-consulting registry). Registry updates between crash and restart cannot alter expansion. Nested expansions: each entry checkpoint carries its own pin; outer run's expansion reconstructed by walking checkpoint trail in commit order applying each pin at its entry boundary.
close_reason: Implemented in 937383b: new internal/core/subworkflowexpansionpin.go with SubWorkflowExpansionPin struct (sub_workflow_ref / sub_workflow_version / resolved_workflow_id per §4.8.EM-034c), Valid() invariants, full JSON round-trip + key-name + Evidence-map type-assertion tests. evidence.go godoc on EvidenceKeySubWorkflowPin expanded with durability protocol (entry checkpoint, EM-016 atomicity, no-registry-on-restart, nested commit-order walk). Reviewer APPROVE.

title: Parent run's checkpoint trail covers nested sub-workflow execution
description: Per EM-035: every durable transition inside expanded sub-workflow MUST emit checkpoint commit on parent run's task branch per EM-023. MUST NOT have separate sub-workflow checkpoint trail.
close_reason: Implemented in 800a4c9 (cherry-pick): EM-035 nested-checkpoint sensor — 5 tests asserting parent run_id, namespaced node_id, no-separate-trail.

title: Sub-workflow entry and exit emit lifecycle events
description: Per EM-036: on entering sub-workflow expansion, daemon MUST emit sub-workflow-entered event per EV §8.1; entry checkpoint MUST carry expansion pin per EM-034c. On exit, MUST emit sub-workflow-exited event; exit's terminal-outcome correlation field composed per EM-036a. Both events correlate via `run_id` and parent namespaced `node_id`. Event names + payloads normative in event-model.
close_reason: SubWorkflowEnteredPayload + SubWorkflowExitedPayload typed records (be8b78c)

title: Sub-workflow terminal outcome is last-expanded-node's Outcome
description: Per EM-036a: `Outcome` escaping a sub-workflow node - consumed by parent's edge-selection cascade (EM-041) on edges leaving sub-workflow node - MUST be the `Outcome` produced by the last expanded-node executed before sub-workflow reached its `terminal_node_ids`. Sub-workflow-exited event's terminal-outcome correlation field MUST carry same `Outcome`. `context_updates` already applied per EM-041a (post-coalesce into em-041); cascade observes post-update state. Multi-terminal sub-workflows reach exactly one at runtime; the outcome that produced terminal-reaching transition is the sub-workflow's terminal outcome. Parent's `sub-workflow` node MUST NOT declare own `Outcome` shape; inherits expanded terminal mechanically.
close_reason: Implemented in 5531848 (cherry-pick): EM-036a sub-workflow terminal outcome = last-expanded-node's Outcome — 7 tests including multi-terminal + context-update ordering.

title: Outcome is the handler-produced node result + kind discriminator (coalesce)
description: Per EM-005 + EM-005a (coalesced per §2.3): `Outcome` carries `status`, `preferred_label`, `suggested_next_ids`, `context_updates`, `notes`, `kind ∈ {default, reconciliation_verdict}`, optional kind-discriminated `payload`. The kind discriminator's wire alias is `outcome_kind` on HC-008's `outcome_emitted` event. Unknown `kind` values route to RC §8.11 Cat 6a (no silent default-degradation).
close_reason: SUBSUMED: EM-005 + EM-005a fully satisfied by existing code. Outcome record fields (status/preferred_label/suggested_next_ids/context_updates/notes/kind/payload) at internal/core/outcome.go:38-73; OutcomeKind discriminator + 'outcome_kind' wire alias godoc + Cat 6a routing at internal/core/outcomekind.go:5-68; discriminated-union enforcement (payload MUST be nil/non-nil) at internal/core/outcome.go:87-105 Valid(); test coverage in outcome_test.go and outcomekind_test.go (TestOutcomeValid_DefaultKindWithPayload_Rejected, TestOutcomeValid_ReconciliationVerdictKindWithoutPayload_Rejected, TestOutcomeKindUnmarshalText/unknown_value_routes_to_error_(Cat_6a_per_§8.11)). All 536 internal/core tests pass. No delta needed; closing as subsumed per Wave-4 implementer A verification (helper hk-b3f.5-impl, no commit).

title: Sub-workflow composition is the ONLY composition mechanism
description: Per EM-037: a workflow MUST NOT extend, inherit, or runtime-rewrite another workflow. Composition MUST be exclusively via `sub-workflow` nodes referencing named sub-workflows resolved at workflow-load time. Proposals introducing runtime rewrites, inheritance, or dynamic node insertion fail this requirement.
close_reason: Implemented in cc58309 (cherry-pick): EM-037 sub-workflow is the ONLY composition mechanism — 7 tests asserting NodeTypeSubWorkflow exclusivity + ref validation.

title: Pre-run validator MUST run before any node executes
description: Per EM-038: workflow-attribute validator MUST run to completion before any node executes. Scope: DOT parseability; sub-workflow resolution transitively (with acyclicity per EM-034b detected during transitive resolution); reference resolution (handler_ref, policy_ref, gate_ref, freedom_profile_ref, budget_ref, required_skills); attribute type checks (enums match, timeouts/budgets positive, required attrs present, start_node_id + non-empty terminal_node_ids declared); reachability (every node reachable from start, every node can reach a terminal); cycle-bound (every cycle has declared traversal cap per EM-043). Any failure prevents start.
close_reason: Implemented in d5e4a4d6: 8-pass PreRunValidator + hand-rolled DOT parser; all malformed-DOT corpus exercised; 23 tests pass. Reviewer APPROVE.

title: Validator is mechanism-tagged
description: Per EM-039: every validator check MUST be mechanism-tagged; delegation to cognition is forbidden. Semantic judgments (policy expression "good", node name "descriptive") belong in reviewer nodes, not the validator.
close_reason: Implemented (rebased): EM-039 mechanism-tag sensor test using go/parser to assert PreRunValidator godoc carries 'Tags: mechanism' line. Reviewer APPROVE.

title: Agents that generate DOT MUST run validation before submission
description: Per EM-040: an agent producing a DOT workflow MUST run the validator before submitting to daemon. Submission paths skipping validation are structural violations of the centralized-controller principle. "Submission" is the daemon RPC ingesting workflow for dispatch.
close_reason: Implemented in 8dd4e47 (rebased): EM-040 dot-validation-before-submit sensor test using stub-submitter pattern (real RPC is process-lifecycle.md §4.10 future work). 6 sensor tests verify call-sequence contract. Reviewer APPROVE.

title: Deterministic edge-selection cascade + context-update ordering (coalesce)
description: Per EM-041 + EM-041a (coalesced per §2.3): on exiting a node, daemon MUST select next edge by deterministic cascade - (a) condition-true edges, (b) prefer label matching `outcome.preferred_label`, (c) prefer edges matching `outcome.suggested_next_ids`, (d) tie-break by `weight` (higher first), (e) final tie-break by lexical `ordering_key`. Identical inputs produce identical output. `outcome.context_updates` MUST be applied to run.context BEFORE cascade evaluates conditions (cascade observes post-update state).
close_reason: Implemented in e95f86b: 5-step deterministic edge-selection cascade + EM-041a context-update ordering, EM-046a + EM-043 failure paths, 23 tests pass. Reviewer APPROVE.

title: Guards reorder; Gates permit/deny; otherwise edges follow EM-041
description: Per EM-042: transition guards per CP §6.4 MAY reorder candidate edge list before cascade runs; MUST NOT add/remove/block edges. Gates per CP §6.2 MAY permit/deny/escalate chosen transition after cascade. Gate denial leaves run in source state and does NOT constitute durable transition (no checkpoint). Guards precede cascade; gates follow it.
close_reason: Implemented in 019d743 (cherry-pick e77087a): EM-042 Guards/Gates dispatch pipeline (GuardEvaluator + GateEvaluator + DispatchEdge) + 13 sensor tests.

title: Gate-deny continuation protocol (gate-pending sub-state)
description: Per EM-042a: when cascade returns `STAY(current_state)` from gate denial, run MUST enter `gate-pending` sub-state of `running`. Daemon MUST NOT re-dispatch source node and MUST NOT re-run cascade against same context+outcome (gate is a deterministic function - would loop). Daemon MUST wait for gate-resolution signal per CP §6.2 (policy-driven context change, operator override, or per-gate-policy timeout). On signal, re-evaluate cascade; permit → advance; still-deny + timeout policy → fail with class `structural` per §8.2.
close_reason: Implemented in f8dab64 (cherry-pick): EM-042a GatePendingRecord + GateResolutionSignal enum (3 values: context-change, operator-override, timeout) + 15 sensor tests.

title: Cycle traversal cap + per-(run_id, edge) counter storage (coalesce)
description: Per EM-043 + EM-043a (coalesced per §2.3): every cycle MUST have at least one edge carrying declared positive-integer traversal cap; cap reach during run → daemon MUST fail transition with class `compilation_loop` per §8 (Kilroy-parity cycle-bounding for MVH). Counter MUST be maintained per `(run_id, edge)` tuple in daemon memory for run duration AND MUST be recoverable from task-branch git history at restart by scanning run's commit trail and counting prior traversals (edge identified by `from_node` / `to_node` on each durable Transition). Daemon-memory counters non-authoritative across restart; git-derived count authoritative. Single edge in multiple cycles shares one counter per-run.
close_reason: MERGED at b2568a1 + a202085 (main, two commits). New CycleCounter at internal/core/cyclecounter.go: per-(run_id, edge) traversal counter store with mutex-guarded map[edgeKey]uint64; methods NewCycleCounter / Increment(traversalCap *int) / Get / Reset / ReconcileFromTransitions; sentinel ErrCompilationLoop wrapping FailureClassCompilationLoop. Cap-reached path does NOT advance counter (verified by test). ReconcileFromTransitions REPLACES rather than adds (authoritative recovery per EM-043a). 10 tests pass under race-detector. Reviewer: APPROVE; one MINOR (fmt.Errorf vs errors.New idiom) addressed by orchestrator inline-amend at a202085. Git-history adapter (the caller that scans task-branch and feeds []Transition) deferred — out of internal/core scope per spec; will live in workspace/daemon subsystem when scaffolded. Wave-4 helper hk-b3f.57-impl.

title: Backtracking is transition_kind plus rollback_to_state_id
description: Per EM-044: `Transition` MAY carry `transition_kind ∈ {forward, local-patchback, architectural-rollback, policy-rollback, context-restore}` and optional `rollback_to_state_id`. Forward transitions MUST omit `rollback_to_state_id`; `architectural-rollback` and `policy-rollback` MUST populate with target earlier state ID. `local-patchback` and `context-restore` MUST omit (do not relocate run's graph position). Hybrid shape (kind tag + optional pointer) is canonical representation of harmonik's four rollback types.
close_reason: SUBSUMED by hk-b3f.77 Transition.Valid() + transition_test.go EM-044 enforcement. transition.go enforces 5-kind rule (forward/local-patchback/context-restore MUST omit rollback_to_state_id; architectural-rollback/policy-rollback MUST populate). 9 EM-044-specific test cases cover every branch. Bead scope was exactly Transition.Valid() invariant — fully delivered.

title: Rollback transitions are recorded as new transitions, not history-rewrites
description: Per EM-045: a rollback MUST be represented as new transition (new `transition_id`, new checkpoint commit) whose `rollback_to_state_id` points at earlier state. Earlier state's checkpoint commit MUST NOT be altered or removed; git history is append-only per EM-020.
close_reason: EM-045 rollback-as-new-transition invariant cited at c037650 on RollbackToStateID field + TransitionKindArchitecturalRollback / TransitionKindPolicyRollback per-constant godoc. Append-only contract per EM-020 cross-cited. Approved.

title: Node type is one of five declared kinds
description: Per EM-006: `Node` declares `type ∈ {agentic, non-agentic, gate, control-point, sub-workflow}`. Five kinds mutually exclusive; validator (EM-038) rejects others.
close_reason: SUBSUMED: internal/core/nodetype.go already declares NodeType with all 5 EM-006 kinds (agentic, non-agentic, gate, control-point, sub-workflow), Valid() rejects others, MarshalText/UnmarshalText round-trip. Likely landed under hk-b3f.80. No new code needed.

title: Context restore is agent-scoped; does not alter graph state
description: Per EM-046: a `context-restore` transition restores prior context window for an agent without altering run's graph state. Checkpoint still lands (durable per EM-023a synthesized-Outcome rule); `transition_kind=context-restore` distinguishes from forward progress. `rollback_to_state_id` MUST be absent. Initiated by daemon or reconciliation verdict, NOT by handler; daemon synthesizes `Outcome{status=SUCCESS, actor_role ∈ {daemon, role of verdict-executing subsystem}}`.
close_reason: MERGED at 452b1b4 (main). EM-046 context-restore behavioral contract: Transition.Valid() godoc rule list now cites §4.10.EM-046 with explicit bullet 'context-restore MUST NOT carry RollbackToStateID (EM-046: does not relocate graph position)'. Pre-existing needsRollback guard already enforces the constraint; pre-existing tests TestTransitionValid_ContextRestoreMustOmitRollbackStateID + TestTransitionValid_ContextRestoreOmitsRollbackStateID cover both directions. +7/-4 transition.go. Daemon-side rules deferred to follow-up beads hk-b3f.107 (initiation-source enforcement) and hk-b3f.108 (synthesized-Outcome path) — both parented to hk-b3f, blocked on daemon scaffold. Reviewer: APPROVE. Wave-4 helper hk-b3f.60-impl.

title: No-matching-edge failure class is structural
description: Per EM-046a: if cascade produces empty match set (no outgoing edge has satisfiable condition for current context+outcome), run MUST fail with class `structural` per §8.2 with reason `no_outgoing_edge_matches`. Daemon MUST emit `run_failed` with class structural + reason field naming node and outcome at which cascade produced no match. Reachable via policy-expression edits rendering all outgoing edges false. Class signals re-planning, not retry.
close_reason: SUBSUMED by hk-b3f.54 edge-cascade implementation: internal/core/edgecascade.go already returns FailureClassStructural / 'no_outgoing_edge_matches' when no outgoing edge matches (lines 151-152). No additional code needed for EM-046a.

title: RETRY outcome re-dispatch protocol
description: Per EM-046b: `Outcome.status=RETRY` MUST cause daemon to re-dispatch same node against run's current state. RETRY's `context_updates` MUST be applied pre-redispatch per EM-041a (cascade itself NOT run after RETRY). Daemon MUST track per-node attempt count (in-memory; re-derivable from git log scan counting commits with matching `Harmonik-State-ID` and `outcome.status=RETRY` in evidence map); transition to class `transient` per §8.1 on retry-count threshold per node's retry policy. RETRY NOT durable per EM-023a; MUST NOT produce checkpoint commit. Internal state machine: `running → retry-armed → retrying → running` (NOT emitted as distinct state machine in §7.1).
close_reason: Implemented in e6c8382 (cherry-pick 1bc363b): EM-046b RetryCounter + 11 sensor tests with cap, isolation, reconcile-from-transitions.

title: Sensor: git is the state-reconstruction source
description: Per EM-INV-001: the git checkpoint trail MUST be sufficient, together with the Beads store, to reconstruct any run's current durable state. JSONL event replay MUST NOT be used for state reconstruction. Every subsystem consuming run state (reconciliation, operator-nfr, process-lifecycle, scenario-harness) MUST honor this precedence. Sensor (corpus-wide): §10.2 EM-031..EM-033 group - restart scenario test that destroys daemon and confirms state reconstructable from git+Beads without JSONL reads; cross-spec scenario harness verifies subsystems consuming run state route reads through git+Beads, not JSONL.
close_reason: EM-INV-001 sensor: spec-text + corpus-scan + restart-scenario layers in internal/lifecycle/gitrecon_eminv001_test.go (b4fb75f)

title: Sensor: no subsystem implements workflow-level transactionality
description: Per EM-INV-004: any subsystem writing to git, Beads, or workspace state MUST NOT implement an undo-previous-N-operations primitive that atomically rolls back prior checkpoints, prior bead status writes, or prior workspace branch advances. Prior writes remain durable; recovery routes via RC §8 categories. The invariant guards composition-level atomic undo: a subsystem could ship a conforming local requirement yet violate this invariant by introducing a primitive (e.g., "rewind to last merge" CLI) that, when composed with other subsystems' primitives, produces atomic multi-subsystem undo. Sensor (cross-subsystem corpus scan): reviewer-persona scan of every subsystem spec for any "undo-previous-N", "rewind-to-merge", "atomic-rollback-of-N-checkpoints" primitive at the authoring surface; spec-edit reject on detection. EM-local sensor predecessor: EM-033.
close_reason: Implemented in 5323f9c (cherry-pick): EM-INV-004 static-grep sensor walking internal/+cmd/ for 17 transactionality-rollback patterns (identifiers + CLI literals).

title: Sensor: git wins on completion disagreement
description: Per EM-INV-005: if Beads reports a bead as `closed` but no merge commit with `Harmonik-Bead-ID` matching that bead exists in the project's git history, OR if a JSONL transition event references a checkpoint commit that does not exist in git, the divergence MUST be treated as reconciliation flag and NOT silently auto-reconciled. Every subsystem observing this divergence MUST route through RC §8.4 Cat 3 and BI §4.7 BI-022. No subsystem may silently prefer Beads or JSONL over git. Sensor (cross-spec scenario test): inject Beads-`closed`-no-merge-commit divergence and assert Cat 3 dispatch (NOT silent Beads-side correction); inject JSONL-references-missing-commit divergence and assert Cat 3 dispatch.
close_reason: Merged in pipeline. EM-INV-005 git-wins-on-completion-disagreement sensor in internal/core/gitwins_b3f65_test.go (618 lines). Placed in internal/core/ because the invariant spans BeadRecord/Checkpoint/CoarseStatus types and the sibling gitauthoritative_bi022_test.go follows the same precedent. Both divergence arms covered: Scenario A (Beads-closed/no-merge-commit) and Scenario B (JSONL-references-missing-SHA), with phrase-anchoring tests on EM-INV-005, BI-022, BI-023, RC §8.4 Cat 3. Forward-doc-marker SKIP on TestGitWinsB3F65_ForwardDocMarkerCat3Classifier is justified — Cat 3 classifier does not exist yet (gated-by-corpus-scale label); pattern matches sibling bi022 test. 7 PASS + 1 SKIP. Reviewer APPROVE no findings.

title: Define `RunID` typed alias (§6.1)
description: Implement `TYPE RunID = UUID` (UUIDv7 per EV §4.1; unique across project). The run identifier carried as `Harmonik-Run-ID` trailer + emitted on every run-scoped event.
close_reason: Merged at HEAD via worktree-agent-a03500a0806efade2. Implementer overscoped (re-fixed .golangci.yml v2 migration / coverage-gate bash-shim / forbid-import alignment in addition to runid.go) — those overlapping fixes had already landed earlier in main as orchestrator-tier infrastructure commits and were dropped during rebase (--ours on conflicting files). runid_test.go was converted from 'package core_test' (failed depguard core leaf rule on self-import) to 'package core' (consistent with sibling stateid_test.go / transitionid_test.go pattern). Implementer's testhelpers_test.go was retained as a robustness improvement (orthogonal to the testhelpers coverage-gate exemption already on main). Final commit: internal/core/runid.go (named type RunID uuid.UUID), runid_test.go, internal/testhelpers/testhelpers_test.go. make check green.

title: Define `StateID` typed alias (§6.1)

title: EM failure-class taxonomy (§8)
description: Implement the 6-class routing table: `transient` (handler `ErrTransient`; bounded retry + exponential backoff; cap exhaustion → reclassify `structural`); `structural` (handler `ErrStructural`; retry only after approach change - typically re-planning edge); `deterministic` (handler `ErrDeterministic`; MUST NOT retry; preserve state for post-mortem); `canceled` (handler `ErrCanceled` or operator `stop --immediate`; graceful cleanup; preserve last durable checkpoint); `budget_exhausted` (CP §4.5 budget counter exceedance OR handler `ErrBudget`; deny dispatch); `compilation_loop` (daemon-observed cap hit at cascade per EM-043; cap further retries; fail run). `structural` and `compilation_loop` are DISJOINT at emission; handler `ErrStructural` does NOT transition to `compilation_loop`. Failure classes emitted as `run_failed` payload field per EV §8.1.
close_reason: Failure-class taxonomy landed at internal/core/failureclass.go (6-value closed string enum: transient/structural/deterministic/canceled/budget_exhausted/compilation_loop; Valid/MarshalText/UnmarshalText). Disjointness invariant (structural vs compilation_loop) called out in godoc. Pattern matches coarsestatus.go modulo closed-enum semantics. Reviewer APPROVE-CLEAN. make check green.

title: MVH composition-root wires no-op PolicyEngine as production interface (resolves §A5)
description:
Per Phase 1 readiness analysis §A5 (MINOR), declare the bootstrap workflow's policy-engine bypass posture explicitly. The bootstrap subset has zero policy/guard/gate touchpoints (CP fully deferred — 0 of 85 beads in bootstrap per `bootstrap-subset.md` §1), but the orchestrator code path MUST be coded such that "no policy engine" is a first-class operating mode (NOT a `policyEngine == nil` branch — that violates SH-018's "no test-mode branches in production" carve-out per `specs/scenario-harness.md` §4.7).

Scope of this bead:
- Document the design decision: MVH composition root wires a no-op `PolicyEngine` (production interface — NOT nil, NOT a test-double) at the EM dispatcher boundary. The dispatcher always calls `policyEngine.Evaluate(...)`; the no-op always returns "permitted, no constraints."
- Surface this as a 2-line clarification in EM spec text (likely §6 schemas where `PolicyEngine` is referenced) or as a stand-alone EM-NN req per discipline §2.9 (mechanism class). Implementation lands in cluster-A composition root work (existing EM bootstrap beads `hk-b3f.*`).
- Tie to `bootstrap-subset.md` §1 ("Out of scope for v0… policy-engine guards") and `core-scope.md` §10 framing.

Why this matters: without the explicit "no-op as production interface" design, the first cluster-A implementer hits the policy-engine boundary and either (a) introduces a `policyEngine == nil` branch (SH-018 violation), (b) stubs with an obvious placeholder (`panic("not implemented")`) that breaks the trivial-slice walkthrough (`hk-kle6.1`), or (c) leaves the call out entirely (creates re-entry pain when CP lands post-MVH).

Companion to: existing CP cluster (`hk-a8bg.*` — 85 beads, 0 in bootstrap per `bootstrap-subset.md`). When CP lands post-MVH, the no-op gets swapped for the real PolicyEngine implementation at the composition root — no orchestrator-code changes required.

Sources: `docs/foundation/phase-1-readiness-gap-analysis.md` §A5 (MINOR), §"Recommended new tracking beads — Under EM implementation epic"; `docs/decompose-to-tasks/bootstrap-subset.md` §1 (out-of-scope); `specs/scenario-harness.md` §4.7 (SH-018 no-test-mode-branches); `specs/control-points.md` (the deferred surface); candidate bead `p1-policy-engine-noop-mode`.
close_reason: Implemented in 06f5db3: NoOpPolicyEngine wired as core.PolicyEngine interface VALUE in cmd/harmonik/main.go composition root (no nil-branch); resolves §A5. Reviewer APPROVE.

title: harmonik run: submit-to-existing-daemon instead of exit-5 on pidfile lock
description: Currently 'harmonik run' exits 5 (pidfile locked) when a daemon is up, so a persistent shared daemon is incompatible with the one-shot run habit: N agents each calling 'harmonik run' collide on the single-flywheel lock (hk-li14r) and serialize via restart-backoff instead of parallelizing. Change: when a live daemon socket exists, 'harmonik run <beads>' should SUBMIT its beads to the running daemon as a stream group and block until terminal, only starting a new daemon when none runs. Lets N agents share one --no-auto-pull --max-concurrent daemon transparently. Anchor: cmd/harmonik/main.go run path (~279-302), pidfile lock (internal/daemon lifecycle), internal/queue/cli/submit.go. Spec: execution-model.md, queue-model.md. Found 2026-05-31 debugging 3-agent daemon contention.
close_reason: done

title: T3 finding: main.go returns exit code 1 for ErrPidfileLocked (PL-008a requires 5)
description: daemon.Start correctly returns lifecycle.ErrPidfileLocked when a second daemon attempts to start. However cmd/harmonik/main.go does not inspect the error type and always calls os.Exit(1) for any error. PL-008a mandates exit code 5 for pidfile-locked. The lifecycle package contains exitcodes_pl008a_test.go with the spec for mapErrorToExitCode but no production implementation exists in cmd/harmonik/. External callers (systemd unit files, orchestrators) expecting exit code 5 to distinguish pidfile contention from other startup failures will receive exit code 1 instead. Reproduced by TestT3_DoubleInvocation (daemon.Start returns correct error; main.go exit code mapping missing).
close_reason: exit 5 on ErrPidfileLocked per PL-008a in main.go; 855bb59

title: Per-project model-selection config: .harmonik/config.yaml schema + loader
description:
## Scope

Define and implement the per-project configuration file that supplies tier-2 of the model-selection resolution chain (parent epic).

## File location

`.harmonik/config.yaml` at the project root (sibling of `.harmonik/daemon.sock`, `.harmonik/runs/`, etc.). YAML chosen for human-editability and comments; project already uses YAML adjacent (none today — first config file in `.harmonik/`).

## Proposed schema (v1)

```yaml
schema_version: 1

agents:
  claude-code:
    model: sonnet           # alias or full name; optional; omitted = let tier 3 default decide
    effort: medium          # one of low|medium|high|xhigh|max; optional
  claude-twin:
    model: sonnet
    effort: medium
  # future: pi, pi-twin, etc.
```

Top-level `agents` map keyed by `core.AgentType` string. Per-agent block carries optional `model` and `effort`. Unknown agent_type keys are ignored (forward-compat).

## Loader

- New helper in `internal/daemon/` (e.g. `projectconfig.go`) — read on daemon startup, cache in Config, surface to claim path via existing config plumbing.
- Absent file → tier 2 absent for all agent types (pass-through to tier 3). NOT an error.
- Parse error → daemon refuses to start with structural error (no silent fallback — config bugs must surface loudly).
- Unknown `schema_version` → refuse.

## Tier-3 defaults (compiled-in)

In the same change, define per-agent-type defaults next to the adapter wiring:

- `claude-code` → `model=sonnet, effort=medium` (matches current operator practice).
- `claude-twin` → empty (twin uses local fixture stream; model flag inert).
- Others → empty until an adapter lands.

## Tests

- Loader: schema_version=1 happy path; missing file; malformed YAML; unknown agent_type ignored; unknown effort/alias values flagged at load (or deferred to claim-time validation — decide in impl).
- Integration: claim with no label + config-present → tier 2 wins; claim with `model:opus` label + config-present → tier 1 wins.

## Refs

- Parent epic: hk-cfhj2.
- specs/execution-model.md §4.3 EM-012a tier-2 reserved slot (this bead is the first occupant of that pattern).
close_reason: project config loader + 4-tier EM-012b resolution chain implemented and tested in 4c93660

title: Fix 3 RED scenario tests from gap batch: happy-path beads use non-committing handler -> no_commit (hk-sk00a + hk-umemp)
description:
ATTEMPT 2 (prior run timed out no_commit @30min — the full -race scenario suite gate ate the budget before any commit). DO THIS: commit the fix the MOMENT the 3 target tests pass; do NOT run the full suite or -race before committing.

3 scenario-tagged tests are RED and must go GREEN (test-harness bug, NOT product):
- TestScenario_QueueSubmit_IdleWake_hk24xn1  (internal/daemon/scenario_queue_submit_dispatch_hksk00a_test.go)
- TestScenario_QueueSubmit_DeferredUndefer_hknbjht  (same file)
- TestScenario_ConcurrentMultiQueue_N2_HappyPath  (internal/daemon/scenario_concurrent_multiqueue_hkumemp_test.go)
MUST STAY GREEN: TestScenario_ConcurrentMultiQueue_N2_MidRunKill, TestScenario_RestartRecovery_QM002bDeadlock, all TestScenario_SingleActivePerName_*, TestScenario_EM015d_*/EM015e_*, TestScenario_PL001/PL002.

ROOT CAUSE: the happy-path bead's handler lands NO commit (events show agent_completed exit=0, commit_landed=false -> run_failed no_commit) so the bead never reaches closed -> IdleWake's run_completed assertion fails; DeferredUndefer's bead A never closes so B never un-defers (timeout); N2_HappyPath's beads never close. Product behavior (idle-wake, undefer, concurrent dispatch) is CORRECT — only 'complete WITH a commit' is mis-wired.

FIX: wire a COMMITTING handler so happy-path beads complete+close. Copy the working pattern from internal/daemon/scenario_happypath_n1_test.go (TestScenario_HappyPath_N1, real harmonik-twin-claude 'single-happy-path' that lands a commit), OR ExportedWorkLoopDeps + a committing WorktreeFactory (cf. mergeToMainCommittingFactory, mergetomain_hkftyvo_test.go:258). For DeferredUndefer: bead A must commit+close so B un-defers, then B commits+closes.

FAST GATE (run THIS, ~30-60s, then COMMIT immediately): go test -tags=scenario -run 'TestScenario_QueueSubmit_IdleWake_hk24xn1|TestScenario_QueueSubmit_DeferredUndefer_hknbjht|TestScenario_ConcurrentMultiQueue' ./internal/daemon/  (NO -race, single package). Commit as soon as these 3 pass. Refs: hk-sk00a, hk-umemp, hk-24xn1, hk-nbjht.

title: MVH-FIX-P0b: production binary doesn't claim beads despite ready queue
description:
After hk-o1sln landed (cmd.Dir wiring), the production binary STILL doesn't claim beads end-to-end:

Reproducer:
  go build -o /tmp/h ./cmd/harmonik
  D=$(mktemp -d); cd "$D" && br init --prefix sx && br create "trace" -p 2
  cd / && /tmp/h --project "$D" &
  sleep 6
  # observed: harmonik daemon banner; daemon_started + daemon_orphan_sweep_completed in jsonl
  # observed: bead remains open; NO run_started event; NO worktree created
  # expected: work loop polls (2s interval) → Ready() returns the bead → claim → worktree → launch handler

The work loop goroutine is launched per internal/daemon/daemon.go:265-282. Either:
1. The work loop never enters its main poll (some setup blocks).
2. Ready() returns empty despite `br ready --format json` showing the bead from the same dir.
3. Claim succeeds but CreateWorktree fails silently and reopens the bead (would emit run_started first).
4. There's an error path that silently `continue`s without emitting.

Investigation:
1. Add temporary stderr tracing to runWorkLoop in internal/daemon/workloop.go: log entry, log every poll iteration with readyIDs count, log every err.
2. Rebuild and run the reproducer.
3. Identify the silent-failure path.
4. Remove the temporary tracing. Add permanent stderr logging at every error branch in the work loop (workloop.go currently has many `if err != nil { continue }` paths that swallow errors silently — at minimum, log to stderr).
5. Fix the root cause.

If the root cause is non-trivial (e.g., Claim returns BrDbLocked and we don't retry), file a follow-up bead and at least surface the error to stderr.

Acceptance:
- Reproducer above results in either: bead status transitions, OR a clear stderr log showing what's blocking dispatch.
- `go test ./...` green.
- `gofmt -d` empty.

Priority: P0. The production binary is non-functional until this lands.
notes: resolve HEAD failed: daemon: resolveHEAD: git rev-parse HEAD: exit status 128
close_reason: fix: pin br subprocess CWD to projectDir via NewForProject; both Run and RunWithTimeout; stderr-log all silent-continue paths

title: queue submit: default omitted group kind to stream + warn on wave when daemon is shared
description:
Interim guard for hk-xyycc (wave-on-shared-daemon lockout), pending named-queues (hk-tigaf).

Problem: harmonik run --beads already defaults to stream (hk-7nbey, cmd/harmonik/run.go:73-83), but the persistent-daemon path uses 'harmonik queue submit <file>' with a hand-written QueueSubmitRequest JSON. internal/queue/cli/submit.go:54-60 passes the raw JSON straight through with NO default for an omitted group 'kind' — so the agent must spell out kind, and a wave there triggers QM-027 single-active lockout (queue_already_active -32010) for any second agent, while QM-024 also rejects appends to a wave (append_target_invalid -32011). This contradicts AGENTS.md daily-loop 'both agents put work into the queue'.

Two interim mitigations (do either/both; low-risk, no spec change required for #1):
1. CLI default: in submit.go, if a group omits 'kind', default it to 'stream' (matches run.go and AGENTS.md guidance 'Use stream for the daily loop'). Make the daily-loop submit safe-by-default. Verify the daemon's QueueSubmitRequest unmarshal does not already coerce empty kind to wave.
2. Soft warning: when 'queue submit' includes a kind:wave group, print a stderr warning that waves are immutable + single-active and lock out concurrent submitters on a shared daemon; suggest stream. (No rejection — wave is still legitimate for solo closed batches.)

Explicitly NOT relaxing QM-027 here — that is the named-queues epic's job (NQ-A1 amends QM-027 single-active -> single-active-per-name, hk-tigaf.2). This bead is the stop-gap until named-queues lands. Anchors: internal/queue/cli/submit.go:54-60; cmd/harmonik/run.go:73-83; specs/queue-model.md QM-027 (553-565), QM-024 (504-515); AGENTS.md daily-loop 35-104.
close_reason: done

title: Parallelism prep: AdapterRegistry needs sync.RWMutex
description:
Roadmap row #2 / blocker B from POST_MVH_PARALLELISM_ROADMAP.md §1.

Location: internal/handlercontract/adapterregistry_hc012.go:40-43

Problem: sealed and adapters fields are guarded by no mutex. ForAgent reads sealed and writes it on first call. Concurrent ForAgent calls = data race on the seal transition.

Fix: Add sync.RWMutex on AdapterRegistry struct. Guard sealed and adapters with the RW pair. ForAgent takes write-lock for the seal transition, read-lock afterward.

Anti-pattern (from §6): Do not use a package-level sync.Once — registry is constructed per-Start and a package singleton breaks concurrent tests.

Acceptance: go test -race ./internal/handlercontract/... passes with a new test that calls ForAgent from N=100 goroutines concurrently before and after sealing.

Size: XS
close_reason: sync.RWMutex added to AdapterRegistry; -race tests pass with N=100 goroutines

title: Model selection: 4-tier resolution chain (epic)
description:
## Problem

Today the daemon launches `claude` with no `--model` or `--effort` flag — the user's `claude` config / latest-default decides. The user wants to set per-project defaults (per coding-harness type) and override them per-task at intake.

Operationally: Opus for orchestrator-main-thread, Sonnet+medium-effort for implementer/reviewer dispatches. This is currently informal.

## Resolution chain (mirrors EM-012a workflow-mode precedence)

Walk in order, first non-empty wins:

1. **Per-task (bead).** A `model:<alias>` label (e.g. `model:opus`, `model:sonnet`, `model:claude-sonnet-4-6`) AND an optional `effort:<level>` label (`effort:low|medium|high|xhigh|max`). Multi-label conflict → emit `bead_label_conflict` and treat tier as absent (same shape as EM-012a tier-1 conflict handling). Unknown alias → same (treat tier as absent + event).
2. **Per-project.** `.harmonik/config.yaml` (new file) with structured schema keyed by `agent_type` (claude-code, claude-twin, pi, …). MVH-tolerable absence — pass-through.
3. **Per-agent-type default.** Compiled-in default per `core.AgentType` (claude-code → sonnet/medium, etc.). Lives next to the adapter.
4. **Built-in fallback.** Empty (let `claude` apply its own default).

Resolution runs ONCE at claim time. The resolved `(model, effort)` pair is sealed into the Run record and surfaced on `run_started`. Per-phase override (Opus for one node, Sonnet for another in a DOT graph) is deferred to Phase 3 — when a node-type spec lands, it gets a tier 0.5 above tier 1.

## Carrier for per-bead override

**Label, not body field.** Rationale: labels are first-class in `br`, already used for `workflow:<mode>` (precedent EM-012a tier-1). Body fields would require parser changes in the claim path and a new schema convention. Label form: `model:<alias>` + optional `effort:<level>`. Open question on whether `model:` should accept full names like `model:claude-sonnet-4-6` or only short aliases — recommend aliases-only at MVH to keep label values regex-clean.

## Edge cases

- **Hard limit (rate-limited opus).** Out of scope at MVH — pass through; let claude error and surface the failure. Phase-3 adapter-capability gate could pre-reject.
- **Stale model name.** Daemon does not maintain an allow-list; if claude rejects at exec, we get a launch failure. Fine for MVH.
- **`--effort` version skew.** Confirmed present in current `claude --help` output. No compatibility gate at MVH.

## Children

- (impl) launch-spec amendment — accept and pass `--model` / `--effort`. Includes HC-055 allow-list update.
- (impl) per-project config schema — `.harmonik/config.yaml` shape, loader, integration into claim path.

## Out of scope

- Per-phase / per-node selection (Phase 3 DOT).
- Adapters other than claude-code (pi/etc. wire-in is trivial follow-up once schema lands).
- Body-field carrier on the bead.

## Refs

- specs/execution-model.md §4.3 EM-012a (precedence pattern to mirror).
- specs/handler-contract.md §4.2 HC-055 (flag allow-list — must be amended to admit `--model` and `--effort`).
- internal/daemon/claudelaunchspec.go (argv construction site, line ~287).
notes: USER CLARIFICATION 2026-05-14: harmonik will gain more handler types over time (codex, pi, ...). The model-selection mechanism must be flexible at the system-to-handler layer: harmonik passes a structured 'model preference' descriptor; the handler validates and resolves it against what its underlying tool actually accepts. Some handlers (pi) can take 'basically any model name' — we cannot maintain a closed enum of valid models at the harmonik layer. Implication: harmonik validates the SHAPE of the model descriptor (non-empty string, no shell-injection chars), not the VALUE; handler-side rejection is the authoritative compatibility check. Add an explicit 'model preference is opaque to harmonik below the descriptor layer' invariant to the spec.
close_reason: all model-selection children landed: spec (hk-7zvh4), launch-spec (hk-xo03m), project config + resolution (hk-bfvk7)

Fix surface: worktree provisioning of .claude/settings.json — the Stop hook may not be wired in worktree contexts (vs the host project), or /exit (claude-code 2.1.145) bypasses Stop hooks where /quit triggers them. Required: (a) audit Stop-hook wiring in the worktree-provisioned .claude/settings.json, (b) add smoke test asserting outcome_emitted lands within stopHookGrace, (c) verify with a follow-up Go-touching dogfood. Blocks 'all Go-touching work through harmonik'.

title: Stop hook not firing after claude completes work in interactive mode
description:
Smoke v11 confirmed: claude reads agent-task.md, edits marker.txt, commits — but Stop hook never fires, so the daemon never receives bead_closed/outcome_emitted. The event stream has 8 events ending at agent_ready/run_started; no Stop, no agent_completed, no outcome_emitted.

The CHB spec assumes Stop fires after each assistant response (CHB-014 stop-hook outcome envelope), but in interactive TUI mode, Stop fires only on session exit (/quit, Ctrl-C). After claude responds, it sits at the REPL waiting for next input — Stop is NOT triggered.

Fix options:
(a) Kick-off message instructs claude to /quit after completion. Requires investigating whether claude executes slash-commands programmatically OR a paste-injected /quit + Enter dismisses the session.
(b) The daemon detects bead completion via a different signal: e.g., agent-side calls 'hk emit-outcome' as the last step (via the RequestHandler socket path that's currently stubbed as noopRequestHandler).
(c) Spec amendment: bead_closed is inferred from the worktree's commit landing AND the session going idle for N seconds.

Operationally: until this is fixed, the daemon's workloop blocks on Wait() forever even though claude is done. Capacity gate jams after first bead.

Evidence: smoke v11 $SMOKE_DIR=/private/var/folders/s9/kq5h9q_17t571w_xq_1q9f2r0000gp/T/tmp.uijvftI60b — events.jsonl ends at run_started; marker.txt has SMOKE-OK; worktree commit 2cc526e exists; bead still in_progress.

Smoke v10 RED diagnosis suggested --dangerously-skip-permissions which is CHB-007 forbidden. This bead surfaces the deeper assumption gap in CHB about interactive-mode Stop semantics.
close_reason: Daemon-side quit injection via pasteInjectQuitOnCommit; session-completion-instruction in agent-task.md; smoke v13 OPERATIONAL GREEN — sess.Wait returned cleanly, bead_closed + run_completed in events.jsonl (commit cbc4725)

title: Daemon br-close false-fails under --wave: BrDbLocked after 10 retries when N siblings close concurrently (idempotent close already succeeded)
description: Under --wave high max-concurrent, multiple daemon workers call br close (brcli.terminalTransitionWrite) near-simultaneously when beads finish in a cluster -> SQLite BrDbLocked -> 10 retries exhausted -> run_failed reported EVEN THOUGH the close is idempotent and actually succeeded (stderr: 'Skipped <id>: already closed'). REPRO: wave-2 hk-tksed run 019e7242-54b7 reported failed 'BrDbLocked persisted after 10 retries (1/11 BrUnavailable, 10/11 BrDbLocked) ... already closed' but the bead WAS closed and the commit a081a45 merged. EFFECT: false failures waste re-dispatch slots + mask real failures. ROOT: internal/daemon br-close path. FIX: (a) serialize daemon br terminal-transition writes behind a process-global mutex/file-lock; and/or (b) treat already-closed/idempotent-success as success not failure; and/or (c) larger retry budget + jittered backoff. Add a concurrent-close test.

title: session-keeper Phase-2 foundation: idle gate (Stop-hook .idle marker) + dispatch gate (.dispatching marker), NON-destructive
description:

NON-DESTRUCTIVE: this bead adds gating + a hook script ONLY; it does NOT add /clear, handoff, or any reset (that is the NEXT bead). Builds on internal/keeper (warn-mode watcher.go).

1. Stop-hook script: add scripts/keeper-stop-hook.sh — a Claude Code Stop hook that touches .harmonik/keeper/<agent>.idle on each Stop (agent from $HARMONIK_KEEPER_AGENT env or arg). Gives the watcher a crisp 'agent is at an await-input boundary' signal (verified: the Stop hook fires only at await-input boundaries). Document installing it in ~/.claude/settings.json hooks.
2. Idle gate (watcher, internal/keeper): add CrispIdle(agent) — true only when .harmonik/keeper/<agent>.idle is NEWER than the last gauge (.ctx) update (agent stopped AFTER its last activity). This is the Phase-2 idle signal; the Phase-1 quiesce heuristic is NOT sufficient for a destructive action.
3. Dispatch gate (watcher): add HoldingDispatch(agent) — reads .harmonik/keeper/<agent>.dispatching; if present the agent owns in-flight queue work and the cycle must defer. FAIL-CLOSED: unreadable/error => treat as holding (true). Add a helper to set/clear the marker.
4. Wire CrispIdle + HoldingDispatch as predicates the watcher COMPUTES and logs but does NOT yet act on (the cycle bead will consume them). No behavior change to warn-mode.
TESTS: CrispIdle true only when .idle newer than .ctx; HoldingDispatch true when .dispatching present and fail-closed on error; stop-hook script touches the marker for a sample invocation.
DONE: build+lint+gofmt green; tests pass; NO destructive behavior added anywhere. Refs: hk-djdng.

title: test2
description: t2

title: Enforce agent_ready timeout: watcher.Done() blocks indefinitely with no deadline
description:
## Problem
internal/daemon/workloop.go:504 blocks on <-watcher.Done() with no timeout or select. If the subprocess never exits (e.g., claude launched interactive TUI on a TTY, or hung waiting for input), hk blocks forever holding the bead in_progress.

## Expected (dogfood-smoke-trace.md §6)
agent_ready must arrive within spec.timeout seconds. A deadline should be enforced via context.WithTimeout or a select with time.After.

## Observed in smoke-2 (2026-05-12)
No hang occurred because claude exited quickly (non-TTY, empty stdin). But with --print or a real agent that blocks, hk would hang indefinitely.

## Acceptance
- A timeout context wraps the <-watcher.Done() wait.
- If deadline exceeded: subprocess is killed, bead is reopened, agent_failed event emitted with sub_reason=timeout.

## Refs
internal/daemon/workloop.go:504; docs/dogfood-smoke-trace.md §6 (timeout field in LaunchSpec)
close_reason: Implemented: agentready.go adds waitAgentReady with time.After timeout; workloop.go HC-056 path kills subprocess, reaps with agentReadyKillReapTimeout guard, reopens bead, emits agent_ready_timeout; reviewloop.go mirrors same pattern. All acceptance criteria met.

title: keeper: resolve tmux target from harmonik-<hash>-<agent> convention, not just --tmux
Cross-queue dedup guard (hk-a11re) misses completed-then-resubmitted bead -> double-run
description: DECISION (named-queues, 2026-06-03): This is a BUG, not intended idempotent re-run. A bead's identity is its bead_id and success is recorded once in git via the 'Refs: <id>' commit; re-running a COMPLETED bead in another queue would double-apply work, waste a dispatch slot, and risk a duplicate/conflicting commit. There is no idempotent-re-run use case for completed beads (retry applies only to FAILED beads, a different path). FIX: extend the cross-queue dedup guard at internal/daemon/workloop.go:1122 so it treats completed (and closed-dispatched-elsewhere) the same as ItemStatusDispatched -> fail the duplicate (do NOT hold), matching the existing dispatched behavior. Add a unit test covering completed-in-queue-A then submitted-to-queue-B. DO NOT expand scope to the P3 parity note (hk-6ri5k in_progress fall-through) -- leave that as a separate follow-up. Soften any commit claim to scope-accurate wording. --- ORIGINAL REVIEW --- Independent review of hk-a11re (2e16d12f, landed unreviewed). The cross-queue dedup guard (internal/daemon/workloop.go:1122) only matches ItemStatusDispatched, NOT completed. A bead that finished in queue A (completed) then submitted to queue B is NOT caught -> re-dispatches (double-runs), though the commit claims it prevents double-dispatch unconditionally. Reviewer confirmed NO TOCTOU (single dispatch goroutine; check+stamp both under the QueueStore write lock) - guard is otherwise sound and correctly fails (not holds) the duplicate. P3 parity (hk-6ri5k): queue-path deferred guard (workloop.go:1075) only special-cases deferred/closed/draft; an in_progress bead falls through to ClaimBead, fails, and burns a bounded retry (hk-6pspu). Minor. Refs: hk-a11re, hk-6ri5k.
close_reason: done

title: Daemon ledger-dep gating is INVERTED — defers depended-upon beads, dispatches beads with open blockers (deadlock + out-of-order)
description:
Observed 2026-05-31 on shared daemon queue 019e802d after appending the named-queues chain (epic hk-tigaf). The queue marked hk-tigaf.1 (NQ-R1, ZERO blocking deps) as deferred-for-ledger-dep, while hk-tigaf.10 (NQ-E1, with THREE open blockers A2/C1/D1) as pending/eligible. Exact inverse across all 11 items: a bead is deferred IFF another IN-QUEUE bead depends on it; eligible IFF nothing in-queue depends on it. The dependency-edge direction is reversed in the queue eligibility / deferred-for-ledger-dep computation. Effects: (1) roots of a dep chain never dispatch = deadlock; (2) leaves with open blockers dispatch out of order, failing or merging against nonexistent prerequisites. GROUND TRUTH: 'br show hk-tigaf.1' Dependencies={parent-child only}; 'br show hk-tigaf.10' Dependencies={->hk-tigaf.8, ->hk-tigaf.6, ->hk-tigaf.3 (blocks)}. ANCHORS: the streamEligible / ledger-dep eligibility check in internal/daemon (workloop dispatch) + internal/queue (deferred-for-ledger-dep). Blocks named-queues AND any dependency-ordered batch on the daemon. NOTE: also setting --status=deferred on the beads did NOT change the queue-item eligibility, so the daemon ignores bead.status for dispatch eligibility — confirm + fix both. Mitigation: hk-tigaf.1-.10 deferred to limit out-of-order dispatch (reverse when fixed).

## Root cause + fix

Root cause: internal/daemon/queueledger_bridge.go:69 brQueueLedger.BlocksEdge. The queue.BeadLedger contract is BlocksEdge(blocker, blocked)==true iff blocker must finish before blocked (i.e. blocked DEPENDS ON blocker); internal/queue Validate/append/rpc are all correct against that contract. But the production bridge impl listed dependencies of the WRONG bead and matched the WRONG edge orientation:
  edges := ListDependencies(ctx, blocker)        // should be 'blocked'
  ... FromBeadID==blocker && ToBeadID==blocked   // should be From==blocked && To==blocker
Beads stores a 'blocks' dep as issue_id=BLOCKED(dependent), depends_on_id=BLOCKER (verified: 'br dep list hk-tigaf.10 --direction both' returns {issue_id:hk-tigaf.10, depends_on_id:hk-tigaf.8, type:blocks}; brcli maps issue_id->FromBeadID, depends_on_id->ToBeadID). So the buggy code returned true exactly when 'a depends on b' — the opposite of the contract — which deferred chain ROOTS (because a dependent in-queue pointed at them) and left dependents with open blockers eligible.

Fix (queueledger_bridge.go BlocksEdge): list dependencies OF blocked and match the edge {FromBeadID:blocked, ToBeadID:blocker, EdgeKind:blocks}:
  edges, err := b.adapter.ListDependencies(ctx, blocked)
  ... if e.EdgeKind==EdgeKindBlocks && e.FromBeadID==blocked && e.ToBeadID==blocker { return true }
streamEligible/waveEligible (internal/queue/state.go) were already correct — they only read the stored item Status, which is computed once at submit/append via BlocksEdge. No queue-side change needed.

Regression tests:
- internal/daemon/queueledger_bridge_hkdv8qv_test.go — exercises the REAL bridge over a mock 'br' binary; chain R<-A<-B asserts BlocksEdge(R,A)=true, BlocksEdge(A,B)=true, inverse=false, and root has no blocker. Verified RED on the pre-fix code, GREEN after.
- internal/queue/ledgerdep_chain_hkdv8qv_test.go — submits chain R/A/B; asserts R pending + EligibleItems=[R] while A,B deferred-for-ledger-dep; and after R closes, A becomes eligible while B (blocked by open A) stays deferred.
Added test seam ExportedNewBRQueueLedger in internal/daemon/export_test.go.

Results: go build ./... PASS; go vet PASS; go test ./internal/queue/... PASS; targeted daemon ledger tests PASS. Full ./internal/daemon/... hits the pre-existing 600s wall-clock timeout from heavyweight claude/tmux subprocess integration tests (goroutine dumps show IO/parallel waits, not assertion failures) — unrelated to this change.

SECONDARY FINDING (separate bug, NOT fixed here — different function, design decision): confirmed the queue dispatch path ignores bead.status. internal/daemon/workloop.go:993-1041 the pre-claim ShowBead status==open guard is EXPLICITLY skipped for queue-path items (queueItemIndex>=0); only the br-ready path runs it. The only mid-flight protection is the ClaimBead-failure branch (workloop.go:1059-1083), which treats a bead as blocked ONLY on 'cannot claim blocked issue' / CoarseStatusBlocked — it does NOT cover status=deferred. So a queued bead set to deferred still dispatches (br allows in_progress<-deferred). Fix would be a pre-claim status guard on the queue path (or extend the isBlocked check to non-open statuses); recommend a dedicated bead since it is a broader gating-policy change, not in the BlocksEdge function.
close_reason: Fixed by be686b41 on main: BlocksEdge now lists deps of the blocked bead and matches From==blocked To==blocker (correct orientation). Regression tests landed (queueledger_bridge_hkdv8qv_test.go, ledgerdep_chain_hkdv8qv_test.go), reviewer APPROVE. Named-queues chain hk-tigaf.1-.10 dispatched in dependency order and fully closed, validating. Secondary finding (queue-path ignores deferred status) spun out to a new bead.

title: Fix nil watcher dereference in workloop when substrate path returns nil watcher
description: When tmuxSubstrate is wired (deps.substrate != nil), handler.Launch delegates to launchViaSubstrate which returns watcher=nil (documented: callers use HookSessionStore.WaitForOutcome instead). The workloop at workloop.go:670-672 spawns a goroutine that immediately calls watcher.Done() without nil-guarding — panics on SIGSEGV. Affects: (1) goroutine at line 670 watcher.Done(), (2) HC-056 timeout path at line 687 watcher.Done(), (3) line 727 watcher.Err(), (4) waitWithSocketGrace at waitsocketgrace.go:74,80 watcher.Done(). Fix: when watcher is nil (substrate path), skip the watcher-based goroutine entirely and let WaitForOutcome be the sole completion signal in waitWithSocketGrace. Also need a nil guard in waitWithSocketGrace for the watcher.Done() calls (lines 74,80). Surfaced by hk-kqdpf.5 smoke run 2026-05-13.
close_reason: nil-guarded all 5 watcher call sites in workloop.go and waitsocketgrace.go; substrate path (watcher==nil) no longer panics; 3 regression tests added; go vet clean

title: MVH-FIX: EV-002b — handler/adapter_claudecode.go imports internal/core
description:
EV-002b sensor in `internal/core/handlereventidroute_ev002b_test.go` forbids any internal/handler-side package from importing internal/core. R8 (hk-prug5) landed `internal/handler/adapter_claudecode.go` + its test, both of which import core for `core.AgentTypeClaudeCode` (a constant) and `core.EventEnvelope` (a type). Sensor is red.

Background: R7 (hk-zxpj2) hit the same constraint and resolved it by adding `handlercontract.NewSessionID()` + `handlercontract.CollectingEmitter` etc. — a boundary layer in handlercontract that re-exports / wraps what handler-side code needs without importing core directly.

Same pattern here:
1. In `internal/handlercontract/`, expose `AgentTypeClaudeCode` as a typed constant (re-export of `core.AgentTypeClaudeCode`, or a parallel constant if EV-002b also forbids handlercontract→core; check the sensor's package allowlist first — handlercontract is probably allowed since it's the boundary).
2. For `core.EventEnvelope`: either (a) handlercontract re-exposes it as a named type alias (`type EventEnvelope = core.EventEnvelope`) if Go lets us, OR (b) define a slim interface in handlercontract that exposes the fields adapter_claudecode actually reads (probably just `Type`, payload accessor, headers).
3. Update `internal/handler/adapter_claudecode.go` and its test to import only handlercontract, not core.
4. Drop the core import from both files.

Verify:
- `go build ./...`
- `go test ./internal/core/ -run TestEventEV002b` — green.
- `go test ./internal/handler/...` — green.
- `go test ./...` — green.
- `gofmt -d` empty.

If EV-002b ALSO forbids handlercontract→core, this becomes a deeper refactor; surface as a hard blocker in your report and we'll re-scope. Check the sensor body first.
close_reason: added handlercontract/coreexports.go with type aliases + const; dropped core import from adapter_claudecode.go and its test; EV-002b sensor green

title: Post-MVH parallelism — N concurrent runs in foreground binary
description:
## Scope

Lift the daemon from `MaxConcurrent=1` to N>1 concurrent workflow runs inside one foreground binary, per `POST_MVH_PARALLELISM_ROADMAP.md` (repo root). MVH (workflow-modes corpus) just shipped — this is the first post-MVH unlock.

## Roadmap rows already landed (v32)

| Row | Bead | Where |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | RunID on `core.Event` envelope | `internal/core/event.go:77` |
| 2 | `AdapterRegistry` `sync.RWMutex` (`hk-cdb9f`) | `internal/handlercontract/adapterregistry_hc012.go` |
| 3 | Per-run Drain on `busImpl` (`hk-fx6zl`) | `internal/eventbus/busimpl.go` |
| 4 | RunRegistry on Daemon struct (`hk-7s9z9`) | `internal/daemon/runregistry.go` |
| 8 | JSONLWriter batching drainer (`hk-5zode`) | `internal/eventbus/jsonlwriter.go` |

## Children (rows remaining)

Children file as separate beads with `next-init` + `parallelism` labels. Order: 6 → 5 → 9 → 7 → 10 → 11. `hk-p4xbw` (br --claim atomicity) pairs with row 9.

## Out of scope (per roadmap §5)

- Multi-project, daemonization, reconciliation, adapter rotation, ntm/tmux, cross-machine controller, JSONL rotation, operator pause/stop RPCs, workflow composition.

## Anti-patterns (per roadmap §6)

- Do NOT introduce a worker-pool abstraction at the work-loop level (goroutine-per-bead is the shape).
- Do NOT shard JSONL per run.
- Do NOT push MaxConcurrent enforcement into bus or adapter.
- Do NOT use a global var for run registry.
- Do NOT fix AdapterRegistry race with package-level sync.Once.

## Acceptance (epic-level)

All 6 child rows land; smoke + throughput tests pass under `go test -race`; queue depth metric emitted.
close_reason: Post-MVH parallelism epic complete — all 6 roadmap rows landed plus claim-guard companion bead.

title: Parallelism row 6: MaxConcurrent field on daemon.Config (default 1)
description:
## Source
`POST_MVH_PARALLELISM_ROADMAP.md` row 6.

## Task
Add `MaxConcurrent int` field to `daemon.Config` (`internal/daemon/daemon.go`). Default value MUST be 1 — preserves MVH single-threaded semantics. Wire the field through the composition root in `cmd/harmonik/main.go` (currently does not set it).

## Acceptance
- `daemon.Config.MaxConcurrent` exists, documented in godoc with the default-1 invariant.
- A zero-value Config still behaves as MaxConcurrent=1 (zero-value compatibility).
- Composition root exposes a `--max-concurrent` CLI flag (or env var; pick what matches existing conventions in `cmd/harmonik/main.go`).
- No behavior change at default; row 5 is what consumes this.

## Out of scope
Lifting MaxConcurrent>1 in the work loop (row 5). Operator-tunable rate-limit on ClaimBead (row 9).

## Anti-pattern
Do NOT push MaxConcurrent enforcement into the bus or the adapter — ceiling lives in the work-loop scheduler.
close_reason: MaxConcurrent field added to daemon.Config (zero=1) and --max-concurrent flag wired in main.go; commit 1bb7c34

title: Parallelism row 5: work-loop goroutine-per-active-bead up to MaxConcurrent
description:
## Source
`POST_MVH_PARALLELISM_ROADMAP.md` row 5 (the GATE row).

## Task
Replace the current single-threaded work loop in `internal/daemon/workloop.go` with a poll-and-dispatch loop that holds up to `MaxConcurrent` (per row 6) goroutines simultaneously. Each goroutine handles one claimed bead end-to-end (claim → worktree → mode dispatch → close/reopen → unregister). Goroutines are tracked via the existing RunRegistry (`internal/daemon/runregistry.go`, landed v32 as `hk-7s9z9`).

Shape (from roadmap):
1. Outer poll loop: every 2s (current poll interval) call `brAdapter.Ready()`.
2. For each ready bead, if `runRegistry.Len() < cfg.MaxConcurrent`, claim and spawn a goroutine.
3. Goroutine: register in RunRegistry → existing dispatch path → on exit Unregister.
4. Outer loop respects context cancellation; on Done, waits for all in-flight goroutines (use per-run Drain — landed v32 as `hk-fx6zl`).

## Acceptance
- With `MaxConcurrent=1`, behavior is byte-identical to today.
- With `MaxConcurrent>1`, two ready beads dispatch concurrently; both close; both events appear in JSONL with distinct `run_id`.
- `go test -race ./internal/daemon/...` clean.
- The current single-threaded smoke tests still pass unchanged.

## Anti-pattern
Do NOT use a worker pool fed by a work-item queue. One goroutine per active bead — the in-flight count must equal `runRegistry.Len()`.

## Dependencies
Row 6 (config field). Row 7 (smoke test) gates on this.
close_reason: goroutine-per-bead loop landed; MaxConcurrent gate via RunRegistry.Len(); TestWorkLoop_TwoConcurrentBeads confirms peak in-flight=2 at N=2

title: Parallelism row 9: semaphore on ClaimBead concurrency in work loop
description:
## Source
`POST_MVH_PARALLELISM_ROADMAP.md` row 9.

## Task
In `internal/daemon/workloop.go`, add a buffered-channel semaphore (size = `cfg.MaxConcurrent`) guarding simultaneous `brAdapter.ClaimBead` calls. Prevents SQLite contention storms at N>5 against `.beads/beads.db`.

Pairs with `hk-p4xbw` (br --claim non-atomicity finding) — the semaphore reduces the contention surface but is NOT the atomicity fix. Both are needed: semaphore for throughput, atomicity guard (status-check-before-dispatch) for correctness.

## Acceptance
- Concurrent ClaimBead calls never exceed `MaxConcurrent` simultaneously.
- Under N=10 ready beads + MaxConcurrent=4, no `BrDbLocked` storm (verify via JSONL `sqlite_lock_retries` field — landed alongside, see row 11).
- Test: 10-bead burst at MaxConcurrent=4, count of `BrDbLocked` retries stays bounded.

## Dependencies
Row 5 (work loop). Row 6 (Config). Coordinates with `hk-p4xbw` (file separately as related, not blocks).
close_reason: claimSem channel (size=MaxConcurrent) in runWorkLoop guards ClaimBead calls; concurrentFixtureLedger ShowBead fix; TestWorkLoop_ClaimSemaphore_BoundsClaimConcurrency passes -race; commit 8f3b9c7
