title: Twin: settings.json reader + Stop hook caller (audit items 1+2, critical path)
description:
## Scope

Items 1 + 2 from docs/twin-parity-audit-2026-05-14.md §4. Tightly coupled; one bead per audit §6.

Twin gains the ability to read the worktree `.claude/settings.json` and execute the Stop hook command at the right YAML-script cue. This is the user's stated capability requirement for hk-wuu5h ("the twin should READ that file and CALL the hook commands in the same way real claude would") and unlocks Fix 9 (permissions.allow verification) and Fix 11b (Stop hook → bead_closed end-to-end).

## Required changes

### cmd/harmonik-twin-claude

1. **New flag `--worktree-path <path>`** on the twin binary. Optional. When set, the twin reads `<path>/.claude/settings.json` at startup.

2. **Settings parser.** Minimal — extract:
   - `dangerouslyAllowedPermissions` (presence is enough; value not interpreted by the twin).
   - `hooks.Stop` (the Stop hook command(s) declared per CHB / WM-040a).
3. **`twin_settings_loaded` wire message** emitted once at startup with fields `permissions_present: bool`, `stop_hook_present: bool`, `stop_hook_command: string` (truncated for log-readability). New NDJSON message type — add to wire.go.

4. **Hook-call-on-cue.** New YAML script step (or implicit cue) `call_stop_hook` that:
   - Reads the loaded Stop hook command (must have been loaded at startup).
   - Executes it via `os/exec` with the env shape real claude uses for Stop hook invocations (look at CHB / claude-hook-bridge.md to confirm env vars; minimum: pass-through of the current process env + `CLAUDE_HOOK_TYPE=Stop`).
   - Hook output goes through normal stdout/stderr; the daemon's hook-bridge ingests it.
   - Wire emit `twin_hook_called` with `hook_type: "Stop"`, `exit_code: <int>`, `duration_ms: <int>`.

5. **Error handling:**
   - `--worktree-path` set but `.claude/settings.json` absent → emit `twin_settings_loaded` with both flags false; do not error. (Realistic: workflows may not need hooks.)
   - settings.json malformed JSON → emit error wire message + exit 1. Operator MUST see this loudly.
   - `call_stop_hook` without prior settings load → emit error wire message + exit 1.
   - Hook command exits non-zero → emit `twin_hook_called` with exit_code; do NOT exit the twin (real claude doesn't either; daemon-side handles outcome).

## Tests

- Unit: parser handles valid settings.json, missing file, malformed JSON, missing `hooks.Stop`.
- Unit: `call_stop_hook` with a fake hook (e.g., `/bin/true` or a `printf` shim) produces the expected wire message.
- Integration: spin up the twin against a test worktree with a real settings.json + a hook script that echoes a known string; assert the wire-message stream contains `twin_hook_called` with exit_code=0 and that the hook script's stdout was captured by the daemon-side reader.

## Spec touchpoints

- `specs/scenario-harness.md` — note the new twin flag + wire messages (additive, no contract change).
- `specs/claude-hook-bridge.md` — twin reuses CHB's Stop hook conventions; confirm env-var contract.

## Out of scope

- commit-on-cue (separate audit item 3, will be in hk-8ys88).
- Other hooks beyond Stop (PostToolUse, etc.) — same mechanism, separate bead.
- paste-receipt / reject-input-before-ready / startup-delay (audit items 4-6).

## Dependency

Parent: hk-wuu5h (scenario-testing umbrella).
Unblocked by hk-cno1z (closed — audit landed).

## Exit criteria

- Twin accepts `--worktree-path` and parses settings.json.
- `twin_settings_loaded` + `twin_hook_called` wire messages emitted at the right cues.
- One unit test file + at least one integration smoke test.
- All daemon tests still pass.
close_reason: settings.json reader + Stop hook caller implemented; twin_settings_loaded + twin_hook_called + call_stop_hook step landed; all tests green on worktree-agent-a0c018baa254f39cf SHA 1d244d1

title: T62: CLI hk queue {submit,append,status,dry-run} subcommand family
description: Scope: add hk queue {submit,append,status,dry-run} to cmd/harmonik/main.go. Pre-flag.Parse dispatch per PL-028c. Each subcommand opens daemon.sock, sends JSON-RPC, prints response, and exits with the exit code per PL-008a / ON §8. Spec ref: process-lifecycle.md §4.4 PL-028 + PL-028c. Files: cmd/harmonik/main.go, internal/queue/cli/{submit,append,status,dryrun}.go. Exit code mapping (applies to all 4 subcommands — queue-submit, queue-append, queue-status, queue-dry-run): (a) Success: exit 0; output is JSON to stdout (machine-readable; the response RECORD per §2.10). (b) Validation error (any QueueValidationReason): exit 1; the full JSON-RPC error body (with typed code and message) is written to stdout — not stderr — so callers can parse the error type. (c) Daemon not running (socket absent or connection refused): exit 17 per PL-008a / ON §8. (d) Transport or protocol error (malformed response, framing error, unknown JSON-RPC error code outside -32010..-32019): exit with the applicable PL-008a code for the error class; if no specific code applies, exit 2 (general client-side error). queue-status returns JSON even when queue is null (the {queue: null} envelope per §2.10 QueueStatusResponse); always exit 0 if daemon is reachable. Acceptance: every subcommand callable from a shell; daemon-down exits 17; unknown verb exits 2; both --flag value and --flag=value forms accepted; exit code table verified by shell-level integration test. Deps: T60 (hk-nomxl), T61 (hk-010vp). Part of epic hk-lj0pb.
close_reason: 083e14a feat(queue/cli): T62 hk queue {submit,append,status,dry-run} subcommands; internal/queue/cli/{submit,append,status,dryrun}.go + cmd/harmonik/main.go

title: MVH-R10: main work loop in internal/daemon
description:
MVH_ROADMAP row #10. The keystone integration: a goroutine inside daemon.Start that polls the bead ledger, claims a ready bead, creates a worktree, launches a Claude Code subprocess, watches it to completion, and closes the bead. After this lands the binary actually DOES something.

Location: NEW file `internal/daemon/workloop.go` + tests; minor edits to `internal/daemon/daemon.go` to launch the loop after orphan sweep.

Loop shape (one in-flight bead at a time per MVH scope — MaxConcurrent comes post-MVH):

  for ctx not cancelled:
    beads = brcli.ListBeadsByStatus(ctx, "ready")
    if len(beads) == 0:
      sleep 2 seconds; continue
    pick beads[0]
    claim = brcli.ClaimBead(ctx, bead.ID, runID)
    if claim.Conflict { continue }
    runID = newRunID()
    wt   = workspace.CreateWorktree(ctx, projectDir, runID, bead.ID)
    spec = handler.LaunchSpec{
      Binary: cfg.HandlerBinary,            // configurable; defaults to "claude" but tests override
      Args:   []string{ ... },
      Env:    cfg.HandlerEnv,
      WorkDir: wt.Path,
      Role:   "implementer",
    }
    sess, watcher, err := handler.NewHandler().Launch(ctx, spec)
    if err { ReopenBead; continue }
    <-watcher.Done           // wait for child to finish
    outcome := sess.Outcome()
    if outcome.ExitCode == 0:
      brcli.CloseBead(ctx, bead.ID, "auto-close: success")
    else:
      brcli.ReopenBead(ctx, bead.ID, fmt.Sprintf("auto-reopen: exit=%d", outcome.ExitCode))
    emit run_completed event
    cleanup worktree

Critical: `HandlerBinary` field on `daemon.Config` (default "claude" if empty). Exploratory testing wave needs to override this with a twin binary; if you hardcode "claude" the wave can't run without burning API credits. See `EXPLORATORY_TESTING_PLAN.md` row #6 anti-patterns.

Wire registration in daemon.Start:
- Construct AdapterRegistry, call handler.Register(reg), seal it.
- Launch the work loop in a goroutine; Start blocks until ctx is cancelled (SIGINT/SIGTERM).

Emit events at key points: `run_started` (after CreateWorktree returns), `run_completed` (after CloseBead/ReopenBead). Both per event-model §8.

Acceptance:
- `go build ./...`
- A new test in `internal/daemon/workloop_test.go` seeds a fake-ready bead via a stubbed brcli adapter, points HandlerBinary at a `sh -c 'exit 0'` stub, runs the loop with a 5-second context, asserts the bead was closed and a `run_completed` event was emitted.
- `go test ./internal/daemon/...` green.

Out of scope: row #11's full end-to-end smoke test with a real twin. Out of scope: MaxConcurrent > 1.
close_reason: MVH main work loop implemented in internal/daemon/workloop.go + daemon.Start wire-in; tests green

title: EV-040: missing heartbeat treated as daemon liveness failure with reconnect backoff
Auto session-lifecycle for long-running agents: context-threshold handoff->clear->resume (no-compaction managed-context mechanism)
description:
Spin-off of the flywheel (managed context, no compaction). User idea 2026-06-01: an external supervisor per long-running orchestrator agent (flywheel, named-queues) watches context fill; at ~80% inject a wrap-up warning; at ~90-95% when IDLE inject /session-handoff -> wait HANDOFF.md -> /clear -> /session-resume NAME. Preserves intent across reset (HANDOFF.md) vs lossy auto-compaction.
FEASIBILITY confirmed (Claude Code expert, 2026-06-01): buildable as an orchestration pattern, NOT a built-in. MECHANISM: (1) SIGNAL = statusLine script; its stdin JSON exposes context_window.used_percentage + token breakdown; persist used_percentage to a per-session file (e.g. /tmp/claude-context-SESSIONID), external watcher polls (updates after each assistant msg, debounced 300ms). Requires Claude Code v2.1.132+. NOTE: the session .jsonl transcript does NOT carry per-message token counts (dead end). (2) IDLE = Stop hook fires after each response before control returns = safe-to-inject. (3) INJECT = tmux send-keys /session-handoff -> wait HANDOFF.md mtime change -> /clear -> /session-resume NAME. (4) BACKSTOP = PreCompact hook fires before auto-compaction and CAN BLOCK it (decision:block / exit 2) -> prevent lossy compaction, trigger handoff instead. HARD PREREQ: session MUST be named (/rename or --name) or /session-resume opens interactive picker and the injector HANGS.
PHASING (simpler-first): P1 = warn-only, non-destructive (statusLine->file->watcher injects an 80pct wrap-up warning; validate signal+inject safely, no reset). P2 = full auto handoff/clear/resume cycle (idle-gated on Stop, named-session, mtime-confirmed handoff, anti-reset-loop guard, daemon-coordination so no reset mid-dispatch).
Reuses harmonik pasteinject + event bus + session-handoff/resume skills; EXTENDS harmonik supervise from bead-implementer claudes to ORCHESTRATOR claudes. Co-design with named-queues (supervise/pasteinject lane). Sibling of hk-uxm0j agent-comms (both = supervisor coordinating long-running agents). Likely a kerf work (codename session-keeper).

title: DOGFOOD-FRICTION: daemon CloseBead fails with 'brcli: BrUnavailable persisted after 3 retries'
description:
2026-05-19 dogfood #2 (hk-75rij). The work landed (02e31e1 = real spec edit + merge to main) but daemon's terminal-transition write failed:

  daemon: workloop: CloseBead hk-75rij: brcli.terminalTransitionWrite:
    br exec: brcli: BrUnavailable persisted after 3 retries: brcli: Unavailable

This caused exit code 1 + queue archived as failed, despite the actual work succeeding. Manual 'br close hk-75rij' worked fine immediately after.

Hypothesis: the brAdapter or br binary became 'Unavailable' (locked? dead? socket dead?) DURING the close-bead transition. Three retries with whatever backoff didn't recover. The lock or unavailability resolved by the time we manually tried, so it's transient.

Likely causes:
(a) Concurrent br ops from a different process (kerf, sub-agents, etc.) held a lock during the close window.
(b) The daemon's brcli adapter shares a database connection that timed out / closed.
(c) The 3-retry budget is too small for normal contention.

Investigation: read internal/brcli/exec.go (or wherever br exec is wrapped). Look at the retry policy + the Unavailable error class.

Fix candidates:
- Increase retry count or backoff window.
- Detect lock-conflict specifically (vs. binary-not-found) and retry differently.
- Add fallback: if close persists fails, write a delayed-close record that the orphan-sweep on next startup will reconcile (like the .beads-intents/ mechanism that's already in .harmonik/ per HANDOFF v49).
- Add observability: log the actual underlying error from br (currently just 'Unavailable' which is too coarse).

Severity P1 — dogfood is operational but every successful run now requires manual cleanup until this is fixed.
close_reason: BrUnavailable retry widened to 10 attempts / 50ms-2s exp backoff at 73ade77; new dblockretry.go constants UnavailableRetryMax/Base/Cap; spec BI-031 step (4c-transient) added

title: Queue dispatch path ignores bead ledger status — deferred/blocked queued bead still dispatches (no pre-claim status guard)
description: Discovered during the hk-dv8qv fix (2026-05-31). The queue dispatch path does NOT honor a bead's ledger status. At internal/daemon/workloop.go:993-1041 the pre-claim ShowBead status==open guard is explicitly SKIPPED for queue-path items (queueItemIndex>=0); only the br-ready path runs it. The sole mid-flight protection is the ClaimBead-failure branch (workloop.go:1059-1083), which treats a bead as blocked only on 'cannot claim blocked issue' / CoarseStatusBlocked — it does NOT cover 'deferred'. Consequence: a queued bead set to status=deferred (or other non-open status) via br update still dispatches. Observed: setting hk-tigaf.1-.10 to deferred did not stop their queue eligibility. Fix options: add a pre-claim status guard on the queue path (defer/skip items whose bead is not open), or extend the isBlocked check to cover non-open statuses (deferred/closed). Anchors: internal/daemon/workloop.go:993-1041 + 1059-1083. Related: hk-dv8qv (the inverted ledger-dep gating, separate root cause).
close_reason: done

title: WriteVerdictExecuted writes lock trailer at fd current offset (no O_APPEND/seek-to-end) — latent stale-lock metadata corruption
description: From retro-review of the unreviewed reconciliation window (hk-63oh.5 area). reconciliationlock_rc002a.go:83: WriteVerdictExecuted writes the verdict trailer at the fd's CURRENT offset (file opened O_RDWR, no O_APPEND, no Seek(0,SeekEnd)). Correct TODAY only because the in-process call sequence leaves the offset at end-of-metadata and nothing reads via this fd. If any future code reads from the held fd before the trailer write, the trailer overwrites creator_pid/run_id, corrupting the stale-lock discriminator. FIX: open with O_APPEND or Seek(0,io.SeekEnd) before the trailer write. LOW (latent, not currently triggerable).
close_reason: done

title: Daemon auto-close path closes bead without merging run branch to main (Phase 2 blocker)
description:
**Phase 2 round-trip failure surfaced during the hk-09tne first-demo run on 2026-05-14.**

## What happened

Drove `hk-09tne` end-to-end via `/tmp/harmonik --project ... --max-concurrent 1`. The agent successfully:
1. Read `.harmonik/agent-task.md`
2. Read README.md (empty)
3. Wrote README.md with the Phase-1 milestone line
4. Committed `d07f324` onto branch `run/019e2870-59eb-74ba-963b-280ec6e0fda0`
5. Exited via `/exit`

The daemon then:
- Observed `exit=0`, no socket outcome, watcher OK
- Took the `case socketOutcome == nil && ei.exitCode == 0 && !watcherFailed:` branch at `internal/daemon/workloop.go:879`
- Called `brAdapter.CloseBead(...)` → hk-09tne is now CLOSED in beads
- Emitted `run_completed{success:true, summary:"auto-close: exit=0"}`
- **Never merged `run/019e2870-59eb-74ba-963b-280ec6e0fda0` into main.**

Result: main HEAD is still `f026016`, README.md on main is still empty, but the bead is closed. The agent's commit is sitting on an orphan run-branch.

## Where the gap is

`internal/daemon/workloop.go:846-907` — Step 9 ("emit terminal event and close or reopen the bead") has THREE branches:
1. `ProgressMsgTypeAgentCompleted` → CloseBead + emit run_completed
2. `socketOutcome==nil && exit==0 && !watcherFailed` → CloseBead + emit run_completed
3. default → ReopenBead + emit run_completed{success:false}

**None of the three branches merge the run-branch into main.** The success branches assume the work is already on main, but in production the agent commits onto `run/<run_id>` inside a worktree that was branched from main's HEAD.

## Expected behavior

On success branches (1 and 2), before `CloseBead`:
- `git fetch origin` (refresh main pointer)
- Resolve run-branch tip SHA
- Fast-forward main to that SHA (or refuse + reopen if non-FF)
- Push origin main (per push-autonomy directive v40)
- Optionally delete the run-branch
- THEN `CloseBead`

The success signal in the hk-09tne bead body is explicit:
> - merge commit on main authored by the implementer
> - bead_closed event in .harmonik/events/events.jsonl
> - outcome_emitted{kind=approved} event

None of these three signals appeared. Only `run_completed{auto-close}` did.

## Repro

1. `br update hk-09tne --priority 0; br update hk-09tne --status=open`
2. tmux new-session -d -s harmonik-phase2 -c /Users/gb/github/harmonik
3. tmux send-keys -t harmonik-phase2 '/tmp/harmonik --project /Users/gb/github/harmonik --max-concurrent 1' Enter
4. Wait ~25s
5. Observe: hk-09tne CLOSED, main unchanged, commit on `run/<run_id>`.

## Event-log excerpt

run_id `019e2870-59eb-74ba-963b-280ec6e0fda0`:
- run_started (bead=hk-09tne)
- handler_capabilities
- session_log_location
- skills_provisioned
- launch_initiated
- agent_ready
- run_completed{success:true, summary:"auto-close: exit=0"}

NO: outcome_emitted, bead_closed, workspace_merged, branch_merged, push events.

## Artifacts preserved

- Run branch `run/019e2870-59eb-74ba-963b-280ec6e0fda0` (commit d07f324) kept as evidence.
- Agent session log: /Users/gb/.claude/projects/-Users-gb-github-harmonik--harmonik-worktrees-019e2870-59eb-74ba-963b-280ec6e0fda0/019e2870-5d7e-702e-aec8-f4918d4596c2.jsonl
- Events log line for run_completed in .harmonik/events/events.jsonl

## Suggested next step

Spec the merge-to-main step in execution-model.md (where in the Step-9 branch order it sits, what to do on non-FF, push policy) BEFORE coding. The merge step is also where outcome_emitted{kind=approved} should fire — currently never emitted in the auto-close path.
close_reason: Daemon merge-to-main + EM-052/053 spec + tests landed (commit f7d735f)

description: Scope: extend daemon startup to read .harmonik/queue.json after daemon.state/daemon.upgrading. Recognized schema_version -> load. This cross-check must complete before daemon reaches ready state and before any dispatch-loop tick. title: agent-comms cursor recv is NOT serialized: false single-writer claim (bounded dup-delivery, latent trap)
description: From retro-review of the agent-comms bus (unreviewed window, epic hk-uxm0j T7/T8 da48cb7f/e6a3add3). socket.go:311 spawns a goroutine per connection with NO op-serialization and NO mutex in the cursor store/recv handler (zero Lock() in commscursor.go / commsrecvhandler_nnwaa.go), yet commscursor.go:22-23 ASSERTS 'single-writer: socket-op serialisation enforces this; no concurrent writes.' That guarantee does not exist. Two concurrent 'comms recv --agent X' both Get cursor C, scan the same backlog, both Advance. File write is atomic (temp+rename) and both scan from C so they advance deterministically -> NO message lost/skipped; worst case is BOUNDED DUPLICATE DELIVERY, which the NORMATIVE dedup-on-event_id rule already absorbs. So at-least-once HOLDS, NOT a data-loss BLOCK. But the code ships on a FALSE invariant that will bite the first time an op mutates more state under the same assumption. Reviewer severity: HIGH (latent). FIX: add a per-agent (or single) mutex around the Get->scan->Advance critical section in HandleCommsRecv, OR correct the commscursor.go doc comment to state the real guarantee (atomic-write + at-least-once + app-layer dedup, NOT single-writer serialization).
close_reason: done

title: Parallelism prep: busImpl needs per-run Drain coordination
description:
Roadmap row #3 / blocker A from POST_MVH_PARALLELISM_ROADMAP.md §1.

Location: internal/eventbus/busimpl.go:50-51

Problem: wg sync.WaitGroup is process-level. When two task runs are live and both dispatch async/observer consumers, Drain() waits for ALL in-flight goroutines across both runs, not just the calling run's. A slow consumer from run A delays shutdown of run B. Destroys fair termination semantics.

Fix: Add Drain(ctx, runID) signature OR per-run drainer registry inside busImpl. Each Emit tags its goroutine with the calling run's ID (read from event envelope per row #1 / hk-n9f51). Drain waits only for goroutines tagged with the given run.

Acceptance: Test with two concurrent runs A and B, A has a 5-second hanging consumer, Drain(ctx, B) returns in <100ms even with A still draining.

Size: M
close_reason: per-run drainer registry + DrainRun method on busImpl; RunDrainer interface for type-assertion access; acceptance test passes in <100ms

title: session-keeper Phase-1 scaffold: internal/keeper pkg + 'harmonik keeper' subcommand stub + single-keeper lockfile + .managed opt-in guard
description:
Scaffold the session-keeper Phase-1 subsystem (codename:session-keeper, parent hk-ekap1). SELF-CONTAINED — do not rely on any .kerf/ bench files (gitignored, absent in worktrees).

BUILD:
1. New Go package internal/keeper/ following project subsystem conventions (see docs/foundation/project-level/subsystem-organization.md). Add a depguard component-matrix entry in .golangci.yml for internal/keeper (it may import internal/core and internal/eventbus; MUST NOT import internal/daemon or internal/workloop).
2. New 'harmonik keeper' subcommand under cmd/harmonik/, following the existing cobra subcommand pattern (model on cmd/harmonik/supervise_cmd.go). Flags: --agent <name> (required), --tmux <target> (optional), --warn-pct N (default 80), --act-pct N (default 90).
3. Subcommand behavior for THIS scaffold (no watcher/statusLine/injector logic yet — those are later beads):
   a. Acquire a single-keeper lockfile at .harmonik/keeper/<agent>.lock with pidfile semantics; if another live keeper holds it for the same agent, print an error and exit non-zero.
   b. Check for opt-in marker .harmonik/keeper/<agent>.managed; if ABSENT, log 'keeper: <agent> not opted-in (.managed marker missing); no-op' and exit 0 (fail-safe: never act on a non-managed pane).
   c. If present, log 'keeper started for <agent>' and block (await signal); release the lock on exit.
4. Create .harmonik/keeper/ dir as needed; it is gitignored.

TESTS: unit-test the lockfile (acquire succeeds; second concurrent acquire for same agent fails) and the .managed guard (absent => no-op exit 0; present => proceeds). 

DONE: build green, gofmt + golangci-lint clean, tests pass. Refs: hk-fzzc6.
close_reason: done

title: session-keeper warn-mode: fix dead warn-injection (idle-gate latches it off) + wire real event emission
description:
DOGFOOD (hk-klyom; findings .kerf/works/session-keeper/13-dogfood-warnmode.md) found Phase-1 warn-mode injects NOTHING despite the warn 'firing' — the core feature is dead. Two defects:

BUG-1 (P1, injection dead): internal/keeper/watcher.go:222-234 gates the tmux injection on gaugeQuiesced ON THE SAME TICK the warn fires. A real upward warn-pct crossing just changed the gauge mtime (or lastModTime is zero at boot) => gaugeQuiesced is false on that tick; the code then latches warnFired=true permanently so injection never retries => effectively dead code. FIX: decouple the warn DECISION from inject DELIVERY — when a warn is due but the pane isn't quiesced, set a pendingInject state and retry the inject on a later tick once gaugeQuiesced; only clear pendingInject after a successful inject. Do NOT permanently latch warnFired before the inject actually lands. (Injector primitive itself works — proven by isolating it.)

BUG-2 (P2, observability): the standalone keeper CLI wires NoopEmitter (cmd/harmonik/keeper_cmd.go:103) so session_keeper_warn / session_keeper_no_gauge are discarded. FIX: give the standalone keeper a real emitter — append typed events to the daemon's .harmonik/events/events.jsonl (same format/path) so harmonik subscribe/comms log see them; at minimum a stdout/slog fallback so a missing gauge is never fully silent.

TESTS (unit tests MISSED this — they used IdleQuiesce:1ms + empty TmuxTarget, never exercising the live inject path): add a test with a realistic idle_quiesce and a SPY injector (fake tmux target) asserting (a) on an upward warn-pct crossing the spy gets EXACTLY ONE injection even when the gauge was just updated (non-quiesced on the crossing tick => must retry on a later tick), and (b) a session_keeper_warn event is emitted.
DONE: warn injection delivers to the pane on a real crossing; events visible; the new regression test FAILS on old code, PASSES on the fix; build+lint+gofmt green. Refs: hk-g4ei7.
close_reason: done

title: Wire queue subsystem into daemon composition root (final wiring)
description:
The queue subsystem (epic hk-lj0pb, 22 child tasks, 28 commits May 14-15) is fully scaffolded and tested in isolation, but the final 3-4 wiring commits never landed. End-to-end `hk queue submit` returns "QueueHandler not registered" today.

## Gaps (verified 2026-05-18)

1. **`newQueueStore()` is never called from daemon.Start.** Defined in `internal/daemon/queuestore_hkj808w.go`, exported only for tests. The composition root needs to instantiate it and inject it into deps.

2. **`LoadQueueAtStartup` is never called.** Defined in `internal/lifecycle/startup_pl005_qm002.go` (PL-005 step 8a) but unreferenced in daemon.Start. Should run during startup after pidfile acquisition, before the workloop starts.

3. **`HandlerAdapter` is never passed to the socket listener.** `RunSocketListener` (daemon.go ~line 600) is called with qh defaulting to nil. Even though `queue/rpc.go` implements the handlers, they are not registered.

4. **`queueStore` is never set on workloop deps.** workloop.go references it but the field is nil at runtime, so the queue-pull dispatch path at lines 457-758 is dead code.

## Acceptance

- `harmonik queue submit <file>` ends with the daemon draining the queue and unlinking queue.json on completion.
- An integration test (under internal/scenario/) exercises submit → daemon dispatch → terminal → unlink end-to-end.
- Existing scenario tests stay green (queue_lifecycle, queue_paused, queue_crash_recovery).
- `hk queue status` against a running daemon returns the live envelope (not -32099).

## Why P1

Unblocks hk-icecw (harmonik run <bead-id>) — once the queue path is live, run reduces to "submit a 1-item queue with exit-on-empty." Also: the EM-015f group-advance gate, paused-by-failure recovery, and crash-recovery startup reconcile are all currently latent. This is the Phase-2 dogfood scale-out blocker.
close_reason: Wired in 9925ce7: QueueStore singleton + LoadQueueAtStartup + HandlerAdapter on socket listener + queueStore on workloop deps. 3 integration tests under internal/scenario/queue_daemon_wiring_test.go cover load-at-startup, absent-queue-nil, drain-and-unlink.

The first 6 are spec-required agent-lifecycle events. Without registration, daemon cannot replay JSONL containing these types → DispatchUnknownEventError. Surfaced by core/eventbus audit 2026-05-18.

title: queue events from handlers + workloop are never emitted to event bus
description: HandleQueueSubmit (rpc.go:98) returns []LedgerDepPair so the caller can build QueueSubmittedPayload + queue_item_deferred_for_ledger_dep events per QM-050 step 5-8. HandleQueueAppend returns []core.Event including queue_appended and deferred-for-ledger-dep events per QM-042. The HandlerAdapter discards both (rpc.go:464, 484). No bus reference is plumbed into HandlerAdapter, so queue_submitted, queue_appended, queue_group_started (for group_index 0 on submit), and queue_item_deferred_for_ledger_dep are never observable. AdvanceGroup events emitted from workloop.go:1491-1497 use deps.bus.Emit but per QM-063 the persist-before-emit discipline is violated because no persist is called between mutation and emit (see hk-ug821). Priority P2: dependent on hk-1fubv + hk-pwfhk fixes which will re-thread the bus into HandlerAdapter.
close_reason: Fixed in 40eeb9b: HandlerAdapter now plumbs event bus; queue_submitted/queue_appended/queue_group_started events emitted post-persist.

title: bridge-integration: wire hook-bridge into daemon + tmux substrate
description:
## Goal
Ship the claude-hook-bridge end-to-end: hk launches real claude as a full interactive session inside a named tmux window the operator can attach to, claude performs real work in a worktree, completion observed via Stop hook through the daemon's Unix socket. Both workflow:single and workflow:review-loop in scope.

## Streams
- A: Spec amendments (PL/WM/HC; CHB no-amendment record)
- B: Tmux substrate (internal/lifecycle/tmux + hk tmux-start)
- C: Bridge daemon wiring (buildClaudeLaunchSpec, workloop, reviewloop, heartbeat, agent_ready timeout)
- D: Completion path (HookSessionStore.WaitForOutcome, socket listener wiring, waitWithSocketGrace)
- E: Dogfood smoke (single-mode GREEN gate + review-loop stretch)

## Closes when
E1 (single-mode dogfood smoke against real claude) is GREEN. May also close hk-w5vra.7, hk-w5vra, hk-1n0cw, hk-nvrvp, hk-do7te, hk-v34bz.

title: Address bridge-integration spec review findings (post-merge follow-up)
description:
## Source
Cold spec review of the bridge-integration corpus (recorded against commit b3240ee main). Reviewer report archived in this bead body. BLOCKING finding (PL-021b §3 / PL-028b reconciliation) was fixed inline in b3240ee — this bead covers the remaining MEDIUM and MINOR findings.

## MEDIUM findings to address

1. **HC-057 ↔ CHB-019 wording tension.** CHB-019 says "the handler-process MUST emit agent_heartbeat..." with no conditional exception. HC-057 carves out daemon-side emission for claude-code at MVH. Add a note to CHB-019 cross-referencing HC-057 so strict readers of CHB don't see the daemon's heartbeat as non-conforming.

2. **PL-021c missing post-ceiling escalation.** PL-021c §4 says the daemon polls 100ms / 2s for window kill, then 'MUST proceed regardless.' What happens if the subprocess is still alive after the ceiling? PL-006 session-level sweep reparents survivors via session kill; window-kill leaves them alive in the operator's session. Add a normative clause for post-ceiling behavior — SIGKILL via PID, log + continue, or named error.

3. **Window-name verifiability.** PL-021b §4 asserts window-naming is replay-stable but the name is not present in any event payload. Add tmux_window_name to agent_started (or handler_capabilities) payload — cross-spec coordination with event-model.md §6.3.

## MINOR findings

4. **CHB v0.5 changelog typo.** The v0.5 row in specs/claude-hook-bridge.md cites HC-026a (heartbeat obligation) where it should cite HC-054 (Session.Attach pty contract). Fix the citation.

5. **No normative pointer for operator-facing window-name surface.** The deterministic window name from WM-002a isn't surfaced anywhere operator-readable. Add a clause (in PL-028 or hk attach surface) for how the operator learns the window name to attach to. Or file as an OQ.

## Acceptance
Each of the 5 findings either resolved in spec or filed as an OQ with explicit deferral rationale. Spec audit clean.

title: session-id persist: persistClaudeSessionID git-adds .harmonik/run-context/*/context.json which is gitignored -> 'git add' exit 1 (non-fatal)
description: Observed in hk-mboii run (019e7f8d, 2026-05-31). The hk-za5mz session-id-capture fix calls persistClaudeSessionID which does 'git add .harmonik/run-context/<run>/context.json' — but .harmonik is in .gitignore, so git add fails: 'The following paths are ignored by one of your .gitignore files: .harmonik'. Daemon logs the error and continues ('iter-2 resume still targets the live session in-process'), so it is NON-FATAL today (in-process resume covers it). But the git-tracked persistence never works, so a daemon restart mid-run would lose the persisted session_id. Fix options: (a) 'git add -f' the context.json, (b) persist outside .harmonik so it is trackable, or (c) drop the git-add entirely if in-process state is the real source of truth and the file is only diagnostic. Low priority; tracks a latent gap in the hk-za5mz mechanism.

title: PL-006 orphan-sweep kills coordinator/orchestrator tmux sessions (flywheel blocker)
description:
The daemon orphan-sweep (PL-006, specs/process-lifecycle.md §4.2) kills every
tmux session matching the `harmonik-<project_hash>-*` prefix. This is too broad:
it includes (and has in practice killed) external coordinator/orchestrator
sessions that should survive a daemon restart.

User-recalled incident: the main agent coordinating harmonik was killed when the
daemon swept its tmux session.

Direct flywheel impact: the planned `harmonik supervise` flywheel pane (proposed
name `harmonik-<project_hash>-flywheel`) would be killed on every daemon restart
unless this is fixed.

Two acceptable fixes (pick during impl):
  (a) PL-006 exclusion clause for sessions tagged with a coordinator/orchestrator
      provenance (e.g. via a sentinel file in the pane, or a name suffix the
      sweep skips).
  (b) Move coordinator/orchestrator session naming OUTSIDE the
      `harmonik-<project_hash>-` scope (e.g. `harmonik-flywheel-<hash>`,
      `harmonik-coordinator-<hash>`).

(a) is more invasive but preserves the project-hash prefix convention for
identity; (b) is a one-line rename but loses the project-scoped grouping for
those panes. Recommend (a) with the sentinel-file approach.

Source: kerf flywheel work,
03-research/harmonik-supervisor-surface/findings.md (§"tmux convention + a SPEC
EXTENSION required") and 04-design/self-managing-architecture.md.

Spec areas affected: process-lifecycle.md §4.2 PL-006.
Blocks: flywheel supervise pane lifecycle.
close_reason: Fixed by hk-j7o3i: PL-006d orphan-sweep exclusion clause landed in specs/process-lifecycle.md (sentinel-file carve-out + stale-sentinel cleanup + coordinator_sessions_skipped payload field). Implementation in B5 (harmonik supervise CLI / hk-qx702) will write the sentinel file at start.

title: queue status: human-readable default output; --json opt-in
description:
Depends on: hk-5553i

## Current behavior

harmonik queue status writes raw JSON to stdout unconditionally.
Code path: internal/queue/cli/status.go RunQueueStatus() → client.go handleResponse() (line 136).
handleResponse() at client.go:143 calls fmt.Fprintf(out, "%s\n", data) with the daemon's raw JSON result.

## Desired behavior

Default: human-readable summary. Example output:

  queue: active
  queue_id: 019fbd3e-...
  submitted: 2026-05-20 09:14 UTC
  items:
    hk-abc123  pending
    hk-def456  in_progress

  (no queue active)        ← when queue is null (QM-057)

With --json / --format json: existing raw JSON behavior, unchanged.

## Implementation sketch

1. Add --json / --format flags to parseQueueFlags (helpers.go) or inline in RunQueueStatus.
2. Pass format choice into handleResponse (or a new handleQueueStatusResponse variant).
3. Unmarshal resp.Result into a typed QueueStatusResponse struct; format fields as prose.
4. Keep --json path identical to today — agents and scripts that already depend on JSON output are unaffected.
close_reason: queue status: human-readable default implemented; --json opt-in works

title: Every event MUST carry the common envelope fields
description: Per EV-001: every event emitted to the bus or appended to JSONL MUST carry envelope fields `event_id` (UUIDv7), `schema_version`, `type`, `timestamp_wall` (RFC 3339), `timestamp_mono_nsec`, `source_subsystem`, `trace_context`, `run_id` (when scoped), `state_id` (when scoped), `payload`. Emitter MUST perform exactly one wall-clock read per emission, reused for both `timestamp_wall` and UUIDv7 generation.

Pre-claim: blocks-on-closed hk-hqwn.53 + hk-hqwn.2 → related. Reviewer APPROVE-CLEAN. make check green.

title: Monotonic time orders intra-process events
description: Per EV-007: within a single emitter process, `timestamp_mono_nsec` (when present) MUST be non-decreasing across emissions in emission order.
close_reason: APPROVED. EV-007 monotonic-time invariant binding test landed at internal/core/monotsmono_hqwn10_test.go (sibling of monotsmono_hqwn6_test.go). 6 tests + 1 forward-doc sensor (skipped, future emitter enforcement deferred to hk-hqwn.50). hqwn10IsNonDecreasing predicate accepts strictly-increasing/equal-tick/all-nil/nil-interspersed; rejects decreasing. Equal-tick allowance locks 'non-decreasing' interpretation per spec. TimestampMonoNsec godoc on event.go now carries EV-007 citation. 0 vet/fmt issues.

title: Partial-order contract
description: Per EV-008: event stream MUST satisfy a partial-ordering contract: UUIDv7 supplies ms-resolution total ordering across processes; EV-002a extends to strict intra-process monotonicity at sub-millisecond resolution; `timestamp_mono_nsec` refines within a process; NO total-ordering guarantee across distinct processes finer than UUIDv7's millisecond precision. Tooling needing stricter cross-process ordering MUST insert explicit causal references via `trace_context.parent_event_id` (§6.1) or payload-specific field such as `triggering_event_id` on `hook_fired`.
close_reason: EV-008 spec-corpus sensor committed at 7e33ab0

title: Subscription is declared at registration, not inferred
description: Per EV-009: consumer MUST register subscription with bus at daemon startup, declaring (a) event types it consumes, (b) its class, (c) its consumer identifier. Dynamic mid-run subscription forbidden; bus is sealed after `daemon.Start()` returns; post-seal `Subscribe` calls MUST return typed sealed-bus error.

title: Synchronous consumer class
description: Per EV-010: synchronous consumer runs on producer's critical path. Failure MUST halt producer's progress on the specific run: producer receives typed error, does NOT retry synchronously, emits `consumer_failed` (§8.8.2), run enters quarantine state requiring operator escalation. At most ONE synchronous consumer per event type (enforced at registration). Synchronous consumer MUST NOT emit events that re-dispatch to itself (directly or transitively); registration path MUST verify acyclicity across declared emission surfaces at startup and fail-closed on cycles.
close_reason: Spec-corpus sensor authored and committed: 11-check EV-010 body scan in internal/specaudit/hqwn13_synchronous_consumer_class_test.go; all checks PASS.

title: Asynchronous consumer class
description: Per EV-011: asynchronous consumer runs off critical path. Consumer failure MUST NOT block producer. Failed deliveries retried per bounded policy (default 3 attempts, exponential backoff 1s base); exhausted retries enqueue to dead-letter queue (§6.2) and emit `dead_letter_enqueued` (§8.8.3). Per-consumer dispatch queue depth MUST be bounded (default 1024; operator-configurable per [operator-nfr.md]). See EV-011a for overflow handling. Async consumers MAY be added at subscription time up to per-type cap of 8.
close_reason: spec-corpus sensor TestEV011AsynchronousConsumerClass in internal/specaudit/ev011_async_consumer_class_test.go; commit c986a3e

title: Non-blocking producer back-pressure
description: Per EV-011a: bus MUST NOT block producer on async-consumer delivery. When per-consumer queue is full, bus MUST shed event according to durability class - `lossy-tail-ok` sheds first, then `ordinary`; `fsync-boundary` events MUST NOT be shed and MUST spill to secondary spill file at `.harmonik/events/spill-<consumer>.jsonl` for out-of-band replay. Every shed event MUST cause emission of `bus_overflow` (§8.8.4). Bus MUST reserve dedicated capacity-1 slot in observer queue consuming `bus_overflow` (guarantees one overflow signal per shed without recursive fill check). If reservation exhausted, bus MUST fall back to direct JSONL append with `fsync-boundary` semantics for that single `bus_overflow` write (promoted O->F at write time; promotion logged). Direct-append fallback blocks producer for one write+fsync (accepted floor-price). Per-consumer spill files MUST be pre-created at registration time with `O_CREAT|O_APPEND|O_DSYNC` semantics; failure to create MUST fail daemon startup with typed error.
close_reason: spec-corpus sensor TestEV011aNonBlockingProducerBackPressure in internal/specaudit/ev011a_nonblocking_backpressure_test.go; commit eaedb35

title: Observer consumer class
description: Per EV-012: observer is a passive consumer whose failures MUST NOT produce bus events or side effects beyond local logging. Observers MUST NOT mutate persistent state. Enforcement: coding discipline + `depguard` rule (observer packages MUST NOT import state-mutating packages: git, Beads, workspace-manager). Observer needing state mutation MUST re-register as asynchronous.

title: Consumer class is opt-in at subscription

title: Dispatch semantics
description: Per EV-014a: `Emit` returns after (a) redaction (EV-035), (b) JSONL append + any mandated fsync per EV-016, (c) synchronous-consumer dispatch (blocking until at-most-one synchronous consumer returns or errors). Async and observer dispatches occur off critical path via bus worker pool (default 4 workers, operator-configurable) and MUST NOT extend `Emit` latency.

title: event_id MUST be a UUIDv7
description: Per EV-002: every `event_id` MUST be UUIDv7 (time-ordered UUID with 48-bit Unix-millisecond timestamp). UUIDv4 / UUIDv1 forbidden.

Reviewer APPROVE-WITH-NITS (single bit-position-comment nit at eventid.go:43); orchestrator inline-amended the comment (bits 76–79 of 128-bit value, upper nibble of byte 6). make check green, internal/core 100%.

title: Consumers MUST be idempotent on recovery and dead-letter replay
description: Per EV-014b: every consumer MUST be coded against tail-truncated event stream on recovery AND against repeated delivery via `DeadLetterReplay` and `ReplayFrom`. Pairs with producer idempotency of EV-018 to make lossy-tail-plus-replay safe end-to-end.
close_reason: EV-014b spec-corpus sensor committed at 8ab7a9c

title: Observer dispatch uses per-observer goroutine plus bounded queue
description: Per EV-014c: `FANOUT_OBSERVERS` dispatches each event to each registered observer via per-observer dedicated goroutine draining a per-observer bounded queue (default depth 1024, operator-configurable; same default as async per EV-011). Observer queues are class `lossy-tail-ok` for shed semantics per EV-011a: when full, event dropped + `bus_overflow{shed_policy=lossy-dropped}` emitted. Slow observer MUST NOT back-pressure producer OR starve peer observers. `fsync-boundary` events that cannot queue follow EV-011a spill-file path identically to async.
close_reason: spec-corpus sensor TestEV014cObserverDispatchPerGoroutineBoundedQueue in internal/specaudit/ev014c_observer_goroutine_queue_test.go; commit c986a3e

title: Consumer-recovery replay contract (closes EV-INV-002 consumer side)
description: Per EV-014d: on daemon startup, for every subscription whose `since` field is non-nil OR whose `offset_checkpoint_event_id` is non-nil, bus MUST perform JSONL-tail replay to the consumer before live-stream delivery resumes. Replay scans `events.jsonl` for lines whose `event_id` is strictly greater than consumer's effective checkpoint; dispatches in `event_id` order. Dead-letter log and spill files NOT automatically replayed (require `DeadLetterReplay` per EV-011). Replay and live-stream serialized per consumer: live events buffered into queue during replay, delivered after replay reaches current tail; `on_tail_truncation` fires (if registered) when tail lost data. Consumers with `since: None` and no `offset_checkpoint_event_id` start from live stream. Synchronous consumers do NOT participate in replay (re-invoking sync handler on restart could double side effects).
close_reason: EV-014d spec-corpus sensor committed at 1a670a4

title: JSONL is the durable on-disk form
description: Per EV-015: bus MUST persist every emitted event (before any shed) to JSONL file at `.harmonik/events/events.jsonl`. Line format per §6.2.
close_reason: sensor: hqwn23_jsonl_durable_form_test.go — spec-corpus sensor for EV-015/§6.2; implementation deps are upstream; sensor is design-level structural audit; commit 3dd9c1f

title: Every §8 row MUST declare durability class; fsync derives from class (coalesce)
description: Per EV-016 + EV-016a (coalesced per §2.3): every §8 row MUST carry `durability_class` in {fsync-boundary, ordinary, lossy-tail-ok}. JSONL writer MUST call `fsync(2)` after appending any event whose class is `fsync-boundary`. `Append` MUST return only after fsync completes for boundary-class; `ordinary` / `lossy-tail-ok` returns without fsync. Operator-configurable timer (default 1 second) MAY additionally flush on `ordinary`; `lossy-tail-ok` MAY be flushed only opportunistically. Class assignment rule: events whose loss forces reconciliation work greater than disk-sync cost are F; high-cardinality granular (chunk, accrual, metric) are L; everything else is O. EV-016a clarification: per-event fsync; bus does NOT guarantee atomicity across multiple boundary events - producer emitting two boundary events sequentially MAY observe post-crash state where first is durable but second is not. Producers requiring two events to be durably persisted together MUST emit single event carrying both payloads OR resolve pair via authoritative stores (git+Beads). `event_id` generation NOT atomic with `write+fsync`: generated-but-not-persisted `event_id` has no durable trace and is acceptable. Multi-event batching is post-MVH amendment; MVH accepts O(N-fsync) cost.
close_reason: sensor in internal/specaudit/hqwn24_durability_class_test.go; commit 28e0a01; hk-hqwn.23 is structural design-level blocker, not a code dependency

title: Event loss between fsyncs is acceptable because git is authoritative
description: Per EV-017: producers MUST assume `ordinary` and `lossy-tail-ok` events emitted between two fsync boundaries MAY be lost on hard crash. Acceptable because git+Beads authoritative for state per [execution-model.md §4.7] and producers emit idempotently per EV-018. Consumers MUST be coded against tail truncation (EV-014b).
close_reason: EV-017 spec-corpus sensor committed at 1a670a4

title: Producers MUST emit events idempotently
description: Per EV-018: every producer MUST emit each event in idempotent form: re-emitting same event (same `event_id`, same payload) during recovery MUST be safe for downstream observational consumers. Producers MUST NOT encode one-shot side-effect semantics into event payloads; side effects belong in run's checkpoint trail (git) or Beads.
close_reason: EV-018 idempotent-emission equivalence sensor landed via 6-chain pipelined merge at 4a316c6. internal/core/event.go: EquivalentTo(other) method comparing EventID + Type + Payload (envelope-only fields excluded); reflect.DeepEqual on JSON-decoded payload for map order-independence; godoc cites EV-018. internal/core/event_ev018_test.go: same-ID/same-payload, same-ID/different-payload, different-ID, payload-order-independent cases. No EventBus/producer interface introduced (deferred to hk-hqwn.57). Reviewer APPROVE-CLEAN. make check green; EquivalentTo at 100%.

title: Panic handler MUST flush structured logs
description: Per EV-019: on Go `panic`, daemon's top-level recovery handler MUST flush structured-log channel before exit. OS-level crashes (SIGKILL, kernel panic) bypass this handler; diagnostics rely on aggressive-fsync log channel + post-restart reconciliation.
close_reason: internal/lifecycle/panicrecovery.go: RecoverWithLogFlush(flusher,logger) deferred helper per EV-019; Flush() before re-panic preserves stack+exit semantics; nil-paths safe. 5 tests cover panic-triggers-flush, flush-before-repanic, nil-flusher safe, no-panic no-flush, flush-error-still-repanics. Reviewed REQUEST_CHANGES with 2 dead-code lint hits (flushOrder field + unused recovered return); orchestrator inline-amend dropped both in a follow-up commit; pure-deletion so re-review skipped per v9 protocol. Host-binding (defer in daemon main) deferred to future daemon-binary bead.

title: Panic handler SHOULD best-effort flush the bus
description: Per EV-019a: on Go `panic`, daemon's top-level recovery handler SHOULD additionally make best-effort flush of event bus after log flush completes. Best-effort obligation; completeness not guaranteed.
close_reason: APPROVED. RecoverWithLogFlush extended to 3-arg signature with BusFlusher interface; bus flush ordered after log flush per EV-019a, wrapped in own defer recover() for secondary-panic containment, nil-safe. 5 new EV-019a tests cover ordering, log-fail-still-flushes-bus, nil-safe, panic-containment, no-flush-on-non-panic. Follow-up bead hk-hqwn.70 filed for real EventBus.Flush implementation. No production callers existed pre-change so signature widening is safe; future cmd/ wiring will adopt directly. Reviewer nit (duplicate helper hqwn28RecoverPanic vs pre-existing panicRecoveryFixtureRecoverPanic) is cosmetic, not flagged.

title: JSONL writes MUST be append-only
description: Per EV-020: JSONL writer MUST NOT rewrite, truncate, or reorder existing lines. Corruption (partial-line write on crash) detected by readers per §6.2.
close_reason: JSONLWriter implemented in internal/eventbus/jsonlwriter.go with O_APPEND semantics; binding tests in jsonlwriter_test.go — commit 02eb7b0

title: UUIDv7 generation MUST be monotonic within a process
description: Per EV-002a: daemon's UUIDv7 generator MUST produce strictly monotonic values within a single process (RFC 9562 §6.2 method 1 or 3). All cross-subsystem event emissions flow through the daemon's single emitter; cross-process monotonicity within a project is a consequence. Same-millisecond emissions from distinct goroutines: monotonic counter MUST break ties. Strengthens EV-008's partial-order contract.
close_reason: EV-002a monotonic EventIDGenerator landed via 6-chain pipelined merge at 717beda. internal/core/eventidgen.go: process-local strictly-monotonic UUIDv7 generator using RFC 9562 §6.2 method 1 (clock-rollback + same-ms increment via 128-bit big-endian +1); sync.Mutex goroutine-safe; cross-process/cross-restart explicitly out-of-scope (deferred to hk-hqwn.4/.5). internal/core/eventidgen_test.go: 6 tests (strictly-monotonic, same-ms, concurrent 8×100, version-preserved normal, clock-rollback, newV7-error). 100% coverage on eventidgen.go. Pre-claim: blocks-on-closed hk-hqwn.2 → related. Reviewer APPROVE-CLEAN. make check green.

title: Observational replay MUST NOT reconstruct state
description: Per EV-021: any tool walking JSONL for debugging, pattern analysis, or dashboard purposes is performing observational replay. Output advisory only. Authoritative state-reconstruction source is git+Beads per [execution-model.md §4.7].
close_reason: Implemented in 6a60e66 (cherry-pick): EV-021 observational-replay advisory invariant sensor (spec + godoc readers, 3 tests).

title: State reconstruction MUST NOT walk JSONL
description: Per EV-022: daemon's startup state-reconstruction path MUST walk git + query Beads; MUST NOT read JSONL to reconstruct state.
close_reason: Implemented in 68b5ea0 (cherry-pick): EV-022 reconstruction-not-JSONL spec sensor + DiscoverActiveRuns godoc citation (2 tests; partial-subsumed acknowledged with hk-b3f.39).

title: Divergence-evidence read with post-crash-window guardrail + inconclusive classification (coalesce)
description: Per EV-023 + EV-023a (coalesced per §2.3): reconciliation detectors and investigator agents MAY read JSONL tail for express purpose of detecting inconsistency between three stores. A divergence-evidence read MUST produce typed `store_divergence_detected` event (§8.6.8) as output. To prevent false positives from lossy-tail event loss, detector MUST: (1) determine whether read covers post-crash window (any JSONL position after last durable fsync boundary preceding most recent daemon startup; uses most recent `daemon_started`'s `event_id` as landmark); (2) set `post_crash_window: true` on any divergence event whose evidence falls in that window; (3) corroborate divergence against git and Beads before flagging - event referencing `commit_hash` MUST be tested against git; bead transition MUST be tested against Beads. Only if on-disk authoritative stores also disagree is divergence real. EV-023a clarification: detector MUST classify evidence supporting every candidate divergence event into `git-corroborated` / `beads-corroborated` / `inconclusive`. `git-corroborated` when payload carries `commit_hash` testable against git DAG; `beads-corroborated` when payload carries `bead_id` queryable against Beads; otherwise `inconclusive`. Detector MUST emit `store_divergence_detected` ONLY for corroborated evidence. For inconclusive evidence MUST emit `divergence_inconclusive` (§8.6.10) carrying same `evidence_ref` + inconclusive classification. Boundary events without authority-reference field (e.g., `daemon_started`, `daemon_ready`, pending `workspace_merge_status`) always fall to inconclusive when inside post-crash window.
close_reason: coalesced spec-corpus sensor TestEV023DivergenceEvidenceReadPostCrashGuardrail in internal/specaudit/ev023_divergence_evidence_read_test.go; commit 8762a50

title: Replay cannot re-establish agent state or re-invoke LLMs
description: Per EV-024: neither observational replay nor state reconstruction MAY re-establish live agent-process state or re-invoke LLMs. Tools that appear to do so are debugging aids; their output is non-authoritative.
close_reason: EV-024 sensor landed: spec-text + godoc + forward-doc marker in replaynoagentstate_ev024_test.go; EV-024 phrases added to ReadJSONLForDivergenceEvidence godoc

title: Each event type has exactly one owning spec for payload shape
description: Per EV-025: each event type in §8 MUST have its payload schema declared in §6.3 of this spec. Subsystem spec that emits the event is normative for the WHEN (timing + preconditions); this spec is normative for the SHAPE. Co-ownership declared in §6.5 of each emitting spec.
close_reason: Implemented in 5d7fda2 + 566cca8 (citation fix): godoc on RegisterEventType encoding EV-025 — §6.3 owns SHAPE; emitting subsystem owns WHEN; ErrDuplicateEventType is in-Go enforcement of one-constructor-per-type-name. Reviewer APPROVE after §4.3→§4.6 fix.

title: Subsystems MAY emit internal events not on this list
description: Per EV-026: internal events (within subsystem's own Go package, never dispatched to bus) MUST NOT cross bus and do not require §8 registration.
close_reason: Implemented in a059272 + 566cca8 (citation fix): godoc on EventPayload interface encoding EV-026 — internal events (subsystem-private, never cross bus) MUST NOT register, do not require §8 declaration; internal payload types MAY implement EventPayload locally. Reviewer APPROVE after §4.3→§4.6 fix.

title: Adding or removing a cross-bus event type requires a foundation amendment
description: Per EV-027: subsystem wanting to emit new cross-bus event type MUST add type to §8 via foundation amendment protocol ([architecture.md §4.6]). Addition amendment MUST provide: type name, emitter, typical consumers, payload fields, four-axis tags, durability class, evidence satisfying every §8.9 criterion. Addition MUST include (a) §8 row, (b) emitter-spec edit adding emission requirement, (c) at least one consumer cited in another spec. Symmetrically, sibling spec removing emission requirement MUST also amend §8 - either by removing event type (deletion amendment) OR by documenting orphan status. Deletion MUST provide: retiring event-type name, emitter-spec edit removing emission, migration guidance for current consumers, statement that retired `EventType` enum value is retired and MUST NOT be reused. Event-type identifiers, once retired, are burned.
close_reason: Landed as 7fb415a

title: Each event type and the envelope carry schema_version
description: Per EV-028: `schema_version` is integer on envelope and MUST match schema version of payload for that `type`. Envelope-level `schema_version` field is normative for envelope shape; per-type schema versions MAY increment independently as declared in amendment protocol.
close_reason: done

title: N-1 readable compatibility window (per-type AND envelope)
description: Per EV-029: readers MUST accept immediately prior schema version (N-1) for every event type AND for envelope. Per-type independence means harmonik maintains up to 71+ independent compatibility contracts (current §8 = 71 event types per spec text - actual tally 78 post v0.3.3 additions). Breaking changes (per §6.4 table) require migration release scheduled at operator pause per [operator-nfr.md §4.3].

Bead hk-hqwn.37 (EV-028) landed as bc6d42d providing the foundation (RegisterEventTypeAtVersion, ValidateEnvelopeSchemaVersion).

title: Breaking-change classification is declared in §6.4
description: Per EV-030: schema change is breaking per §6.4 classification table. Additive changes (new optional field, type widening, new enum variant without required-semantics shift) are non-breaking. Implements the §6.4 breaking-change ruleset (9 row classifications).
close_reason: done

title: Handler-subprocess event_id generation MUST route through the daemon
description: Per EV-002b: handler subprocesses MUST NOT generate `event_id` independently. Handler writes envelope with no `event_id` (or placeholder daemon discards); daemon watcher stamps `event_id`, envelope timestamps, `source_subsystem` at enqueue time. Preserves EV-002a's intra-daemon-process monotonicity as the sole monotonicity contract across all cross-bus events.
close_reason: Landed at 8ba189a. EV-002b 3-shape sensor: `internal/core/handlereventidroute_ev002b_test.go` — Shape A (no internal/handler/* import of internal/core), Shape B (AST: EventID field godoc cites EV-002b + 'MUST NOT populate'), Shape C (spec: 'MUST NOT generate' within ±10 lines of any EV-002b). event.go EventID godoc rewritten to encode the contract. APPROVE-CLEAN. Follow-up note: Shape A over-broad (forbids any internal/handler→core import); tighten when first legitimate handler→core type-import lands.

title: Every event type declared in §8 MUST carry four-axis tags AND durability class
description: Per EV-031: every §8 entry MUST have four-axis tags (`llm-freedom`, `io-determinism`, `replay-safety`, `idempotency`), `mechanism | cognition` tag per AR-005 (the spec text says 'per SC-10' - AR-005 is the canonical equivalent in current AR taxonomy), AND `durability_class`. Event emission itself is mechanism-tagged in every case; cognition-tagged events forbidden. Cognition-tagged operation that produces an event does so by calling mechanism-tagged emission primitive. Payload-registry (§6.3) is authoritative carrier; §8 tables carry only durability class for display compactness, full tags registered per-type in Go registry.
close_reason: SUBSUMED by hk-hqwn.63 (§8 taxonomy lint). Implementer's subsumption analysis: hqwn63FixtureCheckA already enforces durability class ∈ {F,O,L}; hqwn63FixtureCheckB enforces per-event Axes: annotation (all 81 currently violating, pinned to hk-hqwn.67 spec-fix). The mechanism/cognition tag from EV-031 is architecturally deferred to the Go payload registry (§6.3) per spec, not a §8-table-level obligation. No new test needed.

title: Events are tagged-union envelope plus payload-constructor registry
description: Per EV-032: event types in Go MUST be represented as tagged-union - top-level `Event` envelope struct carrying common fields plus `Payload json.RawMessage`; per-type constructor registry `map[EventType]func() EventPayload` decodes `Payload` keyed by `Event.type`.
close_reason: EV-032 payload-constructor registry landed at internal/core/eventregistry.go (EventPayload marker, RegisterEventType, DecodePayload, ErrUnknownEventType, ErrDuplicateEventType, sync.Mutex-guarded). Registry key uses string with TODO(hk-hqwn.59.82) deferral. Reviewer APPROVE-WITH-NOTES (2 NITs: defer-vs-inline unlock; eventRegistryReset(t) signature). Orchestrator merging; NITs stylistic.

title: Type dispatch is deterministic on the type field
description: Per EV-033: given valid envelope, payload-type resolution MUST be deterministic map lookup. If registry has no entry, reader MUST surface typed `ErrUnknownEventType` and skip event (observational consumers) or fail with structured error (synchronous consumers).
close_reason: EV-033 dispatch policy landed at af799c4. internal/core/eventdispatch.go: DispatchObservational (returns ErrSkipUnknown sentinel) + DispatchSynchronous (returns *DispatchUnknownEventError wrapping ErrUnknownEventType). Determinism asserted over 20 iterations. Stale hk-hqwn.33 TODO refs in eventregistry.go removed. Reviewer APPROVE-CLEAN.

title: Registry registration is startup-time
description: Per EV-034: payload-type registration MUST happen at daemon init (via `init()` functions or `RegisterEventType` calls during startup). Runtime registration after first event is emitted MUST be startup-time error. Registry sealed at same time bus is sealed (EV-009).

title: Redaction registry applied before emission
description: Per EV-035: bus MUST apply redaction registry ([handler-contract.md §4.7]) to every event payload before appending to JSONL AND before dispatch to consumers. Fields whose names match `(?i)(secret|token|password|api[_-]?key|auth)` MUST be replaced with `"<redacted>"`. Per-handler additional redaction patterns MUST be applied by same path. Best-effort defense; compile-time check (EV-036) is structural guardrail.

Sensor (cross-subsystem corpus scan): every consumer cited as observer/async/sync subscriber must NOT rely on JSONL for state-reconstruction; reviewer-persona scan + scenario test that destroys JSONL and confirms state reconstructable from git+Beads alone.

title: Sensor: event-loss between fsyncs acceptable; consumers MUST handle it
description: Per EV-INV-002: hard crash between fsync boundaries MAY lose events emitted in that window. Producers satisfy via EV-018 (idempotent emission). Consumers MUST be coded to handle tail-truncated event stream on recovery per EV-014b. Two-sided operational covenant. Sensor (paired contract test): kill daemon between fsync boundaries; on restart confirm consumers resume from `offset_checkpoint_event_id` per EV-014d's replay contract; confirm idempotent re-delivery of recovered events does not produce double side effects.

title: Sensor: at most one synchronous consumer per event type, no reentrant emission
description: Per EV-INV-003: for every event type in §8, synchronous consumers MUST have cardinality at most one AND MUST NOT emit events that re-dispatch to themselves (EV-010 acyclicity clause). Sensor (registration-time check): test fixture that registers two synchronous consumers for same type and confirms daemon-startup typed configuration error per EV-014; test fixture that registers reentrant synchronous consumer (sync handler emits an event with another sync subscriber that transitively re-dispatches to first) and confirms acyclicity-check fail-closed per EV-010.

title: UUIDv7 monotonicity across daemon restart via high-water-mark file
description: Per EV-002c: daemon MUST persist UUIDv7 high-water-mark to `.harmonik/event_id_hwm`. HWM updated on every `fsync-boundary` write (piggyback on JSONL fsync domain - no additional fsync cost; ordered before or within same fsync as boundary event). On startup, generator MUST read HWM and ensure every new `event_id` is strictly greater. If wall clock behind HWM by >1 second, daemon MUST emit `daemon_degraded{reason=clock_regression}` and synthesize UUIDv7 timestamp components ahead of wall clock until clock catches up. Missing HWM (first run / wiped `.harmonik/`): structured warning + seed from wall clock; cross-restart ordering NOT guaranteed in that case.

Sensor (production-load regression): monotonic-counter tiebreaker test under same-millisecond emission load (per §10.2); HWM-restart test that confirms post-restart `event_id` is strictly greater than HWM-persisted predecessor; UUIDv7 shape conformance.

Type-discriminated payload deserialization keyed on `type` field.

title: Define EventBus interface (§6.1)
description: Implement the 6-method INTERFACE: `Emit(ctx, type, payload) -> error` (redacts per EV-035; appends JSONL per EV-015 with fsync per EV-016; dispatches sync/async/observer per EV-014a); `Subscribe(sub Subscription) -> (Subscription, error)` (startup-only per EV-009; post-seal fails; if sub.since is non-nil, replays before live delivery); `Seal() -> error` (called by `daemon.Start` once registration complete); `ReplayFrom(consumer_id, since event_id) -> error` (re-issues JSONL events whose `event_id` is strictly greater than `since`; consumers MUST be idempotent per EV-014b); `DeadLetterReplay(consumer_name, filter?) -> error` (operator-initiated replay from dead-letter log); `Drain(ctx) -> error` (quiescence primitive). MUST invoke optional `on_tail_truncation(ctx, last_durable_event_id)` consumer callback after restart replay completes when JSONL tail was truncated.
close_reason: EventBus interface + 6-method type-shape sensor committed at 060ab0d

title: Define on-disk JSONL line format (§6.2)
description: Implement the JSONL line shape: single-line JSON object per Event envelope at `.harmonik/events/events.jsonl`; primary log appendable; dead-letter log at `.harmonik/events/dead-letters.jsonl` (same line format + top-level `dead_letter: {consumer_name, retries_attempted, enqueued_at}`); per-consumer spill files at `.harmonik/events/spill-<consumer>.jsonl` for `fsync-boundary` events that could not queue per EV-011a. Implements §6.2's read-recovery rules: torn-tail discard (post-crash startup-recovery) OR `store_divergence_detected{divergence_kind=schema_mismatch, post_crash_window=false}` (live daemon read); mid-file corruption emits `store_divergence_detected{divergence_kind=parse_failure}` carrying `byte_offset` + halts reader; empty log valid fresh-project state IFF git+Beads also empty (otherwise emit `store_divergence_detected{divergence_kind=log_missing, post_crash_window=true}`); concurrent-tailer treats currently-growing file's final line as non-authoritative until terminating newline observed (POSIX `O_APPEND` atomicity bounded to 4096 bytes - JSONL lines may exceed); post-fsync tail events past last durable fsync MAY be absent. Reader MUST NOT skip corrupt lines and continue (mid-file corruption escalates to reconciliation Cat 6).
close_reason: jsonlformat_hqwn58.go: path constants, DeadLetterLine, JSONLReadContext per §6.2

Per-row beads are `parent-child` of this umbrella per §2.10's permitted second-level grouping. Umbrella blocks-on the §4 reqs that establish its acceptance criteria: EV-016 (durability-class declaration), EV-031 (per-row tagging obligation), EV-027 (amendment-protocol-required), EV-032 (registry shape), EV-034 (registration startup), EV-001 (envelope), EV-025 (one-owning-spec), EV-026 (internal-events carve-out), EV-033 (deterministic dispatch).

title: timestamp_mono_nsec is process-scoped and NOT cross-process-comparable
description: Per EV-003: `timestamp_mono_nsec` MUST be a monotonic nanosecond reading from the emitter's process. MUST NOT be compared across daemon restarts or across processes; meaningful ONLY for intra-process ordering within emitter's lifetime.
close_reason: Merged at 35c953c. EV-003 process-scope sensor in internal/core/monotsmono_hqwn6_test.go: spec-anchor + code-anchor pattern (mirroring gitauthoritative_bi022_test.go). 4 tests (3 PASS + 1 SKIP forward-doc marker for behavioral enforcement layer hk-hqwn.10/.11). Round 1 reviewer caught MEDIUM/godoc-phrase-mismatch (false-positive sensor — godoc said 'not meaningful across process restarts', weaker than EV-003's normative 'MUST NOT be compared across daemon restarts or across processes'); round 2 fix strengthened both the TimestampMonoNsec godoc in event.go AND the code-anchor assertion to require the canonical phrase. 2-commit branch.

title: Canonical JSONL fixture for envelope + durability + read-recovery tests
description: Per §10.2 (EV-001 - EV-008 envelope + EV-015 / EV-020 durability + §6.2 read-recovery): fixture providing well-formed envelope cases; torn-tail (final line missing newline / failing JSON / failing envelope schema); mid-file corruption (parse-failure on non-final line); empty-log (fresh-project vs git-and-beads-mismatch); concurrent-tail (writer appending while reader tails). Shared across every envelope-test and durability-test.
close_reason: Merged at da86f1a. Canonical JSONL fixture in internal/testhelpers/jsonlfixture.go: 8 functions covering minimal/full envelope, multiple-valid (increasing event_id), 3 durability classes, torn-tail (3 variants), mid-file corruption (with CorruptByteOffset), empty-log (nil vs []byte{}), concurrent-tail. 37 tests pass. Wire-form via package-private jsonlEventWire struct with snake_case JSON tags matching spec §6.2 exactly — sets the precedent for the eventual production JSONL encoder (no encoder exists yet, confirmed by codebase search). Reviewer APPROVE no findings; noted that core.Event lacking JSON tags is a future hygiene gap (filing follow-up bead).

title: Bus-overflow scenario harness for non-blocking back-pressure tests
description: Per §10.2 (EV-009 - EV-014b bus + EV-011a non-blocking back-pressure): per-consumer queue saturation test under each class (F->spill, O->drop, L->drop); reservation-slot consumption + exhaustion -> direct-append fallback; spill-file materialization for F.
close_reason: Implemented in cef96b0 (cherry-pick): bus-overflow harness with spec-content sensors (EV-011a + §8.8.4 bus_overflow payload) + forward-doc scenarios for F/O/L shed-policy; 2 PASS sensor tests + 6 SKIP forward-doc tests.

title: UUIDv7 monotonicity stress test (intra-process + cross-restart)
description: Per §10.2 (EV-001 - EV-008 ordering): same-millisecond emission load; HWM-restart; clock-regression; UUIDv7 shape conformance.
close_reason: internal/core/eventidgen_stress_test.go (335 lines, package core white-box): 6 stress tests covering EV-002 shape conformance + EV-002a intra-process monotonicity (100k real-clock + 50k same-ms-injection + NumCPU×5k concurrent + 10k clock-regression) + EV-002c cross-restart (seeded HWM + injected regressed clock). Concurrent test uses uuidv7FixtureSortAndDedup with index-pair collision reporting. UUIDv7 generator already shipped at internal/core/eventidgen.go (RFC 9562 §6.2 method 1 increment-on-rollback). HWM file persistence (.harmonik/event_id_hwm) tracked under hk-hqwn.4/.5 — not in scope for this stress test. Full suite under 0.8s. Reviewed APPROVE no flags.

title: §8 taxonomy lint (durability class + tags + at-least-one-consumer-in-sibling-spec)
description: Per §10.2 §8 lint obligation: static check that every §8 row carries `durability_class`, the four-axis registry-side tags, and one cited consumer in another spec (the `metric` row §8.8.1 is exempt per §8.9(g)). At MVH the cross-spec-consumer-cite check is a stub; resolves at corpus scale when sibling pilots resolve §6.5 emitter assignments.
close_reason: Merged at d123b95. Event-model §8 taxonomy lint in internal/specaudit/hqwn63_eventmodel_taxonomy_test.go: 3 audit checks for §8 event-type entries — (a) durability class in {F,O,L}, (b) Axes: line within 50-line look-ahead, (c) at-least-one backtick citation in sibling specs. Caught 80 real spec defects: 81 entries fail check (b) due to §8 table format having no Axes: annotation slot (structural EV-INV-005 + §8.9(e) defect → hk-hqwn.67); 6 events lack any sibling-spec citation → hk-hqwn.68. Round 1 reviewer caught MEDIUM/audit-scope-narrow (sibling-file collector missed schemas.md, falsely pinning state_entered which is actually cited there); round 2 fix expanded scope to all *.md files one level deep + removed the false-positive pin + updated hk-hqwn.68 notes. 2-commit branch.

title: Add EventID typed alias (event-model §6.1 EV-002)

The Event struct currently uses uuid.UUID directly with godoc citing EV-002 (UUIDv7 contract); this bead hoists it to a compile-time-distinct type. Pattern: mirror RunID/StateID — named type over uuid.UUID with String(), MarshalText(), UnmarshalText(). Non-breaking change on the Event struct (type is assignable; call sites update mechanically). Filed from hk-hqwn.53 implementation per typed-alias-deferral rule.

title: Add JSON struct tags to core.Event for spec §6.2 wire-form direct-marshal
description: Per hk-hqwn.60 reviewer note: internal/core/event.go has no JSON struct tags, so direct json.Marshal(event) produces PascalCase keys (EventID, SchemaVersion, ...) rather than the snake_case wire keys mandated by event-model.md §6.2 (event_id, schema_version, ...). hk-hqwn.60 worked around this with a fixture-local jsonlEventWire shadow struct. When the production JSONL encoder is implemented, EITHER: (a) add explicit json:"event_id" etc. tags to core.Event so direct marshal is spec-conformant, or (b) keep core.Event tag-free and require all marshal calls go through a dedicated wire-encoder type (the current fixture pattern). Pick the approach the production encoder bead specifies. Priority p3 because no production encoder exists yet — this is forward hygiene.

title: source_subsystem is layout-open

No closed enum exists; open-vocab contract is satisfied across all surfaces.

title: Define BusFlusher interface and EventBus.Flush() method for panic-handler hook
description: EV-019a requires RecoverWithLogFlush to call a best-effort bus flush after the log flush. The BusFlusher interface (Flush() error) is declared as a placeholder in internal/lifecycle/panicrecovery.go (TODO citing hk-hqwn.28). This bead: (1) define the canonical BusFlusher interface in internal/lifecycle or wherever the real EventBus lives, (2) implement EventBus.Flush() on the real bus type, (3) wire it up to the daemon entrypoint. Until then, callers pass nil and the bus-flush step is skipped. Spec ref: event-model.md §4.4 EV-019a.

title: Events are lifecycle-boundary signals, not agent internals
description: Per EV-005: events emitted to bus and JSONL MUST be lifecycle-boundary signals. Agent-internal detail (tool calls, thinking traces, per-token output) MUST NOT be emitted as events; lives in agent session log per [workspace-model.md §4.7]. The per-chunk types `agent_output_chunk` and `budget_accrual` ARE lifecycle-boundary signals routed to bus per §8.9; they are NOT the mechanism by which orchestrator reconstructs agent-internal state.

title: Wall clock is advisory for cross-process ordering
description: Per EV-006: `timestamp_wall` MUST NOT be used for ordering decisions across processes (NTP skew / clock adjustments / container time-sync make it unreliable). Consumers needing cross-process ordering MUST use `event_id` (UUIDv7) per EV-002. `timestamp_wall` is for audit, human display, external correlation.

EV-006 advisory constraint encoded on Event.TimestampWall godoc (MUST NOT for cross-process ordering, redirect to EventID per EV-002, permitted uses: audit / human display / external correlation). New wallclock_hqwn9_test.go (264 LOC) mirrors monotsmono_hqwn6_test.go pattern: spec-phrase sensor + godoc-phrase sensor + forward-doc skip-marker for future consumer-side enforcement. No comparison/ordering helpers using TimestampWall in package; EquivalentTo correctly excludes TimestampWall from idempotency identity.
