title: Scenario Harness spec — implementation
description: Implements specs/scenario-harness.md v0.2.0 (36 §4 reqs SH-001..SH-034 + SH-015a + SH-016a; 5 invariants SH-INV-001..SH-INV-005; 11 §6.1 RECORDs (ScenarioFile, AgentOverride, FixtureSetup, GitSeedOp, FileSeed, EventExpectation, WorkspacePredicate, OutcomeExpectation, ScenarioResult, AssertionResult, SuiteResult); 8 §8 error classes folded into single sh-error.taxonomy umbrella per §2.6 single-table form. 0 §2.3 coalesces, 0 §2.2 multi-step splits. depends-on: [architecture, handler-contract, event-model, workspace-model, execution-model, operator-nfr, process-lifecycle]; all 7 targets loaded.
close_reason: Stale-open spec-parent epic: 100% of children closed, implementation present in internal/, spec landed. Reassessment 2026-06-02 (5-assessor audit; hk-uxm0j precedent). Daemonization-deferred children confirmed landed via the now-live daemon (pidfile/supervise/set-concurrency/orphan-reap). Spec-vs-epic version drift tracked separately.

title: Scenario format is YAML at v0.1
description: Per SH-001: every scenario MUST be declared as a single YAML file conforming to the `ScenarioFile` schema in §6.1. Other formats (Go-as-code scenarios, DSLs, hybrid YAML+Go) are forbidden at v0.1; tracked at OQ-SH-001 for a post-MVH revision.
close_reason: Re-implemented in 9236b16 (cherry-pick of e40ecab+b52662a) after prior-session reset --soft regression. Build + tests green.

title: Missing twin binary fails the scenario with `twin-binary-not-found`
description: Per SH-010: if a scenario's `agent_overrides` references a twin binary that does not exist (absolute path missing on disk, or name not resolvable against the search-path prefix), harness MUST emit `ScenarioResult` with `verdict=error` and `failure_class=twin-binary-not-found` BEFORE attempting to launch the orchestration drive. Error MUST carry the unresolved name and the search paths consulted.
close_reason: spec-corpus sensor sh010_missing_twin_binary_test.go; all checks pass

title: Twin parity is the parity surface declared in HC §4.8
description: Per SH-011: harness consumes the parity surface declared at [handler-contract.md §4.8] (HC-035 through HC-040). Harness MUST NOT inspect twin's internal state, log file shape, or process tree as a means of evaluating scenario assertions; observability of twin behavior for assertion purposes MUST go through the same handler-contract progress-stream / event-bus surface as real handlers. Process-tree inspection at fixture teardown for the SH-INV-002 sanity check is a hygiene operation, distinct from behavioral observation.
close_reason: done

title: Fixture setup precedes orchestration
description: Per SH-012: for every scenario, harness MUST execute a fixture-setup phase BEFORE invoking orchestration drive of §4.5. Fixture setup MUST: (a) create a fresh workspace conforming to [workspace-model.md §4.1.WM-001] and [workspace-model.md §4.2 branching model], seeded from `fixture_setup`; (b) create an isolated event-log directory; (c) prepare the twin-binary search path. Fixture-setup failure → `fixture-setup-failed` per §8 and MUST NOT proceed to orchestration. On partial-success failure, harness executes fixture teardown best-effort against the partially-constructed fixture; teardown errors append to `error_detail` but do NOT change the failure class.
close_reason: BootstrapFixture + ScenarioWorkspacePath + 14 contract tests + specaudit sensor; c742fa0

title: Each scenario uses an isolated workspace
description: Per SH-013: each scenario's workspace MUST be disjoint from every other scenario's workspace and from the operator's working tree. Disjoint = no two scenarios' canonical workspace paths share a prefix; no symlink under one workspace resolves to a path under another. Harness places each workspace at `<fixture-root>/<scenario-name>/workspace/`; per-suite ephemeral root (see SH-016) and MUST NOT reuse any path from a prior suite invocation. Lease-by-run rules of [workspace-model.md §4.3.WM-010] apply unchanged.
close_reason: gap-fill: 6 contract tests (cross-scenario disjoint, symlink discipline, per-suite no-reuse) + specaudit sensor; commit 16dd244

title: Each scenario uses an isolated event-log directory
description: Per SH-014: each scenario MUST capture events into a dedicated JSONL file under the scenario's synthetic project root (per SH-016a), NOT into the operator's `.harmonik/events/events.jsonl`. Because each scenario runs against a per-scenario synthetic project root, the daemon's normal `.harmonik/events/events.jsonl` write target lands inside the synthetic root by construction — no path-override surface mutated in production code. Harness MUST NOT read from, write to, or otherwise mutate the operator's `.harmonik/` tree.
close_reason: SH-014 isolated event-log dir shipped at internal/scenario/eventlogdir.go (5 tests covering path-isolation invariants incl. operator-root-non-mutation). Reviewed APPROVE-CLEAN. Process anomaly: commit landed directly on main due to worktree-escape — protocol patched at 3201f63 to require pwd/branch verification before commit.

title: Fixture teardown runs on every terminal path (5-step ordered, idempotent)
description: Per SH-015: harness MUST execute fixture teardown on every terminal scenario path (pass, fail, timeout, error). Teardown is run-to-completion best-effort: failure in any sub-step MUST NOT halt remaining sub-steps; all errors accumulated. Sub-steps in order: (a) terminate any still-live handler subprocesses honoring [handler-contract.md §4.4.HC-018] cancellation bounds (per-handler T_cancel ceiling; total bounded by `N × HC-018-ceiling`); (b) release worktree leases per [workspace-model.md §4.3.WM-013b]; (c) close event-log file (fsync then close); (d) stop per-scenario daemon via `daemon stop` RPC of [process-lifecycle.md §4.2.PL-003a] (graceful drain bounded by [operator-nfr.md §4.7] ON-029 drain-timeout); (e) record `workspace_snapshot_path` (recording obligation, NOT termination — see SH-015a). Idempotent. On any sub-step failure → `cleanup-failed` per §8.8; verdict-downgrade follows §8.0 precedence.
close_reason: corpus-search sensor: 13 sub-checks for SH-015 heading, all-paths coverage, run-to-completion, sub-steps (a)HC-018 (b)WM-013b (c)fsync (d)PL-003a+operator-nfr.md (e)workspace_snapshot_path, idempotency, cleanup-failed, §8.0; commit 0839726

title: Workspace snapshot mechanism (in-place worktree)
description: Per SH-015a: `workspace_snapshot_path` recorded in `ScenarioResult` MUST point at the per-scenario worktree directory in-place (same path created at SH-012). Harness MUST NOT copy, archive, or otherwise relocate the worktree at teardown; SH-016's "fixture root not auto-deleted" rule preserves the worktree for post-hoc inspection. `workspace_state` predicates (SH-022) resolving `git_ref_at`/`commit_trailer_present` MUST inspect `.git/` directly via `git` plumbing; predicates resolving `file_exists`/`file_contents_equal`/`file_contents_match` inspect working files directly. Snapshot is captured AFTER (a)-(d) of SH-015; merge-back to integration occurred during orchestration per [workspace-model.md §4.5.WM-019] BEFORE teardown.
close_reason: WorkspaceSnapshotPath func + unit tests + SH-015a spec-corpus sensor; commit 61df572

title: Per-suite ephemeral fixture root (operator inspects; not auto-deleted)
description: Per SH-016: harness MUST create a per-suite ephemeral fixture root (under OS-temp directory by default, or under `--fixture-root <path>` override) and place all scenario fixture state inside it. Harness MUST NOT delete fixture root automatically on suite completion — operators inspect it for debugging; deletion is operator-driven (`harmonik harness clean` subcommand or `rm -rf`). Subsequent suite invocations create their own fresh fixture roots; prior fixture roots accumulate. Path MUST be reported in `SuiteResult.fixture_root`.
close_reason: SH-016 + SH-016a per-suite ephemeral fixture-root path helpers shipped at internal/scenario/fixtureroot.go (NewFixtureRoot + ScenarioProjectRoot, 9 tests, harmonik-harness- prefix, no auto-delete invariant tested). Reviewed APPROVE-WITH-NOTES; SH-016a bundling judged defensible (tightly-coupled pure path-helper, zero risk for hk-i0tw.18 downstream consumer). Worktree-isolated correctly under patched protocol.

title: Per-scenario synthetic project root (one ephemeral project per scenario)
description: Per SH-016a: each scenario invocation MUST run against an ephemeral per-scenario synthetic project root at `<fixture-root>/<scenario-name>/project/`. Synthetic root is the working directory the harness uses when invoking the daemon entry-point of SH-017. Daemon's startup per [process-lifecycle.md §4.2.PL-005] writes `.harmonik/daemon.pid`, `.harmonik/daemon.sock`, `.harmonik/daemon.instance-id`, reads `.harmonik/daemon.state` relative to its working directory → those writes land inside synthetic root by construction. Each synthetic root contains: (i) fresh git repository (`git init` + `fixture_setup.git_seed`); (ii) empty `.harmonik/` minted by daemon at PL-005 step 0; (iii) per-scenario Beads SQLite at `.harmonik/beads.sqlite`; (iv) per-scenario event log. One-daemon-per-project per [process-lifecycle.md §4.1.PL-001] satisfied because each scenario IS a different project. PL-005 step 8 reconciliation runs against freshly-seeded fixture state and is a no-op by construction; harness MUST NOT skip the step. OQ-SH-011 tracks daemon-side `--project-root` flag for future flexibility.
close_reason: SH-016b SynthesizeProjectRoot shipped at internal/scenario/synthprojectroot.go (creates fixture dir + git init via exec.CommandContext, NO .harmonik/ pre-creation per PL-005 step 0 ownership, 7 tests). Calls existing ScenarioProjectRoot from fixtureroot.go. Reviewed APPROVE-CLEAN. Edges to hk-8mup.10/.2 converted blocks→related (content-only refs to PL-005 step 0 ownership and pidfile path; not authoring-time deps). Worktree-isolated correctly.

title: Harness uses the production orchestrator entry-point
description: Per SH-017: harness MUST drive scenarios by invoking the same daemon entry-point and same orchestrator startup sequence as production `daemon` mode per [process-lifecycle.md §4.2.PL-005]. "Same entry-point" = same composition-root function in same Go package, invoked with different working directory (per-scenario synthetic project root per SH-016a) and different external configuration (handler-config overrides per SH-008). Harness MUST NOT bypass daemon, MUST NOT instantiate orchestrator outside production composition root, and MUST NOT skip startup-sequence steps (PL-005 steps 0–9 all run; reconciliation in step 8 is no-op against fresh fixture state). Harness MUST detect daemon process death and classify a daemon crash as `orchestration-internal-error`.
close_reason: done

title: Scenario files live under `scenarios/` at the repo root
description: Per SH-002: scenario files MUST be discovered under the repo-root directory `scenarios/` (recursive descent permitted). Repo root is the directory returned by `git rev-parse --show-toplevel` at startup. Absolute or workspace-relative scenario paths forbidden at MVH. File extension MUST be byte-exact `.yaml` (lower-case); `.yml` / `.YAML` rejected at suite-load discovery as `scenario-load-failure` per §8.1.
close_reason: spec-corpus sensor sh002_scenario_discovery_test.go; all checks pass

title: No test-mode branches in production-imported packages
description: Per SH-018: production-imported packages (daemon, orchestrator, agent-runner, workspace manager, hook system, policy engine, event bus, any handler implementation) MUST NOT contain conditional branches keyed off "is this a test?" / "is this scenario mode?" / "is this a twin?" / "is this a harness invocation?". Harness applies production stack with two and only two surface mutations: handler-config overrides (§4.3) and working-directory assignment to per-scenario synthetic project root (§4.4 SH-016a). Forbidden token set canonical (also enforced by §5 SH-INV-001 grep): `scenarioMode`, `isTest`, `isTwin`, `harnessMode`, `isFakeRunner`, `useStub`, `if agent_type == "*-twin"`, `HasSuffix("-twin")`, `cfg.TestMode`, any env var matching `HARMONIK_*_MODE`. Reviewer obligation to reject violating PRs is a §10.2 conformance test obligation.
close_reason: done

title: Orchestration internal error fails with `orchestration-internal-error`
description: Per SH-019: if orchestration drive returns an error from daemon's startup, dispatch, or shutdown that is NOT a scenario-attributable failure, harness MUST classify scenario as `verdict=error` with `failure_class=orchestration-internal-error`. Scenario-attributable failure is the closed set: (i) handler error surfaced as `agent_failed` per [handler-contract.md §4.6.HC-024]; (ii) twin-scripted failure surfaced as same; (iii) gate denial surfaced as node-level outcome; (iv) any condition the orchestrator's edge-cascade resolves into a terminal `run_failed` event. Anything else (daemon crash via process-exit, RPC error from daemon socket, panic in orchestrator goroutine, daemon entered `degraded` state per [process-lifecycle.md §4.4 PL-010] mid-scenario, store-divergence detected by reconciliation mid-scenario) is `orchestration-internal-error`. Original error captured verbatim in `ScenarioResult.error_detail`. Review escalation automatic.

title: Harness reads captured JSONL event log as the assertion surface
description: Per SH-020: after orchestration completes (normal terminal-state emission or timeout cancellation), harness MUST read the captured JSONL event log and evaluate each declared assertion against it. Read MUST conform to [event-model.md §4.5.EV-021] observational-replay rules: harness is performing observational replay; output is advisory for state but authoritative for "what events were observed." Harness MUST NOT reconstruct state from JSONL; declared `expected_workspace` predicates inspect the workspace tree directly per SH-022. Harness MUST also capture each handler subprocess's stdout/stderr to per-scenario files at `<fixture-root>/<scenario-name>/logs/<role>-{stdout,stderr}.log`; not part of assertion surface but paths reported in `ScenarioResult`.
close_reason: done

title: Assertion vocabulary at v0.1 is four kinds
description: Per SH-021: harness MUST support exactly four assertion kinds at v0.1 — (a) `event_present` (event of declared type and optional payload-field-equals predicates emitted); (b) `event_absent` (no event of declared type matching predicates was emitted during scenario's wall-clock window); (c) `workspace_state` (declared filesystem or git-ref predicate over post-orchestration workspace tree); (d) `exit_code` (run-level terminal-emission event payload `run_completed{status=...}` / `run_failed{status=...}` per [event-model.md §8.1.8] carries declared `outcome_status` matching [execution-model.md §4.1 EM-005] `Outcome.status` enum). `payload_match` keys use dotted-path grammar; bracket form for array indices. Equality: numeric value, NFC-normalized strings, identity for booleans/null. Shallow-merge: declared keys MUST appear; actual MAY contain unmatched keys. `event_absent` window: `[fixture-setup-completion, terminal-event-emission OR timeout-cancellation-completion]`. `assertion_results` ordered: `expected_events` declaration order, then `expected_workspace`, then `expected_outcome`. No short-circuit (per SH-023). Composite + ordering predicates → OQ-SH-006.
close_reason: SUBSUMED. AssertionResultKind in internal/scenario/assertionresult.go already defines the four-kind enum (event_present, event_absent, workspace_state, exit_code) with Valid/MarshalText/UnmarshalText and table-driven tests, satisfying SH-021. Implementer's only commits were redundant gofmt fixes already cleaned by hk-fijqi (commit 893dd2b). Worktree dropped without merge.

title: Workspace-state predicates inspect the captured snapshot in-place
description: Per SH-022: `workspace_state` assertion kind MUST be evaluated against the per-scenario worktree tree in-place (per SH-015a) — NOT against a copy or archive. Harness MUST NOT mutate workspace during evaluation. Predicates MUST address files by repo-relative path; absolute-path predicates forbidden so the same scenario is portable across operator machines. Symlinks under the worktree resolving outside the worktree (path traversal) MUST be rejected at predicate-evaluation time as `assertion-failed`. Per-kind interpretation of `expected` field declared at §6.3.
close_reason: done

title: Assertion failure produces verdict `fail` with `assertion-failed`
description: Per SH-023: when any declared assertion fails to hold, harness MUST set `ScenarioResult.verdict=fail` and `failure_class=assertion-failed`. Result MUST carry one `AssertionResult` record per declared expectation. Harness MUST NOT short-circuit on first failure — every assertion evaluated so operator sees full failure picture. Even on `verdict=timeout` (per SH-026) harness MUST evaluate all declared assertions best-effort against the partial event log (impossible-to-evaluate assertions recorded with `passed=false` and explanatory note); verdict remains `timeout`. Full evaluation is deliberate cost-vs-debuggability tradeoff per spec §4.6 RATIONALE.
close_reason: corpus sensor sh023_assertion_verdict_test.go — 10 sub-checks all green; commit 3c7318d

title: Event-log capture failure escalates to `harness-internal-error`
description: Per SH-024: harness's read of captured JSONL is governed by [event-model.md §6.2] post-fsync torn-tail rule — torn tail at end of file (well-formed lines followed by partial trailing record after last fsync) MUST be skipped silently per EV §6.2 (normal post-fsync behavior, not corruption). Harness MUST classify scenario as `verdict=error` / `failure_class=harness-internal-error` (NOT `assertion-failed`) when, and only when, log read fails for: (i) event-log directory or file does not exist; (ii) permissions error; (iii) JSON-parse error at position other than post-fsync tail (mid-file corruption); (iv) disk-full or other I/O error; (v) `EV-011a bus_overflow` event observed in captured log (events shed during scenario, defeating assertion completeness). Harness MUST NOT silently treat absent events as "not emitted" when log is corrupt or incomplete.
close_reason: done

title: Every scenario declares a wall-clock budget (`timeout_secs` 1..7200)
description: Per SH-025: every `ScenarioFile` MUST declare a positive `timeout_secs` field (integer seconds, range `[1, 7200]`); out-of-range values MUST fail at scenario-load time per SH-004. Deadline measured against monotonic clock (e.g., Go's `time.Now()` monotonic component) so wall-clock regressions during NTP corrections do not cause spurious timeouts. Wall-clock timestamps recorded in `ScenarioResult.started_at`/`completed_at` (RFC 3339 millisecond UTC) for operator-facing display only. No harness-default budget; explicit declaration prevents accidental long-runners.
close_reason: spec-corpus sensor sh025_timeout_secs_test.go; all checks pass

title: Timeout exceedance produces verdict `timeout` with `scenario-timeout`
description: Per SH-026: when scenario's orchestration drive does not reach a terminal state within `timeout_secs` (measured per SH-025 from fixture-setup completion to orchestrator emitting terminal `run_completed`/`run_failed`), harness MUST cancel orchestration by invoking per-scenario daemon's `stop` RPC of [process-lifecycle.md §4.2.PL-003a] (graceful drain, escalating to SIGKILL on drain-timeout per [operator-nfr.md §4.7 ON-029]). Each scenario runs against own per-scenario daemon (per SH-016a) → `daemon stop` halts cleanly without affecting other scenarios. Harness then executes fixture teardown per SH-015 and emits `ScenarioResult` with `verdict=timeout` and `failure_class=scenario-timeout`. Per-handler cancellation honors [handler-contract.md §4.4.HC-018] bounded-cancellation; overall cancel-and-teardown bound is `(N × HC-018-ceiling) + ON-029-drain-timeout`. Suite-mode efficiency (per-scenario cancel without daemon termination) is post-MVH per OQ-SH-012.
close_reason: corpus sensor sh026_timeout_verdict_test.go — 10 sub-checks pass; commit cb84707

title: Identical inputs MUST produce identical observable verdicts
description: Per SH-027: given identical inputs (same harmonik build, same twin binaries, same scenario file, same `fixture_setup` seed), harness MUST produce identical values for the determinism contract field set on every run. Contract field set: `ScenarioResult.verdict`, `ScenarioResult.failure_class`, ordered list of `(AssertionResult.passed, AssertionResult.assertion_kind)` tuples, multiset of distinct event types in captured JSONL. Byte-identity of captured JSONL NOT required (UUIDv7 event IDs, wall-clock timestamps, PIDs, `daemon_instance_id` per PL-005 step 0 drift); semantic identity is. Carve-out: twins exercising wall-clock heartbeat mode of [handler-contract.md §4.6.HC-026a] are exempt; such scenarios MUST be tagged `cadence_tag=nightly` and excluded from SH-INV-004 sensor's rerun-diff check.
close_reason: done

title: Scenario file is declarative-loadable without code execution
description: Per SH-003: scenario file MUST be parseable by a generic YAML loader plus the §6.1 schema validator without invoking any scenario-defined code, plugin, or external process. `!eval`-style YAML extensions, `!!python/object` constructors, or any tag executing code during parse are forbidden. Encoding MUST be UTF-8 without BOM. Per-file size ceiling default 1 MiB, parsed-node count ceiling 100 000; either overrun → `scenario-load-failure`.
close_reason: spec-corpus sensor sh003_declarative_loadable_test.go; all checks pass

title: Harness MUST NOT depend on external network access
description: Per SH-028: every scenario MUST execute without any non-loopback outbound network call from harness, per-scenario daemon, orchestrator, watchers, agent-runner, or twin binaries. Permitted: `127.0.0.0/8`/`::1` (loopback, including daemon's own UNIX-domain or local-TCP RPC socket), AF_UNIX sockets within synthetic project root, filesystem-only ops. Forbidden: any non-loopback IPv4/IPv6 connection; outbound DNS to non-loopback resolver. Verification mechanism floor — Linux: `unshare(CLONE_NEWNET)` with only loopback up; macOS: `pf` packet-filter ruleset dropping non-loopback egress (declared `darwin-only`, OQ-SH-013 if mechanism choice changes). Scenario producing detectable non-loopback connection MUST fail with `verdict=error`/`failure_class=harness-internal-error`. §10.2 conformance lane MUST run with sandbox enabled.
close_reason: spec-corpus sensor sh028_no_external_network_test.go; all checks pass

title: Every scenario declares a cadence tag
description: Per SH-029: every `ScenarioFile` MUST declare `cadence_tag ∈ {smoke, regression, nightly}`. Harness MUST support `--cadence <tag>` CLI flag whose accepted values are `smoke`, `regression`, `nightly`, and `all`. Cadence-superset relation: `smoke` → smoke; `regression` → smoke+regression; `nightly` → smoke+regression+nightly; `all` (or omitted) → all three. Filter resolving to empty scenario set MUST emit `SuiteResult` with `suite_verdict=pass` and empty `results` (vacuously true), exit 0. No harness-default cadence assignment for scenario authoring.
close_reason: SH-029 CadenceTag/CadenceFilter enums shipped at internal/scenario/cadencetag.go + cadencetag_test.go (3-tag + 4-filter, 12-cell Includes superset matrix verbatim). Reviewed REQUEST-CHANGES on M1 (panic in production Includes default branch — protocol §Lint rule 4 violation); fixed inline-amend at e3f4852 (panic → return false; godoc updated to SHOULD-validate-via-Valid contract). MINORs deferred. Worktree-isolated correctly.

title: Scenarios MAY declare a parameter matrix (cap 1024 cells)
description: Per SH-030: a `ScenarioFile` MAY declare a `matrix` field — map of parameter-name to list-of-values. Harness MUST expand scenario into one execution per cell of the cartesian product, capped at 1024 cells per scenario; over-cap → `scenario-load-failure`. Synthetic per-cell `name`s render as `<scenario-name>[<k1>=<v1>,<k2>=<v2>,...]` with parameter keys in byte-lex order; values formatted using YAML scalar form. Parameter substitution uses Go `text/template` syntax (delimiters `{{` `}}`); only field-substitution supported (no conditionals, loops, function calls beyond identity). Unknown parameters, unresolvable templates, or matrix-cell name collisions (per SH-005) MUST fail at scenario-load time per SH-004.
close_reason: spec-corpus sensor sh030_parameter_matrix_test.go; all checks pass

title: Scenarios run sequentially at MVH
description: Per SH-031: harness MUST execute scenarios sequentially at MVH — at most one scenario's full lifecycle (per SH-016a synthetic-project-root creation through SH-015 fixture teardown completion) may be active at any time. "Active" = between fixture-up start and teardown sub-step (e) completion, no other scenario MAY have any sub-step running. Pre-fetching scenario N+1's fixture state in parallel with scenario N's teardown forbidden at v0.1. Reconciliation workflows internal to a single scenario (per PL-005 step 8 dispatching against fixture state) are NOT "concurrent scenarios". Concurrent multi-scenario execution → OQ-SH-002.
close_reason: corpus-search sensor landed: sh031_sequential_scenarios_test.go, commit 9961949

title: Harness CLI grammar at MVH (`harmonik harness`)
description: Per SH-032: harness binary is `harmonik harness` (a `harmonik` subcommand). MVH CLI surface: `--cadence <tag>` (per SH-029); `--scenario <path>` (repeatable; subset run); `--fixture-root <path>` (SH-016 override; default OS-temp); `--twin-search-path <path>` (SH-009 override; default `<repo-root>/twins/`); `--list` (print discovered scenarios + cadence; no execution); `--dry-run` (suite-load + matrix expansion only; no orchestration); `--output <human|json>` (default `human`); `--verbose` (operator progress to stderr). `SuiteResult` to stdout in chosen format at suite end; harness-internal logs to stderr. Exit codes: 0=pass, 1=fail (≥1 scenario failed), 2=suite-load aborted (per SH-006), 3=harness-internal error, 130=SIGINT. Two concurrent invocations against same operator project permitted (each creates own per-suite ephemeral fixture root); MUST NOT contend for shared resources since both run against synthetic project roots per SH-016a.
close_reason: spec-corpus sensor sh032_harness_cli_test.go; all checks pass

title: Signal handling and graceful shutdown
description: Per SH-033: harness MUST handle SIGINT and SIGTERM by attempting graceful shutdown — cancel currently-running scenario per SH-026 (treating as timeout-equivalent: `verdict=error`, `failure_class=harness-internal-error`, `error_detail` indicating operator interrupt), execute SH-015 teardown, write partial `SuiteResult` to stdout containing completed scenarios + interrupted scenario's error verdict, exit with code 130 (SIGINT) or 143 (SIGTERM). Second SIGINT during graceful shutdown → exit immediately (code 130) without further teardown — operator-driven escalation overrides cleanup invariant.

title: ScenarioResult durability and emission
description: Per SH-034: every scenario's `ScenarioResult` MUST be written to disk at `<fixture-root>/<scenario-name>/result.json` immediately after the scenario completes (before the next scenario begins). Aggregate `SuiteResult` MUST be written to `<fixture-root>/suite-result.json` AND emitted to stdout per SH-032 at suite completion. `SuiteResult.suite_verdict` is `pass` iff every `result.verdict==pass`; any non-pass result implies `suite_verdict=fail`. Harness crash after per-scenario `result.json` write but before suite-level emission allows operator reconstruction; harness MAY (post-MVH) provide `harmonik harness reconstruct <fixture-root>` subcommand. `ScenarioResult.error_detail`, when present, MUST be a non-empty operator-readable string with at minimum the underlying `err.Error()` and (where applicable) captured panic stack.

title: Sensor: no test-mode branches in production code (4-layer corpus grep)
description: Per SH-INV-001: for every package imported by harness from production tree (daemon, orchestrator, agent-runner, workspace manager, hook system, policy engine, event bus, handler implementations), there MUST be ZERO conditional branches keyed off "is this a test/scenario/twin/harness?". Harness configures production stack via two explicit surface mutations: handler-config override (SH-008) + working-directory assignment to per-scenario synthetic project root (SH-016a). All other behavior identical to production by construction. Sensor: corpus-grep at harness conformance test layer (§10.2) over production-imported package set, with 4 layered checks — (1) token-set grep case-insensitive (`scenarioMode`, `isTest`, `isTwin`, `harnessMode`, `isFakeRunner`, `useStub`, `cfg.TestMode`); (2) regex `if\s+.*[Tt]est|[Tt]win|[Ss]cenario|[Hh]arness.*Mode`; (3) suffix-test pattern `HasSuffix\([^,]+,\s*"-twin"\)` and `agent_type\s*==\s*"\*?-twin"`; (4) env-var pattern `HARMONIK_*_MODE` reads in production code. Excluded: unit-test packages (per HC-035 in-process fakes carve-out) and harness's own packages. Periodic review of token-set per §10.2.

title: Sensor: workspace state fully reset on teardown (descendant-tree, leases, fds)
description: Per SH-INV-002: after SH-015 teardown completes, no live process spawned by the scenario (handler subprocesses, `br` CLI subprocesses spawned by daemon per [process-lifecycle.md §4.1.PL-006], any twin-spawned grandchild orphaned by twin exit) remains; no worktree lease still held by scenario's `run_id`; no event-log file descriptor still open. "No live process" evaluated by enumerating daemon process's full descendant tree at teardown completion (`getppid()`-walk on Linux/Darwin, marker env var `HARMONIK_RUN_ID` per PL-006a for cross-confirmation); zombies (reaped but not yet wait-ed) tolerated. Harness MUST guarantee these on every terminal path including `fail`, `timeout`, `error`. Sensor: post-suite assertion at harness conformance test layer (§10.2) AFTER last scenario's teardown completes AND BEFORE `SuiteResult` written, inspects (i) descendant process tree (no PID with `HARMONIK_RUN_ID` env matching any executed scenario's run_id), (ii) worktree-lease registry (per-fixture-root scan: no `<workspace>/.harmonik/lease.lock` files held by executed run_id), (iii) open fds (`lsof`-equivalent: no event-log file under fixture root remains open). Residuals fail the suite.

title: Sensor: harness operates only on twin binaries (path-prefix + HC-043 hash)
description: Per SH-INV-003: every handler binary launched during scenario execution MUST be a twin per [handler-contract.md §4.8.HC-035]; harness MUST refuse to launch a real-model handler. The "is a twin" predicate: a binary is a twin iff its absolute resolved path is under the configured twin-binary search-path prefix per SH-009 (item (b)) OR scenario's `agent_overrides` declared the binary AND daemon's HC-043 commit-hash check passes against a registered twin entry. Name-only heuristics (`HasSuffix("-twin")`) NOT sufficient and MUST NOT be used by sensor; predicate is purely path-prefix-based + HC-043 hash verification. Sensor: pre-launch check at handler-config resolution (§4.3) verifies predicate; scenario whose resolved configuration would launch a non-twin MUST fail with `verdict=error`/`failure_class=harness-internal-error`. Conformance test: scenario whose `agent_overrides` references `/usr/bin/claude` (or any binary outside search-path prefix) MUST fail at pre-launch check, NOT at orchestration time.

title: SH §8 failure-class taxonomy (8 classes + §8.0 precedence table)
description: Implement the 8-class failure taxonomy + §8.0 precedence ordering. Classes (§8.1..§8.8): `scenario-load-failure` (YAML parse / schema check / unknown workflow / unknown cadence / parameter-expansion / duplicate name); `twin-binary-not-found` (`agent_overrides` references binary not on disk or unresolvable); `fixture-setup-failed` (workspace creation per [workspace-model.md §4.1.WM-003], git seed, file writes, fixture-root unreachable); `orchestration-internal-error` (daemon startup/dispatch/shutdown error not classifiable as scenario-attributable); `assertion-failed` (≥1 declared assertion `passed=false` per SH-023); `scenario-timeout` (orchestration drive does not reach terminal within `timeout_secs` per SH-026); `harness-internal-error` (closed list: event-log unreadable per SH-024 / real-model handler attempted launch per SH-INV-003 / outbound non-loopback network call per SH-028 / harness panic / operator interrupt per SH-033 / per-scenario daemon start failure per SH-017); `cleanup-failed` (fixture teardown sub-step failure per SH-015). EM analogs noted per §8: `twin-binary-not-found` adjacent to EM §8.2 `structural`; `orchestration-internal-error` adjacent to EM §8.2 `structural` when underlying error is `ErrStructural`; `scenario-timeout` adjacent to EM §8.4 `canceled`. §8.0 precedence (highest first): harness-internal-error → orchestration-internal-error → scenario-load-failure → twin-binary-not-found → fixture-setup-failed → scenario-timeout → assertion-failed → cleanup-failed (NEVER overwrites prior verdict; appended to error_detail only) → no failure (verdict=pass). §7.1 lifecycle pseudocode applies precedence consistently. Adding a new class requires foundation amendment per [architecture.md §4.6]; orphan errors recorded under harness-internal-error until pattern justifies a new class. OQ-SH-010 tracks extension protocol.
close_reason: Precedence()/HigherPrecedenceThan() added to failureclass.go; specaudit sensor sh_section8_taxonomy_test.go; all tests pass; blocker hk-zs0.21 is DEFERRED post-MVH

title: Add SuiteID typed UUID alias to internal/core for SuiteResult.SuiteID
description: Define SuiteID as a typed UUID alias in internal/core, mirroring RunID (core/runid.go). SuiteID is the UUIDv7 identifier generated at suite invocation per specs/scenario-harness.md §6.1 RECORD SuiteResult field suite_id. Currently SuiteResult.SuiteID uses a string placeholder (godoc TODO referencing this bead). When this lands, SuiteResult.SuiteID should be changed from string to core.SuiteID (non-breaking: string-constant assignment is assignable to typed-string alias).

title: Suite-load is a discrete discovery + parse + validate phase
description: Per SH-006: harness MUST execute scenario discovery, file parse, schema validation, and uniqueness check in a discrete `suite-load` phase BEFORE launching any orchestration. Suite-load failure (any duplicates per SH-005, parse errors, schema errors) MUST abort the entire suite with a single suite-level error and MUST NOT execute any scenarios. Per-scenario load failures discovered during suite-load are still recorded as individual `ScenarioResult` entries with `verdict=error`.
close_reason: spec-corpus sensor sh006_suite_load_phase_test.go; all checks pass

title: Scenario execution order is deterministic byte-lex of `name`
description: Per SH-007: within a single suite invocation, harness MUST execute scenarios in byte-lexicographic order of their `name` field (locale-independent UTF-8 byte comparison; not Unicode-collation order). Matrix-expanded synthetic names (per SH-030) participate in the same ordering. Determinism on order is required for flaky-test bisection, log-diff comparison across runs, and crash-report localization.
close_reason: spec-corpus sensor sh007_execution_order_test.go; all checks pass

title: Twin substitution is a handler-config override, not a runtime branch
description: Per SH-008: twin-binary substitution MUST be expressed as a scenario-file `agent_overrides` map applied to the workflow's resolved handler configuration at workflow-load time per [handler-contract.md §4.1.HC-003]. Harness MUST NOT introduce any runtime branch in production-imported daemon, orchestrator, watcher, or adapter code that selects between real and twin handlers. This requirement enforces [handler-contract.md §5.HC-INV-002] (twins indistinguishable from real handlers) at the harness boundary.
close_reason: done

title: Twin-binary discovery uses scenario path or PATH prefix
description: Per SH-009: a scenario's `agent_overrides[role]` value MUST resolve to a twin binary by (a) absolute path declared in the scenario, or (b) a name resolved against a configured twin-binary search-path prefix. Harness MUST NOT perform unrestricted `$PATH` lookup; mirrors [handler-contract.md §4.10.HC-042]. Twin-search-path source precedence: CLI `--twin-search-path <dir>`, env `HARMONIK_TWIN_SEARCH_PATH`, default `<repo-root>/twins/`. Resolved twin paths remain subject to [handler-contract.md §4.10.HC-043] commit-hash verification and HC-045 launch-rule conformance.

title: DOT merge-to-main fails (rebase_conflict) when .beads/issues.jsonl is dirty from daemon's own br flush
description: Live DOT run walked to the close terminal (implementer commit aff08aa, reviewer APPROVE) but failed at mergeRunBranchToMain: 'rebase_conflict: cannot rebase: You have unstaged changes' (error: Please commit or stash them). The unstaged file is .beads/issues.jsonl — TRACKED (.gitignore: .beads/* + !.beads/issues.jsonl) and dirtied by the daemon's own br state-flushes during a run (bead claim → in_progress etc.). The DOT merge (WorkflowModeDot branch ~workloop.go:1295-1314 → mergeRunBranchToMain) does a git rebase in the main worktree, which refuses with a dirty tracked file. Blocks completion of every DOT run. NEEDS: determine DOT-specific vs general; match how builtin handles daemon-owned .beads churn before rebase. Fix in progress this session. Source: QA 2026-05-27 live run hk-3yz2d.
close_reason: Fixed (ceeae33) + proven: discardDirtyBeadsLedger handled .beads ledger churn before rebase. Full live DOT run of hk-3yz2d completed end-to-end after this + hk-aiw63 (run_completed, bead closed, 02868b0 on main).

title: Commit-gate does not run //go:build scenario tests before merging a test bead -> RED scenario tests merge green
description: Observed 2026-06-02: 3 scenario-tagged tests (authored by dispatched beads hk-sk00a, hk-umemp) passed the daemon commit-gate + review-loop and MERGED to main while FAILING under go test -tags=scenario. Default 'go test ./...' skips //go:build scenario, so main's normal build stays green and the regression is invisible until the tagged suite is run. Cause: commit-detection only checks 'did HEAD advance'; the review-loop reviewer reviews code quality but does not execute the scenario suite. For a bead whose deliverable IS a scenario test, the gate should run the affected tagged tests and BLOCK merge on red. Proposal: when a dispatched bead adds/edits files under test/scenario, internal/scenario, or with a //go:build scenario tag, run 'go test -tags=scenario' for the affected package(s) in the commit gate and fail the run if red. Anchors: internal/daemon commit-gate + review-loop path; docs/foundation/project-level/testing.md (run gate). Surfaced while filling scenario-coverage gaps (see FIX bead for hk-sk00a/hk-umemp).
close_reason: done

title: MVH-FIX: T3/T6 tests self-signal kills test binary; convert to ctx.WithCancel
description:
After hk-7oz2f made `daemon.Start(ctx, cfg)` accept an external context, the smoke test was converted to use `ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(...)` for clean shutdown. But the exploratory T3 + T6 tests still use `syscall.Kill(syscall.Getpid(), syscall.SIGINT)` to stop the daemon — that kills the entire test binary, not just the daemon goroutine.

Result: `go test ./internal/daemon/...` exits with "signal: interrupt" mid-run.

11 sites to convert:
- internal/daemon/t3_exploratory_test.go: 5 sites (lines 259, 333, 438, 527, 577)
- internal/daemon/t6_scale_shape_test.go: 6 sites (lines 218, 314, 369, 436, 495, 551)

Pattern: every test that constructs cfg + spawns daemon.Start in a goroutine should:
1. Construct `ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())` at the top.
2. Pass `ctx` to `daemon.Start(ctx, cfg)`.
3. Replace each `syscall.Kill(syscall.Getpid(), SIGINT/SIGTERM)` with `cancel()`.

The intent of the SIGINT/SIGTERM-mid-run scenarios (T3-02 and T3-03) is to verify the daemon responds to signals. With ctx-based shutdown, the signal handler in cmd/harmonik/main.go translates SIGINT to ctx.Done — so testing ctx cancellation IS testing the same code path. Acceptable substitution. Document this in test godoc.

Separately: `TestT2_MalformedNDJSON` was broken by hk-9cob3's watcher-failure-check (the test asserted old behavior where watcher errors didn't cause ReopenBead; new behavior is correct, test needs updating). Fix the assertion as part of this bead.

Verification gate:
- `go build ./...`
- `go test ./internal/daemon/...` green WITHOUT signal: interrupt.
- `go test ./...` green.
- `gofmt -d` empty.
close_reason: converted 11 syscall.Kill self-signal sites to ctx.WithCancel+cancel(); fixed T2_MalformedNDJSON assertion for hk-9cob3 watcher-failure contract

title: Killing a wedged daemon leaves queue.json status=active, blocking next 'harmonik run' with no clear command
description: When the orchestrator must SIGTERM a wedged daemon (e.g. stalled on the hk-lckbv buffer-name bug), the daemon dies before finalizing queue.json, leaving status=active. The next 'harmonik run' then exit-1's with 'a queue is already active for this project'. There is NO CLI verb to clear it ('harmonik queue' only has submit/status/dry-run, all of which require a live daemon). Workaround used 2026-05-28: manually 'mv .harmonik/queue.json aside'. FIX: either (a) 'harmonik run' detects no live daemon owns the active queue (stale PID / dead daemon_instance_id) and auto-reclaims/resets it; or (b) add 'harmonik queue cancel|reset' that works without a live daemon; or (c) 'harmonik run --force' to supersede a stale queue. Compounds hk-lckbv (the bug that forces the kill in the first place).
close_reason: done

title: MVH-R3: AcquirePidfile + emit daemon_started in daemon.Start
description:
MVH_ROADMAP row #3. Wire pidfile acquisition and the daemon_started event into the composition root.

Edits to `internal/daemon/daemon.go`'s `Start`:
1. Compute pidfile path: `<cfg.ProjectDir>/.harmonik/run/harmonik.pid`. mkdir-p the parent.
2. Call `lifecycle.AcquirePidfile(path)` early in Start, BEFORE constructing the bus. If acquire fails (another daemon holds the lock), return the error.
3. After the bus is constructed and middleware sealed (i.e. after redaction registry wire), call `bus.Emit` with a `daemon_started` event. Payload struct lives in `internal/core/` (check first — if missing, file a typed-alias-deferral bead AND emit with the existing payload shape; do not block on the type).
4. Defer pidfile release: `defer pidfile.Release()` (or whatever the API's release method is — read `internal/lifecycle/pidfile.go` for the actual signature).
5. Keep Start returning nil at the end for now — orphan sweep (#4) and the main work loop (#10) come in later beads.

Acceptance:
- Running `./harmonik` twice in the same project dir: second invocation fails with a clear "pidfile held by pid N" error.
- One `daemon_started` event lands in the bus (verifiable via in-memory observer in a test).
- `go build ./...` + `go test ./...` green.

Read the existing `internal/lifecycle/pidfile.go` for the exact API before coding.
close_reason: Pidfile acquisition wired; daemon_started emitted post-bus-seal; sensors green. Path note: .harmonik/daemon.pid not .harmonik/run/harmonik.pid.

title: harmonik run --notify-stream: write one-line per-bead completion to stdout/FIFO
description: Today harmonik run is a foreground command emitting a final summary on exit. For multi-bead batches the orchestrator can't tell which beads landed without polling 'harmonik queue status' (JSON) or tailing .harmonik/events/events.jsonl. Required: add --notify-stream flag that prints one line per bead-completion event in human-readable form: '[hk-XXX] success (commit abcdef)' or '[hk-XXX] failed (reason: ...)'. Lines emitted as each bead's run_completed/run_failed event lands, NOT just at process exit. Format: --notify-stream (default stdout) or --notify-stream=path (FIFO/file). Enables the orchestrator to react to per-bead completion in real time.
close_reason: done

title: Add `harmonik run <bead-id>` subcommand for single-bead invocation
description: The Phase-2 dogfood of hk-iuaed.6 had to bump bead priority to P0 to target a specific bead via `harmonik --project DIR --max-concurrent 1`, because the daemon polls `br ready` and grabs the first eligible bead. A direct `harmonik run <bead-id>` would submit a single-bead queue and exit when that bead reaches a terminal state — the canonical Phase-2 invocation. Blocking: makes Phase-2 dogfooding cumbersome. Discovered: hk-iuaed.6 dogfood run, 2026-05-15. Source commit: dcd7f7e.

title: Landing strategy selector: squash-merge vs cherry-pick to target_branch
description:
Parent: hk-8m91i (epic).

## Current state

workspace-model.md §4.5 WM-019 mandates `git merge --squash --strategy=ort <task_branch>` from the task branch onto the integration branch — one commit per task. There is no operator selector for cherry-pick, and no notion that the *destination* of the merge varies per bead.

The user explicitly asked for "merged/cherrypicked into" — both are valid landing strategies. The choice is workflow-shaped:

- **Squash-merge** is right when the task branch represents one logical task that should appear as one commit on `target_branch`. Default.
- **Cherry-pick** is right when multiple sibling beads share a `target_branch` and their commits need to interleave preserving per-node granularity, OR when the operator wants the DOT-graph's per-node commits to appear individually on the target.

## Required change

1. Per-bead carrier (from hk-8m91i) declares `landing_strategy: squash | cherry-pick` with default `squash`.
2. Per-bead carrier declares `target_branch` (default `main` or `harmonik/integration` — TBD by epic).
3. WM-019 amendment: merge-back node reads the bead's `landing_strategy` and `target_branch`, executes the corresponding git operation.
4. Cherry-pick path: walk the task branch's per-node checkpoint commits in order, `git cherry-pick <sha>` each onto `target_branch` (probably inside the scratch merge-worktree per WM-018a). Conflict surfaces per existing WM-024.

## Open design questions for this bead

- Cherry-pick conflict resolution: same conflict-resolver agent as squash, or a different role? Probably same (it's a merge node either way).
- Cherry-pick + per-node commits means the per-node commits' Harmonik trailers (Run-ID, Bead-ID) carry through to `target_branch`. Squash currently re-synthesizes trailers per WM-019. Cherry-pick should preserve trailers as-is; document the divergence.
- Does `target_branch` need to exist before the run starts, or does the merge node create it on first use? Probably must-exist; surface a fail-fast if not.

## Tests to add

- Per-bead `landing_strategy=squash` produces one commit on `target_branch` (existing WM-019 behaviour, now parameterized).
- Per-bead `landing_strategy=cherry-pick` produces N commits on `target_branch` where N = checkpoint count.
- `target_branch` defaults correctly when omitted.
- `target_branch` doesn't exist → fail-fast.

## Dependency

Blocked by hk-8m91i carrier decision.
close_reason: landing_strategy selector (squash|cherry-pick) + LandsOn rename implemented per WM-019b

title: iter-2 resume paste-submit race: feedback Enter intermittently lost -> run_stale (distinct from hk-za5mz)

NEW symptom observed in flywheel wave (B5 hk-qx702): after iter-1 claude /exit (Goodbye), the daemon resumes a fresh claude (real session id, correct) and PASTES the combined iter-2 feedback prompt into its pane, but the Enter to SUBMIT it is intermittently LOST — claude sits IDLE at the input box with the prompt unsubmitted (no 'esc to interrupt', no streaming), -> no iter-2 progress -> run_stale. B6 (hk-e3bnw) submitted fine in the same wave, so it is INTERMITTENT (timing race: Enter likely sent before the freshly-resumed claude's input is ready, or otherwise dropped). CONFIRMED: a manual 'tmux send-keys -t <pane> Enter' submitted the pasted prompt and iter-2 began working immediately. Likely residual of hk-poy7k (paste-submit race). Code: internal/daemon/pasteinject.go (paste+Enter) + the iter-2 resume path in reviewloop.go. FIX: ensure the submit Enter registers — wait-for-prompt-ready before Enter, and/or verify-submitted-then-resend. Blocks reliable harmonik review-loop iteration.

title: imrest — bead in_progress as activity-marker; orphan reset on restart
description: Amends the beads-integration discipline to reframe BI-010 claim (open→in_progress) as an ACTIVITY MARKER (observability-only, recoverable), separate from CLOSE/REOPEN which remain TRUTH CLAIMS (load-bearing for correctness). Adds a daemon orphan-sweep duty: on startup, reset stale in_progress beads belonging to this project (via the existing PL-006a project_hash provenance) back to open when no in-flight run resumes them and no adapter intent file routes them through Cat 3a. Mechanical case complements Cat 2 (non-idempotent in-flight; investigator-required) and Cat 3c (merged-but-not-closed). Spec amendment + daemon work; no event-model change expected (in_progress flip is bead state, not workflow event). Codename: imrest. Post-MVH (daemonization-blocked); preparatory spec work can land pre-MVH.
close_reason: Stale-open umbrella epic: 100% children closed + verified landed code/specs. Reassessment 2026-06-02: o52fm=SDLC corpus 21/21 + specs/examples/*.dot; lj0pb=extqueue v0.1 24/24 + internal/queue; iuaed=imrest 6/6 + orphansweep + hk-11xkn ProvenanceChecker (5c51df8f); fgy9o=lifecycle uplift 81.6% (crashrecovery_uplift_hkfgy9o_test.go).

title: imrest-spec-bi: amend beads-integration.md — activity-marker vs truth-claim split
description:
Amend specs/beads-integration.md to introduce the activity-marker vs truth-claim distinction over §4.4 BI-010. Concrete edits:

(1) §3 glossary: add 'activity marker' and 'truth claim' definitions. Reclassify 'terminal-transition write' to two sub-categories — claim (activity) and close/reopen (truth) — both still routed through the §4.8 adapter, both still idempotency-keyed, but with different recovery posture (claim is auto-resettable on orphan-sweep; close/reopen route through reconciliation).

(2) §4.4 add new requirement BI-010d ('Claim is an activity-marker write'): explicitly state that a claim write asserts 'daemon is currently working this bead' and is observability surface for bv/operators, not a load-bearing truth claim. Recovery from a stale claim (daemon crashed mid-run; no terminal event landed; no adapter intent for close/reopen) is the orphan-sweep duty of PL-006 (extended by sibling bead).

(3) §4.4 BI-010a status-mapping table: add a row 'daemon startup orphan-sweep observes stale in_progress with no in-flight run reattachment AND no adapter intent file for close/reopen on this bead' → transition  → op  → caller . Note:  is a new op-name in the BI-010 op set (was {claim, close, reopen}; becomes {claim, close, reopen, reset}). Reset writes route through the §4.8 adapter and carry §4.10 idempotency keys identically (key = <project_hash>:<bead_id>:reset:<daemon_start_ns>).

(4) §4.4 BI-INV-001 update: amend prose to acknowledge BI-010d reset writes are the fourth permitted write op (intra-run prohibition unchanged).

(5) §6.1 HarmonikWriteStatus enum: no change (still {open, in_progress, closed, deferred, tombstone}); reset is a transition, not a new status value.

(6) Revision history entry.

Diff is drafted in the imrest investigation report; this bead is for applying it after user review.
close_reason: specs/beads-integration.md v0.6.0: activity-marker vs truth-claim split, BI-010d, reset op added

title: imrest-spec-pl: amend process-lifecycle.md §4.5 PL-006 — orphan-reset of stale in_progress
description:
Amend specs/process-lifecycle.md §4.5 PL-006 to add a sixth orphan-sweep enumeration bullet (after the existing five: tmux sessions, worktree locks, subprocess cleanup, stale intent files, stale reconciliation locks). The new bullet:

- **Stale `in_progress` bead markers.** The daemon MUST enumerate beads in coarse status `in_progress` via `br list --status in_progress --format json` (existing surface; see beadsadapter `ListInFlightBeads`) and filter to those whose audit trail's most recent `in_progress` transition was authored by this project's daemon (provenance match via `actor` field carrying the project_hash per PL-006a, OR — if Beads's audit `actor` field is unsuitable — by cross-referencing the daemon's own intent-log entries at `.harmonik/beads-intents/*.json` for `claim` ops). For each such bead:
  - IF the in-memory model rebuilt at PL-005 step 7 re-attaches a live in-flight run to this bead (run survived as a re-parented subprocess and was reaped by the subprocess sweep, OR a `claim` intent file is still present and the adapter's BI-031 recovery will re-drive it), the bead is NOT reset.
  - ELSE IF a `close` or `reopen` intent file at `.harmonik/beads-intents/<key>.json` references this bead, the bead is NOT reset (Cat 3a handles it).
  - ELSE IF a merge commit on the target branch bears `Harmonik-Bead-ID: <bead_id>` (Cat 3c condition), the bead is NOT reset by the orphan sweep (Cat 3c auto-resolver fires the close).
  - ELSE the daemon MUST issue a `reset` write via the §4.8 BI adapter (new BI-010d op): `in_progress → open`. Reset write is idempotency-keyed (`<project_hash>:<bead_id>:reset:<daemon_start_ns>`) and intent-logged identically to claim/close/reopen.
- **Event.** The existing `daemon_orphan_sweep_completed` payload (event-model.md §8.7.14) MUST gain a count field `bead_in_progress_reset` for the number of beads reset; this is a payload extension (consider whether it requires a §8.7.14 schema bump or is additive-tolerated by the schema).

Cross-spec coordination request: confirm EV §8.7.14 payload extension is additive-tolerated; if not, file an EV revision bead.

Diff is drafted in the imrest investigation report; this bead is for applying it after user review.
close_reason: Amended specs/process-lifecycle.md §4.5 PL-006 with sixth orphan-sweep bullet for stale in_progress bead reset (BI-010d)

title: imrest-impl-adapter: BI adapter ResetBead op (in_progress→open)
description:
Implement the BI adapter surface for the new `reset` op declared by BI-010d (per imrest-spec-bi sibling bead). Concrete work:

(1) Add `TerminalOpReset` to `internal/core` (alongside `TerminalOpClaim`/`Close`/`Reopen`).

(2) Add `ResetBead(ctx, beadID, projectHash, daemonStartNS)` to `internal/brcli` adapter. Reuses the existing `terminalTransition` machinery: intent-log write (BI-029/030), `br update <id> --status open`, idempotency key `<project_hash>:<bead_id>:reset:<daemon_start_ns>`. Pre-state assertion: current Beads status MUST be `in_progress`; intended post-state `open`. Conflict handling identical to claim/close/reopen (`BrConflict` retries through BI-031 status-check protocol).

(3) Unit tests covering: clean reset (in_progress → open); reset when bead is already open (status_match → no-op recovery + structured-log); reset when bead is `closed` (Cat 3a divergence-inconclusive — emit and refuse reissue); BrUnavailable / BrDbLocked retry per BI-031 step-4 taxonomy.

Blocked by hk-iuaed.1 (spec amendment must land first to define BI-010d normatively).
close_reason: ResetBead op + TerminalOpReset + ResetBeadIdempotencyKey implemented in worktree; 8 tests green

title: imrest-impl-sweep: extend PL-006 orphan-sweep with stale-in_progress reset
description:
Extend `internal/lifecycle` orphan-sweep (PL-006) to enumerate and reset stale `in_progress` beads belonging to this project per the amended PL-006 sixth bullet (see hk-iuaed.2 for spec text). Concrete work:

(1) New file `internal/lifecycle/orphansweepbeads.go`:
    - Query `brcli.ListInFlightBeads(ctx)`.
    - For each bead, determine provenance: prefer `br audit-log <bead_id> --format json` and match the most recent `status=in_progress` transition's `actor` (or environmental marker) against the daemon's `project_hash` per PL-006a. If `actor` is unreliable, fall back to scanning `.harmonik/beads-intents/*.json` for a `claim`-op intent against this bead_id.
    - For each project-owned in_progress bead, decide reset eligibility per the 4-branch logic in the amended PL-006:
      (a) Live in-flight run reattached at PL-005 step 7 → skip.
      (b) Adapter `close`/`reopen` intent present → skip (Cat 3a handles).
      (c) Merge commit with `Harmonik-Bead-ID` trailer on target branch → skip (Cat 3c handles).
      (d) Otherwise → call `adapter.ResetBead(...)`.
    - Emit `daemon_orphan_sweep_completed` with the new `bead_in_progress_reset` count.

(2) Wire the new sweep into the daemon's startup sequence after the existing stale-reconciliation-locks step and before §PL-005 step 4 Cat 0 pre-check. Note: this sweep DOES make a `br` call, which is permitted within PL-006 (the existing stale-intent enumeration also touches `br`-adjacent files); confirm ordering against the BI-024a version handshake (handshake happens at step 4, but BI write surface depends on step 4 having succeeded — so the reset enumeration may need to land at step 7+ after the handshake instead of within step 3). Sequencing decision is owned by this implementation task; document the chosen ordering in the package doc.

(3) Integration test: scenario harness drives a workflow to `in_progress` claim, SIGKILLs the daemon, restarts, asserts the bead is back to `open` in Beads and `daemon_orphan_sweep_completed.bead_in_progress_reset >= 1`.

Blocked by hk-iuaed.2 (PL spec amendment) and hk-iuaed.3 (adapter reset op).

title: imrest-sensor: BI-010d activity-marker invariant sensors (reset routes through adapter; sweep idempotency)
description:
Sensor bead per discipline §2.5. Two distinct sensors covering the BI-010d activity-marker-vs-truth-claim split:

(1) **Reset-op-routes-through-adapter sensor.** Corpus-wide reviewer-persona scan asserts that every `os/exec` site issuing `br update <id> --status open` against an `in_progress` bead routes through the §4.8 adapter's `ResetBead` (not a direct subprocess call). Mirrors the BI-INV-001 / BI-012 contract-test discipline.

(2) **Orphan-sweep idempotency sensor.** Scenario harness asserts that running the orphan-sweep twice in succession against the same fixture (no intervening daemon work) produces zero additional `reset` writes on the second invocation — the first sweep already reset eligible beads, the second sees them as `open` and skips.

title: Scenario test: live restart-recovery harness + hk-z0pmi deadlock (dispatch->kill->restart->reconcile->resume)
description:
GAP (2026-06-02 scenario-coverage assessment): existing crash-recovery scenario tests (internal/scenario/queue_crash_recovery_test.go, internal/lifecycle/startup_pl005_qm002_test.go) only LOAD a pre-written queue.json; NONE exercise the live sequence dispatch->kill/restart-daemon-mid-run->reconcile->assert. That live path is exactly where hk-z0pmi (stuck dispatched+bead-closed item -> group never completes -> QM-027 blocks ALL submits) and hk-5pg37 (cancel+restart orphans) live; both shipped only unit tests.

BUILD ON existing full-composition-root exemplars: internal/daemon/scenario_orphan_sweep_queue_owned_tutqk_test.go and scenario_reap_a31od_test.go (both use daemon.Start + real br DB + BootstrapFixture + real harmonik-twin-claude). Add a reusable helper that: (1) submits a 2-item queue, (2) lets the twin dispatch, (3) kills/restarts the daemon mid-run (or marks one item dispatched + its bead closed externally to model 'landed via another queue'), (4) restarts via daemon.Start, (5) asserts QM-002b Class A' (internal/lifecycle/startup_pl005_qm002.go:~420-428, landed f82c051e) advances the stuck dispatched+closed item to completed, the group reaches all-terminal, queue.json unlinks, and a SUBSEQUENT submit is ACCEPTED (proves the wedge is gone, no queue_already_active). Cover the deadlock COMBINATION: one failed sibling (cross_queue_duplicate, QM-034 'failed item must not interrupt siblings') + one stuck-dispatched item = previously un-completable group.

CONVENTION (docs/foundation/project-level/testing.md): build tag //go:build scenario; name TestScenario_RestartRecovery_*; testify/require only; assert on events JSONL + queue file presence + AssertBeadStatus. Run gate: go test -race -tags=scenario ./... must pass.
Refs: hk-z0pmi, hk-5pg37, QM-002a, QM-002b, QM-034. Coordinates with (do NOT dep) epic hk-fgy9o (P1 crash-recovery, unit/property scope) — this is its scenario-level complement.

title: harmonik run: rebase worktree onto current main immediately before ff-merge
description: Per hk-ocbh2 diagnosis: harmonik run attempts ff-merge at end of each bead. Required: harmonik run should  immediately before the ff-merge attempt. On rebase-conflict, fall back to current behavior (auto-reopen). title: T3 finding (expected, hk-fgdgz): worktree left behind after SIGINT/SIGTERM mid-run
description: When SIGINT or SIGTERM arrives while the handler is in-flight, the daemon shuts down cleanly but the git worktree created by workspace.CreateWorktree is left on disk. The work loop does not clean up worktrees (known gap hk-fgdgz, filed as post-MVH). On the next startup, the orphan sweep (SweepStaleLeaseLocks) cannot remove these worktrees because the work loop never writes a lease-lock file (workspace.WriteLeaseLockAtomic is not called in the MVH work loop). The stranded worktree is classified as WM-003a bare-worktree-no-lease; it requires reconciliation routing, not the simple PL-006 sweep. Confirmed by TestT3_SIGINTMidRun and TestT3_SIGTERMMidRun.
close_reason: SUBSUMED: duplicate of hk-fgdgz (worktree cleanup, currently in flight)

title: Operational-skills meta-epic — agent-reviewer, beads-cli, agent-config-reviewer, go-subsystem-add skills
description:
Meta-epic tracking the on-disk skill files under `.claude/skills/` that the project's locked build/review pipeline assumes but that do not yet exist on disk. Per Phase 1 readiness analysis (`docs/foundation/phase-1-readiness-gap-analysis.md` §A4 — MAJOR), the corpus has spec-tracking beads describing the contracts these skills satisfy (e.g., `hk-872.34`/`.35` for Beads-CLI agent-facing access path, `hk-a8bg.31` for default skill set including Beads-CLI, `hk-8i31.55..60` for HC §4.11 skill provisioning) but NO bead authors the actual skill file. Per `docs/foundation/project-level/build-practices.md` ("agent-reviewer-every-commit") and `agent-configuration.md` §Skills, four named skills are load-bearing: (a) `agent-reviewer` — the JSON-verdict reviewer that gates non-trivial commits LOCALLY (per 2026-05-05 user clarification: lefthook hook + Makefile target, no GitHub Actions); (b) `beads-cli` — the agent-facing wrapper for `br` per BI §4.4 / HC §4.11 / CP-031; (c) `agent-config-reviewer` — Tier 2 reviewer skill that runs at session boundaries; (d) `go-subsystem-add` — package-scaffold skill that runs at first-add for new `internal/<subsystem>/` directories.

Children file the discrete authoring tasks. Two are `scope:bootstrap` (agent-reviewer, beads-cli — needed for first self-build cycle); two are non-bootstrap (agent-config-reviewer, go-subsystem-add — they fire at session boundaries / first-add events that occur after Phase 1 entry). All four sit under `tag:meta` because they are project-build infrastructure, not workflow primitives.

Sources: phase-1-readiness-gap-analysis §A4 (MAJOR), §B4 (MAJOR), §"Recommended new tracking beads — Under an operational-skills meta-epic". Companion to `hk-pvcs` (build-scaffolding) — `hk-pvcs.2` (Makefile) and `hk-pvcs.4` (lefthook.yml) both reference `make agent-review` invocations and stub-call this skill until it lands here.
close_reason: Subsumed: all four children (.1 agent-reviewer, .2 beads-cli, .3 agent-config-reviewer, .4 go-subsystem-add) closed 2026-05-06..2026-05-08; .claude/skills/{agent-reviewer,beads-cli,agent-config-reviewer,go-subsystem-add}/SKILL.md all on-disk.

title: Custom git merge-driver for .beads/issues.jsonl (union-by-bead-ID, last-writer-wins by updated_at)
description: Ship a custom git merge-driver to replace the lossy 'git checkout --theirs .beads/issues.jsonl' workaround documented in HANDOFF.md:55. Algorithm: parse %O/%A/%B as map[bead_id]row, union on bead_id, pick row with larger updated_at on collisions; union labels + dependencies (monotonic-additive). ~40 LOC Go subcommand 'harmonik beads-merge %O %A %B %P', registered via .gitattributes + .git/config. Daemon runs 'br sync --import-only' post-merge to refresh SQLite. Cases NOT covered (acceptable for quick win): semantic conflicts on same field within seconds (write to .beads/merge-conflicts.log for operator audit); bead deletion (out-of-band); schema migrations (whole-row LWW fallback). title: Wire daemon-side gate evaluator (GateEvalFunc provider) so DOT cascade can dispatch gate nodes
description:
The DOT cascade driver (internal/daemon/dot_cascade.go) errors on gate nodes. The spec contradiction (EM-005b vs CP-058) that originally blocked this is now RESOLVED (CP-058 wins, deny=SUCCESS; see hk-lt0w7 + execution-model.md v0.7.1). The remaining blocker is that NO daemon-side gate evaluator exists: there is no GateEvalFunc provider, no ControlPoint-registry loading from the daemon, and no mechanism/cognition policy resolution.

handler.DispatchGateNode is fully implemented and correct (allow/deny/escalate -> SUCCESS with gate_decision payload + preferred_label; eval-failure -> FAIL no payload), and routing through DecideNextNode on preferred_label is unit-tested. What is missing is the daemon seam that:
 1. resolves a gate node's gate_ref to its Gate ControlPoint,
 2. evaluates mechanism-tagged gates via the policy-expression engine, OR dispatches a cognition-tagged gate as an agent subprocess and reads its verdict (analogous to the reviewer path),
 3. constructs a GateEvalFunc and calls handler.DispatchGateNode, feeding the outcome into DecideNextNode.

Scope: gate nodes only. SUB-WORKFLOW nodes remain separately out of scope. Until this lands, driveDotWorkflow keeps returning a deterministic needs-attention failure for gate nodes (no longer citing the spec contradiction; now citing the missing evaluator seam).

title: session-keeper Phase-2: anti-loop suppression + crash-recovery on boot
description:
=== REVIEWER-FOUND DEFECTS (HARD PREREQUISITES — must fix before the Cycler is wired live; from independent adversarial review of commit 67a74def, verdict APPROVE_WITH_NOTES) ===

DEFECT-1 (HIGH) — empty session_id bypasses the anti-loop gate -> unbounded DESTRUCTIVE re-fire. internal/keeper/cycle.go:182 gate is `if cf.SessionID != "" && cf.SessionID == c.lastFiredSID`. When the gauge session_id is "" the gate is skipped and lastFiredSID stores "" (cycle.go:271), so with pct>=act_pct + idle EVERY tick re-runs the full cycle incl /clear. Empty session_id is REACHABLE: scripts/keeper-statusline.sh:46-50 writes "session_id":"" when jq can't extract it while still writing a valid high pct. FIX (fail-safe): refuse to fire when session_id == "" (cannot establish anti-loop identity), OR persist a per-cycle suppression timestamp independent of session_id. NOT firing when identity is unknown is the safe default.

DEFECT-2 (MEDIUM) — process-monotonic cycle IDs collide with a stale on-disk handoff nonce after crash/restart. cycle.go:94-103 globalCycleSeq is an in-process counter that resets to 0 on restart, so cyc-000001 can be reissued; a leftover HANDOFF-<agent>.md from a pre-crash cycle containing `<!-- KEEPER:cyc-000001 -->` would satisfy the new cycle's pollForNonce INSTANTLY (cycle.go:291) and let /clear fire against a handoff the current session never wrote. FIX: make cycle IDs collision-resistant across restarts (UUID, or seed counter from a persisted high-water-mark / opened_at), AND/OR truncate/rotate the handoff file in step 2 before injecting so a stale nonce can't pre-satisfy the poll. (Dovetails with crash-recovery below.)

DEFECT-3 (MEDIUM, defense-in-depth) — .managed guard is enforced only in the caller (cmd/harmonik/keeper_cmd.go:86), NOT in the destructive code. Add `if !IsManaged(c.cfg.ProjectDir, c.cfg.AgentName) { return nil }` as the FIRST line of MaybeRun (cycle.go:168) so the guard is co-located with the destructive action and no future wiring can bypass it.

DEFECT-4 (LOW) — abort path (cycle.go:227-235) returns nil without recording suppression, so on a stable non-empty session_id the next tick re-injects /session-handoff and re-aborts EVERY tick (~5s) -> pane spam + event flood. FIX: record an abort suppression (set lastFiredSID on abort too, or a short abort-backoff keyed on cycle/session).

(DEFECT-5 — Cycler not yet constructed in production, cmd/harmonik/keeper_cmd.go:97-102 builds WatcherConfig with no Cycler: — is the intended safety posture; live-wiring is a SEPARATE bead that depends on this one. Live-smoke under the supervisor before declaring the wiring done.)

=== ORIGINAL SCOPE: anti-loop suppression + crash-recovery on boot ===

Builds on the cycle core (hk-22i70, internal/keeper/cycle.go + watcher.go). codename:session-keeper, parent epic hk-ekap1 (attach via label, NOT a parent dep).

SAFETY (MANDATORY, same as hk-22i70): DESTRUCTIVE /clear. (a) Act ONLY on a .harmonik/keeper/<agent>.managed agent. (b) ALL tests FAKES (spy injector, injectable clock + session-id source, fake journal path) — NEVER spawn real claude, run real /clear, or target a real agent name (flywheel/named-queues/controlpoints) or convention-resolved real pane.

ANTI-LOOP: After a cycle completes, the Cycler stays SUPPRESSED until BOTH (1) the gauge reports a NEW session_id (!= the session just cycled) AND (2) the gauge pct has been observed BELOW warn_pct (default 80) on that new session_id. Only then re-arm. Subsumes DEFECT-1 (treat empty session_id as un-armable).

CRASH-RECOVERY ON BOOT: On keeper startup read .harmonik/keeper/<agent>.cycle. phase=cleared (died after /clear, before /resume) -> inject /session-resume HANDOFF-<agent>.md, close journal, emit recovery event. phase=handoff (died before /clear) -> discard/close, no injection. phase=complete/closed -> no-op. Respect the .managed guard on recovery. Address DEFECT-2 nonce-collision here (rotate handoff / collision-resistant IDs).

EVENTS: add session_keeper_cycle_recovered (+ any needed) to internal/core/eventtype.go, register in eventreg + pertypecompat, payload type in internal/core/keeperevents.go, emit via the keeper FileEmitter.

TESTS (FAKES only): empty-session_id never fires (DEFECT-1); abort does not re-fire (DEFECT-4); .managed checked inside MaybeRun (DEFECT-3); suppression holds until BOTH new-session_id AND pct<warn_pct then re-arms; boot-recovery injects resume ONLY for phase=cleared, discards phase=handoff, no-op otherwise; stale-nonce after restart does NOT satisfy a new cycle's poll (DEFECT-2); no real session ever touched.

DONE: go build ./... + go vet ./internal/keeper/ + gofmt -l clean + go test ./internal/keeper/ -count=1 all green; tests FAKES-only. Refs the bead ID in the commit.
notes: Independent re-review (flywheel, 2026-06-08) of merged commit 37c356c9: VERDICT APPROVE_WITH_NOTES. Core fail-safe STILL HOLDS (/clear only behind current-cycle nonce confirmation; new gates only suppress, never bypass). DEFECT-1/2/3/4 all genuinely fixed at the right layer with fakes-only tests; anti-loop re-arm correct (no deadlock); crash-recovery sound + managed-guarded; build/vet/gofmt/test green. NOTE N1: Phase-2 (cycle + RecoverFromCrash) NOT wired into prod boot (keeper_cmd.go nil Cycler) -> dormant/safe, latent until the wiring follow-up. NOTE N2 (LOW): TestCycler_AbortDoesNotRefire under-asserts (_ = handoffEvts). Both folded into the wiring bead.
close_reason: done

title: Wire LaunchSpec JSON delivery to subprocess stdin in handler.Launch
description:
## Problem
hk's handler.Launch (internal/handler/handler.go:122-143) starts the subprocess but writes NOTHING to stdin. The LaunchSpec JSON schema described in docs/dogfood-smoke-trace.md §2 and specs/handler-contract.md §4.2.HC-005 is never delivered.

## Observed in smoke-2 (2026-05-12)
claude received empty stdin → had no task description, no bead_id, no workspace_path, no workflow_mode → exited 0 immediately → bead closed as false positive.

## Expected
After cmd.Start(), handler.Launch must write the JSON-encoded LaunchSpec (the full spec from handlercontract/launchspec_hc006.go) to the subprocess stdin pipe within the delivery timeout, then close the write end per HC-005 semantics.

## Acceptance
- handler.Launch writes LaunchSpec JSON to subprocess stdin immediately after Start().
- A real claude subprocess receives the JSON and can parse bead_id, workspace_path, workflow_mode.
- Smoke-2 re-run shows non-empty stdin delivered to claude.

## Refs
internal/handler/handler.go:122-143; docs/dogfood-smoke-trace.md §2; specs/handler-contract.md §4.2.HC-005; internal/handlercontract/launchspec_hc006.go
close_reason: handler.Launch now writes HandlerSpec JSON to stdin via goroutine + CloseStdin; Session.CloseStdin added; 3 tests green

title: Implement internal/supervise/ Go supervisor package (richer --watch-restart shim)
description:
~250-350 LOC Go port of Dicklesworthstone's supervisor.service.ts. Replaces the "30-50 LOC wrapper shim" placeholder in PL-019(f).

Scope:
- Supervisor{spec, state, ...} with New/Run/Stop/Snapshot.
- RestartPolicy enum ("on-failure" | "never" — TS's "always" DROPPED).
- BackoffConfig{Base, Cap, Jitter, MaxRestarts} mirroring handlercontract ProvisioningBackoffConfig (HC-048a).
- Health probe: kill(pid,0) + .harmonik/cognition/heartbeat.json freshness (NOT HTTP).
- Crash-loop guard: N restarts within sliding window → "crashloop" state, exit.

Out of scope: in-process log ring (pane IS the log per PL-028d:238-240); CLI surface (B5).

Spec sections: PL-019(f) — replaces placeholder; no new spec text beyond B0.

Acceptance:
- internal/supervise/supervisor.go compiles, vet+lint clean.
- Run(ctx) handles exit→backoff→respawn with MaxRestarts cap.
- SIGTERM forwarded to child; Stop(timeout) follows PL-011 SIGTERM→bounded-wait→SIGKILL.
- Test: simulated child code=1 ×3 → 3 restarts with monotonic backoff; child code=0 + on-failure → no restart; MaxRestarts=2 → 2 restarts then crashloop + Run() returns.
- Test: heartbeat-file staleness → state.Status=unhealthy, no restart.

Depends on: B0.
