title: Phase-1-validation meta-epic — trivial-slice walkthrough + corpus label reconciliation
description:
Meta-epic tracking the Phase 1 entry-gate VALIDATION artifacts — the "validated" half of the user's reframe ("tasks in place AND validated AND able to start building itself"). Per Phase 1 readiness analysis (`docs/foundation/phase-1-readiness-gap-analysis.md` §E — Validation criterion recommendation), the entry-gate requires:

(a) A paper-walkthrough of the trivial-slice (§D1) mapping the ~25 atomic operations from receive-bead through bead-closed to specific bootstrap beads. Operations with no bead owner are gaps the bootstrap-subset analysis missed. Cheaper than scenario-harness regression (harness doesn't exist yet — circular), live dry-run (no daemon — circular), or `br dep cycles` (already done; only catches one bug class).

(b) A label-reconciliation pass over the 527 untagged beads (823 total − 291 bootstrap − 5 post-mvh = 527 with neither tag, per readiness-gap §A2). After this pass, `br list -l scope:bootstrap` ∪ `br list -l post-mvh` should equal corpus-minus-epic-envelopes — closing the validation surface defect.

Children file the discrete validation tasks. Both are `scope:bootstrap` (mandatory per mission); both are `phase:0` because they MUST complete before Phase 1 entry per the readiness-gap §"Recommended Phase 1 entry gate" items 2 and 4.

Companion to: `hk-pvcs` (build-scaffolding — substrate the corpus assumes), `hk-jhob` (operational-skills — review pipeline), `hk-ahvq.48` (twin-binary scaffolding — first conformance scenarios). The four together close the gap analysis's BLOCKER + MAJOR items (modulo the §A3 readiness-workflow VOIDED per Z.2 user clarification).

Sources: `docs/foundation/phase-1-readiness-gap-analysis.md` §A2 (MAJOR — corpus labelling), §D (first self-build cycle analysis), §E (validation criterion), §"Recommended new tracking beads — Under Phase-1-validation".
close_reason: Both children closed: hk-kle6.1 (trivial-slice walkthrough at docs/foundation/trivial-slice-walkthrough.md) and hk-kle6.2 (corpus label reconciliation, audit at docs/foundation/corpus-label-reconciliation-2026-05-09.md).

title: Author trivial-slice paper walkthrough doc (docs/foundation/trivial-slice-walkthrough.md)
description:
Author the trivial-slice paper walkthrough document per Phase 1 readiness analysis §D1 + §E. Output path: `docs/foundation/trivial-slice-walkthrough.md` (or kerf-scoped artifact at the implementer's discretion).

Scope of this bead:
- Define the trivial slice: a non-agentic single-node workflow exercising the full daemon round-trip (per `bootstrap-subset.md` §1 working-definition item 2). Input: one hand-authored "hello" bead marked dispatchable. Workflow: a 1-node DOT with `kind:non-agentic` that emits an event + commits. Expected: one git checkpoint commit + one terminal event in JSONL + bead closed.
- Tabulate the ~25 atomic operations the trivial slice executes (per readiness-gap §D2 enumeration — daemon-up via PL cluster A 37 beads; bead-in-queue via BI cluster `hk-872.5/.6/.7/.26`; workspace lease via WM cluster B-WM 45 beads `hk-8mwo.1/.10/.19`; workflow execution via EM cluster B-EM+F 65 beads through EM-046b; event capture via EV cluster D 47 beads with 5 of the 78 §8 row types; checkpoint commit via WM-019 + EM trailer set; no reconciliation per Cat 0 path).
- Map each operation to one or more bootstrap beads. Operations with no bead owner are corpus gaps (file new beads or label oversight).
- Surface any "no owner" findings either by labelling existing beads `scope:bootstrap` (if they're untagged from the 527) or by filing new beads (if the operation is not represented).

This is the "validated" criterion at the boundary of "tasks in place" → "able to start building itself." It also addresses §A2's softer concern: an operation that traces to an untagged-bead exposes the labelling oversight; an operation that traces to NO bead exposes a corpus gap.

Companion to: `hk-kle6.2` (corpus label reconciliation — the walkthrough may surface beads to relabel). Both feed into the Phase 0 close (`hk-ahvq.42`).

Sources: `docs/foundation/phase-1-readiness-gap-analysis.md` §D1 (candidate first slice), §D2 (what needs to be working), §E (validation criterion); `docs/decompose-to-tasks/bootstrap-subset.md` §1 (working-definition); candidate bead `p1-trivial-slice-walkthrough` from §"Recommended new tracking beads — Under Phase-1-validation".
close_reason: Implemented in c419225 (rebased): docs/foundation/trivial-slice-walkthrough.md mapping 25 atomic ops across 7 groups to 55 owning bootstrap beads; soft-gap deferrals to hk-kle6.2 + hk-hqwn.59 family. Reviewer APPROVE (MINOR: §3 tally inconsistency 55 vs 58 — not blocking).

title: Apply post-mvh / scope:bootstrap labels to the 527 untagged beads (corpus label reconciliation pass)
description:
Apply a label-reconciliation pass over the 527 currently-untagged beads (823 total − 291 `scope:bootstrap` − 5 `post-mvh` = 527 with neither tag, per readiness-gap §A2 — MAJOR). After SH loads (~50 more bootstrap beads + ~4 post-mvh) the residual untagged count adjusts but the gap remains the same surface defect.

Scope of this bead:
- Walk the 527 untagged beads via `br --json list --limit 0` filtered by absence of both `scope:bootstrap` and `post-mvh` labels.
- Cross-reference the per-cluster bootstrap-subset narrative reports under `docs/decompose-to-tasks/bootstrap-subset/{pl,wm,em,hc,ev,bi,ar,rc,sh}-bootstrap.md` §5 to identify which untagged beads were narratively-deferred (those should get `post-mvh`) vs which were narratively-included but missed labelling (those should get `scope:bootstrap`).
- Apply labels via `br update --add-label` in chunks. Most of the 527 are likely implicit `post-mvh` per cluster-report enumerations — option (a) from readiness-gap §A2 ("the 527 are all implicit post-mvh, label them as such") is the cheap path.
- Verify post-pass: `br list -l scope:bootstrap` ∪ `br list -l post-mvh` equals corpus-minus-epic-envelopes. (Epic envelopes are the meta-parent rows like `hk-ahvq`, `hk-pvcs`, `hk-jhob`, `hk-kle6`, `hk-zs0`, `hk-b3f`, etc. — they are organizational, not work units, and need not carry scope labels.)

Companion to: `hk-kle6.1` (trivial-slice walkthrough — that walkthrough may also surface labelling errors). Together these two beads close the §A2 + §E validation gap.

Don't conflate with the `gated-by-spec-edit` / `gated-by-corpus-scale` transient tags (per discipline §2.8/§2.5) — those are separate from `scope:bootstrap` vs `post-mvh`. A bead can carry both `scope:bootstrap` and `gated-by-spec-edit` simultaneously.

Per 2026-05-05 user clarification (§Z.2 of readiness-gap doc): no parked-state tagging; the reframe targets bead-lifecycle approval, not the scope/post-mvh layer.

Sources: `docs/foundation/phase-1-readiness-gap-analysis.md` §A2 (MAJOR), §"Recommended Phase 1 entry gate" item 2 (Corpus labelling reconciled), §"Recommended new tracking beads — Under Phase-1-validation"; `docs/decompose-to-tasks/discipline.md` §2.5/§2.8/§2.11; candidate bead `p1-corpus-label-reconciliation`.

title: Wave/concurrent run: first bead's commit prematurely ends ALL siblings' implementer phase (no_commit)
description: Batch3 (2026-05-28, queue 019e70c5, --wave --max-concurrent 3): hk-9w9y5(README,doc) committed+APPROVED, but hk-l8rpd and hk-55zv2 (daemon Go-code beads) BOTH failed no_commit_during_implementer at iteration 1 exit=0. SMOKING GUN: both failed runs' implementer_phase_complete fired at the IDENTICAL microsecond (15:52:56.150824 + .150846, 22us apart) — a single daemon action ended both implementer phases simultaneously, coincident with README committing. 55zv2 worktree (019e70c5-186d) SURVIVED with uncommitted work (ast.go+parser.go, saved /tmp/55zv2-uncommitted.diff) — implementer was mid-edit when its phase was force-completed. This is DISTINCT from hk-68pvl (worktree-removal race, fixed 4abfafd) — no worktree-deletion spiral in panes (/tmp/harmonik-batch3-panes-1780008809.txt). Hypothesis: review-loop wave mode has a GROUP-SYNCHRONIZED implementer-phase barrier — when the first sibling commits/completes, the daemon marks the whole group's implementer phase complete (completion_reason=error), killing slower siblings before they commit. Serial (max-concurrent 1) is unaffected (Batch2: 4/4 success). MITIGATION until fixed: run Track-2 code beads serially. Related: hk-wx8z8 (parallel-run stability).
close_reason: Fixed (81e661b: pin pane-PID to slash-free %ID), reviewed APPROVE, EMPIRICALLY VALIDATED: 7-bead 3-wide concurrent wave landed 6/6 non-timeout beads with ZERO no_commit; slower siblings survived faster ones completing. Parallel work confirmed safe.

title: Unify DOT parsers: internal/workflow/dot vs internal/workflowvalidator disagree on fixture dialect
description:
The CLI pre-run check 'harmonik graph validate' (EM-038) is NON-FUNCTIONAL: its parser (internal/workflowvalidator/dotparser.go) passes ZERO of 20 real DOT files in QA, including the canonical specs/examples/review-loop.dot. The daemon EXECUTION path (internal/workflow/dot via workflow.LoadDotWorkflow) parses all of them correctly. Two parsers, wildly divergent.

ROOT CAUSES (dotparser.go):
1. [P0] Bare graph-level attrs unhandled (dominant): 'schema_version="1";' falls through to node-stmt (dotparser.go:168-173), leaves pos on '=', next readIdentifier errors 'unexpected character =' (dotparser.go:285). Breaks 17/20 files. Daemon handles at parser.go:417-430.
2. [P0] Leading // and /* */ comments unhandled: parseDigraph (dotparser.go:60-63) calls expect("digraph") before any comment skip (comment-skip only inside parseBody:99-105). review-loop.dot fails at pos 0.
3. [P2] 'strict digraph' rejected (dotparser.go:62 vs parser.go:337-339).

SEMANTIC DIVERGENCES (even if parse succeeded), workflowvalidator/validator.go vs internal/workflow/dot/validator.go:
4. [P0] validator FORBIDS handler_ref on non-agentic nodes (validator.go:257); daemon REQUIRES it per EM-007 (dot/validator.go:190). Direct contradiction — breaks every noop terminal.
5. [P1] validator reads start_node_id only (validator.go:166); daemon accepts DOT-canonical start_node too (parser.go:538).
6. [P1] node-type enum mismatch: validator accepts control-point (validator.go:245); daemon rejects per WG-001 (parser.go:608).
7. [P1] validator requires idempotency_class on gate/sub-workflow; daemon FORBIDS per WG-008 (dot/validator.go:232,263).

FIX: make 'harmonik graph validate' parse via internal/workflow/dot (workflow.LoadDotWorkflow) + run the C2/EM-038 validator from internal/workflow/dot. Port any unique EM-038 diagnostics workflowvalidator has onto the unified parser output, then retire dotparser. Acceptance: 'harmonik graph validate specs/examples/review-loop.dot' returns 0 diagnostics; all 12 valid corpus files pass; all 8 negatives rejected with correct WG codes (not parse crashes). Source: QA session 2026-05-27, 20-file corpus.
close_reason: Fixed + proven: graph validate now parses/validates via internal/workflow/dot (same as daemon). Landed a90980e. Verified 20/20 corpus: 12 valid files pass incl. review-loop.dot, 8 negatives rejected with correct WG-027/024/028/em038 codes. Two-parser divergence eliminated.

title: DOT: add tool/shell node type + handler (run tool_command, exit-code→outcome/failure_class)
description:
T1 — Tool node: dispatchDotToolNode + built-in shell handler (KEYSTONE). Cite WG-039, WG-024 bullet (§I.10), HC-063 (§III.1), HC-005 note (§III.2), HC-058 note (§III.4), EM-057 item 7 (§II.7), EM-058 non-agentic row + sub-note (§II.8).

WHAT: Split the dot_cascade.go:198-203 non-agentic SUCCESS-synth branch on tool_command presence.

- Parse tool_command (string) and REUSE timeout (int seconds, default 300) into dot.Node (new fields) + core.Node (ToolCommand *string; timeout already exists at core/node.go:28-31 — REUSE Node.Timeout, do NOT add timeout_command). Add tool_command/timeout to the parser node-attr switch (parser.go:601+) and the reserved node-attr handling. Per WG-024: timeout MUST be a non-negative integer (strict error otherwise); tool_command on a node whose handler_ref != "shell" is a v1 WARNING (retain + emit WG-031 warning), reserved-strict at next major.
- Implement the built-in shell handler: when node.Type==non-agentic && tool_command present && handler_ref=="shell", execute /bin/sh -c <tool_command> with cwd = wtPath (the run worktree) and the daemon handler env; kill on timeout (default 300s). MAY run IN-PROCESS (no subprocess/socket/NDJSON/agent_ready/heartbeat per HC-063). Map exit state → Outcome: exit 0 → SUCCESS; non-zero (1..255) → FAIL+failure_class=deterministic; timeout-kill → FAIL+transient; signal-kill (ctx-cancel/operator stop/SIGKILL) → FAIL+canceled. kind=default, no payload, no RETRY/PARTIAL.
- non-agentic node with NO tool_command → preserve today's Outcome{SUCCESS} synth (path 2, start/terminal/pass-through). A non-agentic node bound to a non-shell handler → path 3 (invoke handler_ref) — out-of-scope stub OK at v1 (no live non-shell non-agentic handler), but the branch structure must exist.
- Default axis_tags for shell: io-determinism=non-deterministic, replay-safety=unsafe (author MAY tighten via axis_tags).
- Reuse node_dispatch_requested (already emitted at dot_cascade.go:191-193); NO new event (Part IV).

DELIVERABLES: dot.Node + core.Node field additions; parser/validator changes; dispatchDotToolNode (NEW) in dot_cascade.go + the split in the NodeTypeNonAgentic case; specs/examples/<tool-node>.dot canonical example; unit tests for the exit-state→Outcome map.

ACCEPTANCE: a .dot with a non-agentic tool_command="exit 0" node returns SUCCESS; tool_command="exit 3" returns FAIL+deterministic; a sleeping command past timeout returns FAIL+transient; ctx-cancel → FAIL+canceled. A non-agentic node with no tool_command still synths SUCCESS (no regression — existing dot smoke graphs pass). tool_command with handler_ref != shell → load warning, run still starts. Negative/non-int timeout → ingest strict error.

DEPENDS ON: T0 (hk-jyqxe, spec landed). Foundation for T6a (tool scenario hk-cucz6) and the example beads hk-o52fm.*. Source: kilroy gap analysis 2026-05-28.

title: Daemon iter-2 resume stalls: synthetic-claude-session-ID yields tmux buffer name violating harmonik-<session-id>-<purpose>
description: Under --wave, when a reviewer verdict triggers an iter-2 implementer resume (REQUEST_CHANGES or flagged-APPROVE), the daemon calls WriteLastPane(task) with buffer name 'harmonik-synthetic-claude-session-<ts>-task'. The synthetic session ID (minted by hk-isq02 iter>=2 fallback) CONTAINS DASHES, so the buffer-name parser expecting harmonik-<session-id>-<purpose> (split on '-') sees too many segments -> 'tmux: structural invariant violated' -> feedback inject skipped -> run goes run_stale (no run_completed/run_failed) -> daemon wedges. REPRO: wave-2 runs 019e7242-43df (hk-uidls) + 019e7242-45a9 (hk-d0aqq) both stalled here; recovered by manual cherry-pick of the approved commits. ROOT: internal/daemon/pasteinject.go WriteLastPane/buffer-name construction + synthetic-session-ID minting. FIX: sanitize the synthetic session ID to contain no dashes, OR make the buffer-name parser treat purpose as the last '-' segment and session-id as the remainder. Add a regression test: WriteLastPane succeeds with a synthetic session ID on iter-2.
close_reason: done

title: claude session lifecycle + bidirectional messaging
description:
Umbrella for the gap between 'bridge wiring works' and 'real claude actually receives work and the impl/reviewer cycle closes'. Smoke v3 RED surfaced that the daemon never tells claude what to do: buildClaudeLaunchSpec produces argv ['--session-id', <uuid>] only; claude opens an idle TUI; HC-056 times out.

User-locked constraints:
1. NO headless mode. No --print. Claude runs interactively in a tmux window.
2. Task delivery via a file is OK but NOT CLAUDE.md (user's CLAUDE.md must not be overwritten in worktrees). Pick a different file name (e.g. .harmonik/agent-task.md).
3. Bidirectional arbitrary message passing is core. daemon -> claude (initial task, follow-up messages, impl-resume feedback) AND claude -> daemon (completion, intermediate progress, outputs).
4. NO interactive trust prompts. Worktree must be auto-trusted.

Full audit: docs/claude-session-comms-audit-2026-05-13.md.

Headline gaps:
- argv has no prompt; no task file is written anywhere in the worktree.
- SubstrateSession.SendInput is a silent no-op (substrate.go:110).
- tmux.Adapter has no SendKeys / LoadBuffer / PasteBuffer.
- CHB and HC are silent on how the daemon delivers a task / follow-up messages.
- Twin-claude is fed a YAML script at launch; real claude has no equivalent path.
- Review-loop launches reviewer with no information about what to review.
- impl-resume reopens claude's transcript but never delivers reviewer feedback.

Children (proposed): see audit §6. Spec amendments + tmux adapter extension + task-file write + paste-inject + worktree auto-trust + agent_ready reframing.

Mostly spec-gap, partly impl-gap: the bridge wiring matches the specs that exist; the specs themselves do not define the daemon -> claude task-delivery channel under the interactive substrate.
close_reason: all children closed, epic complete — hygiene closure

title: Fix TestT6_10BeadSequentialDrain regression after hk-1rocd agent_ready reframing
description:
TestT6_10BeadSequentialDrain hangs at 121s after hk-1rocd (commit 5199aa4). The test uses a non-claude handler script that does not fire SessionStart hooks via the hook relay; agent_ready is now relay-synthesized from SessionStart per CHB-013, so the wait times out, the bead reopens, and the 10-bead sequence accumulates retries until test timeout.

Fix options:
- (a) extend the test fixture to inject a synthetic relay agent_ready via the daemon's new SetAgentReadyCallback (introduced in 5199aa4) for handler scripts that bypass real claude.
- (b) add a daemon config knob honored only by non-tmux substrate paths that falls back to handler-emitted agent_ready when no relay is configured.
- (c) make the test handler script POST a synthetic SessionStart hook to the relay socket.

(a) is the smallest change and respects the spec — the daemon already exposes the callback; test fixtures should drive it.

Smoke v3 RED was the LAST known failure mode pre-umbrella; the umbrella corpus (hk-lj1p9) addressed the structural gap. This T6 regression is a downstream consequence of the agent_ready reframing and is the next thing to fix before smoke v4.

Acceptance: TestT6_10BeadSequentialDrain passes in under 60s with -short and full mode. Other T-suite tests in internal/daemon/ continue to pass.
notes: Root cause: hk-1rocd changed pre-exec step 4 in internal/handler/claudehandler_chb006_024.go to emit launch_initiated INSTEAD OF agent_ready, uniformly. But HC-039 amendment says 'Tmux substrate path calls out relay synthesis; Conventional subprocess path unchanged.' Conventional subprocess paths need agent_ready to still be emitted by the handler (since no real SessionStart hook ever arrives). Fix: handler emits BOTH launch_initiated AND agent_ready for the conventional-subprocess path; tmux substrate suppresses the handler-side agent_ready (relay synthesizes it). Either branch on substrate type in the handler, OR have the daemon self-emit agent_ready on conventional-substrate launches (since the daemon knows the substrate).
close_reason: Resolved by hk-xzmpw fix (commit fb86274). Root cause was different than initial hypothesis: WriteAgentTask was failing with ENOENT because .harmonik/ subdir didn't exist in fresh worktree; daemon retried continuously, never completing any bead. MkdirAll fixes it. T6 now passes in ~42s.

title: Isolate EnsureWorktreeTrust from real ~/.claude.json in tests + prevent concurrent-write races
description:
Critical: hk-fdyip's EnsureWorktreeTrust (internal/workspace/claudetrust_wm040b.go) writes to the literal user-home '~/.claude.json'. Two real bugs:

1. **Test runs corrupt the user's real claude config.** TestThroughput_TenBeadsAtMaxFour spawns 4 concurrent daemons; each calls EnsureWorktreeTrust against ~/.claude.json. The atomic temp+rename is per-process atomic, but multi-process races on the SAME target produce torn writes — verified empirically: 10 beads × 4 concurrent caused 'Extra data: line 3917 column 1' (garbage tail). User config was corrupted; orchestrator manually repaired by truncating to first valid JSON object.

2. **Test runs pollute the user's claude config indefinitely.** Even when not corrupted, every test daemon adds projects[<tempdir>] entries. The user's ~/.claude.json had 273 stale '/worktrees/' entries from prior test runs accumulated; the file is now 173KB.

Fix design:
- Honor a CLAUDE_CONFIG_HOME (or HARMONIK_CLAUDE_CONFIG_PATH) env var inside EnsureWorktreeTrust. When set, write to that path instead of ~/.claude.json. Tests set it to t.TempDir() based path.
- Add a file lock (advisory flock) around the read-modify-write so that even within-test parallel daemons don't tear. The atomic rename is necessary but insufficient.
- (Optional) Add a hygiene helper that prunes projects[] entries pointing to non-existent paths on each write; bounds the size of the user config.

Surfaced by TestThroughput_TenBeadsAtMaxFour failure (post hk-9ow36 / hk-lj1p9.2 fixes). T6_10BeadSequentialDrain passes because it's sequential (max-concurrent=1) so the race doesn't trigger; only T11 with MaxConcurrent=4 races.
close_reason: EnsureWorktreeTrust now honors HARMONIK_CLAUDE_CONFIG_PATH and CLAUDE_CONFIG_HOME env overrides; sidecar advisory flock (LOCK_EX) serializes RMW across concurrent daemons. Tests redirect via t.Setenv. Verified: workspace test suite under -race passes; ~/.claude.json mtime unchanged. Commit f6cd256. Optional stale-projects pruning deferred.

title: session-keeper Phase-2: WIRE the Cycler live (enable-switch) + boot crash-recovery + N2 test
description:
THE ENABLE-SWITCH for session-keeper Phase-2 (DEFECT-5 / re-review note N1). codename:session-keeper, parent epic hk-ekap1 (attach via label, NOT a parent dep).

⚠️ THIS BEAD MAKES THE DORMANT DESTRUCTIVE /clear CYCLE LIVE in the shipped `harmonik keeper` binary. Two independent reviews confirmed the cycle core (hk-22i70) + hardening (hk-kct9t, commit 37c356c9) are safe and fail-safe, but BOTH noted the Cycler is not constructed in production: cmd/harmonik/keeper_cmd.go:97-103 builds WatcherConfig with a nil Cycler, and watcher.go:314 guards on Cycler != nil, so MaybeRun/runCycle//clear and RecoverFromCrash are unreachable today.

BLOCKED ON: hk-aalsm (PreCompact backstop) landing + its independent re-review passing — this wiring should also register the PreCompact hook, and both touch watcher.go. Do NOT dispatch concurrently with hk-aalsm.

SAFETY INVARIANT (MANDATORY): even after wiring, the cycle acts ONLY on an agent with .harmonik/keeper/<agent>.managed. NEVER create that marker for a real agent (flywheel/named-queues/controlpoints) in code, tests, defaults, or docs examples. The wiring must not auto-create or imply a default .managed. Tests FAKES-only — never construct a live Cycler against a real pane/agent.

SCOPE:
1. Construct the Cycler in cmd/harmonik/keeper_cmd.go and set WatcherConfig.Cycler = NewCycler(...). Pass ActPct: float64(actPctFlag) (the flag is currently read then discarded). Wire all real deps (real injector, real gauge reader, real journal read/write, real handoff truncate, IsManaged, gates CrispIdle/HoldingDispatch).
2. Call cycler.RecoverFromCrash(...) on boot AFTER the IsManaged gate (keeper_cmd.go:89) and BEFORE w.Run — so a crash that left a session cleared-but-not-resumed self-heals on keeper restart.
3. Register the PreCompact hook (from hk-aalsm) in the keeper subcommand / SPEC if hk-aalsm landed it.
4. Update the keeper_cmd self-description: it currently says "Phase-1 warn-mode" with --act-pct "reserved" — update to reflect Phase-2 active (still .managed-gated).
5. Fold in re-review NOTE N2 (LOW): internal/keeper/cycle_test.go TestCycler_AbortDoesNotRefire currently does `_ = handoffEvts`; assert handoffEvts == 1 (no second handoff_started on abort re-entry).
6. Fold in PreCompact re-review NOTE A (LOW, defense-in-depth): the shell hook scripts/keeper-precompact-hook.sh and the gates.go precompact helpers (precompactMarkerPath / HasPrecompactTrigger / ClearPrecompactTrigger) accept an UNVALIDATED agent name — unlike SetDispatching/ClearDispatching which call validateAgent. A traversal name (AGENT="../../foo") could write/read a marker outside the keeper dir. Fix: (a) in the hook, add `case "$AGENT" in */*|*..*) exit 0 ;; esac` right after AGENT is resolved (fail-open on a bad name); (b) make HasPrecompactTrigger/ClearPrecompactTrigger/precompactMarkerPath early-return / error on validateAgent(agent) != nil, mirroring IsManaged. Tests FAKES-only. (Notes B/C from that review are INFO-only, no action.)

DONE: go build ./... + go vet ./internal/keeper/ ./cmd/harmonik/ + gofmt -l clean + go test ./internal/keeper/ -count=1 green; N2 test asserts the count; tests FAKES-only; no real .managed created anywhere.

DO NOT auto-close after merge: this bead is DONE only after the live throwaway dogfood (bench 14-dogfood-cycle.md) passes BOTH the happy path AND the abort path on a THROWAWAY session under the supervisor — per the daemon-deploy lesson (live-smoke before declaring done). The implementer should land the code; the orchestrator (flywheel) runs the dogfood and only then closes. Refs the bead ID in the commit.
close_reason: done

title: Surface implementer stderr_tail on review-loop path (extend hk-ajhqw; reviewloop.go:340 drops implEI)
description: hk-ajhqw added stderr-tail surfacing on exit=-1 crash for the single-workflow path (workloop.go:1430-1440). The review-loop path (reviewloop.go:340) does NOT propagate ExitInfo — line reads '_ = implEI', dropping all crash diagnostic info. Consequence: when implementer claude crashes silently in review-loop mode (now the default since hk-g0ckv), we get zero error info in events.jsonl. ALL OF TODAY'S RECENT DISPATCH FAILURES (hk-xegej, hk-yejfj, hk-g0ckv first try) hit this — implementer never reached bridge handshake, synthetic-claude-session fallback fired, no stderr surfaced. FIX: propagate implEI.StderrTail into the noChange-timeout fail-reason and into a new log line, mirroring workloop.go:1428-1441. Repro: dispatch any bead via 'harmonik run --beads <id> --notify-stream' where the implementer claude crashes early; verify stderr-tail appears in events.jsonl or daemon stdout. Cite: reviewloop.go:340 ('_ = implEI'); workloop.go:1428-1441 (the equivalent fix for single-mode); reviewloop.go:367-372 (synthetic-session-id fallback that fires when no handshake).
close_reason: Landed as 7e377cf

title: MVH-P1: fixture bead corpus generator for exploratory wave
description:
EXPLORATORY_TESTING_PLAN.md §4 P1. A shell script that generates a `.beads/` SQLite DB pre-seeded with N ready beads of varied shapes, for the exploratory wave.

Location: NEW `test/exploratory/fixtures/seed.sh`.

Contract:
- Accepts `--count N` (default 5) and `--dest DIR` (required).
- Initializes a fresh beads DB in `<DIR>` via the `br` CLI.
- Creates N ready beads with varied bodies (cycle and repeat if N > 5):
  1. ASCII single-line body.
  2. Multi-paragraph body (~500 words of filler text).
  3. Unicode title + body (e.g. CJK characters, emoji).
  4. Body containing typical punctuation (apostrophes, quotation marks, parentheses).
  5. Body that is empty or near-empty.

- All beads start in a state that makes them appear in `br ready --limit 0`.

Validation: `bash seed.sh --count 3 --dest <tmpdir>` succeeds and `(cd <tmpdir> && br list --status ready --limit 0)` shows 3 entries.

Sibling: `test/exploratory/reset.sh` (committed at fc5446e) — match its argparse / error-handling style. Run shellcheck if available.

Idempotent: re-running with the same dest overwrites prior contents.
close_reason: seed.sh at test/exploratory/fixtures/seed.sh — fc08174

title: Surface node 'role' into the agent brief (marquee differentiation; cheap precursor to hk-sdnzj)
description:
T2 (role-surfacing half — cheap precursor to hk-sdnzj's full inline-prompt). Cite WG-040 region, B↔E composition §II.10. Same pre-launch region as hk-sdnzj — SEQUENCE m5lmo before/with sdnzj.

LIVE FINDING (marquee smoke hk-3wbff, dual-review-consolidate): the multi-reviewer-consolidate STRUCTURE runs end-to-end live (proven: implement→review_correctness→review_design→consolidate→close, bead closed), but all reviewer nodes ran IDENTICAL generic review behavior — the per-axis briefs encoded in each node's role= ('correctness+tests' vs 'design&idioms' + write-commit-findings-first discipline) were ignored, and NO reviews/reviewer-*.md were committed. So differentiated multi-axis review (the headline pattern's VALUE) does NOT work today.

CORRECTION (was 'role is parsed but NEVER injected'; the parse claim was imprecise): role= IS parsed — it lands in dot.Node.UnknownAttrs["role"] (NOT a dedicated struct field). What is missing is the READ: dot_cascade.go assembles the agent brief from beadTitle/beadDescription/extraContext only and never reads UnknownAttrs["role"].

FIX (cheap v0, precursor to hk-sdnzj's full prompt attr): read the node's role from UnknownAttrs["role"] (or add a dedicated dot.Node.Role / core.Node.Role field and parse into it), and append a short role descriptor to the agent brief — a one-line suffix to taskBody, or via a claudeRunCtx field set in the pre-launch region of dispatchDotAgenticNode (before claudeRunCtx is built at dot_cascade.go:400). This gives reviewer-node differentiation immediately, without the full inline-prompt replacement.

ACCEPTANCE: a node with role="design&idioms" has that descriptor visible in its rendered agent-task.md.

DEPENDS ON: T0 (hk-jyqxe). Precedes / pairs with hk-sdnzj (component B of attractor-parity); co-edits the same pre-launch region — serialize. Source: QA 2026-05-28 marquee live smoke.
close_reason: done

title: Integration smoke: 4h unattended flywheel run + CL conformance scenarios
description:
Run the flywheel unattended for 4h against the real harmonik daemon. Verify CL conformance scenarios 1-5 hold in vivo.

Spec sections: CL §7 (Conformance acceptance scenarios).

Deliverables:
- A runbook under .flywheel/runbooks/v0.1-smoke.md documenting startup, observed cache-hit rate, refill behavior, the 10-in-flight crash scenario reproduction, defects found.

title: EV-002b sensor RED on main: handler gate_dispatch.go+runtime.go import internal/core for legitimate domain types — narrow Shape A per hk-hqwn.4 follow-up
description:
SYMPTOM: `go test ./internal/core/` FAILS — TestEventEV002b_HandlerPackageDoesNotImportCore (internal/core/handlereventidroute_ev002b_test.go). internal/handler/gate_dispatch.go:43 and internal/handler/runtime.go:31 import internal/core. Main is RED. Introduced when gate_dispatch.go landed (4a74c132, hk-a8bg.5); runtime.go added the import earlier (13bf17b6).

ROOT CAUSE: Both files import core ONLY for domain types/enums — Outcome, OutcomeStatus*, OutcomeKind*, NodeID, GateRef, Run, RunID, GateDecisionPayload, GateDecisionRecordedPayload, FailureClass*, SubWorkflowRef, WorkflowVersion, NodeTypeSubWorkflow, EventTypeGateDecisionRecorded. NEITHER uses NewEventIDGenerator nor generates event_id. The EV-002b Shape A sensor is a blanket 'no handler->core import' guard now catching a LEGITIMATE type-import.

SANCTIONED FIX: hk-hqwn.4 (the bead that ADDED this sensor, CLOSED) explicitly noted: 'Shape A over-broad (forbids any internal/handler->core import); tighten when first legitimate handler->core type-import lands.' This is that moment.

DO (reproduce-first):
1. Read the normative spec for EV-002b (search specs/ + docs/ for 'EV-002b' / event-model §4.1). Confirm the rule is 'handler MUST NOT GENERATE event_id' (NewEventIDGenerator stays daemon-side), NOT 'handler must never import core at all'.
2. PRIMARY: narrow Shape A in handlereventidroute_ev002b_test.go to forbid only the real violation — handler importing/using event_id-minting symbols (NewEventIDGenerator / EventIDGenerator) — while PERMITTING domain-type imports. Keep Shapes B and C intact, and keep a positive assertion that a handler importing NewEventIDGenerator still trips the sensor.
3. FALLBACK only if the spec text genuinely mandates zero handler->core imports: relocate the shared domain types into a neutral package (e.g. internal/coretypes) imported by both. Prefer the smaller sensor-narrowing fix.
4. If event-model.md §4.1 itself conflates 'no event_id generation' with 'no core import', make the minimal spec clarification faithful to EV-002b intent.

DONE MEANS: `go test ./internal/core/ ./internal/handler/` both green; sensor still catches a handler that imports NewEventIDGenerator; no domain-type relocation churn unless the spec mandates it.

title: Daemon work loop idle-waits forever when queueStore non-nil but zero queues loaded -> swallows br-ready fallback, breaks --auto-pull + smoke tests (NQ-B1 regression)
description: VERDICT (investigator, 2026-06-03): genuine production liveness bug, narrow. The 'reconciliation never completes' framing was a RED HERRING -- reconciliation_started (daemon.go:1098) is fire-and-forget _=bus.Emit and does NOT block. The real stall is the work loop blocking at internal/daemon/workloop.go:1043 workloopIdleWait(dispatchCtx, deps.submitWakeC), inside the if !ok branch (lines 1040-1047) of the if deps.queueStore != nil block (begins ~919). workloopIdleWait (workloop.go:2740-2747) blocks on ctx.Done() or wakeC; wakeC only fires on a 'harmonik queue submit'. The smoke fixtures seed beads via br create (NO queue submit), so it blocks until the test cancels (~61s) -> 0 run_started. ROOT CAUSE: the NQ-B1 named-queues refactor (commit a027808d) replaced the old 'if q := queueStore.Queue(); q != nil' guard -- which FELL THROUGH to the br-ready path when the store was non-nil-but-EMPTY -- with selectNextQueue + 'if !ok { idle-wait; continue }', which swallows the empty-store case. The br-ready fallback (workloop.go:1276-1356) is now UNREACHABLE whenever queueStore != nil (always true in prod, daemon.go:1351) AND zero queues are loaded. This breaks the documented --auto-pull mode (cmd/harmonik/main.go:491-492). The everyday --no-auto-pull queue-only flywheel is UNAFFECTED (queue IS submitted), which is why production dispatched its 7-bead batch fine. Both TestMVHSmoke (N=1) and TestParallelSmoke_TwoBeadsConcurrent (N=2) fail identically -- NOT parallel-specific. FIX (P1, restores a published mode): in workloop.go ~line 1040, when selectNextQueue returns !ok BECAUSE zero queues are loaded (DISTINGUISH empty-store from all-queues-paused), skip the idle-wait/continue and fall through to the br-ready path at 1276 (itself still gated by deps.noAutoPull). Simplest: gate the idle-wait on len(loaded queues) > 0. PRESERVE the all-paused case: when queues EXIST but none are eligible, KEEP idle-waiting (do NOT fall through to br-ready). VERIFY before commit: go test -run 'TestMVHSmoke|TestParallelSmoke_TwoBeadsConcurrent' ./internal/daemon/ -count=1 -timeout 90s must go from ~61s hang to fast PASS. Refs: a027808d (NQ-B1 regressing commit), hk-wi3fb (surfaced it), hk-ur428 (gate fail-open masked it).
notes: no_commit_during_implementer: HEAD did not advance past parent fbbde2c178ac6ec45e5f7307c0a5a1629f5782d9 at iteration 1 exit=0
close_reason: done

title: daemon (no-review-loop path): emits outcome_emitted:approved + bead_closed when implementer never committed (false-positive success)

Verification: git reflog --all shows 'branch: Created from <parent>' for each run branch with the parent SHA still as tip.

Root cause hypothesis: hk-9c1v4 fix ('noChange-timeout on implementer phase fails the run; do not advance to reviewer') only covered the review-loop path in runReviewLoop. The single-implementer (no-review-loop) path in beadRunOne probably still treats noChange-timeout as success when there's no reviewer to gate it.

Investigation start: internal/daemon/workloop.go — search for the WorkflowModeSingle / no-review-loop branch around beadRunOne, find where outcome is computed when implementer exits without committing. Apply hk-9c1v4-equivalent logic: noChange-timeout + commit_landed=false → run_failed, not auto-close:exit=0.

Compose with: hk-xegej (AssertEventCausality) — would catch this via 'run_started must be followed by Refs:<bead> commit before outcome_emitted:approved.'

Refs: queue 019e4e31-a208-76b2-8e3d-9b922228bfa9; affected runs 019e4e3b-5534/019e4e44-983d/019e4e57-1637; affected beads hk-wkzlc, hk-8uy6m, hk-jon6r (reopened).

Daemon already knows the base; pass it into the agent's brief as a substitution variable. title: PARA-1: add run_id to core.Event envelope; propagate via busimpl.Emit
description:
POST_MVH_PARALLELISM_ROADMAP.md row #1. The first foundational step to enable concurrent runs: every event must carry the run_id that emitted it.

Today (post-F-001 fix at commit 18002f3): the envelope in busimpl.Emit has event_id, schema_version, type, timestamp_wall, source_subsystem, payload. Two concurrent runs emit `run_started` events that look identical until the reader parses the payload body. This blocks per-run JSONL filtering.

Scope:
1. Add `RunID core.RunID` (or `*core.RunID` if nullable for daemon-level events) to `core.Event` struct in `internal/core/event.go`. Field tag `json:"run_id,omitempty"`. Update event-model.md §4.1 EV-001 to document the new envelope field with `Tags: mechanism`.
2. Update `eventbus.BusImpl.Emit` to accept an optional run_id parameter, OR introduce `EmitWithRunID(ctx, runID, type, payload)` alongside the existing `Emit` to avoid breaking existing call sites. Prefer the latter — fewer call-site changes for the foundational shift.
3. Daemon-level events (daemon_started, daemon_orphan_sweep_completed) emit with empty run_id; the omitempty tag suppresses the field from JSONL when zero.
4. Work loop in `internal/daemon/workloop.go` calls EmitWithRunID for run_started/run_completed/run_failed.

Tests: extend internal/eventbus/busimpl_test.go to assert the new envelope field appears in JSONL for an EmitWithRunID call and is absent (or empty) for a plain Emit call. internal/t5probe/probe_test.go can also gain a "TestParallelism_RunIDInEnvelope" scenario.

Acceptance:
- `go build ./...`
- `go test ./...` green.
- After running the daemon end-to-end, `jq '.run_id' events.jsonl` shows non-empty values for run_started/run_completed lines and empty for daemon_started/orphan_sweep.

Out of scope: row #3 (Drain by run_id), row #4 (run registry). This is the envelope-field landing only.

title: Daemon workloop wedges idle: does NOT re-evaluate deferred-for-ledger-dep beads when a blocker completes (append/submit also fail to wake it)
notes:
ROOT CAUSE (investigated 2026-05-31, durable anchors confirmed):

THREE distinct gaps, two are root, one is a consequence.

GAP 1 (PRIMARY): No code path ever clears ItemStatusDeferredForLedgerDep back to pending. The deferred status is only ever SET — internal/queue/append.go:151, internal/queue/rpc.go:190 & rpc.go:411. evaluateGroupAdvanceWithOutcome (internal/daemon/workloop.go:2526-2663), the per-run completion handler, ONLY marks the completing item terminal (2554/2556) and calls AdvanceGroup. It NEVER scans the group for deferred-for-ledger-dep items whose Beads blocker just closed to flip them back to pending. So once hk-tigaf.2 was deferred (its only blocker hk-tigaf.1 was open at append time), the completion of hk-tigaf.1 did nothing to it. Confirmed in events.jsonl: run_completed for run 019e806f-a35d-7528 (hk-tigaf.1) is event 019e8070-a420-7c9a — the LAST event; zero workloop dispatch followed.

GAP 2 (would also block even if Gap 1 fixed): evaluateGroupAdvanceWithOutcome persists via lq.SetQueue (the LockedQueueStore variant, workloop.go:2641 / queuestore_hkj808w.go:191-193) which does NOT fire wakeC. Only the top-level QueueStore.SetQueue (queuestore_hkj808w.go:93-105) fires wakeC. So the completing goroutine never signals the idle dispatch loop. workloopIdleWait (workloop.go:2203-2210) only returns on wakeC or ctx-cancel — so even a freshly-un-deferred item would sit until the next external nudge.

GAP 3 (explains why append of dep-free hk-c6grw also did not dispatch): append RPC DOES wake the loop (rpc.go:589 calls top-level qs.SetQueue → fires wakeC, hk-24xn1 fix). The loop woke, but streamEligible (internal/queue/state.go:283-302) HOL-blocks: the head item hk-tigaf.2 is deferred-for-ledger-dep, so streamEligible returns nil (state.go:292-295) and the tail dep-free hk-c6grw is never reached. Loop went straight back to workloopIdleWait. So append's wake is real but useless while a deferred head HOL-blocks the stream.

RE hk-24xn1: DISTINCT. hk-24xn1 (CLOSED) fixed wake-on-submit when a PENDING GROUP needs pending→active. That fix works. hk-nbjht is the deferred-item re-eligibility gap: there is simply no un-defer logic anywhere, and the completion path neither un-defers nor wakes.

PROPOSED FIX:
1. In evaluateGroupAdvanceWithOutcome, after marking the item terminal (and especially after a bead_closed), scan the active group (and the next group) for ItemStatusDeferredForLedgerDep items and re-query the ledger (br show / ready) for each; flip any whose blocks-edges are now all closed back to ItemStatusPending. (Re-use the same Validate/buildDeferredSet ledger check used at submit/append in internal/queue/rpc.go:539 / validation.go.)
2. After that re-evaluation, fire the wake channel so the idle loop re-runs EligibleItems. Cleanest: have evaluateGroupAdvanceWithOutcome (or its caller in the goroutine) call deps.queueStore.SetQueue (top-level, wake-firing) instead of lq.SetQueue, OR add an explicit deps.queueStore.Wake() call after every run completion. Firing wakeC on EVERY run_completed is the minimal correct trigger — the woken loop is cheap (EligibleItems + ledger check) and idempotent.
3. (Separate, lower priority — covers Gap 3 generally) consider QM-035 out-of-order stream dispatch so a deferred head does not HOL-block dep-free tail items; but fixing Gaps 1+2 resolves the chain-stall, which is the untenable case for the hk-tigaf.1-.10 dependency chain.

The minimal change that unblocks the named-queues epic: trigger ledger-dep re-evaluation + wakeC on run_completed/bead_closed. WHICH EVENT: run_completed (it always follows bead_closed and is the natural slot-freed signal).

CONFIDENCE: High. The completion path (workloop.go:2526-2663) is fully read; there is no un-defer code anywhere in internal/queue or internal/daemon (grep-confirmed); lq.SetQueue's no-wake is confirmed in queuestore_hkj808w.go; the HOL-block is confirmed in state.go:292-295; events.jsonl confirms zero post-completion dispatch.
close_reason: Fixed: un-defer ledger-dep items on blocker-close + wake dispatch loop (a4dba1dd); reviewer APPROVE; regression tests fail-on-main/pass-on-branch. Gap3 HOL-block split to hk-cb5ow.

title: CAPTURE-ONLY inventory: emergent tooling & safety patterns (aggregate over days, do NOT formalize/dispatch yet)
description:
Running inventory of ad-hoc tools, watchdogs, and safety/observability patterns that emerged organically while building harmonik. Goal: dont lose them; aggregate over the next several days before deciding what (if anything) to formalize. NOT a spec, NOT for dispatch to the daemon. Both agents (flywheel, named-queues) append as more surface.

== DAEMON / RUN-LIFECYCLE SAFETY ==
- Escape detector (implementer_escaped_worktree): fails a run when an implementer writes into main outside its worktree. Known false-positive when main is transiently dirty from a sibling merge/escape or a scratch file (bug hk-77q8e). Triage rule: check if a sibling merged/escaped in the same ~1s window before blaming the named bead.
- Pasteinject quit-on-commit watchdog (hk-trjef): quit -> 30s grace -> kill -> noChange-subsumed check.
- Post-commit /quit watchdog (hk-5s7tg): recovers when the implementer committed but sess.Wait is stuck.
- Merge auto-skip + per-bead dispatch guard (bead_already_dispatched): skips merge-conflicting beads; blocks double-dispatch across queues.
- Reconciler: re-adopts in-flight runs with original run-ids after a daemon restart (no double-dispatch).
- Restart-backoff / rapid-boot guard: delays daemon restart after rapid boots.

== PROCESS / DAEMON SUPERVISION ==
- External keeper script (/tmp/hk-keeper.sh): auto-revives the daemon if it dies; key-stripped (subscription-billed), --no-auto-pull.
- Supervisor-owned DaemonWatchdog (hk-3w50w, b43f46c7): in-daemon revival actor.

== OBSERVABILITY / MONITORING ==
- harmonik subscribe (typed event stream + server-side heartbeat) + events.jsonl tail: agent-side run/health monitoring.
- Agent-armed Monitors: run completed/failed/stale/merge-conflict; daemon-up heartbeat; file tails (AGENT_COMMS / per-agent outboxes).
- Disk-watch + go clean -cache ops pattern (worktree/build disk pressure; ~5 GiB / 98% = hk-dgwf4 OOM/disk crash threshold).

== COORDINATION / DISPATCH ==
- Pre-screen scouts: check already-landed (git log Refs AND actual code) before dispatch; verify against main, not --all branches (avoids false landed claims).
- Per-agent outbox comms + tails (being formalized as agent-comms hk-uxm0j; capture the v0 shared-file / v1 outbox patterns here).
- Serialize shared-file-conflicting bead families (e.g. SDLC hk-o52fm.* all touch specs/examples/README.md -> dispatch one at a time).

== PROPOSED / IN-FLIGHT (idea capture) ==
- Session-keeper (hk-ekap1): context-threshold handoff->clear->resume (statusLine signal + Stop-hook idle + pasteinject-watchdog timing + PreCompact backstop).

@named-queues: please append your daemon-lane tools/internals — you know the escape-detector, watchdogs, reconciler, and named-queue mechanics best.

comments.author: gb
comments.text: named-queues daemon-lane fold (2026-06-03): TMUX-ORPHAN REAP (launch-stall mitigation, hk-4l7zs root cause). The daemon spawns each implementer in a tmux session harmonik-<12hex>-flywheel and reaps its OWN completed-run sessions, but NOT sessions orphaned by failures/kills/daemon-restarts -- they accumulate across dead daemons (observed 128 spanning 3 days). 120+ sessions make tmux new-session hang -> launches wedge at launch_initiated ~10-30min (the ~35%-of-a-wide-batch stall + false run_stale). SAFETY/OPS PATTERN: diagnose with tmux ls | grep -c harmonik-.*-flywheel; to reap safely, first prove no session postdates the live daemon (ps -o lstart= -p <daemonpid>; the daemon reaps its own completed runs so anything older is a dead-daemon orphan), broadcast via harmonik comms, then tmux ls | grep harmonik-.*-flywheel | cut -d: -f1 | xargs -n1 tmux kill-session -t. PRESERVE harmonik-pi / harmonik (main) / hk-daemon-supervise / kerf. CAVEAT: reaping clears congestion for NEW launches but does NOT un-wedge a launch already stuck before session-creation (that needs the daemon's launch-timeout retry ~30min) -- observed live: a fresh bead launched instantly post-reap while a pre-stuck peer bead stayed wedged. REAL FIX = daemon boot-reap of orphaned sessions not matching an active run (extend boot reconciler hk-5pg37); filed P1 hk-4l7zs.

comments.author: gb
comments.text: TOOLING LESSONS (2026-06-03 named-queues, from the hk-9vp51 P0): (1) MONITORING — ad-hoc grep/python-json parsing of .harmonik/events/events.jsonl filtered by run_id gives FALSE NEGATIVES (run-filtered lines didn't expose event_type to naive parse -> a watcher twice reported 'stall/timeout' on a healthy run). Authoritative live signals: 'harmonik subscribe --json' (daemon NDJSON) or the hk-daemon-supervise tmux pane. Stop hand-rolling event greps. (2) ORPHAN LEAK is per-dispatch: each run leaks ~1-2 dead harmonik-<hash>-flywheel sessions (implementer+reviewer) on completion; hk-9vp51 #4 reaper does NOT cover them (project-hash scoped). Safe-reap = verify pane_dead=1 + predates live daemon, preserve harmonik/-pi/supervise/kerf/secure-dev. (3) DEPLOY-VERIFY discipline that WORKED: a live smoke dispatch caught a P0 that 2 reviewers + unit tests missed — always live-smoke a daemon-code deploy under the real supervisor launch context before declaring done.

title: T6 finding: br show --format json field name mismatch (description vs body)
description: br create accepts --body (or --description) as the CLI flag for the bead body. br show --format json returns the same field as 'description', not 'body'. The CLI create flag and the JSON show output use different names for the same field. This caused a test assertion failure in TestT6_1MBBeadBody and TestT6_UnicodeHeavyBody when checking body round-trip: the test checked items[0].Body but the JSON field is items[0].Description. This inconsistency is minor but can mislead consumers of br show JSON output (e.g., a handler agent calling br show to get the bead body would need to know to use description). Recommendation: either alias body in JSON output or document the field name mapping in br show help.

title: F-T2-01: worktrees not cleaned up after handler failure — accumulate on each retry
description:
## Repro
go test ./internal/daemon/... -run TestT2_WorktreeLeftAfterFailure -v

## Expected
.harmonik/worktrees/<run_id>/ should be removed (or registered for orphan sweep) when ReopenBead is called after handler failure.

## Actual
After twin-fail exits 1 and ReopenBead is called, the worktree directory remains on disk. Each bead retry creates a new worktree; none are cleaned up. Accumulation rate = 1 worktree per failure per retry cycle.

## Code location
internal/daemon/workloop.go lines 227-266 — no cleanup call in either the worktree-creation failure branch or the normal run-failure branch after step 10.

## Note
Already tracked as bead hk-fgdgz (smoke_test.go:26). This exploratory run confirms it with measured accumulation under repeated retry conditions.

title: MVH-FIX-P0: brcli.Adapter does not set cmd.Dir; production binary queries wrong .beads/
description:
Discovered via end-to-end sanity check of /tmp/harmonik built from main: production binary runs daemon_started + orphan_sweep then idles. No run_started ever emitted. Root cause: brcli.Adapter.Run uses exec.CommandContext but never sets cmd.Dir, so every br invocation runs in the daemon's process CWD instead of cfg.ProjectDir.

Smoke test (internal/daemon/smoke_test.go) is GREEN because it wraps `br` with a shell script that injects `--db <ProjectDir>/.beads/beads.db` at every invocation. Production main.go does no such wrapping. Therefore: tests pass but the binary is broken when invoked from any CWD other than the project dir.

Fix:
1. Add `WorkingDir string` field to `brcli.Adapter` struct OR thread ProjectDir into Adapter construction.
2. In `Adapter.Run` (internal/brcli/adapter.go around line 96), set `cmd.Dir = a.workingDir` (or whatever the field is named) BEFORE `cmd.Run`.
3. Wire ProjectDir → Adapter.WorkingDir in the construction site (likely internal/daemon/workloop.go where the Adapter is constructed, or in daemon.Start).
4. Update existing tests that already inject `--db <path>` to still pass (they can; setting cmd.Dir is additive).
5. Add a test asserting cmd.Dir is set when WorkingDir is non-empty.

Acceptance:
- `go build ./...`
- `go test ./...` green.
- Manual end-to-end: build harmonik, run it against a fresh tmp project with a ready bead, observe run_started/run_completed in JSONL, bead status transitions to closed.

Reproducer:
  go build -o /tmp/h ./cmd/harmonik
  D=$(mktemp -d); cd "$D" && br init && br create "test" -p 2
  /tmp/h --project "$D" &
  sleep 5; kill %1; wait
  # Before fix: bead remains "open" in $D/.beads, but ~/work/.beads gets polled instead
  # After fix: bead transitions to closed (or reopen if claude not installed; either way claim attempt is logged)
close_reason: NewWithWorkingDir sets cmd.Dir; workloop wired to cfg.ProjectDir; 3 new tests green

title: kerf: .kerf/ in repo and ~/.kerf/projects/{id}/ on bench drift with no reconciliation tool
description:
The repo's .kerf/ directory contains orphan dirs (claude-hook-bridge, extqueue, recon, workflow-modes) that only partially overlap with the bench's authoritative works at ~/.kerf/projects/gregberns-harmonik/ (bridge-integration on bench but not in .kerf/; recon in .kerf/ but not on bench). No 'kerf doctor' / 'kerf localize --reconcile' mode surfaced to detect or fix the drift. First-time agents have no way to know which side is canonical.

title: Daemon worktree factory: honour base_branch instead of HEAD
description:
Parent: hk-8m91i (epic).

## Current state

`internal/daemon/workloop.go::productionWorktreeFactory` calls `resolveHEAD(projectDir)` and passes the resulting SHA to `workspace.CreateWorktree` as `parentCommit`. There is no per-bead branch selection; whichever branch the operator has checked out at daemon-start (typically `main`) is the implicit base.

## Required change

The worktree factory MUST consult the bead's `base_branch` field (carrier per hk-8m91i decision) and:

1. `git fetch origin <base_branch>` (or local equivalent — open question for the impl bead: do we fetch from a remote or only resolve a local ref?).
2. Resolve `<base_branch>` to a commit SHA (`git rev-parse refs/heads/<base_branch>` or `origin/<base_branch>`).
3. Pass that SHA as `parentCommit` to `workspace.CreateWorktree`.

Default `base_branch = main` when the bead carries no override (preserves current behaviour modulo "main" being explicit instead of "current HEAD").

## Tests to add

- Unit: factory selects HEAD of `base_branch` when bead specifies it; selects `main` when bead does not.
- Integration: create a bead with `base_branch: integration/feature-x`, create that branch in a test repo, run the workloop, assert the resulting task branch's first commit's parent is the tip of `integration/feature-x`.
- Failure case: `base_branch` doesn't exist → fail-fast with a clear error and a reconciliation-routable event.

## Spec touchpoints

- workspace-model.md WM-005 (task branch base derivation).
- workspace-model.md WM-003 (worktree creation — parent_commit derivation now bead-aware).

## Edge cases to think through

- HEAD of `base_branch` advanced after the run claimed the bead but before the worktree was created — race window is small but real. Resolution: snapshot the SHA at claim time, store on the run record, and CreateWorktree uses the snapshot.
- `base_branch` is a remote-tracking ref (`origin/main`) vs a local ref (`main`) — pick one convention; document.

## Dependency

Blocked by hk-8m91i carrier decision (need to know HOW the bead carries `base_branch` before we can read it).
close_reason: parseBranchingSection + resolveParentCommit in branching.go; beadRunOne uses start_from SHA per WM-005b/BI-009b; 14 unit tests all pass

title: harmonik queue set-concurrency N: adjust --max-concurrent at runtime with no daemon restart; lowering drains-down (gate stops new dispatch until running below N, never kills in-flight)
description: User-requested 2026-06-01. Make the daemon concurrency ceiling adjustable live with NO restart. SEMANTICS: maxConcurrent becomes a runtime-mutable value the dispatch gate reads each tick. Raising N lets the gate immediately permit up to N concurrent. Lowering N below the current running count makes the gate STOP dispatching NEW work until running drops below N (natural running-less-than-maxConcurrent behavior); NEVER kill or interrupt in-flight runs. IMPLEMENTATION: (1) internal/daemon/workloop.go: replace the startup-sealed maxConcurrent (Config.MaxConcurrent, EM-051) with an atomic the dispatch gate reads each loop tick. (2) Add a daemon RPC handler for queue set-concurrency n (n>=1) on the existing socket alongside submit/append/status/list/pause/resume/cancel; validate n>=1, update the atomic, return new value. (3) Add CLI verb harmonik queue set-concurrency n in cmd/harmonik queue subcommands that calls the RPC and prints old to new. (4) Surface the current effective ceiling in queue status and list. DONE MEANS: harmonik queue set-concurrency 6 changes the live ceiling with the daemon running (no restart); a test asserts raising lets the gate dispatch more, lowering below running count stops new dispatch without killing in-flight, and n<1 is rejected. ANCHORS: internal/daemon/workloop.go dispatch gate (maxConcurrent EM-051); the queue RPC verb handler + internal/queue/cli; cmd/harmonik queue subcommands. Foundational for hk-ymav1 (the auto-tuner sets this same atomic).

title: DaemonWatchdog revive-cap is LIFETIME not windowed: after 3 total revivals the watchdog gives up permanently -> daemon runs UNPROTECTED for supervisor's life
description: From retro-review of unreviewed b43f46c7 (hk-3w50w). daemon_watchdog.go:77,104: MaxRevives=3 is a monotonic lifetime total, not a windowed rate. A long-lived supervisor that legitimately revives 3 times over days (clean one-off crashes, healthy between) hits the cap and permanently gives up (Run returns err; shim.go:196 only logs, never restarts the watchdog goroutine) -> daemon unprotected thereafter. Fails OPEN. FIX: reset revives=0 once the daemon is confirmed alive on the post-revival probe (windowed crash-loop count, like the daemon's 1h restartBackoffWindow). COMPOUNDED BY restartbackoff.go:73: applyBootBackoff sleeps with the socket unbound, so a legit slow backoff makes the watchdog isDaemonAlive probe fail and burn phantom revive attempts toward the cap.
close_reason: done

title: Spec amendment: WM-005/005b/006/008/019 + BI parse contract for start_from/lands_on
description:
## Scope

Single bead covering the full normative spec amendment for the branching epic (hk-8m91i). One bead by design: WM and BI edits are tightly coupled and must land atomically.

## Changes

### specs/workspace-model.md

1. **WM-005.** Replace "branch is created off the current integration branch (§4.2.WM-006)" with: "branch is created off the commit at the tip of the bead's `start_from` ref per §4.2.WM-005b. Default `start_from` is `main`." Preserve existing language about `run/<run_id>` naming and checkpoint commits.

2. **NEW WM-005b — Per-bead branching refs (`start_from` / `lands_on`).** Define both fields as bead-scoped git refs.
   - `start_from`: ref the worktree branch is cut from at worktree-create time. Default `main`.
   - `lands_on`: ref the task branch's squash-merge (or cherry-pick per WM-019) lands on at run-terminal-success. Default `main`.
   - Resolution chain (highest precedence first): (a) `## Branching` section in the bead body, (b) `.harmonik/branching.yaml` project defaults, (c) daemon default (`main` for both).
   - The two refs MAY differ. Multiple distinct `lands_on` refs MAY be in flight simultaneously across concurrent runs.
   - Resolution happens once at workspace-create time; mid-run rebase of `start_from` is OUT OF SCOPE for this amendment (track as follow-up).

3. **WM-006.** Demote the parent-bead-derived `harmonik/integration/<parent_bead_id_refsafe>` template from "MUST target" to "MAY be used as a `lands_on` value when the project's branching config or the bead body selects it." WM-006a (ref-safe substitution) remains normatively defined but no longer auto-applied. Keep the existing parent-bead-context resolution machinery available for opt-in use.

4. **WM-008.** Retire as a separate operator-policy knob. Small-scope-collapse is now expressed as `lands_on: main` in the bead or project default. Replace the body with a pointer to WM-005b and a migration note: pre-amendment operator policy `merge_target=main` maps to `lands_on: main`.

5. **WM-019.** Add a `landing_strategy` selector with values `squash` (default, preserves existing behaviour) and `cherry-pick` (required when multiple sibling runs share one `lands_on` and need their commits to interleave). Resolution chain identical to WM-005b. The squash path is unchanged. The cherry-pick path: `git cherry-pick <task_branch_tip>` against the `lands_on` ref inside the scratch merge-worktree per WM-019a option (b); conflict detection per WM-020 unchanged; commit-trailer preservation per WM-019 unchanged (cherry-pick preserves trailers natively).

6. **WM-019a, WM-020, WM-024.** Read-through pass to confirm no contradicting language. Update cross-references where needed.

### specs/beads-integration.md

7. **New section under §4.x (place adjacent to existing bead-body parse rules).** Declare the `## Branching` section parse contract:
   - Section heading `## Branching` (exact, case-sensitive) inside the bead body.
   - YAML-fenced or key:value-list body. Allowed keys: `start_from`, `lands_on`, `landing_strategy`. All optional.
   - Parser runs at claim time; unknown keys ignored with a warning event; malformed YAML surfaces as a workspace-create failure (do NOT silently fall back to defaults).
   - Specify the precedence wiring back to WM-005b.

### Other touch-ups

8. Cross-spec coordination: scan specs/event-model.md and specs/handler-contract.md for stale references to "integration branch" semantics; flag (do not fix in this bead) any that need follow-up.

## Out of scope

- Daemon implementation (separate bead hk-oe6zt).
- Config file schema/loader (separate bead hk-zy9s3).
- Landing-strategy selector impl beyond the spec text (separate bead hk-icgp1).
- Mid-run rebase of `start_from`.
- Operator UI / CLI surface for declaring branching config (separate later bead).

## Exit criteria

- WM-005, WM-005b, WM-006, WM-008, WM-019 in `specs/workspace-model.md` reflect the amendment.
- BI parse-contract section landed in `specs/beads-integration.md`.
- `git grep -n "current integration branch"` returns no remaining matches except in retired-text annotations.
- One commit on the worktree branch; commit message references hk-8m91i (epic) and this bead.

## Notes for implementer

- Spec is normative. Match the prose register of surrounding WM sections (see WM-005 / WM-019 / WM-019a for style).
- Do NOT touch code under internal/ — this bead is spec-only.
- Do NOT close the parent epic hk-8m91i; only close this bead.
close_reason: Spec amendment landed in 72224d5: WM-005b adds start_from/lands_on; WM-019b adds landing_strategy selector; BI-009b declares ## Branching parse contract.

title: Ship specs/examples/standard-bead.dot: implement -> commit-gate(build+vet+test+scenario) -
description: FULL: Ship specs/examples/standard-bead.dot: implement -> commit-gate(build+vet+test+scenario) -> independent review -> close, with review APPROVE as the SOLE inbound edge to close; golden parse/validate test asserting that single-inbound-edge invariant | SCOPE: specs/examples/standard-bead.dot (new) + golden test — union of implement-review-fix.dot + green-build-merge-gate.dot, no daemon code change | Source: harmonik-productization-design workflow 2026-06-02. codename:productization; integration-branch P0s gate the work-project deploy; the 3 beads editing mergeRunBranchToMain (target-branch threading, start_from retarget, post-merge build gate) MUST be dispatched SERIALLY.
design:
DESIGN (engine-validated by design agent against workflow.LoadDotWorkflow / dot.Validate / workflow.DecideNextNode — graph parses, validates, and every cascade route resolves).

DELIVERABLE: ship specs/examples/standard-bead.dot + a golden test. NO daemon change in this bead (that's hk-30vlb).

NODES (6): start (non-agentic/noop) · implement (agentic, claude-implementer) · commit_gate (non-agentic, SHELL) · review (agentic, claude-reviewer) · close (non-agentic/noop terminal) · close-needs-attention (non-agentic/noop terminal).

commit_gate tool_command:  go build ./... && go vet ./... && go test ./... && go test -tags=scenario ./...
(D3=v1: run the whole scenario suite, matching the green-build-merge-gate precedent. Affected-package scoping is a v1.1 follow-up.)

EDGES (10, v1 dialect, unconditional fallback listed LAST per dialect):
- start -> implement (unconditional)
- implement -> commit_gate (unconditional)
- commit_gate -> review            [outcome.status=='SUCCESS']
- commit_gate -> implement         [FAIL && deterministic, traversal_cap 3]   (fix-loop)
- commit_gate -> commit_gate       [FAIL && transient, traversal_cap 2]       (timeout retry / self-loop)
- commit_gate -> close-needs-attention   (fallback: canceled / structural)
- review -> close                  [preferred_label=='APPROVE']   (SOLE inbound edge to close)
- review -> implement              [REQUEST_CHANGES, traversal_cap 3]
- review -> close-needs-attention  [BLOCK]
- review -> close-needs-attention  (unconditional fallback)

D1=A: single shell commit_gate node (NOT separate build/test/scenario nodes).

GOLDEN TEST: assert (a) the single-inbound-to-close invariant (only review->close[APPROVE] terminates at close) and (b) the cascade routes resolve as above (SUCCESS->review, deterministic-fail->implement, transient-fail->self-loop, BLOCK->close-needs-attention).

REFERENCE for exact v1 syntax: specs/examples/*.dot + specs/examples/authoring-notes.md. Engine facts confirmed: dot.Parse takes a string; traversal_cap is retained by the parser (caps fire); dispatchDotToolNode (internal/daemon/dot_cascade.go:747-782) already maps exit->outcome (timeout->transient, genuine RED->deterministic, no blanket reopen).

title: Parallelism prep: br --claim is not atomically exclusive — needs harmonik-side guard or br-level fix
description:
## Finding

`br --claim` (br v0.1.45) does NOT enforce exclusive claim at the application level. Two concurrent `br --db <path> update <id> --claim` invocations both return exit 0 even when the bead is already `in_progress`.

Surfaced by characterisation test `internal/daemon/t5_realdb_concurrent_test.go` (sentinel `FINDING_DOUBLE_DISPATCH`). Test passes regardless of which outcome occurs — it documents observed behaviour.

## MVH posture

Safe today: MVH ships a single work loop per daemon, so contended claims cannot occur.

## Why this becomes a problem post-MVH

Once concurrent runs land (foreground binary with concurrent runs is the first post-MVH unlock per memory `project_harmonik_mvh_daemon_deferral`), two daemon goroutines could each see a bead as available, both call `br update --claim`, both get exit 0, and dispatch the same bead twice — double-dispatch.

## Options

1. **Harmonik-side guard.** Before dispatch, read bead status; only dispatch if `status==open`. Cheap, lives entirely in harmonik. Still has a TOCTOU window but small.
2. **br-level atomicity.** Patch br to make `--claim` a true compare-and-set (SQL `WHERE status='open'` returning row count). Right fix; requires upstream PR.
3. **Both.** Belt-and-suspenders; harmonik guard now, upstream patch when scheduled.

## Acceptance

- Decision on option (1) vs (2) vs (3) recorded in spec / handoff.
- If (1): guard implemented + test asserting double-claim is rejected at harmonik layer.
- If (2): upstream PR landed and version bumped; harmonik test re-run shows only one `--claim` succeeds.
- `FINDING_DOUBLE_DISPATCH` sentinel either removed or downgraded once fixed.

## Pointers

- `internal/daemon/t5_realdb_concurrent_test.go` — the characterisation test
- v33 HANDOFF.md — original finding context
- memory `project_harmonik_mvh_daemon_deferral` — why concurrent runs are the first post-MVH unlock
notes:
## Decision (orchestrator, 2026-05-12)

**Option 1 — harmonik-side guard.** Implementer should:

1. In `internal/daemon/workloop.go`, before calling `brAdapter.ClaimBead`, read the bead status via `brAdapter.Show` (or equivalent) and confirm `status == "open"`. Only proceed with the claim if so.
2. Surface a `bead_claim_skipped` log line (or existing equivalent) when the pre-check fails — this is the visible signal that a competing claim won.
3. Acknowledged TOCTOU window: this is not a true atomic compare-and-set. Acceptable at MaxConcurrent>1 because the work loop's claim semaphore (row 9, `hk-e61c3.3`) already serializes claims on this daemon to N at a time. Cross-daemon (post-MVH multi-daemon) is out of scope here — that's where the upstream `br` patch (deferred option 2) would land.
4. Test: extend `internal/daemon/t5_realdb_concurrent_test.go` so the `FINDING_DOUBLE_DISPATCH` sentinel is REMOVED — after the guard, only one of the two concurrent dispatches should succeed; the other observes the pre-check and skips.

## Out of scope
- Option 2 (upstream `br` atomic claim) — file a separate parallelism-prep bead if/when we decide to engage upstream.
- Option 3 (belt-and-suspenders) — revisit after measuring whether option 1 alone is sufficient under N=10.

## Dependencies
This bead touches `internal/daemon/workloop.go`. It is held until row 5 (`hk-e61c3.2`) merges to avoid workloop conflict. After that, this is the next dispatch.
close_reason: Option 1 guard implemented: ShowBead pre-claim check in beadLedger interface; FINDING_DOUBLE_DISPATCH sentinel removed; T5 test asserts exactly 1 run_started; commit c2ce491

title: test-blocked-bead-for-rnsjs
Review-loop iter-2 STILL no-progress-fails after 7x7ea: feedback file written but resumed implementer never acts on it (unchanged diff)
description:
CONFIRMED ROOT CAUSE (investigator 2026-05-29): paste-submit race in internal/daemon/pasteinject.go pasteInjectImplementerResume (~lines 686-736). On iter-2 resume the daemon pastes TWO messages — (1) the task ('read agent-task.md', SendEnter ~line 707) and (2) the reviewer feedback (WriteLastPane ~line 727 + Enter ~line 732) — back-to-back with NO synchronization. The second Enter is sent while Claude is still processing the first message and has NOT returned to the REPL input prompt, so the feedback message is buffered/dropped/dequeued without starting a new user turn. The resumed implementer never sees the feedback -> reproduces the identical diff -> reviewloop.go no-progress detector (~lines 640-649) fires 'no-progress detected at iteration 2: diff hash unchanged' -> run_failed.

REPRO: wave-8 hk-a8bg.18 (run 019e7305-7241) + hk-a8bg.35 (run 019e7305-78ab) both failed this way WITH reviewer-feedback.iter-1.md present in their worktrees (so hk-7x7ea's file-write is fine; the gap is delivery).

FIX (pick one, add a regression test): (a) after the task-message Enter, WAIT for Claude to return to input-ready (poll for a heartbeat/activity-idle signal, or as a minimum a bounded time.Sleep) BEFORE the feedback WriteLastPane; OR (b) combine task+feedback into a SINGLE paste buffer (double-newline separated) submitted with one Enter, eliminating the inter-message race. Prefer (b) if the resumed implementer can consume one combined prompt. Add a test asserting both messages reach the resumed session as distinct readable content. Companion to hk-7x7ea (file-write, fixed) + hk-lckbv (buffer-name, fixed).
close_reason: done

title: MVH-R8: ClaudeCodeAdapter implementing handlercontract.Adapter
description:
MVH_ROADMAP row #8. Concrete adapter so the daemon knows how to detect Claude-Code-specific runtime conditions on the child subprocess.

Location: NEW file `internal/handler/adapter_claudecode.go` + test, same package as session.go/handler.go.

Implement the `handlercontract.Adapter` interface (read `internal/handler-contract/adapterregistry_hc012.go` and any sibling adapter contract files to learn the EXACT method set — the bead summary may lag the spec). At minimum per MVH_ROADMAP row #8:

- DetectReady(line []byte) bool — returns true when the stdout NDJSON line indicates the child has reached "ready to accept input" state. For Claude Code, this is the event line carrying `type: "ready"` or equivalent — read internal/handlercontract/watcher_hc011.go to see what events the watcher already parses and key off that.
- DetectRateLimit(line []byte) bool — true on Claude Code's rate-limit signal (HTTP 429 surfaced via the headless protocol).
- CleanExitSequence() []string — the lines to send on stdin to gracefully exit (e.g. ["/exit"] or "" + EOF).
- RotateAccount(ctx context.Context, current AccountID) (AccountID, error) — for MVH return an error like `ErrSingleAccountOnly` since rotation is post-MVH; the interface stub exists so callers compile.

Registration: expose `NewClaudeCodeAdapter() handlercontract.Adapter` and a `Register(reg *handlercontract.AdapterRegistry)` helper. DO NOT auto-register from `init()` — explicit registration only, called from `daemon.Start` later.

Tests: feed canned NDJSON lines and assert DetectReady/DetectRateLimit return correct booleans. The clean exit sequence and RotateAccount can have simple table tests.

If the actual `Adapter` interface has methods beyond the four above (likely), implement them all consistent with `handlercontract` godoc. If the interface as specified is broader than what MVH needs, fill in minimal stubs that return well-typed zero values and a TODO godoc.

Out of scope: actually wiring the adapter into `daemon.Start` — that lands when row #10 (work loop) is dispatched.
close_reason: ClaudeCodeAdapter + Register + tests in internal/handler/adapter_claudecode.go; all 4 Adapter methods implemented, tests green

title: Build/test scaffolding meta-epic — local Makefile, golangci, lefthook, coverage tooling
description:
Meta-epic tracking the local build/test scaffolding that the bootstrap subset assumes but that does not yet exist on disk. Per Phase 1 readiness analysis (`docs/foundation/phase-1-readiness-gap-analysis.md` §B2 — BLOCKER), there is no `Makefile`, `go.mod`, `.golangci.yml`, `lefthook.yml`, coverage tooling, or test-helper scaffold in the repo. Per locked decisions in `docs/foundation/project-level/build-practices.md` and `quality-checks.md`, the project commits to: (a) three-tier `make check-fast` / `check` / `check-full` gauntlet runnable identically locally and (post-MVH) in CI; (b) golangci-lint v2 with the \"depguard\" component matrix from `subsystem-organization.md`; (c) 95%-core / 90%-floor / <0.3% regression coverage gate; (d) agent-reviewer on every non-trivial commit, invoked LOCALLY (this children of this epic file beads for the local invocation only — no GitHub Actions, no CircleCI, no remote runners per user 2026-05-05 clarification). Goal: a developer (human or agent) can `git clone harmonik && make check` and have the checks run quickly and easily.

Children file the discrete artifacts: `go.mod` initialization, `Makefile` with the three-tier targets, `.golangci.yml` with depguard, `lefthook.yml` for the local pre-commit hook, the scenario-harness test wrapper scaffold, the coverage-gate script, and a `BUILDING.md` developer-onboarding doc. Implementation comes after the parent meta-epic loads; this pass is task-filing only.
close_reason: Decomposed into 11 child beads (hk-pvcs.1–hk-pvcs.11), all closed. Children cover: go.mod, Makefile three-tier targets, .golangci.yml with depguard, lefthook.yml, coverage-gate script, test-helper scaffold, forbid-import tool, BUILDING.md, post-pvcs.4 doc cleanup, agent-review timeout, and BIINV002 surface2 fix.

title: Initialize go.mod + initial go.sum + extend .gitignore
description:
Initialize the Go module that everything else depends on.

What:
- `go.mod` at repo root pinning `module github.com/gregberns/harmonik` (per `quality-checks.md` §Imports gci-prefix; `subsystem-organization.md` §Go module layout).
- `go 1.25` directive + `toolchain go1.25.x` per `quality-checks.md` §Decisions.
- An initial `go.sum` (empty until first dep is added).
- `.gitignore` extension covering `/bin/`, `/dist/`, `coverage.out`, `*.test`, `.harmonik/` (already excluded), `/.tools/`. Per `build-practices.md` §Git hygiene.

Why:
Every subsequent build/test target depends on Go module discovery. Without `go.mod` no test can run, no lint can resolve imports, no `make build` can succeed.

Open question (flag for follow-up):
Should the repo be a single-module or use a `go.work` multi-module layout? `subsystem-organization.md` describes a flat `internal/` tree which suggests single-module is sufficient; multi-module is only useful if separate semver lines are needed. Default to single; revisit only if a real split appears.
notes: Implemented and merged via worktree pilot 2026-05-06. Commit bc0688a on main. go.mod (module github.com/gregberns/harmonik, go 1.25, toolchain go1.25), empty go.sum, .gitignore extended per build-practices.md §Git hygiene. go mod verify passes (with GOTOOLCHAIN=local on Go > 1.25 hosts). Review surfaced one toolchain-naming correctness issue (go1.25.0 → go1.25) and two .gitignore consistency fixes; all addressed before merge.

title: Enforce timeout on make agent-review pre-commit target
description:
The lefthook.yml pre-commit hook runs 'make agent-review' which invokes the agent-reviewer skill (an LLM call). Without a hard wall-clock timeout, an unbounded LLM call would violate the cheap-hook invariant (Tier 1 <15s budget) and tempt --no-verify usage.

Acceptance criteria:
1. The 'make agent-review' target (Makefile) wraps the skill invocation with a hard timeout (recommended: 60s via 'timeout 60 ...' or equivalent).
2. When the timeout fires, the target exits non-zero with a message like: 'agent-review: timed out after 60s; retry manually or add Trivial: true for trivial commits.'
3. Timeout value is configurable via a Makefile variable (e.g., AGENT_REVIEW_TIMEOUT, default 60).
4. quality-checks.md §Three-tier identical gauntlet already documents this requirement (landed in hk-pvcs.9).

Refs: hk-pvcs.9 (documented the timeout requirement); lefthook.yml agent-review pre-commit command.
close_reason: Implemented. Added AGENT_REVIEW_TIMEOUT variable (default 60, operator-tunable) and wrapped skill invocation with 'timeout $(AGENT_REVIEW_TIMEOUT)'. Exit 124 emits actionable message and exits non-zero. Commit 5ae4900 on worktree-agent-bi-2.

Likely cause: the checkpoint-trailer Bead-ID parsing path canonicalises differently from the BI surface, but this needs verification by reading the test and the parser.
