title: Author Makefile with three-tier check targets + build/test/lint/agent-review helpers
description:
Author the repo-root `Makefile` with the three-tier check gauntlet plus build/test/lint helpers.

Required targets (per `docs/foundation/project-level/quality-checks.md` §Three-tier identical gauntlet):
- `make build` — `go build ./...` (no-op until first package lands, but the target must exist).
- `make test` — `go test ./...` (no race; quick smoke).
- `make check-fast` — Tier 1 (<15s target): `gofumpt -l -d` + `gci diff` + `go vet` + `go build` + `golangci-lint run --new-from-rev=HEAD~1` + `go test -short` on changed packages.
- `make check` — Tier 2 (~3-5 min): full `golangci-lint run` + `go test -race -count=1 ./...` + `go mod tidy` diff check + coverage gate + `govulncheck`.
- `make check-full` — Tier 3 (~10-15 min): everything in check + `go test -race -tags=integration` + `go test -race -tags=scenario` + `go test -tags=crash` (fast subset).
- `make lint` — `golangci-lint run` shorthand.
- `make agent-review` — invokes the local `agent-reviewer` skill against the diff vs. last commit. Runs LOCALLY (per 2026-05-05 user clarification — NOT CI). Stub if the skill doesn't exist yet (skill bead is filed separately under the operational-skills meta-epic per phase-1-readiness-gap-analysis §A4).
- `make tools` — installs pinned dev tools (`gofumpt`, `gci`, `golangci-lint`, `govulncheck`, `lefthook`) into `./.tools/` to avoid global pollution.

Constraints:
- Local/CI parity (per `quality-checks.md` §invariant): every CI gate is the same make target. Since user has explicitly excluded CI for MVH, this just means \"local is the single source of truth.\" If CI lands later, it will invoke these same targets verbatim.
- Fast and easy is the user's bar: a fresh clone + `make tools && make check` should Just Work.

Depends on `hk-pvcs.1` (go.mod must exist for any go tool invocation).
close_reason: Merged at HEAD (post-rebase onto main with pvcs.3 already in). Round-2 CLEAN review after fix-agent addressed: (1) BLOCKER go mod tidy restore-via-cp pattern; (2) MAJOR check-fast scoped to changed Go packages with no-op fallback; (3) MAJOR forbid-import graceful-skip stub in Tier 2 mirroring coverage-gate.sh. All eight required targets present. Tool versions pinned in make tools.

title: Author .golangci.yml with depguard component matrix + LLM-SDK ban (PL-INV-002)
description:
Author `.golangci.yml` v2 schema with the linter set + depguard component matrix per `docs/foundation/project-level/quality-checks.md` §Linter meta-runner.

Content baseline (verbatim from quality-checks.md §Linter meta-runner code block):
- `version: \"2\"` (March 2025 GA schema).
- `run.timeout: 5m`, `run.go: \"1.25\"`.
- `linters.disable-all: true` then explicit enable list: errcheck, govet, staticcheck, ineffassign, unused, gosimple, errorlint, nilerr, copyloopvar, testifylint, errchkjson, exhaustive, bodyclose, rowserrcheck, sqlclosecheck, contextcheck, noctx, containedctx, fatcontext, gocritic, revive, misspell, unparam, unconvert, prealloc, nakedret, nolintlint, gosec, forbidigo, depguard.
- linters-settings per the spec block (revive rules; gocritic tags; nolintlint require-explanation; forbidigo banning fmt.Print + bare panic; testifylint enable-all).
- `depguard.rules` component matrix: at minimum a `core` rule denying `internal/*` imports (stub from spec). Full matrix per `subsystem-organization.md` §Dependency layering enforcement — but at MVH only the few packages that exist need rules; rest can be added as packages land.

Critical constraint (cite `docs/foundation/project-level/quality-checks.md` §depguard \"⚑ assumption\"): NO `go-arch-lint`. The component-graph rules live in depguard alone.

Critical constraint per PL-INV-002 (process-lifecycle invariant — see `specs/process-lifecycle.md`): the depguard matrix MUST forbid LLM-SDK imports (e.g., `github.com/anthropics/*`, `github.com/openai/*`) anywhere in the daemon's transitive closure. Encode this as a denylist on the daemon-cmd component when that package lands.

Depends on `hk-pvcs.1` (go.mod) and `hk-pvcs.2` (Makefile, since lint runs via `make lint`).
close_reason: Merged at 7462e99 (origin/main). CLEAN review: linter enable list + settings block + depguard core + LLM-SDK ban (global scope at MVH; comment directs narrowing to cmd/ + internal/daemon/ when those land); subsystem rules present-but-commented per spec; no go-arch-lint.

title: Author lefthook.yml wiring local pre-commit + pre-push to make targets
description:
Author `lefthook.yml` at repo root wiring local git hooks to the make targets. Runs LOCALLY only — NOT a CI workflow (per 2026-05-05 user clarification).

Required hooks (per `docs/foundation/project-level/quality-checks.md` §Three-tier identical gauntlet + `build-practices.md` §Agent review on every commit):
- `pre-commit`: `make check-fast` (~15s) on staged files. Blocks commit on failure.
- `pre-commit` (second step): `make agent-review` (skill stub OK if the skill bead hasn't landed yet — see `p1-skill-agent-reviewer` from phase-1-readiness-gap-analysis §A4). Required per `build-practices.md` decision \"agent reviewer on every commit\" — but NOT a CI gate; the hook is the enforcement point.
- `pre-push`: `make check` (~3-5 min Tier 2). Catches what pre-commit's delta scope misses.
- `commit-msg`: validates Conventional Commits format + required trailers (`Reviewed-By:`, `Review-Verdict:`) on non-trivial commits per `build-practices.md` §Commit conventions. JSON trailer schema-validation lives here too.

Choice rationale: lefthook is Go-native, single binary, no Python/Node dep — locked decision per `quality-checks.md` §Decisions.

Constraint per `build-practices.md` §Git hygiene: `--no-verify` is forbidden. The hook MUST be cheap to keep that policy realistic.

Depends on `hk-pvcs.2` (Makefile targets exist).
close_reason: Merged at HEAD (post-rebase onto main with pvcs.{2,3} in). Reviewer flagged 2 MEDIUMs that orchestrator judged out-of-scope vs. authoritative bead acceptance: (a) Trivial: true bypass keyword undocumented in build-practices.md — implementer reasonably filled doc-silent gap; (b) agent-review timeout unenforced — acceptable while skill is stubbed (hk-jhob.1's concern). Both deferred to hk-pvcs.9 along with .lefthook.yml typo MINOR. lefthook.yml + scripts/validate-commit-msg.sh meet bead acceptance: pre-commit→check-fast+agent-review, pre-push→check, commit-msg validates Conventional Commits + Reviewed-By/Review-Verdict trailers, jq+python3 fallback, LOCAL-only.

title: Author coverage-gate script enforcing 95%-core / 90%-floor / <0.3% regression
description:
Author `scripts/coverage-gate.sh` (or equivalent small Go program at `tools/coverage-gate/main.go` if shell becomes unwieldy) implementing the locked coverage gate per STATUS.md \"Decisions in force\".

Targets (all locked):
- 95% line coverage on `internal/core/**` (the deterministic skeleton — must be tested heavily).
- 90% floor on every other `internal/**` package (no package may regress below this).
- <0.3% regression gate vs. `coverage.baseline` checked into the repo. Bumping the baseline requires a \"protected rule file\" commit per `quality-checks.md` §Agent-enforceability.

Inputs: `go test -coverprofile=coverage.out -covermode=atomic ./...`. Tool reads the profile, applies the rules above, exits non-zero with a per-package report on failure.

Wiring: invoked from `make check` (Tier 2) per `docs/foundation/project-level/quality-checks.md` §Tier 2.

Open question (cite `quality-checks.md` §Deferred): the doc says \"coverage thresholds — wait until testing methodology settles.\" STATUS.md and the user reframe both lock the targets. If the implementer hits a real gap between settled-methodology and locked-targets, file a follow-up spec-edit bead — do NOT silently relax the gate.

Depends on `hk-pvcs.2` (Makefile target hookup).
close_reason: Merged at HEAD. Round 2 implicit-CLEAN after orchestrator inline-amend of MEDIUM (one-line off-by-one on regression boundary: spec '<0.3%' requires fire at >=0.3%, not strict >). scripts/coverage-gate.sh + coverage.baseline; vacuous-pass on empty repo; per-package failure report; wired via existing make check stub.

title: Author test-helper scaffold (internal/testhelpers + test/{scenario,integration,crash} build-tagged stubs)
description:
Author the test-helper scaffold packages so tests can be authored consistently from the first cluster A bead onward.

Scope (intentionally small at MVH — fast and easy):
- `internal/testhelpers/` package with the bare minima: deterministic clock fixture, temp-dir + cleanup helper, `t.Helper()` wrappers for common assertions, a `testenv` helper that builds a sandbox `.harmonik/` directory layout (per PL §4.1).
- `test/scenario/` directory placeholder with a build-tag stub (`//go:build scenario`) and a one-line README pointer to `specs/scenario-harness.md` — wires up the `-tags=scenario` invocation referenced by `make check-full`.
- `test/integration/` directory placeholder with the `//go:build integration` tag.
- `test/crash/` directory placeholder with the `//go:build crash` tag.

Explicitly OUT of scope here:
- Full scenario-harness implementation. That is owned by SH spec beads (`hk-i0tw.*`) and the SH-pilot load (per phase-1-readiness-gap-analysis §A1). This bead is just the directory + tag scaffold so `make check-full` doesn't fail on missing build-tagged paths.
- Twin-binary test fixtures (those will land with the twin-binary scaffold beads under HC).

Why now: test code lands as soon as the first impl bead does. Tests need a place to live and a helper-package shape that's already been thought about — without this, every cluster will reinvent its own helpers and the eventual SH integration becomes a refactor.

Depends on `hk-pvcs.1` (go.mod) and `hk-pvcs.2` (Makefile targets reference these directories).
close_reason: Merged at HEAD. CLEAN review with one MINOR (testenv.go:47 cites PL-004 but .harmonik/worktrees/ is actually a PL-006/WM directory — citation imprecise, no functional defect). internal/testhelpers/{clock,tempdir,assert,testenv}.go + test/{scenario,integration,crash}/ build-tagged stubs + go.mod toolchain strip (tidy-required).

title: Author forbid-import tool — third-party allowlist enforcement (Tier 2 check)
description:
Author `tools/forbid-import/main.go` — a small Go program that runs as part of `make check` (Tier 2) to enforce a curated import allowlist beyond what depguard catches.

Why both depguard AND a custom tool: depguard handles component-graph rules (which package can import which other internal package). The forbid-import tool handles the **transitive** import graph against a hard allowlist of acceptable third-party deps — caught a class of supply-chain footguns that depguard's per-file scoping misses. Per `quality-checks.md` §Agent-enforceability item 5 (\"protected rule files\") — this tool's allowlist IS one of the protected rule files.

Initial allowlist (small at MVH):
- standard library (always allowed).
- `github.com/gregberns/harmonik/...` (self).

title: Doc cleanup: surface Trivial: true bypass + lefthook agent-review timeout + .lefthook.yml typo (post-pvcs.4 review)
description:
Three doc cleanups surfaced by the hk-pvcs.4 + hk-pvcs.7 reviews. All four are spec/doc concerns, not implementer defects — the implementations were merged as-is.

1. **Document `Trivial: true` bypass trailer in build-practices.md §Commit conventions.** The hk-pvcs.4 implementer needed an operationalizable trivial-vs-non-trivial distinction; the spec says trivial commits 'MAY omit' trailers but defines no bypass mechanism. Implementer chose `Trivial: true` as the explicit opt-out trailer (validated by scripts/validate-commit-msg.sh). Bless this in build-practices.md so it's discoverable.

2. **Define agent-review timeout strategy.** The lefthook.yml pre-commit `make agent-review` step is currently safe because the skill is stubbed (Makefile target exits 0). When hk-jhob.1 (agent-reviewer skill) lands, an unbounded LLM call would violate the cheap-hook invariant and tempt --no-verify. Add timeout enforcement to hk-jhob.1's acceptance criteria.

3. **Fix `.lefthook.yml` → `lefthook.yml` typo in quality-checks.md §Agent-enforceability.** The doc lists `.lefthook.yml` (with leading dot) in protected rule files; lefthook's own convention and the hk-pvcs.4 bead body specify the un-dotted form. One-line fix.

4. **Fix `tools/go-linters/forbid-import` → `tools/forbid-import` in quality-checks.md.** The doc references the old path at lines 136 and 170 (§Tier 2 + §Agent-enforceability item 5). The hk-pvcs.7 bead body and Makefile are now aligned on `tools/forbid-import/` (Go program at `./tools/forbid-import`, invoked via `go run`). Bring quality-checks.md into alignment.

Refs: hk-pvcs.4 + hk-pvcs.7 reviews (orchestrator-merged with these as deferred follow-ups, not blockers).
close_reason: Landed (P3 doc cleanup): 4 sub-items addressed in 2 docs. (1) Trivial: true bypass trailer documented in build-practices.md §Commit conventions, matching scripts/validate-commit-msg.sh §6 grep-^Trivial: true$ semantics. (2) Agent-review timeout requirement documented in quality-checks.md §Three-tier identical gauntlet; follow-up bead hk-pvcs.10 filed for the actionable Makefile work (judgment call: closed bead hk-jhob.1's acceptance criteria are immutable). (3) .lefthook.yml → lefthook.yml typo fixed. (4) tools/go-linters/forbid-import → tools/forbid-import path fixed at both occurrences. Reviewer APPROVE — no findings.

title: queue-append: no in-memory mutation visible to running workloop (append-while-running broken)
description: Append-while-running cannot work as designed: HandlerAdapter.HandleQueueAppend (rpc.go:478-494) loads queue.json from disk via HandleQueueAppend (rpc.go:229), mutates that in-memory copy via AppendItems, but the adapter then discards the mutated *Queue, never calls queue.Persist (rpc.go:484 discards return value), and never writes back to QueueStore. The workloop reads exclusively from deps.queueStore.Queue() (workloop.go:457-458, 519, 1424), so even if Persist were called, the running workloop would not see the newly-appended items until it re-loads — which it never does. Net effect: append succeeds at the socket layer (no error), but the appended items never get dispatched in the current daemon session. This is the 'append-while-running test gap' flagged in plans/003_extqueue/_plan.md:23. Fix: same surgery as the submit gap — HandlerAdapter must hold *QueueStore and use LockForMutation around AppendItems so the running workloop sees the mutation atomically; then Persist; then emit events.
close_reason: Fixed in 40eeb9b: HandleQueueAppend now mirrors submit pattern — LockForMutation -> AppendItems -> Persist -> SetQueue. New AppendUpdatesQueueStore test.

title: Replace 5-line README.md with human-facing what-is/why/install/quickstart (~120-180 lines)
description: FULL: Replace 5-line README.md with human-facing what-is/why/install/quickstart (~120-180 lines) INCLUDING the mandatory 'daemon merges+pushes to origin/main' safety banner verbatim; link AGENT_OPERATING_MANUAL.md + AGENT_INDEX.md | SCOPE: /Users/gb/github/harmonik/README.md — closes hand-a-link dead-end | Source: harmonik-productization-design workflow 2026-06-02. codename:productization; integration-branch P0s gate the work-project deploy; the 3 beads editing mergeRunBranchToMain (target-branch threading, start_from retarget, post-merge build gate) MUST be dispatched SERIALLY.
design:
IMPLEMENTER CONTEXT (orchestrator-injected 2026-06-03):

Rewrite /Users/gb/github/harmonik/README.md (currently ~5 lines) into a 120-180 line human-facing doc: what-is / why / install / quickstart. Audience = someone handed a link who must stand harmonik up on their own repo.

INSTALL section — pinned commands:
- harmonik: `go install ./cmd/harmonik` from the cloned repo root (path cmd/harmonik/ confirmed to exist).
- br (the beads_rust task ledger, REQUIRED): a Rust CLI at https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/beads_rust. Document install as `cargo install --git https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/beads_rust` (installed binary is `br`). NOTE: this exact invocation is UNVERIFIED on a clean machine — present it as the install path, do NOT claim it is tested.
- kerf (OPTIONAL — the planning/prioritization layer, NOT required for the core daemon loop): internal sibling tool (source ~/github/kerf, built with `go install`). The basic submit->dispatch->merge loop runs WITHOUT kerf via `harmonik queue submit`. Document kerf as optional/advanced and link docs/components/internal/kerf.md. Do NOT make the quickstart depend on kerf.

QUICKSTART (minimal happy path): clone -> `go install ./cmd/harmonik` -> `harmonik init` (bootstrap) -> start the daemon (`harmonik --project <dir> --no-auto-pull --max-concurrent 4` inside a detached tmux session) -> `harmonik queue submit <batch.json>`. Cross-reference AGENTS.md for the full daily loop.

SAFETY BANNER (MANDATORY, near top, blockquoted): the EXISTING banner is now STALE — it claims integration-branch targeting is "not yet implemented." That is WRONG as of 2026-06-03: the integration-branch P0 gate LANDED (codename:productization beads hk-6r6xv/hk-mkxw1/hk-sul12/hk-eun55 + config/branching.yaml `protect_branches` + `--target-branch`/`--protect-branch`/`--forbid-default-main` flags), enforced fail-closed at boot, dispatch, and in-merge. REWRITE the banner to the current truth, roughly:
  "SAFETY — read before pointing the daemon at any repo. By DEFAULT the daemon merges and pushes to origin/main on every successful bead. To target an integration branch and protect main, configure config/branching.yaml protect_branches (or pass --target-branch/--protect-branch/--forbid-default-main); the daemon then fail-closes and refuses to push main. Until you configure that, do NOT run against a work repo or any branch that must not be auto-committed; personal/throwaway repos where auto-pushing main is acceptable are fine."

LINKS: link AGENT_OPERATING_MANUAL.md (authored in parallel under hk-3nabd; forward-reference is fine) and AGENT_INDEX.md.

SCOPE: only README.md.

title: harmonik run ignores SIGINT and SIGTERM when daemon dispatch is wedged — only SIGKILL works
description: Repro 2026-05-20 ~05:30 UTC. After daemon stalled mid-dispatch (see sibling bead — hung post run_started with no claude spawn), 'kill -INT 24377' had NO effect, 'kill -TERM 24377' had NO effect, only 'kill -9' (SIGKILL) terminated the process. Expected per hk-ppt32 fix landed 2026-05-19 (5e651c2): SIGINT/timeout should drain queue to 'cancelled' and rename queue.json. Actual: signal handler appears not to fire when the main dispatch goroutine is wedged. queue.json was NOT renamed — orchestrator had to mv manually before retrying. This escalates hk-8jh26 (harmonik run: hang on bead failure + always exits 0 + silent queue overwrite). Hypothesis: signal handler not goroutine-isolated from the wedged dispatch loop, OR cancel context is awaited on the same channel that's deadlocked. Fix should: (1) signal handling runs in its own goroutine independent of dispatch state, (2) cancel triggers an unconditional process-exit timer (e.g., 5s grace then os.Exit), (3) the queue.json rename + transition-to-cancelled paths must be reachable even when individual handlers are wedged.
close_reason: Subsumed: landed as 625efcf

title: MVH-R5: Unix socket listener in internal/daemon
description:
MVH_ROADMAP row #5. The daemon needs to accept connections from agent subprocesses to receive their outcome reports and serve claim-next requests.

Location: NEW file `internal/daemon/socket.go` + test.

Contract: `RunSocketListener(ctx context.Context, sockPath string, h RequestHandler) error` — binds a Unix-domain socket, accepts connections, dispatches each request to `h`, returns when ctx is cancelled.

Request shape (JSON over the socket, one request per connection for now — keep it simple):
{"op":"emit-outcome","run_id":"<id>","bead_id":"<id>","outcome":{...}}
{"op":"claim-next","role":"<role>"}

Response: {"ok":true,"result":{...}} or {"ok":false,"error":"<msg>"}.

`RequestHandler` is an interface with `EmitOutcome(ctx, req) (resp, error)` and `ClaimNext(ctx, role) (bead, error)`. The daemon will register a concrete handler in `Start` (a follow-up bead — DO NOT wire here, just expose the listener).

PROTOCOL NOTE: `net.Listen` is banned by lint — use `(&net.ListenConfig{}).Listen(ctx, "unix", sockPath)`. Set socket permissions to 0700 (`os.Chmod` after Listen succeeds). Remove a stale socket file at sockPath before Listen if present.

Tests: spin up the listener on a temp dir socket path, dial it with `(&net.Dialer{}).DialContext`, send each op, assert request reaches the handler and response shape is correct. Use a stub `RequestHandler` for the test.

Out of scope: the concrete handler that talks to bus/brcli — separate bead after this lands. The wire-into-`Start` step also separate.
close_reason: RunSocketListener + RequestHandler in internal/daemon/socket.go; 7 tests green

title: EV-039: heartbeat carries last_event_id + active_runs for gap detection
Implement `harmonik supervise` CLI surface (start/stop/status/attach/restart/logs)
description:
The harmonik supervise [start|stop|status|attach|restart|logs] subcommand family per PL-028d. start acquires flock at .harmonik/cognition/supervisor.lock, writes pid + sentinel (PL-006d) + config.json (atomic per WM-026), launches Pi extension in a tmux pane named harmonik-<project_hash>-flywheel with `remain-on-exit on`. --watch-restart invokes internal/supervise.Supervisor (B3).

Scope:
- cmd/harmonik/supervise/{start,stop,status,attach,restart,logs}.go.
- config.json schema fields (schema_version, model, token_cap, max_concurrent, budget_cap_usd_per_day, instructions_path, priority_source, areas, epic, debounce_ms, watchdog_ms, warn_threshold, force_threshold, restart_policy, restart_max, restart_base_ms, restart_cap_ms, started_at, daemon_instance_id).
- Pre-flag.Parse dispatch in cmd/harmonik/main.go (after the queue case).
- stop follows PL-011 SIGTERM→bounded-wait→SIGKILL.
- status is file-surface only (no daemon socket).
- attach execve tmux attach-session.
- restart re-reads config.json.
- logs is tmux capture-pane -p -S -<n>.

Acceptance:
- harmonik supervise start --watch-restart launches a tmux pane with respawn-on-crash via internal/supervise.
- harmonik supervise stop cleanly terminates shim + child; flock released; sentinel removed.
- Integration test: fake supervisee exits code=1 → shim restarts 2x then crashloop visible via supervise status --json.
- start refuses with exit 17 when daemon socket missing; exit 25 when supervisor.lock held.
- config.json schema_version bumped; N-1 readers tolerate new fields.

Depends on: B3, B1.
close_reason: done

title: MVH-R9: DeadLetterSink JSONL appender
description:
MVH_ROADMAP row #9. Append-only JSONL sink for events the bus could not deliver (consumer error, panic in observer, redaction-config-missing, etc.).

Location: NEW file `internal/handlercontract/deadlettersink.go` + test.

Contract:
type DeadLetterSink interface {
    Record(ctx context.Context, env Envelope, reason string) error
}

Implementation: `OpenDeadLetterSink(path string) (DeadLetterSink, error)` opens (creates if needed) the file at `path`, defaults to `<project>/.harmonik/events/dead-letters.jsonl`. The path is supplied by the caller — the sink does NOT compute the project root itself. Append one JSON object per record:

{"recorded_at":"<RFC3339Nano>","reason":"<short>","envelope":{...full Envelope...}}

Concurrency: serialize writes via a mutex around `os.File.Write`. Each Record call fsyncs (durability is more important than throughput here — dead-letters are rare).

Reuse the existing `internal/eventbus.JSONLWriter` if its shape fits cleanly; do NOT re-implement file open/close/rotate logic from scratch. If JSONLWriter's contract assumes a different schema, prefer a thin wrapper that adds the dead-letter envelope around the raw payload.

Tests: write three dead-letter records; reopen the file; assert 3 lines, each valid JSON, each with `recorded_at`, `reason`, `envelope` keys.

Out of scope: actually wiring the sink into `busimpl.Emit`'s consumer-error branch — that's a follow-up (file a bead at end of dispatch citing this one). Sink existing and tested standalone is the goal.
close_reason: DeadLetterSink JSONL appender implemented in internal/handlercontract/deadlettersink.go; WatcherDeadLetterSink rename resolves name collision; tests green

title: Review-loop silently OFF daemon-wide since ~06-01: queue submit --beads mints items with empty workflow_mode + daemon has no WorkflowModeDefault -> all dispatched beads land UNREVIEWED
description: ROOT CAUSE (independent review 2026-06-02): reviewer_verdict events: 60 on 05-31, ZERO on 06-01 (86 runs) + 06-02 (31 runs). cmd/harmonik/main.go never sets Config.WorkflowModeDefault and there is no --workflow-mode flag/env fallback, so the composition root normalises to WorkflowModeSingle (daemon.go:574-576) = NO review. Reviews ONLY fire when a queue ITEM carries workflow_mode:'review-loop' (capturedItemWFMode path, workloop.go:1520). 05-31 batches set that per-item field; 06-01/06-02 'queue submit --beads' mints minimal items with empty workflow_mode -> unreviewed. CLAUDE.md asserts 'review-loop on by default (hk-g0ckv)' but the persistent-daemon path has no such default. IMPACT: ~117 beads landed unreviewed on main over 2 days (all 3 agents). FIX (requirement): the persistent daemon must default dispatched items to review-loop unless explicitly overridden — either set Config.WorkflowModeDefault=review-loop (add --workflow-mode flag defaulting to review-loop in main.go + have the supervisor pass it) OR default a queue item's workflow_mode to review-loop when unset at submit/dispatch. Add a regression test asserting a --beads-submitted item dispatches with review-loop. INTERIM MITIGATION until this lands+daemon redeploys: dispatch via JSON with per-item workflow_mode:'review-loop'.
close_reason: done

title: Flywheel cache-warmth gap: no byte-stable prefix + no cache_read_input_tokens probe (gates B11 scenario 2)
description: Cache CADENCE is fine (event-driven + 60s subscribe heartbeat + 400ms debounce fires <5min when work in flight; CL-022 mandates 1h cache TTL). But the STABLE PREFIX that makes caching pay is NOT implemented: CL-021 specifies [tool schemas|identity|goal|contract|skill index]--breakpoint--[digest|conversation|fullness] and CL-INV-003 requires byte-stability, but .pi/extensions/flywheel/index.ts buildMinimalDigest() (~:343) is a v0 scaffold and the /reset recycle (:328-340) reseeds via newSession with no byte-identical prefix; prefix ownership is split with claude-launchspec.md §4. Also NOTHING monitors cache_read_input_tokens (CL-023). So B11 acceptance scenario 2 (cache_read >= 0.8 x stable_prefix post-recycle, cognition-loop.md:196) is currently neither satisfiable NOR measurable. FIX: (a) seed a byte-stable prefix above a cache_control breakpoint (launchspec/Pi), (b) add a cache_read_input_tokens probe in the turn_end/context hook (index.ts:286,259). Gates VALIDITY of B11 scenario 2 (other 4 scenarios can run without it).
close_reason: done

title: DOGFOOD-BLOCKER: daemon emits heartbeats for ~15 hours after claude pane disappears (no agent-termination detection)
description:
2026-05-19 v50 dogfood retry of hk-aievp fix (commit fd5d268). The pane-misdirect bug is FIXED — pane_target=%29 was the correct new pane. agent_ready fired at 17:41:03. But the daemon then kept emitting agent_heartbeat events every 5 minutes for ~15 HOURS (08:06–08:51 today) while pane %29 had silently disappeared from tmux.

Symptoms:
- Daemon PID 87959 alive.
- queue.json still 'active' with hk-x0y2k still 'dispatched'.
- bead status: in_progress (never closed).
- tmux: pane %29 gone (claude exited or was killed externally without /quit injection).
- daemon-pane_write logs show daemon never detected the missing pane; kept sending heartbeats to a vanished session.

Earlier visible bridge_dial_failed errors in the OLD pane %22 capture: 'daemon rejected message: status=unknown_session reason=session window closed or not registered for run_id=...'. So the daemon DOES detect bridge_dial_failed in the relay path — but doesn't propagate that to agent-termination detection in the work loop.

Hypothesis: heartbeat goroutine emits ticks unconditionally; agent-liveness check is missing OR uses tmux session_state instead of pane_present check; or daemon only times out via agentReadyTimeout (which fires only BEFORE agent_ready, not after).

Fix surface (best guess):
- After agent_ready, the daemon should periodically check pane_present(paneID); if missing, treat as agent_terminated_silent and fail the bead (re-queue or paused-by-failure).
- Or: rely on bridge_dial_failed to escalate to agent_failed instead of swallowing.
- Or: add a session_window_disappeared event detector.

Severity: P0 BLOCKS dogfood (any pane killed externally hangs the bead indefinitely with no detection).

This is the SECOND half-built-systems instance found in v50 dogfooding (hk-aievp was the first). Naming both as evidence the dogfood-as-test loop is working.

title: MVH-FIX: PL-020 — move RunOrphanSweep from lifecycle to daemon pkg
description:
PL-020 (specs/process-lifecycle.md §4.6) forbids peer-subsystem imports except from internal/daemon. The MVH chain landed `internal/handler` importing `internal/lifecycle` (for WaitOwner + SpawnChildSysProcAttr), and `internal/lifecycle/orphansweep.go` imports `internal/workspace` (one call: `workspace.SweepStaleLeaseLocks` inside `RunOrphanSweep`). Transitively handler→workspace, which the PL-020 sensor in `internal/lifecycle/compositionroot_pl018_pl020_test.go` correctly flags as red.

Fix: move just `RunOrphanSweep` (the orchestrator of the 6-bullet sweep) into the daemon subsystem. The leaf sweepers (SweepOrphanTmuxSessions, SweepOrphanHandlers, SweepOrphanBr) stay in lifecycle — they don't import workspace. Daemon is the only caller of RunOrphanSweep anyway (per MVH-R4 / hk-60uvn).

Scope:
1. Create `internal/daemon/orphansweep.go` containing the `RunOrphanSweep` function moved from `internal/lifecycle/orphansweep.go`. Bring its godoc with it. It now imports `internal/lifecycle` (for the leaf sweepers + the config struct) and `internal/workspace` (for SweepStaleLeaseLocks). PL-020 allows both since daemon is the composition root.
2. The `OrphanSweepConfig` and `OrphanSweepResult` types: pick whichever home matches PL-020 — if either is referenced only by RunOrphanSweep, move it too; if leaf sweepers also use them, keep them in lifecycle and import.
3. Delete `RunOrphanSweep` (and any moved helpers) from `internal/lifecycle/orphansweep.go`. Drop the now-unused `internal/workspace` import from that file.
4. Move/rewrite the tests for `RunOrphanSweep` that live in `internal/lifecycle/orphansweepcomplete_pl_inv_test.go` (and any other test files exercising RunOrphanSweep) into `internal/daemon/`. Leaf-sweeper tests stay put.
5. Update the call site in `internal/daemon/daemon.go` — `lifecycle.RunOrphanSweep(...)` → local `RunOrphanSweep(...)`.

Verify:
- `go build ./...`
- `go test ./internal/lifecycle/...` (PL-020 must pass; PL-018 must pass)
- `go test ./internal/daemon/...` (moved tests must pass)
- `go test ./...` (everything still green)
- `gofmt -d` empty

Do NOT change RunOrphanSweep's behaviour or its public contract — pure move.
close_reason: moved RunOrphanSweep + OrphanSweepConfig + OrphanSweepResult to internal/daemon; removed workspace import from lifecycle; PL-020 sensor green

title: WindowPanePID falls back to session active pane for long window names — beadRunOne.Wait() deadlocks

Reproduces in v5 smoke run: window name = worktree absolute path, after Kill the window is gone or renamed, but Wait sees the daemon's own window (sleep 120) as alive. Fix: use window index in handle instead of window name; or disambiguate with per-pane PID stored at SpawnWindow time.
close_reason: Wait now uses stored per-pane PID + syscall.Kill(pid, 0) for liveness; decoupled from tmux name resolution. New regression test. Commit e430807.

description: Implements specs/operator-nfr.md v0.4.1 (60 first-class §4 req beads from 60 active §4 entries — 1 envelope + 59 topical — after 0 §2.3 coalesces and 0 §2.1a collapses, 4 invariants — ON-INV-001/003/005/006; ON-INV-002 + ON-INV-004 retired in v0.3 never reusable, 0 §6 schemas — §6.1/§6.2/§6.3 explicitly omitted per §6 declaration, see F-pilot-ON-3, 1 §8 single-bead error-taxonomy on-error.taxonomy covering 23 exit codes — single-bead form per §2.11(c) + SHAPE-not-COUNT tiebreaker per F-pilot-WM-2, see F-pilot-ON-4, 1 multi-step split — ON-027 8-step graceful-shutdown drain SPLIT into umbrella + 8 step beads, DEVIATES from PL precedent per F8b independent-code-path tiebreaker, see F-pilot-ON-5, 11 test-infra beads from §10.2 obligations). depends-on: [architecture, event-model, execution-model, handler-contract, control-points, process-lifecycle, reconciliation, beads-integration]. title: Foundation-cross-cutting envelope declaration
description: Per ON-ENV-001: declare the foundation-cross-cutting envelope (voluntary publication; AR-052 exempts foundation-cross-cutting specs from AR-053). (a) Events produced (operator_pause_status paired-phase, operator_resuming, operator_stopped, operator_upgrading, operator_upgrade_completed, operator_upgrade_rejected, operator_command_rejected, dispatch_deferred — emitted from §4.3/§4.6 operator-control path, registered in EV §8.7); (b) events consumed (daemon_ready, reconciliation_category_assigned, budget_warning/exhausted/accrual, agent_warning_silent_hang); (c) types introduced — none (this spec references existing types owned by EV/CP/WM); (d) handlers implemented — none; (e) state owned — none persistently (operator-control state-machine is daemon-in-memory; reconstruction per ON-030 via git+Beads); (f) control points provided — none; (g) NFRs inherited — ON IS the NFR home; (h) boundary classification per operation table.
close_reason: done

title: Pause and upgrade respect the between-task invariant
description: Per ON-008: an operator `pause` or `upgrade` issued while daemon status is `ready` MUST NOT interrupt any in-flight run (per §3 `in_flight(run)`). Daemon MUST transition `running → pausing`, allow every in-flight run to proceed to its next durable checkpoint per [execution-model.md §4.5], AND complete the full drain sequence of ON-027 (all eight steps) before transitioning to `paused`. The `pausing → paused` transition is gated on drain-completion: entry into `paused` is forbidden until (a) no run satisfies `in_flight(run)` AND (b) every drain step of ON-027 has completed (or drain-timeout escalation per ON-029 fired). `upgrade` further transitions `paused → upgrading → (exec-replace) → running` under §4.6.
close_reason: done

title: `stop --immediate` is the only control that aborts in-flight runs
description: Per ON-009: `stop --immediate` and SIGKILL (treated equivalently) MUST abort in-flight runs (per §3 `in_flight(run)`). Aborted runs MUST emit `run_failed` with class `canceled` per [execution-model.md §8.4] once daemon restarts and reconciliation classifies them per [reconciliation/spec.md §4.3]. No other operator control is permitted to abort in-flight runs; proposals to add `pause --immediate` or `upgrade --immediate` MUST be rejected as ON-008 violations.
close_reason: done

title: Reconciliation carve-out: pause queues during `reconciling`
description: Per ON-010: pause MUST NOT interrupt reconciliation workflows per [reconciliation/spec.md §4.1]. Daemon's status progression is `starting → (optional degraded) → reconciling → ready` per [process-lifecycle.md §4.2]; the between-task invariant of ON-008 applies only after daemon reaches `ready`. An operator pause issued during `reconciling` MUST be queued and applied at the boundary event "all reconciliation runs have either resumed into normal flow or produced a verdict."
close_reason: done

title: Operator-control state machine + serializable transitions
description: Per ON-011: daemon MUST implement the operator-control state machine of §7.1. States: `running`, `pausing`, `paused`, `resuming`, `stopped` (terminal-recoverable via `start`), `upgrading`. Improvement-pause is NOT a distinct state — reuses `pausing/paused` with `pause_reason=improvement` discriminator per ON-012. Operator-control transitions MUST be serializable: daemon MUST hold a single mutex (or equivalent CAS primitive) guarding the transition function. Concurrent operator commands (per OQ-ON-004) are arbitrated by mutex acquisition order; the loser observes the post-winner state. The mutex MUST be acquired before evaluating a transition guard and held until the transition's emission and durable-marker write per ON-030a complete.
close_reason: spec-corpus sensor TestON011StateMachineSerializableTransitions in internal/specaudit/on011_state_machine_serializable_test.go; commit bfceadf

title: Improvement-pause is a subtype of pause
description: Per ON-012: S09 improvement cycle MUST NOT introduce a new operator-control state. An improvement-pause MUST transition `running → pausing → paused` via the same path as operator pause, sharing identical state table with `pause_reason` discriminator distinguishing operator-initiated (`pause_reason=operator`) from improvement-initiated (`pause_reason=improvement`). Additional invariant: `paused → resuming` is triggered automatically when improvement loop completes (no operator action required). Discriminator carried on `operator_pause_status` per [event-model.md §6.3] structured-fields mechanism; NOT a separate pair of state-machine states. Earlier framing of separate `improvement-pausing/improvement-paused` states is retired.
close_reason: spec-corpus sensor TestON012ImprovementPauseIsSubtypeOfPause in internal/specaudit/on012_improvement_pause_subtype_test.go; commit 1697470

title: Operator-control events emitted per state transition
description: Per ON-013: daemon MUST emit one typed event per operator-control state transition. Per paired-phase rule [event-model.md §8.9(h)], pause `pausing` and `paused` are lifecycle phases of a single pause emitted as `operator_pause_status` with `status ∈ {pausing, paused}` (forbidden as keepalive re-emission). Emission timing: `operator_pause_status{status=pausing}` on `running → pausing` (incl. improvement-pause path); `operator_pause_status{status=paused}` on `pausing → paused` (only after all ON-027 drain steps complete) — with `drain_summary` field naming each ON-027 step's completion timestamp + drain-timeout escalations (cross-spec coordination request to EV: extend §8.7.6 payload with `drain_summary?`); `operator_resuming` on `paused → resuming`; `operator_stopped` on entry to `stopped` with `mode ∈ {graceful, immediate}` per [event-model.md §8.7.8]; `operator_upgrading` on `paused → upgrading` with `upgrade_version` field per [event-model.md §8.7.9]; `operator_upgrade_completed` on `upgrading → running` after exec-replace; `operator_upgrade_rejected` when ON-005 commit-hash check fails or cross-version schema check refuses; `operator_command_rejected` (§8 code 16) when invalid for current state; `dispatch_deferred` (§8 code 18) when blocked by ON-041 machine-ceiling. ON owns *when*; EV owns *shape*.
close_reason: spec-corpus sensor TestON013OperatorEventsPerStateTransition in internal/specaudit/on013_operator_events_per_transition_test.go; commit a600dff

title: Per-command supervision (defer recover() barrier)
description: Per ON-013a: every operator-command-dispatch goroutine (handling `pause`, `stop`, `upgrade`, `attach`, `enqueue`) MUST install a `defer recover()` barrier. On panic the barrier MUST: (a) emit `operator_command_failed{command, panic_class, run_id?}` (cross-spec coordination request to EV); (b) revert any partial state-machine transition by clearing the `.harmonik/daemon.state` marker's pending-transition field; (c) escalate to `degraded` if panic occurred during a drain step. The top-level PL-018a panic barrier remains the daemon's outer-defense; ON-013a is the operator-command inner-defense.
close_reason: done

title: Operator-command idempotency on no-op transitions
description: Per ON-013c: operator commands MUST be idempotent on no-op transitions. `pause` issued while already `paused` MUST return success (exit 0) with `operator_pause_status{status=paused}` re-emitted at most once per command (deduplicated via session_id). `stop` while `stopped` MUST return success with no event emission. `resume` while `running` MUST return success with no transition. The operator's CLI MUST NOT see a different exit code for "already in target state" vs "transitioned successfully."
close_reason: spec-corpus sensor TestON013cOperatorCommandIdempotencyNoOpTransitions in internal/specaudit/on013c_command_idempotency_test.go; commit 1697470

title: Reconciliation operator override (pre-execution verdict pause)
description: Per ON-014: spec-draft pass MUST produce a normative per-reconciliation-workflow policy option to pause daemon's verdict-execution step (per [reconciliation/spec.md §4.5]) until an operator confirms or vetoes. Naming convention: `harmonik confirm-verdict <run_id>` / `harmonik veto-verdict <run_id> [--promote-to escalate-to-human]`. Default: execution proceeds without operator confirmation; operators opt in by policy. Applies to all investigator-dispatched reconciliation categories (Cat 2, 3, 6a per RC §4.2 and RC §8.12). Foundation owns naming convention; [reconciliation/spec.md §4.5] owns execution-step specifics.

All pass; gofmt clean. Commit 2f58779 on worktree-agent-on-4.

title: Beads is the queue; overlay schema is harmonik's half
description: Per ON-015: operator's pending-tasks list and harmonik's dispatchable queue are the same store: Beads (SQLite, `Dicklesworthstone/beads_rust`) per [beads-integration.md §4.1]–[beads-integration.md §4.3]. Queue-format compatibility MUST be union of (a) Beads schema compat (managed upstream) AND (b) harmonik's overlay schema compat: `Harmonik-Bead-ID` trailers in checkpoint commits per [execution-model.md §4.4], bead-ID references in events per [event-model.md §6.3], session-log bead-ID metadata per [workspace-model.md §4.7]. Both halves MUST be N-1 readable.

title: Operator-observable exit codes are structured
description: Per ON-001: every operator-invoked harmonik command (`daemon`, `attach`, `enqueue`, `status`, `pause`, `stop`, `upgrade`, all multi-daemon commands per §4.10) MUST return a structured exit code. Zero MUST mean success. Non-zero codes MUST map one-to-one to a failure category declared in the §8 exit-code taxonomy. The mapping MUST be stable across releases within the N-1 compat window.

title: Queue schema version check on daemon startup
description: Per ON-016: on daemon startup, daemon MUST check both Beads SQLite schema version and harmonik's overlay schema version against the running binary's supported set (current N + prior N-1). Unsupported version MUST cause startup failure with the exit code assigned to category "queue-format-unsupported" per §8 (code 2), naming required migration release in failure event payload. Check is part of the startup failure-mode catalog of ON-003.

title: Beads pre-1.0 breakage absorbed via `br` adapter, not forked
description: Per ON-017: harmonik MUST version-pin Beads per the external-inputs protocol (problem-space §External inputs) and MUST route all Beads interactions through the `br`-CLI adapter of [beads-integration.md §4.2]. A Beads breaking change MUST produce one localized adapter update; harmonik MUST NOT fork Beads. This is a structural obligation on the adapter boundary, not on every caller.
close_reason: SUBSUMED: TestON017_BeadsBreakageAbsorbedByAdapter and TestON017_AdapterBoundaryIsVersionPinned in internal/operatornfr/queueformatcompat_test.go fully cover ON-017 — adapter-boundary fixture, version-pin naming check, single-caller-count invariant; all pass.

title: N-1 compatibility is the MVH compat window
description: Per ON-018: every versioned on-disk or wire artifact declared by foundation specs — event-envelope schema ([event-model.md §6.1]), event payload schemas ([event-model.md §6.3]), checkpoint trailers and sibling files ([execution-model.md §4.4]), queue overlay (ON-015), policy schema ([control-points.md §6.3]) — MUST maintain N-1 readability. A reader pinned to N-1 MUST successfully parse and interpret artifacts written by N, with additive fields treated as unknown but non-fatal. Breaking changes MUST be accompanied by a migration release and MUST NOT be introduced mid-run; they MUST land at an operator pause per §4.3.
close_reason: SUBSUMED: internal/operatornfr/schemacompatwindow_test.go — hk-sx9r.78 harness declares N-1 compat window for all 5 ON-018 artifacts (event-envelope, event-payload, checkpoint-trailer, queue-overlay, policy-schema) via compatMatrixFixtureTable + TestON018_NMinus1ReadabilityForAllArtifacts + TestONINV001_CompatMatrixCoversAllArtifacts; no separate specaudit sensor needed for the declaration bead

title: Migration releases are operator-paused boundaries
description: Per ON-019: a migration release (any release that bumps an N-1-covered schema version to break the compat window — i.e., a change no longer readable by readers at current N) MUST require an operator pause before installation. The `harmonik upgrade` contract of §4.6 MUST refuse to exec-replace into a migration release unless daemon is in `paused` state AND on-disk state's schema version is within the new binary's supported set. Installing a migration release MUST NOT auto-migrate on-disk state; a dedicated migration workflow (post-MVH) is the path.
close_reason: spec-text-binding sensor added; 8 checks PASS; harmonik upgrade handler absent — code-corpus TODO deferred to hk-sx9r.24

title: `harmonik upgrade` contract obligation (7 sub-rules including rollback)
description: Per ON-020: spec-draft pass MUST produce a normative `harmonik upgrade` contract specifying: (a) binary-source mechanism (repo-relative path and/or explicit hash-supply flag); (b) operator-supplied expected commit-hash check per ON-005; (c) drain-vs-reconciliation interaction (MUST queue if reconciliation in-flight per ON-010; MUST NOT interrupt); (d) cross-version state contract (MUST succeed for same and N-1; MUST refuse for broader mismatches per ON-019); (e) socket continuity — daemon MUST preserve bound listener fd across exec-replace via fd-passing per [process-lifecycle.md §4.9 PL-027(iii)]: outgoing daemon clears `FD_CLOEXEC` and passes fd via `HARMONIK_LISTENER_FD`; new binary adopts via `net.FileListener` and re-sets `FD_CLOEXEC`. Adoption is gap-free; clients observe no `ECONNREFUSED` window; (f) rollback as first-class — on `harmonik upgrade --rollback` while `paused` after successful upgrade, daemon MUST exec-replace back to prior binary at `.harmonik/daemon.binary.prev`; expected_commit_hash for rollback is prior binary's stamp; live-window rollback unsupported; (g) post-exec-replace failure — operator recovers by `harmonik upgrade --rollback` (removes stale pidfile/socket, restores prior binary, consumes `.harmonik/daemon.upgrading` marker for hash determination).
close_reason: Subsumed: landed as cca00f5

title: Upgrade-intent durable marker (`.harmonik/daemon.upgrading`)
description: Per ON-020a: when `harmonik upgrade` enters drain phase, daemon MUST atomically write `.harmonik/daemon.upgrading` containing: (a) operator-supplied `expected_commit_hash` per ON-005; (b) upgrade-initiation timestamp; (c) operator's session_id (per ON-013b daemon-instance handshake). Write MUST follow temp+rename+fsync atomicity. On daemon startup, [process-lifecycle.md §4.2 PL-005] step 0 MUST read this marker; if present and on-disk binary's commit hash matches the marker's `expected_commit_hash`, startup proceeds normally and marker is removed; if hash does not match, daemon MUST refuse startup with §8 code 14 (`upgrade-hash-mismatch`) and emit `daemon_startup_failed{failure_mode=upgrade-hash-mismatch-on-restart}`. The PL-027(iv) marker, currently informative, is hereby promoted to normative per this requirement (cross-spec coordination request to PL).
close_reason: done

title: Upgrade preserves in-flight run recoverability (iff drain-completed)
description: Per ON-021: an `upgrade` operation MUST NOT make any in-flight run unrecoverable. The recoverability invariant holds iff entry into `paused` implies drain-completion (per ON-008 and ON-027): because the `pausing → paused` transition is gated on all eight drain steps completing AND no run satisfying `in_flight(run)` remaining, the state the new binary inherits MUST be reconstructible from git + Beads per [execution-model.md §4.7] and the restart RTO of §4.8. Cross-version state contract of ON-020 MUST reject upgrades that would violate this invariant.
close_reason: done

title: Secrets injected at handler launch; never logged; redactor fail-closed
description: Per ON-022: secrets (API keys, tokens, credentials) MUST be injected at handler launch per [handler-contract.md §4.7]. Secrets MUST NOT appear in event log. Secrets MUST NOT appear in session logs without redaction. Redaction is mechanism-tagged and MUST be enforced pre-emission: handler implementations MUST apply prefix-regex matching and per-handler redaction patterns before any write to a durable sink (event bus, session log, audit record). The redactor MUST fail-closed: if redactor itself panics or returns an error during emission of any event/log/audit-record carrying typed `Secret` fields, daemon MUST abort the emission, MUST emit `redaction_failed{event_type, run_id?, error_class}` (cross-spec coordination request to EV: add `redaction_failed` to §8 taxonomy), and MUST NOT fall through to a non-redacted emission. Repeated redactor panics within T_redact_fail (default 60s, operator-tunable) MUST escalate daemon to `degraded` per ON-037.
close_reason: Subsumed: landed as 2af7dfa

title: Secrets-redaction compile-time payload-schema check
description: Per ON-023: event payload schemas declared per [event-model.md §6.3] MUST NOT declare any field typed as `Secret` or equivalent. A compile-time check (lint pass or generated-code assertion) MUST reject any payload schema that would carry a secret through the event bus. This closes the redaction-obligation loop: redaction cannot be forgotten at an emission site because no emission site is permitted to carry secret-typed fields.

title: Command-execution sandbox invariant
description: Per ON-024: agents MUST execute within a leased workspace directory per [workspace-model.md §4.3]. Escape attempts (symlinks resolving outside workspace, path-traversal patterns, git hooks sourced from untrusted paths) MUST be prevented. Specific enforcement is owned by subsystem specs for S04 (handler runner) and S06 (workspace manager); this spec states the cross-cutting invariant. Note: WM cite is informational per F-pilot-ON-6 (WM not in ON depends-on).

title: Exit-code taxonomy obligation
description: Per ON-002: spec-draft pass MUST produce a normative exit-code taxonomy naming every non-zero exit code emitted by any operator command. Per code: failure category, operator-observable symptom, emitted event type (if any), operator remediation pointer. The taxonomy lives in §8 of this spec; cross-references from other specs (e.g., [process-lifecycle.md §4.10 harmonik status]) MUST resolve to §8 entries. Obligation is satisfied when the §8 table exists per §10.1 conformance bootstrap.

title: Network egress and skill-injection policy enforcement
description: Per ON-025: network egress MUST be governed by policy per [control-points.md §6.3]; a policy MAY whitelist domains for agent access. Skills provisioned per [handler-contract.md §4.11] MUST honor egress policy: a provisioned skill that would require egress to a non-whitelisted domain MUST fail provisioning, and handler MUST emit `skills_provisioned` event (per [event-model.md §8.3]) listing only the skills actually installed. Skills requiring filesystem access outside the workspace MUST fail provisioning per ON-024.
close_reason: done

title: Prompt-injection defense is handler-owned
description: Per ON-026: input sanitization for user-provided content in the input workspace MUST be the handler's responsibility per [handler-contract.md §4.1]. Handlers MUST NOT let user-provided content in the input workspace alter the agent's system-prompt instructions. This spec states the obligation; the enforcement mechanism lives in handler-contract.
close_reason: done

title: Graceful-shutdown ordering (8-step drain umbrella)
description: Per ON-027: on `stop --graceful` or SIGTERM — AND on `pause` / `upgrade` per the ON-008 drain-gate — daemon MUST execute the shutdown/drain sequence in the following order, each step completing before the next begins: (1) orchestrator stops pulling new tasks; (2) every in-flight run proceeds to its next checkpoint then suspends per [execution-model.md §4.5]; (3) agent runners wait for handler subprocesses to complete or reach drain timeout; (3a) `br`-CLI adapter intent-log per [beads-integration.md §4.10 BI-029/BI-030] MUST be drained to completion; (4) event bus flushes pending events (fsync per [event-model.md §4.4]); (5) memory layer flushes indexing; (6) workspace manager unlocks leased workspaces and cleans up incomplete adze setups per [workspace-model.md §4.3] [WM cite informational]; (7) orchestrator exits with code 0 if clean, or §8 code 11 "drain-timeout-escalated" if any step exceeded its bound. In pause/upgrade path the exit of step 7 is replaced by "enter `paused`" (no process exit); step sequence is identical. Completion of ALL eight steps is the precondition for `pausing → paused` transition of ON-008. Per §2.2 + F8b "diverse code paths" tiebreaker (F-pilot-ON-5), this requirement SPLITS into umbrella + 8 step beads (s1, s2, s3, s3a, s4, s5, s6, s7).
close_reason: SUBSUMED by hk-sx9r.80 (closed). securitydrain_sx9r80_test.go covers ON-027 (8-step drain ordering), ON-027a (drain step atomicity + crash-recovery), ON-028, ON-029, and all structural drain-sequence constraints. The umbrella bead's sensor obligations are fully covered by the sibling harness.

title: Step 1 — Orchestrator stops pulling new tasks from the queue
description: Per ON-027 step 1: at drain entry, orchestrator's dispatch loop MUST stop pulling new beads from Beads (`br ready` query loop terminated). No new run dispatch is permitted from this point until daemon exits or transitions back to `running`. Step 1 completion is a single boolean toggle on the dispatcher; no subsystem coordination required. Per ON-027a, completion MUST be marked durably in `.harmonik/daemon.state` before step 2 begins. On crash mid-step, restart resumes from step 1 (idempotent — re-toggling the dispatcher off is a no-op).

title: Step 2 — In-flight runs reach next checkpoint, then suspend
description: Per ON-027 step 2: every run for which `in_flight(run)` holds (per §3) proceeds to its next durable checkpoint per [execution-model.md §4.5], then suspends. Bounded by `timeout.step_2` per ON-029. On step-2 timeout per ON-029, escalation path fires (SIGKILL surviving subprocesses at step 4 wait window per ON-040 silent-hang synthesis). Step 2 completion signal: `in_flight(run)` evaluates to false for every run via dispatch-status JSON-RPC per [process-lifecycle.md §4.1 PL-003a]. Per ON-027a, durably marked.

title: Step 3 — Agent runners wait for handler subprocesses to complete
description: Per ON-027 step 3: agent runners (HC watcher goroutines per [handler-contract.md §4.3 HC-011]) wait for handler subprocesses to complete or reach the drain timeout `timeout.step_3` per ON-029. Step 3 is bounded by HC-018 SIGTERM→SIGKILL escalation interval. On step-3 timeout, surviving agent subprocesses are SIGKILLed AT step 4's wait window with synthesized `agent_warning_silent_hang{reason=drain_forced}` per ON-040. Per ON-027a, durably marked.

title: Step 3a — `br`-CLI adapter intent-log drain
description: Per ON-027 step 3a: the `br`-CLI adapter intent-log per [beads-integration.md §4.10 BI-029/BI-030] MUST be drained to completion: every pending intent-log entry's terminal-transition write MUST resolve (success or BI-031 status-check classification) before step 4 proceeds. Drain failures (e.g., `BrUnavailable` per [beads-integration.md §6.1a]) escalate to step 4 with the failed entries marked for next-restart Cat 3a routing per RC. Bounded by `timeout.step_3a` per ON-029. Per ON-027a, durably marked.

title: Step 4 — Event bus flushes pending events (fsync)
description: Per ON-027 step 4: event bus flushes all pending events to JSONL with `fsync(file_fd)` + `fsync(parent_dir_fd)` per [event-model.md §4.4]. Bounded by `timeout.step_4` per ON-029. fsync failure produces §8 code 21 `drain-step-errored` (not code 11 — code 11 is timeout-escalation; code 21 is non-recoverable error). Step 4 also synthesizes `agent_warning_silent_hang{reason=drain_forced}` per ON-040 BEFORE SIGKILL emission to any still-running agent subprocess from step 3 timeout. Per ON-027a, durably marked.

Bounded by `timeout.step_6` per ON-029. Lock-release failure produces §8 code 21 with `drain_error.step=6` discriminator. Per ON-027a, durably marked.

title: Startup failure-mode catalog obligation
description: Per ON-003: spec-draft pass MUST produce a normative startup failure-mode catalog co-owned with [process-lifecycle.md §4.2]. For every daemon-startup prerequisite failure (git bad state, Beads SQLite unavailable, schema-version unsupported, stale-pidfile race, filesystem unwritable, disk-full during checkpoint commit, socket bind failure), catalog MUST specify: detection rule, exit code per ON-001, operator remediation procedure, emitted event type per [event-model.md §6.3], reconciliation Cat 0 classification per [reconciliation/spec.md §4.3]. Catalog is the authoritative input to `harmonik status`'s infrastructure-prereq reporting per [process-lifecycle.md §4.10].

title: Step 7 — Orchestrator exits (or enters `paused`)
description: Per ON-027 step 7: orchestrator exits with code 0 if clean, or §8 code 11 (`drain-timeout-escalated`) if any prior step exceeded its bound. In the pause/upgrade path, the exit of step 7 is replaced by "enter `paused`" (no process exit) — the step sequence is identical. Completion of step 7 is the precondition for the `pausing → paused` transition per ON-008.

title: Drain step atomicity and crash-recovery (F8c constraint)
description: Per ON-027a: the eight steps of ON-027 MUST execute strictly sequentially on a single goroutine; no parallel execution between steps is permitted. Each step's completion MUST be marked durably (in `.harmonik/daemon.state` marker per ON-030a, augmented with `drain_step_completed` field) before the next step begins. On crash mid-drain, next daemon startup MUST resume drain from next-uncompleted step rather than restart from step 1; resumption MUST be idempotent on completed steps (each step's effect is replay-safe). Per §2.2 F8c, this is a constraint requirement adjacent to the ON-027 umbrella; it gets its own bead with `blocks` edge from the umbrella (the umbrella cannot close until atomicity discipline is met).
close_reason: done

title: `stop --immediate` skips drain steps 2–3
description: Per ON-028: on `stop --immediate` or SIGKILL, daemon MUST skip steps 2 and 3 of ON-027. In-flight agent subprocesses MUST be killed (SIGTERM with a short bounded window, then SIGKILL). In-flight run state is recoverable on next startup via checkpoint + reconciliation per [reconciliation/spec.md §4.2], but in-flight agent subprocesses are not gracefully stopped.
close_reason: SUBSUMED+extended: subprocess SIGTERM->SIGKILL kill-sequence and recoverability tests added in stopimmediate_sx9r42_test.go (hk-sx9r.80 had steps-2-3 skippability, this adds kill sequence and recoverability)

title: Drain timeout per-step + optional sum-bound (F8c constraint)
description: Per ON-029: the drain timeout of ON-027 is configurable per-step (`timeout.step_2`, `timeout.step_3`, etc.); the §7.2 pseudocode is normative for the per-step apportionment. A single `drain_timeout_total` MAY be declared as the sum-bound; if declared, individual `timeout.step_N` MUST sum to ≤ `drain_timeout_total`. Drain timeout knobs MUST be operator-configurable per the config inventory of ON-004. Default values are specified in the config inventory; change-takes-effect semantics is "next daemon start" (drain timeouts are read once at startup). Per §2.2 F8c, this is a constraint requirement adjacent to the ON-027 umbrella; own bead with `blocks` from umbrella.
close_reason: done

title: Restart reconstruction path (git + Beads, no JSONL replay)
description: Per ON-030: daemon restart MUST reconstruct the in-memory model by walking the git checkpoint trail per [execution-model.md §4.7] and querying Beads via `br` per [beads-integration.md §4.2]. The JSONL event log MUST NOT be replayed for state reconstruction (per [event-model.md §4.4] and [event-model.md §4.5], and locked decision #12 — no DTW). Reconciliation workflows MUST spawn for in-flight runs (per §3 `in_flight(run)`) per [reconciliation/spec.md §4.2].
close_reason: done

title: Pause-state durable marker (`.harmonik/daemon.state`)
description: Per ON-030a: the operator-control state machine of §7.1 MUST persist its current state via an atomic-written marker file `.harmonik/daemon.state` containing current `DaemonStatus` plus pause-reason discriminator (when applicable; one of `operator`, `improvement`, `upgrade-prepare`). Write MUST use temp+rename+fsync+parent-fsync per [workspace-model.md §4.7 WM-026] [WM cite informational per F-pilot-ON-6] and MUST happen synchronously on every state transition that produces `paused`, `pausing`, `upgrade-prepare`, or `stopped` state. On daemon startup, [process-lifecycle.md §4.2 PL-005] step 0 MUST read `.harmonik/daemon.state`; if marker indicates paused/upgrade-prepare state, daemon MUST initialize into that state rather than `running`, preserving operator intent across crashes. Marker MUST be removed on clean transition to `running` or process exit via the normal-startup path.
close_reason: done

title: Restart RTO target (30s p95 nominal / 300s hard ceiling)
description: Per ON-031: restart MUST reach pre-restart state within RTO target, measured from SIGTERM (or crash) to daemon emitting `ready` status event per [process-lifecycle.md §4.2]. MVH RTO target: **30 seconds nominal fixture target** (p95 under standard fixture defined in ON-032 criterion 1) with a **300-second hard ceiling** (ON-032 criterion 3). Sensor: restart-RTO test harness backed by the standard fixture (`≤ 500 open beads`, `≤ 50 in-flight runs`, git-log depth `≤ 10,000` commits, `≤ 100` Cat-3-pending runs, `≤ 10` active investigator workflows). See OQ-ON-005 for residual ambiguity on auto-escalate vs notify-only on ceiling breach.
close_reason: done

title: RTO criteria and hard ceiling (3 criteria)
description: Per ON-032: RTO target MUST be set against three criteria. **Criterion 1 — operator expectation (nominal fixture).** MVH assumes single-operator, single-instance deployment. Target: **≤ 30 seconds at 95th percentile** under the standard fixture (`≤ 500 open beads, ≤ 50 in-flight runs, git-log depth ≤ 10,000 commits since the oldest open bead's first checkpoint, ≤ 100 reconciliation-Cat-3-pending runs, ≤ 10 active investigator workflows`). These bounds are the harness's reference state. **Criterion 2 — reconstruction complexity.** Restart time is proportional to (a) git-log walk depth since oldest open bead's first checkpoint, and (b) Beads query latency for ready + in-flight bead sets. JSONL event count is NOT a restart-time factor. **Criterion 3 — hard ceiling: 300 seconds.** Beyond this the operator MUST be notified (daemon MUST enter `degraded` reporting `reconciling` with progress markers). Criterion 3 is non-negotiable. Reconciliation-workflow dispatch time is part of the RTO; reconciliation-workflow execution time (investigator-agent LLM calls per RC §4.4) is NOT.
close_reason: SUBSUMED by hk-sx9r.81 (closed). restartrto_sx9r81_test.go covers ON-032 (RTO criteria and hard ceiling): criterion-1 standard fixture bounds (500 beads / 50 in-flight / 10k commits / 100 Cat-3 / 10 investigators), criterion-3 hard ceiling (300s), exit code 12 (rto-hard-ceiling-exceeded), and spec-section existence. All three ON-032 criteria are structurally encoded and validated.

title: RTO measurement boundary (monotonic-corrected clock)
description: Per ON-033: RTO of ON-031 MUST be measured using a monotonic-corrected clock source: SIGTERM receipt and `daemon_ready` emission timestamps MUST both carry an `_at_ns_since_boot` companion field (cross-spec coordination request to EV: add `shutdown_at_ns_since_boot` and `ready_at_ns_since_boot` to `daemon_shutdown` and `daemon_ready` payloads in §8.7.2 / §8.7.3). On boot-transition (post-reboot), monotonic-clock comparison is undefined; RTO MUST be marked `rto_undefined` for the boot-transition cycle and excluded from the p95 measurement. SIGKILL terminations have no `daemon_shutdown` emission; RTO is `rto_undefined` for those cycles. Measurement MUST NOT start from `harmonik daemon` invocation time; the boundary is SIGTERM (or daemon crash timestamp recorded by the OS) to daemon's `ready` status event emission per [process-lifecycle.md §4.2].
close_reason: done

title: Post-panic forensic file (`.harmonik/panic-<timestamp>.log`)
description: Per ON-053: when daemon's top-level panic barrier ([process-lifecycle.md §4.6 PL-018a]) intercepts a panic and exits with §8 code 19 (`runtime-panic`), daemon MUST atomically write a forensic file to `.harmonik/panic-<timestamp>.log` containing: (a) the Go runtime panic message and stack trace; (b) daemon's PID, PGID, project_hash, and binary commit hash; (c) timestamp of the panic in both wall-clock (RFC 3339) and `time.Since(boot)` monotonic forms; (d) last-emitted run_id / node_id / event_id (best-effort from in-memory cursor). Write MUST follow temp+rename+fsync+parent-fsync atomicity discipline of [workspace-model.md §4.7 WM-026] [WM cite informational per F-pilot-ON-6]. File is NOT consumed by reconciliation; it exists for operator post-mortem inspection. Multiple panic files MAY accumulate; ON does NOT mandate cleanup at this revision (tracked under OQ-ON-010).
close_reason: done

title: Config inventory obligation
description: Per ON-004: spec-draft pass MUST produce a normative config inventory enumerating every operator-configurable knob across foundation specs. Per knob: precedence layer (runtime override / operator-policy file / workflow definition / default per CP-037), default value, allowed range, change-takes-effect semantics. Coverage at minimum: timer-flush cadence (EV §4.4), budget warning threshold (CP §4.5), drain timeout (§4.7), RTO thresholds (§4.8), queue-empty re-query cadence (PL §4.4), Cat 0 pre-check retry cadence (RC §4.3), per-Cat reconciliation budgets (RC §4.4). Inventory home is OQ-ON-001; default-if-unresolved is sibling file under specs/operator-nfr/config-inventory.md.
close_reason: done

title: Every subsystem emits typed events
description: Per ON-034: every subsystem MUST emit events per [event-model.md §6.3]. Event taxonomy additions introduced by a subsystem MUST be declared via the subsystem envelope (per [architecture.md §4.4] AR-013) and registered per [event-model.md §4.6]. Every event MUST carry the four-axis and mechanism/cognition tags per [architecture.md §4.1].
close_reason: Spec-sensor tests added in eventshb_sx9r50_test.go: four-axis fixture (all 4 axes), event-model §6.3/§4.6 cross-ref tests, AR-013 subsystem envelope ref test, Tags: AR-005 sensor. Sibling observability_sx9r82_test.go already covers TestON034_SpecSectionExists. Commit 6a73e89 on worktree-agent-on-5.

title: Every subsystem emits structured logs (NDJSON wire format)
description: Per ON-035: every subsystem MUST emit structured logs. Unstructured log lines (free-form text only) are forbidden at spec-declared emission points. Structured logs are MVH substrate for observability; Prom/OTel formats are post-MVH per ON-043. Wire format owned by this spec (promoted from unreferenced `[event-model.md §3.8]` cite). Minimum NDJSON record carries: `ts` (RFC 3339 with ms), `log_schema_version` (current `"1.0"`), `level ∈ {debug, info, warn, error}`, `subsystem`, `source_subsystem` (per [event-model.md §4.9 EV-034a]), `run_id?`, `node_id?`, `event_id?` (UUIDv7 correlation per [event-model.md §4.1] when log corresponds to event emission), `msg`, `fields`. Secrets-redaction per ON-022 MUST apply pre-emission; producer-side; consumers MUST NOT re-redact. Log files MUST rotate at 100 MiB or 24 hours; prior files moved to `.harmonik/logs/<subsystem>-<rotated_at>.jsonl`. `log_schema_version` is under N-1 compat per ON-INV-001 (cross-spec coordination request: add structured logs to ON-018 enumeration; tracked OQ-ON-011). Detailed schema deferred to OQ-ON-007 (`quality-checks.md`).
close_reason: done

title: Every subsystem exposes a health-check interface
description: Per ON-036: every subsystem MUST expose a health-check interface returning `health_status ∈ {OK, degraded, failed}` with optional reason string. Orchestrator MUST aggregate subsystem health into a harmonik-wide health status exposed via `harmonik status` per [process-lifecycle.md §4.10].
