title: Every subsystem emits liveness heartbeats
description: Per ON-037: every subsystem MUST emit a liveness heartbeat event on a defined cadence. Missing heartbeats beyond tolerance MUST trigger `degraded` classification for that subsystem and raise the aggregated harmonik-wide health accordingly. Cadence and tolerance are operator-configurable per ON-004.
close_reason: Spec-sensor tests added in eventshb_sx9r50_test.go: heartbeat-knob fixture (cadence + tolerance), knobs cross-checked against ON-004 config inventory fixture, harmonik-wide aggregation spec text verified, Tags: and Axes: AR-005 sensors. Knobs heartbeat_cadence and heartbeat_miss_tolerance added to obligationsFixtureConfigInventory per ON-037 operator-configurable declaration. Sibling covers TestON037_SpecSectionExists + TestON037_MissedHeartbeatTriggersDegraded. Commit 6a73e89.

title: Audit records are a subset of traces
description: Per ON-038: audit records MUST be produced as a subset of transition records per [execution-model.md §4.4]: the subset where `actor_role` is in a privileged role (per [architecture.md §4.8]) AND `chosen_action` affected policy, role permissions, or budget. No separate audit-log store is introduced; audit is a query over transition-record sibling files and their projections.
close_reason: done

title: All observability operations are mechanism-tagged
description: Per ON-039: every observability operation (health-check evaluation, heartbeat emission, metric emission, log emission, audit-record derivation) MUST be mechanism-tagged per [architecture.md §4.1, §4.2]. Any operation that requires cognition to produce the observability signal MUST be represented as a separate verification node per [architecture.md §4.3], NOT folded into the observability protocol.
close_reason: Spec-sensor tests added in eventshb_sx9r50_test.go: 5-operation fixture (health-check-evaluation, heartbeat-emission, metric-emission, log-emission, audit-record-derivation), cognition-separation obligation text verified ('separate verification node', 'NOT folded into the observability protocol'), architecture §4.3 cross-ref test, Tags: AR-005 sensor. Sibling covers TestON039_SpecSectionExists. Commit 6a73e89.

title: Silent-hang detection obligation + drain-forced synthesis
description: Per ON-040: detection of silent hangs (handler subprocesses producing no output and no heartbeat within bounded window) is obligated under [handler-contract.md §4.6]. This spec names the obligation to ensure operator-observable consequence (`agent_warning_silent_hang` event per [event-model.md §8.3] + subsystem `degraded` classification per ON-037) is not silently deferred. Enforcement mechanism lives in handler-contract. If a drain timeout escalation per ON-029 produces a SIGKILL to a still-running agent subprocess, daemon MUST synthesize an `agent_warning_silent_hang{reason=drain_forced, run_id, node_id}` event prior to the SIGKILL emission, even if no prior silent-hang detection had fired. The synthesis MUST occur within drain step 4's wait window per ON-027.
close_reason: done

title: Multi-daemon commands obligation + per/cross-daemon ceiling distinction
description: Per ON-041: spec-draft pass MUST produce normative definitions for machine-level operator commands: (a) `harmonik list` enumerate running daemons machine-wide with project path, pid, socket path, current status; (b) daemon-identification flags on all daemon-communicating commands (stop, pause, attach, status, upgrade) — at minimum `--socket <path>`, `--cwd <path>`, `--daemon-id <id>`; (c) machine-level agent-subprocess ceiling — cross-daemon bound on concurrently running agent subprocesses enforced by shared lock or machine-level coordinator. **Scope clarification:** per-daemon concurrency ceiling per [process-lifecycle.md §4.5 PL-014a] applies WITHIN one daemon; the machine-level ceiling above is a SEPARATE cross-daemon bound. Multi-tenancy deferral of ON-042 applies to multi-daemon coordination concerns (shared LLM budgets, shared identities, shared skill registries) only; per-daemon and machine-level ceilings are both in-scope for MVH. **Daemon-discovery:** `harmonik list` scans operator's home dir for `**/.harmonik/daemon.pid` (default scope: `$HOME` and any path in `$HARMONIK_PROJECT_ROOTS`). Pidfiles whose socket is unreachable or whose PID does not respond to `kill(pid, 0)` are reported `stale`. **Output columns:** `daemon_id` (project_hash from PL-006a), `project_root`, `pid`, `status`, `socket_path`, `started_at`, `last_exit_code`, `budget_summary` (rolled up to per-daemon totals per ON-049 attribution). Operators MUST be able to filter by status and project-root substring.
close_reason: done

title: Multi-tenancy is explicitly deferred post-MVH
description: Per ON-042: per-project daemon isolation (one daemon per project per [process-lifecycle.md §4.1]) is the MVH answer to multi-tenancy. Per-tenant cost attribution is out of scope for MVH; running N daemons does not auto-partition costs. Acknowledged-and-deferred concerns: shared operator LLM budgets (Anthropic quota is per-account; running N daemons does not create N quotas — machine-level budget coordinator required post-MVH); shared operator identity and auth (`harmonik attach` across N daemons is the same human with same skills and same `br` binary; global install conflicts and access controls are shared concerns); shared skill registries (skills installed machine-wide e.g. `~/.claude/skills/` — provisioning failure in one project is global failure surface). "Deferred" means "not solved by per-project-daemon isolation"; NOT "dismissed."
close_reason: spec-corpus sensor TestON042MultiTenancyDeferredPostMVH in internal/specaudit/on042_multi_tenancy_deferred_test.go; commit c8fe6c7

title: Metrics exposition format is deferred post-MVH
description: Per ON-043: Prom and OTel wire formats for metric exposition are deferred post-MVH. MVH observability is structured logs (ON-035) plus typed events (ON-034). An implementation MAY additionally expose Prom/OTel endpoints but MUST NOT require them for MVH conformance.
close_reason: Spec-sensor tests added in eventshb_sx9r50_test.go: Prometheus named, OpenTelemetry named, MAY-but-MUST-NOT-require posture verified, MVH substrate statement (structured logs + typed events) verified, Tags: AR-005 sensor. TestON043_SpecSectionExists partially subsumed by multidaemon_sx9r83_test.go; remaining structural constraints fully covered. Commit 6a73e89.

title: Commit-hash integrity gate is the MVH binary-install check
description: Per ON-005: pause-to-upgrade path MUST verify the to-be-installed binary's source-commit hash against operator-supplied expected hash before exec-replacement. Check MUST fail-closed: on mismatch or missing hash, daemon MUST NOT exec-replace and MUST remain `paused` with `operator_upgrade_rejected` emitted per [event-model.md §8.7]. Handler binaries installed via [handler-contract.md §4.10] MUST also carry the commit-hash check; this requirement names the daemon-level invariant.
close_reason: done

title: Distributed tracing across daemons is deferred post-MVH
description: Per ON-044: distributed tracing across multiple harmonik instances is deferred post-MVH. Per-project daemon isolation means multi-instance tracing is an OS-process-isolation concern, not a harmonik-code concern — each daemon is a separate process with its own event log and own state. Cross-daemon correlation (if ever needed) is an external-tooling layer, not a foundation spec.
close_reason: spec-corpus sensor TestON044DistributedTracingDeferredPostMVH in internal/specaudit/on044_distributed_tracing_deferred_test.go; commit c8fe6c7

title: `harmonik attach` minimum surface (5 sub-rules)
description: Per ON-050: `harmonik attach` MUST: (a) connect to `.harmonik/daemon.sock` and verify daemon-instance handshake per ON-013b (when ON-013b lands; for MVH, accept any daemon); (b) stream a live event tap (subset of `daemon_*`, `run_*`, `node_*`, `operator_*`, `error` events per [event-model.md §8]) to operator's terminal; (c) present a periodic status snapshot (output of `harmonik status`) every T_attach_status (default 10s, operator-tunable); (d) accept operator commands inline (subset of `pause`, `resume`, `stop`, `enqueue`); (e) detach cleanly on operator SIGINT or `:detach` command without affecting daemon's state. Attach session_id MUST be carried in any operator-command emission for audit-trail correlation per ON-039.
close_reason: SUBSUMED+extended: attach sub-rule tests added in attachsurface_sx9r61_test.go (hk-sx9r.83 had 5-clause + session_id, this adds T_attach_status 10s default, 4 inline commands, 5 event-tap classes, SIGINT/:detach triggers)

title: Multi-attach arbitration
description: Per ON-051: multiple operators MAY attach simultaneously to the same daemon. Each attach session has a unique session_id; each operator-command emission carries the originating session_id. Concurrent commands are serialized per ON-011's mutex discipline; losers observe the post-winner state per ON-013c idempotency. Detach by one operator MUST NOT affect other attached operators or daemon's state.
close_reason: spec-corpus sensor TestON051MultiAttachArbitration in internal/specaudit/on051_multi_attach_arbitration_test.go; commit 7234e06

title: `harmonik status` reports pause-reason discriminator
description: Per ON-054: when daemon is in `paused` (or `pausing`) status per §7.1, `harmonik status` MUST report the pause-reason discriminator: `operator-pause` (issued via `harmonik pause`), `improvement-pause` (per ON-012), `upgrade-prepare` (when `harmonik upgrade` is in progress per ON-019). Discriminator MUST match `operator_pause_status` payload's pause-reason tag (per [event-model.md §6.3]) and is sourced from durable pause-state marker of ON-030a. Operator inspecting `harmonik status` during pause MUST be able to distinguish these three reasons without consulting the event log.
close_reason: done

title: Budgets declared, enforced, attributed cross-subsystem
description: Per ON-045: resource budgets (token, wall-clock, iterations) MUST be declared in policy per [control-points.md §4.5], enforced at dispatch by agent runner per [control-points.md §4.5], and attributed in observability per run, per role, aggregated to per-workflow and per-harmonik-instance. Cost attribution per tenant is out of scope for MVH per ON-042.
close_reason: done

title: Budget events are operator-observable
description: Per ON-046: budget-threshold events (`budget_warning`, `budget_exhausted`, `budget_accrual` per [event-model.md §8.4] and [control-points.md §4.5]) MUST be operator-observable via `harmonik status` and the attach UI per [process-lifecycle.md §4.10]. Operator-observable MUST NOT require parsing raw JSONL; a summarized view is adequate.
close_reason: done

title: Category defaults for resource budgets (5-row table)
description: Per ON-047: every policy MUST have a declared default value for each budget category when a node/role/workflow does not declare one explicitly. Foundation-level category defaults: Token budget (per-run) = 200_000 tokens, applies to any agentic node, override at node/role policy. Wall-clock budget (per-run) = 30 minutes, override at node/role. Iterations budget (per-run) = 50 iterations (tool-use cycles), override at node/role. Wall-clock budget (per-reconciliation-workflow) = 10 minutes, override at RC §4.4 policy. Warning threshold = 80% of budget across all categories (per CP-025). Defaults are operator-configurable via policy schema [control-points.md §6.3] and registered in config inventory per ON-004. Make "no policy declared" a safe state.
close_reason: done

title: Exhaustion protocol (4-step sequence)
description: Per ON-048: on budget exhaustion (any category reaches 100%), enforcing subsystem (agent runner per CP §4.5; reconciliation policy for reconciliation-workflow budgets) MUST: (1) emit `budget_exhausted` per [event-model.md §8.4.3]; emitter MUST tag `category` and `scope` via EV's structured-fields mechanism (payload shape — `run_id`, `session_id?`, `budget_ref`, `attempted_dispatch_cost` — is EV-owned). (2) Terminate in-flight LLM call or tool invocation at next safe boundary (post-chunk for token budgets; post-iteration for iterations budgets; post-step for wall-clock). (3) Route through exhaustion-routing policy: default `pause-and-escalate` — run transitions to failed state with fallback verdict per [reconciliation/spec.md §4.4 RC-018]; daemon MAY enter paused if policy declares `pause-on-exhaustion=true` (default false). (4) Emit `dispatch_deferred` per §8 code 18 if exhaustion cascades to multi-run ceiling breach. Exhaustion protocol is deterministic; pause-vs-escalate decision is per-policy.
close_reason: done

title: Attribution shape (who-consumed-what 5-tuple + delegation_path)
description: Per ON-049: budget attribution MUST be surfaced at conceptual shape `(run_id, role, node_id, category, amount)` plus `delegation_path` identifier per [control-points.md §4.8 CP-039] for cost incurred by a cognition-tagged step, so cost can be traced to the specific model-class invoked. Concrete payload-field placement on `budget_warning`, `budget_consumed`, `budget_exhausted` is owned by [event-model.md §8.4]; ON is normative for the attribution surface (5 fields + `delegation_path` for cognition-tagged steps), EV is normative for on-wire field names and envelope placement. Aggregation levels: per-run (`run_id`), per-role (`role`), per-workflow (`workflow_id`), per-harmonik-instance (total over daemon's lifetime). Aggregation is read-side projection; emission side MUST surface attribution on every budget-affecting operation.
close_reason: done

title: Sensor: N-1 compat window holds across every versioned artifact
description:
Per ON-INV-001: every versioned on-disk or wire artifact declared by foundation specs MUST hold the N-1 readability property of ON-018 simultaneously. A release that breaks N-1 for any single artifact is a migration release per ON-019 and MUST require operator pause for install. Constrains event-model, execution-model, control-points, beads-integration together.
Sensor: corpus-wide compat-matrix test harness — for every artifact declared by foundation specs (event envelope, event payload schemas per [event-model.md §4.7], checkpoint trailer per [execution-model.md §4.4], queue overlay, policy schema per [control-points.md §6.3]), produce writer output at version N and parse at a reader pinned to N-1; failure of ANY pair flips the invariant. Sensor runs corpus-level per [architecture.md §4.1] AR-004.
close_reason: sensor harness landed in 5856de2 (cherry-picked from orphaned worktree commit d13512b) — internal/operatornfr/oninv001_sx9r69_test.go, 9 test functions all green

title: Commit-hash source via build-time ldflags stamp
description: Per ON-005a: daemon's `actual_hash` for ON-005's gate MUST be computed from a build-time embedded ldflags stamp (`-ldflags="-X main.commitHash=<sha>"` at compile). Binaries lacking the embedded stamp MUST fail the integrity gate immediately on `harmonik upgrade` invocation with §8 code 14 (`upgrade-hash-mismatch`); failure mode `failure_mode=binary-stamp-missing` distinguishes this from operator-supplied-hash-mismatch. Operator's `expected_commit_hash` source is operator-discretion (Slack, release-page, `git rev-parse`); ON-005's gate is a version-identity check, not cryptographic-integrity (signing is post-MVH per ON-006).
close_reason: done

title: Sensor: secrets never appear in durable sinks unredacted
description:
Per ON-INV-003: for every event-model-declared sink (event log per [event-model.md §4.4], dead-letter log per [event-model.md §4.3], session log per [workspace-model.md §4.7]), secrets MUST NOT appear unredacted. The invariant holds jointly across ON-022, ON-023, and the handler-contract secrets-injection rule — losing any one breaks the invariant.
Sensor: two-part — (a) compile-time schema linter (per ON-023) that rejects any payload field typed as `Secret`; (b) regression test harness that writes each durable sink under a fixture whose secrets-injection set is known, then scans sink's output for any fixture-secret substring. Sensor failure on either part flips the invariant. WM session-log cite is informational per F-pilot-ON-6.
close_reason: done

title: Sensor: every subsystem reports its reconstruction contribution
description:
Per ON-INV-005: every subsystem MUST expose a reconstruction-contribution interface such that restart-recovery per ON-030 can enumerate and verify its part of the reconstructed state. Specific interface (Go method or startup-probe event) is per subsystem, but invariant requires: (a) NO subsystem reconstructs silently, (b) every subsystem's reconstruction terminates (bounded) before daemon emits `ready`, (c) any subsystem that cannot reconstruct MUST cause daemon to fail startup with a categorized exit code (per §8) rather than enter silently-partial `ready` state.
Sensor: fixture-backed restart-recovery test harness — inject a known pre-restart state across every subsystem, restart daemon, assert each subsystem emits a reconstruction-completed signal before `ready`. Missing or silent reconstruction flips the invariant.
close_reason: done

title: Sensor: no subsystem introduces a control surface bypassing between-task invariant
description:
Per ON-INV-006: no subsystem MAY introduce a new control surface (CLI command, API endpoint, signal handler, socket protocol action) that aborts an in-flight run without routing through `stop --immediate` per ON-009 OR the drain-gated pause/upgrade path of ON-008. Subsystems MUST NOT add local escape-hatches (e.g., `kill-run`, `abandon-run`, `skip-reconciliation`) that bypass drain gate or reconciliation carve-out.
Sensor: corpus-wide grep-plus-reviewer audit — every subsystem spec's §4.a Subsystem envelope (per [architecture.md §4.4] AR-013) is inspected for operator-control-affecting operations; any such operation not routing through state machine of §7.1 flips the invariant. Reviewer-enforced pending mechanical lint for control-surface declarations.
close_reason: ON-INV-006 sensor added: spec-text binding + CLI/socket-op/signal allowlist guards in internal/specaudit/oninv006_no_control_surface_bypass_test.go

title: ON exit-code taxonomy (§8) — 23-code authoritative table
description: Implement the §8 exit-code taxonomy: 23 codes (0=success, 1=generic-failure, 2=queue-format-unsupported, 3=checkpoint-schema-unsupported, 4=event-schema-unsupported, 5=pidfile-locked, 6=socket-bind-failed, 7=git-bad-state, 8=beads-unavailable, 9=filesystem-unwritable, 10=disk-full, 11=drain-timeout-escalated, 12=rto-hard-ceiling-exceeded, 13=upgrade-requires-paused, 14=upgrade-hash-mismatch, 15=upgrade-schema-incompatible, 16=operator-control-invalid-state, 17=multi-daemon-target-missing, 18=machine-ceiling-exhausted, 19=runtime-panic, 20=signal-terminated, 21=drain-step-errored, 22=ntm-unavailable, 23=orchestrator-agent-unavailable). For each: failure category, detection rule, emitted event (if any), remediation pointer. Taxonomy is AUTHORITATIVE for the corpus — PL §8.x and other-spec consumers cite ON §8 by reference (per ON-002 obligation; cross-references resolve here per §10.1 conformance bootstrap). Code-to-category mapping MUST be stable across releases within the N-1 window per ON-001. Additions MAY land within the N-1 window as long as existing mappings remain stable. Per discipline v0.9 §2.11(c.2), edges run `<consumer-req> → on-error.taxonomy`; the taxonomy bead's own predecessors are limited to schema-shaped beads it consumes (none in this pilot — the taxonomy is leaf-shaped on the producer side). Cross-spec consumer→on-error.taxonomy edges from PL/RC/etc. live in those pilots, not here.
close_reason: Landed at eedf498: operatornfr/exitcode.go 24-entry ExitCodes registry (codes 0-23) with all six per-code fields per ON §8; LookupExitCode + FixtureAndRegistryAgree cross-check tying production to .74 fixture. Reviewer APPROVE; compound-event spec rows (codes 10/19/22) encoded as primary-event single-string consistently with fixture (known simplification).

Verify config-inventory artifact existence; verify startup failure-mode catalog co-owned with PL §4.2 covers all enumerated prerequisite failures; verify `harmonik status` resolves §8 entries on every non-zero exit observed.

title: queue append: human-readable default output; --json opt-in
description:
Depends on: hk-5553i

## Current behavior

harmonik queue append writes raw JSON response to stdout unconditionally.
Code path: internal/queue/cli/append.go RunQueueAppend() → client.go handleResponse() (line 136).
handleResponse() at client.go:143 calls fmt.Fprintf(out, "%s\n", data) with the daemon's raw JSON.

## Desired behavior

Default: human-readable confirmation. Example output (success):

  appended: 1 bead(s) to group 0 (queue_id=019fbd3e-...)
  hk-xyz789  added

Example output (validation error):

  error: append rejected (code -32011)
  reason: queue_id mismatch — expected 019fbd3e-... got 00000000-...

With --json / --format json: existing raw JSON behavior, unchanged.

## Implementation sketch

1. Add --json / --format flags to the extraFlagFn callback in RunQueueAppend (append.go:31).
2. Add a renderQueueAppendResponse function that extracts appended bead IDs, group index, queue_id from result JSON.
3. On error, render code + message as plain prose.
4. --json path unchanged.
close_reason: queue append: human-readable default implemented; --json opt-in works

title: Daemon misfires no_commit when implementer DID commit (timing race in commit detection)
description: ROOT CAUSE (investigated 2026-05-27): Implementer emits ZERO agent_heartbeat events (0 of 269 in events.jsonl join to these run_ids). Watchdog pasteInjectQuitOnCommit (internal/daemon/pasteinject.go:382-453) runs blind, falls back to hasChildProcess (pasteinject.go:130-137 = pgrep -P shell-pid, DIRECT children only). During claude deep-think with no tool subprocess, PaneHasActiveProcess (tmuxsubstrate.go:458-471) returns false, fireNoChangePath suppression is skipped, SendQuitToLastPane+Kill fire. git commit lands during kill race (commit timestamps 2-6min AFTER no_commit verdict). Detection site reviewloop.go:493-527 is correct; bug is upstream watchdog killing too early. FIX: (1) PRIMARY wire implementer agent_heartbeat emission so 8-min staleness clock resets per turn; (2) DEFENSE make hasChildProcess recursive (descendant tree) or check pane CPU/output activity. NOT a concurrency bug — per-run panes are isolated.
close_reason: Landed 7cbbc44, reviewer APPROVED. Root cause: tmux exec's sh into claude so pane PID=claude with no children during think-phase; hasChildProcess now also matches agent-named self PID

title: NQ-B1: two-level capacity gate + round-robin cross-queue dispatch policy
description: Component B (highest risk -- load-bearing dispatch path). Today workloop.go:618 has a single global gate runRegistry.Len()>=effectiveMax over ONE queue. Add QueueName to RunHandle (runregistry.go:32-61); per-queue tally = filtered Len(), bare Len() stays the global ceiling. Dispatch = min(group_pending, per_queue_workers minus queue_running, global_cap minus global_running). Cross-queue policy: name-ordered round-robin among active queues with pending work under their own cap; the cursor MUST be daemon-state advancing EVERY tick (NOT reset to 0 -- else 'investigate' sorts first and starves 'main'). Per-queue workers default = today's --max-concurrent, MUST be <= global cap; oversubscription allowed + logged once as a warning at create/submit. Spec delta: rewrite QM-062; new per-queue-worker-count rule + cross-queue-dispatch-policy rule (explicitly NOT weighted fairness -- that is N3/v0.2). Files: types.go (Queue.Workers), runregistry.go, workloop.go:618,670-744, daemon.go, supervise/config.go:70. Done: main=3 + investigate=1 with global cap 4 -> at most 4 sessions ever, no starvation over a long run; tests green.

title: pasteinject quit-on-commit timeout fires no recovery — daemon hangs forever on sess.Wait when claude self-quits without committing
description: Follow-up to hk-ocbh2 (noChange-misclassification). Observed in session 2026-05-20 batch 1: hk-2m3bq's work was already merged earlier as commit 3e8a0a2. Claude correctly detected nothing-to-do and typed /quit. Stop hook fired. But the daemon hung: pasteinject.go:98 commitPollTimeout = 10min fires only a stderr log + goroutine return (pasteinject.go:134-138); waitsocketgrace.go:65-115 calls sess.Wait(ctx) which blocks until the tmux pane closes. The comment at pasteinject.go:114-115 says 'waitWithSocketGrace will eventually time out via stopHookGrace (3s)' — that's wrong; stopHookGrace only applies AFTER sess.Wait returns. So when /quit doesn't actually close the pane (or stop-hook didn't deliver outcome correctly), the daemon waits forever. Required recovery: when commitPollTimeout fires, (1) send /quit via SendQuitToLastPane unconditionally, (2) if sess.Wait still blocks 30s later, sess.Kill, (3) inspect HEAD for a 'Refs: <bead-id>' trailer in recent main commits — if found, close-as-subsumed; otherwise mark failed with reason=noChange-timeout and advance the queue. Cites: pasteinject.go:93-156, waitsocketgrace.go:40-115, workloop.go:1247-1349. Source: harmonik dogfood batch 1 session 2026-05-20; analysis agent a43001e.
close_reason: done

title: DOGFOOD-BLOCKER: BrUnavailable persists past 10-retry widening — root cause not transient
description:
2026-05-19 dogfood #3 (hk-pxrv6) — VALIDATION RUN for hk-ekz5v's 3-retry → 10-retry widening (commit 73ade77).

Outcome: same failure. BrUnavailable after 10 retries (vs. previous 3). Work itself landed (54fa28a = HC-057 keep-alive spec). The widened budget did NOT fix it.

  daemon: workloop: CloseBead hk-pxrv6: brcli.terminalTransitionWrite:
    br exec: brcli: BrUnavailable persisted after 10 retries: brcli: Unavailable

Timing:
- 09:22:34 agent_ready
- 09:26:10 outcome_emitted (claude produced work)
- 09:27:33 agent_heartbeat (1m 23s post-outcome — daemon doing post-work)
- 09:28:11 run_failed (~38s after heartbeat; consistent with 10 retries × 50ms-2s backoff)
- 09:28:12 queue_paused

This means the BrUnavailable is NOT a transient SQLite-busy. It's something STRUCTURAL:
(a) A separate br process is holding the DB lock for ~14+ seconds.
(b) The daemon's own brAdapter has a held transaction that conflicts with itself.
(c) The error class is mislabeled — actually a different failure (binary-not-found, permission denied, etc.) and the diagnostic 'Unavailable' is the catch-all.

NEXT INVESTIGATION:
1. Wrap `br exec` to log the underlying stderr/stdout of the failed invocations. Look at the ACTUAL error message, not the abstracted 'Unavailable'.
2. `lsof` snapshot at the time of CloseBead — who holds .beads/beads.db?
3. Check if the daemon's brAdapter long-lived connection holds a write transaction that conflicts with the CloseBead subprocess.

Severity: P0 — every successful dogfood run requires manual close, blocking unattended Phase-2 operation.

This is the THIRD half-built-systems instance in dogfood-2/3 testing: code shipped + tested + reviewer-APPROVED but breaks at runtime under realistic conditions.
close_reason: Diagnostic surfaced at ed3a1d7: per-attempt brErr class + exit + stderr now in escalation message. Next dogfood will reveal real failure mode (DbLocked vs Unavailable).

title: workloop: completion path never persists queue mutations or calls CompleteAndUnlink
description: evaluateGroupAdvanceWithOutcome (workloop.go:1419-1498) mutates the in-memory queue (item status, group status, queue status) and emits events, but never calls queue.Persist. Two consequences: (1) On daemon crash mid-drain, queue.json on disk still reflects pre-dispatch state — restart re-dispatches completed items. (2) When the final group reaches complete-success, q.Status is left as active; no code transitions queue → QueueStatusCompleted and no code calls queue.CompleteAndUnlink (only test code calls it; grep shows zero production callers in internal/daemon, internal/lifecycle, cmd/). Consequence: queue.json never gets unlinked after drain (QM-003 / QM-053 violated), and on restart the daemon would re-load and re-process the just-finished queue. The dispatch-time mutation at workloop.go:558 (lq.SetQueue) also never persists, so a crash between dispatch-stamp and outcome leaves item RunID/status only in memory. Fix: persist after each mutation; on final all-groups-terminal, call CompleteAndUnlink + queueStore.ClearQueue.
close_reason: Fixed in 40eeb9b: workloop now Persists after dispatch-stamp/RunID-patch/claim-revert mutations; calls CompleteAndUnlink + ClearQueue on all-groups-complete. New SubmitThenDrainAndUnlink test.

title: Scenario commit-gate (hk-i2ie5) false-blocks on timeout/compile-error; gates pre-rebase outside mergeMu
description: LIVE REPRO 2026-06-02: hk-81n9r (reviewed APPROVE) was FALSE-BLOCKED by the scenario gate: scenario_gate_failed go test -race -tags=scenario ./internal/daemon/...: signal: killed. The gate runs the FULL daemon scenario suite under -race, which OOMs/times-out and gets SIGKILLed; the gate treats killed as RED and blocks the merge even though the code passed review. This BLOCKS every scenario-test-touching bead from landing via the daemon (hk-81n9r, hk-sul12, hk-eun55 all qualify). FIX: the scenario gate must FAIL-OPEN (non-block + log a warning) on gate-INFRASTRUCTURE failures — signal:killed (OOM/SIGKILL), context.DeadlineExceeded (timeout), and compile failures — distinguished from a genuine RED test result (only a real test FAILURE blocks). ALSO: drop -race from the gate (too heavy for the full suite) and scope the gate to only the affected package; run the gate inside mergeMu (it currently gates the pre-rebase worktree outside the lock). IMPLEMENTATION NOTE: test this via UNIT tests of the gate decision logic (scenariogate_test.go, NOT a new //go:build scenario test) so this fix does not self-trigger the gate it is fixing. Source: controlpoints review of hk-i2ie5 + named-queues live repro. Refs: hk-i2ie5.

title: Agent comms through harmonik: agent_message bus event + harmonik comms send/recv (delivered via subscribe)
description: Step 3 of the multi-agent comms ladder. v0 = shared AGENT_COMMS.md; v1 = per-agent outboxes at .harmonik/comms/NAME.md (NOW WORKING this session); Step 3 = route comms through harmonik itself. Motivation: concurrent Claude sessions (flywheel, named-queues) coordinating hit append-races, tail-replay-on-rewrite, and escape-detector false-positives from untracked repo-root files. Proposal: add an agent_message event type on the event bus; agents publish via a harmonik comms send verb (with a recipient) and receive via the existing harmonik subscribe (already NDJSON + heartbeats). Benefits: total ordering, zero append-races, durable typed log in events.jsonl, recipient addressing/filtering, no working-tree files so no escape-detector interaction, and generalizes to N agents (the outbox tail hardcodes peers). Cross-cutting: event-model + a CLI verb (maybe a node type). Co-design with the named-queues thread which owns the event-model/daemon lane. Likely a kerf spec work (codename agent-comms).

title: Run claude-code handler subprocesses inside tmux panes for live inspection
description:
## Problem
When hk dispatches a real claude CLI subprocess via the claude-code handler, the operator has no way to watch the subprocess in real time. We want each handler subprocess to run inside a tmux pane (or window) the operator can attach to and observe.

## Motivation
- Live debug of what claude is doing inside the worktree during dogfood smokes.
- User has standing reminder (HANDOFF v34, v35) to enable this; we are now running hk itself inside a tmux session, so the substrate is in place.
- Required precondition for the DOT slow-roll experiments (multi-agent arrangements; one node = ralph review-loop) — operator needs to be able to attach to any single agent's pane.

## Acceptance
- When the claude-code handler spawns the claude subprocess, the subprocess runs inside a named tmux pane/window that the operator can attach to.
- Naming convention is predictable enough that a `tmux attach -t <name>` derived from run_id / bead_id is documented.
- Lifecycle: pane is created on subprocess start, removed (or marked dead) on subprocess exit.
- Behavior is opt-in via config (default-off) so headless/CI runs don't require tmux.
- Documented in handler spec or a new dogfood note.

## Notes
- Existing hk codebase already references tmux in several files (lifecycle/runnerentrypoint, orphansweep, eventbus, etc.) — start by auditing what's there before adding new wiring.
- May overlap with daemonization work (locked decision 2026-05-08); coordinate so the daemon's pane-management story is consistent.

## Refs
- HANDOFF.md v35 "Carry-forward reminders" — tmux integration line.
- HANDOFF.md v34 "DOT experiments" — multi-agent inspection requires pane attach.
- internal/lifecycle/runnerentrypoint_pl028_test.go and siblings — existing tmux references.
close_reason: Superseded by hk-gql20 bridge-integration epic (Stream B handles tmux pane integration as part of the full bridge wiring)

title: Spec drift: queue-model.md §8.7 input-event vocabulary doesn't match Go EventType names
description:
Surfaced by hk-9als7 reviewer 2026-05-18. specs/queue-model.md §8.7 lists input events as operator_pausing / operator_paused / operator_stopping / operator_resuming, but internal/core/eventtype.go declares: EventTypeOperatorPauseStatus (operator_pause_status, consolidated with a status enum) + EventTypeOperatorResuming + EventTypeOperatorStopped (post-fact only). No operator_pausing / operator_paused / operator_stopping Go EventTypes exist.

The hk-9als7 implementation correctly used the actual Go types; the spec needs to align. Two options:
(a) Amend the spec to list operator_pause_status (with status: pausing|paused) + operator_resuming as the input events, drop operator_pausing/operator_paused/operator_stopping references.
(b) Add Go EventType constants matching the spec names (heavier change).

Prefer (a) — the consolidated event-type is already the implementation.

title: MVH-P3: hanging twin binary
description:
EXPLORATORY_TESTING_PLAN.md §4 P3. A Go binary that starts cleanly, writes nothing to stdout, and hangs (e.g., select{} after handling SIGINT trap) until SIGKILL. Models "subprocess hung" for T2/T3.

Location: NEW `test/twins/hang/main.go`.

Acceptance:
- Builds cleanly: `go build -o /tmp/twin-hang ./test/twins/hang`.
- After start: no stdout, no exit. Process stays alive indefinitely.
- Responds correctly to SIGKILL (i.e., the OS reaps it; not catching SIGKILL is the default — just don't install a handler).

Out of scope: SIGTERM trap behavior — for hang scenarios we WANT the test to need to escalate to SIGKILL. A separate "twin-slow-shutdown" binary may be added later if needed.

title: claude subprocess receives no task context: bead body, workspace, and workflow not delivered
description:
## Problem
Even after LaunchSpec delivery (hk-keb6o), claude does not know HOW to behave as a harmonik handler. The real claude CLI:
1. Does not know to emit agent_ready NDJSON on stdout.
2. Does not know to read the bead body via br show.
3. Does not know to write artifacts to workspace_path.
4. Does not know the progress-stream protocol (HC-007).

This is a protocol/integration gap that requires either:
(a) A CLAUDE.md / system-prompt in the worktree that teaches claude the harmonik handler contract, OR
(b) HandlerArgs injecting --system-prompt / --print / --output-format=stream-json flags, OR
(c) A thin wrapper script around claude that handles the protocol translation.

## Observed in smoke-2 (2026-05-12)
claude ran in the worktree, received empty stdin, produced 0 bytes of output, exited 0. Bead was closed as success with no actual work done.

## Acceptance
Define and implement the mechanism by which a claude subprocess in a harmonik worktree learns: (1) what work to do (bead body), (2) where to write artifacts (workspace_path), (3) how to signal readiness/completion (progress-stream protocol or exit-code-only mode).

## Refs
internal/handler/adapter_claudecode.go; docs/dogfood-smoke-trace.md §6; specs/handler-contract.md §4.2.HC-007; hk-keb6o (prerequisite)
close_reason: All 11 sub-beads closed (CHB env-var schema, forbidden-flag guard, session-id mint, settings.json materialization, hook-relay, twin-claude binary, handler-process CHB-006..024, stop-hook dedup, relay semantics). Umbrella resolved — hk-w5vra.7 confirmed smoke re-run; remaining integration test tracked separately in hk-1n0cw

title: Rename existing twin to generic test handler
description:
## Problem
The existing twin (harmonik-twin / handler.TwinHandler) emits harmonik-native NDJSON progress-stream messages directly. Per CHB-021 amendment this is the wrong shape to call 'the claude twin' — it tests the back half of the pipeline only. Rename it to make its narrow scope explicit (generic test handler / harmonik-twin-generic) so a new harmonik-twin-claude can be built alongside per CHB-021.

## Acceptance
- Rename binary cmd dir and Go types/files. No behavior change.
- Update all test imports + references in docs and specs that name the twin.
- go test ./... clean. go vet, gofmt clean.

## Refs
specs/claude-hook-bridge.md CHB-021, CHB-022 (twin parity rules).
docs/dogfood-smoke-run-2026-05-12.md §10 Gap B (why this rename matters).
close_reason: Renamed cmd/harmonik-twin-claude → cmd/harmonik-twin-generic; all references updated; go build/vet/test clean

title: CHB amendment: concurrent handler+relay socket serialization semantics
description:
## Problem
Per spec-review finding 7.1. CHB-022 declares the daemon twin-blind with zero 'if isTwin' branches. But a real run produces N+1 concurrent socket connections (handler long-lived + N relay one-shots) while a twin run is 1 connection. The watcher's serialization semantics for concurrent arrivals are nowhere specified — yet CHB-022's twin-parity claim depends on identical wire-level output.

## Acceptance
- Amend specs/claude-hook-bridge.md §4.6 (or specs/handler-contract.md HC-045b) to spec the daemon socket acceptor's serialization: how messages from concurrent connections are ordered into the per-session event stream (FIFO per arrival? merge by emitted_at_ns? per-connection ordered, across-connection unordered?).
- Verify twin parity in conformance §10: a real run and a twin run produce same wire sequence (modulo timestamps), implying the serialization rule is observable.
- Update hk-w5vra.6 (daemon durability) work to satisfy the chosen rule.

## Refs
docs/review-claude-hook-bridge-spec.md Finding 7.1.
close_reason: CHB-025 spec amendment committed: per-connection FIFO, across-connection unordered rule. SHA 0f6570a.

title: CHB-025 implementation: daemon last-received-wins dedup for outcome_emitted (watcher field + drop-after-close)
description:
## Problem
CHB-025 (specs/claude-hook-bridge.md §4.10) specifies that the daemon watcher MUST apply last-received-wins dedup for outcome_emitted messages keyed by (run_id, claude_session_id). This bead implements that in the daemon watcher.

## Acceptance
1. The per-session watcher struct holds a latestOutcome field (type *OutcomeEmittedPayload or equivalent) updated on each outcome_emitted receipt within the open session window.
2. After cmd.Wait() returns, the watcher uses latestOutcome (not the first) for terminal-event derivation per CHB-020.
3. Any outcome_emitted arriving after the session window is closed (cmd.Wait() returned) is dropped via the existing unknown_session typed-error path per §6.2 CHB-025.
4. The implementation goroutine is the watcher goroutine (no additional locking).

## Refs
specs/claude-hook-bridge.md §4.10 CHB-025

title: Build harmonik-twin-claude per CHB-021
description:
## Problem
There is no test double for the real claude CLI's lifecycle. Per CHB-021 the twin MUST emit the same NDJSON wire sequence the real handler+relay combo would produce for a claude session — WITHOUT spawning a hook-relay subprocess and WITHOUT materializing .claude/settings.json. Scenario-script-driven.

## Acceptance
- New cmd/harmonik-twin-claude/ binary.
- Scenario scripts cover: single-mode happy path; 3-iteration review-loop; rate-limit (StopFailure error_type=rate_limit); dial-failed; daemon-not-ready; claude exits 0 without outcome_emitted; claude crashes non-zero.
- Daemon is twin-blind (CHB-022) — zero 'if isTwin' branches in daemon code; routes by (run_id, claude_session_id) only.
- Reads a scenario name from env or arg, drives emission via the same UDS connection path the relay+handler would.
- Tests: at minimum one end-to-end run-loop test that exercises the daemon via harmonik-twin-claude scenario=single-happy-path.

## Refs
specs/claude-hook-bridge.md CHB-021 (twin emission), CHB-022 (twin-blind daemon), §10 Conformance (scenario list).
hk-1n0cw.2 smoke surfaced why the old twin is inadequate.
notes: Reopened: 9b6f397 was built on a pre-rename base. NEW files (scenarios.go, e2e_chb021_test.go) preserved at /tmp/. Fix-up agent to dispatch with corrected context.
close_reason: done

title: Implement harmonik hook-relay subcommand (CHB-010..017, CHB-INV-003)
description:
## Problem
Per CHB-010..017 the main harmonik binary must expose 'harmonik hook-relay <event-kind>' that reads Claude's per-hook JSON on stdin, builds one NDJSON progress-stream message, and ships it to the daemon socket per the one-shot connection regime in HC-045b.

## Acceptance
- 'harmonik hook-relay {SessionStart,Stop,SessionEnd,StopFailure,Notification}' implemented.
- Other event-kinds: no-op exit 0 (CHB-011).
- Stdin schema validated (CHB-012) — session_id and hook_event_name mismatches → exit 1 with named sub-reason on stderr.
- Mapping table per CHB-013 implemented exactly (including the reviewer-verdict file read at CHB-014).
- 5s dial timeout, 5s read deadline, daemon-not-ready retry per CHB-016, exit codes per CHB-017.
- Tests: canned-stdin/canned-env per event-kind; daemon-not-ready retry test; dial-failed test; malformed-payload test.
- Mechanism-tagged — no cognition.

## Refs

title: Implement .claude/settings.json materialization in workspace manager (CHB-001..005)
description:
## Problem
The workspace manager must write ${workspace_path}/.claude/settings.json declaring the bridge-required hook entries, between WM-003 (worktree creation) and WM-016 (workspace_leased emission), folded into the WM-026 atomic-write discipline.

## Acceptance
- Hook entries per CHB-003 (SessionStart, Stop, SessionEnd, StopFailure, Notification → harmonik hook-relay <kind> with 30s timeout, type=command/args form).
- User-settings merge per CHB-004 (append bridge entries; overwrite if malformed JSON; strip disableAllHooks:true).
- Gitignore hygiene per CHB-005.
- Atomic write per WM-026; parent-dir fsync BEFORE workspace_leased emits.
- Tests: clean-workspace materialization; merge-with-existing-user-hooks; malformed-existing-JSON; disableAllHooks-strip.

## Refs

title: Implement claude-code handler-process responsibilities (CHB-006..009, CHB-018..020, CHB-024)
description:
## Problem
The harmonik handler-process for agent_type=claude-code must set up env, mint/resume claude_session_id, perform pre-Claude-exec emission of four progress-stream messages, run a timer-driven heartbeat, map Wait-return to terminal events, and verify settings hierarchy.

## Acceptance
- CHB-006 env-var schema set on the Claude subprocess (HARMONIK_RUN_ID, _DAEMON_SOCKET, _WORKSPACE_PATH, _HANDLER_SESSION_ID, _CLAUDE_SESSION_ID, _WORKFLOW_ID, _NODE_ID, _AGENT_TYPE; optional _WORKFLOW_MODE/_PHASE/_ITERATION_COUNT/_BEAD_ID; HARMONIK_SECRET_* redacted).
- CHB-007 forbidden flags blocked (--fork-session, --bare, --no-session-persistence; env CLAUDE_CODE_SKIP_PROMPT_HISTORY blocked).
- CHB-008/009 claude_session_id mint (UUIDv7) for single/implementer-initial/reviewer; reuse from LaunchSpec for implementer-resume; reviewer always fresh.
- CHB-018 pre-Claude-exec emission ordering: handler_capabilities → session_log_location → skills_provisioned → agent_ready BEFORE exec'ing claude.
- CHB-019 timer-driven agent_heartbeat at 300s while Claude alive.
- CHB-020 Wait-return terminal-event mapping (WORK_COMPLETE/REVIEWER_VERDICT → agent_completed; FAILURE_SIGNAL → agent_failed with class+sub_reason; missing outcome → agent_failed with claude_exit_without_outcome / claude_crashed).
- CHB-024 startup verification of .claude/settings.local.json shadowing; fail-fast with bridge_settings_shadowed.
- Tests against harmonik-twin-claude scenarios (hk-w5vra.2).

## Refs
specs/claude-hook-bridge.md §4.2, §4.3, §4.7, §4.9; specs/handler-contract.md §5 HC-INV-002/004/006/007.
close_reason: Implemented CHB-006..009/018..020/024 in internal/handler/claudehandler_chb006_024.go; tests green on ea4464f

title: Daemon persists claude_session_id to Run.context before claude exec (CHB-023)
description:
## Problem
On receipt of the handler's handler_capabilities progress-stream message carrying claude_session_id, the daemon must persist it into Run.context.claude_session_id with checkpoint-commit-class durability (EM-023a) BEFORE returning the connection-accept ACK that gates 'claude --session-id <uuid>' exec. Otherwise a mid-launch crash can leave (a) no persisted id but a real Claude session orphaned in the world, or (b) inconsistent durability.

## Acceptance
- Daemon socket acceptor extracts claude_session_id from handler_capabilities payload (HC-009 + CHB-008).
- Run.context.claude_session_id written and a transition_event landed to git before the ACK that releases the handler to exec claude.
- Recovery test: kill daemon between handler_capabilities and ACK; restart; verify either no-id-persisted OR id-persisted-and-resumable; never split-brain.
- Existing single/review-loop tests still pass.

## Refs
specs/claude-hook-bridge.md §4.6 CHB-023; specs/execution-model.md §4.3 EM-012/EM-015d, §4.5 EM-023a, §4.7 EM-031.
close_reason: done

title: CHB amendment: Stop-hook dedup gate (relay or daemon idempotency)
description:
## Problem
Per spec-review finding 2.1 (docs/review-claude-hook-bridge-spec.md). Claude's Stop hook fires once per turn-loop completion. In a multi-turn session, the relay emits multiple outcome_emitted messages with no deduplication. Spec is silent on what the daemon watcher does with a second outcome_emitted from the same session.

## Acceptance
Decide and spec one of:
- (a) Relay gate: relay reads transcript_path or local state to determine if THIS is the final Stop; first-Stop emits no message, only the run-ending Stop emits outcome_emitted.
- (b) Daemon idempotency: watcher accepts outcome_emitted but treats only the LAST one (or FIRST one + ignore-rest) as authoritative per (run_id, claude_session_id).

Amend specs/claude-hook-bridge.md §4.5 (or add a new CHB requirement). Update hk-w5vra.3 (relay) and/or hk-w5vra.6 (daemon) implementations accordingly.

## Refs
docs/review-claude-hook-bridge-spec.md Finding 2.1.
close_reason: amended with CHB-025 (option b — daemon last-received-wins), follow-up bead hk-w5vra.11 filed for implementation

title: CHB amendment: relay OOM-kill mid-write error taxonomy entry
description:
## Problem
Per spec-review finding 3.1. If the relay process is killed after opening the daemon socket but before writing its NDJSON line, the daemon receives an abrupt EOF with no routable (run_id, claude_session_id). The recovery via CHB-020 'no outcome_emitted' branch is correct but the partial-connection case is silent in §8.

## Acceptance
- Add a §8 error-taxonomy entry: sub_reason 'bridge_partial_write' (or similar), class ErrTransient.
- Add a brief CHB requirement defining daemon behavior on abrupt EOF before envelope arrival (drop the connection silently, no terminal event since session is unidentifiable).
- No code change required if existing socket-acceptor already drops orphan connections; verify under test.

## Refs
docs/review-claude-hook-bridge-spec.md Finding 3.1.
close_reason: done

title: Exploratory-test hygiene sweep: 5 pre-existing failures observed v32–v33
description:
## Scope

Sweep over 5 pre-existing test failures that surfaced opportunistically during workflow-modes implementation (v32–v33). None block any current bead; collected here so they don't rot.

## Failures to triage

1. **`TestRunEM012_Spec*` (`internal/core`)** — fixture-clipping picks up the EM-012a paragraph instead of EM-012. Observed during T-WM-002. Likely: fix the clip boundary in the test fixture or the harness regex.
2. **`TestHC004LaunchIdempotentOnRunIDNodeID` (`internal/specaudit`)** — spec text the test scans for is not present in the spec. Either the assertion is stale (spec moved) or the spec is missing language the test expects. Observed during T-WM-004.
3. **`TestT4_ReopenThenRedispatch`** — flaky exploratory; needs root-cause or quarantine with a clear annotation. Observed during hk-7s9z9.
4. **`TestT6_UnicodeHeavyBody`** — long-running, timeout-prone. Either reduce fixture size or split into a `-short`-gated long test. Observed during T-WM-008.
5. **`TestT6_10BeadSequentialDrain`** — pre-existing timing flake. Same treatment options as #4. Observed during hk-5wbzj.

## Approach

Per failure: classify as (a) fix in place, (b) quarantine with `t.Skip` + TODO + follow-up bead, or (c) deletion if the test no longer reflects intended behaviour. Document the decision in the commit body.

## Acceptance

- All 5 entries resolved (fixed, quarantined, or deleted) with rationale recorded.
- `go test ./...` produces no new pre-existing-failures noise for these names.

## Pointers

- v33 HANDOFF.md "Pre-existing test failures observed this session" — original list
close_reason: All 5 tests resolved: HC-004 needle fixed (stale phrase → spec.run_id,spec.node_id); other 4 were already passing at HEAD

description:

Target files: specs/handler-contract.md (new §4.2a + §5.6) AND specs/execution-model.md (EM-005 v0.3.3 → v0.3.4 bump + EM-005a enum extension + new EM-005b/c).

- NIT (CI-6): Amend EM §6.1 RECORD Outcome.payload row at execution-model.md:1086 to widen its type, OR amend the §6.1 INFORMATIVE note at execution-model.md:1103 to clarify VerdictPayload as a union alias of VerdictEvent | GateDecisionPayload. Prefer the latter (preserves EM-005a's umbrella-alias design).

title: T3 finding: ReopenBead cannot recover in_progress bead after SIGINT/SIGTERM mid-run
description: brcli.ReopenBead calls `br reopen` which only transitions closed→open. When SIGINT/SIGTERM kills the in-flight handler, the bead is in in_progress state. `br reopen` on an in_progress bead is skipped by br ("already in_progress"). The bead remains stuck in in_progress after the daemon shuts down cleanly. On the next daemon start, brcli.Ready() (which returns open beads only) will not see this bead; it is stranded. Correct fix: ReopenBead should use `br update --status open` for in_progress→open, or the work loop must detect the bead status before choosing reopen vs unclaim. Reproduced reliably by TestT3_SIGINTMidRun and TestT3_SIGTERMMidRun.
close_reason: ReopenBead now uses br update --status open; covers in_progress→open for crash-recovery after SIGINT/SIGTERM

title: Add a TRUE concurrent locked-path regression guard for the hk-zguy6 escape-detector fix: two-goroutine test driving the LOCKED beadRunOne escape check vs an in-flight sibling merge, asserting no implementer_escaped_worktree fires (current predates-baseline test is characterization-only — passes with/without the production lock)

title: CloseBead errors silently discarded in workloop.go — run_completed emitted on close failure causing JSONL/bead-state split-brain
description: workloop.go:278 uses _ = deps.brAdapter.CloseBead(...) discarding the error. After CloseBead fails the loop calls emitRunCompleted(success=true) anyway, then continues to the next bead. Result: JSONL records run_completed=true while the bead remains in_progress or un-closed in the DB. Reconciliation on next startup will detect the split-brain but operator JSONL tooling reads the incorrect success event. Repro: TestT4_CloseBeadError and TestT4_EventOrderingOnCloseError in internal/daemon/t4_exploratory_test.go (T4 exploratory wave). Spec ref: workloop.go:278; specs/event-model.md §8.1.
close_reason: workloop now checks CloseBead error and emits run_failed instead of run_completed to prevent JSONL/bead-state split-brain

title: run_stale event: daemon emits when active run has no events for M minutes (configurable)
description: Periodic scan of active runs in workloop. When an active run has no event of any kind for M minutes (default 10, configurable per-queue via stale_after_seconds in queue JSON, overridable per-bead via stale_after label), emit run_stale event with payload {run_id, bead_id, age_seconds, last_event_type, last_event_at, snapshot:{active_run_count, goroutine_count}}. Emit once at M, re-emit at 2M, 4M (exponential, not spammy). Lands in events.jsonl for replay. Orchestrator's Monitor grep matches like any other event and decides whether to kill. Independent of subscribe-op (useful via JSONL tail). New EventTypeRunStale constant in internal/core/eventtype.go; payload type in eventreg_hqwn59.go; ~120 LOC stalewatch.go.

title: Scrub MVH terminology from project — top-level docs + open bead titles + CLAUDE.md guardrail
description:
USER DIRECTIVE 2026-05-18: 'MVH terminology has led to agents only half finish features — justifying stopping because the minimum amount has been done. That term was used to get us to a functional product.'

hk-ux915 fixed 4 drifted lines but did NOT scrub MVH from the project. Audit findings:
- Top-level docs: STATUS.md (19 hits), POST_MVH_PARALLELISM_ROADMAP.md (7 + filename), TASKS.md (6), HANDOFF.md (4), ROADMAP.md (1).
- Open beads: 25 of 143 reference MVH (e.g. 'Handler-pause (post-MVH): ...' titles). These shape future agent work.
- Go code: ~100k occurrences across ~3400 files — most are spec citations / test names. Out of scope for this bead.

SCOPE (do):
1. Rewrite the top-level docs to remove MVH framing. Replace with 'operational milestone' (project-level threshold achieved 2026-05-14) or 'phase-1' or just delete the phrase if it adds no information. The project is past MVH; no future work is 'MVH-scoped'.
2. Rename POST_MVH_PARALLELISM_ROADMAP.md -> POST_OPERATIONAL_PARALLELISM_ROADMAP.md and update references.
3. Retitle the 25 open beads that say '(post-MVH)' or 'MVH-required' or similar — replace with concrete framing ('post-phase-1', 'baseline', 'follow-up to <bead>', or delete the qualifier).
4. Append a CLAUDE.md '## Terminology — avoid MVH' section: 'MVH (Minimum Viable Harmonik) was a project-level milestone achieved 2026-05-14. It is NOT a per-feature scope label. New plans/beads/specs MUST NOT use MVH framing — it has historically licensed half-built features. Use Done means... criteria per plans/README.md instead.'

OUT OF SCOPE:
- Go test names / spec citation strings (these encode historical contracts; cannot be blindly renamed).
- Closed beads / archived plans / kerf history (immutable artifacts per file-discipline).
- spec body text (separate per-spec audit, file as follow-up if needed).

ACCEPTANCE:
- grep MVH in HANDOFF/STATUS/ROADMAP/TASKS returns 0 (or only deliberate historical 'MVH-achieved-2026-05-14' context lines).
- br list --status=open titles: 0 hits for 'post-MVH' / 'MVH-required'.
- CLAUDE.md has the new section.
- POST_MVH_PARALLELISM_ROADMAP.md renamed; references updated.

This bead is the structural fix for the half-built-systems pattern at the terminology layer; hk-37zy8, hk-2hb2y, hk-yjduq were the symptom.
close_reason: Top-level docs scrubbed (5 files); 6 open beads retitled; AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md guardrail added: a61805e

title: MVH-R11: end-to-end smoke test
description:
MVH_ROADMAP row #11. The proof-of-life integration test: one ready bead → workspace → twin run → bead closed → run_completed event appears in JSONL.

Location: NEW `internal/daemon/smoke_test.go` (or `test/smoke/` if separation is preferred — pick whichever matches sibling conventions; smoke_test.go in the daemon package is fine and keeps test discovery simple).

Shape:
1. `t.TempDir()` for projectDir. mkdir-p `.beads/` and `.harmonik/events/`.
2. Build (or use the prebuilt) `br` binary path via `exec.LookPath("br")` — if not present, `t.Skip` with a clear message (the integration test environment requires `br` on PATH; CI sets this).
3. Use `br init --here` (or whatever br supports) to seed a real beads SQLite DB, then create one ready bead via `br create`.
4. Build `test/twins/fail-immediately` (already landed at `840f500`) — wait, that's the WRONG twin for a happy-path smoke test. Use a tiny inline twin: `sh -c 'echo "ready"; exit 0'`. The fail-immediately twin is for T2 of the wave, not for the smoke test.
5. Construct `daemon.Config{ProjectDir, JSONLLogPath, BrPath, HandlerBinary: "/bin/sh", HandlerEnv: nil}` with `HandlerBinary` pointing at sh and a tiny script via `HandlerArgs` (note: HandlerArgs may need to be added to Config — if it isn't there, file a NEW bead inline as a row #11 prerequisite and use `HandlerBinary` to point at a fully-baked `/bin/sh` wrapper script written to t.TempDir()).
6. Run `daemon.Start(cfg)` in a goroutine with a 30-second deadline context. Wait for the bead status to flip to closed via polling `br show <id>` (10ms poll interval, 20s budget).
7. Read `<JSONLLogPath>` and assert it contains a `run_started` event and a `run_completed` event keyed to the same bead.
8. Assert the worktree was cleaned up (or, if cleanup is not yet wired in R10, file a follow-up bead and accept presence as the MVH state).

Acceptance:
- `go test ./internal/daemon/... -run TestMVHSmoke` passes within 60 seconds.
- `t.Skip` if `br` is not on PATH.
- One full happy-path run from claim to JSONL-event-visible-in-file.

Read `internal/daemon/workloop_test.go` (just landed at `5720333`) for the stubbed-handler test pattern — the smoke test is the REAL-handler analog. The work loop poll cadence is 2s; the smoke test's timeout budget should respect that.

If R10's work loop has gaps the smoke test reveals (e.g., worktree cleanup missing, run_completed payload incomplete), surface them as new follow-up beads at the end of dispatch; do not block the smoke test on fixing them — accept the gaps and file beads.
close_reason: TestMVHSmoke passes: one ready bead → workspace → run_completed in JSONL; 3 follow-up beads filed (hk-4e5b5, hk-7oz2f, hk-fgdgz)

title: Scenario-testing umbrella: cover operational 8-stage flow with twin
description:
Goal: scenario tests covering the 8-stage operational flow (daemon_started -> orphan_sweep -> bead_picked -> workspace+trust+agent-task.md -> claude launch -> splash dismiss + paste-inject -> claude work+commits -> daemon /quit-injects on commit -> Stop hook -> bead_closed) with the canonical twin (cmd/harmonik-twin-claude) in place of real claude.

Acceptance: each of the 11 umbrella fixes from 2026-05-14 (hk-lj1p9.3, hk-tjl40, hk-smuku, hk-lj1p9.4, hk-yngq2, hk-o5eww, hk-zchbu, hk-rf4ux, hk-53y35, hk-cmybm layer-1, hk-cmybm layer-2) has at least one scenario test that would catch its regression — or, where the twin cannot exercise the mechanism (splash render, pane physical timing, /quit-on-commit detection), a documented carve-out with a real-claude conformance test cited in its place.

Background:
- specs/scenario-harness.md (SH spec, v0.2.1) defines the harness contract; test/scenario/scenario_stub.go is placeholder. Implementation tracked by hk-i0tw.* (not in open list).
- Canonical twin: cmd/harmonik-twin-claude with YAML script-driver (scriptdriver.go, scenarios.go). Speaks CHB wire protocol on UDS or stdout. Does NOT simulate tmux pane render, welcome splash, agent commit on disk, /quit detection timing.
- Operational-green run: docs/dogfood-smoke-run-2026-05-14-operational-green.md

Children: twin parity audit, twin extension (splash+commit-on-cue), harness implementation hookup, CI cadence wiring.
notes: USER CLARIFICATION 2026-05-14: the twin's job is BEHAVIORAL parity, not visual parity. It does NOT need to render the UI the real claude renders. It DOES need to: start on cue, accept commands (paste-inject equivalents), produce output, and call hooks (or the corresponding daemon-side mechanism) in the same shape as real claude. The hooks point matters specifically: harmonik writes hooks into the worktree's .claude/settings.json (per CHB / WM-040a); the twin should READ that file and CALL the hook commands in the same way real claude would. This converts the hook-relay end-to-end path into a twin-testable scenario without needing TUI emulation.
close_reason: All five scoped children landed: hk-cno1z (parity audit), hk-e66ht (twin settings+hooks), hk-mg1ya (scenario harness, 5 twin-feasible scenarios), hk-8ys88 (twin commit-on-cue + startup-delay), hk-4tttc (CI smoke+nightly). Scenario-testing umbrella complete.

The run completed successfully (APPROVE@iter2). The first alarm (iter-1, just-slow) was pure noise; the second usefully surfaced the hk-ip33d paste-submit stall, but only by coincidence. FIX OPTIONS: scale the stale threshold with bead size/complexity, make it configurable, or distinguish 'no events but pane actively streaming' from 'genuinely hung' (e.g. check pane activity / token progress before declaring stale). Low priority — informational alarm, did not break the run.

title: Launch-spec: pass --model and --effort to claude
description:
## Scope

Amend `internal/daemon/claudelaunchspec.go` (buildClaudeLaunchSpec, argv construction at line ~287, OQ3 allow-list) to append `--model <alias>` and `--effort <level>` when the resolved values from the resolution chain (parent epic) are non-empty. The resolved pair travels in `claudeRunCtx` as two new fields (`model`, `effort`).

## Argv shape after change

```
claude --session-id <uuid> [--model <alias>] [--effort <level>]
```

Or `--resume` form for implementer-resume — same trailing optional flags.

## Spec updates required

- specs/handler-contract.md §4.2 HC-055 — extend allow-list to admit `--model <alias>` and `--effort <level>`. Add a sentence noting both flags are optional and absent when the resolution chain produces empty.
- specs/claude-hook-bridge.md — note the new optional flags in CHB-008 if the section enumerates argv.

## Tests

- Unit: buildClaudeLaunchSpec emits flags when ctx fields populated; omits them when empty; preserves ordering (--session-id first, then --model, then --effort).
- The CheckForbiddenFlags pass MUST NOT reject `--model` / `--effort` (currently it shouldn't — they're not on the deny-list — but add a regression case).

## Out of scope

- Resolution chain itself (separate bead).
- Per-project config loader (separate bead).
- Label parser on claim path (separate bead — likely folded into resolution-chain impl).

## Refs

- Parent epic: hk-cfhj2.
- specs/handler-contract.md §4.2 HC-055.
close_reason: launch-spec --model/--effort argv wiring implemented in 0f8c014

title: Wire LifecycleState into Session/watcher; emit lifecycle_transition events
description:
Integrate the FSM from B2 into live handler/Session/watcher; emit the new lifecycle_transition event via the existing event bus; twin parity per HC §4.8.

Scope:

- internal/handlercontract/adapter.go: on each watched event (agent_ready/started/completed/failed/rate_limited), call Machine.Transition with the appropriate reason. Heartbeat does NOT transition (call RecordActivity instead).
- internal/daemon/workloop.go: SIGTERM path → StateTerminating; Wait return → StateTerminated or StateFailed.
- internal/eventmodel/types.go: register lifecycle_transition event type (additive, schema-version bump shared w/ decision_required).

Twin parity: claude-twin AND pi-twin drive the same Transition calls.

Acceptance:
- Scenario test of happy-path run: emits Spawning→Initializing→Ready→Executing→Terminating→Terminated in order.
- Silent-hang scenario: transitions to StateFailed{reason=silent_hang} BEFORE run_stale; run_stale carries the lifecycle snapshot.
- hk-za5mz reproduction: iter-2 stuck-in-Ready surfaces as a deterministic transition-timeout event (NOT a 10-min run_stale).

Depends on: B2, B0.

title: Escape-detector (hk-77q8e) sibling-merge exclusion masks a real same-file escape (false-negative)
description: PREFERRED FIX (named-queues): hk-zguy6 now holds mergeMu across the escape check (checkMainWorkingTreeDirty, workloop.go:2311), so the check can NEVER observe a transient sibling-merge dirty window (the sibling's update-ref->reset-hard is also under mergeMu). That likely makes siblingMergeChangedPaths (workloop.go:3735, the false-negative source) REDUNDANT. VERIFY: confirm the escape check is ALWAYS invoked under mergeMu (no other unlocked call site). IF redundant -> REMOVE siblingMergeChangedPaths entirely (eliminates the false-negative), keep the snapshotUntrackedFiles baseline, and ADD a same-file collision regression test (sibling merges foo.go AND implementer escapes into foo.go -> escape IS still detected). IF the lock does NOT fully cover it -> fall back to controlpoints' blob-compare (only suppress a path whose dirty working-tree blob MATCHES the merged version). ORIGINAL FINDING (controlpoints): siblingMergeChangedPaths drops a path from escape-candidates if it appears in git diff preRunMainSHA..currentTip REGARDLESS of why the tree is dirty -> a sibling merge touching foo.go masks a genuine implementer escape into foo.go (false-negative). Tests only cover different-file escapes. P3 sub-note: AGENT_COMMS.md allowlist (workloop.go:3817) targets a RETIRED file - stale, harmless. Refs: hk-77q8e.
close_reason: done

title: MVH-R9-followup: wire DeadLetterSink into busimpl.Emit consumer-error path
description:
Wire the new handlercontract.DeadLetterSink into internal/eventbus/busimpl.go.

In busimpl.Emit, the sync-dispatch and async-dispatch loops currently discard consumer errors (observer panic recovery and subscription dispatch errors). Per MVH_ROADMAP row #9, a DeadLetterSink should receive those undeliverable events.

Wiring points:
1. Add a DeadLetterSink field to the EventBus struct (or its constructor options) in internal/eventbus/busimpl.go.
2. In the observer panic-recovery path (if one exists) call sink.Record(ctx, env, "observer_panic").
3. In the subscription dispatch error path call sink.Record(ctx, env, "consumer_error").

The sink is opened by daemon.Start (MVH_ROADMAP row #2 context) and injected at bus construction time. If the sink is nil, the bus MUST NOT panic — silently drop or log as before.

Depends on: hk-qyue9 (DeadLetterSink interface + OpenDeadLetterSink impl, merged to main).
close_reason: NewBusImplWithSink + panic recovery + consumer_error wiring in busimpl.go; commit 2fd2858

title: Wave group on shared daemon queue locks out concurrent agents (single-active + immutable wave = no 2nd submitter)
description:
On the persistent daemon, one active queue is allowed (QM-027 single-active) and a kind=wave group is immutable. So a SECOND agent/orchestrator can neither submit (queue_already_active, -32010) nor append (append_target_invalid, -32011) while agent A's wave is active. This breaks the multi-agent shared-queue model the updated AGENTS.md describes ('both agents put work into the queue'). Observed 2026-05-31: agent A submitted a wave (8-9 beads); agent B (named-queues track, hk-tigaf) was blocked from BOTH submit and append; switching to a stream group is the only way to share. Investigate + recommend: (a) should the daemon warn/reject a wave submit when a shared/multi-agent daemon is in use, or default the daily-loop submit to stream; (b) is this fully subsumed by named-queues (codename hk-tigaf, separate queues per agent) or does it need an interim guard now; (c) does QM-027 single-active need relaxing sooner than named-queues lands. Anchors: specs/queue-model.md QM-027 + GroupKind 2.4 (wave/stream) + error codes -32010/-32011; internal/queue/validation.go submit+append validation. Deliverable: root-cause note + ranked recommendation; file follow-up beads for any concrete fix.

## Investigation (2026-05-31)

ROOT CAUSE. Two normative rules combine. QM-027 (specs/queue-model.md:553-565): queue-submit is rejected if the daemon holds any queue whose status != completed -> queue_already_active (-32010), enforced at internal/queue/validation.go:230-242. QM-024 (queue-model.md:504-515, §7.1 QM-040 line 616 'Wave groups are immutable after submit'): append requires target kind==stream and status in {pending,active}; append to a wave returns append_target_invalid (-32011), enforced at validation.go:285-296. So agent A's active wave both blocks a fresh submit (QM-027) AND refuses appends (QM-024) -> agent B is fully locked out. Code matches spec exactly; design gap, not a bug.

GUIDANCE-vs-ENFORCEMENT GAP. harmonik run --beads already defaults to stream (hk-7nbey, cmd/harmonik/run.go:73-83). But the persistent-daemon path uses 'harmonik queue submit <file>' with a hand-written QueueSubmitRequest JSON. internal/queue/cli/submit.go:54-60 forwards the raw JSON with NO default for an omitted 'kind'. AGENTS.md (35-104, lines 102/152/158) only *advises* 'Use stream for the daily loop' — nothing prevents a wave submit. The friction is a human/agent picking wave on a shared daemon.

NAMED-QUEUES SUBSUMPTION. The epic (hk-tigaf) DOES subsume the root cause: Comp A / NQ-A1 (hk-tigaf.2) explicitly amends QM-027 single-active -> single-active-PER-NAME (03-components.md; 07-tasks.md:127-140) and re-words QM-061 from 'one queue' to 'one submitter, N queues'. Once landed, each agent owns a named queue -> zero single-active contention. BUT all 11 children are still OPEN (none landed); critical path NQ-R1 -> NQ-A1 -> NQ-A2 -> NQ-B1 -> ... -> NQ-E1 is multi-bead. An interim window remains.

RECOMMENDATION: (a)+(b) — interim guard NOW, fully subsumed later. Ranked: (1) make the daily-loop submit safe-by-default: in submit.go default an omitted group kind to 'stream' (matches run.go + AGENTS.md). (2) soft-warn (not reject) when a kind:wave group is submitted, noting it locks out concurrent submitters on a shared daemon. Do NOT relax QM-027 here — that is NQ-A1's job; premature relaxation would fork the spec. Wave stays legitimate for solo closed batches, so reject-on-wave is too blunt. Filed interim fix: hk-c6grw (P2, label queue, blocks hk-xyycc). Named-queues epic left independent of the stop-gap.

CAVEAT: no spec/ or internal/ edits made; live queue + daemon untouched.
close_reason: Subsumed: named-queues (hk-tigaf) landed — QM-027 single-active is now per-name, so each agent uses its own --queue, no shared-daemon lockout. Investigation + recommendation complete in bead; interim default-stream guard tracked separately as hk-c6grw.

title: fix: WriteAgentTask must MkdirAll .harmonik/ before atomic write
description: WriteAgentTask (CHB-028) fails with 'no such file or directory' when the worktree's .harmonik/ subdirectory does not exist. git worktree add creates the worktree root but not its .harmonik/ subdirectory. The fix is to add os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(target), 0o755) inside WriteAgentTask before the atomic write. This unblocks TestT6_10BeadSequentialDrain which was failing in 120s timeout with repeated WriteAgentTask errors.
close_reason: Fix implemented: added os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(target), 0o755) in WriteAgentTask before atomic write. TestT6_10BeadSequentialDrain passes in 42s. MkdirAll is idempotent and safe; git worktree add creates the worktree root but not the .harmonik/ subdirectory inside it.

title: queue submit: human-readable default output; --json opt-in
description:
Depends on: hk-5553i

## Current behavior

harmonik queue submit writes raw JSON response to stdout unconditionally.
Code path: internal/queue/cli/submit.go RunQueueSubmit() → client.go handleResponse() (line 136).
handleResponse() at client.go:143 calls fmt.Fprintf(out, "%s\n", data) with the daemon's raw JSON.

## Desired behavior

Default: human-readable confirmation. Example output (success):

  submitted: queue_id=019fbd3e-...
  items queued: 2 (hk-abc123, hk-def456)
  status: active

Example output (validation error):

  error: validation failed (code -32010)
  reason: bead hk-abc123 is not in a claimable state

With --json / --format json: existing raw JSON behavior, unchanged.

## Implementation sketch

1. Add --json / --format flags to RunQueueSubmit argument parsing.
2. Add a renderQueueSubmitResponse function that extracts queue_id, item count, status from the JSON result and prints them as prose.
3. On error path, extract code + message from errorBody and render as plain English.
4. --json path unchanged.
close_reason: queue submit: human-readable default implemented; --json opt-in works

title: Commit-gate should build+vet the MERGED tree before finalizing (parallel beads can redeclare)
description: Observed 2026-06-02: 7 parallel coverage beads (hk-j3hrn children) each passed their own ISOLATED worktree tests, but merging them one-by-one to main redeclared shared package-level test helpers (drawNonNilUUID, validSideEffect, ...) so 'go test ./internal/core/' build-fails on main though every bead was individually green. The commit-gate checks 'HEAD advanced' + reviewer + (hk-i2ie5) scenario tests, but does NOT compile/vet the MERGED tree, so cross-bead integration breaks land invisibly. PROPOSAL: after merging a bead to main, run 'go build ./... && go vet ./...' (ideally 'go test' for touched packages) in the merge gate; on failure, do NOT finalize that merge (skip/quarantine) and emit a merge_build_failed event. Sibling to hk-i2ie5. Anchors: internal/daemon merge-to-main + commit-gate path. Refs: hk-j3hrn.

title: DOGFOOD-BLOCKER #2: nil pointer panic in Watcher.Done from runReviewLoop goroutine
description:
Second dogfood revealed: after hk-2hb2y fix (pane race resolved), daemon now panics in review-loop:

panic: runtime error: invalid memory address or nil pointer dereference
goroutine 2906 [running]:
internal/handlercontract.(*Watcher).Done(...) at watcher_hc011.go:233
internal/daemon.runReviewLoop.func3() at reviewloop.go:512
created by internal/daemon.runReviewLoop in goroutine 56 at reviewloop.go:510

The goroutine spawned at reviewloop.go:510 calls Watcher.Done() on what appears to be a nil watcher. Beads got claimed (IN_PROGRESS) — pane race is fixed and tasks were paste-injected (47-byte payloads logged) — but the watcher wiring fails. Likely related to the substrate-attachment fix (a7bcd49) — possibly the watcher isn't being attached when substrate is non-nil. Inspect reviewloop.go:505-515 and the Watcher initialization.
close_reason: Fixed at HEAD: nil-guards added in reviewloop.go at the two direct revWatcher.Done() call sites (510-516, 531-536). waitWithSocketGrace was already nil-safe; only the agent_ready-timeout direct calls needed fixing.

Actual work landed on main (daemon auto-merged the run branch), but bead remained IN_PROGRESS — required manual reconciliation. title: Auto-tune --max-concurrent to Claude subscription bandwidth: rolling 5h token-rate from ~/.claude transcripts vs a configured ceiling, with 429 backstop
description: Investigated 2026-06-01. SIGNAL: Claude Code writes per-message token usage to ~/.claude/projects/*/*.jsonl — each assistant line has timestamp(RFC3339)+usage{input_tokens,output_tokens,cache_creation_input_tokens,cache_read_input_tokens} (what ccusage parses). Sum over trailing 5h = rolling token-rate. Subscription cap is NOT exposed as headroom — only surfaces on hit as a 429-class agent_rate_limited line. EXISTING DORMANT PLUMBING: internal/handler/adapter_claudecode.go:117 DetectRateLimit() (retry_after_seconds); event agent_rate_limit_status (internal/core/agentevents_hqwn59.go:436); budget_accrual/warning/exhausted (internal/core/budgetevents_hqwn59.go) — ALL currently unemitted under the tmux/ClaudeCode substrate (0 in events.jsonl). Dispatch gate: internal/daemon/workloop.go maxConcurrent, sealed at startup (EM-051). PROPOSAL (simplest viable): ~60s tuner sums (input+output+cache_creation, EXCLUDE cache_read initially) across ALL ~/.claude/projects (shared subscription) over trailing 5h; effectiveMax=clamp(round(N_max*(ceiling-used)/ceiling),1,N_max); make maxConcurrent a runtime atomic the gate reads. Keep DetectRateLimit as emergency multiplicative-decrease (snap toward 1, back off retry_after_seconds). One knob: --subscription-token-ceiling (per-5h). RISK: per-tier limits undocumented → ceiling is empirical (start conservative, raise until a 429); cache_read may not count toward cap.
close_reason: done

RCA confirmed via sub-agent reading hk-wkzlc session log (run 019e4e3b-5534, 219-line JSONL):
- Implementer engaged correctly: read brief, explored codebase for ~7 min (29 Bash calls, mostly Reads + greps)
- Started editing at ~9:58 wall mark (5 file mods: 4 Edits + 1 Write in lines 197-213)
- /quit arrived at ~10:00 mid-Edit; no stop_reason=end_turn, no model wrap-up
- 0 of 29 Bash calls were 'git add' or 'git commit'
- Class: BUDGET-EXHAUSTION mid-edit, not tool-error and not self-quit

Fix (landed): .claude/implementer-protocol.md gained 'Commit-early discipline' section instructing implementer to commit WIP after first file modification, then amend/squash before exit. Protects against /quit mid-multi-file-edit.

Compose with hk-mmh8f (no-review-loop false-positive) — that's the daemon-side bug; this is the implementer-side workaround.

Status update: closing as duplicate of hk-mmh8f + implementer-protocol fix once verified via next batch.

In PRODUCTION the daemon is DESIGNED to merge one-at-a-time (AGENTS.md daily-loop), so this should not occur. VERIFY: with named queues + per-queue workers + a global cap, can two runs from DIFFERENT queues completing simultaneously both enter mergeRunBranchToMain concurrently? If yes, the loser gets a non-FF push rejection -> bead reopened (EM-053) -> wasted slot/churn. Check whether there is a GLOBAL merge-serialization point in the workloop or only per-queue. Anchors: internal/daemon/workloop.go mergeRunBranchToMain + the 4 beadRunOne call sites; lockedMergeRunBranchToMain (test-only mergeMu, nil in prod). If a real race exists, fix = promote mergeMu to a production daemon-level merge mutex. Low priority unless concurrent multi-queue completion is common.
