title: DOT run misclassified as FAILED ('reached terminal node X') when success terminal is reached via a non-agentic intermediate node
description:
DAEMON BUG (confirmed against spec). The DOT cascade driver classifies run success by INBOUND-EDGE TOPOLOGY instead of terminal-node identity — the exact inference WG-021 forbids. Effect: a workflow where the success terminal is reached via a non-agentic intermediate node (e.g. reviewer→[APPROVE]→finalize→close) is misclassified run_FAILED ('dot: reached terminal node "close"') and the bead is reopened, even though it reached the declared success terminal with a SUCCESS outcome. Limits DOT to the exact review-loop shape.

ROOT CAUSE: internal/daemon/dot_cascade.go — on reaching a terminal (line ~284), success is decided by dotTerminalIsSuccess(graph, currentNodeID) (lines ~620-638), which returns success iff the terminal has ≥1 APPROVE-conditioned inbound edge AND no unconditional/non-APPROVE inbound edge. review-loop's close has inbound 'reviewer->close [APPROVE]' → success. With a finalize node, close's inbound is 'finalize->close' (unconditional) → misclassified failure. The classifier never reads the terminal ID or the outcome status.

SPEC (normative, daemon contradicts it):
- EM-015c (execution-model.md:319-321): a run reaches terminal/completed when current node_id ∈ terminal_node_ids AND last outcome.status ∈ {SUCCESS, PARTIAL_SUCCESS}.
- EM-015b (execution-model.md:314): run_completed emits on entering a terminal_node_ids node with outcome.status ∈ {SUCCESS,PARTIAL_SUCCESS}.
- WG-021 (workflow-graph.md:292-298): terminal disposition MUST be communicated by distinct terminal node IDs; spec does NOT direct consumers to inspect the last edge's preferred_label. WG-022 reserves close (normal) + close-needs-attention (operator-attention).

FIX (pure code, NO spec change — brings daemon into conformance):
1. In dot_cascade.go terminal branch (~line 284), classify disposition by TERMINAL NODE IDENTITY per WG-021/WG-022, not edge topology. Success = the run reached a success terminal (the reserved 'close', or more generally any terminal that is NOT 'close-needs-attention'); needs-attention = 'close-needs-attention'. Prefer threading the caller-resolved success-terminal id (see header comment lines ~47-48) rather than hardcoding the literal.
2. The last outcome.status is already SUCCESS at the terminal hop (lines ~205/596), satisfying EM-015c clause (a); the existing decision.Failed branch (~292-310) already handles genuine FAIL/cap-hit, so the terminal branch should trust terminal identity + the SUCCESS status.
3. DELETE dotTerminalIsSuccess and edgeConditionMentionsApprove (lines ~609-652) — both encode the forbidden heuristic.

TEST: add a daemon test driving a graph where the success terminal is reached via a non-agentic intermediate node (reviewer→[APPROVE]→finalize[non-agentic]→close), asserting the run is classified SUCCESS (not 'reached terminal node' failure). Keep the existing review-loop APPROVE test green.

ACCEPTANCE: a DOT workflow with a non-agentic node between the approving node and 'close' completes successfully (run_completed, bead closed). Verified live by QA re-running /tmp/dot-qa-complex1.dot. Source: QA 2026-05-28 + investigation (run 019e6d71).
close_reason: done

title: Stuck-dispatched queue item deadlocks ALL dispatch: 'main' wedged active, group never completes (QM-027 blocks every submit)
description:
ROOT CAUSE (confirmed by investigation 2026-06-02, HEAD STILL REPRODUCES — not a stale-binary artifact): a daemon restart mid-run abandons the in-flight run goroutine, leaving the queue item status=dispatched on disk with no process to transition it dispatched->completed/failed. Startup reconciler QM-002a (internal/lifecycle/startup_pl005_qm002.go:266-354) reverts dispatched->pending ONLY when the bead is open (~line 294); if the bead was closed by another path (e.g. the work landed via a different queue), QM-002a SKIPS it. reconcileThreeWay (startup_pl005_qm002.go:390) Class A advances pending+bead-closed->completed, but the non-pending/non-terminal branch hits a bare 'continue' at line 428 — so a dispatched+bead-closed item is NEVER advanced. Combined with a legitimately-failed sibling (QM-030 all-terminal check, internal/queue/state.go:233), the group is permanently un-completable -> queue stays status=active -> QM-027 single-active blocks ALL submits on every queue name (both orchestrator agents silently wedged).

FIX (daemon-dispatchable, fully specified): in internal/lifecycle/startup_pl005_qm002.go ~420-428 (reconcileThreeWay Class A scan), ALSO advance ItemStatusDispatched items whose bead is closed/tombstone to ItemStatusCompleted — mirror the existing Class A 'pending+bead-closed' branch and its persist-before-emit pattern. Fires at startup before the workloop runs; idempotent. Add a unit test for the new 'dispatched+bead-closed -> completed' (Class A') branch.

EVIDENCE: run_id 019e8606-68ab-75c8-b265-9c09abb0ab02 (hk-vhped) fired run_started + agent_ready then NO run_completed/run_failed (goroutine abandoned on daemon binary swap); sibling hk-11xkn failed=cross_queue_duplicate (also queued on fw-11xkn). Original wedged queue: main 019e8601-294d-7cc5-95f4-564df6aca4fc.
close_reason: done

title: Review-loop iter-2 STILL no-progress after lckbv+7x7ea+poy7k — claude --resume likely not processing pasted feedback as a new turn; REPRODUCE BEFORE FIXING
description: THREE targeted fixes have NOT cracked iter-2: hk-lckbv (buffer-name, landed), hk-7x7ea (reviewer-feedback.iter-N.md now written, landed), hk-poy7k (combined task+feedback into single paste eliminating the submit-race, landed). Yet iter-2 STILL fails 'no-progress detected at iteration 2: diff hash unchanged'. REPRO: wave-8 a8bg.18+a8bg.35 (pre-poy7k) AND wave-10 a8bg.35 (run 019e7342-b280, POST all 3 fixes, fully-rebuilt daemon) all failed identically. poy7k made NO observable difference to the outcome => the root cause is NOT the paste mechanics. LIKELY ROOT: claude --resume on the tmux substrate does not process pasted input as a new implementation turn — the resumed session receives the feedback buffer but never starts a new user-turn / does new work / commits a changed diff (cf hk-isq02: claude --resume doesn't re-fire SessionStart). This may be an architectural limit of driving claude --resume via tmux paste. DO NOT fix-and-pray (4th blind fix). NEXT STEPS (reproduce-first): (1) build a minimal repro: a 2-iteration review-loop on a trivial bead where iter-1 commits, reviewer REQUEST_CHANGES, observe the resumed implementer's tmux pane + claude transcript — does the pasted feedback appear AND does claude start a new turn + edit + commit? (2) instrument: capture the resumed pane content + claude session jsonl at iter-2. (3) if claude --resume ignores pasted turns, consider an alternative iter-2 mechanism (fresh claude session with feedback in the launch prompt/agent-task.md, NOT --resume). Worktrees preserved: .harmonik/worktrees/019e7342-b280* (a8bg.35 iter-2). Companion to hk-7x7ea, hk-poy7k, hk-lckbv, hk-isq02.
notes: ROOT CAUSE FOUND 2026-05-30 (investigator, evidence-backed): NOT paste mechanics. Under tmux substrate handler.launchViaSubstrate returns early at internal/handler/handler.go:303 (subSess.Stdout()==nil) so the SessionIDInterceptor (reviewloop.go:291-321) never fires; iter-1 falls back to rlSynthesiseClaudeSessionID (reviewloop.go:614-619), iter-2 does 'claude --resume <synthetic>' (claudelaunchspec.go:358-359) against a session that never existed -> no commit -> no_progress. Evidence: events.jsonl shows synthetic claude_session_id on all 3 stalled runs; claude_session_id_persisted fires 0 times; iter-2 commit_landed:true is a false positive (HEAD!=parentSHA satisfied by iter-1 commit). FIX: capture real session_id from hook-relay payload (already validated via bridge_session_id_mismatch), not the never-firing stdout interceptor. Prior fixes lckbv/7x7ea/poy7k all fixed wrong layer (paste delivery).
close_reason: Resolved: session-id capture-real-minted-id-under-tmux fix landed bc9d9beb, reviewed, validated in-situ (B6/B5/wave-2 all iter-2 APPROVE). Sibling paste-submit race fixed separately as hk-ip33d (3870ddc0).

title: Escape-detector false-positive: a sibling run transient dirty-main attributed to the WRONG bead completion -> false implementer_escaped_worktree
description: ROOT CAUSE CONFIRMED (investigator 2026-06-02): the escape check checkMainWorkingTreeDirty at internal/daemon/workloop.go:2311 runs OUTSIDE mergeMu (this run acquires mergeMu later, :2366). All daemon runs share ONE main working tree. A SIBLING run's merge (lockedMergeRunBranchToMain) does update-ref (:3407) -> git reset --hard HEAD (:3435) under mergeMu, transiently dirtying the shared tree; a different run's UNPROTECTED escape check sees that transient dirt and blames itself. Existing exclusions miss it: snapshotUntrackedFiles (:3808) excludes only pre-existing UNTRACKED files; siblingMergeChangedPaths (:3923) excludes only files whose committed SHA advanced AFTER preRunMainSHA. Incident: hk-qgzso committed 9613bf24 BEFORE hk-ulp7v captured preRunMainSHA, so no SHA advance was recorded and the later transient reset-hard edit was not excluded -> hk-ulp7v FALSE-failed. Neither bead escaped; the dirt is the daemon's own merge flow. This is a NAMED-QUEUES concurrency hazard (concurrent cross-queue merges). FIX: wrap checkMainWorkingTreeDirty (:2311) in mergeMu.Lock()/Unlock() (nil-guard for the test override) so the check is mutually exclusive with any sibling's update-ref->reset-hard window. ADD regression test: sibling commit PREDATES this run's baseline + a transient reset-hard window (existing TestEscapeDetect_SiblingMergeRaceNotFlagged covers only the SHA-postdates case). Cheaper + more correct than widening the diff baselines. DISPATCH NOTE: hk-zguy6 and hk-ulp7v BOTH edit workloop.go -> dispatch serially (one stream group) to avoid a merge-conflict skip.
close_reason: done

title: Extend .harmonik/branching.yaml v1 with protect_branches (add to knownKeys at branching.go
description: FULL: Extend .harmonik/branching.yaml v1 with protect_branches (add to knownKeys at branching.go:159, parse into Defaults); daemon resolves project-level lands_on/protect_branches as target/deny-list when CLI flags absent (precedence: flag > branching.yaml > built-in) | SCOPE: internal/branching/branching.go parser + Defaults struct; daemon target resolution | Source: harmonik-productization-design workflow 2026-06-02. codename:productization; integration-branch P0s gate the work-project deploy; the 3 beads editing mergeRunBranchToMain (target-branch threading, start_from retarget, post-merge build gate) MUST be dispatched SERIALLY.
design: PRIOR ATTEMPT BLOCKED (iter 1, flags: ordering-bug/feature-broken/safety-bypass). Implementation was ~90% correct — parser, Defaults struct, tests, and reflect.DeepEqual migration ALL GOOD; keep them. THE SINGLE FIX is ordering in daemon.go: the WM-005b wiring block (reads branching.yaml lands_on into cfg.TargetBranch and protect_branches into the deny-list) was placed AFTER `resolvedTargetBranch := resolveTargetBranch(cfg.TargetBranch)` (line ~634) AND AFTER the hk-sul12 protect_branches safety check (lines ~637-640). Both consume pre-wiring state, so (1) lands_on is dead-on-arrival because resolvedTargetBranch is already fixed to "main", and (2) YAML-sourced protect_branches entries bypass the deny-list guard (safety-bypass). FIX: move the WM-005b wiring block to IMMEDIATELY BEFORE line 634 so resolveTargetBranch consumes the updated cfg.TargetBranch and the hk-sul12 guard validates the fully-resolved config. Precedence must stay flag > branching.yaml > built-in.

close_reason: Merged at b8d25f0. AR-001 four-axis Axes: line binding test in internal/specaudit/ar001_axes_test.go (validation-only scope per bead body). Caught 5 real spec defects: 3 in beads-integration.md (BI-025a/b/c idempotency=safe copy-paste error) and 2 in reconciliation/spec.md (RC-015 invalid llm-freedom=delegated and io-determinism=non-deterministic vocabulary). All pinned in expected-violations skip-list with stale-entry guard mirroring AR-005 sibling. Reviewer APPROVE no findings, all violations confirmed real. Follow-up beads hk-zs0.59 + hk-zs0.60 file the spec fixes.

title: Verification is a role-function, not a node-type enum value
description: Per AR-029: verification MUST be a role-function of a workflow node, NOT a `node-type` enum member. Canonical enum (per execution-model) is `{agentic, non-agentic, gate, control-point, sub-workflow}`; no `verification-node` value admitted. `agentic` + `actor_role=Reviewer` is verification-capable (cognition-tagged); `non-agentic` deterministic-checker is verification-capable (mechanism-tagged). The term `verification-node` in this and other specs refers to so-configured nodes, not an enum value.
close_reason: SUBSUMED: specs/architecture.md:283 — AR-029 text fully present in spec corpus since 2026-04-24 (commit 715b3c7); all required content (role-function framing, enum exclusion, cognition/mechanism tagging, term disambiguation) is normative

title: Verification-result shape (no RETRY admissible)
description: Per AR-030: a `verification-result` MUST be the outcome of a `verification-node` (per AR-029) and conform to the outcome shape declared in execution-model with `status ∈ {SUCCESS, FAIL, PARTIAL_SUCCESS}`. The `RETRY` outcome status is NOT admissible for a verification-result (a verification node returning RETRY MUST fail-hard — classification is a category error). Mechanism-tagged verification nodes populate `evidence`; cognition-tagged populate `notes`. Hyphenated form is canonical.
close_reason: SUBSUMED by specs/architecture.md:289 — AR-030 spec text present in corpus (precedent hk-zs0.30 / commit a79c2a7)

title: Quality-gate is a gate that reads a verification-result
description: Per AR-031: a `quality-gate` MUST be a gate (per control-points) whose evaluator references a prior verification-result. Quality-gates are gates that read verification outcomes; they are NOT themselves verification. Three hyphenated forms (`verification-node`, `verification-result`, `quality-gate`) are distinct and MUST NOT be used interchangeably.
close_reason: SUBSUMED by specs/architecture.md:295 — AR-031 spec text present in corpus (precedent hk-zs0.30 / commit a79c2a7)

title: Seven roles named (canonical role vocabulary)
description: Per AR-032: foundation names seven roles drawn from the AlphaGo north-star: Planner, Researcher, Builder, Reviewer, Verifier, Scheduler, Governor. These names MUST be canonical across all specs. A subsystem spec inventing alternatives for an already-covered function fails review.
close_reason: Merged in pipeline (after hk-872.17). AR-032 canonical role-vocabulary binding test in internal/specaudit/ar032_roles_test.go (476 lines, Path A — 4 syntactic frames within normative requirement bodies: actor_role=<v>, role=<v>, **role**=`<v>`, `role` value `<v>`). Validates against 9 canonical names (7 AR-032 roles + daemon + reconciliation per EM-046; cross-checked against internal/core/actorrole.go without import). Caught 2 real spec defects in specs/reconciliation/spec.md (RC-015 line 453, RC-015a line 470 — both use 'investigator' as a non-canonical role name). All pinned in expected-violations skip-list with stale-entry guard mirroring AR-001/AR-005 sibling pattern. Reviewer APPROVE no MEDIUM/BLOCKER findings. Follow-up bead hk-zs0.62 captures the spec fix (rename or AR-032 amendment).

title: MVH-required vs declared-but-deferred role split
description: Per AR-033: Planner/Builder/Reviewer MUST be implemented at MVH (sufficient to run the minimum self-build cycle). Researcher/Verifier/Scheduler/Governor are declared-but-deferred (named in foundation so subsystems do not invent alternatives, but not required at MVH). Each deferred role MUST be activatable when its triggering pattern appears in a workflow without foundation revision.
close_reason: SUBSUMED by specs/architecture.md:309 — AR-033 spec text present in corpus (precedent hk-zs0.30 / commit a79c2a7)

title: Per-role definition scope (purpose + typical actions + exclusions)
description: Per AR-034: this spec MUST define per role: purpose, typical actions, what it does not do. Concrete permission schemas are owned by control-points. AR describes WHAT a role IS; control-points describes WHAT A ROLE IS ALLOWED TO DO. Specs do not duplicate.
close_reason: SUBSUMED by specs/architecture.md:315 — AR-034 spec text present in corpus (precedent hk-zs0.30 / commit a79c2a7)

title: Merge responsibility is not a top-level role
description: Per AR-036: merge responsibility MUST NOT be a top-level role. A workflow MAY contain a distinct merge node assigned to whatever agent type the policy names. Merge node MUST operate in the run's leased worktree (per workspace-model), not a new worktree. Merge is a node function, not a role.

title: Centralized-controller is the explicit inverse of Gas Town decentralization
description: Per AR-038: the centralized-controller invariant (AR-INV-007) is the explicit inverse of Gas Town polecats/mayors. Proposals introducing file-based agent-to-agent handoff or per-agent worktree ownership for the same run fail this principle and MUST be rejected or refactored.

title: Merge agent operates in the run's worktree
description: Per AR-039: a merge agent (when workflow uses a distinct merge node) MUST operate in the same worktree the implementer used; the worktree is leased by the workflow run, not by any individual agent.

title: Acknowledged centralized-controller tradeoff (no graceful degradation)
description: Per AR-040: the centralized-controller principle carries a real cost — if the daemon dies mid-run, every agent goes silent and reconciliation is required on restart. A decentralized alternative (Gas Town) would offer graceful degradation; harmonik foregoes that property in favor of routing simplicity. Acceptable within the MVH envelope (single-user, single-project, dev-machine). Pivot to decentralized requires re-evaluating under remote-daemon / multi-operator-single-daemon scenarios; pivot is a foundation amendment.
close_reason: SUBSUMED by specs/architecture.md:353 — AR-040 spec text present in corpus (precedent hk-zs0.30 / commit a79c2a7)

title: Sensor: centralized-controller invariant

Agents MUST perform only cognitive work. Agent-to-agent coordination MUST route through the daemon. File-based or ad-hoc-IPC agent-to-agent coordination is forbidden. **All cross-subsystem registries (policy, control-point, handler, skill) are daemon-owned and in-process for MVH**; relocating a registry outside the daemon requires the AR-020 amendment protocol. **Sensor (reviewer-agent scenario test, per §10.2 AR-038..AR-045 group):** proposals introducing file-based agent handoff, per-agent worktree ownership for the same run, or out-of-daemon cross-subsystem registry are rejected.

title: Sensor: three-artifact separation

No spec MAY introduce a fourth compositional artifact (e.g., `feature`, `story`, `epic`, `capability-plan`, `initiative`). "Compositional" = a durable, authored artifact carrying work-shape; configuration artifacts (policy YAML, skill manifests, `.harmonik/*` sidecars) are not compositional and are exempt. **Sensor:** corpus-lint per §10.2 AR-047..AR-051 group (`feature` is non-normative; no fourth compositional term introduced).

title: real auto_status: derive non-SUCCESS outcome from work-product or embedded status marker
description: Future feature deferred from attractor-parity v1 (hk-gv5n5). At v1, non_committing controls exactly one axis (commit-or-not) and auto_status is reserved/not-accepted per workflow-graph.md §4 WG-041. The real auto_status semantics: allow an engine-side derivation of a non-SUCCESS Outcome (e.g. FAILURE, REQUEST_CHANGES) from (a) inspection of the node's work-product (e.g. test results, lint output), or (b) a structured marker embedded in the handler's output stream ({"status": "FAILURE", ...}). This is distinct from non_committing, which only controls whether a commit is required for SUCCESS. Design questions: (1) should the marker be a progress-stream NDJSON message type or a post-run artifact? (2) which Outcome values are derivable vs always-human (REQUEST_CHANGES vs FAILURE)? (3) interaction with the reviewer loop — does a marker-derived REQUEST_CHANGES re-enter the reviewer path or short-circuit it? Spec section to extend: workflow-graph.md §4 (add auto_status attribute semantics), handler-contract.md §I (add status-derivation protocol), execution-model.md §7 (engine-side derivation logic).

title: MVH-R7: Handler.Launch — exec Claude Code + wire SpawnWatcher
description:
MVH_ROADMAP row #7. Concrete `Handler.Launch` that takes a `LaunchSpec` (binary path, args, env, working dir = worktree path, role), execs the child via `exec.CommandContext` + `lifecycle.SpawnChildSysProcAttr`, wraps it in a `Session` (from row #6, internal/handler/session.go), and attaches a `handlercontract.Watcher` to the session's Stdout pipe via `SpawnWatcher`.

Location: NEW file `internal/handler/handler.go` + test. Same package as session.go.

Sketch:
type LaunchSpec struct { Binary string; Args []string; Env []string; WorkDir string; Role string }
type Handler interface { Launch(ctx context.Context, spec LaunchSpec) (Session, handlercontract.Watcher, error) }
func NewHandler() Handler { ... }

In Launch:
1. `exec.CommandContext(ctx, spec.Binary, spec.Args...)`
2. `cmd.Dir = spec.WorkDir`, `cmd.Env = spec.Env`
3. `cmd.SysProcAttr = lifecycle.SpawnChildSysProcAttr()` (or whatever the actual symbol is — read internal/lifecycle/spawndaemonchild_*.go)
4. `sess, err := NewSession(ctx, cmd)` (row #6)
5. `watcher, err := handlercontract.SpawnWatcher(ctx, sess.Stdout(), spec.Role)` (use existing SpawnWatcher from internal/handlercontract/watcher_hc011.go — read its signature)
6. Return session, watcher, nil.

Tests: use a tiny `sh -c "echo '{\"event\":\"hello\"}'; sleep 1"` as the binary so SpawnWatcher has something to parse. Assert the watcher receives at least one event, session.Wait returns clean.

Out of scope: the ClaudeCodeAdapter (row #8 — separate bead) and the work-loop integration (row #10).

title: Project-level branching defaults config file (.harmonik/branching.yaml)
description:
Parent: hk-8m91i (epic).

## Rationale

Most beads in a project will share the same base/target/landing-strategy. Forcing every bead to carry these fields is boilerplate. A per-project default file lets the operator say once: "for this project, base=main, target=harmonik/integration, landing=squash" and only the exceptions need per-bead overrides.

## Proposed shape

`.harmonik/branching.yaml` (sibling to existing `.harmonik/` daemon files):

```yaml
defaults:
  base_branch: main
  target_branch: harmonik/integration
  landing_strategy: squash
```

Per-bead overrides (carrier per hk-8m91i) win when present; otherwise the project default applies; otherwise the spec-level default (`main` / `main` / `squash`).

## Loaded by

The daemon at workloop claim-time, NOT at daemon start (so operator edits take effect on the next bead claimed without daemon restart). Cache invalidation by mtime is sufficient for MVH.

## Open questions

- Is this file checked into the project repo (yes — it's a team convention, should travel with the project) or kept local (no, that defeats the team-workflow purpose)? Recommendation: checked in, gitignored only if operator explicitly wants per-developer overrides via a `.local.yaml` sibling.
- Schema version field? Yes — `version: 1` for forward compat.

## Dependency

Lower priority than the carrier decision (hk-8m91i) and the worktree-cut impl bead — this is the ergonomic layer on top.
close_reason: Loader package internal/branching landed; Load/LoadCached + typed errors + 10 unit tests; follow-up wiring bead hk-umxx4 created
